CARTOGRAPHER’S CORNER
In Cartographer’s Corner, you will put your geographical knowledge to the test by building a map in front of you round after round.

CONTINENTAL SHAKE UP
In Continental Shake Up, the group of players build a map in the center of the table as they play. But watch out! Only those cards that are correctly placed will remain on the table. Additionally, the riskiest placements are the ones that will give you the most points.

Security systems analyst Ryan Bosley and his wife, Monica, book a vacation rental home by a lake to relax and reconnect for a month. But after the couple gets into an argument at a neighbor's cookout, Monica finds herself alone in their dark and empty rental house.

When Ryan sees Monica again, she's in a pool of her own blood, dead. The police suspect Ryan, but he believes another guest at the cookout is the killer.

Can you unravel the relationships around the lakeside housing community and clear Ryan's name?

Difficulty 2/5

The Jaws of Itzl were once part of a grander warhost sent against the Skaven. Yet a terrible cave-in saw most of the Seraphon buried, including their Starmaster. Only the blessed Kro-Jax, a living wellspring of ferocious Azyrite energy, and a handful of his reptilian fellows escaped. Kro-Jax receives intermittent telepathic commands from the trapped Starmaster, but magical interference from emberstone leaves them scrambled. The Sunblood attempts to heed these orders regardless, even if they drive him to seemingly nonsensical or ferociously violent acts.

Saurus Oldblood Kro-Jax, the Aegis, leads this savage Seraphon warband across Embergard. Two brutal Saurus unleash their reptilian rage by his side – mace-wielding So-Kar, and the terrifying Ro-Tak, the Guardian, with their long-spear. These three mighty warriors are accompanied and assisted by a congregation of small venomous creatures known as Sotek's Venomites.

The Jaws of Itzl are a Strike-style warband which would synergise well with a Strike Rivals deck (available separately).

This kit includes a full Jaws of Itzl warband for use in games of Warhammer Underworlds, along with fighter cards and warscroll cards for the warband.

The conflict in the Despénsico Sea between the nations of Salsacia and Conservia was a long and tumultuous war. Every conflict has a story behind it, and fortunately, this one ends with a happy conclusion.

Salsacia vs Conservia includes 3 games that tell the story of the conflict: The Conquest of the Tower, The Training Field, and The Reconciliation Match.

In The Conquest of the Tower, you'll have to battle the opposing team for possession of the tower. Use your skill and aim to knock down the opponent’s cans and achieve the objective.

In The Training Field, you'll learn about the origins of the conflict. Practice your skill and precision to position your cans as close to the puck as possible. But be careful not to leave them exposed, as your opponent might use your cans as targets for their shooting practice.

In The Reconciliation Match, the happy ending of the story will be revealed. Use your skill and strategy to win the most chaotic soccer match in the Despénsico Sea. Score more goals than your rival using your cans as true athletes.

Game based on the successful Chilean TV comedy show "31 Minutes".

Urban Operations: Second Edition is a game that simulates modern tactical combat in a range of historical and hypothetical urban settings. It uses unique mechanics for chain of command, logistical support, collateral damage and close-quarters combat resolution. Blocks for units and concealed (face-down) elements of the board capture the fog of war.

The game was initially developed as a training tool for France's National Centre of Excellence for Urban Warfare Training. It will appeal to anyone interested in learning and applying modern urban combat fighting techniques, and re-creating the action from the 16 scenarios in the game, including:


     Mogadishu (1993) - six scenarios of Western units battling Somali militia
     Fulda (1985) - six scenarios of a hypothetical WW3 battle between Soviet and NATO forces
     Other single scenario settings including USMC in Fallujah (2004), the British in Basra (2003), and the Russians in Groszy (1999).


The Second Edition has the following changes:

     New player aids
     Clarification of key rules and all errata incorporated into 


a new rulebook

     Updates to boards and maps to improve 3D effects and ease of line of sight assessments
     New advanced rules for Combined Combat actions
     Various other minor improvements to playability

description:
The classic "Tapa Certo" with the characters from Monsters Inc. Have fun collecting Monster Cards with your plastic hands with suction cup.

From 2 to 4 players, everyone plays at the same time. At the beginning, all the "round shaped" cards are spread out on the playing area. Then, a matching "square shaped" card is revealed and the first player to tap the corresponding "round shaped" card with his plastic hand gets that card. The process repeats itself until every card is taken. The winner is the player with more cards at the end of the game.

It's a Kids game, and there are a lot of variants of this game with other IPs, or regular animals, or anything else...

The game is similar to "Lince" but is played with those plastic hands with suction cups, and as the cards are getting taken off the board, the game becomes easier. The only other game that come close to this one is "Monster Mash".

description:
Japan attacked the Allied powers on 7 December 1941 and ran rampant across the Pacific Ocean and Asia for six months. But in August of 1942, the Allies would finally strike back. On 7 August, ships appeared off the coast of a small island in the Solomons called Guadalcanal to disgorge troops of the United States Marine Corps. Guadalcanal Diary: The Battle for Henderson Field allows you to recreate the Guadalcanal campaign from the initial landings in August 1942 until the end of November, when the Japanese were all but finished.

You have command of all the major units that fought in the campaign across ground, sea, and air.  As the Allied player, you have everything that can be cobbled together to take the airfield on Guadalcanal and hold it. You need to maximize force but be cautious enough to withhold forces to fend off the Japanese counterattacks. As the Japanese player, you must use what forces you have to defeat the Allies before they become too powerful.

Guadalcanal Diary is the first in a series of games that will cover at a tactical and operational level the fight for the Southwest Pacific Area. This game covers the initial Allied landings on Guadalcanal until the end of November 1942, when effective resistance by the Japanese ended. Tactical combat on the ground can be combined with operational-level combat in the air and on the seas to make a comprehensive simulation of the Guadalcanal campaign.

Product Information:

Complexity: High
Solitaire Suitability: Medium
Players: 2
Playing Time: 2-40+ Hours
Map Scale: 300 yards per hex (Abstract on Operational Map)
Time Scale: Variable, 1 turn = 4 hours or 1 day
Unit Scale: Platoons and Companies

Sapling Scramble is a set-collecting card game for 2-5 players that takes 3 minutes to learn and 10 minutes to play. Make deals or deceive your opponents, pluck cards right out of their hand, and collect ingredients from the forest floor as you scramble to be the first to brew 3 potions. But be careful! Taking cards from another player might help them more than it helps you!

You play as the adorable Sapling, scuttling about the forest floor gathering ingredients from beneath a tree. The deck of ingredients cards is placed face-down in the center of the table and is referred to as the Tree. To start the game, you "Shake the Tree", revealing 8 face-up cards around the Tree. The Sapling of the group, or the youngest player, goes first.

Each turn, you take 1 of 3 actions before play continues to the left:

     Steal a card from an opponent's hand
     Play a Berrybomb
     Brew a Potion


Any time a player takes a card from your hand, immediately grab one of the face-up cards from beneath the Tree. If a Berrybomb is grabbed this way, the Berrybom is discarded and you "Shake the Tree", re-filling any of the empty slots around the Tree with new face-up cards.

If you play a Berrybomb on your turn, "Shake the Tree" and grab a face-up card.

To brew a Potion, discard the 3 ingredients listed on your Potion recipe and flip the card over. The first to brew 3 Potions wins the game!

Sapling Scramble is best enjoyed with lots of table talk. Make deals with other players to get rid of cards you don't want, or lead them into your Berrybomb traps! This simple but socially exciting card game is a perfect for families, game nights, class rooms, or games on the go!

—description from the publisher

Several changes have been made to this game, incorporating concepts such as the promotion of Generals and game-enhancing variants from long-time enthusiasts, Volunteers Magazine, and the designer himself.  A completely new Solitaire Version has been added, along with several charts to guide the single player through the experience.

The time is spring, 1861. The place is America, poised on the edge of armed conflict between the new Confederate States and supporters of the Union. Civil war will decide the issue.

boardgame: Forever Island: An Eco-Apocalyptic War Game
description:
In the distant future, a nuclear volcano has emerged from the sea covering what was long ago known as Florida. It is here that the last remaining humans, reduced to Stone Age technology, survive on a diet of mutant wildlife and radioactive coconuts. Do you have what it takes to unite them under your leadership, or will human ambition lead once again to environmental collapse? Manage your resources and alliances to expand your power, but beware volcanic eruptions, betrayal, and the ecological limits of the islands on which you depend.

Forever Island is a turn-based competitive strategy game played with 2 to 6 players. Players collect resources to expand their tribe through building, traveling, trading, raiding, and taxing, while being careful not to over-exploit nature. Forever Island can be played either in teams or individually. Play time can range from as little as 30 minutes with 2 players to as much as 2 hours with 6.

—description from the designer

Panambí is an educational card game about native butterflies and plants from Uruguay. In this playful biology lesson 2 teams of up to 3 players will face each other to build the best garden for butterflies.

Admire almost 50 species of native butterflies. Devise your garden and your strategy to win, adapt to different events and prepare for unexpected visitors that will sabotage your plans.

Meet more than 20 species of host or butterfly-attracting native plants, and learn whether they are medicinal, edible or bird-attracting.

Delight yourself with 80 illustrations to learn with your family, have fun with friends, bring to class or use as a field guide.

Love nature and a scientific curiosity about the world around us.

—description from the designer

Panambí es un juego de cartas educativo sobre mariposas y plantas nativas de Uruguay. En esta clase de biología en formato lúdico 2 equipos de hasta 3 jugadores cada uno se enfrentarán para crear el mejor jardín para mariposas.

Maravíllate con casi 50 especies de mariposas nativas. Idea tu jardín y tu estrategia para ganar, adáptate a diferentes eventos y prepárate para visitas inesperadas de personajes que sabotearán tus planes.

boardgame: Las Ligas de la Ñ: La Ciudad de las Damas
description:
Las Ligas de la Ñ: La Ciudad de las Damas ("The Ñ Leagues: The City of Ladies") is a race board game for 2 to 4 players, similar to the Game of Goose, but featuring heroines from the Spanish culture as the main characters.

In this new Ñ game (the ninth in the series), players must control one of the playable heroines (each with her own unique ability) to reach the City of Ladies square and become the chosen one to join the distinguished city founded by Christine de Pizan and governed by Mary (mother of Jesus).

All of these Ñ heroines are determined to claim citizenship of the City of Ladies, competing fiercely to outshine the other candidates. There can only be one! The time has come for Catalina de Erauso, Isabel la Católica, Lubna de Córdoba, María la Balteira, Teresa de Jesús, Wallada la Omeya, Zulema la Astróloga, Egeria and other famous women from classical Spanish literature!

Each board features 24 citizens of the City of Ladies (each corresponding to a unique square with distinct characteristics). The first board highlights illustrious women connected to Abrahamic religions, while the second focuses on women from the ancient world.

After another harsh winter in Catvale, 2 to 6 players take control of ambitious cats who emerge from their homes ready to race for dominance over the land. What might appear to humans as a simple valley is an epic realm to the cats, filled with enchanted forests, perilous foes, and countless adventures offering opportunities to gain luxury and prestige. The goal? Become the most feared, worshipped, or envied ruler of Catvale - how you get there is entirely up to you.

The game unfolds on a dynamic, tile-based map that creates a unique, ever-changing environment, ensuring no two games are ever the same. A fresh take on the adventure genre, Catvale blends exploration, resource management, and tactical decision-making. Players can choose from different playstyles—eco-focused, brawler, builder, adventurer, or a hybrid approach. Action dice, rolled at the start of each round, determine the strength of the actions players can take, adding an element of strategy and unpredictability. As the game progresses, players can level up their cat once, improving their base stats.

Each player shapes their cat’s path: will you be the aggressor, stealing prestige and robbing opponents of their dignity? Or perhaps you’ll focus on foraging and construction, fancying up your backyard with luxurious structures? Live off adventure by completing quests, fulfilling ambitions, and dueling for glory, or dominate the market through cunning auctions and hiring the best workers? With multiple paths to victory and high replayability, Catvale offers a delightful mix of strategy, luck, and creativity, where the future of the land rests in the paws of the most cunning and ambitious cat.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Nanty Narking: The Rise of Cthulhu
description:
A veil of darkness and despair has shrouded London. The natural order of things is shifting. The Great Old Ones emerge from the never-lifting mist, bent on taking over the feeble minds of humans – and each has its own purpose, a malevolent goal to fulfill. Rejoice as London descends into madness, and reshape it into your own ghastly image!

Nanty Narking: The Rise of Cthulhu is a dynamic expansion for Nanty Narking that allows 2-4 players to become a Great Old One of myths. Summon lesser gods and entities to do your bidding, help you achieve your secret goal, and battle with other powerful Great Old Ones, equally as hell-bent on total domination and destruction.

The expansion introduces new components – including Great Ones' giant miniatures – and a slew of new mechanisms. You'll take on the personalities of Great Old Ones, and use new card abilities: Insanity, to ramp up the madness level, and Summon, to beckon lesser entities to join you. Each such Great One is a unique agent with powerful ability, difficult to eliminate and eager to sow destruction, now easier and more fun than ever. But beware – if you overdo it and the Doomsday track is filled, you all lose!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rook & Raiders ルーク＆レイダーズ
description:
Rook & Raiders is a board game themed around castle defence, as suggested by its name, "Castle Walls and Invaders." Players must hire powerful soldiers to protect their castle and fend off the relentless onslaught of demonic forces.
This is not a cooperative game.
Each player has their own battlefield (personal board) and must control the battlefield with their own judgment.
If you can defeat the monster threat faster than anyone else, you win!

boardgame: Masha and the Bear: Memory Match
description:
Masha and the Bear: Memory Match is a team memory game with characters from the popular animated series “Masha and the Bear”.

The game is played in two teams and lasts for 8 rounds.
Each round a new challenge is revealed, which shows you which cards you’ll need to find.
Flip a card – if the image fits the challenge card, you can keep flipping. If it doesn’t – pass the turn to the other team.
As soon as a team finds the 7th card fitting the challenge, they take this Challenge card as a reward and open the next one, passing their turn to the other team.
When all 8 challenges are fulfilled, whichever team has the most challenge cards wins.

—description from the publisher
family: TV Shows: Masha and the Bear

description:
In Masha and the Bear: Hide-and-Seek the players will have to search for pairs of cards with matching pictures.

The deck of Challenge cards is put in the middle of the table. All the hide-and-seek cards are spread on the table blue side up (or green for an easier game), so that they are all seen.
The game starts once the Challenge deck is flipped. Players need to find a matching character or item faster than the others. As soon as they do, they announce it, show to the others and take the top Challenge cards. The game immediately resumes.
When the Challenge deck runs out, the game ends and the player with the most Challenge cards wins.

—description from the publisher
family: TV Shows: Masha and the Bear

description:
In the bustling afterlife city of Floatborough, everyday souls drift through the streets, each eager to prove their worth in a lively competition of soul-energy. From Lynn the Lively Librarian, always at the center of the action, to Hank the Hilarious Hotdog Vendor, ready to take on anyone with a smile, these quirky spirits bring their unique charm and competitive spirit to light up the afterlife.

In OverSoul'd, players take turns playing the top card of their deck, each with a base value. The goal is to increase the value by stacking additional Souls on top, creating powerful chains to outplay opponents. With each player holding their full deck but only seeing the top card, strategy and timing are key to winning the trick.

—description from the designer

Get ready to spell it out with this quick-fire word game! Roll the 12 lettered dice and then it’s a race to see who can use the letters to spell out as many words as possible before the timer runs out.

With a countless number of possible letter combinations for you and your family to find, this game is just the thing to test your vocabulary and find out who really knows their ABC’s!

Whoa, Nellie! The band is on the field! The Bush Push. Miami vs. Florida State. Harvard vs. Yale, USC vs. UCLA. Schembechler vs. Hayes. From Bo Jackson, Peyton Manning, and Caleb Williams to the Four Horsemen, Mr. Inside & Mr. Outside, and the Galloping Ghost. This was Brian’s Song, Rudy, and We Are Marshall. Whether it’s “All About the U,” “Roll Tide,” or you have your own argument about which university rightfully earned the use of the “Quarterback U” moniker, if you want to go out there and “win one for the Gipper,” Match-Up College Football is for you. If you want to play Johnny Unitas at Louisville, Walter Payton at Jackson State, Bart Starr at Alabama, Tom Brady at Michigan, John Madden at Cal Poly, or Mean Joe Green at North Texas State, you will be able to do so with Match-Up College Football. This is the game designed to bring you deep inside the history of college football.

With unique game mechanics that allow every player on the team to be a part of the team’s array of successes and failures, Match-Up College Football pairs the team’s strengths and weaknesses with the players strengths and weaknesses against the opponents in a way that is unique to college football gaming.

The game can be played solely with dice and the rule book or it can be played with our base FAC deck and the 198-card supplemental FAC deck that further dials in the statistical accuracy and the variety of the match-ups that decide the results of the plays. An exciting, playable game with a wide range of options and variations, Match-Up College Football is sure to meet your love of the sport.

—description from the publisher

Uncover the trinkets and the critters of the forest in this tiny, forest floor inspired set collection card game for two!

Players take turns collecting beetles, berries and mushrooms to create the highest scoring collections. Forage the right cards to score perfect pairs and use abilities to snatch up the goods before your opponent!

Underleaf is designed to be quick to learn and easy to play in 20 minutes - perfect for your next coffee date, camping trip or chill afternoon! Every card in Underleaf is hand illustrated and features over 15 unique, real life species and scientific names!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Las Ligas de la Ñ: Reinas Arcanas
description:
Las Ligas de la Ñ: Reinas Arcanas ("The Ñ Leagues: Arcane Queens") is a 2 for infinite players card betting game that, in its first edition, contains 50 different cards (49 Arcane Queens from the universe of The Ñ Leagues and 1 rule card). All the heroines illustrated so far are here!

In this new Ñ game (the tenth in the series), the winner is the one who scores the most points. In each round, the Ñ, S, F, and M values must be used to place hidden (face-down) card bets. If you have a higher value on the card (according to the bet made: Ñ, S, F, M, or the sum of these), you score a point; if it’s equal, no one scores.

Each Arcane Queen card contains the following information: 1. Image; 2. Origin (birth and death date for historical characters, and author, work, and date for fictional characters); 3. Description (each Arcane Queen has her own biography, including origin, occupation, attributes, and a representative quote); 4. League (group of power, faction, or order in the universe of The Ñ Leagues); 5. Arcana (number and name of the arcana according to the tradition of tarot archetypes); 6. Ñ Value (the Ñ value indicates the significance of each Arcane Queen in the universe of The Ñ Leagues); 7. S Value (the S value indicates the wisdom of each Arcane Queen in the universe of The Ñ Leagues); 8. F Value (the F value indicates the strength—mental, physical, technological, political, and magical—of each Arcane Queen in the universe of The Ñ Leagues); 9. M Value (the M value indicates the mobility—the number of movement spaces or type of movement—of each Arcane Queen in the universe of The Ñ Leagues).

Additionally, this deck of cards is compatible with other games in the series, such as The Ñ Leagues: Anthology or The Ñ Leagues: The Great War, and can also be used as a divination tarot. The rules state the following: “You are in group therapy, in the Arcane Mind, a theory of the mind. Use the cards to better understand the other participants. You can emulate a role-playing game or even our beloved Dixit; you can feel like Freud or Jung (in a dangerous therapy full of fatal archetypes); you can surrender to the signs of the I Ching or the divination tarot; or you can mix it all together. Only you are the mastermind!

Hello, my name is Eli and welcome to Twosocks. I only wish it was under better circumstances.

Once a thriving and prosperous market town, Twosocks, under the rule of the Evil Lord Zadorf, has become a deceitful, uncivil place populated by thieves and beggars.

For many years now, I have compiled a compendium with the aim of defeating the evil lord and liberating my town from his tyranny.

Alas, just when I have the secrets to his defeat, age and ill health render me powerless against his might.

I therefore implore you, my friend, to undertake this quest for me. I know I ask a lot but the rewards are great.

I will entrust you with my manual, which offers advice on how to prosper in the town, the secrets to Zadorf’s defeat and, ultimately, the untold riches that will follow.

Twosocks is a mini Print ‘N’ Play adventure that can be played as a solo experience or as a multi-player, cooperative, or competitive game.

Designed for Black and White printing and to fit US and UK A4. 
For the solo game, you will need to print six A4 single-sided sheets with just cutting and folding required. More sheets will be required for the multi-player games.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Baseball Highlights 2045: Dad Bresnan Promo Card
description:
A new free agent to add to the free agent deck for Baseball Highlights: 2045 from Dad vs Daughter.

Released as part of the Bases Loaded campaign (2024).

Description:

Dad Bresnan - Cyborg - Cincinnati

Cost: 10, Revenue: 1

Defense ability: Sweep Plus: If ahead or behind Cancel all Hits and add one of them to the end of your Hit Box.

Hit Box: Home Run, Home Run

Speed: Slow

Tier Up is a lively party game that challenges you and your friends to create the ultimate tier list across a variety of fun and quirky topics!

Each round, a category is chosen, and players secretly draw a tier card ranging from S (the best) to D (the worst). Based on their card, each player names something from the category that fits their assigned tier. The group then collaborates to build a shared tier list by placing their tier cards where they believe they belong.

But here’s the twist: after the initial placements, it’s time to debate! Each player has an arrow they can use to adjust the position of one card—moving it up or down a tier — until everyone is either satisfied or out of moves.

Once the tier list is locked in, all cards are revealed to see how well the group aligned with the secret tiers. For every correct placement, players score points. Continue through the set number of rounds to see how well your group did!

Tier Up combines strategic thinking, persuasion, and a dose of hilarity to create a unique social experience that will have everyone laughing, debating, and cheering as you rank everything from fast food to superheroes.

—description from the designer

Step into the world of Grape, where strategy meets spatial mastery.
This two-player game combines the simplicity of rotation mechanics with the depth of strategic gameplay. Command your tetromino-shaped animal pieces – from zebras and orangutans to vampires and tigers – as you outmaneuver your opponent on a 10x10 grid.

Gameplay Overview:
Objective: Win by either eliminating your opponent’s orangutan or positioning your own orangutan at the center of the board.
Mechanics: Rotate your animal pieces around one of the squares they occupy. Each rotation can capture opponent pieces, creating endless tactical possibilities.
Balance: The game is meticulously designed to ensure fair play regardless of who goes first. Mistakes are opportunities, making every game unique and unpredictable.
Why Grape Stands Out:

Elegant Design: Minimalist aesthetics meet artistic craftsmanship. Pieces are shaped like tetromino blocks and adorned with animal illustrations, bringing life to the game.
Quick to Learn, Deep to Master: The rules are simple, but the strategies are vast – a game perfect for both casual players and hardcore strategists.
Dynamic Gameplay: Unlike games with predefined moves, Grape thrives on creativity, rewarding those who think outside the box.

The Designer’s Vision:
Grape was born from a love for strategy games like chess but with a desire for fresh mechanics. The unique rotational movement adds a new dimension to gameplay, while the animal themes bring a playful edge. After countless playtests and refinements, Grape is ready to challenge players of all levels.

—description from the designer

Materials: The five Platonic solids as dice (d4, d6, d8, d12, d20).

Gameplay
Each player rolls all five dice and may re-roll up to two times per round.

Upper Section
Categories: Ones, Twos, Threes, and Fours.
Bonus: A bonus of 50 points is awarded if the total score in the upper section is at least 30 points, which corresponds to three Ones, three Twos, three Threes, and three Fours.

Lower Section
The following categories are similar to Yahtzee:
Pair
Two Pairs
Three of a Kind
Four of a Kind
Full House
Chance

Special Category
Platzy: Five of a Kind (all dice showing the same number), worth 50 points.

Instead of the normal straights, Platzy features two new straight categories:
Arithmetic Straight: At least three dice with a constant difference (e.g. 2+4+6=12).
Geometric Straight: At least three dice with a constant ratio (e.g. 1+2+4+8+16=31).

Additionally, there are two number-theory-based categories:
Primes: The sum of all dice showing prime numbers.
Composites: The sum of all dice showing composite numbers.

Winning Condition
The player with the highest total score wins.

—description from the designer

Dungeon Dynasty is a cooperative card and board game designed for solo play or two players, focusing on dungeon creation. Players expand their dungeon while attempting to thwart invading adventurers.
Combining tactical combat and maneuvering with a campaign-level context of dungeon creation and population, this game creates dynamic interactions between these two modes, offering interesting and meaningful creative and tactical choices through card play and strategic planning.
The game's flavor and atmosphere align with classic fantasy themes. Players have a wide variety of monsters, traps, and other elements to add to their dungeon, enhancing the depth and complexity as the game progresses.
With its blend of creative dungeon design and tactical combat, Dungeon Dynasty offers a rich and immersive fantasy gaming experience that promises extensive and engaging playtime.

—description from the publisher

Tranpsecopia is a dice placement game in which players contend for resources without knowing the full extent of each other's intent. It takes calculated risk and a modicum of psychology to decide where you commit your resources. Choose a character, collect resources, and access the reality-defying elements birthed by the spacetime rift to upgrade your submarine. The first person to finish upgrading their submarine will enter the Transecopia and win the game.

In Transecopia, each ship’s captain races against the others to salvage resources and upgrade their submarine. But resources are limited, competition is fierce, and working within the crushing depths makes your efforts unreliable. Each captain must weigh needs, probabilities, and assets to decide where to commit their efforts.

Do you commit all your effort to collecting and building? Do you explore the depths, trusting luck to bring you caches of resources and unexpected boons? Do you try to master the tides of fate by tapping into the alien artifacts? Your choices will either win you glory within the Transecopia or consign your name to oblivion.

—description from the designer

Erupei is a dynamic card game designed for 3 to 10 players, offering 30 to 60 minutes of engaging playtime. The goal of the game is to collect themed card sets, with each set requiring a unique strategy to complete. The game offers three visually distinct editions: Animal Kingdoms, Cute Creatures, and Human Kingdoms. While the gameplay remains consistent across editions, the varied themes provide different aesthetic experiences for players.

A standout feature of Erupei is its skill cards, which introduce strategic depth and unpredictability to each session. Each pack contains 24 skill cards with nine unique abilities, allowing players to influence the game in creative ways. Expansions like "Pirates & Traders" and "Ancient Aura" add even more variety, increasing the total number of themed collections and enhancing the replayability of the game.

Produced in Romania by Galactic Board Games, Erupei is celebrated for its high-quality components and simple yet strategic gameplay. Its design appeals to a wide audience, making it an excellent choice for both casual family gatherings and competitive game nights with friends. The game’s balance of accessibility and strategic depth ensures that players of all experience levels can enjoy it.

Erupei is available in two versions: the Silver Pack, which includes the essential cards and skills for the core game, and the Gold Pack, which features additional expansions and content. Players and reviewers alike commend the game for its engaging mechanics and beautiful presentation, solidifying its place as a favorite among card game enthusiasts.

—description from the publisher

Clever card play meets feline fun! Can you pounce on the right cards?

Whiskers & Tails is a cat-themed set collection and hand management game.

The game contains 100 cat cards numbered 00-99. Three of these are dealt face up in numeric order into the middle of the play area and five cards are dealt to each player to start the game.

On your turn, you will play one card into the middle, keeping the cards in numeric order. Take one of the cards that is adjacent to the card you just played, and place that card into your scoring area.

Cards you take are grouped in sets according to the last digit (the tail) and will score based on the first digit (the whiskers).

When the deck runs out, any of your sets that contain at least 3 cards score positively, and any sets with less than 3 cards score negatively.

Most points win!

—description from the publisher

In Sankei, you'll embark on an unforgettable adventure through Japan's majestic jinjas, exploring ancient Shinto temples and immersing yourself in the rich spirituality of this fascinating tradition. Your mission is to uncover the hidden magic within these sacred places while competing with other travelers to become the most revered pilgrim.

Fulfill the predictions of your omikuji, face the fearsome yokai, and make offerings to the powerful kami who dwell in these venerated temples. Once you cross the torii, every step along the path will bring you closer to new destinations filled with secrets and rewards. But beware! The trails can be unpredictable and full of surprises.

Get ready for a unique experience that blends strategy, culture, and spirituality in every move!

Sankei is more than just a game; it’s an invitation to discover the connection between the land, history, and the divine within the captivating Shinto temples of Japan. Will you be the most respected pilgrim and achieve the most memorable journey? Let the adventure begin!

—description from the designer

En Sankei, te embarcarás en una inolvidable aventura a través de los majestuosos jinjas de Japón, explorando antiguos templos shinto y sumergiéndote en la rica espiritualidad de esta fascinante tradición. Tu misión será descubrir la magia oculta en estos lugares sagrados mientras compites con otros viajeros por ser el peregrino más venerado.

Cumple las predicciones de tu omikuji, enfréntate a los terribles yokai y realiza ofrendas a los poderosos kami que habitan en estos venerados templos. Una vez que cruces el torii cada paso en la senda te acercará a nuevos destinos llenos de secretos y recompensas, pero ¡cuidado!, los senderos pueden ser impredecibles y llenos de sorpresas.

¡Prepárate para una experiencia única que combina estrategia, cultura y espiritualidad en cada movimiento!

Sankei es más que un juego; es una invitación a descubrir la conexión entre la tierra, la historia y las divinidades en los fascinantes templos shinto de Japón. ¿Serás el peregrino más respetado y lograrás el viaje más memorable? ¡Que comience la travesía!

—description from the designer (Spanish)

It's time to get silly and make some funny faces! Race against your friends to create silly dragon expressions in a fast-paced competition. The player who completes the creates the most matching expressions the fastest wins the Face-Off!

In Dragon Face-Off, players can adjust how many rounds they want to complete or choose a speed based quick play. Players start by flipping over one of their dragon cards and begin flipping their 4 dragon blocks around until they create a matching expression.

With it’s bright and colorful designs, and multiple ways to play, this game offers a fast and fun way for kids to develop their visual matching, strategic thinking, and communication skills.

—description from the publisher

Sound Check is a game about up-and-coming band managers looking to win a series of Battle-of-the-Bands. Venue cards represent the multiple locations in each round and usually effect one of the three avenues of the game. These avenues include Crown Favor, Venue Favor, and Energy.
By leading in the majority of the avenues at the end of a round, or reaching 11 in any avenue, a manager wins a show. This is achieved by recruiting band members with certain set lists. By playing a card that matches the Theme die, players can get ahead as well.
The first player to win three rounds is declared "Best Band Manager." The game comes with lots of flavor text and enough band members and venues to bring replay ability to 11!

—description from the designer

Swords and Stones is a fantasy sandbox/open map tabletop kingdom building wargame. You are chosen to lead your people in the aftermath of a cataclysmic flood that wiped all civilization off the face of the earth.

Oversee and harvest resources, research technologies and upgrades, and grow your small camp in the woods to a mighty kingdom, an island of hope and prosperity in a sea of danger and unknown.

S&S is a complete tabletop sandbox kingdom building wargame experience, where the player has full control over how they play; be it a fast aggressive rush style, or a slow-and-steady defensive build, a highly specialized kingdom, or a multi-purpose general one. The main way to win a game of S&S is either By Sword – where you must defeat/slay all rival Kings and Queens (that represents the players), OR By Stone – where you must build a mighty monument and have it stand for 25 turns to win!

—description from the designer

A game where you roll 6 dice and remove any combination that results in a 7. Then you can either re-roll the remaing dice, up to two times, or add all face values to your score as points. Note that if you re-roll and get another combination of 7, you must remove those dice too.

The game goes for seven rounds, and at the end, the player with most points wins the game.

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: Attack of the Empire
description:
In this game, each player commands an Imperial fleet that is trying to advance into Alliance territory and capture the capital city of Heinessen.

However, in order to advance deep into enemy territory, several star systems must be captured as supply bases. Players advance by playing advance cards, defeating major star systems and enemy fleets with Imperial fleet cards, and aim to be the first to reach Heinessen.

Will you be able to reach the planet Heinessen?

Card play is reminiscent of the game Mille Bornes. It is a game in which the object is to be the first to reach the goal (distance). During play you can interfere with other players and gaining an advantage in one's own progress.  This is characterized by advancing obstacles (allied fleets, etc.), and by winning a "fleet" dice.

Example of play:

In this Imperial Advance, the player takes on the role of one of the Empire's Fleets and plays an Advance Card to advance in light years. Other players will send you an alliance fleet as a blocking card, which you need to destroy to move forward. If you are defeated by an allied fleet, you will lose your advance card and retreat. At this time, if you have secured a star system in the course of your advance, you can retreat to the star system without further retreat. In other words, it has the effect of securing a base.

Comes with 150 cards, rulebook, a card holder, two dice, and a sleeve.
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description:
Welcome to Rollin’ Campus, a print-and-play, multi-combo, roll & write game in which you have to juggle studies, parties, friendships and finance at the university campus. Whether you are trying to pass Beginner Pottery, enjoying a BBQ at the Fraternity House, or training with your coach for a badminton scholarship abroad, each decision you take could bring you closer to graduation... or to drop out.

And if you cannot manage to graduate... well, just enjoy the journey.

—description from the designer

A Circular Chess variant adopted from 10th C. Byzantine Chess. Which adheres to the rules of classical chess with some necessary rule modifications due its circular nature, proximity of pieces, centre of the board, etc. as well as introducing some new concepts. The board has 2 hemispheres subdivided into 16 segments, 8 zones and 128 divisions (like squares). The centre of the board is key, which may inhibit or regulate movement through it. There are 2 types of games: the Standard Half-Range game (or shortrange) and the Advanced Full-Range game (or longrange).

HALF-RANGE game: this is a bit restricted but gets players familiar with how the system operates. The movement of the longrange pieces is limited, and The High Queen (formerly known as Queen) and the Medium (formerly known as Bishop) have a unique spiralling movement,(formally known as Diagonals) and the latter is higher in rank than other Minor pieces. 
The Knights can be equipped with 2 movement patterns: the standard semi-oblique "L" 3 divisions, and optionally an extended perpendicular 6 divisions (in two 3x3 jumps and a max.12 ZOC), to compensate for their limited range. 
The adjacent Castles are separated by staged Battlements which the Knights cannot jump over. Pawn Promotion occurs on the 8th zone and can go into Reserve status when no captured piece is immeadiately available. The centre of the board is called the Centre Zone, and in the Half-Range game it is green and allows all pieces to pass through it, so it is referred to as the Bypass Zone.
The Medium and High Queen must maintain original movement arc when passing through it in both forwards and reverse directions. The King cannot be checked through the Bypass Zone, and Castling is performed as in classical chess.

FULL-RANGE game: this is where the players can go allout, and must keep alert as there is a lot happening, and pieces swooping in from all directions. The range of the pieces is unlimited, and the Pawns and the Kings may move up to 2 divisions/turn and the Castles' Pawns may triple move on their opening turns.
Inactive Castles replace the Battlement ruling, where they start inverted, (but can still defend their own Pawn) and then to Activate, must move forwards and cross the opposite hemisphere or naturally through Castling (however the King cannot be checked by the opponents inverted Castle, that can move off the basezone to Activate). Castles can act as Garrisons and absorb a friendly piece until they deploy out of them. Optionally Border neutrality can be observed, that assists with the development of the Knights onto the Castles' Segment, however this is lifted when pieces from either side cross over to the opponents' territory. Knighting is introduced as special movement between Knight and King, where the Knight jumps into the battlefield relative to the number of spaces the King slides - witch assists the rapid development of the Knight. Subsequently a Jousting Tournament may be triggered if the Knight captures a Major piece, witch sets off a chain reaction of mandatory Knight movements for both sides, and if any of the Knights is captured during this process, then the capturing Knight increases in rank and point value.  The Centre Zone in the Full-Range game becomes multi conditional in that whoever controls the centre by occupation can change its status: to the green Bypass Zone, to the red Inhibited Zone (no thru zone) acting a bit like traffic lights, or to a Dark squared Conditional Zone (allowing orthagonal alternation, or dark square spiralling), or to a Light squared Conditional Zone (allowing orhtagonal alternation, or light squared spiralling). 
Checking is permissable through valid Centre Zones, and Pawn Promotion can still occur on the 8th Zone (but without Reservation), but also when reaching the opposite perimeter of the board, and in this case Enpassente may be conducted. 
Decombination is the ability of the parent High Queen to seperate into Castle and Medium component parts, whereas Recombination is the opposite process where Castle and Medium combine on the same division to assemble the High Queen back again.

This whole system was Patented by the Designer, and is fully operational, and is significantly different from the other circular chess variants - board, setup and rules.

This is not just a 2 player game, but can be 4 player in teams of two, each taking turns in moving as we did on many drinking game nights. Novices that have an appreciation of chess, and who normally get flogged in classical chess, can actually beat more experienced players, -believe me, I've seen it happen!
Everyone I know that has played this and assisted with testing
were impressed and commended it.
Hunger knows no bounds! Order everything you want! Clear it all, Finish first and win. You play a card real time. when you empty your hand. You Win!

Primarily a learning game for IT specialists. 
Either you and your teammates secure your server as an admin or you try to take over the opponent's server in your hacker team, whereby the game is played like a tower defense with deck building.

Computer scientists can learn key terms from the IT industry and acquire important soft skills such as team management, cooperation, strategy and out-of-the-box-thinking. While non-computer scientists are challenged by an exciting game and learn about the dangers of hacking and possible cyber security countermeasures.

There are 3 different game modes that can be played with the same cards: 
“Siege”, where each team takes on a specific role (admins or hackers). 
“Warfare”, where both teams have to attack as well as defend. 
“Strike”, an entertaining, fast-paced game played by two players in about 5 minutes.

The essential decks offers a total of 2x 42 cards (admin deck and hacker deck) to play all 3 game modes and learn the most important mechanics. Over 200 cards will also be available in booster packs and are constantly being expanded.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Path of Civilization: Cannes Promo Card
Path of Civilization: Mont Saint-Michel Promo Card

description:
Nanaro no Go is a children's version of Go aimed primarily at children and is a step up from よんろのご: IGO puzzle. It is based on the Japanese Go Association's beginner method. Nanaro is Japanese for "seven lines". And so Nanaro no Go is played on a 7x7 grid.

Each player tries to surround carrots (located at each intersection) with their horses (stones). Iyama Yūta, the first Go player to hold all the seven major Japanese Go titles simultaneously recommended the game as "The perfect introduction to Go".

Promo card that was given to everybody whom pre-ordered the game from Tesla Games.

It is a pink level II bonus card, marked Cp3.

Effect: Pay 2 coins and 1 stone to build a workshop construction, but do not gain the connected garden bonuses.

Roll’n’Coaster (Bau dir deinen Freizeitpark) is all about the perfect theme park. As a manager, each player tries to lead his park to great success. This requires attractions, stalls, parking spaces and enough green spaces so that visitors feel comfortable and experience enough adrenaline, variety, consumption and fun. But be careful, the architects, engineers, craftsmen and landscape gardeners have to comply with many regulations before the building inspection takes place - because the testing organization does not tolerate any construction sins in the park.

In Roll'n'Coaster the game is played simultaneously, so there is hardly any waiting time. The basic game uses one game sheet and pen per player as well as two 6-sided standard dice that all players use at the same time. The extended game includes 12 task cards, so that additional victory conditions are added to the various minimum requirements from the basic game. This means that the game Roll'n'Coaster always remains varied and attractive. Roll'n'Coaster can be played both in single mode and in a large group.

—description from the designer

September 2076: Why hasn't a signal been sent from Mars Base Alpha for weeks?

In Adventure Games: Mission Mars, the United Earth Government is sending a rescue mission to the first settlement on Mars. The team: one to four players aged ten and over. The astronauts have special abilities that will help you slip into exciting roles in the analog PC adventure so that you can experience the story for yourself. Many challenges await over 100 million kilometers away...

boardgame: Gosu X: Astraia, Queen of the Lacertids Expansion
description:
Faced with the threat of Abunakkashii and Rukgnarl Bonesreaper, the Lacertids, guided by their queen Astraia, leave their mystical lake to confront the dangers facing Jalaanx.

The cycle continues! Add the Lacertids clan, immortal aquatic beings, to your Gosu X games and shake up the power balance! In this new expansion, recruit Astraia's new clan and exploit the full potential of the Immortal cards.

The expansion contains 19 cards (15 Lacertids + 4 Evolutions) and 8 tiles.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: DC Deck-Building Game: Teen Titans Go! – Expansion
description:
Packaged in a stylish custom pouch, it comes with 19 new game cards that add additional main deck, Weakness, Event, and Nemesis cards to Teen Titans Go! It also includes a summary of the rules updates for buyers of the first edition of Teen Titans Go!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: SCRxBBLE: Print and Play Words Games using Scrabble Tiles
description:
SCRxBBLE is a series of over 20 print and play word games that make use of existing Scrabble tiles.

By expanding your use of the Scrabble tiles, you get endless entertainment, from simple solo play to group competition. The possibilities are as limitless as the words you can create or decode!

The games include:

SCRxBBLE Alley  
SCRxBBLE Battle  
SCRxBBLE Bingo  
SCRxBBLE Blitz  
SCRxBBLE Connections  
SCRxBBLE Decoders  
SCRxBBLE Dice  
SCRxBBLE Emojis  
SCRxBBLE Golf  
SCRxBBLE Math  
SCRxBBLE Matrix  
SCRxBBLE Patterns  
SCRxBBLE Pyramids  
SCRxBBLE Rotation  
SCRxBBLE Scramble  
SCRxBBLE Scratch  
SCRxBBLE Shuffle  
SCRxBBLE Spaces  
SCRxBBLE Squares  
SCRxBBLE Stacks  
SCRxBBLE Streaks  
SCRxBBLE Tac Toe  
Secret SCRxBBLE

Each game comes with a single 8x10 sheet to print along with separate instructions for set up and game play.

Solo and multi-player versions.
A simple way of expanding Scrabble and Bananagram tiles.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Inventors of the South Tigris: Townsfolk Promo Cards
description:
Originally available during the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign (pack D "dagon").

Six Townsfolk Cards that are shuffled into the Device Card Draw Pile during Setup. These cannot be placed onto Device Boards. Instead, they can be played for 1 of 2 effects:

1. To gain the indicated Influence and 1 pip increase.
2. To resolve the indicated effect on the black banner. This is always to pay a specific Craftsperson for a nice reward.

-description from publisher
family: Series: The South Tigris Trilogy (Garphill Games)

description:
In a world where cunning, strength, and strategy collide, AWARWAR lets you command powerful factions, each with their own unique warriors. Lead the disciplined Crimson Legion, the wild Azure Berserkers, the unyielding Sun Keepers, or the strategic Jade Sect in a battle for dominance. Build cities, train deadly dragonoid troops, and slay fierce monsters to earn Renown Points for the Awarwar Empire, all while outsmarting and overpowering your rivals. With a dynamic map, unpredictable combat, and endless tactical possibilities, only the most daring and strategic player will rise to claim victory.

—description from the designer
artists: José Francisco Alzate de la Pava

boardgame: Call of Duty: K/D Party Game
description:
Bring your COD skills to dare night with the Call of Duty: K/D Party Game. Move your operators around the map and battle to capture dares before the other players. When you land on a dare, assign it to an enemy and watch them complete it! Down other operators, use the Loadout cards and collect K/D tokens along the way. Prepare for deployment - it's time to drop with your squad.

—description from the designer

Dive into Chasseur de coeur! This competitive game takes you on a journey where laughter and love are king. Gather your friends and play character cards to match them in couple according to their style of life. The active player have to choose witch character is the best choice in couple for the protagonist to let him win heart tokens. Chasseur de coeur promises memorable moments and laughter at every turn!

Every card of the game can be mixed with Chasseur de tête and vice versa.

Competitively and simultaneously, guess 10 love partners, the player with the most heart points win the game!'

—description from the designer

boardgame: Zoo King: Dice Tower Promo Cards
description:
Originally available during the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign (pack A "Area 51").

Promo pack of 3 cards to add to Zoo King:
- Shoebill (Water)
- Pygmy Hippo (Jungle & Water)
- Facilities Supervisor (Staff)

Facilities Supervisor: Draw five cards from the Staff & Facilities deck. You may keep one Facility card you draw for free. Shuffle the remaining cards back into the deck.

- description from card

It’s 2225 and you’ve been traveling across the galaxy as part of a developer-run space station. Your main mission was a success, it’s time to go home. But just as a return course is set, a bug appears. If you don’t catch it before reaching your home planet, it will wreak havoc on everything you love. Your new mission is simple: find the bug.

One player will take on the role of the elusive bug, invisibly moving around the station. Everyone else will play as the developers, tracking down the bug and eradicating it before it’s too late.

—description from the publisher

The two player mini tear-and-play version of Colourbrain.

Each turn, players reveal a question or prompt card, and then must select the correct colour or combination of colours to win that prompt.

This set contains eight questions, and two sets of six colour prompts.

What the Cluck? is a game about playing chicken through auctions. While you might want to bid high, sometimes that might not be the wisest of options, and it might benefit you better by just trying to get by. But let’s be honest, this game only comes with 19 items.

Blockhunters is a thrilling board game based on the technology behind bitcoin. It incorporates concepts like mining, nodes, nonces, private keys, halvings and the mempool! Like in real life, Bitcoin rewards become more increasingly scarce, and it's important to protect your private key!

Players must balance cooperation and competition to win the game. The ultimate goal? Collect as much Bitcoin as possible.

Here’s how it works:
Players fill the mempool with transaction cards and, as secretly as possible, place bitcoins on transactions that match the private key(s) they hold.

Every round, players “mine” by drawing nonces (number tiles) from a bag, aiming for the nonce closest to the sum of the transactions in the previous block. The winner of the mining round gets to choose which transactions are added to the block, confirming them – and earning a bitcoin reward!

The game continues for 9 blocks, and at the end, all cards are revealed. The player with the most bitcoins on transactions corresponding to their private keys is crowned the winner!

But beware – being too obvious about your private key’s identity can make you a target. If another player gets the opportunity, they can guess which key is yours... if they're correct, they steal it and claim all your hard-earned coins.

Blockhunters involves luck, strategy, memory and bluffing. It is made for bitcoiners, precoiners and anyone with a competitive spirit (ages 14+) to enjoy! It's the perfect way to share the importance of the monetary revolution with your friends and families!

—description from the designer

Part of the Black Site Studios Zine line of simple rulesets, Ghostwatcher is a tabletop miniatures game where players control a team of ghost hunters that are live streaming their exploration of a mansion in search of paranormal activity.

Ghostwatcher consists of three main phases:

Stream phase: the hunters search the rooms in the mansion hoping to find the figments that will provide necessary information about the house's ghost.

Spirit phase: the spirit tries to increase the hunters' fear level by doing things like moving furniture, triggering special events or straight up attacking them. Once the hunters find all the the figments and the spirit materializes in the physical realm, the spirit phase changes to the Banishment Ritual where the ghost will directly target the hunters.

Audience phase: hunters can spend earned Audience Points to gain special benefits to assist them in their hunt.

The main gameplay is based on a simple D6 system where hunters roll dice equal to their skill value. Result of 6 are successes while a 1 result will increase the team's fear level. If the fear level gets too high, the team becomes too scared to finish their investigation and the players lose.

Choose your General among six distinct factions in the fantastical world of Mythron. Summon and command minions on the grid battlefield, cast an array of spells, and utilize artifacts in an attempt to defeat the opposing General(s) while protecting your own.

Based on the Duelyst II computer game, this adaptation contains multiple game modes, including drafting and teams, and some cards that could not exist in the computer game.

A little history. The original Duelyst video game was designed by board game designer Eric Lang. The game was shut down in 2020, but multiple efforts by the community brought it back in various forms. The original developers even made the game files open source. Duelyst II is the most prominent continuation of the original video game's legacy. Duelyst II: The Board Game branches off of that game with new mechanics and cards that are well-suited to a tabletop game.

—description from the designer

Do you love The Keeyp but are looking for a new experience? Do you have an endless need to dig holes in the middle of a dungeon? Or maybe you wish you could play The Keeyp without fighting?

Well, then Garden Games is the right expansion for you!

It’s up to you to collect tools, explore, and dig in search of the illusive Key. After that, make sure to get away quickly before your friends, or foe, steal it. Just be careful not to uncover any traps in the process.

—description from the designer

Snowcrest: Frost is a 9-card expansion pack for Snowcrest that adds extra white Villager cards to your game.
These cards increase the variety of possible actions to your village, including some "interactive" thief cards that will keep everyone on their toes!

This pack was made available during the crowdfunding campaign for Snowcrest.

boardgame: Firefighters on Duty: Fuel & Electrical Fires
description:
The expansion introduces two new types of fire, fuel and electrical. It also comes with two new types of extinguishing agents, foam and CO2. The pouch provided allows you to randomize all three types of fire.
—description from the publisher

The Tow Truck mini-expansion introduces a new module and presents a new challenge in your scenarios as you now also need to handle wrecked and abandoned cars that are blocking the streets.
—description from the publisher

One More Player!

This set of foiled character cards expand the game allowing a 5th Player to join in on the fun and trickery.

Included as part of the achievements in the Kickstarter Campaign. It is packaged separately from the base game in a small plastic bag.

boardgame: World at War 85: Operation Red Gauntlet
description:
World at War 85: Operation Red Gauntlet, Crossing the Mighty Weser is an expansion pack for BLOOD AND FURY, the latter being the SECOND volume in The WORLD AT WAR 85 SERIES of games of fast and furious platoon-level combat. Set in 1985, in an alternate history of World War III, when the Warsaw Pact armies storm across the border of East Germany in a powerful attempt to seize northern West Germany, and the whole of Free Europe.

Operation Red Gauntlet features nine new game maps and five new scenarios. Four of the scenarios in this expansion can be played with maps from it as well as units and maps from Blood and Fury.

One of the expansion scenarios uses eight of the new maps and you will need Volume 1 of the series, Storming the Gap, and its expansions, the Defense of Frankfurt, and Storm and Steel.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Series: World At War 85 (Lock 'n Load Publishing)

description:
Turbo Tiere is a fast-paced racing game where you win by being the first Turbo Tier to cross the finish line. Each round, you draw up to three green standard chips to move your vehicle forward, block opponents, or activate tunings.
The track reveals itself progressively, introducing sudden obstacles and tricky sections, which can lead to both lucky and unlucky events.

You can freely combine the vehicles with the animal drivers.

Neuroshima Hex: Battle is a fast-paced and dynamic version of Neuroshima Hex designed for two players to enter this rich world.

The box features the armies of Steel Police and Beasts. The Steel Police brings law and order fanatics clad in experimental power armor with a unique reflection ability that turns enemy attacks back on them. The Beasts represent feral survivors of Moloch's attack, introducing friendly fire mechanisms and a primal, unpredictable style of play. Both factions add unique strategies and flavor to this intense two-player experience. The game includes a playing mat, two armies, tokens, and a new rulebook.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
This drag racing journey of board games now turns the pages back to an exciting era in its exciting history, 1980’s covering as many classes as possible, and may cover more decades in the awesome 21st century. These vintage eras of old were action packed, and a lot more dangerous with the lack of safety standards, with racing excitement and grandstands always full of enthusiastic race fans. Full Throttle Drag Racing uses a unique system of blending section movement or space movement depending on what happened on a turn for a driver. I wanted a game that also was more event based as I love a little a bit of a story, but some races still may go event free. With this blend of unique movement gave me an opportunity to do some incorporating of this mix with various event and trouble tables. The scale of the raceway is considered a quarter mile for all classes and no matter what class you race it has a three-turn racing system. I used the terms 330, 660, and Finish for what is called section turns for breaking it up into close to one third marks. Each driver receives a time slip at the end of the race which documents times at these important marks, time slips would also include reaction times, 60-foot time, and average and top speeds.

Get Ready for More Fast Action Short Season, yes, they had short seasons, Exciting Drag Racing!

—description from the designer

Deep beneath the ruins of Seattle, a hidden civilization thrives in defiance of Moloch's oppressive grasp, a society forged in secrecy that unites rebellious cyborgs, escaped mutants, and the descendants of atomic researchers. With cutting-edge cybernetic implants and stolen Moloch technologies, they wage a relentless war to survive. But at what cost? As humanity and machine merge, the line between ally and enemy blurs.

Neuroshima Hex: Wiremen introduces exciting new gameplay mechanisms, including innovative technology tokens. Designed as a flexible and aggressive army with straightforward rules, Wiremen adds a bold new layer of strategy and decision-making to Neuroshima Hex.

—description from the publisher

At Pete’s Plaice, hungry diners wait to served a selection of seaside favourites.  Beware of the greedy seagulls though.  They will swoop down to try to steal the food off your diner’s plates, unless you can stop them.  Use power cards to ward off the seagulls on to other player's or else feed the pesky birds.

On your turn you will have a choice of serving a meal to a diner, play an action card, or draw from the deck.  By the end of the game, you will score points for each meal served depending on the number of items in that meal.  Larger meals score larger points, but also means that opponents will send seagulls & crabs your way to steal food (points) away from you.

Do you have what it takes to out serve your opponents & deliver the most meals at the Pete’s Plaice restaurant?

-description from publisher

A team trivia game where teams win a tug of war by answering yes or no questions.

This time with questions about the Czech Republic.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Golden Pig is a reimagining of Scopa, the classic Italian fishing card game, for younger kids and those who aren’t yet comfortable with numbers or arithmetic: all the math in Golden Pig is safely tucked away so that everyone can focus on the fun of collecting cute farmyard animals.

-- description from the designer

90s-themed set of extra questions for the Smart10 game, in Swedish.

The questions in this question pack revolve around the years 1990-1999, of life, popular culture, phenomena, historical moments, and everything else that happened then.

Requires a Smartbox from SMART10 games.

These questions are of true crime, including well-known cases, modus operandi, perpetrators, reporters, researchers, and everything to do with true crime.

The pack has 50 two-sided question cards, 100 new questions and 1000 new answer choices combined.

In order to play, you need the Smartbox in addition to the question packs.

These questions are in the realm of sports, athletes, equipment, clubs, and champions. For sport fans and enthusiasts! The pack has 50 two-sided question cards, 100 new questions and 1000 new answer choices combined.

Each card contains ten possible answers. On your turn, answer a question. If you got it right, you get a token and can keep answering on your next turn. But go for an incorrect answer and you are out of this round, with no points for your tokens. Decide when to pass and when to play – And be the first player or team to reach 30 points and win!

In order to play, you need the Smartbox in addition to the question packs.
Deeds is a mini expansion to Omerta: The Five Families, introducing special round goals that award players rewards for specific actions they perform in the game.

boardgame: Omertà: The Five Families – Death Contracts
description: Mini expansion for Omertà: The Five Families. Players assign death contracts to other players who must then either kill one of the targets on the contract card or the Godfather of the family that assigned them the contract.

no TABOO! is a board game where you have fun guessing words, without being able to say some of them, like for example guessing "foreskin" without saying "glans, skin, unhood, circumcision, penis". This game allows you to put the right words to sometimes little-known or delicate concepts around sexuality. The themes covered are anatomy, pleasure and feelings, sexual practices and crimes, sexual orientations and gender identities, protection against STIs and contraception.

For adults, guaranteed laughter to share very fun moments with friends, which can also be an opportunity for more serious discussions.

—description from the publisher

In Council of Bees, players take the role of Senior Bees of the Council. Address issues in the Beeconomy while reaching your own agenda. Make laws by passing bills with the aid of junior bees,(dice) and honey.

The first player to reach or exceed 50 points among their Agenda cards will claim victory. Cards reflecting the 7 issues in the game are played in one of the 5 regions of the game board.

As senior bee, players assign which region a bee will vote in, known as beemanduring.

Then there is honey which can earn a player more action points and persuade others to help pass a bill. Dirty honey is from Lob-bee-ists to exchange for favors that don't help and may even hurt the hive. You will find many more bee puns in this sweet game of bee politics!

Spellpath is a word-based hidden movement game for 2-7 players. The game is played in a cemetery made up of tombstones with letters on them.

One player is the Ghost trying to hide from the rest of the players who are Ghost Hunters. To move about the cemetery, players form words with the tombstones. With the help of a Spirit Board, these words can compel the Ghost to communicate and just maybe reveal where they are.

Ghost Hunters try to guess the Ghost's most recently recorded word. They do this by moving in such a way that their own word has letters in common with the Ghost's word. The Ghost must indicate this by pushing Mama tokens on the Spirit Board to the common letters. If all of the Ghost's letters have been found or a Ghost Hunter moves, but does not reveal a new letter, the word is scored. This is done by comparing the amount of Mana on the number section of the Spirit Board with the letter section.

More letters is a win for the Hunters and a mana is moved to the fountain. Otherwise, the Ghosts wins and adds a letter of its win tracker. This tracker is either the word, "Goodbye" or "Bye" for and easier game for the Ghost. With the addition of Query and Curse cards, the game may be played as a more competitive variant.

Much as you might like to forget them, these bizarre creatures stick like hooks in the back of your mind.

Shallow Regrets: Lingering Remorse 6-card expansion complicates your fishing plans with an assortment of bewildering fish. These new creatures have lingering effects that are ongoing, forcing you to adapt your strategy to your current collection. With the ability to add a 4th player, there's even more competition for the best catches!

Add the 6 expansion cards to the core 18-card game and shuffle. Deal out 6 shoals (or 8, if including a 4th player) and play as normal.

The expansion creatures feature lingering (ongoing) effects that are active unless the creature is exhausted.

boardgame: Shallow Regrets: The Fisher & the Frod
description:
Frod like to fish too. Blup. You fish with Frod? Blupp. Frod think Frod fish better than you. Blup-blup.

Defeat Frod in a fishing competition in this 6-card solo expansion. Scripted decision-making and a small selection of strategies for Frod to follow keeps Frod’s turns moving quickly while still capturing the unpredictability and interaction of the core game. With multiple difficulty levels, even the most skilled angler should find a challenge in Frod!

Set the game up as normal, shuffling the Frod deck separately. Player turns proceed as normal. Both the human player and Frod use a new layout, placing their exhausted fish in a column and their available fish in a row next to it, to help clarify Frod’s abilities and fishing attempts.

On Frod’s turn, he will always use one ability from the previous turn’s strategy card, if possible. Next, reveal a new strategy card to guide Frod’s fishing decisions. Then he fishes, catching the single largest fish he can using his available strength. Lastly, he’ll move a fish from one shoal to another.

Game end and scoring follow the core game rules, with the exception that Frod never takes a penalty for Foul fish. Outscore Frod to win the competition!

OBJECT OF THE GAME: Players are asked to write humorous responses to questions and situations, and everyone must guess who wrote what.
HILARIOUS PARTY GAME: What is something you should avoid doing in prison? What should grandpa stop exaggerating about?
CLEAN OR DIRTY: Can be as clean or as dirty as you want, which makes this the perfect party game for teens, families, and adults.
WHAT? plays well with large groups, and is perfect for dinner parties, neighborhood block parties, office parties, and even college game nights.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Forest Folk: Twisted Muse / Hope & Fear

description:
You’ve become so successful as a New York City dog walker that you're ready to build your own dog-walking empire. As your team grows, you venture into new boroughs and face new opportunities to branch out your business and upgrade your gear. Some of your clients are even asking you to walk multiple of their own dogs at once!

Kings and Queens adds 3 new modules:

The new BOROUGH MAP BOARD is a tight worker placement bonus collection mechanism where you’ll send your Helper to the boroughs of New York City to race other players for bonuses, training at the doggie day camp, and new gear - new ITEM CARDS give you one-time or permanent effects that no other players have, giving you asymmetric abilities.

DOUBLE DOG CARDS let you walk two dogs at once on a single card with new puzzles to solve, taking up two slots on your player board!

The menagerie is on the move with the bonus CONCRETE JUNGLE module with JUNGLE CARDS - 27 unique animals that you can legally own in New York City who are along for the ride. Walk bunnies, chinchillas, cats, and even horses in this wild expansion - where the favorite activities are tagging along to Coffee Shops and Treat Shops.

Do you have what it takes to become the next kings and queens of dog walking?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ransom Notes: Not Safe for Work Expansion
description:
Get ready to take your Ransom Notes to the next level with the Not Safe For Work Expansion!

Gather your most twisted friends, crack open a bottle of something strong, and craft sentences that will make HR faint and your mother disown you. Just remember - we warned you.

Includes: 180 new word tiles guaranteed to make you blush. Packed with vulgar & raunchy words that would make a sailor's mouth seem clean. 100 exciting new prompt cards that will have you hiding your face.
publishers: Evan and Josh's Very Special Games Company (Very Special Games)

description:
Two envious Jelly merchants compete for the most customers in this Gem combining, order filling game for two. The jellies use a magical Gem Positioning System to locate tokens for customers. little do they know, they are also a source of their jealousy.

Get the most victory points after 6 rounds to win the coveted Sea Barters Crown! Each round has two phases. The first phase, known as the Finding Phase, involves secretly choosing Gems to combine with opponents Gems. The second phase, known as the Fulfillment Phase, involves filling customer orders to gain Victory Points and Perks.

By giving up a resource during the Finding Phase, players may manipulate the Gem outcome to get some of the resources they may want. This as well as the theme set it apart as a stand alone game from the original Jealy Jellies game.

-description from designer

Чёрная книга: Дурак ("Black Book: Durak") is the classic game of Durak enhanced with magic cards.

There are two variants of the game: traditional Perevodnoy Durak and Magic Durak version.

In Magic Durak mode, you have enchanted cards in your deck that replace the classic ones. These cards have special abilities, such as beating any card regardless of trump or having immunity to trump. They also have a suit that is assigned by the player, and other special abilities. The game plays according to the same rules as the classic mode, but it is now even harder to predict what your opponents will do.

The objective of both game modes is to get rid of all your cards when there are no more cards left in the deck. At the end of the game, the player with cards in their hand is declared the durak or 'fool' (the loser).

Also, this game contains 7 exclusive Basic Request cards for the game Black Book: The Board Game.

The Element of Surprise is a dynamic card game where strategy, teamwork, and bluffing are key to victory. Players are dealt 14 cards and engage in a series of rounds, where they bid on the total points their team can win. The game features a unique twist with players calling trump suits and hidden partners, adding layers of intrigue as teams are formed and secret alliances emerge. The cards themselves vary in point value, and the elemental cards serve as powerful trump cards in the game.

The game is played through several phases: dealing, bidding, banking, calling partners, playing, and scoring. In the bidding phase, players compete to claim the bid, which determines who will call the trump color and partners for the round. The winner of the bid also claims the Bank, selecting cards to boost their team's strategy. Partners are kept hidden until play begins, and called cards are revealed. Teamwork is crucial as players work together, sometimes unknown to the other partners, to meet their bid and accumulate points.

To win, players must strategically manage their cards and work with their partners to collect points. The goal is to be the first to reach 1500 points by successfully winning rounds and meeting the points pledged in the bid. However, failure to meet the bid results in penalties, making each round a tense and exciting challenge of skill and deception.

Ultimately, The Element of Surprise combines tactical bidding, clever card play, and shifting alliances to create a game where every decision counts, and no two rounds are ever the same.

—description from the designer

Released via the Japanime Games patreon.

Jack Frost is promo card # KBP-020

Act: Discard x blue Warriors to reduce the cost of any Warrior by x (cannot reduce to zero).
React: Pay 1 Energy. Change both chaining colors of the warrior being played to blue.

This mystery blind box introduces 10 exclusive new characters to add to your arsenal. Each of these vinyl mini figures includes an Unstable Unicorns promo card with effects that you’ve never seen before. Collect them all and build the most magical army of all time!

Buy one figure at a time for a mystery surprise, or get the pack of 18 with a guarantee that you'll find all 10 figures! The surprise is half the fun, so all Vinyl Figure Blind Box sales are final.

Card Text:
Chimeracorn
Ultimate Unicorn card'
When this card enters your Stable, SACRIFICE a Unicorn card. If this card is in your Stable at the beginning of your turn, you may play 2 cards during your Action phase.

This mystery blind box introduces 10 exclusive new characters to add to your arsenal. Each of these vinyl mini figures includes an Unstable Unicorns promo card with effects that you’ve never seen before. Collect them all and build the most magical army of all time!
