If you play a number higher than the number on the table and play all of your cards, you win! This is a game in the genre known as Daifugō/Go Out.
This game has the restriction that you cannot swap cards in your hand, but if certain conditions are met, you can combine your cards and play them all at once. For example, you can combine "21," "15," and "53" to make "2153."
On your turn, you can choose to either "play a number from your hand that is greater than the number on the table," or "take one card from the left or right of the number card on the table and pass."

A fusion of trick-taking and draw poker
The important thing is to decide whether to take a trick or make a strong hand.
Using trump cards and special cards is effective for gaining points.

Aiming for second place!

Place your cards in your hand and "push" them out to get cards! Earn points!

Basic play is for 3 or more players. Rules for 2 players are also available.

boardgame: 陰陽符戦 (Yin Yang Sign Battle)
description:
陰陽符戦 ("Inyofu") is a strategic card game set in a mystical world where players act as exorcists, using enchanted talismans to banish supernatural spirits. The game is designed for 2 to 4 players and blends elements of trick-taking and hand management, with sessions lasting around 15 to 20 minutes. Its fantasy theme immerses players in a battle between light and dark forces.

The gameplay unfolds over alternating phases called "Yin" and "Yang" phases, each with distinct rules. During the Yang phase, players engage in a "Go Out" style similar to classic shedding games, where the goal is to get rid of cards based on numerical strength. In contrast, the Yin phase introduces trick-taking mechanics, where players play cards to win tricks and collect cards played by others.

Players must effectively utilize their hand of talisman cards, each marked with numbers and associated with either the Yin or Yang phase. The challenge lies in predicting opponents' moves, managing the timing of playing specific talismans, and adjusting strategies to the shifting phases. Each decision could be pivotal in achieving victory by being the first to empty one's hand.

The ultimate goal is to strategically employ your talismans and adaptability to become the first player to have zero cards, mastering the delicate balance of offense and defense that this enchanting battle demands.

boardgame: Middle-earth Strategy Battle Game: War In Rohan
description:
Inside this 128-page hardback supplement, you will find:

– History of Rohan: A guide to the realm, from its founding by Eorl the Young to the time of Helm Hammerhand and the troubled reign of King Théoden at the end of the Third Age.

– Scenarios: 24 narrative play Scenarios ranging in size from small skirmishes to mighty clashes let you refight your favourite battles from the books and films, from the burning of the Westfold to the fateful siege of Helm’s Deep.

– War in Rohan Campaign: Link the Scenarios together into a flowing campaign, with each battle having an impact on others.

– Sieges: Brand-new rules for fighting sieges, including siege equipment, profiles for various items and equipment that you might encounter during a siege, as well as internal and external doors found within a castle. Also includes two Siege-specific Scenarios to allow you to recreate siege battles of your own creation.

– Armies: Background, tactics and army lists for Rohan, Lothlórien, Fangorn, Isengard, and Dunland. Includes new profiles for a range of Heroes.

– Legendary Legions: Nine stand-alone army lists representing the forces that fought in famous battles of the War of the Ring including: The Defenders of Helm’s Deep, Théodred’s Guard, Riders of Éomer, Paths of the Druadan, Uglûk’s Scouts, Wolves of Isengard, Assault upon Helm’s Deep, and Army of Dunland.

– Miniatures showcase: A selection of Citadel and Forge World models for each of the armies in this supplement.

Naval battle in European waters is a simulation game that uses the system of the "Solomon Night Naval Battle" to handle naval combat in European waters during WW2.

The game system is almost the same as that of the "Solomon Night Naval Battle", but with additional optional rules for crossfire and additional damage caused by fire.

The ships that appear include almost all of the main vessels of Germany, Italy, France and the Soviet Union, from cruisers upwards, and the British Navy includes all of its battleships, battle cruisers and main cruisers. Destroyers also appear in units of one ship.

There are a total of 16 scenarios, including the famous Battle of the Denmark Strait, Battle of the Barents Sea and the multiple naval battles between the British and Italian navies in the Mediterranean.

There is also a hypothetical scenario in which the Soviet Navy appears.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Time of Crisis: The Roman Empire in Turmoil, 235-284 AD – Deluxe Edition
description:
Time of Crisis Deluxe Edition contains the original base game AND everything from The Age of Iron and Rust expansion that provides even more deckbuilding options, multiple options for Emperors, and AI bot players for solo play! PLUS, for the first time ever:


     A combined rulebook that incorporates all errata/clarifications plus several minor rule changes for improved balance
     Updated Amphitheater tokens
     Updated Foederati cards
     An all-new mini expansion containing 3 new types of Improvements that you can build in your provinces for even more strategy options.


If you already own Time of Crisis and The Age of Iron and Rust, we are offering a Deluxe Upgrade Kit that will get you the mini expansion plus the updated rules, Amphitheater tokens, and Foederati cards.!
 In Time of Crisis, 2-4 players take the reins of Roman dynasties, gathering and wielding influence among the senate, military, and people of Rome to ensure that their legacies are remembered by history instead of being lost to the mists of time. Starting from control of one province and a few low-value cards, you are challenged to establish your base of power during this fragile period of Roman history. You must build your armies, take control of valuable provinces, develop your support, and defend yourself against barbarian incursions, inopportune events, and the machinations of your political opponents.

Time of Crisis is easy to learn and playable in about 2-3 hours. It incorporates popular game mechanisms such as deckbuilding and hand management but also delivers a true light wargame experience. The historical flavor is strong with many meaningful decisions and opportunities presented to players on the board. This game finally fully closes the loop between deckbuilding card games and board games. Your cards dictate what actions are available to you on the board, and, in turn, your position on the board directly determines how you can add or remove cards from your deck to make yourself stronger for future turns. 
Will you specialize in manipulating the Senate, wielding the power of the Legions, or being a man of the People? 
Or will you master a blend of all three? 
In a twist unlike most deckbuilding games, you have the privilege of selecting the cards that compose your hand each turn! However, you still need to eventually work through your entire deck before you can use your cards again, so deck management and timing is critical.

description:
Love Letter Stories is a card game where you can play Love Letter up to eight times, and the story branches out each time, allowing you to enjoy a unique story experience.

The deck of cards changes gradually depending on the branching that occurs depending on the outcome of the game or the player's choices, and the cards that represent the characters that appear as a result.

Cards that represent the interactions and relationships with various people surrounding the love letter are displayed to the players. While some players will successfully win the love of the princess, others may form new bonds with others and have an unexpected future.
What path will the your character take, and how will you end up?

Love Letter Stories, the culmination of the Love Letter saga, is also packed with content.
There are 203 cards that cover the story branches, and the storybook with the ending is 88 pages long.
Become a character in the world of Love Letter and enjoy the story to your heart's content.

Not a legacy game. It can be replayed for different outcomes each time.

The Earth was recently attacked by aliens, and all defense systems seem ineffective against alien weaponry. As a result, governments worldwide have recruited explorers to venture into ancient ruins scattered across the globe in search of lost civilizations, hoping to uncover methods to resist the alien invasion. In this game, players take on the roles of these explorers, embarking on archaeological expeditions to various ruins. At the end of the game, the player with the highest score (representing the greatest archaeological achievements) emerges victorious.

boardgame: Khalsa! A Guide to Wargaming the Anglo-Sikh Wars 1845-1846 and 1848-1849
description:
Historical miniatures wargaming has been a popular hobby since at least the 1960s, and has become a diverse and multi-faceted pastime for thousands of people all over the globe. Players can walk the paths of military history in a game format, choosing any of the myriad campaigns of recorded military history from any time period and any part of the world. Such games often use carefully painted miniature model soldiers, each looking as close to its full size historical human counterpart as the wargamer can produce. This hobby is, for many, a visual feast as well as an intellectual entertainment and challenge.

This book details the two wars fought by the British-Indian Army against the Sikh Empire of Northern India in the middle of the nineteenth century. These hard-fought wars eventually resulted in the addition of the Punjab to the territories of British India and the introduction of the famous Sikh regiments to the British-Indian army. The descendants of these regiments still survive in the armies of India and Pakistan today. As a guide to wargaming these fascinating and colourful conflicts this book provides detailed information on the organisation and uniforms of both armies together with advice on how to build armies of model soldiers, and which rules are suitable to enable the enthusiast to bring these enthralling battles to the wargames table. Additionally the book also contains detailed narratives and orders of battle of both sides for all of the major battles, as well as scenarios for the wargamer to translate to his or her tabletop.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Keymaster: A Dungeon Escape Game
description:
The Keymaster | A Dungeon Escape Game is a tabletop strategy game, where adventurers risk their souls on a gambit of survival, sabotage, and luck to escape the underworld.

"You are dead. You’ve made a deal with the devil: If you can escape the 7th circle of hell, you will be reborn as the Master of Death. If you fail, you will be subjected to horrors unimaginable for all of time..."

Players break into a rat race treasure hunt for the key to escape the underworld. They find springs of resources, a river of fire and blood, and dark runes. They can collect up to 9 unique resources, sacrificing and combining them to progress in the game. They are not alone, and must avoid the minotaur—a dangerous beast who can take them temporarily or permanently out of the game. They must march through the land’s 3 power levels to find the key. Unlocking a player's freedom requires some forethought and a little luck...

The game's simultaneous gameplay ensures everyone is always engaged, its repetitive nature reinforces the rules and reveals new strategies, and the different paths to victory make winning more attainable and rewarding.

—NL Games

Embark on perilous Sikarin Missions to seek the elusive Celestial Shards, transforming Elite Ninjas into powerful Celestial Assassins and, ultimately, Celestial Immortals.

These missions will test your skills in fierce battles, rival assassins, and treacherous challenges. Only the strongest will survive and ascend to the pinnacle of power.

This thrilling expansion brings stunning new cards, dynamic missions, and high-stakes strategy to Ninjas Unleashed. Will you rise to the challenge and achieve immortality, or will you be lost to the shadows?

The latest TCG of the LoveLive! series.This game is played with energy cards, member cards and live cards. Players each construct a 60-card-deck to play, use energy cards to bring members onto the stage, make the live successful by bringing more member cards onto the stage. The player who successfully makes the live on the live card three times wins.

Have you ever wanted to know what your friends really think of you? Anxiety gives you the chance to talk trash about one another, but don’t forget, what goes around comes around. Step out of your realm of comfort and face hilarious accusations drawn by the group. Complete challenges in order to see what other players think of you.

How to play:
1. Player 1 chooses an Anxiety Card and reads it to themselves.

2. Player 1 answers which of the other players fits the Anxiety Card the best out loud. The accused doesn't know what they are being accused of. The player whose name gets called then picks from the Admit/Dare Card pile and has to do what that card says in order to know what the Anxiety Card question was.

3. The player who gets called out then has to complete a Dare or Admit Card to find out what they are being accused of. If the Admit/Dare Card gets completed, that player gets to keep the card for the points. The player to the right of whoever picked the Anxiety Card repeats this process.

4. The first player to 15 points wins. The winner then has to pick any other player to do the punishment they had previously written in the "Biggest Loser Book."

—description from the publisher

A quick duel of lies for 2 players.

Accumulate secret actions for Wibi with your opponent, then reveal them in order. The player who stomps the most grapes wins, or the player who crushes the fetuses immediately loses.

Will you be able to hold your opponent's gaze after trying to sneak it in?

Expand your copy of Rolling Realms or Rolling Realms Redux with this set of 6 promo cards, inspired by Finspan.

To play this realm you write numbers in a matching zone in the ocean. Depending on your placement, you will earn resources or the opportunity to write more numbers. Can you fill the entire ocean to earn 6 stars?

In the heart of enemy territory, one ship must survive.

Goliath is a solo print-and-play game that places you in command of the United Galactic Confederation's most formidable dreadnaught. Stranded after a hyperspace anomaly, Goliath must navigate a hostile sector, fend off relentless enemy ships, and destroy a heavily defended enemy target.

This strategic wargame combines tactical decision-making, resource management, and thrilling combat. Use your ship's firepower, repair drones, and clever maneuvering to outwit enemy ships and defense platforms. Victory depends on how well you adapt to escalating challenges while preserving the pride of the fleet.

Features:

Customizable setup, ensuring high replayability.
A thematic blend of tactical combat and survival under pressure.
Fully accessible as a print-and-play game with minimal components required.
Do you have what it takes to lead Goliath to victory—or will it become a tragic legend?

—description from the designer

Tir Janag is an ancient Persian game for two players that abstractly simulates war and border skirmishes between two kingdoms. This game is played on a cloth board marked with fields where units are placed and the King and Queen are moved. The winner of the game is the player who manages to bring their King to the central field, called the Throne, at least twice in three rounds.

Board has multiple spaces which are used for different purposes. Each space has a name.

Palace is where King and Queen are placed at the beginning of each game.

Queen's Gambit is space that is used to move the Queen by placing duplicates of  stone pebbles (called units from now on in the text).

Staging Area is the place where one unit is always placed each turn. All three yellow spaces are called a Skirmish Area.

War space is the last space in the row.

Ascent Path is going around the game board, from each Palace, meeting in the middle in space called the Throne.

Each Ascent Path, in the corner of the board has a Skirmish place, specially marked.

There are four different units in the game:

10 Footmen
8 Archers
8 Horsemen
6 Generals

Each unit has a Movement and Strength value, written on the board. Movement is represented with a Roman numeral and the Strength with Arabic numeral. Units also follow a hierarchy:
General > Horseman > Archer > Footman.

Footman has Movement of 1 and Strength of 2.
Archer has Movement of 3 and Strength of 1.
Horseman has Movement of 2 and Strength of 3.
General's Movement and Strength depends on the number of other units in his space, including other Generals.

King and Queen are special game pieces. Each of them moves only on the Ascent Path. Queen can't move further then a Skirmish space, and King can't move further than a Throne space. Both players have a King and a Queen and they start on the Palace space.

Bag is used to hold all 32 units. Inside units are randomized.

Each turn first player will draw two random units from the bag, place one on the Queen's Gambit space and the other on the Staging space. Then the other player will do the same. One unit always must be place in both named spaces on the board.

Depending which unit is placed where and of which type, different things will happen. You can't have two same unit types in Queen's Gambit or Skirmish Area spaces. Multiple units of the same type can be in the War space.

The moment you have two identical units in the Queen's Gambit space, move the queen forward on the Ascent Path by the number of spaces equal to the Movement power of the type of unit you duplicated.

The moment you have two identical units in the Staging Area, or any Skirmish area (spaces with yellow border), move a duplicate of a unit you just place and one other unit of a different type number of spaces equal to the Movement power of the type of unit you duplicated. This way, multiple units can be moved from one space to the other until they eventually end in the War space. Units can't move further then the War space.

Once Queen is in the Skirmish space Skirmishes will start. From War and Skirmishes combined unit strengths will affect King's movement. Once King is in the Throne space that player wins that round.

Player that wins best of three rounds is the winner of Tir Janag!

—description from the designer

Take on the role of a clever patron at the bar, passing drinks and avoiding “The Tab” in this lively, social card game. Each round, you’ll use strategy, bluffing, and deduction to pass drink cards to your fellow players.

Play seamlessly with the Standard Edition, focusing on simple drink-passing dynamics, or spice things up with the Deluxe Edition, where action cards add twists like reversing the passing order, forcing unexpected exchanges, or exposing hidden cards.

The game evolves as players navigate shifting alliances and unpredictable turns. A single move could change the balance of power, and when you think you’re in the clear, “The Tab” might land in your hand!

It’s easy to learn, fast-paced, and filled with suspense—perfect for game nights, parties, or any gathering where trust is in short supply. Will you play it safe, take bold risks, or deceive your way to victory? One thing’s for sure: no one wants to end the night with “The Tab”!

—description from the designer

Ahoy: Fang & Fortune is an expansion for Ahoy, featuring the Leviathan and the Coral Cap Pirates.


        The Leviathan: A terrifying beast that consumes all in its path, growing gargantuan.



        Coral Cap Pirates: Jolly rascals who just want to throw a party ... after some light mercenary work.


Mix and Match. you can use the Leviathan and Coral Cap Pirates to replace one or both Smugglers from the core game. Play with any combo you want!

Detour Deluxe is the first ever set released by HexPath.

It uses HexPath's patented rotating-tile mechanics which make every game diverse and dynamic.

The game comes with instructions for 5 games out of the box:
Detour, their most popular game, is a typical race to the finish game using dice, but with the rotating tiles, the paths are constantly changing, sometimes forcing a player to backtrack and find another route, hence the name Detour.
Drag race is mainly for 2 players on a head to head race to the finish but only on a straight line, and the dice dictate movement and tile rotation.
Two of the games, Mexican Standoff and Predator, are strictly strategic since there is no dice and no counting of moves.
Solitaire is for those who want to practice on their own.

—description from the designer

Similar to Clue games
The difference is in the rules
CLUE can only ask the next opponent, but Moe Detective has no such restriction.
It feels more free to play
The winning method is the same as CLUE

Calamity has struck Aeon! A once vibrant land lies on the brink of complete desolation. The collapse, an event whose very existence is shrouded in doubt, has shaken the fabric of Aeon to it's core. In it’s wake, Flagbearers (carriers of immensely powerful artifacts that unite the wills of all who stand behind them) have formed factions that are the last bastion of protection in Aeon’s increasingly dismal landscape. You will become one of these flagbearers and lead your faction in their struggle to control Aeon’s few remaining habitable territories.

Gameplay

The Flagbearer Expandable Card Game is a 2+ player strategic card game with each player utilizing a 40-card deck led by a flagbearer. Flagbearers provide players consistent access to powerful effects. Cards are played using pip-dice (you roll an increasing number of pip-dice each turn). Cards can be Monsters (cards that allow you to disrupt your opponents by attacking their Monsters or relics), Relics (cards that hide behind monsters and provide resource generation or various utility functions), or Actions (single use cards with a myriad of effects). The goal of the game is to reach 20 influence over your opponent. Influence is gained by having more spirit (a core stat that is present on many cards) than your opponent.

—description from the publisher

Each player starts with his piggy bank in a corner of a 10x10 grid, surrounded by 9 coins of his color.
Players can move the bank or a coin one space any direction. If after moving, his pig (which equals 2 coins) forms a line in which he has more coins then his opponent, he captures the coins.

Grimgrove is a challenging, solo board game where you must survive the perils of a cursed forest. As you explore, you'll face dangerous creatures, overcome obstacles, and manage your resources of time, health, food, and energy. Your ultimate goal is to escape the forest by finding a special exit card before succumbing to its dangers.

Key Features:

Dynamic Exploration: Every playthrough offers a unique experience as you uncover new locations and face unexpected challenges.

Strategic Resource Management: Balance your time, health, food, and energy to survive the harsh conditions.

Intense Atmosphere: Immerse yourself in a dark and foreboding world filled with danger and uncertainty.

Solitary Challenge: Test your wits and skills in this challenging solo adventure using Trick-Taking-like thinking.

—description from the publisher

Each player has their own deck of Fl!p52 playing cards(regular deck of cards + 5 Wild Cards), or the Fl!p52 App which can take the place of a deck of cards. 
The game board represents the 52 cards in a regular deck. Players place their 50 chips in "stacks" on the board to represent the cards they want. 
Players then start flipping cards off their deck in sequence. If they flip a card the matches their stack on the board, they remove that stack.
The players race to be the first to remove all of their stacks from the board. Chips remaining on the board are counted and tallied.
There are factors such as round variations, "Twinning" and Wild Cards that add to the strategy and dynamics of the game.

—description from the designer

The Zilch deck has 98 letter cards and 6 wild cards. In each round, the dealer quickly deals 8 cards face up to the table. The first player to call out a valid word of 4+ letters that can be spelled from the visible cards claims those letter cards. The dealer then replaces them for the next round. After the deck is used up, the player with the most card wins.

For specific cases:


     If no one can find a word, a player may call "Zilch!", after which the others continue trying to find a word. If no one can, the player who called Zilch collects all 8 cards; if someone can, that player gets all 8 cards. 



     If someone calls an invalid word, any other player can call "Challenge!" and the word is looked up in an agreed-on dictionary.  If the word is invalid, the challenger gets all 8 cards; if valid, the one who called the word gets all 8. 



     There are two more difficult levels: 
         Pro: deal out 10 cards per round, minimum word length is 5.
         Genius: deal out 12 cards per round, minimum word length is 6.
    
     Players of different skill can play together with handicaps: weaker players can call out words of 3+ letters; others need a minimum length of 4 or 5 or 6.

Forest Floor is a strategic card game that thrives on a shared deck, creating an element of tactical depth as players carefully manage resources and anticipate their opponents' moves. The ability to influence the order of draws by putting cards back on top of the deck adds a layer of complexity, rewarding players who can predict and manipulate the game's flow. At the heart of the game lies the "Mulching" mechanic, allowing players to interact with their opponents' cards before their effects resolve. This dynamic interaction encourages both cooperation and competition, as players work together to prevent any single player from gaining an overwhelming advantage.

Designed with accessibility in mind, Forest Floor boasts simple rules and an intuitive learning curve, making it enjoyable for players of all skill levels. However, beneath its approachable surface lies a wealth of strategic depth. Discover powerful card combinations, unlock hidden synergies, and navigate the ever-changing deck as you strive for ecological dominance. Each turn presents exciting choices: will you focus on rapid growth, bolster your defenses, or gather crucial information?

Prepare to embark on a journey through the forest, where every card played has the potential to shift the balance of power. In Forest Floor, the fate of your garden rests in your hands. Cultivate your ecosystem, outmaneuver your opponents, and emerge victorious in this engaging and rewarding card game.

—description from the publisher

If you can play Romme, you can also play Up and Down. With simple rules and lovingly designed playing and special cards. Mix these with a little strategy, a bit of luck and the right moment when laying them out - and you have an interesting card game for the whole family.

The players take  turns to place matching cards from their hand on the cards already laid out by the other players. You can use the special cards to double the number of cards you can place, give other players extra cards, swap colors or block one end of the card queue.

The winner is the first player to discard their cards.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Wer Romme kann, kann auch Up and Down spielen. Mit einfachen Regeln und liebevoll gestalteten Spiel- und Sonderkarten. Mischen Sie diese mit ein wenig Strategie, einer Portion Glück und dem richtigen Moment beim Auslegen – und schon haben Sie ein interessantes Kartenspiel für die ganze Familie.

Die Spieler legen abwechselnd passende Handkarten an den von den Mitspielern bereits ausgelegten Karten an. Mit den Sonderkarten können Sie die Ablagemöglichkeiten verdoppeln, den Mitspielern extra Karten geben, Farben tauschen oder Sie blockieren ein Ende der Kartenschlange.

Gewonnen hat, wer als erstes seine Karten abgelegt hat.

—description from the publisher
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach the finish wins the game.

Brutal Beatdown - Infestation is a solo only, fighting-themed enemy elimination dice game for ages 12+ that plays in 20-30 minutes.

In Brutal Beatdown, not all fighters are created equal. Each fighter is unique, offering their own Health, Skill, Energy, Speed and XP stat. Each fighter also has a specific Trait to help set them apart from all the others. Some are easier or harder to fight as or against.  Some are better suited for different modes of play. Choose wisely!

Brutal Beatdown has numerous interesting Items for you to use to your advantage as you fight for your survival. Each Item has its own requirements to meet in order to Use and Contest. But that's not all. Each also has its only success rate for a small dose of probability outcome.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Slaughter at Ponyri: Battle of the Northern Kursk Salient 7-9 July 1943
description:
Operation ZITADELLE was the German attempt to cut off the Kursk salient and surround five Soviet armies. While Army Group South attacked the southern end of the salient in what would lead to the massive tank battles around Prokhorovka, Army Group Center, led by General Walter Model’s 9. Armee, attacked from the north on 5 July. Lying directly in the path of this assault was the village of Ponyri.

SLAUGHTER AT PONYRI is a Historical Advanced Squad Leader (HASL) module that portrays the fighting around Ponyri during the three most crucial days, 7-9 July, on a beautiful historical map designed by Don Petros from Luftwaffe aerial photographs, supplemented by on the ground review, and executed by Charlie Kibler. A wide range of 12 individual scenarios, each recreating a portion of the battle on part of the map accompany the Campaign Game that covers the battle in six CG Dates over the three days featuring a familiar CG system covering the battle that was to become known as the “Stalingrad of Kursk.”

SLAUGHTER AT PONYRI includes special rules and counters for German sappers, elite Russian Airborne troops, mine belts, and fanatic outposts with rules kept simple to allow players of all levels to easily pick up the game, leaving the challenge as how to deploy and maneuver combined-arms forces over the large campaign game map. Three map sheets join to cover the battlefield, with a separate fourth sheet covering a central area where two of the main maps join. Included are enough additional counters to conduct the full Campaign Game with nothing other than BEYOND VALOR.

Contents:

        four maps
        eight countersheets
        one Chapter SP rules
        12 scenarios
        one Campaign Game
        three Chapter Dividers
        one box and lid


-description from publisher

Brothers, Sisters, Thieves is a 2-4 player tabletop card game in which players compete to be the last gang leader standing.

Players collect sets of 3 Siblings from 16 families to score points, recruit new gang members and risk stealing points for themselves. All the while they must avoid landing on the central Bust space, which could spell the end of their gang for good!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: SETI: Search for Extraterrestrial Intelligence – Gateway to Mars Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of A Pack from the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

Name: Gateway to Mars
card#: SE EN 02

Free-action corner: [1 data]
signal corner: [Blue signal]
income corner: [credit]

Main action:

Cost: 1 credit
Effect: triggerable mission
When you [orbit/land] on Mars (or its moons) or when you play a card mentioning "Mars" in its flavor text: 
[2 publicity] [5 points]

Flavor text: 
For man to reach Mars, we require the coordinated development of new propulsion systems, life support systems, radiation protection, and other key technologies.

The game mechanics are straightforward. Players take turns rolling the dice and moving around the board. There are several areas on the board that cause you to go to jail, return back to start, and screw other players over in a nasty way. You are trying to make it to the end/finishline to win, but there will be many pitfalls in your way, causing you to start over from the beginning. The game includes aspects of Monopoly, The Game of Life, Sorry, and Chutes and Ladders in a backstabbing and grimy world of ruthless competition. Parts of the game have you dealing with a shady public defender, two crooked police officers, the sudden death of elimination by another player, and collecting children only to pay child support to the brink of bankruptcy.

Dreamwood is a worker placement game where each player represents an animal species.

During each round, and event card is revealed and players will secretly place their workers on their player board to advance on 5 different tracks that represent the progress of each specie. The tracks are food, offspring, exploration, shelter, and leisure.


    Food track awards one time (x2) bonuses to worker placements.
    Offspring track awards additional workers.
    Exploration track awards a random income each round.
    Shelter track awards secret endgame bonuses.
    Leisure track awards immediate random advances on tracks. 


The game ends when at least 1 player reaches the end of a track. Players score victory points depending on their position on each track (relative to the other players). The player with the most victory points wins.

—description from the designer

SCENARIO PACK #4

This fourth scenario pack includes twenty more scenarios (bringing the total count of Dawn of Battle scenarios to 100!) that focus on medieval Japan, from the early Genpei war through the Sengoku period to further expand the enjoyment and playability of Dawn of Battle. Battles in this set include:

Uji, 1180 CE
Kurikara Pass, 1183 CE
Awazu, 1184 CE
Ichi-no-Tani, 1184 CE
Minatogawa, 1336 CE
Okehazama, 1560 CE
Kawanakajima, 1561 CE
The Anagawa, 1570 CE
Mikata-ga-Hara, 1573 CE
Nagashino, 1575 CE
Tedorigawa, 1577 CE
Yamazaki, 1582 CE
Shizugatake, 1583 CE
Komaki, 1584 CE
Nagakute, 1584 CE
Hitadori Bridge, 1586 CE
Hetsugigawa, 1586 CE
Suriagahara, 1589 CE
Sekigahara, 1600 CE
Tennoji, 1615 CE

-description from designer

Cat-In-8 is an innovative cat-themed abstract strategy game that challenges players to outwit their opponents in a race to score points by aligning three or more cat tokens in a row. Drawing inspiration from the word “catenate”—to link things together—this clever title takes the concept of connection to new heights through its unique tile-shifting mechanics.

The game’s dynamic 4x4 grid is in constant motion, with players rotating, sliding, and rearranging tiles to create scoring opportunities while thwarting their rivals. Adding to the unpredictability are Chaos Cards, which introduce playful twists to the game. These special cards let players swap tokens, rotate tiles, or push rows to shake up the board and keep everyone on their paws.

For those skilled (or lucky!) enough to align eight cat tokens in a single row, the ultimate victory is theirs—immediately ending the game in a thrilling feat of strategy and chaos.

Whether you’re a fan of lighthearted competition or calculated maneuvers, Cat-In-8 delivers a perfect balance of thoughtful gameplay and chaotic fun. With its easy-to-learn rules, replay ability, and original artwork, this game promises an engaging experience for casual gamers, families, and seasoned strategists alike.

Can you master making real money trading unreal assets? In Crash, compete to get richer by navigating a fluctuating and unpredictable market of “Real Digital Assets.” The cash value of cards can rise, and fall depending on the current state of the market and can change in an instant. Buy low and sell high to come out on top in this fast-paced trading game but try not to be caught out when your cards’ value crashes to nothing! See who can amass the most wealth in an unpredictable and ever-changing market! This game parodies the crypto craze. You don't need to know anything about cryptocurrency to play and enjoy. It's all about having fun and making the most cash with non-real digital assets!

Shadows over Europe 1920 is a game set in the heart of Europe in the aftermath of the Great War. All countries are in a period of restoration, while their governments stand on fragile legs, providing an opportunity for various cults to seize power and do as they please. 
In this game, you shall embody the president of a clandestine society formed to thwart the cults that seek to summon forth from the shadowed realms various creatures and beings. Their ultimate endeavor is to perform a ritual that will eventually conjure one of the eldritch gods from beyond the stars. To that end, you will manage resources, funds, and assets, employing investigators and dispatching them across Europe to thwart the cultists in their dark designs.

This is a single-player game that employs the so-called 'state of siege ' mechanics, where on various fronts you must prevent attacks from different creatures and factions.

Objective of the game:

During the game, the investigators' task is to fight against the cult and follow clues that will help them destroy the cult itself. They do this by preventing monsters from wreaking havoc across Europe, restoring political stability in the countries, and ultimately discovering the necessary artifacts for a ritual to prevent Eldritch gods from entering our world. 
Specifically, this is possible after the investigators complete both expeditions (in the Amazon and in Nepal).

Game turn sequence:

Each turn in the game consists of three main phases: the Event Phase, the Action Phase, and the Cleanup Phase.

During the Event Phase, you will draw one card from the top of the main deck (which you prepared according to the instructions) and resolve everything written on that card, from top to bottom.

During the Action Phase, you may perform two types of actions: actions undertaken by the investigators and actions of your secret society (special actions). 
Depending on their location on the map and with whom (or what) they share their place in the country, the investigators may perform one of the these actions: 

     Investigate one Rumour
     Attack one cultist
     Unmask one Cult
     Do a Political agitation
     Do a Monster Hunt
     Venture through Expedition


And, depending on the number of Resources secret society has, it can:

     Move Investigators
     Provide Ship and/or Airplane Tickets
     Employ Investigators
     Employ Police and/or Soldiers 


After all Investigators have spent their one action (or you decide to skip their action) turn has ended and it's time to re-fresh and cleanup the game-board. Do this in this order:

     Return all rumour Tokens that doesn't have a top right 'timeless' icon to their supply
     Flip all used tokens from your Charm and Supply placeholders
     Flip all Investigators to their 'ready' side (side with their portrait image)


You win the game at the end of the turn where both Expedition tokens are on the last numbered spot (5). This means that your Investigators have successfully found ancient relics that will help them to perform a powerful ritual that will cleanse Europe from the Cult and their attempt  to bring and Elder god to our world.

Another way you can possibly 'win' is if you go through the whole deck of cards with at least one Investigator still alive on the map. If this happens, consult the end of this game-book about the story of your endeavor and the of the Europe.

—description from the designer

Letter Duel's deck has 50 cards which each have 4 letter pairs on it, e.g. SI AE OL UR, or DE EL EP PO.  The deck is dealt out evenly into face-down piles for each player, except for 2 cards face-up in the center.

In each round players race to find 4-letter words made from a letter pair on each of the center cards, e.g. RULE or POOL or SIDE from the cards above. The first to find and call out a valid word gets to place one of their cards atop one of the center cards, starting the next round. The first player to get rid of all their cards and then call out another valid word wins the game.

Glade is a cozy, strategic gardening game for 3-6 players. Build up and customise your own garden over the course of 4 seasons. Seed mystical plants, surround your well-placed fields with powerful decorations and use your curious cultivators to unleash the unique spirit of your Glade.

The game begins with each player drawing two cultivators, mystic beings that reward players for accumulating certain types of plants (trees, flowering plants etc). These cultivators are then placed face-down next to one another to form a "Homestead" from which each player will grow their garden throughout the game.

Glade is played over 4 seasons (or rounds). At the beginning of a season, each player will draw a hand of cards that will include a mix of "Plants" and "Decorations". After "planting" a card by placing it down from their hand next to an already existing card, players will then pass their remaining cards to the player on their left or right. This continues until all players are given two cards. They will then plant one and place the other in their "Reserve", a collection of cards to be planted all at once at the end of the final season. Players then flip their planted cards face up and begin the next season after resolving any "Decoration" effects.


     Plants are the key component of each player's garden. By collecting matching plants and placing them next to one another, players will create "Fields" which are then scored for points at the end of the final season. By creating fields of plants that match their hidden Cultivators, players will gain even more! Each plant also has a unique "field requirement" which must be adhered to in order for the field to be considered valid at the end of the final season. Some examples include "This field must contain an even number of plants" or "Plants in this field must be planted at least 3 spaces from your Homestead".



     Decorations are special, rare cards that activate powerful effects if surrounded on all sides by matching plants at the end of a season. There are 8 of these powerful cards in the game, each with their own unique effect. Many decorations even allow players to modify their opponents' gardens, sabotaging existing fields or stealing coveted plants!


The drafting element of Glade gives players a lot to consider during a given season. "Do I want to further my own garden or stop the player next from me from acquiring a particular Plant?" "Can I predict what types of Plants are contested among the group?".

With a combination of drafting mechanics and a deep pool of unique plants, decorations and Cultivators, Glade is not just a game of developing your own garden, but a game of deduction and decisions. In Glade, the actions of you and your fellow players will determine whether the flow of each game leans more towards harmony or sabotage!

—description from the designer

boardgame: 偏見プロフィール (Henken Profile)
description:
"Don't judge people by their appearance!" That's what we've been taught all our lives, but how is it really? Everyone tends to guess a person's hobbies, personality, etc. from their appearance, right? Prejudice Profile is an immoral board game that goes beyond such taboos and lets you enjoy the coincidence of prejudices!
In each round, some players give clues by specific question cards and others will try to find out which face is the answer, who find out the answer would get one point. The players who give the best clue would get one point. After all players have taken turns playing two roles, the game ends and who have most points wins.
publishers: ゲーム工房カコムタク(Game Factory KAKOMUTAKU)

description:
Hunt for items matching the description on your card.

When you find something stuff it into your giant pants!

Action Game: Get everyone moving (and laughing) with this fast-paced family game! Played in pairs, it’s a race to grab a crazy collection of items and stuff them down your giant underwear

Hilarious Scavenger Hunt Game: Go on a lightning-quick journey to find something fuzzy, something red, or other surprises found in the cards. No need to overthink; just loads of laugh-out-loud fun

Fill Your Pants is a whole new kind of scavenger hunt. Players race to collect random things described on their game card, stuffing a wonderfully random assortment of things into their giant wacky pants as they go. Extremely easy and awesomely funny it’s a pant-filling race to collect random items. Whether they're tracking down items that are “Something furry”, “Something long”, “Something shiny”, or “Something floppy”, kids will be giggling the whole time as they expand their search. And with fifty categories to search for, there is always a ridiculous new combination in every game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Host Your Own Escape Room: Jungle Edition
description: Talking Tables escape room jungle edition. Players work together to solve puzzles and “escape” their doom. Cooperative puzzle mystery game.

boardgame: トライアングルコネクター (Triangle Connector)
description:
An abstract game with a dynamically changing board
Move your piece on the tiles with roads drawn on them and push the tiles to change the way the roads are connected.
The goal is to catch up with or push your opponent out.

盤面がダイナミックに変わるアブストラクトゲーム
道の描かれたタイルの上でコマを動かし、タイルを押すことで道の繋ぎ方を変えていきます
相手に追いつくか相手を追い出すことが目標です
Expand the board with more tiles and pair up to play against each other in teams (2 vs 2) as you strategize together in this team-based expansion.

Collect the most valuable cards among the most famous Trading Card Games!
Sell and buy cards in a changing market, control the stock flow and interfere in your opponent's plans activating the abilities linked to each item.

Card Game Traders is a fun and fast game where each turn you would play a card, changing their value, maybe activate their abilities and buy other cards in order to grow your collection (hand).
By the end of the game, played in less than 40 minutes, the player with the most valuable set of cards in their collection wins the game.

Card Game Traders reimplements CGT, revamping the game art wit fabulous homages to the most recognizable trading cards around the world, adding new elements and streamlining the game.

—description from the publisher

During  a high-seas expedition, you find yourself shipwrecked on an uncharted  island. In the distance, a storm is brewing. Repair your ship and set  sail before it destroys everything in its path!

Objective:
Explore  the island, gather resources, construct buildings, and recruit your  crew. Stock up on provisions and fix your ship before time runs out!

What You Need:
1 island sheet per castaway
1 pencil
1 white die and 1 black die

Are you ready to face the storm?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Taco Phở Cá Đá Trà Đào
description:
Taco Phở Cá Đá Trà Đào!
Keep this crazy string of words in mind. As soon as there is a match between a card and a spoken word, race to slap your hand on the central pile of cards.
The last person to do so must take them all! Be quick to get rid of all your cards, but watch out! Your mind will play tricks on you. This addictive game is as unique as its name!

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Taco Cat Goat Cheese Pizza

description:
Imagine harnessing the full energy output of an entire star by constructing a colossal megastructure around it. In Dyson Sphere, that’s precisely what you do. Orbiting a star, you deploy self-replicating robots to build massive sphere segments, capturing vast amounts of solar energy to secure victory.

Dyson Sphere is a game of straightforward rules, quick turns, and tough decisions. On your turn, you move your orbiter as many spaces as you wish, then take two different actions on your new space. The modular board ensures that each game presents a fresh set of actions to choose from. The game is also interactive – you’ll gain bonus actions when other players land on the same space as you. Furthermore, each time you construct a sphere segment, you add a new action to the board, keeping things dynamic.

Your primary resources are various self-replicating robots, which can be upgraded throughout the game to help you use your resources in unique ways. You begin with a space station, granting you a unique power, and as the game progresses, you can recruit crew members to gain additional special abilities.

You’ll also advance along tech tracks to boost your resource production and unlock new technologies. Plus, planets and asteroids orbiting the star can be explored for their valuable benefits.

Welcome to the final frontier of civilisation!

—description from the designer

Your task is to observe the actions of the three Targets threatening the world by jumping back and forth in time and its planes, and to shape and organize their sequence. If you skillfully manipulate the daily life of the Targets, you can get all three of them to appear at the same time. This is when the trigger must be pulled so that the Dimensional Crossing cartridge will cover all three Targets at the same time with a single shot. But you have to hurry, because you only have 25 minutes until the time loop closes!
Special skills and items are available for all of this.
But be careful because the Targets are extremely dangerous.

—description from the publisher

A stand-alone adventure gamebook set in the world of Brighthelm. Travel to Hatcheria to discover the secrets of the far-off mine. This special edition also includes a brand new adventure, The Cult of the Canary.

—user summary

You’ve lived in the sleepy seaside village of Baileytown your whole life. Under the watchful eye of your grandad Dagris Screbin, you’ve learned tricks from the local fishermen and heard tales of wonder from the passing merchants. Yet here you’ve stayed, happily watching the tide come and go.

But now it’s time to leave your home and see some of these wonders for yourself. Your grandad’s old friend Alf Blomwell has sent word from the far-off swamp town of Hatcheria. Something is amiss. Something that will threaten all Brighthelm. An ancient evil has awoken. Or has it?

Dagris says Alf has been going slowly senile for years, and he was pretty odd to start with. However, he’s not taking any chances. He’s sending you, his best adventurer across lands unknown to find out what has panicked old Alf.

The Curse of Saltash Mine is an adventure gamebook in which YOU are the hero. Armed with pencil, eraser and dice, you will decide which paths to take and which dangers to risk.

This new special edition adds difficulty settings and an achievements list to the original game. It also adds Story time with Dagris - two brand new adventures set in Brighthelm’s distant past!

—description from the publisher

Explore the city of Reutlingen, Germany. Move your token from the start to several destinations, event cards spice up the traveling experience.

This game was made on the initiative of parents for the 1984 bazaar of the Freie Georgenschule Reutlingen, Germany.

Publisher Blurb:
FIND THE RHYME IN TIME
Obama Llama is a mix between Articulate, charades and a 30-second rap battle.

BIG HIT, LITTLE BOX
The mini version of the game contains over 200 of our favourite rhymes from its bigger brother, Obama Llama 2.

KING KONG PLAYING PING PONG
Mime and describe rhymes to your team mates before the time runs out.

Box blurb:
Obama Llama returns with Obama and Tina the Llama coming back with even more rhyming drama.

It's the Return of the rhyming charades game with the strange-sounding name!

You've got 30 seconds to act out and describe as many ridiculous rhymes as you can for your team to shout out. The more they get right, the more chances you'll have to uncover the Rhyming Pair cards and win the game.

Can you find the rhyme in time?

Make It Quick is a fast-paced word game where players compete to name items in various categories, using letters on a spinning wheel, within a 10-second timeframe. Each player must quickly think of a unique answer that hasn't been previously mentioned, and the player who fails to come up with an answer or repeats an answer is eliminated from that round. The goal is to collect the most category cards by the end of the game to be declared the winner, ensuring plenty of competitive fun and quick thinking!

Play the Sushi Stax Game to become a chopsticks pro. Stack the sushi & pray they don't topple over!
Includes 10 stacking pieces, 1 beginner chopsticks, 1 base bowl, 1 instruction sheet
A fun activity to get your kids familiar with using chopsticks.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Adorable Demons: An Infernally Cute Card Game
description:
Enter the delightfully chaotic world of Adorable Demons, a fast-paced card game where you and your friends compete to become the ultimate Demon Lord! This family-friendly game is packed with strategy, humor, and irresistibly cute demon characters. Easy to learn but filled with tactical depth, it’s the perfect addition to game night for players of all ages.

In Adorable Demons, you’ll command a mischievous team of demons, each with their own unique abilities, to complete tasks, sabotage rivals, and collect powerful rewards. The game’s streamlined mechanics ensure quick turns and constant action, while the charming artwork keeps the experience lighthearted and fun. With a blend of card-drafting, resource management, and unpredictable power-ups, every playthrough feels fresh and exciting.

The game’s 50-card deck includes Demon, Task, Power-Up, and Mischief cards, each offering a variety of strategic opportunities. Whether you’re completing hilarious challenges or cleverly outmaneuvering opponents, your path to victory will be as entertaining as it is adorable. For those who prefer solo play, Adorable Demons includes a solo mode where players face off against a rival Demon Lord, that the rules allow to play itself. This gives an extra layer of challenge and replayability to the game. Especially if you don't have a group to play with.

Perfect for families, casual gamers, and experienced players alike, Adorable Demons is as adorable as it is diabolical. The game boasts engaging gameplay, unique theme, and whimsical art. This game is sure to capture the hearts of anyone who loves fun, strategy, and a little bit of mischief. Will you rise to the challenge and claim the Underworld throne? The choice (and the chaos) is yours!

—description from the designer

Rock Paper Scissors Word is a simple game, based on the finger game rock paper scissors, on the children's card game War, and on word spelling.

It includes: 

     32 cards (10 each of Rock, Paper, and Scissors, and 2 Sneaky Bear wild cards)
     20 letter tokens (enough to spell ROCK PAPER SCISSORS, plus an extra AE and a T)


The letter tiles are mixed face down, and the cards are shuffled and dealt among all players, kept in a face-down stack.  In their turn, a player challenges each other player by each revealing their top card. Whoever wins according to Rock-Scissors-Paper takes a token from the center. If it's a tie, the current player turns a center tile face up instead. The Sneaky Bear beats Rock and Scissors and Paper, and lets the winner take any token, either from the center or from another player.

A player wins immediately if the have the letters to spell ROCK or PAPER or SCISSORS. Otherwise, after all the tokens are claimed, players spell words using their letters won, scoring 1 point per letter used in a word, minus 1 for each letter not used; the highest score wins.

When first unlocked or used, the golden tent is placed on any available tent space, gaining its bonus after the coin penalty. On subsequent uses, it moves to a new space, freeing the previous one.

Available as an add-on or free for returning backers.

Word Wizard has 40 cards, which are sorted into separate piles of 10 vowels and 30 consonants. On their turn, a player chooses 6 cards from those piles and places them face up into the tray, then everyone has a minute to write down as many words as they can think of using those 6 letters. A player's score is one point per letter in each valid word they write down.  After every player has had 2 turns, the highest score wins.

The environment is at a tipping point. Australia’s wildlife habitats face devastation, but with your leadership, their future can be rewritten. It's time for you to take action and restore balance before it’s too late!

Step into the most ambitious Endangered expansion yet, packed with Bold new characters, Immersive new scenarios, Groundbreaking mechanics, Strategy deeper than ever before.  Every scenario is a battle for survival. Each one more intricate, more demanding, more thrilling than the last.

Endangered: Australia includes:

    The Great Barrier Reef – Defend one of the world’s natural wonders! Guard predator-prey chains, restore coral health, and protect an ecosystem in constant flux.
    The Giant Barred Frog – For the first time, you must remove animals to heal the balance. Stop invasive Cane Toads before they drown the land in chaos.
    The Koala – Fuzzy, iconic, and game-changing. Koalas bring Attachments, a brand-new mechanic that forces you to rethink every move.
    The Prime Minister – Wield the might of government itself! Eight unique cards give you unparalleled authority to sway the course of history.
    New Ambassador Type – A power broker unlike any other, building influence automatically and unlocking daring new tactics for victory.

The festive crew of Panda Pirates navigate the high seas on a giant turtle ship who they call their friend and their home. Sometimes, their fun occurrences call for a smaller moving platform, whether at sea or on land.

The minigames included here use the Raft tiles to test extreme balance skills.

boardgame: Party Panda Pirates: Avian Trilogy Party Cards!
description:
As the Panda Pirates sail the Avian Archipelago, they discover all sorts of charismatic birds and the different hectic activities in their day-to-day lives. Now, chicken warriors, dodo racers, and voracious kiwi meet the most festive panda crew and find themselves spicing up their routine with a dose of fun.

The minigames included here are based on three games from the Creature Kingdoms series: War for Chicken Island, Dodos Riding Dinos, and Kiwi Chow Down.

To play each minigame, you will need components from the respective game. Make sure you have the boxes at hand if you want to party through the Avian Archipelago!

—description from the publisher

The T10 Collection consists of 23 trick-taking games playable using a Rage deck (or other decks as the specific cards needed for each game are listed at the beginning of each set of rules). These games were designed for the T10 trick-taking event in January 2025.

Racket: Sugar Rush is a real-time, tabletop party game where all players play together simultaneously, making for fast-paced and frantic gameplay with equal parts strategy and chance. Your objective is to become the candy racketeer by being the first player to total 50 points over several rounds of play. All players will be dealt a handful of randomly shuffled candy cards to begin each round. You will then make blind trades with opponents in an attempt to collect all cards of the same candy before someone else does.

When the game starts, begin trading by removing cards of the same candy from your hand. You may trade candy with any opponent looking to trade that same number of cards. Continue trading until one player gets all cards of the same candy and calls out “Racket!"

Mixed amongst the candy cards are a Candy Jar card and a Dentist card. The Candy Jar card can act as a bonus card (if you win) or a penalty card (if you lose). The Dentist card is always a penalty card.

Each Racket deck contains 47 cards — 45 candy cards, a Candy Jar card and a Dentist card. There are 5 different types of candy cards with 9 cards of each type per deck. One pack of Racket cards can allow for game play of 3 to 5 players. For game play of up to 10 players combine both decks.

—description from the publisher

In Alphabet Loop, the rules are simple. Be the first player to work your way around the board from A-Z, while running into prompts with different categories.

For example, if you are starting at the Letter "A", and you get the prompt "Restaurants", an acceptable answer would be "Arby's".

Have fun and be the first one to go all the way through the alphabet to win the game!

—description from the publisher

Spell Wizard has a bag with 205 letter tiles, and a box with 196 cards which each have three letter groupings that commonly occur in words, e.g.:

lis
age
ent

A turn consists drawing a card, taking 13 tiles from the bag, and using those tiles to make one word for each of the three letter groupings, e.g.:

lis ten
eng age
c ent er

The points for each word are 1 for each letter added to the grouping. Blank tiles can stand for any letter, but do not add to the score. The word's points are doubled if there are letters placed on both sides of the grouping (as in c ent er); doubled if five or more letters are added to the grouping; tripled if both. After an agreed-on number of turns per player, the one with the highest score wins.

Also included are a 3-minute timer, and a pocket dictionary. Several variations are also included in the rules.

Set sail for blood, sweat, and terrors on Isla De Sangre with resident evil mad doctor Akira Lorca. Lorca's experiments with evolution and a jungle plant serum derived from man=eating trees creates a hideous head-hunting, body swapping zombie beast that terrorizes the island!

When a Vietnam Veteran and an ex-war buddy team up for a top-secret mission to the mysterious Blood Island, they encounter monsters, mutations, and black magic. Their mission becomes a tale of heroes, heroines, and horrors when the veteran's estranged daughter and adopted son come along for the ride.

In Tales of Blood Island Game, each player's hero character must escape Blood Island while avoiding the Beast of Blood in it's many bits, pieces, and forms. Players collect valuable talismans that are worth positive or negative points. Re-connecting the Beast of Blood's head to it's dismembered hand, foot, or body is also part of the fun!

Discovering the relics hidden beneath the tiles of an ancient palace will be a challenging mission.
Goal: Be the first to find 3 relics of the same type.
Dynamics: Before starting, each player decides which relic they want to look for: gem, bottle, coin or key.

1- Mix the tiles very well and place them face down on the table, without looking at the images of the relics, forming a board of 4 tiles x 4 tiles.
2- Place the explorers outside the board, in any location and check what color they have on their base.
3- Choose the “Genius Power” tokens. To do this, mix the 8 tokens and separate 1, without looking at them. The others will be passed from the first to the last player and each one will secretly choose a token.
4- The round begins with the youngest player.
5- Both dice are thrown.
According to the indication of the colored die, choose the explorer who has that color on his base, showing it to confirm the success. Here you have to remember the location of that color! If the player misses the explorer's color, he loses his turn.
If the die shows the clover symbol, an explorer of any color can be chosen. It must also be shown.
According to the indication on the numerical die, the explorer moves horizontally and/or vertically the number of tiles that were shown on the die (see examples). Movements cannot be diagonal, except with the use of the "Genie's Power" token, which allows it.
a- If the player lands on a tile of the relic he is looking for, he leaves it face up on the board and places the explorer on it.
b- If the player does not find the object he is looking for, he leaves the tile face down and places the explorer on it.
6- The first to turn over his 3 relics will be the winner.
7- Each player in the turn he chooses may use his "Genie's Power" token. He may only use it once in the game. Each token explains the special action that is allowed.

—description from the designer

You’re a lemonade entrepreneur!

Build your lemonade stand, buy ingredients, and sell lemonade to customers. Manage money and inventory. A fun game for children and adults that combines puzzles and board games!

GAMEPLAY SUMMARY

You’re an entrepreneur who has launched a LEMONADE STAND business.

Sell LEMONADE to CUSTOMERS to earn MONEY.

The player with the most MONEY wins!

Build your LEMONADE STAND by selecting your favorite puzzle segments and assemble the puzzle. Pay $1 for each piece.

Buy a POWERUP to gain a competitive advantage.

Buy LEMONADE ingredients from the GROCERY STORE. REGULAR CUP costs $1 for two and FANCY costs $1 for one.

WEATHER affects the number of CUSTOMERS that arrive each day.

Sell LEMONADE to CUSTOMERS by drawing a CUSTOMER tile and placing it on the DAY FRAME. Try to match the CUSTOMER'S LEMONADE order to the inventory you have available. REGULAR CUP of LEMONADE sells for $1 and FANCY sells for $2.

Exchange MONEY at the BANK.

Players take turns serving and competing for CUSTOMERS.

The Duit set has over 500 thick cardboard tiles, each being either a letter or a black square, and a booklet for 10 different games, most of which are actually puzzles:

- Crossword-O-Grams. 5 crossword puzzles, with clues to construct a the puzzles as well as solve it.

- Scrambled Letters. About 60 scrambled words in different categories, which players can unscramble using the tiles.

- Word Ladders. 10 different puzzles which transform one 3-letter word into another (e.g. ANY to WAS) by repeatedly removing the last letter and adding a letter in front to make a new word.

- Stepping Stones. 24 puzzles to change one word to another (e.g. BEAT to LOST) by changing one letter at a time, to make another word.

- Anagrams. 10 phrases which can be rearranged to make the names of presidents of the US.

- Game of Anagrams. The classic game where in your turn you reveal a letter, and if you can, make a word using it and either other unused letters, or all the letters of an already-formed word, stealing it from the word's owner.  The first to have 10 words wins.

- Hidden Word. 10 puzzles where you find a word hidden across several words in a sentence,

- Ghost. The classic game where you add a letter to a growing word fragment without finishing an actual work.

- Scrambled Word Squares. 16 arrays of 3x3, 4x4, 5x5 letters, which are to be unscrambled to make word squares.

- Checkers. There is a checkerboard on the box bottom, and the tiles can be used to play checkers -- black squares vs. letter squares.

Giant Word Game has 100 large durable plywood letter tiles, 10cm x 10cm, suitable for playing on the floor or outside on the lawn.

The rules are minimal. Players each draw a number of tiles, then the 2 blank tiles are added back and the remainder are spread face down.  Then players "race to complete the biggest word first".

Filling in the rest, presumably: 

     There are no turns; play is simultaneous. 
     The blank tiles are wild and can be used as any letter.
     At any time a player may discard one of their tiles to take one from the "table". 
     The first player to use all their letters to spell a valid word wins. 
     If no one wins before a certain amount of time has passed, the player with the longest valid word wins. 


—user summary

Soccer simulator game, exclusively manufactured in Brazil, using miniature players with an articulated leg and different foot models for various types of kicks. This game was created in 1979 and is still played and sold in the country, though it is little known outside of it. It features a fragile fabric field, which many players prefer to replace with carpet. Like any soccer simulator, Futebol Club Gulliver aims for immersion in the game of football, and many of its rules are the same as those adopted by the sport, with only a few adjustments to fit the game's structure. The game uses point-to-point movement and skill as its mechanics. The objective is to defeat the opponent by scoring the most goals, just like in football.

boardgame: The Gatekeeper: The Keymaster Mini Expansion
description:
Resurrection is no more. The first player caught by the minotaur merges souls with the beast and is engulfed by its dark spirit. Their new objective: catch all other players to win.

—NL Games
This Fled expansion features a scary solo and multiplayer variant.

Choose one of the puzzles and try to fit the pieces together. It's easy at first, but then... Be careful! Use all your reasoning, otherwise... you could fall into a TRAP!

On a 2 player game, you face-off each other trying to block your opponent from laying down his pieces on the board. You start by choosing a random level, and split all pieces equally, if one player get one additional piece than the other, then he must start the game. Player take turns putting his pieces on the board, when no player can put any more pieces on the board the game ends, and the player with less pieces remaining is the winner. If both players have 0 pieces left it's a win for both, or a draw. It's really similar to Blokus in that regard, but here the board is small, you have less pieces, and you and your opponent might not have the same pieces on their hand, bringing a similar feeling of a game of Dominoes.

There are variants, such as: being able to see what pieces your opponent have, to block more efficiently. or play with a time limit for your turn (Like 10s).

You could try playing with more players, but with 2 players the game is already fast enough and the game already have few pieces.

There's also a solo, or co-op, variant, which the goal is to lay down all pieces on the board, and is more close to a puzzle game. But you can play with a time limit so you can either win or lose.

Guess the famous figures using as few clues as possible. Just one thing - the clues are written as snarky social posts. Guess the greats before everyone else to earn points and move along the board. If you have a sense of humor, an interest in pop culture, and has at least a D+ average in school, this game is for you.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Game of Space A Family Game
description:
The Game of Space is a wonderfully elaborate Space exploration game from Japan around 1969. The 3D game board has a winding path for players to travel through the Solar System. The path has a space station that is the central command post, players travel around the planets passing 3D red and green satellites, cosmic graveyard, Space Swimming pool and Solar flares. The players path has a plethora of pictures of real and fictional craft from the 60's. On the playing board are an placed an Alien, a Space beauty, monsters, gold and Uranium Ore.

Players construct a rocket that contains 4 astronauts, along the way they may lose astronauts and if all are lost the player is out the game. Players also receive various value cards to help their travel and include, energy, space food, Lifesaving certificates, License's and accident Prevention certificates.

Players spin a roulette Space station wheel with a ball that determines a number to move. As you travel around the board you can collect monsters, Alien, Space beauty, bet for food or energy with others using the Roulette wheel. Players can directly land on a planet and receive a merit badge to wear and receive points.

A player is deemed the winner by returning to earth and having the most points at the end of the game

Gasolina de Sangue is a racing game that will make you fight, gain and lose a lot of gasoline, defeat violent opponents, and try to survive until the end of the race...if you can.

The game Gasolina de Sangue will make you calculate your fuel consumption with each acceleration, use intelligence combined with strategy in a race for your own life, so get ready.

Game for 2 to 5 participants, with 3 types of missions, one of which can be played in pairs, the game contains 14 road tiles, which you assemble in countless ways, and you also have the possibility of choosing more aggressive players to use.

Now it's up to you, will you face this road full of enemies?

Cosmos is a relatively obscure vintage space game of skill from France. The board consists of the cosmos with each corner having the moon, Venus, Mars or Jupiter. Central to the board is a rotating 3D metal half earth (Northern Hemisphere). Attached to the Earth is an arm that has a flying saucer suspended from the end.

The game requires players to stop the rotating earth so the flying saucer is over a planet or moon. The player can then claim title to the planet and earn points.  If others land on the conquered world then they pay a fee for right of passage.

The object of the game is to obtain titles for all the celestial bodies and the player with the most money wins

boardgame: Road-Trip à la Découverte de l'Ardèche
description:
Jump in your vehicle and go explore the Ardèche (a French department) to add as many Tourism cards as possible to your road trip. But watch out for route errors or other breakdowns, because your opponents can delay your progress! A card game for the whole family to discover the Ardèche tourist attractions while having fun!

Saute dans ton véhicule et pars explorer l’Ardèche afin d’ajouter le plus de cartes Tourisme à ton Road-trip. Mais attention aux erreurs de parcours ou autres pannes, car tes adversaires peuvent retarder ta progression ! Un jeu de cartes pour toute la famille pour découvrir en famille les sites touristiques ardéchois tout en s’amusant !

And the light-hearted shepherding championship is about to begin! Each shepherd will direct the sheep in his pen to form the right trios in the field. Be vigilant and, if necessary, let the dogs out! For the wolves have done their best to disguise themselves. And if you happen to find the special sheep, you can use your skills, because the wolves don't count on a SHEEP IN A WOLF'S SKIN!

—description from the publisher

In this game, your goal is to create trios of sheep from the same color or creating a family with sheep of two basic colors and one with the combination (like Blue, Yellow and Green sheep).
Every round you buy a card and discard a card, trying to improve your hand. Is you make a trio, you can place in front of you and, according to the sheep used, you can draw more carts into your hand.
But during your turn, other players assume the role of wolfs. If any opponent have a wolf in the hand, he can try to kill one of your sheep in already created trios.
At the same time, if you have a dog in your hand, you can avoid this king of attack.
The first player to have three trios win the game.

Death Front - Bloody Europe is a tactical counter-plot game with the combat group as the basic unit. As the first installment of the Black Gate War series, Bloody Europe takes us to the European theater after the Normandy landings in 1944. In the game players can play as commanders of American mechanized infantry and German armored bombardiers. The setup is completely in accordance with the real establishment to set the combination of soldiers, players can play squad leader, platoon leader or even company commander to command the implementation of different combat tasks. Depending on the mission, you will have the opportunity to lead the U.S. or German troops at the squad, platoon or company level to the end of the battle with the opponent.

War of Novoland is the first original card tabletop game of Novoland, combining role-playing, land-occupation, strategy and puzzles, and reasoning and fighting. Game elements include pieces, cards and maps, etc. It is suitable for 2-8 players to play at the same time. Best experienced in 3V3 battles.
Players take on new roles each round to represent characters they hire in order to help them acquire gold and erect buildings. The game ends at the close of a round in which a player erects his/her eighth building. Players then tally their points, and the player with the highest score wins.

Thirty famous characters from the world of Novoland, each with two unique character skills, allow you to lead familiar heroes against each other in The Legend of Novoland.
The Legend of Novoland is a card game based off the Chinese series of fantasy novels, Novoland, and shares many rules with BANG!
We have three camps, the Wasteland, the Ruins, and the Lord of Revelation. The standard rules had it that if an even number of people were playing, then there would be only two sides, Desolation and Market, with an equal number of people; if an odd number of people were playing, then one person would fill the role of the Lord of Revelation. And so the victory conditions are simple:
Lord of Revelation: Bring in two reinforcements.
Wasteland: (if the Lord of Revelation doesn't win) overthrow the Ruins.
Ruins: (if the Lord of Revelation doesn't win) overthrow the Wasteland.

The tabletop game “History of the Party” has a map of the Party's history and allows up to 7 players to participate at the same time. After each player selects the pieces for different games, he or she rolls the dice once, and the player with the largest number starts from the beginning and moves his or her piece forward by the corresponding number of squares according to the number rolled out by the dice. When a piece stays in a year with a picture, the player is required to interact with the Party History Quiz, if the answer is correct they can get various rewards such as moving forward, doing it again, etc. If the answer is wrong, they are required to move backward or receive other minor penalties. When a player stays or passes the highest point (2011) the game is finished and that player is the winner, if more than one player reaches the highest point (2011) at the same time in the same turn then the victory is shared.

Emberdire is a big game in a tiny package, a light RPG dungeon crawl on a single poker card. The premise is simple: Choose your character and move from room to room, defeating monsters (mobs) and get to the end of the dungeon alive.

All you need to get started is a six-sided die and a few coins to represent the treasures you'll find along the way. You cut the top section of the card, cutting out the character card and the standee; both are double-sided so you can choose which character you want to play.

You set your standee at the start of the dungeon and you're ready to go! Each turn you choose which room you will move to next, and as you do you'll encounter various denizens of the Emberdire and challenge them to mortal combat. You strike your enemy by rolling the die and seeing if the result is equal to or higher than your "to hit" value, the number next to the six-sided die on your character card.
