If you successfully strike you deal 1 damge to your adversary, if they survive then they get to strike back at you, rolling against their own "to hit" number. This continues until only one is left standing.

Succeed and any loot in the room is yours for the keeping and you move on to the next room of the dungeon. Fail and head back to the beginning to try again, maybe the Fates will shine brighter upon you on your next attempt!

—description from the designer

Little Kingdoms is an addictive high-stakes card game where two rivals clash in thrilling, fast-paced duels for control over four kingdoms. Recruit fantastical characters with powerful abilities, strategically manipulating the tide to tip the balance in your favor. But beware...the Queen, a ruthless force, reigns as an unforgiving referee, and her judgment is swift. She will banish any weakened characters from her presence and unlock ancient portals that connect to the elemental forces of this universe. With each portal that opens, the stakes rise dramatically, unleashing epic confrontations.
Your goal? Reduce your opponent’s mana to zero before they do the same to you. In this game of merciless strategy and unpredictable twists, only the most cunning will emerge victorious. Every decision could tip the scale toward glory—or doom! Will you conquer the kingdoms, or will the Queen’s judgment seal your fate?

—description from the publisher

Get ready to blast into space with this crazy alien adventure game! Use the bouncy UFOs to abduct farm animals to bring back to your home planet, but keep an eye out for the Alien Antics Cards. Grab the farm animals and make some cosmic combinations to get the most points to win! Kids love the non-stop slinky action as they race to capture the most cards!

How to play:
1.) Use your UFOs super boing action!
2.) Collect as many characters as you can. 
3.) Score the most points to win!

Contents:
- 3 UFOs
- 16 Farm Cards (4 Chickens, 4 Sheep, 3 Cows, 3 Pigs, 2 Farmers)
- 4 Alien Antics Cards

The 12 cards with a white are shuffled and put face down on the table. The remaining cards are also shuffled and put on the different places next to the shops so that there are 6 cards for every shop.
Every child gets a pedestrian and a cyclist in one color. The goal of the game is to find a good way to visit all shops according to the traffic signs and traffic lights. The first child who manages to reach the target field with cards of each shop wins.

This is a cooperative game for children up to 2 years. The goal is to get the duck to the pond.
Place the duck on the start field and put the die next to the board. Divide the task tiles into 3 piles by colour, and place them with the pictures facing down so that all children have access to them.
The youngest player begins. In their turn, they roll the die and move the duck to the nearest field in the color indicated by the throw. Next, they take a tile in the corresponding color and perform the task. After performing the task, the player takes the tile for themselves. Then, the next player to the left begins their turn.
The game ends as soon as you get the duck on the pond and stand on the tricolor bridge.

boardgame: Да, но это другое (Yes, But not the Same)
description:
Every coin has two sides and every "Yes, but" meme has two parts.
Time to pair them up!

Match cards, share the stories they tell and check if its what the author meant. Match three correct pairs to win!

Да, но это другое ("Yes, But not the Same") is a second fun card game based on Anton Gudim's meme comics with new card sets! Exchange cards, match pairs and don’t forget to check if you were correct about what the author meant. Match three comic strips before everyone else and you win!

—description from the publisher

The goal of the game is to collect six petals and create a flower with them.
The youngest player starts and takes the cup with the 3 balls and moves it over one of the flowers so that the balls drop into the petals. For every ball on a colourful petal, the player can take a petal from this flower. For the balls on a green petal, the player doesn't get anything.
As soon as one player has six petals, they create a flower and win.

Challenge your friends and family to estimate lengths using unique challenge cards and the tape measure. Suitable for ages 12 and up and for 2 to 6 players, this is a great game of skill and strategy.

Simple game concept makes it easy to pick up and explain to friends and family members of all ages. Gameplay is approximately 20 to 30 minutes.

—description from the designer

Cat-egories is a fast-paced, creative card game where players match quirky cat characters to clever descriptions, aiming to win over the judge with the best fit. Fun, easy to learn, and filled with laughter!

—description from the publisher

The robber Hotzenplotz is back in town!

The players must therefore put together search parties - consisting of Kasperl, Seppel, Constable Dimpfelmoser and other allies.

Who will be the quickest to fulfill the requirements for the search parties and discard the matching friend cards?  
Whoever has got rid of the most cards after 9 rounds has shown the best intuition in the search for the robber Hotzenplotz.

boardgame: Vicious Gardens: Chaos Critters & Fancy Plants
description:
Fancy Plants
Work all season to grow the fanciest of Fancy Plants. These secret goal cards provide bonus points at the End of the Season. Don't make your intentions too obvious though, your fellow gardeners might discover your plans and try to thwart your progress.
Chaos Critters
Add wildlife to your garden with Chaos Critters! Incorporated in the Specialist Deck, these critters will give your garden unique perks or inhibit your neighbors. You must keep them fed with a steady supply of plants. Otherwise, they may find their way to your competitors! Chaos Critters have no loyalty, they just want to nosh.

boardgame: Vicious Gardens: Cruel Summers & Noxious Weeds
description:
Cruel Summers
This expansion adds a mini board to Vicious Gardens and introduces a group event. When drought hits in the Cruel Summer, all players have the potential to lose some (or all) of their plants. Time the Cruel Summer right and you can take advantage of your neighbor's misfortune.
Noxious Weeds
Add a new category of plants to your gardens with Noxious Weeds. These cruel plants are worth negative points. However, if your Plot is large enough you can harvest them and steal a Victory Card!

All cards are shuffled and each player gets 15 cards. The remaining cards are put face down on the table. 
The first player takes a card from the pile and tries to create a word from his hand cards. The word should at least contain 5 letters. if he can't create a word, he just put a card on the discard pile. 
The player who manages to get rid off all cards first wins the game.

Jugglegrams has three cardboard sheets with punch-out letter tiles that can be used for several word puzzles and games. For individuals, the tiles are used; for groups, the letters are read out so people can write them down on their sheet -- a pad of blank white sheets is provided. Solutions are also provided on separate papers, some of them stapled shut.


     Jugglegrams. There are 36 puzzles on the sheets, each with 9-12 jumbled letters and a clue (e.g. "1. ADEEIIJNOORR A beautiful city a long way from New York")
     Words Within Words. This is a challenge to find all the words possible for each of the Jugglegrams puzzle sets of letters. (Their solution has 76 words from puzzle 1.) 
     Word Squares. Four puzzles where 36 or 25 letters are given, and the player arranges them into a 6x6 or 5x5 word square. Individuals can use the appropriate tiles to help in solving
     Juggled Slogans. 17 puzzles, each of which is a scrambled well-known advertising slogan (from the early 1930s). Each one has its letters listed in a way that shows the words' length (e.g. "AAE EOO OSS NNN WWHH MKT"). 
     Anagrams.  "After you have broken up all the letters in the Juggled Words, you have enough letters to play Anagrams." The sheets have a total of 432 tiles, 24 of which are blank, and each alphabet letter appears at least twice. Anagrams being a very popular game of the era, they do not explain how to play it.

Fluxx is a card game in which the cards themselves determine the current rules of the game. By playing cards, you change numerous aspects of the game: how to draw cards, how to play cards, and even how to win.

At the start of the game, each player holds three cards and on a turn a player draws one card, then plays one card. By playing cards, you can put new rules into play that change numerous aspects of the game: how many cards to draw or play, how many cards you can hold in hand or keep on the table in front of you, and (most importantly) how to win the game.

In Word Fluxx, the word-building version of the ever-changing card game, the keepers each feature a letter, and the goals involve making words of various types. With twice the typical number of keepers, you'll have plenty of letters to build nouns and verbs, palindromes, three-letter acronyms (TLAs), Roman numerals, and more.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
Jurassic Roll is a roll & write board game in which you will have to feed the dinosaurs surviving on an unexplored wild island, and keep them away from the erupting volcano in the center of the island.
Roll 5 dice each turn and at the same time players will use the dice values on their player sheet as they wish, being able to follow different scoring paths. 
You will be able to get dinosaur eggs with which to unlock new missions or rewards, explore fossils or get bonuses for rescuing groups of herbivorous, carnivorous or marine dinosaurs.
The player with the most victory points at the end of the game will be the winner.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Power City Arena: Vengeance in Sight
description:
Vengeance in Sight is the first expansion of Power City Arena.
It includes a new gameboard with a new level that brings new mechanics, 16 extra characters and new games mode.

Connect your ludiminos to the one of the other players.
Each player gets 11 ludiminos completed by 2 joker, 1 ban and 1 hand. That is a total of 16 tiles which are open.
In the course of the game, the player who does not manage to connect his ludiminos to the others, must put one in the bag and draw a new one.

The player who first places all his ludimino tiles wins.

The game consists of a die, an hour glass and 36 cards: 12 for sports (blue), leisure (orange) and nature (green) each.

The oldest player starts and roles a die. The color of the die decides the color of the card deck to select from. The player has to do the exercise described on the card for the number of times the die gives. If the player does that successfully, he gets the card.

The player who manages to get 4 cards of the same color first wins the game.

A meteorite is about to fall on us, the earth trembles and herds of dinosaurs try to rescue their eggs to escape as far as possible.
Reveal 2 of the cards face down, you must match eggs of the color of your herd to rescue them.
But beware, because the T-Rex will eat your dinosaurs and those of your rivals, the traps will kill any dinosaur and the velociraptors and pterodactyls will take the eggs.
The game will end with the fall of the meteorite, the player with the most eggs rescued will be the winner.

—description from the publisher

We open our family farm, a business in which we will plant carrots, tomatoes, peppers and lettuce, hire workers, improve our tools and facilities, hire workers and establish shipping routes to bring our products to our customers during the 4 harvests.
With a dice draft system, players will plant in their plot the dice obtained trying to plant them consecutively to obtain bonuses. 
Get rewards square by square by continuously combing in this roll & write game where the player with the most market points at the end of the game will win the game.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the pharmacy of chaos with Dr Gribouille!

Patients are rushing to get their medicine, but there's a problem: Dr Gribouille's prescriptions are illegible! It's up to you to decipher the squiggles and get everyone treated in time.

A hilarious game where observation and speed are essential.

Will you be able to understand Dr. Gribouille's prescriptions and help the most vulnerable patients? Get ready for games full of laughter and tension.

—description from the publisher

We open our family farm where we will collect dice of: carrots, tomatoes, peppers and lettuce in our warehouse. We will use our tools to modify the dice and ship the orders to our customers.
With a dice draft system, players in this roll & write game will try to get the best orders and combine customers to be the player with the most market points at the end of the game and win the game.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Kansas, where fertile fields, tough choices, and ambitious farm owners come together in this Print & Play roll-and-write adventure! In Kansas: Roll & Write, you’ll build your farm, grow vegetables, upgrade your tools, hire workers, and deliver goods across the state — all with clever dice drafting and placement mechanics.

How to Play:
Each round, players roll dice into a box. The value of each die and the vegetable zone it lands in determine what you can plant.
You’ll place vegetables on your player sheet (your farm), trying to group the same crops adjacently for bonus points.
Hire specialized workers, buy powerful tools, and upgrade your farm to fulfill orders more efficiently and score big!

With asymmetric characters, each player has a unique ability, and a solo mode lets you test your farming skills anytime.

At the end of the game add up the score obtained in the sheet of the base game and the expansion to know which player has obtained more points becoming the winner.

Test your math skills and strategy in Equation Hi-Lo, the ultimate blend of poker-style betting and equation-building fun! Compete to create equations that get as close as possible to 1 (low) or 20 (high) while outsmarting your opponents. Do you have what it takes to claim all the chips?

How It Works

Bet & Build: Use randomly dealt numbers from 1-10 and arithmetic operation cards (plus, minus, divide, multiply, square root) to construct equations that are close to 1 or 20. Bluff or go bold—your strategy could make or break the game!

Two Ways to Win a Hand: Bet on low (closest to 1) or high (closest to 20) and have the equation that is closest to that value. You may even be able to win a hand on a bluff. Feeling confident? Swing bet on both by making two equations!

Why You’ll Love It

Challenging & Rewarding: Perfect for math lovers and strategy enthusiasts alike.
Simple setup: No complex and dense rules – get started in 5 minutes!
Replayable: Each round is different, keeping players engaged every time!

Equation Hi-Lo is great for families, friends, students learning math and adults who want to dust off their skills and keep their mind sharp! Similar to the game played in Devil’s Plan on Netflix'

—description from the designer

Cosmil is a French strategy game between 2 players from Earth and Mars. The checkered board is divided into 5 zones being Earth and Earths field of attraction, the cosmos, Mars and its field of attraction. Players have 3 types of playing pieces with a value; being one Rocket, value 1 point. Six Bip-Bips, value 1 point and Eighteen Energy forces, valued at 2 points.

Play has some similarity to checkers in that some pieces diagonally move and jump opposing pieces of the same value. But pieces movement is horizontal or vertical to jump pieces of a different value. Some pieces have restricted movement depending what zone they are in. Example, Bip Bip can  move back or forward in the cosmos but only forward in the Field of planetary attraction.

There are 3 possibilities to win the game.

     That a players rocket lands on the opposing players planet.
     An opposing rocket is destroyed on ground or captured in flight
     That an opposing rocket is blocked, without the possibility of being cleared to move.


—user summary

Mission Planets, Conquer the galaxy is a super fun, easy to learn and very replayable board game from the MP05 studio that self-funded the game production and now already for sale on e-commerce platforms without publishers.

The History of the Game:

Year 3055. Our Sun threatened to explode and destroy the entire galaxy. Only 6 ships managed to escape before the catastrophe. We astronauts faced a journey in a distant galaxy to explore new planets and build space bases, confronting meteor showers and alien invasions that threaten the survival of our species. Our mission is just beginning...

Welcome astronauts! Start the Mission.

Mission Planets is a boardgame from 2 to 6 players where your mission is to get 8 points by building bases. You can earn points building bases, per combination of bases in planets of the same categories or using hidden points from spacial cards.

The game dynamics is roll and dice, receive cards, act building, attacking or moving your space craft through the universe.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Devabhaasha is the world's only Sanskrit card game. A game for which the players need not know the language - but can learn the language using colours, patterns, visuals and game mechanics. The players do need to be able to read the Devanagri script.

Players make sentences to gain points. They can use cards to steal points from the previous player. And use cards to impede the progress of the next player.

The player who reaches 48 points is the winner of the game
The game has solo mode of play as well as multiplayer mode.

The first player occupies the middle point of the game, the other 3 players, who must try to capture the first player, occupy 3 points of their choice.

The first player moves from his position to a point first, then the 2nd player, then the 1st player again, now the 3rd player, then the 1st player again and now the 4th player.
The 4 players take turns moving until the first player can no longer do so.
If there are 2 players, one takes the 1st token (escapee) and the other takes the 3 remaining tokens (pursuers).

In the game, that promotes Negus-Kaffee, three white boys try to catch one black boy.

We are now checking into the roaring sport of power boat drag racing. Some of the same concepts apply on water as they do on land but one big difference is that power boat drag racing use a moving start, approaching countdown and timing the green light…very challenging for pilots. This moving start is how Top Fuel Hydro’s can achieve a 3.0 second elapsed time in a quarter mile and over 270+ mph. This moving start is covered in the game along with a cool breezy system between pilot cards, event and finish tables. The game covers a nine-event competition between the greats of power boat drag racing. The simple dice system incorporates fast action with exciting results which coincide with this fast dangerous sport. Did I mention winds play a factor…” It was All Fine When I Crossed the Start Line”.

Get Ready for a Fast Action Short Season with The Greats!

—description from the designer

Players get cards with their hidden characters.
Then, a YouTube video tells the narration, which tells sequentially the different players to open their eyes and perform various actions.

Description from the publisher:

VaguFulus used to be a simple and fun game that maldivians used to play using rolled up pieces of paper with dhivehi character names written on it.

It was a deduction based game, where the goal was to catch the thief amongst the players. This was mainly played between the younger players and resulted in punishments given in the form of very light or slightly harder beatings to the losing individual.

VaguFulus card game is the first classified card game in Maldives which has been classified as 15+, as some texts or concepts of the game maybe inappropriate for children under the age of 15.

This is the Italian Version of Circadians: First Light, it is a Big Box with Expansion and bonus from Chur Games and Tesla Games. (Italian description below)

The aim of Circadians: First Light is to lead a team of researchers on the planet of Ryh. Players need to manage their crew (dice) to visit various parts of the planet for trade, farming, construction and research. Players score points for negotiating with the locals, harvesting resources for the depository, upgrading their research base, exploring the planet, and collecting gems. The game is played over eight rounds. At the end of the final round, the player with the most points wins.

L’Ambientazione

Eravamo ad anni luce dalla nostra casa, distanti galassie, quando per la prima volta abbiamo scoperto questo corpo celeste. Un pianeta pieno di forme di vita affascinanti e intelligenti, non troppo diverse da noi. Alcune hanno costruito regni al di sotto della superficie di mari verdeggianti, mentre altre controllano pianure desertiche e altipiani. Tra di loro abbiamo trovato scienziati, inventori, contadini, commercianti e combattenti.

Benché la nostra presenza abbia preoccupato alcuni, abbiamo avuto pochi inconvenienti con gli abitanti del posto. Comunque, noi Circadiani, rinomati esploratori Terrestri, dobbiamo fare il possibile per assicurare la pace. Dobbiamo rispettare questo pianeta e i suoi abitanti.

I capitani della Moontide ci hanno inviato ordini dall’alto. Apriamo le negoziazioni con i tre clan, con la speranza di ottenere il loro favore e la nostra sicurezza mentre siamo su questo pianeta. Dobbiamo anche raccogliere degli esemplari organici per il nostro magazzino sulla Moontide. Questo è un territorio nuovo per tutti ma dobbiamo essere coraggiosi e intraprendenti. Il futuro dei Circadiani dipende da questo.

Il Gioco

Lo scopo di Circadians: Prima Alba è di guidare una squadra di ricercatori sul pianeta Ryh scoperto di recente. I giocatori devono gestire il proprio equipaggio (i dadi) visitando i vari posti sul pianeta per commerciare, coltivare, costruire e fare ricerca. Il giocatore che accumula più punti al termine dei 7 round è il vincitore.

La big Box contiene:
- Circadians: First Light 2nd Edition
- Circadians: FIrst Light – Allies Expansion
- Circadians: FIrst Light – Specialists Expansions
- 2 Nuovi Leader Inediti “CHUR”

—description from designer

description:
In this card game up to 6 players try to react the fastest to gain cards until one player has them all and wins.

The FUCK and the SHIT cards are put in the middle of all players, then all other cards are distributed among all other players. they put thir cards face down in front of them. Clockwise players reveal their top card and put it face up in front of their card pile. Then the next player does the same. New cards revealed are put on top of the former revealed card.
As soon as 5 of the same symbol on all visible cards can be seen, players try to be the fastest to shout "FUCK" and put tzheir hand on the FUCK card. fastest player gets all revealed vcards.

If mistakenly shouting fuck the player loses his face down cards.

Additionally there are is the "SCHEISS" and the "CHAOS" card.

the SCHEISS card: if its revealed everybody has to try to put his hand on the SHIT card and say shit. The fastest player gets all revealed cards same as with the 5 symbols and shouting FUCK.

the CHAOS cards requires 6 instead of 5 symbols to be present to correctly shout FUCK and get alkl cards. another CHAOS card changes it from 6 back to five and so on.

This unique memory game features 28 Maldivian animals that look different as babies than their adult counterparts. 
This game can be played as a matching game with young children and as a more challenging memory game for older children and adults. 
Do you know how a baby batfish looks like? Let’s find out!

boardgame: Dhiveli Dhirun: Maldives Wildlife Card Game
description:
This card game features fun facts about Maldivian Wildlife. Learn about 48 local species categorized in 12 groups! 
There are many ways of playing Dhivehi Dhirun and fun is guaranteed for all players. 
Do you know what makes the parrotfish special? Let’s play Dhivehi Dhirun and find out!

Wild Side Number Sense Cards  
For kids in preschool through 8th grade!

An elegant and original game made of solid wood for hours of fun with family or friends.

Goal of the game:
Each player faces a square board that he will have to fill with the colored pieces.
These coins will be imposed on them by the roll of a dice.

The winner will be the first player to fully fill his square board with the wooden pieces.

Rules of the game:
Players each roll the die that indicates the piece to take (the first one must be placed at an angle of the board).


     the 1: red piece (square)
     the 2: green piece (rectangle, equal to 2 squares)
     the 3: white piece (in "L", equal to 3 squares)
     the 4: yellow piece (large square, equal to 4 squares)
     the 5: blue coin (in "L", equal to 5 squares)



     the 6: allows the player to:
         take the coin of your choice from an opponent's board
         move a piece from its tray
         remove a piece from its tray
    


Warning: Any piece placed cannot be moved during the game (unless the player makes 6).

Variant:
You can introduce a rule that prohibits placing 2 pieces of the same color side by side.
They can only touch each other from an angle.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Legendary: A James Bond Deck Building Game – Thunderball
description:
The iconic James Bond returns in this all-new Legendary expansion.

Relive the classic 007 film from the Sean Connery era: Thunderball. Step into the world of espionage, enlist new allies, pilot new vehicles, and acquire new gadgets as you dive into all new missions and defeat Emilio Largo and the agents of SPECTRE! Submerge yourself into the world of this newest expansion and join 007 for one more secret mission!

—description from the publisher

A fast paced vintage themed sci-fi card game where you take on the role of an alien gathering parts to escape from Earth and go home, or a government agent trying to keep the aliens from leaving by keeping them from getting as many parts as possible. 
This is a competitive card game that can be played solo, or up to 4 people. The game lasts between 20-30 minutes and is appropriate for ages 8 and up.

A single player, or a max of four players place cards in the middle of the table in a style similar to Spades. The player with the highest number retains their card in the Capture Zone. These cards are in the form of Junk Cards, Allies, Ship Parts and cards that grant advantages and disadvantages in gameplay. The player that collects all cards and meets pre-requisites to leave the Earth, or gets the most points at the end of the game after various point adjustments, wins the game.

Players must strategically play cards, or get rid of cards in a way to avoid penalties, or avoid strategies of other players whether aliens or government agents trying to block them from getting parts.
The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach space 90 wins the game.

boardgame: Pocket Battles: Mobile Suit Gundam Operation Odessa
description:
Pocket Battles: Mobile Suit Gundam Operation Odessa is a Japanese doujin based on the game Pocket Battles from Z-Man Games.  It features land combat in the world of Gundam, an animated Japanese TV show.

Play is similar to the other games produced in this series.  Each counter has specific abilities, and there are some additional ones produced for this game that are not in others.

The game is sold as a kit and requires assembly.

description: It wasn't the best idea of the mayor, to save money by closing the local police station. Now cars are hijacked while parking, but nobody knows how this happens nor where the cars go. It's the task of the players to find out cooperatively, what's going on.

boardgame: Kingdoms: The Battle of Five Realms
description:
The Kingdoms in the Realms of Ëravor are in disarray.
But hope is not lost. For the prophecy tells, "as the world begins to fall, one will emerge... Destined to rule all."

Kingdoms: The Battle of Five Realms is a strategy-driven game of conquest, diplomacy, and survival set in the immersive, lore-rich world of Ëravor. It is a time of rising kingdoms and ancient magic, of cunning assassins and valiant defenders, where players battle for dominance in a land divided by treacherous terrain and unpredictable powers.

In Kingdoms, each player commands an army seeking to conquer territories, claim kingdoms, and rule realms. Players will strategically deploy forces, utilize powerful Kingdom and World Cards, and leverage the unique benefits and dangers of Ëravor’s diverse terrains. With every decision, they must balance offense, defense, and resource management to outwit their rivals and secure their place as the ultimate ruler.

Each player begins the game with their armies in reserve, a hand of Kingdom Cards, and the opportunity to shape their strategy based on the dynamic terrain and their opponents’ actions. The game is driven by a combination of tactical moves and card play: players place armies using coordinate cards, disrupt opponents with assassins and conquerors, or protect their forces with defenders and spells. Meanwhile, the map’s active terrains—forests, mountains, rivers, and more—offer both rewards and threats, keeping gameplay unpredictable and interactive.

Kingdoms rewards skill and planning over luck. While World Cards introduce variability through natural disasters, terrain benefits, and other events, players have tools to mitigate these risks with careful preparation. Combat is resolved through calculated choices, not random rolls, making every battle a test of strategy rather than chance.

Victory comes from ruling realms. To dominate a realm, players must conquer and defend 2 out of its 3 kingdoms by occupying key spaces and linking their armies to the kingdom. The path to victory is fraught with danger, as opponents can use their own armies, and magic to overturn even the most fortified kingdoms. Players must adapt their strategies to evolving threats, seize opportunities to expand their influence, and make critical decisions about when to attack, defend, or retreat.

From the treacherous Scorched Wood to the resource-rich Emerald Isles, each terrain offers unique opportunities for players to shape their paths to victory. The dynamic interplay of army movement, card play strategy, and terrain effects creates a deeply replayable experience that feels fresh every time.

Kingdoms: The Battle of Five Realms is a game of relentless competition, clever tactics, and thematic immersion. Will you rise as the ruler of Ëravor, or will your armies fall to the chaos of war?

—description from the publisher

The Alphabet deck has 54 letter cards, and its rule book has rules for 18 different games, which fall into three main categories:

Word games, where the letters are used to make words. 

     Grab. Like Snap, with players claiming the exposed cards if they see a word among the visible letters.
     Words. A rummy game where players try to make a word using all the letters in their (small) hand.
     Disco. Players try to be the first to empty their hand by adding letters to the current growing word fragment.
     Straight Poker. 7-card draw poker, either with or without betting. 
     Show Poker. Straight poker where players reveal a letter from their word when placing a bet. 
     Puzzles. 9 different solo challenges for word-making using part or all of the deck
     Crosswords. Solo game to try build up a crossword using all the deck, from a hand of 7 cards.
     Patience. A solo effort to make crosswords out of 3 sets of 18, 4 sets of 13, 5 sets of 10, or 6 sets of 9 cards. 


Children's games, based on the individual letter values of the cards. 

     Pairs. Concentration / Pelmanism using a 6x6 array of cards.
     Switch. Players try to be the first to empty their hand by playing a letter adjacent to the visible letter, or a Switch card. 
     The Clown. A version of Old Maid.


Gambling games, usually based on the relative positions of the letters in the alphabet, with players taking turns being the banker. 

     In Between. Players bet on whether their 3rd card will fall between their 1st 2 cards. 
     Banker. The banker, for each other player, bets on whether their 3rd card will fall between their 1st 2 cards.
     Ascot. A horse-race betting simulation.
     Blind Monkey. Players bet on whether their turned up card beats the dealer's card.
     Kitty. A Michigan variant, trying to play out your hand first, going low to high. 
     Slippery Jam. Players bet on whether either of their 2 cards will beat the dealer's card.
     Ponto. Players bet on whether their pair of cards will beat the dealer's pair.

Discover the special version of your favorite game, created for the 2024 Rummikub World Championship taking place in Gdańsk - Rummikub Gdańsk. In addition to elements from the standard version of Rummikub Classic, this game features:

     2 special tiles with the Fountain of Neptune (symbol of Gdańsk)
     A unique card with a description of the city of Gdańsk inside
     Unique design of the game's packaging


—description from the publisher

In addition to elements from the standard version of Rummikub Classic, this game features:

     2 special tiles with Stork
     Unique design of the game's packaging

Test your knowledge about Broadway history, award-winning plays, and showstopping musicals with this 200-card trivia game from Playbill, the most trusted magazine in theatre, with an included instructional booklet.

Whether you’re a devoted Broadway theatregoer or a fan of the stage who has never been to New York, now is your chance to step into the limelight and prove your passion. Answer questions both challenging and easy, like:


     Which play is MY FAIR LADY based on? 
     In 2019, André De Shields was honored with a Tony Award for his performance of what mythological god in the hit musical HADESTOWN?
     Name the real-life rock-and-roll group that inspired the jukebox musical JERSEY BOYS. 
     Which 1965 musical uses the classic novel Don Quixote as the basis for its story?


Compete in teams, set up a trivia night in your living room, or simply pull out the cards at your next festive evening with friends. However you choose to play, get ready for your curtain call to win the title of Playbill’s trivia champion. Break a leg!

—description from the publisher

Microbes or Yours? is a thinky card game for those who don't mind playing dirty. In the not-so-distant future, humans have created so much waste that awesome disgusting creatures have grown from the grossness… Hairy, Rashy, Rusty, and Slimy! Each earning deep enjoyment from spreading its germs around the kitchens, bathrooms, and bedrooms of the world.

As one of these great nasty beings, you and your fellow players will take turns, choosing and playing microbe tokens from your hand. Token values act as action points, allowing you to perform actions from the item cards around the house. You can use your own actions, but some situations allow you to use other players’ actions too!

After taking actions, you’ll then take your spent microbe tokens and place them onto the board. To spread all your own germs first and win the game you’ll need to outwit your opponents and beat them to the dirty finish!

—description from the publisher

In Street Library, players browse local street libraries to find books to read and add to their collection. The only catch is, you'll need to drop off a book in order to take one home.

The goal of this casual card game is to collect sets of various books and other second-hand items. You'll score by collecting the most valuable items and by achieving set collection goals. There are 3 base goals in the game, which are extended with game specific goals each time you play.

There are a number of different themed libraries players can choose to visit, laid out in the middle of the table. Every round players choose a card from their hand and reveal it simultaneously. The type of the card determines which library they will visit. If multiple players are going to the same library, they will visit in the order of the numbers on their revealed cards. But watch out - if you go to a library after another player, you’ll pick up the card they dropped off there, not the original card that was there!

Once all cards have been played from every player’s hand, points are earned for individual items and collected sets. The winner is the one with the best book collection overall!

—description from the publisher

A party game for the rest of us!

"Play stupid games, win stupid prizes", right?

This game is sarcastic, snarky, and full of NSFW words and references. It is not meant to hurt anyone or anything; but your funny bone should get a rough workout.

Similar to games like Cards Against Humanity, where your friends will judge your answers to wacky and messed up scenarios.

The Most Epic Game You Can Play in 20 Minutes! Legends of Gadia is a highly thematic fantasy card game sprung from the pages of the upcoming fantasy novel series GADIA by Su Mon Han. As in -- this is the same game the characters actually play in the books (in taverns and what have you)!

Legends of Gadia (or "LoG") immortalizes the 12 historical heroes from the world of GADIA whose final quest saved all humankind from impending doom at the cost of their own lives. To honor their sacrifice, the people of their world created this card game bearing their 12 likenesses that keeps their stories alive in the very moves of the game itself.

For 2-4 players, LoG looks like a 48-card deck of beautifully illustrated fantasy tarot cards, but plays like a fast-paced, surprisingly strategy-rich and engaging card game along the lines of rummy or bridge, but with a thematic fantasy twist in the moves. With deep lore and extremely well-balanced game play perfected over 10+ years of playtesting, Legends of Gadia is bound to be your new go-to filler game as it plays in 20 minutes, but is so fresh, addicting and fun that you and your friends will be reaching for this game again and again.

—description from the publisher

Once upon a time, two allied realms dwelled in peace. But between them stood an ever-changing labyrinth.
Every morning, each ruler would send two messengers to visit the other realm. It was paramount that these messengers reached their destination, for the wellbeing of all depended on it…

Mazing is a solo/two-player cooperative game. To win, you must make sure that the green Messengers reach the starting point of the pink Messengers, and vice versa. To do so, you’ll have to move the pieces on the board shrewdly, making sure to avoid creating too many roadblocks or dead-ends.

—description from the publisher

As day breaks, the men are already lined up in the trench. Some have shaky hands, but all grip their rifles tightly. On the other side of the parapet, the no man's land is deathly quiet. As the sun's rays reflect off the bayonets, the officers look nervously at their watches. But everyone is confident, because the pigeons that arrived the day before have brought good news: the nation, the generals and the headquarters are doing their best to support this new offensive!

Go over the top and renew your games with the Great War Commander Strategy Cards Expansion. Benefit from the efficiency of nine new Strategy Cards for each Nation (France, Germany, United States, United Kingdom and Commonwealth, Russia, Austria-Hungary) and dedicated rules that allow you to easily integrate them into your Great War Commander games.

—description from the publisher

The city of Jarren’s Outpost has once again fallen into disarray. Although no-one has been formally charged with the minor crime of regicide, the search for a new democratically elected leader has commenced. Unfortunately the people of Jarren’s Outpost aren’t quite sure what ‘democratically elected’ means so instead a week long competition has been set up to find the next King and/or Queen for this fine city. Surely the new ruler will know what to do about that dragon. And the blood cult. And the rampant wererat problem. And the orcs. And the taxes…


     For 2-5 players, inspired by the D&D is for Nerds podcast.



     Gain Prestige in a mission-based programming game with resource management and dice-based combat.



     Plan your perfect day using unique Movement Cards for each character while you compete for quests and resources.



     25 different locations, a variety of items, NPCs and quests, with every playable character having their own abilities, movements and playstyles, will ensure no two games are the same.


—description from the publisher

Dragon Eyes is a greedy, push-your-luck party game, in which players take on the role of immortal dwarves, respawning in town every time they die out on adventures.

Chasing riches and fame, the dwarves attempt to pillage more treasure from a nearby dragon’s cave than all the other dwarves, to see who is the bravest and smartest dwarf of them all! But they often get too greedy, and wake the dragon in their haste. Any dwarf that perishes in the dragon's flames wakes up in town again, but without any gold or glory.

During a round of Dragon Eyes, players take turns reaching into a bag of gems, choosing how many to take out without viewing them first. Drawn gems are pooled in the center of the table, and then the active player picks one color and moves all gems of this color from the center to their supply.

Beware though! Seven dragon eyes lurk in the bag. When drawing one or more dragon eyes, the active player must keep them, symbolizing how often they’ve been spotted, and after receiving too many, the dragon will awaken and gobble them up!

—description from the designer

Strategic tile laying with uniquely shaped leaves and set collection of forest animals
Upgraded FSC certified wooden leaf tiles and tokens
A deluxe box with a full piece of art for the box back
INCLUDES SEASON OF THE BEAR EXPANSION
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Trings is an abstract game played on an hexagonal grid, each player starting with three kinds of pieces : 4 rings, 4 pipes and 4 pins. The pin fits into the pipe which fits into the ring.

The aim of the game is to complete four "Towers" : a pin within a pipe within a ring, outside of the starting positions.

Rules
Red starts, then turns alternate.
In his turn, a player may move a piece to an adjacent empty intersection, or he may jump over another piece (combined or not) if the next intersection in the same direction is empty.
Multiple jumps are allowed, as well as changing the direction after each jump. Players may stop jumping at any point.
Passing is not allowed.
The player may end his move or jump by placing his piece "in" or "over" a piece of his own color. A pin can only be placed in a pipe, and a pipe in a ring; a ring can only be placed over a pipe and a pipe over a pin.

     Combining pieces in a starting position (marked with a dot) is not allowed.
     Combining a ring over a pin or a pin in a ring is not allowed.


When an intersection is occupied by 2 or 3 pieces, they can't be moved again.
The first player to make four Towers on non-dotted intersections win.

Extra
There are two board sizes : a small one for two players, and a large one for 3-4 players.
Red starts then other players alternate turns clockwise. Other rules are identical.

Discover the brand-new, totally insane expansion, Dimensions of Madness. Travel and unravel the secrets of the unstable multiverse. Meet the scriptweaving Librarian, who crafts custom adventures based on your literary genre choices. Prepare to rewrite your story or watch it spiral into utter horror!

—description from the publisher

Expedición Marítima is a deck of cards about the fauna of the Argentine Sea.

Navigate the Argentine sea, the islands of the South Atlantic and Antarctica, discovering different species of marine fauna, along with famous characters who ensured to protect them.

Knowing our nature we can take better care of it.

Dive into the charming world of beavers in this delightful puzzle strategy game for the whole family.

In Bivì, you become part of a beaver community dedicated to constructing the most efficient and stable dam along a serene stream. Surrounded by lush forests, your task is to transform nature's resources into engineering masterpieces.

The game revolves around placing colorful polyomino tiles to optimize space and achieve stability. Each turn introduces new challenges, testing your planning skills and creativity. Rules are easy to grasp, but mastering the strategy will keep you coming back for more.

Highlights:


     Charming and immersive theme: Beavers are brought to life through beautiful art and thematic mechanics. 
     Accessible strategy: Simple yet deep gameplay appeals to a wide audience. 
     High replay value: Variable layouts and objectives ensure no two games are the same.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Things in Rings: Board Game Promo Pack
description:
25 new Thing Cards and 9 new rule cards for your Rings

including cards like:
Wheat
Gavel
Miniature
Werewolf

and more

This expansion for the game Abstratus will challenge your building skills with amazing new words!

This time, all the words are titles of works of art, which will inspire you in the game to create new constructions!  
The four groups of cards bring a total of 144 names of works by 78 visual artists from Brazil, divided into 4 levels of difficulty.  
And, of course, we couldn’t leave out blank cards for you to fill in with titles of other works you know or even invent names for new works of art that don’t exist yet. How cool is that?

In addition to the new cards, the expansion includes brand-new pieces that join the set of original pieces, giving you even more chances to experiment with new builds.

This expansion invites you to navigate the intriguing waters of visual arts.  
Dive into this new creative adventure!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Lambada is a game of skill and planning.  Strategically play letter cards on top of the word Lambada, while avoiding the forbidden color.  Each turn demands planning as you navigate shifting colors and dodge penalties, while racing to try to empty your hand.

Juven Isle is a tile-laying (actually card-laying) game where you create a map of islands – building connections and deciding when to play your scoring tokens to maximize your score.

When you play a scoring token, you claim a port for one of the 6 factions, and score a point for each matching faction symbol that the new port can connect to. Or... you could play the scoring token as a pirate, which permanently disrupts the map and scores unclaimed ports.

When you score, you draw cards from the deck into your score pile. The total number of points available is limited by the cards remaining in the deck, so if you wait too long your opponents will have claimed them all.

—description from the designer

Players take turns adding colored acrylic tokens to a hexagonal board. The game is won by the player who ends the game on their turn by completing a line of four hexes with different colored tokens on them.

Strife and Valor is a duel format, strategy board game. In the game, you have to navigate a chess-like board in order to reach your opponent’s leader and defeat them using an asymmetric deck of cards, units and a leader. Take on the role of a mighty leader and defeat your opponent!

Highlights:

     Different domains, each ruled by a Leader with unique abilities and an asymmetric playstyle.
     Deck of 30 Cards of 5 different types: Attack, Defense, Spell, Reaction, and Event.
     Army consisting of 3 distinct types of Units: Melee, Magic, and Ranged, each with various subtypes and unique characteristics.
     Fast-paced - the average estimated time for a match is 40 minutes.
     Easy to get into but challenging to master.
     Rich in tactics and strategy.


—description from the designer

Farmer’s Market is a simple auction game, using the Vines and Postas deck, for 2 to 6 players.

Players take it in turns to remove all the cards of one suit from the Market and either keep them or send them to the Farmer's Hand.

The Market is replenished from the deck, adding one more card than was removed.

Once the deck is exhausted, cards in a player's hand score points depending on the number of cards of that rank in the Farmer's Hand.  Players lose points for cards in their hand that do not have a matching rank in the Farmer's Hand.

Highest score after six hands is the winner.

It can be learned in seconds for all ages. What you need is creativity, speed and, of course, humor at the same time. Everyone gets their own letter cards, which determine which initial letter you can give a one-word answer. The fastest player who gives a correct answer can put down the letter card what used for the answer. But it's important where you put it, because if you complete a shape, you'll get a bunny for it! If all 4 cards from your hand run out, you get another Bunny!

Collect 5 Bunnies and your team wins!

Be fast, creative and, of course, loud! It is often worth a lot, just like humor.

—description from the publisher

Escape Quest is a series of extraordinary book-games. They combine elements of a paragraph adventure with escape room tasks. Experience an incredible adventure in which you are the main character. You make decisions, solve tasks and try to get out of various traps set for you.

Superheroes exist. You hear about their deeds every day as they fight evil by helping the police and special services. You play as an ordinary person who has been unexpectedly invited to the Superhero Academy. But do you have undiscovered, supernatural abilities? Will you be brave enough to fully step into your role? With great power comes great responsibility.

The deck of cards is shuffled, with 4 cards dealt to each player and the remaining forming a face down draw deck. The cards are either labelled as credits (having positive value) or debits (having negative value).

Beginning with the player to the left of the dealer, players play one card from their hand with the objective to make the sum total of card values equal to 25. This forms a “book” which is kept by the player achieving this objective, with the player to the left then playing a card to start a new pile.

If a player cannot play a card without exceeding the value of 25, they must place a card in front of them “in reserve”, similar to Skip-Bo. Cards may be played from a player’s hand or from their reserve, drawing a card afterwards from the face down deck when playing from their hand (to maintain a hand size of 4).

After all cards have been played, each player counts the “books” they have made subtracting the number of cards in hand and in reserve from that amount. The winner is the player with the highest total.
Which kids knows most concerning Austria? To make it easier, for every question there are three answers to choose from.

Good things come in threes! The third expansion deck for Forest Shuffle offers three sets of cards to add to the base game:

First, there is an official Automa deck of 20 cards with 12 challenges to complete.

Next, a set of five brand new caves with individual powers to replace the regular caves of the base game.

Last but not least, this deck contains a selection of 15 special playing cards featuring selected and rare species from around the world. These cards have been available as promo cards in 2024.

With 55 cards in total, this set complements the flora and fauna of Forest Shuffle base game and offers endless possibilities for exploration and adventure.

—description from the publisher

Contains Promo cards: 
P001-Moor Birch
P002-Whinchat/Fire Salamander
P004-Palm Tree
P005-Gnat/Red Panda
P006-Robin/Water Vole
P007-Bison/White Stork
P008-Cardinal/Say's Firefly
P009-Genet/European Badger
P010-Cuckoo/Chanterelle
P011-Elk/Troll
P012-Mistletoe/Wild Tulip
P014-Turkey Oak
P015-O Christmas Tree
P016-Arctic Fox/Sable
P028-Brimstone/Marsh Cinquefoil
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Colorful Farm is a creative dice game in which players color funny farm animals. There is a lot to think about, as some herds of animals have to be colored in the same colors and others differently. Depending on how the dice fall and which pencil is chosen, good planning is required.  
 Roll the dice, choose a die and a colored pencil. Color in one of the items as shown on your chosen die, be it a cow, sheep, chicken, horse, fruit basket or haystack. The first person to color in 5 groups of animals correctly wins the game.

The funny farm animals and easy game play turn the roll & write mechanic into a roll & draw—something entertaining for young and old

—description from the publisher

A quick party game about Sharks and puns. Go fishing for Sharkenegger, Sharkira and many others!

In your turn you


     Fish for Sharks: draw a random card from a player 
     Pair the Sharks: if you have a pair of the same Shark type, score them.
     Special Effects: reveal a Shark from your hand and apply its effect. Watch out for the Shaaaaaaaaaa! card, because you'll have to quickly reach out for the squeaky Shark!


If you end your turn with only the Sharks Party in your hand, the game is over you compare your score with other players.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: G-Generation SD Gundam Digiboard Mission Official Strategy Guide
description:
This is an official strategy guide for the G-Generation SD Gundam Digiboard Mission game.

This book contains rules, special plans, data, and strategy for the Digiboard Mission game.

Facing up to three opponents, draw your cards and use the elements of Leaf, Ember, and Dew in battle to form 1 of 6 patterns (of varying difficulty).

Choose or roll the dice and let fate decide a pattern to achieve. After that, Draw your hand of cards and start taking command of the board spaces.

May it be a stronger element or higher number of the same element, overtake your opponent (or yourself if needed) and claim the space for your or block your opponents from making the next best move.

Play smart and be the first to achieve the pattern to win in this game that's easy to learn, but hard not to have fun.

—description from the designer
The Feasts & Fury expansion adds 13 new cards, introducing fresh abilities, greater replayability, and enhanced strategy while working within the same core mechanics of the base game.

In recent years, a handful of billionaires and political powers are facing off in a new conquest of space. In an interview with Elon Musk, he wanted to embark Space X on a trip to Mars...

Using one of 9 characters, quickly and carefully build your mini-dice rocket one die at a time, ensuring you have entered the correct launch codes to have a successful launch! First stop is the Moon. 
Once your moon bases are established, build a new rocket again, enter the new launch codes and be the first to launch towards the ultimate destination - Mars. A beautiful destination to die.

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Spearhead – Fire & Jade Gaming Pack
description:
Spearhead is an exciting way for both new and experienced players to enjoy Warhammer Age of Sigmar. In this game mode, you'll fight small-scale battles that are fast, tactical, and different every time.

This pack contains the cards, terrain, and accessories for playing Spearhead games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar. The gameboard is 30”x22.4” – the specified size for Spearhead games – and features pre-printed objective markers for your armies to fight over. The four pieces of terrain are ideal for adding extra dimensions to your games, creating opportunities for cover and ambush. Also included are themed twist cards that ensure games are close right until the final dice roll, and battle tactics cards, which dictate how to score victory points and offer powerful one-off effects.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Be the first to build a structure of 7 blocks following the directions on the cards you draw. Young children learn and practice basic prepositions and colors while the whole family can play a fun quick paced building game!
Be the first Player or team to get four stones in a row on the grid, first get your mug, then switch off between taking a drink of your brew, and eating your snacks.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Cosmic Ghost Rider
description:
“Human or Alien, Evil Must be Punished.”

In another timeline, the Punisher’s life ended abruptly as so many do: at the hands of Thanos. Desperate for a chance at revenge, the deceased hero entered a deal with Mephisto to return with a vengeance as the latest Ghost Rider. But the devil never plays fair, and by the time his transformation was complete, Earth was dead. And Thanos was gone.

Driven mad by grief and loneliness, the man once called Frank Castle jumped at any chance for redemption, revenge, or even someone to talk to. Encounters with Galactus, Odin, and Thanos himself have finally led the rider home: back to Earth-616, with a new lease on life and more power than any sane man knows what to do with.

—description from the publisher

The stage is set on the distant planet "Virtus." This planet is home to four distinct species: "Teo", "Bitter", "Plu", and "Mick". Each species lives by making the most of their unique abilities. In recent years, the most flourishing industry on this planet has been "Space Business". With countless planets and satellites in this planetary system, there is a growing movement to efficiently utilize the energy and resources available from each celestial body.

You are the ambitious CEO of a space exploration venture. You will acquire numerous planets and build the strongest team to work with. At times, you will receive support from major corporations, while competing against other venture companies to grow your business. Your aim to become the No. 1 space exploration venture company!

Astro Out is a medium-weight board game themed around space exploration. Players take on the role of CEOs of venture companies engaged in the space business, traveling across planets, constructing buildings, and gathering allies.

Each round consists of 4 phases: Action, Income, Settlement, Cleanup. The game ends after the Settlement phase during the 4th round and points are counted. The player with the highest point total from all the categories is the winner and is recognized as the most outstanding CEO.

—description from the publisher

Few things are simple on Necromunda. Gangers thought lost in battle sometimes resurface later, seemingly back from the dead. Mysterious figures rise to plague an area, taunting the local gangs and working towards their own agenda. Settlements grow, falter, or change as the tides of fate shift. One thing is certain in the underhive – there’s never a dull moment.

This campaign book offers a collection of rules intended to enhance your campaigns, including new options for adding a nemesis to your games, dynamic living settings, and even bringing back dead gangers. You’ll also find updated and collated rules from classic publications, to help you add new elements and fun options to your ongoing campaigns.

This 128-page hardback supplement contains:

– Underhive Nemeses: Rules for single individuals who help shape the events of a campaign as foils for your gangs, fleshed out with their own unique abilities.


     Living Settings: A guide to creating organic, evolving settlements to set your campaigns in, with variable population, commerce, and environments. Includes six example settings.



     Resurrection Packages: 10 ways to bring fallen fighters back from the dead… for a price.



     Seven scenarios: Including four Arbitrator Scenarios and three Gang Raid plots.



     Uprising Campaigns: A complete narrative campaign based around the breakdown of Imperial law and the rise of recidivist elements, including six scenarios.



     Outlaw Brutes: Four new horrors and abominations to test your gangs... or bolster them.



     House Gang Vehicle Rules: New vehicle options for Van Saar, Escher, Goliath, Orlock, Cawdor, and Delaque gangs, including rules for Delaque Vehicle Gang Tactics.


This is an expansion to Necromunda – you'll need a copy of the Necromunda: Core Rulebook, and Necromunda: Book of the Outlands, both available separately, to use all the contents of this book.

-from the Publisher

Foster and rehome cute dogs, cats and exotic pets in Fosteringo, where Pet Rescues use dice intuition tactics in a cozy, light strategy bingo variant.
Players compete for rescue points by fostering, rehoming or transferring overpopulated pet habitats.  Use rescue cards to predict shelter rolls and try to avoid pet perils as you aim for bingo bonuses on your Fosteringo board.
Simultaneous action continues until two active bingos trigger end game scoring.
A special creature pack that presents a new Mystling to the game - Luminesca!

In this expansion, you will find 6 types of I.C.E. Breakers, each present in 2 miniatures, for a total of 12 great bicolor miniatures. These programs will help the Avatars during their raids within the Gene.sys., in particular, they will be of great help in countering and eliminating the threats of I.C.E.'s.

Fairy tale, thriller or love story? Card by card, you invent a wild story together until someone drops out and suddenly turns against you. Then it's time to get your memory up to speed and correctly reproduce the story motifs before time runs out! Filmriss - the crazy card game that throws family and friends into chaos.

—description from the publisher

Grab your friends and family and dive into the world of Voll Verschätzt! - the craziest guessing game of all time. 350 weird and funny questions from all subject areas are waiting for you, which will make even the best guessers sweat! No matter whether you are a walking encyclopedia or rather a know-nothing: Fun is what counts here. Knowledge is a minor matter.

—description from the publisher

Grab your friends and family and discover the junior world of Voll Verschätzt! - the craziest guessing game of all time. Here you can expect 350 colorful questions from all subject areas that will make even the best guessers into a sweat! No matter whether you are a walking encyclopaedia or rather the know-nothing: fun is what counts here - knowledge is secondary matter.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Grab your friends and family and dive into the world of Voll Verschätzt! - the craziest guessing game of all time. Here 350 spicy questions from all subject areas that will make even the best guessers into a sweat! No matter whether you are a walking encyclopaedia or a know-nothing: fun is what counts here - knowledge is secondary matter!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Adult Versions of Family-Friendly Games

description:
Put your Disney knowledge to the test with our exciting Guess the Film game! Roll the dice to select which window to open and reveal part of an image from an iconic Disney film. Be the first player to get the most correct answers and win the game!

Magic in the details

Includes: Guess the Film stand with decorative frame, 50 sheets with 100 Disney film images, 1 dice and 20 Mickey tokens
3 levels of difficulty: Green - Easy, Blue - Medium and Red - Advanced
Roll the dice and open a window of the same colour as shown on the dice
Guess the film that's revealed by the window
If you guessed the film correctly, you earn a Mickey token
The first player to earn 4 Mickey tokens wins the game

A tactical card game where memory and thinking are key to channelling your inner goose and honking your way to victory.

Players aim to get the lowest score in their individual card grids by swapping and shedding cards or activating special abilities to either lower their own grid score or confuse and increase their opponents. Players take turns to take from the play pile or discard pile and swap a card into their own grid. Alternatively they can activate a Goose card's special ability which can either aid a players own endevours or hinder your opponents. If a card in any grid matches the top card of the discard pile, you can slam it to remove it from the playing field, and if it were an opponents grid you slammed, you can give them one of your own cards. If you think you have the lowest score then HONK to end the round. Be careful though, if another sneaky player has a lower score this round you get penalty points. Play as many round as you want, the more players the more mayhem!

Perfect for occasion, any age and any location if you want a quick, fun and frantic game.

Collect ships and captains to build your fleets. Build up your stash of treasure to bribe your foes. Play actions cards to deceive, loot and steal. Use your fleets to plunder you enemies. Complete 3 full fleets to win the game.

Build Yer Fleets
Each player works to amass three full fleets of ships. To do this, players must draw and play cards, adding ships to their armada while defending against other pirates' schemes. Successfully completing and protecting these fleets from the treacherous actions of opponents is key to becoming the most notorious pirate on the high seas. Plan your moves wisely, and let the quest for maritime supremacy begin!

HOW TO PLAY
To claim victory, gather three full Fleets of Ships before any other player.

Build Yer Fleets
Each player works to amass three full fleets of ships. To do this, players must draw and play cards, adding ships to their armada while defending against other pirates' schemes. Successfully completing and protecting these fleets from the treacherous actions of opponents is key to becoming the most notorious pirate on the high seas. Plan your moves wisely, and let the quest for maritime supremacy begin!

Plunder Yer Enemies
The action heats up with a variety of Plunder cards that add strategy to the game. These cards allow you to steal treasure from other players. If you have either of the fleets depicted on a Plunder card, you can execute a plunder action, stealing the corresponding amount from your opponent based on the number of cards you have of that fleet. A Supply Ship adds 150 to the plunder amount, while a Warship adds 200. Using these cards effectively can turn the tide of the game in your favour, making them essential for any pirate seeking dominance on the high seas.

Stash Yer Treasure
Stashing treasure is critical for any aspiring pirate Lord. Your treasure stash serves as both a shield and a sword, protecting your assets from rival pirates' devious schemes while providing the means to pay off debts and fund your exploits. Carefully managing your treasure withstand raids, plunders, and trades without losing your hard-earned ships. Remember, in the race for maritime supremacy, a pirate's fortune is only as secure as their treasure.

Take Command
Powerful Action cards that offer strategic advantages and defensive manoeuvres. Cards like "Parlay" players to negate enemy actions, protecting their assets from rival pirates' schemes. Cards like "Commandeer" and "Overthrow" let players take command of their ships or entire fleets, their armada and weakening an opponent's. Mastering the use of Action cards is crucial for not only defending your own fleets and treasure but also for asserting your power and securing ultimate pirate glory.

—description from the publisher

This expansion allows you to add expansion cards into your standard deck. Represented by 50 cards which includes 20 new cards. This expansion will challenge you more with exciting new game play.

Includes 10 trap cards
10 fake trap cards
28 life tokens
30 action and quick cards.

Have fun and challenge your chums as you match Heroes cards to make surprising connections. A game of sharp observation, quick thinking and cunning!

Match the symbols on the cards of famous historical people and be the first to get rid of all your cards.

Help Miffy get home as quick as she can. She can travel in many different ways: by boat, plane, bicycle or scooter.

The youngest player starts. Each player takes turns to roll the dice. The colours thrown determine which vehicles move ahead. For example, if green and yellow are thrown, then Miffy on the Bicycle (green background) and Miffy in the car (yellow background)with move forward one step.

The winner is the first player who can take Miffy home to the field of flowers regardless of vehicles.

Loyalty TCG is inspired by nostalgia for Triple Triad, this game combines an easy-to-learn format with strategic game play.
Its beautifully designed full-art cards take players on a journey through the steampunk city and surrounding lands of Ravaan, a world like no other.

What to play, how to play your cards, when to play them and where. This is what makes the game incredibly fun and gives it limitless replay value.

In Loyalty TCG, cards are played on a 4x4 game board, where players claim cards by turning them, marking ownership, and creating Loyalty. The game features unique rules, such as equal card rarity strength and the absence of filler cards, ensuring fairness and lasting enjoyment. It removes obstacles like high costs and pay-to-win mechanics, rather focusing instead on balanced game-play and replay value.

Designed with the community in mind, Loyalty TCG emphasizes accessible organized play, limited annual releases, and strategic deck-building, appealing to both competitive players and casual enthusiasts. Loyalty TCG was created out of dissatisfaction with traditional trading card games (TCGs) and the desire for something new. As a former collection buyer, we collected years worth of feedback from players disengaged with their hobbies, so we combined all of the good bits that had drawn them in and omitted the bad bits that caused them to lose passion.

Experience Loyalty TCG yourself and tell us if we tick the boxes!

—description from the publisher

Prepare for some prehistoric fun with the Jurassic World Path Game.

Kids will love exploring a dangerous world filled with dinosaurs when they play this exciting game. To start, players need to spin the spinner to stampede across the gameboard. They'll need to collect tokens as they go!

Careful, though. There are lots of dinosaurs that could ruin the fun. The player with the most tokens at the end of the path wins the game.

Hive Secundus, once the shining pinnacle of Necromunda, is now a haunted, alien-infested hell.

A bastion of education and enlightenment, Hive Secundus was where Necromunda’s greatest minds were encouraged to prosper... until the Inquisition discovered the insidious truth lurking at the heart of Necromunda’s second city. The cancer was so deep-seated that Hive Secundus was razed to the ground, and the ruins declared quarantine extremis for more than a century. But, even the most diabolical weaponry in the Imperial arsenal couldn’t destroy the horrors below the Jewel of Necromunda... or the lost treasures that draw gangs into its grasp.

It is time to leave Hive Primus as Necromunda: Book of Desolation introduces gangs to the perilous landscape of the abandoned hive. They will clash in search of priceless, ancient archeotech, all the while hunted by xenos horrors and the corrupted servants of the Malstrain.

Inside this 144-page supplement, you will find:
– Beyond the Dust Wall: Details about the terrifying Malstrain threat that lives in the Secundan abyss and Lady Haera's journey into the depths of the Underhells.
– New Gangs: Rules for fielding Spyre Hunting Parties and Malstrain gangs, as well as modifying gangs to be Secundan Incursion gangs or Malstrain Corrupted gangs.
– Desolation Campaign: A complete campaign for all Necromunda gangs in the Secundan Exclusion Zone.
– Scenarios: Six new scenarios for the Desolation Campaign, including rules for using Roaming Horrors.
– Ferryman Teams: Rules for including these guides to the Secundan Exclusion Zone in your Necromunda campaigns.
– Vehicle Rules: A new Custom Rig profile and new vehicle Upgrades and Wargear.
– Gang Tactics: Two new sets of gang tactics for players fighting in the Underhells of Hive Secundus and Spyrer Hunting Party gangs.

This is an expansion to Necromunda – you'll need a copy of the Necromunda Core Rulebook, available separately, to use the contents of this book.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Winter Offensive Bonus Pack #16: ASL Scenario Pack for Winter Offensive 2025
description:
2025 edition of the Winter Offensive Bonus Pack series for Advanced Squad Leader.

Bonus Pack #16 features three new scenario designs by Andy Goldin and a new board (93).

—description from the publisher

Are you a source of information, useless or otherwise? Put your knowledge to the test as you battle it out with friends and family!

Complete with 480 challenging questions on the arts, history, TV & film, food, celebrities and general knowledge, see who will be the fastest to buzz in with the right answers in your quest to find the ultimate brainiac!

—description from the publisher

My Word comes with a board portraying a horse race track, with five circled stations labeled 3, 4, 5, 6, and 7-Finish. There is a metal horse token for each of the four lanes. The card deck has 67 letter cards, 2 jokers, 2 picture cards and 1 number card.

The main game has each player start with their token at the start and a 2-card hand. During each turn, they draw a card, and try to spell either one word or two intersecting words using as many letters as their next circle. For example, to get to the first circle (3), they could spell a 3-letter word or two 2-letter words that share a letter (e.g. IF and IT). If they succeed at either one they advance their token to the next circle; if they spell one word of that length they can take another turn.

Words can be re-used in later turns, and plurals with S are acceptable. Circles on the track cannot be skipped. Picture and number cards have instructions for spelling a word (e.g. "Spell any number from one to ten") which allow advancement to the next circle. Jokers can stand for any letter, but must be discarded at the end of the turn.

The first player to reach Finish gets 10 points; second place gets 5. The player with the highest score after and agreed-on number of races is the winner.

There are also rules for two other games:

Lexigrams -- Very similar to Kan-U-Go or Crossword Lexicon: players use turns to add words to a growing shared crossword. When one player plays their last card, the others add the points on their remaining cards to their total. Once one player has a total of 100 or more, the player with the lowest score wins the game.

Word Solitaire -- Eight cards are dealt out, each being the start of a column. The player repeatedly turns up the next card of the deck and adds it to a column so as to spell a word fragment or a complete word. Once a word is complete it can either be extended, or the last letter can be considered the first letter of another word.  If a letter cannot be added to make a valid word or word fragment in any column, the player must lay it face down at the end of one of the columns, closing that column for the rest of the game. Once all columns are closed, the number of face-up cards played is the player's score.

—user summary

If you're a general knowledge pro or have friends or family members who (think they!) know it all, then it's time to put everyone's IQ to the test with OMG! Trivia Game. As the must-have gift for clever clogs, the ultimate addition to your games cupboard and fantastic family entertainment for ages eight upwards, the fun, fast-paced and quick-thinking OMG! Trivia Game features over 2500 trivia facts... but only half of them are true! Bet your IQ to identify fact or fiction but if you get it wrong, you lose all your IQ and you have just one chance to win it back... then it's game over. Players are challenged with a fact read out by the Question Master such as 'The earth is gradually slowing down', 'Male clownfish can turn into females' and 'Crows fly in straight lines'. They then need to use their IQ answer tokens and decide if the fact is real or fake. If you think it's fake slam down your IQ answer token with the red cross side facing up but if you're convinced it's fact, slam down the IQ answer token showing the green tick. The aim of the game is to hold onto your IQ answer tokens as the players with the highest IQ wins the game however help is at hand if your IQ starts to drop. You can then use your Brain Donor card or Brain Transplant card to collect some more IQ from the Question Master. If things get bad you're pronounced Brain Dead, which means you have zero IQ, but all is not lost as you get one more chance to claw your way back into the game and do your brain proud.

ASL Journal 15 is a 36-page magazine for Advanced Squad Leader and features articles, the latest errata and scenario updates in the Debriefing, 11 new scenarios printed on card stock, and three overlay sheets (to replace the versions with no center dots that came in DOOMED BATTALIONS 4th edition).

ASL Journal 15 articles include:

ASL Hall Of Fame Report by MMP about first six inductees.
