boardgame: Baseball Highlights: 2045 – Starter Team 4 New York
description: Fifteen new cards for Baseball Highlights: 2045: Starter Team 4 New York (V4) - Veteran Mix

Kids can now set up this fun game by gently opening the shark’s jaw and placing all the items in his mouth. Players take turns rolling the dice and fishing the corresponding items from his mouth. Be careful! If the shark bites, you’ll be eliminated. Play continues until just one winner remains.

Spin the spinner, draw a card and perform the action required. The better the performance, the more chance the other players will guess what you are doing! If they do, you get to keep the card. First player to get 10 cards, with at least 1 from each category, wins the game.

This is a general knowledge game with a completely different approach than what you've seen before.

In Who Said That, you'll come across more than 350 iconic phrases from around the world and have to guess the author of each one - and laugh trying to remember those things that have stuck in your memory.

There are movie lines that you swore you knew, celebrity catchphrases that have stuck in your head, phrases that you learned in school (or at least should have learned), songs that were blasting on the radio and even commercial slogans that you can't forget, no matter how hard you try. Sounds easy? Just wait until the card turns over.

Whatever your repertoire, each card will definitely have something for you.

But wait! This game has a twist that makes everything simpler and funnier.
Whoever gets the answer wrong gets a special card to mess with everyone. There's a card to ask a friend for help (even if they don't want it!) and there's even a card to steal someone else's answer!

Oh, and Who Said That? is 100% Brazilian: songs, celebrities, advertisements, TV shows. An authentic Brazilian juice.

This game is perfect for playing with friends and even better for playing with the family and testing the knowledge of each generation.

So, are you ready to find out if you really know who said that?

Advisors are here to help speed up construction, recruit troops faster, or thwart other players.

Players will also be able to go on Quests that provide amazing rewards such as end game score for completing specific tasks or provide in game benefits.

—description from the publisher

A perfect game for groups of friends and families who love music and fun. Sing along without words and make your team guess the name of the song!

     Easy to learn, light and accessible for all ages. Ideal for livening up any gathering.
     Quick and hilarious games that guarantee good laughs and unforgettable moments.
     Well-known and nostalgic songs that everyone will recognize (or at least try!).


The game that turns your musical memory into fun!
In LaLaLa, it doesn't matter if you're in tune or not – the fun is in the trying! You can only use "lalala" to hum and help your team guess the name of the song. The tension of trying to remember the right melody, mixed with the laughter of those listening, makes this game a unique experience full of memorable moments.

Fun that fits anywhere
Light, compact and perfect to take to any gathering, LALALA adapts to any environment: parties, family gatherings or that relaxed night with friends. With simple rules and fast dynamics, the game brings together people of all ages and musical tastes around something that everyone loves: music.

Nostalgic moments full of laughter
The songs chosen in the game are those that have marked generations, from hits that stuck in our memories to classics that never go out of style. Get ready to sing songs that you didn't even remember you knew and to have fun watching others try to guess (or fail miserably!). It's guaranteed fun, round after round.

boardgame: Baby Shark's Big Show: Wooden Balancing Game
description:
Create the jigsaw and then place the stacking stand on top, then you can start to stack all the characters with the Baby Shark Big Show Wooden Stacking Game
Kids and adults will love trying their best to stack as many of their favourite characters as possible without falling!
Includes: 1 Jigsaw Board, 1 Wooden Stacking Stand, 8 Wooden Characters.

boardgame: Peppa Pig Grandpa Pig: Balancing Boat
description:
This is a rare and exciting game for young children! Grandpa Pig's Wooden Balancing Boat from Golden Bear Toys is a perfect addition to any Peppa Pig fan's toy collection. The game is suitable for children aged 3 and above, and provides hours of fun and imaginative play.

Take turns to balance Peppa Pig and her friends on Grandpa Pigs wooden rocking Boat.  Take it in turns to place the coloured shapes on the rocking boat, choose the wrong piece and they all fall off.  The player to not topple the pieces over, wins!

This toy is a pre-school/ young children's toy and is perfect for enhancing your child's cognitive and hand-eye co-ordination skills and is sure to bring a smile to their face.

Includes wooden boat and 18 balancing pieces.

Get ready to answer a variety of fun questions:

Who in the couple pays more attention to the expiration date of their food?

Who in the couple would be the best TV presenter?

Who in the couple believed in Santa Claus until late?

You and your partner have to agree on the answer to score points.

Will your knowledge be enough to beat the other couples playing?

"Who in the Couple?" is the perfect game to take to a date with 2 to 4 couples. Each couple will team up in a pair to play against the others and prove that they really understand each other.

Win or lose, laughter and fun are guaranteed in this short festive game for couples.

Each has its own exclusive rules and scenarios booklets, army and city display cards, and other displays relevant to those titles. 
The exclusive rules have been extensively revised based on player feedback.

description:
Coming In Noodle Twist:

Personal Ships: Featuring a new player board that represents your ship. Optimize it to obtain useful effects to face new threats and challenges, like the Red Dragons!

New Character Cards: Increase your deckbuilding options with new character cards to shuffle into the main deck.

New Location Cards: Add new locations to give more variability over multiple plays.

Immersive Fugitives: New fugitives feature more immersive effects, adding more depth to the gameplay.

Noodles: Collect this new resource in order to feed your crew and power up your plays!

Match Tac Toe fuses Tic-Tac-Toe with Memory Matching, creating a light abstract strategy experience. The objective is to secure three squares in a row (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally) on a shared board by matching corresponding pairs of tiles.

Players flip over tiles to find matching pairs. Correct matches allow the player to claim the corresponding square on the board. When no tiles remain and the game is not won, tiles are reshuffled back to the matching area and all squares become contestable again.

Once a square is claimed, it remains under the player's control until reclaimed in a tie breaking round. after all tiles are reshuffled to the matching area following a "Cat’s Game" or tie.

Marralhinha is a traditional game from the Azores Islands that is played with dice and marbles, much like the games Aggravation, Fast Track, and Wahoo.

This adaptation of the commonly known game of Aggravation/Fast Track/Wahoo is called Marralhinha which is widely played in the Azores Islands - Portugal. This version uses 5 marbles instead of 4 marbles and the board itself is cross-shaped and made out of 2 pieces of wood. It's a game for 4 players (one in each end of the cross) but recently, due to its popularity as a pub/café game, a new version for 6 people was invented.

Its origin is not entirely known but older people in the Azores say they remember a cardboard game American soldiers used to have and play in the 60's. Then locals tried to copy and adapt it with wood and marbles, resulting in a new game with different rules.

Families and enthusiasts of Marralhinha will gather around the dining table or at the village community center to play or compete with several teams.

The player rolls a die, moves their token along a relatively free path, and collects cards containing various types of information until they feel confident that, with three of the collected elements, they can identify—among films historically released on the market—one whose title they know. At this point, they make their declaration and begin to claim seats in a movie theater. The first player to take control of two of the three available theaters wins.
It’s a challenge for true cinema lovers, but even more fun is the competition between groups, which can bring together up to 18 people around a table.

boardgame: Ungentlemanly Warriors: A Rogue Warriors Solo Campaign
description:
Dive into the heart of World War 2 clandestine operations with Ungentlemanly Warriors, the action-packed narrative campaign expansion for Rogue Warriors. This new adventure unfolds across Europe's war-torn landscape, offering a series of gripping fictional missions inspired by the SOE's covert operations that shaped the war's outcome.

As darkness envelopes Europe, a special forces team of elite Warriors emerges to challenge the Axis's might, engaging in operations that blur the lines of conventional warfare. Ungentlemanly Warriors invites players to step into the boots of these daring operatives known simply as 'The Gang' or take command of the Axis special forces, The Schwarzschild, led by the sinister General Friedrich von Luger.

This expansion deepens the core rules found in Rogue Warrior and introduces Solo and Co-op Rules that pit you against the cunning AI of the game. Choose your path: the tense and strategic PvP experience, where minds battle minds across the war-torn landscapes of Europe, or go it alone, orchestrating daring raids, sabotage missions, and covert operations against a relentless AI adversary. Included are:


     Solo/Co-op rules that can be applied to other Rogue Warrior games 
     Sentry Cards & Patrol Paths 
     AI Charts to control the Enemy Warriors 
     6 new Solo/Co-op Missions 
     Templates 


Every mission within Ungentlemanly Warriors will put your Warriors to the test, with victory or defeat carrying weight into the next encounter. Triumph brings bonuses and advantages, weaving each outcome into an evolving story of resistance and aggression. From the dimly lit streets of a French town to the secretive compounds hidden high in the Alps, each setting is a homage to the era, blending historical inspiration with fiction.

Whether you're orchestrating a daring rescue in the heart of the occupied territory, sabotaging critical supply lines in the dense forests, or engaging in a decisive confrontation on an island garrison, Ungentlemanly Warriors offers a deep, strategic experience that honors the spirit of those who fought from the shadows.

Prepare for a campaign of cunning, bravery, and ungentlemanly acts of warfare. Will you undermine the Axis's plans and pave the way for Allied victory, or will you be swallowed by the rising tide of the Enemy determined to strengthen their hold over Europe? The fate of the war rests in your hands. Let's see what you're made of!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tour of Duty: A Rogue Warriors Jungle Campaign
description:
Players are transported back to the humid and hostile jungles of Vietnam during the tumultuous era of the Vietnam War. This gripping expansion offers a series of six intricately linked missions that cast players as members of a Free World special forces team or a larger and more formidable Communist force.

In Tour of Duty, each mission is more than just a skirmish; it’s a story of survival, strategy, and bravery against overwhelming odds. Players will embark on daring operations to rescue downed pilots from enemy territory, liberate POWs from heavily guarded camps, and sabotage Communist convoys disrupting crucial supply lines. The narrative further intensifies as the Team conducts sweeping searches through suspicious villages, secures vital landing zones, and navigates the treacherous terrain that only a jungle can offer.

The expansion introduces new game mechanics that delve deep into the asymmetrical warfare typical of the Vietnam War. Players will find themselves outnumbered but not outsmarted, employing guerrilla tactics, setting up ambushes, and using the environment to their advantage. The new rules build on the core Rogue Warriors gameplay but introduce innovative elements such as:


     Reinforcements & Reserves 
     Tunnels and Spawn Points 
     Booby Traps 
     Air and artillery Support 


The expansion also brings the era's unique military aspects to life through new Specializations and weapons, with Players equipping their squads with period-specific firearms and gear, including M16 rifles and AK-47s. Tour of Duty is designed for players who crave tactical complexity and historical immersion. It challenges them to think like commanders of the time, adapting to rapidly changing battlefield conditions and making split-second decisions that could mean the difference between a triumphant victory and a crushing defeat.

As players navigate through the campaign, they will experience the harrowing reality of jungle warfare, where visibility is low, the terrain is unforgiving, and the enemy could be hiding just feet away. The dense foliage may conceal not only the Communist soldiers but also the resolve and resourcefulness of the Free World forces determined to disrupt their plans at every turn.

Prepare your gear, brief your Team, and embark on a tour of duty that promises to test the limits of your strategic skills and courage under fire. Welcome to the jungle, Commander. Let the campaign begin!

—description from the publisher

Twittens is an old Sussex word to describe the little alleyways and lanes that run between houses and fields. They thread their way through towns and suburbs like veins, walled by red bricks, garden fencing and rusted chicken wire. Elsewhere they're known as ginnels, snicks and vennels.

Twittens is a tile laying game set in an everyday English suburb. Work your way through the seasons exploring your local twittens, discovering their houses, wildlife, allotments, playgrounds, railways and electrical substations. Complete your wanderings and get home in time for tea.

—description from the publisher

Part bird, part harvest...

From the distant lands of who knows where come these strange feathered vegetables.

Now you too can have a flying carrot as a companion.

Drawn and designed by my nephew Tommy Williams, The Rootwings is a tiny expansion that was given away to everyone who pre ordered the 2nd edition of Paupers' Ladder.

—description from the designer

The player who puts together the highest-scoring setup by the end wins.

description:
The board game that spices up your pizza debates!

Pizz'Arène is a flick game for 2 to 6 pizzaiolos. During a meal with friends, a heated debate breaks out between two groups: for or against pineapple and olives on pizza?

In the game Pizz'Arène, place as many of your favorite ingredients as possible close to the center of the pizza while preventing your opponent from doing the same.
Only ingredients touching the surface of the pizza (including the edges) count.

Each ingredient from the same team counts as one point if no opposing ingredient is closer to the center (similar to pétanque).

A Pizz'Arène match is played over multiple rounds. The first team to reach 6 points wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Yell Spell has a deck of 100 cards -- 50 consonants, 42 vowels, 4 wilds, and 4 shuffle cards -- 7 start cards, and a bell. To start, the bell is set out and the start cards placed around it, and the deck is dealt to all players.  Going around the circle, each player quickly places their top card face up on a start card. If any player sees a word of 3+ consecutive letters, they ring the bell, say the word, and collect the cards that spell it. A wild card can be any letter, and shuffle card lets players make words from any letters, not just consecutive letters. After all the cards are placed and no one sees any more words, the player with the most cards won wins.

Clichés criminels is an investigative game based on photos and documents. You're a private investigator helping the French police on difficult cases. Try to solve the enigma by looking crime scene photo and documents and give your conclusions via the application, which reveals nothing (you can go back to the investigation!) and leaves you immersed. you just have to answer police chef questions or come back to the photos and documents if you want . After the last questions , police chief evaluate your theory and you can read the newspapers article published after the case was solved to see if your theory was right.
An inclusive and very simple investigative game, but for adult only cuz it's very realistic!

Aliens Mini-Expansion: It's ET! The module adds a new tile type, tokens, and a new Exploration board. If you include the base game, the How I Wonder Expansion, and the Aliens Mini-Expansion, you have access to 14,784 setups!

Sprites & Sprouts is a magical farming game for 1-5 players designed by Manny Vega (Flamecraft). Players take on the role of Sprites, helping the Farmer tend and grow their sprouts!

Over a series of rounds, guided by the Farmer's journey across the seasons, players take turns during the Sunrise phase, strategically placing their Sprite workers to Plant, Tend, Prep, or unleash Magic. Each action allows you to manipulate the vibrant Sproutling tiles on the 8x8 game board, aiming to complete Crop Challenge cards for points and powerful skills.  But beware! During the Sunset phase, the Harvest arrives, removing sections of the board and challenging your carefully laid plans.  Anticipate the Farmer's next move, adapt to unpredictable weather and events, and maximize your harvest to emerge as the most successful Sprite!

Threshold is a massive open-world role-playing game that blends tactical card-based combat, deep character customization, endless loot and a living world that responds to your choices.

Explore vibrant regions, complete quests solo or cooperatively, harvest and craft powerful gear, brave dungeons and raids, or test your skill in competitive PvP.

Welcome to the world of Terratory!

Lead your animals to victory: build habitats, play animals, and use Power Moves to outsmart opponents — with the Lustrous Leaves and Roaring Waves starter decks.

The game is lane-based and highly strategic, which means that every micro-decision you make will influence your chances of winning the game!

Both starter decks provide a unique set of 54 cards. The Lustrous Leaves set focuses on Jungle, Grassland, and Mountain habitats, whereas the Roaring Waves deck includes Savanna, Sky, and Ocean habitats.

—description from the publisher

The expansion to the critically acclaimed solo game Dustrunner! Along your journey across the wasteland, you will now have the opportunity to stop and trade at settlements for needed supplies. A new radiation mechanic has been added, creating another level of tension and strategy.

Expansion includes 50 New cards and 2 minis:

12 Exploration Cards, bringing many new locations and mutant enemies to deal with.
3 double-sided Vehicle Cards, which increase the total number of possible combination of builds up to over 200!
5 double-sided Map Cards. One side is standard difficulty, while the other is hardcore mode!
6 Settlement Cards
2 Dustrunner minis that you can use to track your position on the map cards and the position of the pesky raiders!
New vehicle modification cards have been added and well as new some new resolution cards.
RADIATION!!!

— description from the publisher.

IntroductionHausta (from Old Norse: haust, meaning "harvest" or "autumn") is a drawless finite game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 4 cells per side, but boards of 3 or 5 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stackable pieces of their own color in two types: peasants and seeds.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A peasant is a set of pieces located on the same cell. A single piece is also a peasant.
     To harvest a seed is to incorporate it into a peasant, so that the seed stops being a seed and becomes part of the peasant.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Move your peasant in a straight line to a distance equal to its height to an empty cell or a cell occupied by a friendly seed to harvest it. When moving, the peasant increases its height by 1 and uses an extra piece off the board to grow; however, if the peasant harvests a seed, it uses it to grow.
     Sow a friendly seed on an empty cell in a straight line from your peasant to an empty cell at a distance equal to your peasant's height, then remove one piece of your peasant, unless the peasant is 1 of height.


You can move your peasant or sow seeds through pieces of any color.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when a peasant cannot move or sow seeds, in which case the opponent is the winner.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places two peasants of height 1, one black and one white, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the two-piece pie rule.

—description from the designer

Progressive Logomachy is an extension of Logomachy to include 16 players at 4 tables, and multiple games played with constant switching of tables and partners.  The contents are 4 Logomachy decks, 16 partner cards, 16 score cards, 16 invitations, and adhesive stars.

First, partner cards are assigned: 1, 1a, 2, 2a, ... 8, 8a. Players X and Xa are initial partners, and teams 1 and 2 play at table 1, 3 & 4 at table 2, 5 & 6 at table 3, 7 & 8 at table 4.  Each table plays a game of Logomachy, with table 1 ringing a bell to start the games, and also when they finish -- other tables just stopping play if not done yet, and calculating the current scores.  The winning pair at each table gets a gold star on their score cards, and the losing pair a red star. Then partners rotate tables -- the losers at table 1 going to table 4, and the winners at the other tables going to the next table up (4 to 3, 3 to 2, 2 to 1) -- and then the players at each table with the 'a' partner card switch partners, and everyone plays the next game of Logomachy. At the end -- after a certain amount of time, or of games, or someone attaining a certain total score -- the X and Xa players with the most gold stars get a prize, and those with the most red stars get a booby prize.

The rules are written assuming that mixed couples are playing, with the gentlemen getting the X partner cards, and the ladies the Xa cards, but any 16 people can play by those rules. Similarly, fewer than 4 tables of players could play -- or more, if more card decks are added.

The Progressive Logomachy decks have 56 letter cards each, the same as in the sets of the first publisher F. A. Wright, and the early McLoughlin sets; their later sets had 72 cards. Their art is black ink only, like those of Wright, and while the non-Prize cards all have the same songbird art, there is new art for each of the Prize letters JKQVXZ. These decks have smaller cards than from all other sets -- 2.25" x 3.25" as opposed to 2.5" x 3.5".

You play Collectionomics with YOUR collection. When you set out your treasures to show off and share you're playing as a curator at the 'Museum of You'. Brag and boast or tell tall tales about the stuff you love in the silliest way possible; by tagging your items with insane descriptions.

In an Apples to Apples adjacent format, you match items from your real life collection to fun Tag Cards. Players get points in two main ways: by getting items voted onto their Tag Cards or by matching the symbols on their hidden Goal Cards with the symbols on any Tag Cards in play. The difficult part is convincing other players to vote in a way that benefits you. How convincing can you be?

The player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tin Helm: Satyr/Rat Catcher Promo Card
description:
This Race/Class promo card for both Tin Helm and Tin Realm is included in the mint tin of Dustrunners (an expansion for Dustrunner).

The double-sided card features the Satyr race and Ratcatcher class:

     The Satyr card gives the starting stats of 7 health/8 energy/2 rations and states "The satyr may spend 3 favor to avoid a combat with any non-undead enemy."
     The Ratcatcher class starts with the Staff, Toolkit, & Charm items and will contribute 2 health and 3 energy to you starting totals.


Also included is the corrected Vile Shores card for Tin Realm.

Each player gets four pawns(a large one and three small ones), which he places in waiting position. These four pawns represent the four raw materials required for beer brewing (malt, water, hop, yeast). The large pawn is the main raw material MALT.
Each player is allowed to roll thrice, until he rolls the first 6. Then he proceeds to the starting position MALT. Also the small pawns are to proceed to their starting positions WATER, HOP and YEAST, only on the roll of a 6. When you have thrown a 6, you are allowed to throw again.
ATTENTION: If you stand in a space and another player comes to the same space, then you must go back to the last red space passed.
These large red spaces are control fields which must always be reached, by throwing the correct number. If you have failed to throw the correct number, you must wait until the next round and try it again. But if you have reached the control field, you cannot be shoved back by any other player, as long as you are there. You are the winner if your finished beer (with all four ingredients) stands first on the table in the final space.

—user summary

Push your agenda from district to district in Hamburg by winning exciting duels, conducting successful coalition negotiations and answering tricky questions.

Take control of one of six characters with different goals. The investor wants a flourishing economy and transport, the left-wing radical wants culture, the hipster wants security, the educated citizen wants more schools, environmentalists want green spaces and the worker wants to have at least a minimum of pretty much everything.

When you complete your random secret mission or earn 20 profile points, you have achieved your goal and take over the city of Hamburg.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The publisher Piatnik celebrates its 200th birthday. How to do this better than by a game, which contains the companies history. Turn the dial to guess the correct year, or lead your opponents into guesses which are too early.
You don't need to guess the exact year, but you will loose, if you put the event into an era too far in the past.

A special game, available as a giveaway.

boardgame: All Time Wrestling: All or Nothing
description:
All Time Wrestling is a card and dice game featuring legends such as Randy Savage, Andre the Giant, Kurt Angle and the British Bulldog. The box set also comes with 4 fictional wrestlers (Jax Felix, Mukundi Shumba, Oda Tsurugi and Rey Jaguar).

This is an asymmetric game where each wrestler comes with their own unique player boards, abilities, and deck of attack cards.

All or Nothing Edition includes tag team matches, cage matches, and last man standing matches, 2 Additional Wrestlers: Rey "El Intrepido Jaguar" and Oda Tsurugi. More importantly, it comes with a full 4-hour solo campaign that is highly replayable, featuring thousands of storylines and possibilities.

Includes 1-4 Player Game Modes, Solo, Tag, Last Man Standing, Cage Matches.
Featuring Andre the Giant, Randy Savage, Kurt Angle, the British Bulldog, Jax Felix, Mukundi Shumba, Rey "El Intrepido Jaguar" and Oda Tsurugi

Includes:
Full Solo Campaign Mode with Hundreds of Storylines
8 Wrestlers, each with a unique Double-Recessed Board
Campaign and Cage Match Boards

+ 300 Cards

6 Custom Dice and 4 Regular Dice
Momentum Meter and 33 tokens

Montaña Pingüino (Penguin Mountain) is a racing game, in which the animals run at full speed to see who reaches the finish line first. But be careful, because as a good race with animals you will have objects to throw, knock down the opponent, move position and much more.
A racing game easy to set up and with miles of fun.

Your race track is assembled in a modular way with cards that have both sides, with which we add acceleration for vehicles to go faster and faster.

Each player has a deck of actions with which you can program your actions to be the best racer and a character card which indicates the speed and life of each racer.

Like the great cartoon style racing games, items are added to make the game more and more crazy.

The first to win is the player to pass the finish line at the fastest speed and with the least damage possible.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warts and All! Easy and Fast playing Rules to Battle and Campaign during the English Civil war!
description:
Warts and All! rule set is designed for the beginner or experienced war gamer who is looking for a realistic and fun game that can be played in less than 90 minutes.

In these 84 Pages as well as the core rules, you will also get optional advanced rules to make the game more interesting and add more friction, 5 scenarios for quick play and learning, a fully crafted campaign and army cutouts to get into the game even if you don't have minis.

Get ready to be transported to the 17th Century and into the age of pike and shotte warfare where gallant cavaliers and stoic roundheads clash in miniature on your tabletop. 
May your battles be many and victorious.

—description from the publisher

Insects are vital to our ecosystem, so that's why you want to take credit for putting them into play in Bugs Buddy.

Each player starts with a secret hand of four double-sided insect tiles. Nine types of insects are included in the deck, each appearing eight times. On a turn, either add a new insect to the display, then place one of your colored cubes underneath it, or add an insect to the column of matching insect tiles already present, then replace another player's cube(s) underneath this insect with the same number of cubes of your color, plus one. (If you place a bee, for example, in a column that has two bees, you remove the opponent's two cubes, then place three of your own.) To end your turn, draw a tile from the deck.

Each insect type will have a single column, and you cannot replace your own cubes when placing a tile. If you don't have enough cubes to play any of your tiles, skip your turn. If you have no more cubes in front of you, you win; if all players pass in turn, whoever has the fewest cubes remaining wins.

Chit Chat is an incredibly fun communication game where talking and guessing take centre stage. You’ll receive quirky questions, and the answer is always a number. Your task: Arrange the answers in the correct order – from smallest to largest. Can you achieve the perfect score as a team, or will you be left scratching your heads at the end?

1. Place a card with any number in the center and then randomly draw 8 question cards, stacking them in front of you.
2. For each question, consider whether the answer is higher or lower than the number in the center, and sort the questions accordingly.
3. At the end, turn over all the question cards and check the answers. What’s your high score?

A simple game for the whole family and all your friends.

McNugget Buddies Bingo is a McDonald's branded children's travel bingo game. The goal of the game is to teach children recognition of common objects. This simple bingo game is designed to be played in the car, picturing a variety of common objects seen on the street, including stop signs, school buses, and of course, McDonald's. Each bingo card can be flipped to an at-home version of the game, depicting various household items, such as the sofa, a lamp, and a newspaper.

Gameplay combines sight-spotting children's games such as "I Spy" and classic bingo. When an object on the card is spotted, players mark that item on their card. The first player to mark 5 "McNugget Buddies" in a row wins the game.

Wars of Eagles and Empires is a rule set of division-level Napoleonic engagements.  
The rules are based on the popular Wars of Ozz system.  
The Double Random Activation(TM) system and the Reaction Test mechanic give the rules a bit of an old-school feel with new, streamlined mechanics.  
The product consists of three PDF files: the rules book, a scenario book, and the army lists.  

     The rule book includes rules for creating ad hoc scenarios and solo play.  
     The scenario book includes many division-level scenarios, which are difficult to find.  These were used in the playtesting of the rules.  
     The army lists provide a comprehensive listing of unit statistics for the game.

Wars of Ozz is a fantasy miniature game set in the Marvellous world of Ozz, first chronicled by L. Frank Baum in 1900, later turned into movies by MGM and Disney and now the inspiration for a totally unique mass battle tabletop experience.

Wars of Ozz is:

     A new genre for mass fantasy tabletop gaming
     Horse & Musket ‘Fantasy’
     Designed for solo, 2 player & team play
     Features a balanced Magic System
     Rulebook contains full background, army lists & painting guide
     Supported with over 70 packs of unique miniatures
     Backed with new factions and new releases for years to come
     Fun to play, streamlined, intuitive & based around reactive game play that keeps both sides continually involved
     Game length 2-3 hours

boardgame: March of the Ants: Evolved Edition – Tiny Titans
description:
A mini-expansion for March of the Ants: Evolved Edition featuring 7 new cards that bring strategy and intrigue to the wormholes in the Meadow!

NOTE: This mini-expansion was included as a reward in some pre-launch promotional offers from Weird City Games as well as being offered as an add-on.

boardgame: March of the Ants: Evolved Edition – Deluxe Edition
description:
The Deluxe Edition includes the Predator and Prey expansion ($39 value), full art food tokens, thick 2mm punchboard Nest Mats and Major Worker boards, and a deluxe box with a full art back that holds everything (base game and expansion) inside! The Tiny Titans: Mini Expansion is not included

The Predator and Prey expansion combines the best elements from the first edition Minions of the Meadow and Empire of the Earth expansions. The deluxe pledge includes the expansion inside the base game box. The expansion will only be available in retail as a separate box.

The land of Miralune stands at the dawn of a new era, a time of renewal and transformation. For centuries, the realm was ravaged by bitter wars between powerful kingdoms, each vying for control, land, and resources. The scars of these conflicts ran deep, leaving the people weary and the landscape fractured. However, after decades of strife, the warring factions, devastated by constant wars, have finally come to a fragile peace, signing a historic treaty that has brought an end to the violence. Now, with the ink still fresh on the accord, the people of Miralune look forward to a new beginning, as the golden age of prosperity dawns upon them.
    
Citizens of the warring factions, recognizing the unique opportunity before them, have decided to unite in a way that has never been done before. Rather than continuing to live in isolated territories, they have pledged to build a perfect empire—an unparalleled beacon of harmony, culture, and advancement—where settlers from every kingdom, of every race and creed, will live together in unity. It will be a place where innovation and tradition coexist, where knowledge and artistry are celebrated, and where a new generation of Miralune’s people will forge their shared destiny. A new island that will become the start of the empire was discovered. At the centre of the landmass, travelers discovered a giant magical crystal that could in the future become a hub for transport across the whole Miralune.
    
Kingdom Reborn is a roll & write pattern building game where you divide the land of your future empire between the settlers of five different tribes.  You are tasked with creating the empire in a way that will fulfill the needs of settlers of various fantasy races and cultures. Divide the land for your future citizens and bring forth the era of peace to the land that only knew conflict.

—description from the publisher

Players take on the role of rescuers in a valley inhabited by endangered animals. A massive flood threatens to engulf the valley, and the animals have scattered in panic. You must reunite animal pairs and save as many as possible before the floodwaters consume the playing field. The game is inspired by the story of Noah's Ark, where the objective is to gather pairs of animals and protect them from the rising waters.

The game is played on a grid of cards representing different animals and environmental elements like "HILL" and "FLOOD" cards. Players move their chips across the board using dice rolls, collecting animal cards and forming pairs in their "SHELTER." Special animal abilities and trading mechanics add strategic depth, allowing players to exchange cards, use unique powers, and avoid dangers. The playing area gradually shrinks as "FLOOD" cards appear, making movement and decision-making increasingly critical.

The objective is to collect as many animal pairs as possible while avoiding "FLOOD" cards, which can cause players to lose their collected animals. Points are awarded based on the number and type of pairs collected, with bonuses for completing sets of the same animal group. The game ends when no more cards can be placed or when the flood reduces a side of the board to a single card. The player with the most points wins, having best fulfilled the mission of saving the animals from the flood.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
From designer Emilio Samuel comes a new strategic worker placement Euro about trading in the canals of Venice during the Renaissance era.

La Serenissima is a medium-heavy, holistic-strategic Euro that emphasizes player choices and consequences featuring network building, worker placement, and resource management.

FEATURES

Tight, satisfying mechanics: Everything you do, from placing your workers and trading to influencing the policies of Venecian trade has limitations and payoffs.

Structured gameplay: The game is designed to facilitate various avenues of interaction and trading in mind, and everything is laid out traditionally organized and represented by readable icons.

Emergent systematic approach: Players are encouraged to do lots of things in the game, pushing forward player agency and giving the tools to the players to achieve multiple ways of victory.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Wargames Rules for Armoured Warfare at Company and Battalion Level 1925 to 1950
description:
Between 1973 and 1993 the Wargames Research Group (WRG) published a very successful and influential series of tactical wargames rules, designed mainly by Phil Barker, covering WW2 and the post-war period. These were:

1. War Games Rules, Armour & Infantry 1925-1950 (Jun 1973)
2. War Games Rules, Armour & Infantry 1950-1975 (Jan 1974)
3. Wargames Rules for Armoured Warfare at Company and Battalion Battlegroup Level, 1950 to 1985 (Jun 1979)
4. Wargames Rules for All Arms Land Warfare from Platoon to Battalion Level, 1925-1950 (Jun 1988)
5. Wargames Rules for All Arms Land Warfare from Platoon to Battalion Level, 1950-2000 (Jan 1993)

The Jan 1974 set were, I believe, the first published set of miniature wargames rules to cover present-day warfare; in doing so, they spawned a whole new branch of the hobby. The astute reader will notice that there are three sets of modern (1950 to 1975, 1985, or 2000) rules, but only two (both 1925 to 1950) covering WW2.

Some wargamers still play using the original 1925 to 1950 set of rules, and the middle set of modern rules remains the favourite of others. This set, then, published with Phil Barker's permission, is intended to fill the gap left by the “missing” set, using the same rules approach as embodied in the 1950 to 1985 rules, but covering the earlier time period. Almost the entire body of the rules has been shamelessly cribbed from the 1950 to 1985 rules, with modifications as necessary to suit the WW2 era. Rules for guided missiles, helicopters, and night vision equipment have been removed, and the categories of anti-tank guns below 100mm in calibre refined to give more differentiation. Anti-tank rifles and SMG-only infantry groups have been added, and a few oddities such as the Püppchen, the spade mortar, and the Kartukov ampulomyot. While the armour classes are much the same, the vehicle listing is somewhat complicated by the relation between frontal and side armour being less regular during WW2 than later.

Some changes have been made; the artillery rules have been amended to prevent non-linear effects from increasing the number of guns firing on a target, and suppression is no longer automatic, but there is also a hazard outside the intended beaten zone. The anti-aircraft sequence has been simplified, and air defenders who wish to use flak and fighters simultaneously must risk fratricide.

Time HAMMER Free 4 All, is for 2-4 players age 12+ and takes about 30-60 minutes. Players set Agents in past and future decades for assets, powers, Skill/Repair Bonuses (to win).
 Interfering with time increases the risk of creating paradoxes—dangerous anomalies that destabilize the entire timeline. Managing these paradoxes is crucial, as one misstep could trigger a catastrophic collapse of reality. The tension between risk and reward is central to the game, where players must carefully navigate their interventions to avoid disastrous consequences while still striving for victory.

—description from the designer

The complex of 1001 games which occurred in one thousand and one tails and stories . the game is based on Alibaba and forty thieves story and you playing the role of the leader of the thieves who's planning to commit a huge robbery.

Your goal is to:
find the best spot with the best score to lead the thieves and collect point the more you gain thieves with the valued colors the more your chances of winning increases unless you encounter other characters which they might cause you real troubles.

The Wanderer is an interactive strategy card game for up to 6 players that puts you in direct competition with the forces of the universe!

Play as an emissary of a higher power representing timeless forces like Fortune, Insight, and War. Using a meld-making core mechanic, craft Intrigues (3-card runs or sets) out of Maiden, Magician, or Horseman Intrigue cards, and activate them to reduce your opponents’ score to 0.

The fun ramps up as you discover how different emissaries work together, and use them to work out your favorite Intrigue strategy. Are you a healer, a charmer, a warrior or something else? Along the way, you’ll use the emissaries at your command to test your opponent’s psychic ability, control the Wanderer, and keep weighing your options in a game that gives serious vibes!

What's unique about the Wanderer?
-16 asymmetrical player-emissaries with their own play styles!
-Custom configure every game with your favorite emissaries!
-One of a kind play & pass dynamic!
-Hidden tarot-card theme and mechanics built right into the essence of the game!

The Wanderer is a grand game with a tiny footprint -- no meeples, tokens, or mats. But don’t be deceived! You’ll find this is a timeless game that you'll want to keep coming back to, and with so many different combinations to explore, it’s sure to be a winner every game night!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Gulf Coast (fan expansion for Age of Steam)
description:
This is a fan made map for Age of Steam based on the region of the Gulf Coast from Texas to Florida for two to four players. It incorporates a new shipping mechanic by using ports.

Features of this map include ports and swamps.
Players may build ports in coastal cities and ship goods between them.
Both ports and swamps incur an additional build cost.

In this two-player duel, artists draw cute creatures that come to life in a battle to save the magical land of Tempera. Each player chooses a Visionary (artist) and constructs a 39-card deck of Splatterkins (creatures), Techniques, Alterations, Finishes, and alternate doodles of a Splatterkin to upgrade their chosen combatant or stimy an opponent's efforts. The goal is to win 10 Paint Drops by defeating enemies.

Paint Drops are acquired by defeating the Opponent's Splatterkins in battle. For each Splatterkin the Player defeats, they gain 1 Paint Drop. A Player usually cannot obtain Paint Drops by using techniques, however, there are some cases where a technique will grant the Player a Paint Drop.

family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

description:
Riftlands - The War Within is a fast-paced PnP MOBA-inspired game where two teams of heroes battle to defend their territory and destroy the enemy Nexus.Players collect Shards, fight monsters, and adapt to dynamic activation orders based on skill priority and dice selection.

Hero defeat unlocks new items and artifacts, strengthening yourself or your team.

With no fixed rounds, every move is a race for survival in an ever-changing battlefield.

Faster than anyone and more deliver to you and earn points! Dotabata Food Delivery Game!

What kind of game? 
(1) You will receive 12 cooking cards, 3 dice and 1 coin. 
(2) Take one card from the deck of your event card and use components to clear your written subjects and earn points.
(3) When the deck runs out, the highest points will be the #1 delivery member of the card.

This is the highlight! The cooking card is designed on both sides. There are many other dishes that you can't find if you turn it inside out, so you are sure to have it covered! The elements caused by the eyes of the dice and the front and back of the coin create lucky feel.

Rensou Bingo combines word association with Bingo into a fun party game. Each round there will be a new theme such as "What comes to mind when you hear `Japan`?" or "What is something short?" that players choose to answer with a single word.

Players reveal their selected answers and circle the number on their Bingo Card equal to how many people they were able to "Rensou" with. (Rensou is the Japanese word for `associate`)

The key to winning is having a good grasp on what answers the other people you are playing with might choose. Test how well you know your friends to match your answers in a manner that gets you a Bingo before anyone else!

boardgame: Rebel Princess: Deluxe Edition – Happily Never After
description:
A year has passed since their last gathering and the princesses have sent out invitations for their annual five-day all-princess party! Rumor has it that Red Riding Hood & the Swan Maiden will be making an appearance. Hopefully, those pesky princes keep their distance as we wouldn’t want anyone to get Bequeathed or have a Surprise Wedding on our hands! Who will manage to stay single after this year’s affair?

Happily Never After is the first expansion for the modern classic trick taking game Rebel Princess Deluxe Edition and adds:

     2 more punchboard princess tiles
     6 more Tarot-sized round cards
     1 more scorepad


—description from the publisher

Note: the 6 expansion tarot Round cards have Greek letters in the upper left corner (alpha, beta, gamma, delta, epsilon, zeta), and these cards plus the 2 new Princess cards have yellow sun icons in the upper right corner.

The Fate Experiment is a social card game best played with many participants, referred to as “subjects.” 
Subjects will draw a Game card and follow its instructions. Each game lasts two to three minutes. If a subject loses too many Games, they are eliminated from the Experiment. Some Games can be won by chance or fairness, but subjects must lie or mislead others if they are to gain any advantage.
The nine Fate cards are used in various ways each Game. They may assign roles based on the names of the Fates, or their number values may be used.
Three strikes and you are out of the Experiment. If a subject loses a Game, they receive a “cautionary” Subject Status Tag. If the subject loses again, they flip their Status Tag to “critical”. One more loss and they are eliminated from the Experiment.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Cryptid Cafe: The Chef's Apprentice – Christmas Dish Pack
description:
Put a merry twist on game night with this Christmas themed dish pack for Cryptid Cafe: The Chef's Apprentice.

Includes 84 Christmas dish tiles in 18 creepy flavors and Krampus line cook card.

boardgame: Cryptid Cafe: The Chef's Apprentice – Halloween Dish Pack
description:
Put a spooktacular twist on game night with this Halloween themed dish pack for Cryptid Cafe: The Chef's Apprentice.

Includes 84 Halloween dish tiles in 18 creepy flavors and Werewolf line cook card.

In Azardtia: First Impact, players compete on a ruthless prison planet, using strategy and resource management to overcome obstacles and outmaneuver opponents. Only the most cunning will escape in this dice-and-card-driven board game.

On their turn, players roll two ten-sided dice to determine movement, choosing between their sum or the difference between both values. Landing on certain spaces may lead to confrontations or force the drawing of action or penalty cards. Additionally, players draw and play cards that provide strategic advantages, hinder opponents, or alter the game state. Effective hand management and timing are crucial to reaching the extraction point.

Players can challenge each other to duels, where the winner gains a level and the loser drops one, sometimes facing the penalty of moving backward on the board. Each character also has asymmetric abilities, allowing different strategic approaches to escape.

—description from the designer

Fast Fables is a game which has a group of adventurers setting out to complete quests and make a name for themselves. One player acts as the Storyteller and takes on the role of non-player characters while framing the narrative for the players. Each player builds a character out of a Trait and Job, take turns describing actions to complete narrative goals, and uses their character's inspiration to gain bonuses.

As the Storyteller and Players build onto the narrative, the adventurers win by completing quests. After each quest players may make new characters, continue playing with their previous, or may become the new Storyteller to continue playing another game.

Game is packaged in a tuckbox with rules printed inside the box, and containing 27 Cards featuring a Character Side and Quest side.

boardgame: Harry Potter: défense contre les forces du mal
description: During this end-of-year Defense Against the Dark Arts course, the creatures merge from all sides. Play spell cards wisely to repel them and make your house win as many points as possible!

Following the rules of battle and Quidditch, players compete in each round: the beater knocks the keeper or chaser out of play, the keeper stops the chaser's shot - unless it is countered by a "feint" card. The chaser who remains in play can finally score! The player who has scored the most goals wins... unless another player manages to catch the Golden Snitch

Players gather the ingredients needed to make their potions by drawing cards from the playing area, from the draw pile, or directly from their opponents' deck (watch out for the Cauldron Exchange card!) - and can even choose an ingredient using the Professor's Favorite card. The first to make 3 potions wins the game and obtains their Universal Elemental Witchcraft Certificate, with an Optimal rating.

The local boss has just fallen. Who will take over? 1 city, 5 gangs... You play as one of the leaders, with a single goal: to make Poison City YOUR city. Recruit members, bribe the Officials, release hordes of Pickpockets into the streets... All means are good to become the most respected crook. But be careful! The Vigilantes are waiting for you at the turn. A deckbuilding and placement game in the heart of the Série Noire universe.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
You are baby bunnies that don’t want to go to bed! Compete against your siblings to snatch one of the Items along the racetrack, while also vying to be furthest back when someone reaches the bed.
Use your Baby Bunny’s special Ability to your advantage and poke your siblings ahead of you.
Take turns playing Move cards to move your baby bunny (mostly) forward along the racetrack. Go too fast and you gain Momentum +1 cards which will propel you even faster later in the game. Too slow and you will miss out on the Items to pick up along the way. Momma and Bedtime story mechanics push laggards and slow front runners.

You are renovating your home and need to add a deck to your house. Find 5 random objects nearby and stack them on the card as the playing surface balanced off the edge of a table. Strategic players will be careful not to cover bonuses on the card. The longest overhanding deck wins! You build an overhanding deck on the card... it's a deck builder!

—description from the designer

In INKtentions, players become cunning octopuses vying for the most food in the treacherous depths of the ocean. Using ink clouds, sneaky hiding spots, and well-placed tentacle maneuvers, they must outwit their rivals while dodging hungry sharks and relentless eels. Strategy and deception go hand in hand as players secretly place cards, protecting themselves—or misleading their opponents—while striving to collect the most valuable food.

Each round, players take turns playing one of their 9 cards (one for each brain) facedown near a scoring pile. After 8 cards are played (one for each arm) the cards are revealed and resolved. Ink cards protect everyone in a pile, but at the cost of helping opponents. Hide cards offer personal safety but leave others exposed. Punch cards shift the tides, moving threats or disrupting well-laid plans. With every decision, players must weigh risk and reward—protecting their own octopuses while ensuring their rivals don’t get ahead.

At the end of each round, surviving octopuses claim food, with set collection bonuses encouraging competition for the best meals. After three rounds (one for each heart) the player with the most food wins. INKtentions is a game of calculated risks, clever plays, and just the right amount of betrayal, where only the craftiest cephalopod will reign supreme!

—description from the designer

Providing the North Pole with supplies for each Christmas is tough work, work that falls on Santa's hardiest allies- the reindeer.

In Reindeer Games, players control a team of reindeer to try and earn as much as they can before Christmas by gathering supplies then flying sleigh-shipments up to the North Pole for big payouts. This particular town is also charged with raising the up-and-coming lead reindeer, Juergen the Yellow-nose reindeer. By wisely maneuvering this budding "Commander-in-Chief" players can get a steady stream of points that may put them ahead of their fellow deer while also disrupting their industry.

Reindeer Games is a traditional worker placement game with one key difference: almost every deer/worker you place can be turned to your opponents' advantage. You also will very often be at the mercy of others to return your deer to you and set up big payouts.

—description from the designer

You are the lone sniper on a mission to kill the enemy general. Moving cautiously through the rubble of once great city of Stalingrad you must hold off forces of the Wehrmacht. With limited number of ammo and supplies, you have to be very careful to not overexpose yourself to enemy fire. Remember, you have only one life. Sniper is a solo card game set in the ruins of Stalingrad during the World War II, 1942.

This is a micro-game, it consist only of 4 double sided map cards, deck of 5 Action cards, 2 dices (one d6 and one special focus die), 1 Soviet Sniper token (representing the player), 1 Soviet soldier (representing the ally reinforcements), 4 x German’s soldiers, 4 x German’s snipers, 2 Panzer tank tokens, 2 Officers, 1 German general token and 1 Bombardment token.

Objective of the game:

If you manage to kill a German general you win the game.

Turn order:

Each turn starts when player draws 1 Action card, that card is not used during that turn.
Then player draws 2 Action cards into his hand and decides which one to use for its ammo, focus, health or supply power, and which one as an enemy activation and special ability for that turn. By rotating the card 90 degrees, you’re setting your available actions and status for that turn.

Player loses if:

- He is killed.
- Enemy unit moves onto his spot (he is captured and shot)
- Any Soviet spawn point is captured by the enemy unit
- If there is 3 or more enemy units in the same sector of the map (on the same card)

Primosole Bridge offers you the opportunity to fight tactical battles with historical units. Know the strengths and weaknesses of those units and use them to decide the battle in your favour. The unique dice system ensures action-packed gameplay. Combine this expansion with Gela Beachhead and no game will be the same.

description:
Ships and Tactics is a Japanese doujin card game produced by doujin card and game club Phoenix Command.  It is currently in its 4th edition.

The game is based on Modern Naval Battles Card Game from 3W games and uses anime ships instead of modern naval ones. Each player has ships and cards that are unique to their force.

The sequence of play is:

     Draw phase
     Special Weapon Attack Phase
     Aircraft Attack Phase
     Fleet Attack Phase
     Declaration of Contact/Melee
     Special Action Phase
     Hand Adjustment and Untap Phase


Basic game play involves each player determining the action that they will do in each phase. With regards to shooting, ammo cards are played to fire at another player's ships, but to fire a card you must have a ship that can fire that type of ammo.  Ships are arranged into rows, and certain weapon types, such as beams and missiles, can attack based upon their position in the fleet. Beam weapons, for example, can shoot from front row to front row, while missiles can shoot from the front row to the front and back rows. Players can also use special cards to defect or intercept shots, as well as send out fighters and mecha from their carriers, use tactics, or other special abilities.  Cards are identified with their offensive weapons, defensive capabilities, and hit points.

If you are a fan of anime shows dealing with mecha, then this is a game for you.

The starter set included a deck for:


     Legend of the Galactic Heroes - Galactic Empire
     Mobile Suit Gundam - Principality of Zeon
     Mobile Suit Gundam - Earth Defense Force
     Space Battleship Yamato - Earth Federation


Additional decks are sold. There are currently over 40 different decks. Some of these include:


     Macross, Macross Frontier, Macross 7
     Gundam 0079, Gundam Seed, Z Gundam, ZZ Gundam, Gundam 0083, Gundam Unicorn, Char's Counterattack
     Yamato 2199, Yamato 2205, Garmillas Empire, Comet Empire, Gatlantis, Dark Star Nebula
     Nadesico
     LOTGH Free Planet's Alliance
     Gunbuster

Do You Even Know Me? is the game every family and friend group has been waiting for!

It GAMIFIES connection by allowing people to see how well they really know each other!

With 300 multiple-choice questions ranging from simple to deep, your group will have a blast discovering more about themselves, the people they love, and those they are just getting to know.

But it’s not just conversation cards…it’s competition! Everyone is involved in every turn, and the person who knows people best is declared “The Very Best BFF!”

If you pay close attention, you will discover truths that help you understand each other better and fun facts that make it easier to bond in the future.

This game will keep your family and friends engaging, laughing and shouting, “Do you even know me?!”

How to Play:

Setup:

Give each player one set of A-B-C-D cards. 
    Place all BFF (best friends forever) cards in the middle of the table.

Game Play:

The youngest player is the first reader; they read one Question Card out loud–including all four answers. 
    All other players guess which answer the reader will say is “correct” and place their A-B-C-D card face down in front of them. 
    The reader reveals their truthful answer. 
    Each player who guessed correctly gets one BFF card. 
    The role of the reader is passed clockwise to the next person.

Game End:

The game ends when

A certain number of rounds have been played, or
    A certain number of BFF cards have been won, or
    A certain time limit has been met.

We like the round to end when someone reaches 10 BFF cards.

Introduction:Harbustaz (from Proto-Germanic: harbustaz, meaning "harvest" or "autumn") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 5 cells per side, but boards with 4 or 6 cells are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stackable pieces of their own color in two types: peasants and seeds.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A peasant is a set of pieces located in the same cell. A single piece is also a peasant.
     To harvest a seed is to incorporate it into a peasant, so that the seed ceases to be a seed and becomes part of the peasant.
     A territory is a maximal set of empty cells surrounded by stones of any color. You own a territory if more than half of the stones surrounding it are of your color. 


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Move your peasant in a straight line a distance equal to its height to an empty cell or a cell occupied by a friendly seed to harvest it. Upon moving, the peasant increases its height by 1 and uses an extra piece from the board to grow. If the peasant harvests a seed, it uses the seed to grow instead of taking a piece from the board.
     Sow a friendly seed in an empty cell in a straight line from your peasant to an empty cell at a distance equal to the height of your peasant. Then remove a piece from your peasant unless the peasant is 1 height.
     Pass your turn.


You can move a peasant or sow seeds through friendly pieces.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass in consecutive turns. At the end of the game, each player counts the number of empty cells in friendly territories, and the player who made the last action receives an additional half point. The player with the highest score wins.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places two peasants of height 1, one black and one white, and the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called the two-piece pie rule.

–description from the designer

Expansion 1 adds even more fun to your copy of The Uzzle. Use the two new blocks with the challenges in the base game to allow for new possibilities, or take on the 50 new Level 5 Challenge cards that require all 7 different blocks to solve!

You are Ellen Ripley, the last survivor of the spaceship Nostromo. There is an Alien onboard hunting you and you must finish a few things before abandoning the ship.

In this solo game you have to finish 3 Missions on 3 different Locations to win. Game is consistent of 54 cards split into:


     Mission Items
     Special items
     Locations (Missions)
     Close Call cards
     Alien cards
     Main Character (Ellen Ripley)


Game Turn and Objective:

Each turn you can do one of the actions from the Main Character card. Those actions are:


     Move then draw one card
     Move then use one cards
     Draw two cards


You always draw cards from the Nostromo deck.
Your objective is to collect enough Mission Items for 3 Missions written on Location cards. If you manage to do that before the Nostromo deck runs out, or the Main character dies, you win the game.

Each card you draw goes into your hand. Your hand size is 4, so if you have to take more cards, you have to discard some from your hand. Discarded cards go out of the game.
When moving your character moves from one adjacent Location to another. If you are at the end of the column or row, you may ‘wrap around’ by moving from the rightmost to the leftmost Location (or from the bottom to the top if you set your game in a column fashion).

You always use cards from your hand. In case of the Mission Items, just place them near the Location. In case of the Special Items, most of them are discarded out of the game one used.

—description from the publisher

The guns are loaded. The trenches are dug. The enemy fortress looms ahead, its walls standing between you and victory. As a British commander in the Napoleonic Wars, your mission is clear: reduce the enemy stronghold to rubble and take it by storm before time runs out.

In Siege Works, a solo roll-and-write wargame, you will oversee every aspect of the siege. Dig trenches to advance under fire, deploy artillery to batter the defenses, and choose your moment to launch a final, desperate assault. Every decision counts, do you bombard the walls for a major breach, risking delays, or charge through a minor gap before reinforcements arrive? The defenders won’t sit idle; expect sudden counterattacks, deadly sorties, and unexpected setbacks from the event chart.

Featuring action allocation, point-to-point movement, dice-based combat, and siege attrition mechanics, Siege Works puts you in command of a tense Napoleonic siege. Every decision matters as morale dwindles and time runs out. Will you claim victory, or be forced to abandon the siege in defeat? The fate of the fortress and your army rests in your hands.

Shuffle the cards and arrange them in two piles.

To start, each player receives 7 pretty cards and 2 trigger cards, and importantly, they do not show them to the other players.

Ready? Now choose the youngest player to start the game and reveal one trigger card with flaws. This player acts as the show and assessor, and does not show their own cards.

The other players choose one of the pretty cards from their cards that they think best matches the flaw shown. THEY CHOOSE THEIR IDEAL CANDIDATE, and pass the card face down to the player who is assessing.

Bonus 1 - to diversify the game, the player laying down does not peek and takes one random card from the pile with beauties. He introduces the card into the game (this option is recommended for 3 players, above 3 this element can be omitted)
Bonus 2 - during the entire game, each player can change the fuse card twice and mix it up a bit;)

Now it's time for a twist! The player who chooses, reveals all the cards with beauties and chooses the one that he likes the most and that most hilariously reflects the flaw on the card. The winner takes their card and automatically gets a point.

We have it, the first round is over! Players take turns drawing cards with beauties so that they always have 7 in their hand. In clockwise order, the next player becomes the new player to lay out and evaluate, and the game continues.

The game ends when one of the players collects 5 points, i.e. 5 cards with beauties or one of the piles runs out of cards.

The Vietnam War is a 1 to 2 Player game, suitable for solitaire of US battles in The Vietnam War. The Vietnam War follows in the boot steps of other Combat books games by giving the player the right mix of just enough tactical flavor without overwhelming the player with data.

Vietnam, 1965. North Vietnam rapidly increase its infiltration of men and supplies to support the Viet Cong's growing influence over rural South Vietnam. After a series of post-WW2 communist aggressions, western democracies are afraid. In 1948, Stalin seals off West Berlin, leading to the Berlin air lift. In 1949, communists overrun China. In 1950 North Korea launches the Korean War, a bloody three-year war leading to hundreds of thousands of deaths.
 Communists overrun French force at Dien Bien Phu in 1954, and the US becomes more involved. At this point, fearing the piece-by-piece elimination of the free world, most US citizens, as well as Congress, favor intervention.

The US initiates Operation Rolling Thunder, a bombing campaign over North Vietnam. Army and Marine troops begin ground operations, seeking to find and destroy enemy forces. US involvement in the Vietnam war escalates to a full boil.

—description from the publisher

Is it a puzzle? Or is it a card game? It’s … both! The Puzl It game is the competitive puzzle card game for ages 7 and up! In this addictive, easy-to-play game, players lay down cards from their hand to build a puzzle. The more cards they connect in the puzzle, the more cards they can steal from another player’s deck. But if a player gets a Blocker card, their deck is off limits! Be the last one with cards left to win, and then complete the puzzle. With 72 thick, sturdy cards, the Puzl It family card game can be played over and over, so it’s just right for family game night. It even comes with a handy reference poster to show players what the completed image should look like—an eye-popping pizza party scene. Puzl It and all related properties are trademarks of Hasbro.


     CONNECT ‘EM, STEAL ‘EM, BLOCK ‘EM: The more cards a player connects, the more they can steal from another player’s deck. Unless someone gets the coveted Blocker card! Then no one can steal their cards
     BE THE LAST WITH CARDS LEFT TO WIN: The last player with cards in their hand wins the game—and as a bonus, they get to complete the puzzle
     72 THICK, STURDY CARDS: The thickness of the 72 cards give them a premium feel, and together they create an image of a detailed, comic pizza party scene. Enjoy playing the Puzl It game again and again
     EASY TO SET UP AND PLAY: Enjoy instant fun with this easy-to-teach and easy-to-learn game! Plus, it’s portable, so this picture puzzle game is an ideal travel game for vacations and on-the-go fun
     FAMILY GAME NIGHT: Accessible and fun for a wide range of ages, the Puzl It strategy card game is a great game for family game night! Connect, compete, and make memories together


—description from the publisher

Ace Run is a disc golf board game. It features a nine-hole disc golf course with trees, water hazards, and out of bounds. Players take turns throwing and competing for the lowest overall score after nine holes. Each hole has its own tee pad and basket that are numbered accordingly. Each tee pad is numbered and has four spaces that a player may choose to tee off from. Each basket is also numbered to match the tee pad which you are throwing from and is indicated on the board as a wheel with spokes surrounded by eight orange spaces. The orange spaces are what is called the "gimme" zone. "Gimme" zones make for easy tap in putts. If a player lands on the basket space on their throw from the tee pad, then they have just aced. All holes in Ace Run can be aced so it is anyone's game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Landscape X: Das Erbe des Stahlbarons
description:
You are a detective specializing in inheritance cases and are hired by the foundation of a recently deceased steel baron because there are apparently problems with his estate. The investigation turns out to be an exciting case that takes you, together with the Ruhr original Herbert, to various places of interest in the Ruhr region. Whether it's a colliery, steelworks, gasometer or pumping station, you'll get to know many facets of the Ruhr region - including exciting facts about the locations.

Will you succeed in unraveling the mystery of the steel baron's legacy? Find out on this exciting puzzle rally through the Ruhr region.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Du bist ein auf Erbschaftsfälle spezialisierter Detektiv und wirst von der Stiftung eines jüngst verstorbenen Stahlbarons engagiert, weil es offenbar Probleme mit dessen Nachlass gibt. Die Ermittlungen erweisen sich als spannender Fall, der dich zusammen mit dem Ruhri-Original Herbert zu diversen Sehenswürdigkeiten des Ruhrgebiets führt. Ob Zeche, Stahlwerk, Gasometer oder Pumpwerk, du lernst viele Facetten des Reviers kennen - inklusive spannender Fakten zu den Schauplätzen.

Wird es dir gelingen, das Rätsel um das Erbe des Stahlbarons zu lüften? Finde es heraus auf dieser spannenden Rätsel-Rallye durch das Ruhrgebiet.

—description from the publisher

You are the manager of a soccer club seeking to build your Best Eleven, a team to represent your club on Matchday.

Recruit players to your team by bidding on Free Agents over seven transfer windows. Each Free Agent you add to your team can add speed, savvy, strength, and skill and help increase the performance of your Best Eleven.

Win the game by having the team with the most points accumulated from team synergy and tactics on Matchday.

—description from the designer
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
From the team that has launched five payloads to the International Space Station comes a trading card game unlike any other—featuring cards embedded with genuine spaceflight materials. Star Heroes is a sci-fi trading card game filled with galactic conquest and exploration, blending the epic scale of 4X board games with strategic card play. Set 500 years into a post-apocalyptic future, every card showcases cinematic 3D sci-fi artwork made exclusively by human artists and designed to immerse players into dark, distant galaxies.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Star Trek: Captain's Chair – Promo Pack
description:
Five new cards and a new ship standee for use with Star Trek: Captain's Chair:

USS Enterprise-B Standee
USS Enterprise-B Ship Card
Sehlat Cargo Card
Wesley Crusher Encounter Card
Flight Training Accident Incident Card
Whale Probe Incursion Incident Card

An expansion for Final Girl containing the following items:


     11 secret envelopes
     5 Signature Action cards
     5 Vehicle Action cards
     15 Proxy Event cards
     3 Proxy Item cards
     4 Final Girl Promo cards
     Carolyn’s Revenge Character Pack
     Grandma’s Grudge Character Pack
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The quiet morning air is disturbed by the sounds of growling stomachs as the valley awakens - the Fruit-O-Saurs are hungry!

Skilfully gather a combination of Fruit-O-Saurs and their favourite fruit to be crowned the Win-O-Saur in Sugarrr!

Sugarrr! is a card game for 2-4 players. On your turn you can either swap fruit cards from your hand with fruit cards in a market row, or play fruit cards to one of the five columns in the play area. Each fruit card played must match either the colour or number of the previous fruit card in the column. Then, if the number of the last fruit card played matches the number of fruit cards in the column you will gather all the fruit cards in the column into your scoring pile. If the number on the last fruit card played does not match the number of cards in the column, you will instead attract a number of Fruit-O-Saur cards equal to the number of cards you played. When the game ends, each Fruit-O-Saur will multiply the value of their favourite fruit from your score pile. The player with the most points is crowned the Win-O-Saur!

DUELING HORNS is a two-player competitive game of Viking Warfare, pitting two armies against each other in the field battle. Strategy and wit will win you the day.

Each player controls an army of nine warriors and one king in an epic showdown for control of the battlefield or to slay each other's king!

Warriors and kings can turn the tide of the battle and are able to move any number of spaces horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. Players take turns moving one piece at a time, vying for position and striking down their foes. Movement is hindered by the shaded spaces in the center of the battlefield called the Mound. Players may also use their pieces to charge their enemies, stunning them for one turn.

In order to eliminate an enemy warrior or king, players must "Flank" their opponent's pieces by positioning their warriors or king on either side of an enemy piece.

The game ends when one of the kings have been eliminated, or when one player has gained control of the battlefield by removing all of their opponent's pieces from the Mound.

Each game box includes 20 painted game pieces, 5 metal coins, 1 Formation die, 1 battle board, and 1 barrows board.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Armoured Strike: Wargame Rules for Combined Arms Warfare 1950-2000
description:
Armoured Strike is a set of miniature wargame rules for playing cold war and modern, combined arms land battles. The rules are designed to cover the period from the advent of the guided missile in the 1950s to the advent of the fully digital modern battlefield, around the year 2000.
In most games players will command a battlegroup comprising of a number of companies or battalions and games last a couple of hours and can be played on a 4 foot by 6-foot table using 1/300 scale miniatures.
The rulebook includes all the rules needed to play as well as army lists for the major nations fighting a fictional third world war during the 1980s.
Additional army lists will be made available on the publisher website.

DESIGN PHILOSOPHY
SEQUENCE OF PLAY
Various sequences and methods of unit activation were considered however it was decided to go back to the classic “I go, you go” sequence as this provides a good way of making the chaos of battle manageable, especially for large numbers of units. The addition of an overwatch system however means that during a players turn, their opponent is not stood idle.

TROOP QUALITY
The quality (training and morale) of troops has been heavily simplified to a single dice roll at the end of each turn for any units that have come under fire and been suppressed. This determines if they fall back, continue fighting or give up, surrendering or leaving the battle area. Although simple this means that even if units are not killed or destroyed, they can as in real life still be put out of the battle.

GROUND SCALE
The approximate ground scale of 1” =50 metres was chosen as it would allow for realistic weapon ranges to be depicted on a reasonable sized table while still allowing for emphasis on manoeuvre and positioning of units.

boardgame: Battle Captains: Company Level Combined Arms Maneuver from WWII to the 21st Century
description:
Battle Captains is an innovative game placing the players in the command experience of a company commander in a combined arms maneuver environment.
