The main book provides the core rules to fight any battle from the dawn of mechanisation in WWII to the networked battlefields of the 21st century. The rule book only contains two example forces from WWII with which to test the mechanisms.

Gameplay in Battle Captains embraces the concepts of friction, fog of war, and maneuver warfare. Players should expect to confront difficulties from not just their opponent but circumstances as well. The ability to adapt to a changing environment, subordinates failing to exercise good judgement, and all the other vagaries of war is paramount to victory in Battle Captains.

National lists, armies, units, and all other statistics required for play are found in various theater books. 
Each theater book provides the information and stats necessary to fight in a particular theater or war. 
Players can purchase only the theater books relevant to their collections or interests.
Updates and corrections to Battle Captains will occur and reflect within the rule book occasionally.

boardgame: Western Desert Force: Operation Compass – North Africa Campaign Part 2
description:
Focusing on Operation Compass in 1941. The British operation originally planned as a raid on Italian camps in Egypt, turned into a much longer campaign across North Africa. Part 2 covers the replacement of the British 4th Infantry Division with the Australian 6th Infantry Division and begining with Bardia, continues through Beda Fomm. This battalion level scenario pack contains 7 exciting miniatures war game scenarios written in campaign form and playable by 2-4 players.

The scenarios include data sheets for vehicles and uses Fist Full of TOWs 3 rules. 
Scenarios can be easily converted to your favorite game system.

The six scenarios can be played as a campaign. A campaign score sheet is included, enabling players to track the campaign progress and final score.

These scenarios put you in the Gasoline Cowboy seat, making you and your gaming partner(s) the tank drivers as you play through the Western Desert Force: Operation Compass - North Africa Campaign.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Jounce, you’re a frog bouncing around a pond, snacking on bugs, and trying to out-hop your friends and end the game with the prized water lily. Every turn, everyone will simultaneously play a movement card to leap to a new lily pad, but there’s a catch—you can only jump in certain directions. Figuring out the best way to jounce from pad to pad can be challenging, and rewarding!

Every time you land, you grab a bug. If you collect several of the same kind, you can use special abilities to get ahead, But collecting a variety of bugs will be worth more at the end of the game! The game ends when one type of bug is completely eaten, and whoever has the most points wins.

It’s a game of tricky jumps, clever planning, and a little bit of chaos. Sometimes you’ll land exactly where you need to, and sometimes you’ll get stuck in a spot that makes you rethink everything. Either way, it’s all part of the fun—just don’t let the other frogs eat all the good bugs before you get there!

—description from the publisher

In Little Pig Little Pig, players take on the roles of four clever and competitive pigs racing to build their three homes—one of hay, one of wood, and one of brick—before their opponents. But building isn’t as simple as it seems, especially with the Big Bad Wolf lurking nearby.

Each turn, players move across a dynamic, randomly generated 5x7 tile grid, collecting resources, sabotaging rivals, and preparing for potential wolf encounters. The wolf, controlled by dice rolls, can appear at any time to huff and puff away resources or the hay or wood homes. Pigs must strategize when to build, when to gather, and when to disrupt their opponents, as only the first to complete all three homes will claim victory.

With a mix of strategy, luck, and player interaction, Little Pig Little Pig offers a playful yet competitive experience where timing and tactics are key. Whether dodging the wolf or outmaneuvering fellow pigs, every decision matters in this race to build.

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Three Little Pigs

description:
In the game, we take on the role of a knight and race against opponents through a maze of doors, where a surprise awaits behind every corner. Thanks to action cards, we open and close passages so as to be the first to get out of the Dungeon, leaving opponents behind. A clever strategy and the right cards will allow us to slow down opponents and use the advantage.

—description from the publisher (translated)

W rozgrywce wcielamy się w rolę rycerza i ścigajmy się z przeciwnikami przez labirynt drzwi, gdzie za każdym rogiem czeka niespodzianka. Dzięki kartom akcji otwierajmy i zamykamy przejścia, tak by jako pierwszy wydostać się z Lochu zostawiając w tyle przeciwników. Sprytna strategia i właściwe karty pozwolą nam spowolnić przeciwników i wykorzystać przewagę.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: てか、ただのゆるい日常じゃないですか! (Isn't this just a relaxed daily life?)
description:
Be the policeman to catch the thief, or the thief to steal from the rich musician, or the musician to bribe the policeman. This is a simple card game in which players win by playing a pair of main character cards (policeman, thief or musician) and successfully catching their target.

Players have a hand of three cards. On your turn, you play a card to use its effect, and then draw a card. The action of the policeman, thief and musician is only triggered when you play your second card. You must then choose a player and see whether they have the target you need. If they do, you win. Otherwise you lose one of your cards and the game continues.

Other than the three main characters, there are 13 other characters in the game with different abilities. Some let you swap cards with other players, some let you see cards of other players.

Vår planet ("Our planet") is a game full of practical ideas and ways to make a difference for our planet. You can learn about the factors affecting environmental decision making where individual actions have less influence.

The resources of our planet are plentiful but limited. It is time for us to show our gratitude and take care of it. Let us act for sustainability together through positivity.

Complete the assignments you've been given by our planet. Answer questions from four categories together. Correct answers leads you closer to saving the planet while incorrect ones will put environmental damage in your way. Remember, the only way to save our planet is to do it together.

The questions of the game have been developed in cooperation with students from University of Lapland, Finland.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

This is a board game created by the Federal Senate as part of its bicentenary celebrations, initially being distributed to Brazilian public schools.

Each player takes on the role of an undergraduate student, receiving training in some area of knowledge that gives them a small asymmetrical power valid during the game, with the ultimate goal of being elected a senator, effectively winning the game.

The game has a path that leads to the electoral circle by rolling and walking, performing the action of the space where your movement ends. However, before carrying out the location action, players can use action cards that can have various effects, including altering their position and that of the other players on the board.

The actions of the spaces on the board are quite simple, ranging from moving more or less to the most important, the Brazilian general knowledge questions.

To answer a question, an opponent draws a card from the pile and reads it aloud. The player in turn gives their answer and, if they get it right, they receive a number of votes (usually 50,000). There is no penalty for getting it wrong.

A player wins the game by reaching the constituency, accumulating at least 500,000 votes and being elected senator.

The dinosaurs love to eat leafs and mushrooms. Place your tiles on the board in that way, that as many edges as possible touch an according edge on a tile already placed. The better you do this, the further your pawn will advance on the scoring track.

Humanity as we know it has ended. The origins of the pathogen that erased more than 99% of the population remain unknown, but its aftermath is clearly visible to every survivor.

While settlements are being founded by the remaining refugees, you've been appointed as the Leader of a small encampment. Are you up to the task? Can you keep your people safe, while facing new disasters?

Gameplay Overview
By the End is a worker placement, city building survival game. During the course of the game you'll assign your workers to various actions, like building districts, improvements, researching technologies and more. Be sure to also keep discontent and hunger at bay, or your rule may end.
By the End is a scenario driven game: fulfill its objective before time runs out or you are deposed. Scenario may also alter setup and rules to fit their needs.
The game is portable, can be played in 10-15 minutes and offers deep strategy.

—description from the designer

The Tennessee Central Railway Company (TC) underwent many ownership changes over the course of its operation. During this game, each player secretly holds company shares that are gradually revealed and reacquired by other players. Building track affects the value of each company, so time your investments carefully with locomotives. The player with the most money wins the game.

Deck Express is an exciting series of railroad investment games where players purchase stocks, build track, and profit from locomotives. Each rail line features a unique train game, custom artwork, and rulebook using a standard deck of playing cards for the game components. All aboard!

It's a light strategy game with a touch of luck. Players take on the role of one of eight cricket teams competing in a championship. In each round, players bat and bowl one over using six dice. As the game progresses, players gain experience, allowing them to enhance their skills. The game lasts for five rounds (45-60 minutes) and offers four different ways to earn winning points.


    Game does not require cricket knowledge to play.


-description from publisher

This expansion comprises 12 new Crew cards that can be enlisted to accompany you on your seafaring escapades. Now, during your turn, you can discreetly hire a Crew member. Once revealed, your Crew will offer you one of their special skills every turn.

In addition, 5 new base cards have been added for a little extra spice

This brings the total to 80 unique designs and 106 cards in total. Additionally, this edition contains 4 rules cards to help you get started with your first game.

Minecraft Labyrinth plays much like the original Labyrinth game. Each turn, you slide a tile into the maze of walls to try to create a path to whichever object you need to claim this turn. To stay on theme, each player has a pickaxe that they can use once during the game to dismantle a closed wall and walk through it.

Translated from the blurb:

Ever since the Murdio Island City Council decided to abolish the police force for financial reasons, the island has become a center of crime. You, the police students, are the last resort and step up to prevent the worst from happening.

Jacob Funfair is beside himself: someone is blackmailing him and his theme park. Five Banners Murdio Island, If the park manager doesn't pay a ransom of 200,000 dollars, unknown persons want to cause serious accidents in the park. She has two tasks on site: Discover the sabotage and find out who is blackmailing the park. But you have to hurry - time is running out.

Murdio Island: the interactive audio game
- Interactive gameplay with game board and free audio app: combination of board game and audio game
- Easy to start the game, no long preparation: a tutorial playfully explains the game principle
- Exciting puzzles and combinations - alone, with friends or with the whole family.


Klappentext:Seit der Stadtrat von Murdio Island beschlossen hat, die Polizei aus finanziellen Gründen abzuschaffen, ist die Insel zu einem Zentrum des Verbrechens geworden. Ihr, die Polizeischülerinnen und -schüler, seid die letzte Rettung und tretet an, um das Schlimmste zu verhindern.

Jacob Funfair ist außer sich: Irgendjemand erpresst ihn und seinen Freizeitpark. Five Banners Murdio Island, Wenn der Parkleiter nicht 200.000$ Lösegeld zahlt, wollen Unbekannte schwere Unfälle im Park auslösen. Vor Ort habt ihr gleich zwei Aufgaben: Entdeckt die Sabotage und findet heraus, wer den Park erpresst. Doch ihr müsst euch beeilen - die Zeit drängt-

Murdio Island: Das interaktive Audio-Game
- Interaktives Gameplay mit Spielbrett und kostenfreier Audio-App: Kombination aus Brettspiel und Hörspiel
- Einfacher Spielstart, keine lange Vorbereitung: Ein Tutorial erklärt spielerisch das Spielprinzip
- Spannendes Rätseln und Kombination - allein, mit Freunden und Freundinnen oder mit der gesamten Familie.

It is May 1917 and you are in command of No. 10 (Naval) Squadron. A few weeks ago, the Royal Flying Corps (RFC) experienced the disaster of "Bloody April". Approximately 250 pilots were lost in combat against Germany’s best, including the famous Red Baron, Manfred von Richthofen. The RFC begged for relief and the Royal Naval Air Service (RNAS) stepped in to hold the line.

Everyone expected a massacre. The leadership of the RFC, expected that “Bloody April” would extend and “Bloody Summer” would follow as the hastily assembled relief squadrons from the Royal Navy, almost all Canadian pilots, were swept from the skies. Yet the Canadians of RNAS proved everyone wrong. Not only did they not get massacred, they turned the tide.

One squadron among them, Naval 10, as it was called, bore the brunt of the challenge. Within that squadron, one Flight – just five pilots and aeroplanes –  known as “Black Flight” and lead by Flight Sub-Lieut. Raymond Collishaw, took to the skies over the Ypres Salient and set a record in aerial combat that stands to this day – in eleven weeks, Naval 10 downed 87 German aeroplanes, many of them drawn from among Germany’s finest.

Today, despite being the highest scoring naval aviator of all time, the name of Raymond Collishaw is little known. In skill, he rivalled the prowest of the Red Baron himself. Flying his Sopwith Triplane, over the course of those eleven weeks, personally he downed 30 of the Germans.

Black Flight is a solitaire air war game set in the Great War, often called the First World War. As with all wargames from Historic Wings, Black Flight is deeply researched. You learn about history as you play the game – not the Hollywood version, but the real thing. The rules are built to mirror history, carefully crafted to recreate the true circumstances faced by Collishaw’s Canadian pilots. Yet the outcomes are not guaranteed. You will be called on to fly historic missions across the trenches and over No Man’s Land – and beyond, where the Germans wait patiently with their Albatros and Halberstadt scout planes.

Your orders will reflect the actual patrols of the time – OPs, Offensive Patrols that penetrated deep behind enemy lines; LPs, Line Patrols that ran north and south over the trenches; escort missions where you protect bombers, reconnaissance aeroplanes, photo reconnaissance flights, and artillery direction missions. The routes you fly will be historically accurate – yet through the careful construction of weekly changing tables, each game will be a new and different experience, ensuing extraordinary replayability.

At the core of the game is that you manage your own Naval 10 squadron. You will recruit your pilots and each will have unique characteristics as defined by a mix of 45 Trait Cards and 45 Vice Cards that give each the very strengths and weaknesses that reflected the times. With they lived in. Each time you play, your squadron will be different, your challenges will be unique, and your struggles will be memorable. Unique among aviation games today, Black Flight recreates the diverse personalities of the pilots themselves – some may be reckless, others ruthless, in the wake of bad experiences, their condition will deteriorate. Your best ace pilot, seeing one to many of his friends shot from the sky, may take to alcohol. Your most trusted and reliable cornerstone of the squadron may suffer a nervous breakdown. Yet the orders keep coming. Just as experienced by the real Naval 10 from May through July 1917, your losses will mount and your men will become progressively exhausted and stressed.

Black Flight is the first in a series of solitaire Great War titles from Historic Wings that, as the series is published, will enable players to fly against one another – pitting the Canadians of the RNAS against the Imperial German Air Serice, taking to the skies with the RFC, or, between glasses of wine, kicking the tires of your Nieuports and flying into the teeth of battle. Each nationality will have its own unique Trait Cards and Vice Cards that reflect the times, the traditions, and the historic personalities of each.

Suitable for gameplay with miniatures – including with 1/350th or larger models, right up to 1/72 if you have a dining table playing space!!

Will the fifteen pilots of your own Naval 10 fare better than Collishaw? Will your leadership help turn the tide against the Germans? Will you reverse the disaster of “Bloody April” and open the way for the upcoming Messines and Ypres offensives or will you experience a “Bloody Summer of 1917”?

For God, King, England, and the Commonwealth!

-description from designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Series: Air War Book Games (Historic Wings)

description: This entertaining memory game is a fun way to train your memory. A color is hidden under each game piece. This is only revealed when the game piece is removed from the board. The color dice determines which color you have to find. However, if a piece of the wrong color is removed from the board, it must be put back and the next player takes their turn. Your memory is therefore required throughout the game. Remember the wrong colors well so that you can score points in the next round.

boardgame: Syncro: Extension Chapitre III – La Menace Venue de l'Océan
description:
A sea monster threatens the coasts. As a mage, protector of the kingdom, you must face the danger.

This third chapter is the continuation of Syncro
You are a mage who has taken a vow of silence. You will have to face an unprecedented threat and fight monsters that endanger the kingdom.

Cooperate without speaking to defeat all the monsters!


     At the beginning of the game, communicate the potential of your cards with a gesture.
     In turn, place a face-down card on a monster of your choice.
     At the end of a turn, reveal the cards placed on the monsters and find out if your attacks were powerful enough to defeat them.


You must estimate the value played by your fellow players while having limited communication and information.

As the game progresses, you will advance in the adventure and unlock new effects that will spice up your games.

This expansion brings a more difficult challenges, new monsters, a new Boss and a hard mode for Chapter 2.

The primary goal in Happy Heights is to strategically match symbols between adjacent buildings while efficiently managing your building plans, reserve options, and placement opportunities to maximize your final score.

Each card in Happy Heights represents one of the following:

Building Cards: 
Serve as the foundational level of a multi-story building or can be stacked/built an existing building to add additional levels.

Storefront (Shop) Cards: 
These feature random symbols on the left and right sides and a multiplier icon on the top, along with a distinctive sloped roof. A shop can function as a standalone storefront or, when stacked, as the penthouse floor of a building. Once a shop becomes a penthouse, it loses its shop status and no further floors can be added on top. The multipliers on these cards score based on the number of unbroken, connected symbols from the bottom floor upward.

Park and Bus Stop Cards:
These cards do not have symbols. Instead, they serve to exponentially increase in point value the more parks or bus stops you have in your neighbourhood.
Each card also displays bonus indicators (for example, “worth 2 points if your neighbourhood has a butcher shop”), adding extra layers of strategy and scoring opportunities.

Scoring & Objective
At the end of the game, when each player has drafted their 12th card, scoring is conducted based on several criteria:

Buildings:
Earn 1 point for each main floor building constructed, plus additional bonus points as indicated on the cards.

Adjacent Matching Symbols:
Gain 1 point for every matching symbol between adjacent buildings.

Parks and Bus Stops:
These cards score exponentially, so a well-planned cluster can significantly boost your score.

Penthouses:
Multipliers on penthouse floors contribute extra points, based on the connected symbols from the bottom floor upward.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game – Marvel Studios: What If...? – Season 2
description:
Ready to return to the Multiverse? Legendary What If…? Season 2 expansion adds to the Heroes, Villains, and Masterminds first introduced to Legendary in the Legendary What If...? core set. Prepare to meet the Mohawk hero Kahhori with the ability to reshape reality with her powers, and the first-in-game Avengers Across Time, a Hero set with 14 unique cards. Utilize powerful new keywords like Reshape, Showoff, and more! This set is compatible with all previous Legendary Marvel core sets and expansions.

Features:


     Cards will feature images from the popular television series.
     Happy “The Freak” Hogan, Kahhori, and Avengers from across space and time join the Legendary lineup!
     4 New Heroes, 3 New Masterminds, and 3 New Villain Groups!
     New Keywords like Reshape, Showoff and more!
     Requires a Marvel Legendary Core Set to play.


—description from the publihser

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Black Panther Hero Pack
description:
As princess of Wakanda, Shuri aided her brother and king, T’Challa, through her genius intellect and talent for invention. When T’Challa was severely injured, Shuri took up the mantle of the Black Panther and became the protector of her homeland in his stead, and now she’s darting into your games of Marvel Champions: The Card Game! With her versatile array of signature upgrades and an adaptable fighting style, Shuri is a potent hero that can take on any foe. Within this hero pack, you will find Black Panther (Shuri), her fifteen signature cards, and a full assortment of Justice cards inviting you to thwart the villains’ schemes. This pack also includes a bonus modular encounter set featuring Joystick of the Thunderbolts!

Anyone looking to take on the agents of A.I.M. will want to add the Black Panther Hero Pack to their collection! This expansion comes with a 40-card pre-built deck, giving you the chance to start playing from the moment you open the box.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

description:
Nomenclutter has 144 small square (2.5" x 2.5") cards: 

     33 Starting (letter) cards
     99 Letter cards 
     12 Action cards


Plus: 

     17 Secret Mission cards


The starting cards are shuffled, and 25 of them laid out to make a 5x5 grid of letters.  Each player gets a hand of 7 letter cards, plus 3 action cards, and 4 secret mission cards, of which they keep 3.  On their turn, a player places 1-2 letter cards from their hand on top of letters in the grid, and optionally uses one of their action cards. Then if possible they reveal one of their secret missions, and point out 2 words of 3+ letters in the grid that match that mission (e.g. "Colors", "Things in the sea"). The letters in the word must be in a straight line, in any direction.  The first player to complete all 3 of their missions wins the game.

Nomenclutter is available through TheGameCrafter.com.

Nicholas, the benevolent and generous Bishop of Myra, has just been removed as Bishop, and a new bishop must be named!

Players compete for a chance to be named the next Bishop. Since Bishop Nicholas was known for his generosity and gift-giving you must show a willingness to both give and humbly receive gifts.

Players in Bishop of Myra play 12 tricks, aka rounds, with must-follow trick-taking rules going clockwise around the table each round. The player who plays the card with the highest number in the lead suit wins each round.

The player(s) who do not win the trick receive gift cards that give the players special powers or are used to create sets to earn points.

—description from the designer

"Destroy the biosphere in the quest for your PARTY(Kalas)" - A family game about climate change.

The objective of the game is to prepare a party. The players embark on exciting air travels around the world. The journey goes to poor countries where conflict minerals are mined, to the amazon that's being deforested and so on.

Two to six players fly around the world to gather letters to form the word KALAS (party). The letters represent things needed for a party, for example palm oil, lithium and cocoa. Whoever collects all the letters and return to the goal wins.

PARTY/KALAS is a satirical game about pollution and climate change. Lars Arrhenius and Eric Ericsson have earlier designed the board games "Welcome to society" and "Panama" that are both part of the collections of Norrköping art museum.

“Life is a temporary lapse, we are eternal. Our horde increases with the bodies of our enemies. We do not rest, we do not surrender, we are the Death.”

Zarokk’s words as he watches the advance of his hordes of Raiders across the fields of Albea

Each side is different in this fantasy world in which two Heroes fight for control of the battlefield.

Pikemen, musketeers, golems and the rest of the troops of the dwarven army will face reborns, fallen, chimeras and the great horde of the Risen with agile, fast and full of strategy games.

With a 3 and 4 players system in its box, as well as an advanced mode where you can configure your deck of cards.

News in Drums of War – Conquest
A stand-alone installment, it does not require TdG Enclave to enjoy 2-player games.
Expands Drums of Wars – Enclave, it contains game rules for 3 and 4 players. Join both games and challenge your friends.
Brings 2 new complete armies: Dwarves and Risen armies.
Cooperative: Includes a cooperative game mode to play against the AI of the solitaire mode (2 players vs. AI).
You can configure your armies: Includes an advanced game mode that allows you to configure your army as you wish, and you can even use cards from differtent armies in your deck.

—description from the publisher

«La vida es un lapso temporal, nosotros somos eternos. Nuestra horda aumenta con los cuerpos de nuestros enemigos. No descansamos, no nos rendimos, somos la Muerte»
Palabras de Zarokk mientras observa el avance de sus hordas de Alzados por los campos de Albea.
Cada bando es diferente en este mundo de fantasía en el que dos Héroes luchan por el control del campo de batalla.

Piqueros, mosqueteros, gólems y el resto de tropas del ejército enano se enfrentarán a renacidos, caídos, quimeras y la gran horda de alzados con partidas ágiles, rápidas y cargadas de estrategia.

Con un sistema de juego para 3 y 4 jugadores incluido en la caja, así como un modo avanzado donde podrás configurar tu mazo de cartas.

Novedades de Tambores de Guerra – Conquista
Un juego independiente, no necesita de TdG Enclave para disfrutar partidas a 2 jugadores.
A la vez expande Tambores de Guerra: Enclave, pues contiene reglas de juego para 3 y 4 jugadores. Une ambos juegos y reta a tus amigos.
Contiene 2 nuevos ejércitos completos: Enanos y Alzados.
Cooperativo: Incluye un modo de juego cooperativo para jugar contra la Inteligencia Artificial del modo solitario (2 jugadores vs IA).
Podrás configurar tus ejércitos: Con un modo de juego avanzado que te permite configurar tu ejército a tu elección, puedes incluso utilizar cartas de varios ejércitos en tu mazo.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Gigantic portals open in the sky above Tokyo:

MasterMindbug and other monsters have traveled from another dimension to lay claim to the city. However, they are not alone: small, tentacled mindcontrolling creatures, the Mindbugs, are coming to your aid.

What could be their powers to ensure their domination of Tokyo?

—description from the publisher

Makara, tailored exclusively for the Turkish market, is a dynamic party game inspired by the public domain game Celebrities. Makara 90s Edition stands out with its strong focus on nostalgic Turkish cultural nuances and humor, making it particularly enjoyable and relatable for those familiar with Turkish traditions and jokes.

The game boasts an extensive collection of 250 cards, each card being a gateway to laughter and fun. These cards are divided into 7 distinct categories, encompassing a wide range of words that resonate with various aspects of Turkish life and culture.

In the first round, players try to describe these personalities or concepts without mentioning their names, weaving in culturally specific references and humor. The second round challenges players to convey the same with just a single word. Finally, in the most exhilarating round, verbal communication is completely off-limits, and players must resort to gestures and charades, often drawing upon shared cultural experiences and jokes unique to the Turkish audience. This structure makes Makara not just a game of guessing but a celebration of Turkish culture and wit.

boardgame: ÜBERmorgen: Ein Spiel um die Zukunft
description:
In ÜBERmorgen, you take on the role of a super-rich space traveler and must survive together (cooperatively) with your fellow players for three years on a deserted planet in space. Together you build the city of your dreams. But beware: conflict is inevitable! Limited resources and angry teammates can make things difficult for you. Only those who manage to reconcile all interests and overcome the major challenges of the future together will be victorious in the end.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In ÜBERmorgen schlüpfst du in die Rolle eines superreichen Weltraumreisenden und musst gemeinsam (kooperativ) mit deinen Mitspielenden drei Jahre auf einem verlassenen Planeten im Weltraum überleben. Gemeinsam baut ihr euch die Stadt eurer Träume. Aber Achtung: Konflikt ist vorprogrammiert! Begrenzte Ressourcen und wütende Mitspieler*innen können dir ganz schön zu schaffen machen. Nur wem es gelingt, alle Interessen unter einen Hut zu bringen und gemeinsam die großen Herausforderungen der Zukunft zu meistern, kann am Ende den Sieg davon tragen.

—description from the publisher

Serve up some excitement with the Order Up! Diner Game Set — the fast-paced stacking game everyone will enjoy!

Experience the fast-food frenzy with Order Up! Diner, the stacking game that will have you serving the most delicious dishes in the quickest time. Games Hub brings you this high-speed conveyor belt challenge where timing is key. In this game, players work to stack orders of delicious dishes before the conveyor belt takes them away. It's the perfect blend of strategy and fun for family game nights or competitive play with friends. Get ready to serve up some entertainment with Order Up! Diner.

—description from the publisher

You are fresh-faced adventurers earning renown by encountering monsters in the Dungeon. Build a stylish outfit by collecting equipment from the Stylomancer, then use your sweet style to romance, befriend, and slay monsters!

Dungeon Date: Roll with Style is a roll-and-write game based on the humorous card game Dungeon Date. Each turn, you will roll four dice, then each player will simultaneously choose one die result to use as an action on their sheet. To play, you will need four six-sided dice of the following colors: green, purple, orange, brown.

Any four dice of different colors will do in a pinch – just note on your sheet which die you will use for each monster/ treasure color. For instance, you might use a red die for orange monsters and equipment, a blue die for green, or a black die for brown.

When at least 1 player has crossed off all of the boxes in one of the dungeon locations, play 1 more turn and calculate your scores. For each section (Romance, Befriend, Slay), score points by having multiple monsters of the same type. You also score points for how well your equipment represents your personal style. Whoever has the most points wins the game!
family: Versions & Editions: Dice Versions of Non-Dice Games
Versions & Editions: Roll- or Flip-and-Write Versions of Non-Writing Games

description:
Chios 2025 is a small, battalion scale wargame about hypothetical conflict between Turkey and Greece and battle for the key island Chios which lays pretty close to the Turkish coast, yet is controlled by Greece.

Game is a part of the WW3 system by TiS and uses its mechanics. Ownership of any game of the series is required to play, because it comes with no rules, only with board, counters and scenario set up.

Chios 2025 was published in the 50th issue of Taktyka i Strategia magazine.

Alternative history game that starts with an attack of a combined Russian & North Korean force against north-eastern Poland and Lithuania, followed up with a NATO counteroffensive. The NATO forces include a Polish, US, Czech, German and Lithuanian contingents and a small free Tatar force.

The game features two large scenarios (divided into 2 smaller sub-scenarios each), the first focuses on the Russian offensive south, the second one on the NATO counteroffensive. Victory points are scored for control of key cities around the map.

During the KS campaign for Nova Aetas Renaissance, we decided to indulge our love of crossovers, and some of the unlocked Stretch Goals were designed to be played not only in NAR but also in our other flagship game, Black Rose Wars and its sequel, Rebirth!

BRW and NAR are set in more or less the same historical period (one of several that make up our world's lore), and some of the main characters are present in both games, such as our beloved Rebecca, a young Apprentice mage in NAR and a powerful Necromancer with a dark destiny in BRW.

So it was a lot of fun for us to create these crossovers, which we then put into a special package called the Magister Box, which you can find right inside the NAR Core Box.

—description from the publisher

When used in Black Rose Wars: Rebirth / Black Rose Wars, this box adds:
 - 3 mages (Agorix, Boogeyman and Onamor).
 - 1 Personal Familiar (Eracleto) that Agorix can summon with his Personal Spell.
 - 3 Personal Evocations (3 Doll) that Boogeyman can summon with his Personal Spell.
 - 2 Forgotten Evocations (Jack [the Scarecrow] and Chimera), which can be summoned with the two Forgotten Spells included in this box.

When used in Nova Aetas Renaissance, this box adds:
 - 1 Druid Hero (Agorix) + his personal familiar (Eracleto).
 - 6 Enemy miniatures with their respective cards: 3 Doll, 1 Technomancer, 1 Scarecrow and 1 Dark Magician.
 - 1 special Enemy that will be the protagonist of a Hunt Mission during the NAR campaign, the Chimera.

Bitwy XXI Wieku (Battles of XXIth Century) is a wargame system taking place in the first decades of XXIth century. The game contains maps for northern Poland (Malbork area), Ukraine (Hostomel area), Belarus (Grodno area) and Kinmen Island. The units belong to the Polish, Russian, American, Chinese, Taiwanes and, Ukrainian armies.

The counters use NATO symbols, while each units has points table for different equipment for different conflicts. Each hex represents one kilometer and each counter represents a company.

Game is the third instalment of the series, after part I of the series and WOT 2025.

Friedland, Heilsberg, Mohrungen 1807 simulates the Battle of Friedland, one of the biggest battles of the "First Polish War", fought on 14 June 1807, between Napoleon's Grande Armée and the Imperial Russian Army, who were reinforced by Prussians.

The game box also contains a few additional maps (and counters) for two other battles that took place at that time in that area: battle of Heilsberg and battle of Mohrungen.

Game uses TiS Napoleon system mechanics with new, updated combat mechanics.

Game simulates hypothetical Russian naval landing operation against Denmark's forces at Bornholm in the modern era.

Game uses 21st Century Battles System, ownership of any game of the series is required to play, since game comes with board, counters, tables and scenario set up only.

The Battle of Pułtusk took place on April 21, 1703 in Pułtusk during the Great Northern War. The Swedish army under the command of Charles XII defeated the Saxon army under Adam Heinrich von Steinau.

This is smaller scale battle for the Husaria system by TiS. Ownership of any game of the series is required, since game comes with board, counters and set up only.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Today is a good day to die... for the enemy crew!

Battlefront Valkyrie is a groundbreaking 3D printable wargame that delivers the thrill of epic fleet battles right to your tabletop, all at a cost that's just a fraction of traditional games!

This expansion set includes: Kurgun C-5A Heavy Cruiser and D-3A Destroyer models and ship cards.

All models are optimized for printing on FDM printers and print without supports.

Expand your Battlefront Valkyrie game with other Expansion Sets and have massive starship battles.

—description from the publisher

Commonagrams includes: 

     51 consonant cards
     21 vowel cards
     17 extra credit cards
     10 notebook cards
     10 dry erase markers
     1 sand timer


Each player gets a marker and a notebook card to write their words on. The decks are shuffled, and 3 extra credit cards are revealed, showing goals for players to achieve, e.g. "Most 4-letter words", "Most words with 2 vowels", "Longest word with 1 vowel".

There are 8 rounds. In each round a (gradually decreasing) number of consonant and vowel cards are set out, after which players have 30 seconds to write a word using at least 3 of the exposed letters.  Each player whose word for that round matches the word of 1 other player gets 1 point; if it matches the words of 2+ other players they get 2 points.

After all 8 rounds, the player(s) whose words best fulfill each extra credit card gets 1 point for that card. The player with the most points overall wins the game.

Piskorzowice 1945 is a small game about the fierce combat between Soviet and German units around village Piskorzowice (Peiskerwitz) near Breslau at the end of january 1945. Game uses company scale.

Game uses B-35 (Battles of WW2 1943-45) system, ownership of any game of the series is required, since game comes with board, counters and scenario set up only.

Piskorzowice 1945 was published in 50th issue of Taktyka i Strategia magazine.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Bitwy II wojny światowej 1943-45 (Dragon / Taktyka i Strategia)

description:
Glowno 1939 is a small game, covering attack of the Polish Wielkopolska Cavalry Brigade on the positions of German 10th Infantry Division during the battle of Bzura, the biggest battle of the Polish campaign 1939.

Game uses B-35 (Battles of WW2) system, ownership of any game of the system is required, since game comes with board, counters and scenario set up only.

Game was published in the 50th issue of Taktyka i Strategia magazine.
family: Series: Bitwy II wojny światowej 1943-45 (Dragon / Taktyka i Strategia)

description: Mount Cleverest gameplay is so simple. Deal out the true or false tokens, and take turns to read out facts. Everyone bets on whether the fact is true or false, and you lose your token if you are wrong. The last person with a token is the winner.

boardgame: Camino a Xibalba: La senda del sacerdote
description:
The Path of the Priest is a solitary strategy game, in which you must walk a path made up of 14 cards, composed of 8 masters and 6 false masters. The objective is to take the apprentice (card 0) from the beginning of the path to the end and connect with the deity (become adjacent to it).

La Senda del Sacerdote es un juego solitario de estrategia, en el cual debes caminar una senda conformada por 14 cartas, compuesta por 8 maestros y 6 falsos maestros. El objetivo es llevar al aprendiz (carta 0) desde el inicio de la senda hasta el final y conectar con la deidad (quedar adyacente a ella).
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Spellebration has 45 letter cards, each with a vowel and its point value on one side and a consonant with its value on the other; and 6 colored sets of 6 markers.  Each player takes one set of markers and is dealt 6 cards, consonant side down, and then N+1 cards (N = number of players) are dealt to the table in a column, consonant side up.

In their turn a player plays a card from their hand to either end of one of the N+1 rows, and places a marker of their color on it. Each letter placed must make a valid word or build toward a valid word.  Instead of playing, a player may pass and then discard any number of cards and draw that same number from the deck.  The hand continues until every player passes consecutively, and then each row of letters is scored.

In any row, if it is a complete word, the player who has the most markers on cards in that row gets points equal to the sum of all that word's letters' values. The other players get the sum of the values of the cards with their markers on them. If there is a tie for the most markers in a word, the total value of the word is split among the tied players.  If a row does not make a complete word, then the player who last played a card to that row loses points equal to the total points in that row; a player's total for a hand may not be less than zero.

After an agreed-on number of hands (e.g. 3 or more), the player with the highest total points from all the hands wins the game.

KONIVRER (pronounced Conjurer) is a strategic Trading Card Game where players build powerful, single-copy decks using a strict rarity-based construction limit. The game combines streamlined combat and resource management and randomized 40-card decks made from a pool of 60 cards.

Key Features:

Single-Copy Deck Construction:

Decks are built using a 40-card limit, with no duplicates allowed. This forces players to explore the full card pool and build versatile, flexible strategies.
Players must carefully select their cards, balancing power, synergy, and versatility within the constraints of the deck construction limit.
Rarity-Based Deck Construction Limit:

Players must adhere to a strict rarity-based deck construction limit: 25 Commons, 13 Uncommons, and 2 Rares.
This rarity restriction adds a layer of strategy to deck-building, as players must decide how to allocate their limited higher-rarity slots for maximum impact. The commons will form the backbone of the deck, while uncommons provide critical support, and rares are the game-changing powerhouses.
The limitation encourages creativity and thoughtful deck composition, as players need to find the right balance between consistency and high-impact plays.

    Dynamic Deck Generation *:


While players can build their decks manually, they can also opt to use randomized decks, These decks are unique, offering a pre-built experience that still fits within the construction requirements.
Streamlined Combat and Resource Management:

The game uses a simplified resource system where cards can either be played for their effects or used as resources to play other cards. This system ensures that gameplay is fast-paced and accessible, yet deep and strategic.
Combat is centered around creatures, with each player trying to break through their opponent’s defenses and have them take one of their life cards.
Resource Management and Life Card System:

Players have a Resource Pool that grows each turn, enabling them to play increasingly powerful cards. The Life System is a player’s life total, requiring opponents to break through their defenses before they can attack directly.
Each life card can have hidden effects that trigger when taken, adding an element of surprise and strategic depth to the game.
Element Identity:

The game features 6 elements, each with its unique playstyle and mechanics. Players can mix and match cards from different elements within the constraints of the deck-building rules, creating hybrid strategies that leverage the strengths of multiple elements.
Some elements might focus on aggressive strategies, while others might excel in control, combo, or defensive playstyles.

—description from the designer

In Arboro, you’re an employee at a paper company, ambitious to make the most profit and become ‘Papermaker of the Month'.

Managing your resources and general productivity is key. You’ll also need to manage the papermaking process, planting trees, making pulp or manufacturing paper. Too much focus on one area can create inconsistency, so you’ll need to keep a balanced view.

Although every player is competing to be the best, player actions are connected, so you must also work together to sustain the circular economy. No pulp means no paper, no planting means no trees, and so on. Meanwhile, don’t forget that everyone needs access to essential resources such as water and energy.

In Arboro you play actions cards in order to go through the process of producing paper. With the produced pulp, energy and water you make paper, that you can sell to various customers and win by making the most money.

Arboro is a promotional game from the paper manufacturer Sappi. Created as a Sappi original concept and developed in partnership with board game specialists Game Brewer.

The game is designed to show the progressive route racers take by working their way through Stock classes on through Super Stock, and so on through Alcohol Dragster. The game was designed to give young people an idea of how drag racing is structured.

—description from the rulebook

The game is based on a simple roll and move around a monopoly type board purchasing car upgrades…. Owning all three upgrades for a class will provide better dividends, landing on race day or Nationals event spaces which are resolved by drawing cards for results or rolling on a players tech card to get results of a race.
Race Day: Draw Cards for results
National Events: Roll on Tech Card to determine class and results.

The rules are one page and are very vague needing a little more explanation.

—user summary

boardgame: Persuasion: a Journey of Two Hearts

description:
Join one of the Critter Clans and challenge your friends to a game of wits and focus. Will you team up with the Gnomes, the Realm’s magical builders, or explore the unknown deep in the Realm’s waters with the Merfolk? Will you join the Pixies and soar through the skies, play games high in the treetops with the Woodfolk, or become a knight with the Dino clan?

Whichever you decide, in your journey you are not alone. The Clan Heroes, human adventurers like you, will assist you in your time of need.

The Critter Realm is a magical place with so much to discover!

Includes:
25 Gnome Cards
25 Merfolk Cards
2 Hero Cards
2 Spell Decks with 6 cards each
Full-colour rule book

Barbarity is a competitive card adventure game themed around barbarians in popular culture, such as Robert E. Howard stories, and '80's sword and sorcery films and comics. It's very brutal and gory, but played for laughs, and has plenty of easter eggs referencing the films and stories it's based upon.

Each player is represented by a hero card in the game, and the objective is to win Prize Cards, which can be useful gear, companions, pets (think giant boars and wolves, rather than kitties and puppers) or land. Prize Cards have a victory point value and abilities that can be called upon after they have been captured. Opponents can use battle cards and special cards to try and prevent players claiming prize cards, so players must play their own special cards and battle cards to counter and defeat these.

The game ends when a player collects five prize cards, victory points are calculated, and the player with the highest number wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Warfighter: Chtorr Expansion #1 – Show Low, AZ
description:
Expansion #1 Show Low, Arizona

Soldiers, Weapons, Gear, Skills, Missions, Locations, Objectives, and of course lots of Cthorran hostiles from Kites to Nerve Burners to a Swarm of Stingflies.

Expansion #2 Gallup NM

Soldiers, Weapons, Gear, Skills, Missions, Locations, Objectives, and of course lots of Cthorran hostiles from Lone Worms, to Scavenging Civilians, to Night Stalkers.

Everything you need to experience the battle of Gallup, NM.

Expansion #3 Gear Up!

More Skills, including – Hyper Focused, Precise Shots, and Combat Medic.

More Gear, including – Pressurized Tank, Optic Scope, and a Chest Rig.

and of course, More Hostiles, including – Charging Snufflers, Enforcers, and Shrike Vines.

boardgame: Warfighter: Chtorr Campaign Expansion #1 – San Francisco, CA
description:
Campaign #1 San Francisco

They built a nest near San Francisco, and you need to burn it out. The Chtorrans seem to be everywhere. They survive in every environment.

Take your Humvee and head out through the dangerous landscape to find and eliminate the deadly hostiles. Stay in contact with Command so they can send you necessary Gear and help you find your targets.

boardgame: Warfighter: Chtorr Campaign Expansion #2 – Boulder, CO
description:
Campaign #2 Boulder, Colorado

A Huge Chtorran Worm has made its home near Boulder, Colorado. It’s up to you and your team to remove it before it and its fellow Chtorran hostiles consume their favorite meal – Humans.

Use the gear available to you, including  the M20 Flamer, the Dragonsbreath Shotgun, C1 Cryo Shard Pistol, M10 Incendiary Rifle,  and an entire 40MM Grenade Belt.

Battle your way through Boulder, increasing your Skills, Abilities and Gear as you level up from levels 1 through 6.

Bor 2013 covers the fights that took place in the southern Sudan at december 2013 around the city of Bor, where rebel groups of SPLA-IO started their rebelion and attacked goverment's foces, loyal to SPLA party.

Game uses B-21 mechanics. Ownership of any game of the series is required, since game comes with board, counters and scenarios set ups only. Game contains 4 scenarios, covering various stages of the battle.

Game was published in th 48th issue of Taktka i Strategia magazine.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Liw 1831 covers small scale battle between Polish and Russian foces near the town of Liw at april 1831, during the Polish Nobember Uprising 1831.

Game uses Napoleon system. Ownership of any game of the series is required, since game comes with board, counters and scenarios set ups only. There are two scenarios, covering different stages of the battle.

Game was published in the 48th issue of Taktyka i Strategia magazine.

Harry Estate is a negotiation-based game from designer Emilio Samuel, set in the backdrop of 20th century America in which members of a corporate family struggle to consolidate their position as the heir to the family’s patriarch, Mr. Harry.

Each round, after the Patriarch announces a decree, the current Successor sets a Deal, vote on whether this deal is good for the family, then Invest on it, and finally Profit off of it.

In this negotiation game, whoever is the current Successor at the end of the 6th round wins.

—description from the designer

You’ve got an opportunity to build the biggest and best casino this city’s ever seen. Take a chance on your own skill and hopefully it’ll pay off.

Bet on yourself with Place Your Bets 6-card expansion that adds extra goals to work towards! Each game you’ll add two new goals (in addition to your normal set of 3) that must be completed to achieve victory. You’ll also need to consider the two Mega Structures in the deck. These 4-block buildings must be placed on The Strip and can’t be covered, so build wisely!

Draw 3 Casinopolis goals as normal, then choose in which order to place them to create the one-, two-, and three-chip bets. Draw 2 of the new goal cards, placing one underneath the lowest-value base goal (with the “lowest” side showing) and one underneath the highest-value base goal (with the “highest” side showing). These goals vary in difficulty based on which end is showing as well as their position, but both must be completed to win.

Two expansion cards are shuffled into the Casinopolis deck with their Mega Structures showing. These cards have 4 matching blocks as well as normal icons. They can only be placed on The Strip and cannot be covered once placed. While they count as only one block for the purpose of scoring casino size, their sprawling size makes them useful for completing scoring goals!

Embark on an extraordinary journey to the magical world of Encanto with the Lisciani Encanto Magical Super Game board game! This fantastic board game takes players to the colorful Madrigal house, where exciting challenges and adventures await them.

The goal of the game is to be the first to reach the finish line, overcoming various obstacles and difficulties along the way. The path through the Madrigal house is full of secrets and puzzles that players must solve to reach their goal. However, they are not alone on this journey - the Madrigal family is ready to help them overcome difficulties and achieve success.

Challenge your friends and acquaintances to find out who will be the best traveler in the magical world of Encanto! The Lisciani Encanto Magical Super Game board game is great fun for the whole family, full of excitement, competition and the joy of achieving the goal.

Setup
1. Deal each player 3 face down reserve cards.
2. Deal each player 3 face up reserve cards, placed on top of the face down reserves.
3. Deal each player 3 cards for their hand.
4. The remaining cards are placed into the centre to form a draw pile.
Swapping Reserves
Before starting players may swap any number of their face-up reserve cards with cards from their hand.

Your Turn
On your turn you need to play a card from your hand that matches or outranks the top card on the play pile.
A card may only be played onto any card with a higher rank than its own.
If there are still cards in the draw pile you need to pick up a card to maintain a minimum of 3 cards.
If you are unable to play a card then you must pick up the play pile.

Once you have played all your hand cards and there are no draw cards left in the pile you can play your face up reserve cards.

Once you have played all you hand cards and face up reserve cards you must now blindly play your face down reserve cards.
Winning
No cards left to play? Congratulations soldier! You've won! Consider yourself one of the Top Brass.

Be aware of Direct Order cards, they have the power to drastically change the dynamic of the game, so use them wisely!

Punch and Kick other players to Knock them out over 5 bouts!
Score the most points to win!

Setup
Shuffle the deck and deal each fighter 7 cards and place the rest of the deck in the centre to form a draw pile.
Get a pen + paper OR use the online Punchy Kicky score tracker:

Swap up to 3 cards from the draw pile to make a better fighting hand.

FIGHT!
Get ready and start swinging!
Attack players with attack cards.
Defend incoming attacks with defence cards.

Scoring
If someone cannot defend your attack then you get 2 points!
Only one to make it all the way to the end of the round? Congratulations, youre the last fighter standing and get another 2 points!
Make use of our handy score tracker if youre struggling to keep track after a few blows.

Winning
The fighter with the most points at the end of the 5th round wins!

On The Clock is a trick-taking game where players are bidding on items from the "oBoy" online auction site. Once an item is up for auction, players will engage in a "must follow" trick-taking mini game to determine who will be "On The Clock".

The "On The Clock" player will be the one who played the highest value of the Lead Currency. Every other player will then have an opportunity to "snipe" that bid by playing a card of a different currency ("must not follow").

Each other currency has a set value modifier. The "On The Clock" player has a final opportunity to take the bid by playing another card of the Lead Currency to add to their current bid value.

The game ends after 3 rounds. Players will then score points based on the number of items they won during bidding, plus bonus points for owning a complete set of the three types of items on offer.

—description from the designer

REFIVO stands for the three monster types: REptile, FIsh, and VOgel (German for bird).

Cards from the draw pile are quickly flipped over and placed on the discard pile one by one, while the words RE, FI, and VO are repeated in sequence.
If the spoken monster type matches the type of the revealed monster card, you must shout "REFIVO!" and swiftly place your capture card on the monster to catch it.
But only the fastest player gets it!

Meanwhile, the so called "Oh-Oh!-cards" provide an advantage or set all players back.

Collect two monsters of each monstertype to win!

—description from the designer

REFIVO steht für die 3 Monstertypen REptil, FIsch und VOgel.

Der Reihe nach werden Karten vom Ziehstapel schnell umgedreht und auf den Ablegestapel gelegt, während die Worte RE, FI und VO wiederholt werden. 
Stimmt der gesagte Monstertyp mit dem Monstertyp der gelegten Monsterkarte überein, musst du REFIVO rufen und flink deine Fangkarte auf das Monster legen um es einzufangen.
Doch nur der Schnellste bekommt es.
Die sogenannten "Oh-Oh!-Karten" sorgen währenddessen dafür, dass du einen Vorteil bekommst oder jeder Spieler zurückgeworfen wird.

Sammle 2 Monster jedes Monstertyps um zu gewinnen.

—description from the designer (German)

boardgame: The Yanks Are Here! The Saint Mihiel Campaign, September 1918
description:
"It was like a moving picture battle. Tanks were crawling up along the muddy roads and khaki-colored figures could be seen moving about in ones and twos and fours along the edges of the woods and across the grassy plains...Overhead the shells were still screaming from our heavy artillery with a good deal of answering fire from the German batteries which caused most of our losses.” –Father Frances McDuffy, 165th Regiment of the US 42nd Infantry Division.

The Yanks Are Here! is a moderate complexity level game of the battle of Saint Mihiel. This was the first large scale offensive by the United States Army in WWI. General Pershing had insisted since he first arrived that US soldiers would only fight under the command of US leaders and as a unified force. The offensive of Saint Mihiel was first planned as two-part effort to first reduce the German held salient that threatened Verdun, and then take Metz to breakthrough the German fortified lines. Allied commanders, however, wanted the US Army to more directly support their offensive along the Aisne and Somme Rivers further north. Only four days of supplies and material were granted General Pershing for the Saint Mihiel offensive. Although the US First Army was successful in reducing the salient, the German Army was in the process of withdrawing anyways and were able to limit the American’s advance to just short of their newly established fortified line. It would be American’s Meuse-Argonne offensive in October that would result in a more decisive decision, but at a much higher cost in American lives.

Can you do as well or better than your historical counterpart?

—description from designer

Heredity: Goodies Promo Cards is a free print-to-play promo cards. You can download its from the official game website.

There are currently 4 goodies:

     Festival Souvenir (950)
     Old Comic Book (951)
     Jolly Chimp (952)
     Rhino Tape (953)


Only one goody can be added to the Play Characters' inventory at the beginning of each chapter. If you have more than one, you can play with a different goodie for each chapter, but never more than one in the game at the same time.

Once a goody is in the game, it becomes part of your inventory and is saved like the rest of your Objects.

If you lose a goody, you can't add it again to your inventory during the campaign.

Fast-Paced Family Fun for All Ages: 
Protect your carrots from a sneaky rabbit in this quick and easy family card game full of laughs, comebacks, and unexpected twists. Perfect for kids, teens, and adults who want fun game night moments without complicated rules.

Draw, React, and Save Your Carrots: 
On each turn, draw a card and take action. Gain carrots, lose carrots, or grab them all. But watch out, if the Carrot Bomb appears, you must defuse it fast or lose every carrot you collected.

Quick to Learn, Easy to Replay: 
Designed for ages 6 and up, Carrot BOOM plays in just 10–15 minutes and can be learned in about 5 minutes. With pure luck and no strategy, it is easy to bring to the table again and again for family game night.

Portable Fun for Home, Travel, and Gifts: 
With 85 cards and a compact box, Carrot BOOM is easy to take on vacations, camping trips, sleepovers, and family gatherings. A great pick for screen-free fun and gifts for kids

Fun First, with Skills as a Bonus: 
Beyond the laughs and surprise moments, Carrot BOOM keeps players alert with fast turns and unpredictable outcomes. Staying ready for action helps support focus, quick reactions, and attention in a fun and natural way for kids, teens, and adults.

Marcher: Empires at War, is a tabletop miniatures wargame set in an alternate history diesel-punk sci-fi setting deeply rooted in the retro-futuristic aesthetics of the early 20th century, where the industrial revolution happened much earlier than in real life.  The year is 1938 and players will recreate company-level battles from The Great War using 28mm heroic scale miniatures.

Marcher is a D10-based alternating activation wargame played on a board the size of a standard dining room table. It features immersive aspects such as armor facing, blast templates, and suppression, but is also designed for relatively quick games.

Key features:

    Armies: Each Player fields armies of infantry, vehicles, and artillery, usually consisting of around 10 unique Units, but can range greatly in army size with a maximum number of Units capped at 20.
    Actions: Each Unit may perform two different, standard Actions per activation from their available list. Some Units have unique Actions specific to their stat sheet.
    D10-based Checks: Outcomes of Actions are determined by Checks, which involve the roll of a D10. An unmodified roll of a 1 always fails, and an unmodified roll of a 10 always succeeds.
    Alternating Activation: Unit activation in Marcher is carried out in an alternating fashion - opponents alternate activating individual units one at a time.
    Command Squads: Officers are separate models but are attached to squads before the start of gameplay. Each Platoon includes one officer leading them into battle.
    Measurements: All measurements use the Imperial system and are taken from the bases or hulls of the models.

Oniria is an immersive, asymmetrical trick-taking game built around an initiatory dream journey.

One player takes the role of the Soñadora (the Dreamer), while the other players embody the Sombras (the Nightmares). The game unfolds along a symbolic path — the Senda — which structures each round and defines the constraints of play. These constraints are known in advance, turning every round into a game of anticipation, misdirection, and careful reading of intentions.

The Soñadora plays alone against all. The Sombras must cooperate to block her progression, yet they score individually, creating a constant tension between alliance and rivalry. Power is deliberately uneven: success depends less on optimization than on timing, coordination, and reading the table.

Players take turns embodying the Soñadora across several rounds. When all players have faced the dream, the one with the highest total score emerges victorious.

Inspired by Mesoamerican and Aridoamerican conceptions of dreaming and initiation, Oniria does not aim to reproduce folklore literally, but to translate symbolic journeys and inner trials into a tense, strategic card game.

—description from the publisher

Meio (you should read 'mayo', like the condiment) is a Portuguese word that means "Half". In this simple but clever trick-taking game, each player must split their cards in 2 'halves' and win the same number of tricks in each of their halves. The most consistent player wins the game!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Final Girl: Series 3 Bonus Features Box
description:
A small box containing a Feature Film Rulebook which collects the rules from all 5 Series 3 Feature Films and also has Achievement lists.

Also includes 5 Epic Finale cards, one for each of the 5 Killers from S3.

The box can also store the Gruesome Death Books and Lore and Scenario Book from Series 3.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A set collection card game of baking pastries and satisfying discerning customers in Paris at the start of the 20th century.

At the start of the game, players select unique early 20th century baking tools. During game play, bakers collect ingredient cards and combine them to create amazing baked goods, matching ingredients with those required by an array of recipe cards. Bakers also seek to create baked goods matching the flavor preferences of customers to boost their scores.

Ingredients may be drawn two at a time; alternately, a baker may choose to draw ingredients until they choose to stop or until they "bust" by drawing a duplicate card. A baker who "busts" gets to keep only one card. This "push your luck" mechanism adds suspense and tension to the game, increasing player excitement.

Hot Dog is a trick-taking game that asks: why do hot dogs come in packs of 6, but buns come in packs of 8??

Each card in the deck has two different values: the dog value and the bun value.

When you or the player across from you leads a trick, you’ll play your cards for their dog value. When the other players lead a trick, use the bun value instead.

Earn the most points by winning an equal number of tricks with dogs and buns.

—description from the publisher

Numberwang is a must-follow trick-taking game with a trump suit that cannot be led until it has been broken. Shared incentive bidding warps the gameplay of this simple trick-taking game with a standard deck of cards.

Before each hand, look at each player’s cards and bid how many tricks you expect them to take. Everyone’s seen your cards, so trip them up by making counterintuitive bids and surprising plays.

Score points for bids that you hit exactly, and score bonus points if you can force other players to miss their bids. Earn the most points to win.

Let’s play Numberwang!

—description from the publisher

Descend further into darkness with Aberration: Spellcasters Expansion, where powerful new threats emerge—including the terrifying Eldritch Gnoll Monstrosity and its relentless minions.

This expansion brings even more depth to your battles with two new heroes, Ithral and Lyra, alongside four spellbinding classes: Arcanist, Vindicator, Inquisitor, and Spellsword—each wielding potent Spell cards to vanquish their enemies. Discover new Relics and Items to turn the tide of battle and embrace the arcane like never before!

Nothing scares me more in the deep wood than the honking of the witch's goose. The Giant Gasdra and its spawns have become warped, ravenous creatures that seek to destroy. Can you hold them back and protect the village or will their insatiable hunger for destruction be too much? 
This expansion includes new terrifying aberrations to add to your games of Aberration!

Auctions of Alchemists is a new unique mystical auction game set in a fantasy world by Tanax Games. Players race to create six elixirs by compounding four colored ingredients obtained through strategic bidding.

Each round one player will get the choice : to auction or to perform an alchemy action.  
During the auction they the ingredient type (color) is revealed, but 1 card that shows the amount of liquid is placed face down so only the active player knows.  Players take turns bidding and the player who bid the highest wins both ingredients.  
But watch out!  The unknown amount of liquid could push your test tubes limit over the top, or cause all the liquid to be lost.

If they choose to perform an alchemy action they can either transmute for gold, convert ingredients to another, or combine and create their elixirs.

Every time someone does an alchemy action day changes to night, and night to day and the value of ingredients will change.

You'll need to time your alchemy actions wisely, and outwit your opponents in the auctions to gain the ingredients you require.  
The first to create six elixirs wins!

Auctions of Alchemist uses a very clever and interesting way of auction and managing your resources!

—description from the publisher

In Chrono Team Go!, you and your team of heroes must work together to uncover powerful relics, navigate shifting pathways, and escape the ever-changing Rift before it consumes you. Every decision shapes your destiny - will your teamwork be enough to survive the void?

Chrono Team Go! is a cooperative board game for 1–4 players, blending strategy, teamwork, and adventure. Designed as a perfect gateway game, it introduces new players to exciting board game mechanics while keeping seasoned gamers engaged.

Whether you're a casual player or a strategy veteran, this game brings families and friends together for thrilling cooperative fun!

boardgame: Ages of War: Battle for the Bronze Age
description:
Play as the general of a bronze age army and fight an epic battle - move, attack and destroy your enemy on the battlefield encircled by terrain.

You begin by creating the game area, then draw and deploy warrior unit cards to intercept opposing army’s formations and take turns moving or attacking.

When attacking, roll a die to score damage and cross off health boxes, until the unit is destroyed (removed from game area). To win, eliminate all your opponent’s units from the battlefield!

—description from the designer

Lincoln 1141 is a small game that covers the battle of Lincoln, fought on 2 February 1141 in Lincoln, England. Army of king Stephen of England faced the forces loyal to Empress Matilda. Stephen was captured during the battle, imprisoned, and effectively deposed while Matilda ruled for a short time.

Game is a part of the Medieval system by TiS and uses its mechanics. Ownership of any game of the series is required to play, because it comes with no rules, only with board, counters and scenario set up.

Lincoln 1141 was published in the 49th issue of Taktyka i Strategia magazine.

La Corunna 1809 covers the battle of Corunna, fought on 16 January 1809. French corps under Jean de Dieu Soult attacked a British army under Sir John Moore which was on its way to evacuate from Spain.

Game is a part of the Napoleon system by TiS and uses its mechanics. Ownership of any game of the series is required to play, because it comes with no rules, only with board, counters and scenarios set up. Game contains two scenarios, showing both days of the battle.

La Corunna 1809 was published in the 49th issue of Taktyka i Strategia magazine.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Jakowiecki Las 1709 is a small game covering one of the initial stages of the battle of Poltava, fought on 8 July 1709 between Russians and Swedes during the Greath Northern War.

Game is a part of the Husaria system by TiS and uses its mechanics. Ownership of any game of the series is required to play, because it comes with no rules, only with board, counters and scenario set up.

Porzecze Dolne is an expansion for WOT 2025, coming with new board and counters, simulating a situation where Russian combat battalion group is trying to make its way through the big river and captured the bridgehead.

Game is a part of the B-21 system by TiS and uses its mechanics. Ownership of WOT 2025 game is required to play, because it comes with no rules, only with board, counters and scenario set up.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Pax Hispanica: Promo 3 – Omens and Treasures
description:
This promo pack includes 30 Treasure chits and 12 Omen tiles, allowing for greater strategic variety and replayability. Customize the game’s calendar setup with up to 8,000 possible configurations while keeping the same thrilling historical events. Elevate your Pax Hispanica experience with new twists and endless possibilities!

—description from the publisher

A simple card game for simple people who enjoy the good old tradition of stabbing other into the back when they are not looking.

In Stab your friends you aim to be the last remaining player of the bunch. Each player starts the game with five lives. The most fearsome player, obtains the crown card, is henceforth known has "their royal highness" and starts the game. Should a player not address the current holder of the crown card as such, they will loose a life.

The game has three phases: Draw, Play and Activation

In the draw phase, players each draw up a hand of four cards from the face up draw deck, concealing cards once drawn. In the play phase, they will now play two cards face down and move into the activation phase, where the cards are resolved in the order of priority.

Whomever is the last player standing wins the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Who will make the cut? Never has the question been more fitting! To make the most out of every throw of the dice, you need to cut a piece of paper into the 10 most lucrative pieces during phase 1 of the game. But caution is advised, because during phase 2, these pieces need to be reassembled as quickly as possible, or the point penalty will cost you dearly!

Weather Warz is a competitive deck building, worker placement, duel game (2 players) where wizards are controlling the weather to unleash devastating catastrophes to destroy the enemy's fortress. Command your dragon to gather resources, conjure the weather and rule the land.

You must use your chosen wizard's special abilities across several rounds to build your deck with new, more powerful WeatherSpells, strike your opponent with catastrophes from the Book of Destruction, or even upgrade your fortress. For the latter two actions, with the help of your dragon, you will need to collect runes at various locations on the map. 
To build your deck you'll need to gain mana from your unused spells during your round.

To use your spells effectively, it is essential that the sun shines or the sky is cloudy above your opponent's fortress. But as always, the weather is unpredictable. You will need it however,  to deal damage to your opponent with your weather spells.

The game ends when all the walls and tower of one player are destroyed.

The game is in development /prototype/, however fully playable. Had been introduced at local game expos, and playtests. Follow if you are interested in the game, and the development process.

—description from the publisher

Croterra   
The Final War Has Begun

In a dying realm where darkness looms rival Kingdoms wage war over the last hope for salvation—the newly emerged land of Croterra and the powerful artifacts buried within it. Ancient gods whisper, Arcanic power surges, and only those who master the balance of war, faith, and the unknown will endure.

Croterra is a competitive strategy boardgame for 1-6 players, where every choice has lasting consequences. Players command a kingdom with unique strengths, but raw might alone won’t win this war. To survive, you must outthink your rivals, manipulate fate, and wield the realm’s unpredictable forces to your advantage.

At the heart of Croterra lies a distinct resource system that demands strategic foresight and adaptability:
⚡ Gold accrues interest over time, rewarding those who plan ahead.
⚡ Arcanic Cores are never discarded, but remain perilously scarce—a powerful edge for those who control them.
⚡ Prayers can summon divine intervention—but will it be a blessing or a curse?

To claim victory, you must multiply your Arcanic, Personnel, and Divinity points—neglect any one, and your kingdom will fall into ruin.

War Has Changed Forever. The rise of Arcanic power has shattered the old ways of battle. Some fear it as an unnatural force, while others whisper of it belonging to an age yet to come. What's certain is that those who wield it alongside divine blessings and military strength will reshape the realm itself. But beware—the gods are fickle, and the power you summon may not be the one you intended.

Throughout the game, you must balance risk and reward—building your kingdom's future, seizing fleeting opportunities, and outmaneuvering opponents whose strategies may be more deceptive than they appear.

⚔️ Progress Cards offer unpredictable yet powerful advantages.
⚔️ Bolsters provide costly but reliable boosts to production and scoring.
⚔️ Divinity Cards introduce celestial forces that can reshape the very fabric of the game.

Take Avarice, for example—a divine boon that turns every resource, whether yours or your opponent’s, into pure Gold. Will you claim this "gift" for yourself or curse your enemies with its burden?

A Rival Unlike Any Other awaits in the Solo 1 Player Mode.
For those who dare accept the challenge, Morvena Dreadmore awaits—a relentless and cunning solo opponent that will push even the most strategic rulers to their limits.

Will you master the ever-shifting balance of power, or will your kingdom be swallowed by the darkness?
Stake your claim. Command your kingdom. Shape the future... or perish.

—description from Zachary Shore, Designer

Trumped! is a multi-player card game that is won using strategy and cunning to capture cards and trap unwary opponents.
It is hosted on Steam Workshop via Tabletop Simulator and uses two standard decks of playing cards to create a battle grid and to deal player cards. Player tokens are used to move around the grid and chips are used to capture cards.
A partially scripted game setup deals cards to players and allows players to vary the size of the grid depending on player numbers or game length required.
The goal of the game is to capture more cards than your opponents and avoid being trapped by their capture chips.
Can be played by all the family or during a break in a heavier game.

—description from the designer
