From 1 to Z has 108 cards, each of which has both a letter of the alphabet (A to Z) and a number corresponding to that letter's place in the alphabet (A is 1, B is 2, Z is 26). It also has a task die, and a one-minute timer.  At the start of the game each player is dealt 6 cards, which they lay out on the table before them.

For each round, the task die is rolled and the timer started. During the minute, players each try to use their cards to accomplish the task indicated by the die: 

     WORD. Use up to 4 of the cards to make a valid word. 
     MATH. Use up to 4 of the cards to make an equation using addition and/or subtraction whose result is a number that ends in 0. 
     EITHER. Fulfill either the WORD or the MATH task.
     BOTH. Use up to 6 of the cards to make both a word and an equation ending with a number ending in 0. 


After the time runs out, the players then show what they made, and if it's valid they put those cards into a personal points pile, and draw cards to replenish to 6 cards. After 5 rounds, the player with the most cards in their points pile wins he game.

If there is a tie, the tied players play a tie-breaker round: Each is dealt 6 new cards, and they try to make the highest-scoring word, where the word's value is the sum of the numbers on the cards used.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Thermal Wrappings Promo
description:
The Thermal Wrapping is a gear card with the following text:

When you depart, add [1 armor] to all hit locations with heavy armor.

[Green affinity]: +1 while you are insulated.

Together with a Living Glossary card with relevant rules, the Thermal Wrapping was available as a promo with the Frozen Survivor — Camellia miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Seed Pattern: Jackel
description:
Seed Pattern - Jackel is packaged in a graphic box that includes:

1 x Seed Pattern - Jackel hard plastic miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
1 x Spina Sickle seed pattern card
1 x Spina Sickle seed pattern gear card
1 x Living rules glossary card
1 x Large matte art print

Once, Jackel was poised to strike the killing blow, when a brave survivor surged from behind, stealing her triumph. Frozen in thin-lipped silence, she could not offer even the most insincere accolades. A one-sided rivalry flourished in her embittered imagination. Never missing the chance to depart with her competitor in the hopes of glimpsing his bloody defeat. Finally, the seed of her bitterness bore fruit, the image of a rending weapon, one to leave her daring rival spineless.

As well as being fun, the game also has an educational function. Its task is to familiarize participants with Polish history and the people associated with it. The game board consists of four circles, each corresponding to a different stage in Polish history: the Piast era, the Jagiellonian era, the era of elective kings and the period from the third partition of Poland to the regaining of independence in 1918.

During the game, players move around a circular board with 100 fields. There are 70 special fields closely related to the characters of the era. When a player stands on such a field, they are subject to a penalty or reward associated with that character. In addition, the player must answer the historical questions assigned to that field. A correct answer is rewarded with a prize of coins and an incorrect one with a loss of some.

In addition, players during their turn can buy or sell property that reacts with the penalty and reward cards, decide how to dispose of debt and dispose of their property freely.

When moving from a lower circle to a higher circle, special rules must be met to make this possible. One must have a certain amount of coins or stand on a specific field.

The winner is the player who stands on the field number 100.

Ostrołęka 1807 covers the battle of Ostrołęka, fought on 16 February 1807. French corps under Savary was attacked by Russian group under under general Essen that tried to capture town in the daring attack

Game is a part of the Napoleon system by TiS and uses its mechanics. Ownership of any recent game of the series (ie.  Preussisch Eylau 1807) is required to play, because it comes with no rules, only with board, counters and scenarios set up. Game contains two scenarios, showing both days of the battle.

Ostrołęka 1807 was published in the 50th issue of Taktyka i Strategia magazine. It is completely different game than Ostrołęka 1831 (that also contained scenario for 1807), using different board, counters and rules, even if being a part of the same system.

Imagine the thrill of Children's game meets the chaos of a variety show, all wrapped up in a wild party game full of laughter, twists, and epic battles for entertainment supremacy!

Each player will play mini-games (children's games) to actively attempt to win orbs. The more orbs gained, the better chance when one of the players pull and Auction Card. The player who pulls an Auction card will start a silent Auction to bid for the artifact. Each artifact have different value points. The first player to gain 6 points wins the game.

It is a 2 to 4 player game. Sometimes, there are players who does not want to play, and instead they are Audience. Audience gets to vote of each mini games to gain more orbs for each player.

—description from the designer

In Kastle, build the most stunning sandcastle, but watch out—the waves are coming! Collect tools, manage your resources, and overcome challenges to create a true masterpiece. Who will claim the beach?

Build the most impressive sandcastle by strategically placing your cards and completing various objectives. Use your tools wisely to optimize your construction.

Players strategically place cards in their tableau for each turn in Kastle to shape their sandcastle. After all players have placed a card, a wave card is revealed, affecting their construction.

Some cards activate tools and bonuses, while others help complete objectives. Some tools can be used to enhance and refine your sandcastle.

The game ends when a player fills all spaces in their tableau, triggering the final scoring.

The player with the most points wins!

—description from the designer

As an intergalactic plumber, you are tasked with de-clogging the space lanes. Jaunt from rock to rock using your plunger to remove asteroids, gaining points along the way.  But beware! Other pesky plumbers are looking to clean up too... and will leave you stranded in space!

Roll 2 dice to determine how far you can move and what colour asteroid you want to collect.  Use a suction cup on the top of your player piece to pick them up.
Small asteroids are worth 3 points, mid sized are 2 and large are 1 point.  Keep playing until there are no moves left.

boardgame: Splatterkins: Ardent Dreams Pastel Starter Deck
description: Provides a new Visionary to play as, Amir Yilmaz, along with a thirty-nine pastel splatterkin-themed deck to use in the quest to save Tempera, with a full set of inspiration and paint drop tokens.

A booster box containing 168 cards, providing the "doodle" version of a lot of splatterkins, some foil, some alternate art cards, and three new Visionaries, plus four Duality tokens to go with the Eugene Winters Visionary.

Each Box Contains:
24 Booster packs (7 cards per pack. Each pack contains 5 commons and 2 Foils)
4 Acrylic Duality Tokens

Rarity Breakdown:
39 different basic commons (3 copies of each common per box)
5 different Alternate Art commons (3 random cards per box)
39 different Foil cards (48 random cards per box)
5 different Alternate Art Foils (1 random card per box)

Designed to provide a fun way to review knowledge in the areas of Sonic Operations, Sonic People, Compliance, Management Skills and Sonic Trivia.
Points are accumulated for each correct answer.  The first person to reach 500 points wins the game.

It is a beautiful day in the Yellow Pine forests of Arkansas and through the trees you hear a light humming. Is it a train? No, you hear the snap of a branch, then another, then a quick, “TIMBER!” as a tree is being logged. Was that on your property?

Acres is a game about controlling harvest lanes, leveraging long term investments, and planning fifteen years at a time. Acres is a game of simple rules but sharp decisions centered around the Lumber industry.

Players will draft actions from a set of four options: Growing trees, Thinning an acre, Harvesting lanes, and Go to market. While the players have drafted specific actions, all players will resolve all actions. The players who drafted specific actions receive action specific bonuses.

Throughout the game players have the opportunity to purchase: fences, blocking other players from harvesting lanes, additional trucks, to harvest more trees at a time, and Assets investments such as starting their own sawmill company or increasing truck capacities. The player at the end of six rounds with the most money wins!

—description from the designer

The late 1980s into the early 1990s are called the Junk Wax Era, where the hype of baseball card collecting was only outpaced by the volume of cards flooding into the market.

Relive the glory days of card collecting with this new card drafting and dice rolling game from designer Danny Devine.

Junk Wax combines a a fun dice rolling method to drive the value of your baseball cards. Collect your favorite players and teams, from Rookies, to Commons, to All Stars. Then depending on how well their teams do, your cards can increase in value.

The game will play across 5 rounds where all of the teams face off in head to head matchups each round. You'll place dice next to the teams playing allowing you to draft cards from the display, then store those cards in your personal collection binders.

At the end of each round, the teams will play their head to head games, the winner being determined by the rolls from those dice placed next to them during the card drafting phase. Each winning team will increase the point values of all of the cards from baseball players on that team currently in your personal card collection. Plus you will earn even more points at the end of the game from their locations in your binders based on the communal objectives based on card company brand, connected runs of the same team, and player roster positions.

Will you build the best baseball card collection? Let's crack some packs and find out!

—description from the publisher

Lots of Leftovers? Licking Old Lolipops? These are answers that will make you LOL in the hilarious game of Wrong Answers Only! Players secretly write clever wrong answers to everyday topics while the other players try to correctly match them with their answers. This game is so wrong, it just feels right!

Objective
The real goal of the game is to create fun lifelong memories, but if you care about the whole winning thing, then try to score the most points by correctly matching other players to their Wrong Answers.

boardgame: Railways of the Lost Atlas: Landmarks of the Lost Atlas
description:
Landmarks of the Lost Atlas introduces four new modules to expand your Railways of the Lost Atlas games. These modules can be included individually or in any combination:

Landmarks add new strategies as well as added flavor to maps that include them.

The Metropolitan Transit Department adds a neutral Corporation to the game that players can use to benefit their own interests and/or hinder others.

Events create temporary changes to the core rules of the game that players will need to adapt to.

New Minor Companies and Major Corporations add variety to the Company abilities available in the game.

—description from the publisher

Blob Party: Neon Nights is a cooperative party game where players start as individuals with a bit of dough and a googly eye and try to become one big blob by all giving the same answers!

Let’s say the group starts with the Category of “Furniture” and the Word is “Castle.”  All players write an answer, maybe “Throne,” or “Round Table.” Everyone reveals, and anyone who wrote the same thing becomes a blob – mushing together their dough and eyes – and will work together for the rest of the game.

A new Category and Word are revealed for the next round, where players and blobs once again come up with answers, hoping to match with others. As the game continues, blobs and individuals merge until (hopefully!) everyone gives the same answer, becoming a Mega Blob and winning the game!

Blob Party: Neon Nights is a complete game on its own with 100 new words and 25 new categories! It can be combined with the original Blob Party for even more combinations, allowing up to 16 players to play at once!"

—description from the publisher

In Pre-existing Conditions, you play as the CEO of a healthcare insurance company, doing whatever you can to keep your profit going up each quarter. Fail, and you're out, replaced by some other anonymous white guy named Tim.

To begin, assign medical conditions – ranging from tooth decay to cancer – to 10 customers. Each condition has a Premium (what the customer pays to be insured by you) and a Cost (what you'll pay out if that customer makes a claim). Every time their number comes up (literally), you cross off the next highest number on their Cost table, meaning the more often they claim, the more they'll cost you.

The game is played over 24 rounds (2 years of 12 months each). Each 'month' you'll work out your Premiums, then roll two dice to see what claims you need to pay out. The difference is your profit for that month. Every quarter you can choose a condition to deny, meaning you won't have to pay out if its number comes up.

You can also use your dice to invest in lobbying, marketing or personal security, to do things like purge customers from your books, add new customers, and get your security level high enough to deal with potential threats.

At the end of Year 2, if you have more positive quarters than negative, congrats, you win the game!

—description from the designer

The Titan Edition includes the content from the Platinum edition of the base game as well as from the expansions Binding Ties, Overload and Nomad. 
The Gold Foil version of the Titan Box was exclusively sold through a Kickstarter campaign and also contains the Holographic Upgrade Pack. After the campaign only the Silver Foil version (without the Holographic Upgrade Pack) is sold. 
All components are pre-packaged in the Titan Box. Expansion boxes are not included.

Content included:

    Titan Box
    Moonrakers: Platinum Edition
    Binding Ties Expansion
    Overload Expansion
    Nomad Expansion
    Game Trayz Custom Insert
    Holographic Upgrade Pack (only in the Gold Foil version) 


—description from publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
'The people of Massilia were worn out by all their misfortunes. They had been reduced to a catastrophic lack of food supplies, defeated in two naval battles, routed in frequent sorties, and even afflicted by a terrible epidemic caused by their long confinement and change of diet'
Caesar, Civil War, book 2, 10.22.1 (translated by Kurt A. Raaflaub)

A Long Confinement is a postcard game about the siege of Massilia in 49 B.C. The design is intended to demonstrate the Roman Republic practice of siegecraft (land blockade, construction of siege ramps) alongside with certain features that make the siege of Massilia noteworthy (unusual siege works, Gallic allied tribe of Albicii helping out besieged Massilians). At the same time, it is meant to be an accessible and relatively fast playing game.

The postcard game uses multiple six sided dice (in three different colours), a single, reduced deck of regular playing cards (Aces and 2's to 6's of all suits = 24 cards total). Dice are used to represent troops on the map, cards are used to pay for actions and resolve combat.

The game can be played by one player - who controls the besiegers, the Caesarian army - or by two players, when the second player controls the besieged Massilian defenders. The goal is to force the city to capitulate before Julius Caesar comes back from Spain (after his victory in Ilerda campaign) - in short, to do as well as the historical commander or better. Any other result besmirches the good name of Julius Caesar and his military reputation.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Challenger Deck: Azorius Control
description:
Permission denied! Negate your opponent's resources through counterspells and removal, and then finish the job with a flock of fearsome flying creatures.

Contents

     Battle-ready 60-card deck
     15-card sideboard
     6 DFC helper cards
     5 double-sided tokens
     Deck box


—description from the publisher

Dive into the gripping mystery and develop your theory about what happened. Analyze suspect conversations, articles, evidence, and more. When you're ready, open the solution PDF to check your deductions and calculate your score. Can you solve the case and achieve Legend status?

Murder mystery plot:
A man is found dead in his apartment. A single needle mark and signs of substance abuse—it seems like an open-and-shut case. But the deeper you dig, the more suspects and motives you uncover. Was it an accident, or something far more sinister?

—description from the publisher

Two choose-your-own adventures on two decks of cards, to be played alone or voting together the choices.
"Il grande cacciatore" (The Great Hunter) puts players in the role of a cartoon cat, such as Sylvester or Tom, trying to catch a pray for lunch.
In "Una mattina in Catai" (A Morning in Cathay) players are the young Marco Polo, entering with his merchand caravan in the capital of China and trying to retrieve a medaillon that a kid robbed.

boardgame: Hex General: Fantasy Edition – Flight from Saint Ardot
description:
Hex General: Fantasy Edtion is a hex and counter scenario-based wargame. Gameplay is based on asymmetrical scenarios between an Attacker and a Defender. Each side has it's own unique forces, objectives, advantages and disadvantages. The strong emphasis on Attacker and Defender roles, combined with the detailed scenarios, makes Hex General perfect for solo gamers - all of the guidance needed is contained in the book. For player vs player action it is recommended to use 'challenge rounds' where players run through each scenario twice, once as the Attacker and once as the Defender.

Hex General has a simple game engine which lets players focus on strategy. Each counter represents a hundred-strong company of Infantry, Cavalry or Flyers. Every company have three stats - Movement, Attack and Defence. There are three types of attack - Melee, Missile, Magick - and two types of defence - Physickal (which can defend against spears and arrows) and Magickal (which can defend against everything). All the stats you need are written on the counters, with the exception of a few companies who have special rules (such as infiltration).

Gameplay is divided into three phases: 1) The Battle Phase - where players move their troops and shoot with missile and magick attacks. 2) The Melee Phase - where melee companies in contact with the enemy get to make their melee attacks. 3) The End Phase - where players check if they scored objectives, clean up the battlefield and so on. Archers and magick-hurling mages can move and shoot or shoot and move, giving them a tactical advantage against melee troops.

All companies project a zone-of-control (ZoC) which limits the movement of hostile companies. However allied companies can move into a hostile ZoC and allow their allies to pass through unimpeded, creating 'bridges' across the battlefield. Some companies such as scouts and rangers can move through ZoCs without penalty, but most troops must navigate a hostile battlefield and support their comrades to succeed.

—description from the publisher

Rhino tape goody can be added to the Play Characters' inventory at the beginning of each chapter. If you have more than one, you can play with a different goodie for each chapter, but never more than one in the game at the same time.

Once the goody is in the game, it becomes part of your inventory and is saved like the rest of your Objects.

If you lose the goody, you can't add it again to your inventory during the campaign.

The card was available in Plato magazine 172

You take on the role of an average villager who must face the greatest challenge of his life: venturing into the town of Greentown, infested with horrific creatures, in search of his sister. However, it won’t be easy—a grotesque entity will stand in his way, corrupting everything in this place.

This game focuses on a simple yet challenging mechanic. The player rolls two six-sided dice to determine the action for the turn, then checks for an encounter with a beast and repeats these two steps until completing all five levels of the game.

With a classic dungeon exploration style, resource management, and deep exploration, the player must reach the final stage and defeat the final boss. However, if their health or sanity reaches zero a certain number of times, they will lose the game. Each playthrough will be unique in this roguelike filled with horror and secrets!

—description from the designer

You thought you had bad dates?

Each player gets an A card, a B card and a Death Card.  The Dater picks 2 cards from the deck, reads tehm aloud then puts them on the "A" and "B" spaces on the game board, face up.  The dater chooses who they would date by placing their "A" or "B" card face down on the table. The other players choose who they think the dater chose by placing their "A" or "B" card face up on the table.  Each player that guesses correctly, earns a point, first to 15 points is the winner.  There are also create your own, where another player creates the nightmare scenario of dates, or Hate 'Em card where another player must guess correctly or lose all their points.  Then the death card that you can play, but only if you have at least 5 points.  Then all other players receive 2 points.

An evolved memory game where you have to find the three steps of the most famed scenes in Fantozzi's movies. The large, full color cards depict movie frames with the balloons of the best puns in the films. 
Released by Ravensburger on the 50th anniversary of the first movie in the epic saga of tis iconic Italian unlucky clerck.

Rated 14+ for a couple of 4-letter words, as far as difficulty is concerned it is actually playable from 7 years up.

Tangrams for real brainiacs!
An even more challenging Tangram game! For 1 or 2 players, with a red and a green set. 7 tangram shapes with endless combinations. Play wherever you go, alone or with a friend.

Avert a global pandemic crisis in this worker placement game from designer Emilio Samuel.

In Dispatch Unit, each player controls a team of public service workers, scientists, and analysts to help research, develop and distribute vaccines across the globe. Each continent has their own capacity that players can dispatch their workers into. Once they have done that, they will earn points based on the workers they have dispatched, making development and transport of vaccines more viable.

The game ends after all vaccines have been distributed, and the player with the most points wins.

-description from designer

This KS Exclusive expansion adds a new Guardian and a new Avatar to increase the variety of your games with new combinations and mechanics.
Frida and Soundwave's skills will turn the Database into a deadly concert! Are you ready to mosh?

Expansion contents:

1x Soundwave miniature (40mm)
1x Frida miniature (40mm)
1x Guardian sheet
12x Activation cards
1x Description cards
1x Avatar cards
7x Malware cards

—description from the publisher

Two players pool dice, attack, shield and use potions and polish.

Desription from the publisher:
Shields Dice

I love polyhedral dice. While many games utilize them, the special ones like d4s, d8s and d12s are only rolled a few times in the course of many gaming sessions (such as with tabletop roleplaying games).

Rolling dice is an activity that is enjoyable in and of itself. And the excitement that builds when people try to get a certain number, against all odds, is contagious.

But I also love strategy games. Shields is a dice game I created that combines the joy of probability with the calculation of strategy. It’s quick to set up and easy to play. It’s a two-player game about breaking through your opponent’s shields with your colored attack batteries.

What you’ll need
Shields is played entirely with dice. No cards, no boards, no markers. The following is the list of dice you will need. You can swap colors if that makes it easy to get all the dice (as long as everybody is clear what each color represents).
[...]
34 dice total, 24 unique die and color sizes.

Shields is played one-on-one.

Supershazam is the heartbeat of your next gathering! It blends the best of two worlds: the fun of strategic board games and the universal appeal of music.

It's a dynamic and engaging game where players race to identify songs/artists while using their Instant cards or Game changer cards to outwit their opponents with smart tactics, strategic thinking and game-changing twists.

Whether you're hosting a friends party, a family get-together, or any other event, Supershazam is designed to create unforgettable memories filled with friendly competition. You need at least 4 players to play. The maximum? It's up to you! Basically, pretty much everyone can join in the fun, making it the perfect game for any group size, any setting.

How to play?
1. Choose a host. The host defines the teams, picks the songs to be guessed and manages the card usage and distribution.
2. When the song starts, players raise their hands as soon as they believe they know the title or artist for that song.
3. The host pauses the music and defines the priority to answer, starting by the team who raised the hand first. 
4. At this stage the players have the chance to play any immediate cards they see fit, in order to shake things up and shift the game in their favor. Any amount of immediate cards can be played until the players guess both the title and the artist - if the same team guesses both, it's a SUPERSHAZAM!
5. Before the next song, any team can use a Game Modifier card to tailor the game to their advantage, increasing their odds to score more points.

By guessing songs, combined with the strategic usage of the cards, you the teams will earn points. The first team to achieve 30 points, wins the game!

-description from publisher

Hoff 1807 covers the battle of Hoff, fought on 6 February 1807. It was a rearguard action fought between the Russian rearguard under Barclay de Tolly and the advancing French during the Russian retreat before the battle of Eylau. Battle became famous because of french cuirassiers charge that broke Russian infantry.

Game is a part of the Napoleon system by TiS and uses its mechanics. Ownership of Preussisch Eylau 1807 is required to play, because it comes with no rules, only with board, scenario set up. All the units should be taken from Preussisch Eylau 1807 game.

Hoff 1807 was published in the 50th issue of Taktyka i Strategia magazine.

Joker’s Court is a solo or two-player roguelike deck-building game where players must survive 10 unpredictable Court Trials using a deck of 32 powerful Joker cards. Each round, the Court presents a new challenge, forcing players to adapt their strategy, manipulate the rules, and outwit the game itself.
Your goal? Complete all 10 Trials without running out of cards or failing a required challenge.

In Forest Leader, five animal clans — Fox, Rabbit, Badger, Bear, and Deer — compete for the throne of the forest. Each turn, you play cards to influence the Council of Chiefs and your Tribes.

On your turn, perform the following actions:

• Draw : Take 3 cards from the draw pile
or the 3 visible cards in the river.
• Play : Place 2 cards from your hand into two
different zones:
   • The Council of Chiefs,
   • The left Tribe,
   • The right Tribe.
Cards are placed in visible columns by clan.
• Discard : Place one card in the river.

If the river exceeds 3 cards, remove the oldest card.

The Council of Chiefs determines the points each clan will bring you. Your goal is to accumulate the most points by having the most prestigious clans in your tribes.

-description from designer

The commercial fishing ship Mare Istra disappeared near the coast of Iceland in 1926. It was one of the biggest Croatian ships made in Kraljevice, heading toward Iceland on it's 18th voyage. It had experienced crew of 20, with an old, sturdy captain Mate Horvat. Somewhere near the volcanic island of Surtsey they were swept by the storm and got  stranded on the uncharted reef. Their messages for help were received by the nearby 'Columbia', but due to the heavy storm they couldn't reach them until 24 h later. By that time Mare Istra sunk, never to be found again. Their last radio message was: 'They are not human...'

Mare Istra is a cooperative horror survival game for up to 4 players. You, as players, will re-live the last 24 hours of the cursed ship, attacked by the Deep Ones and other aquatic nightmares. To survive you will have to radio for help and fire the signal flare, as instructed on the Crisis card. Besides that there will be few random crisis that needs to be averted to successfully finish the game.

Players use actions during their activation phase to move through rooms and fight monsters. They start with one crisis card in play that they have to resolve before time runs out (by the 24-th turn).
In the meantime, monsters are spawned on the game board (representing decks of a ship) and moved toward players, trying to capture or kill them.

WINNING

Players win if they finish the first Crisis (They are not Human) and abandon ship before it is destroyed by some other crisis. If they can't abandon ship because both Life Boats are destroyed, they must finish any crisis that will cause the destruction of the ship and must survive till the end of turn 24. SAFE ROOM can be used for this, as they can hide there and wait it out (if the ship is not in danger), as long as they finished the main crisis.
If you are completely safe in the SAFE ROOM, you may end the game earlier, as monsters can not enter that room.

Using a standard Rider-Waite Tarot deck (or any variant), play as drag queens trying to win a beauty contest by getting the most votes from the judges!

The four suits represent each player's attributes:
Cups represent your Physique.
Pentacles represent your Talent.
Wands represent your Luck.
Swords represent your Bitchiness.

The cards are ranked Ace low, Queens high (because this is a Tournament of Queens, after all).

To start the game, dealer shuffles the deck and deals out the row of judges. 6 are placed face up (open), and 3 are placed face down (hidden) and only revealed at the end of the game. Another 4 cards are placed in a pile as the judge reserve pile.

Each player gets 6 cards.

If a judge is one of the Minor Arcana, that judge will vote for whoever has the best of that particular attribute at the end of the game. For example, if a judge card is the Four of Cups, that judge will vote for whoever has the best cups (the highest total in Physique). The only exception is Swords, where the judges will vote for the sweetest contestant, not the bitchiest.

If the judge is one of the Major Arcana, the judge will vote according to a table.

On your turn, you draw a card (until the deck is empty) and then play a card.

You have 5 options:

1. Replacing Your Attributes

You may play a Minor Arcana card from your hand to a spot in front of you. This card is now called an Attribute Card, and it changes your base value for that attribute.

2. Being a Bitch

You can play a Minor Arcana card from your hand on top of one of anyone else's Attribute Cards of the same suit. Your opponent's card is called the Base Attribute Card, and the card you play is called either a positive modifier or a negative modifier, depending on which direction it faces.

Restrictions: You can only play a modifier card with a value less than or equal to your Bitchiness. This is why having a high Bitchiness can be good, although it may cause you to lose votes if you have a high Bitchiness at the end.

3. Bribing Judges

You may play a card from your hand on top of an existing open judge. The judge is now bribed, and will vote according to the card you just played. The new judge must be ranked strictly higher than the old judge, but may be of any suit. Minor Arcana are ranked lowest, with 2 being at the very bottom and Queens at the top of the Minors. Ranked next highest are the Major Arcana in numerical order, with 0 being the lowest and XXI the highest. Aces are ranked higher than the Major Arcana for purposes of bribing judges, but are still ranked lowest for all other purposes.

4. Use a Special Effect

The Special Effect cards are the Major Arcana. There are two types of special effects: instant effects and continuing effects. The effects of each of the Major Arcana care detailed in a table.

5. Discard

Only do this if you have no better options. Discards are placed in a pile face up and are out of play.

Ending The Game

There are two ways the game can end. The first is when Judgement is played. Then, everyone else gets one more turn (the player who played Judgement does not get another turn) and then the game is over. The other way the game can end is if everyone runs out of cards.

When the game is over, the judges vote for the players they prefer. Cup judges only care about who has the highest total Physique. Pentacle judges only care about who has the highest total Talent. Wand judges find all the contestants quite nice, so they attempt to vote for whoever has the highest total Luck. Sword judges only care about who is the sweetest, meaning who has the lowest total Bitchiness. Major Arcana judges vote according to the table. Note that some of the Major Arcana judges won't vote at all, and some can cast more than one vote. Also, if there is a tie, votes are split equally. Many games have been decided by half a vote!

Whoever gets the most votes wins! In case of a tie, the players must have a cat fight to determine the winner.

Assemble your crew, captain your ship, and take on the high seas in this legacy co-op trick-taker!

In Sail Legacy, you and your best matey will go on an adventure across thirty unique missions. Upgrade your ship, outfit your characters, and tune the deck itself to avoid a watery grave!

Sail Legacy evolves from the core Sail game mechanisms as you cooperatively use "must-follow" trick-taking to take on the unforgiving ocean — and the mythical beasts that call it home. Based on the combination of symbols in the trick, you'll steer the ship, charge ahead, or fire the cannons. Throughout the game, you'll tweak your pirate's asymmetric player powers. Will you have a well-balanced team or specialize in one area?

An epic campaign awaits. Live with the consequences of numerous branching decisions. Express your creativity with customization. Most of all, don't sink. No two tales will be the same!

—description from the publisher

Are you willing to do whatever it takes to survive against both the living and the undead in Mortal Reckoning? This gritty expansion brings raw new art, vengeful mechanics, and fresh Characters and Hideouts that will transform your Zombie: Survival Infection games.

Mortal Reckoning not only gives you 2 new starting Camps and enough Survivor chips to raise the player count of the game from 6 to 8 it also includes 2 new Play Modes. ALLIED CAMPS: A new way to play your 2-3 player games where each player control 2 friendly camps. NEW VICTIMS: Changes out Characters & Hideouts for 2-6 player games.

This expansion also includes all new Character and Hideouts including the Loyal Dog. As well as new Actions and Reactions that bring new combos and tactics to all player count games.

Includes:
    27 Cards
        4 Characters
        4 Hideouts
        8 Actions
        5 Reactions
        2 Starting Hideouts
        2 No Hideout/ Zombies
        2 Phase
    12 Survivor/ Zombie Tokens
    1 Rules Sheet

-description from designer

It’s a long, mind-numbing flight; despite your best efforts, your thoughts wander. The mountains waiver, aeons old frost-shattered rocks sliding together into stairs roughly hewn
by inhuman hands.

You wander fearfully down the ragged steps, drawn by a flickering light until you see - two ancient priests, beckoning you to join them in their cavern; beyond a pillar of flame lie further steps, descending, descending into darkness. The priests speak, strange syllables you’ve never heard, let alone the language. Yet they seem satisfied with your response, stepping aside to allow you to reach those distant steps...

So begins your journey into insanity. Can you survive the madness which awaits, as Lovecraft’s Dreamlands and more sap your faculties as you attempt to fly a route across the world?

-o-

The Doomed Flight is an expansion for Route-Finder: Intercontinental. A copy of RF:I is needed to play TDF.

TDF brings a Lovecraftian horror element to a normal game of RF:I, adding a new DOOM Table to the FLIGHT and CITY tables used in the flying part of Route-Finder. The DOOM Table has 3 levels of somewhat connected storylines, which the player progresses through as they lose more and more of their FACULTIES, their life getting worse and worse until they reach their destination - or Insanity!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Earth Under Siege: Flashpoint – Nemesis Expansion
description:
Nemesis Mode
In this new game mode, Earth Under Siege becomes a boss battler, where your operative team engages in a series of mini-boss battles that culminate with a full overlord battle!

Everyone starts with an identical set of 10 cards.
Each round, players secretly select a card to play then reveal it simultaneously.
Depending on the cards in play (ogres, wizards, positive or negative hobgoblins): various cases and effects are triggered and resolved.

After 9 rounds, the player with the highest score (positive hobgoblins) wins.

Quint is the Latin word meaning “five”. This is a climbing/shedding card game where players race to earn points by playing all their cards first. Additionally, you’ll be competing to have the first pick of the score cards. Score exactly 5 points to earn a bonus. After three rounds, the highest score wins!

A promo pack of 17 cards offered as part of the "Beast Within Novel" crowdfunding campaign.

Includes:
It Starts...
A Victim Found
Shanty Red
Where Are You Going?
The Crime Scene
Prove It
Safe Haven
Have A Heart
Duct Tape
An Ill Omen
You've Got Red On You
Your Tombstone
A Summer Home
The Awakened
Dante Sinferno
Karen Needlemeyer
A Very Dark Power

boardgame: Superclub Rivals: Pink River City vs Green Hill Rangers
description:
Add these two fan favourites, known from the Top Six Superclub expansion, to your Superclub Rivals.

Includes ~80 cards in total: 

    Player cards
    Developed player cards
    Stadium cards
    Style cards
    Key staff cards


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Superclub Rivals: Orange Beach FC vs Brown Park FC
description:
Add two historic teams, known from the Powerhouses Superclub expansion, to your Superclub Rivals.

Includes ~80 cards in total: 

    Player cards
    Developed player cards
    Stadium cards
    Style cards
    Key staff cards


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Superclub Rivals: Black Lake FC vs White Peak FC
description:
Add two historic teams, known from the Powerhouses Superclub expansion, to your Superclub Rivals.

Includes ~80 cards in total: 

    Player cards
    Developed player cards
    Stadium cards
    Style cards
    Key staff cards


—description from the publisher

SEBANGAU is a game about you doing your job photographing birds in the wild. The first one to complete the target number of photos requested by the customer is the winner. It's not an easy job, as they will always moving.

Each turn, players can move markers from one Location tile to another, to secretly fulfill the conditions on their task cards, and prevent their opponents from fulfilling their conditions.
The player gets points if the condition on the task card is fulfilled and performs the Snap action.

The first player to fulfill 3 points wins the game.

Target Ahead is the first book in the series of World War 2 Air War wargame rules in 1/600th scale and covers the classic fighter v bomber engagements. Each game is designed to be a balanced contest lasting around two hours. Target Ahead has two games based on the same objectives and structure featuring day and night raids.

Players can use the prepared air forces for the 6 major national WW2 combatants or research their own battle scenarios. A Battle of Britain scenario is included.

The rules use three distinct time periods. 
Early War 1939 - 1940,  
Mid War 1941 - 1943 &  
Late War 1944 - 1945

Flights & Squadrons

     In Target Ahead the fighting unit is the flight which has either three or four aircraft. A squadron has 12 aircraft of either three or four flights. Two additional flights of 4 aircraft support the squadron giving a total of 20 aircraft in each air force. Special rules apply to jets and night-fighters.


Attacker & Defender

     The attacking player has a squadron of bombers with additional support flights that can operate independently. The mission is to destroy a target (e.g. aircraft factory, oil refinery, docks, etc) and deplete the opposing fighter force. The defending player leads a squadron of fighters and support flights from an airfield, which is also a potential target for the attacker.


Movement & Combat

     Aircraft have movement factors (in inches) and combat uses six-sided dice.


Winning & Losing

     The game is won and lost by the number of points accrued based on force size, game set-up, aircrew quality, target damage and aircraft lost.


Action Cards

     Players have 12 Action Cards to enhance their forces. 


Equipment

     Players will need a playing table of 48”x 48” and 6”x 6” scenery templates for the Target and Airfield. 12 standard six-sided dice (D6) are used; 6 of one colour and 6 of another colour. A tape measure, ruler, or pre-marked measuring sticks in inches and a supply of markers are needed. Aircraft models are mounted on flat or elevated flight bases.

This is a multiplayer pencil-paper game, which is kinda like dots and boxes but more complex. It's a fun-sized party game made for people to use strategy and creativity. You draw a large shape on a piece of paper to set up the game, then the players take turns drawing lines. Not only are there multiple different types of lines that you can draw, but every line must start at an angle but end at a line or another angle, without overlapping another line. When a person draws a line that player has to put down a tally mark near their name outside of the shape. The first to get 30 tally marks gets eliminated and then the other players can continue on that shape or start a new one without the eliminated player(s), the last one standing wins. But if you make a triangle on your turn you have to go again until you lose or don't make a triangle.

This is the short version of the official ruleset that I have right here, https://drive.google.com/file/d/18t2rtogqkLto6LAeFWSPNFCCLt5MnaMk/view?usp=sharing

—description from the designer

There is an ongoing battle. The Speculator(s) are organised and committed to privatising every resource available in society: environmental, urban, health and intangible (knowledge based) goods. You must cooperate with other players to prevent privatisation and liberate as many goods as possible. Your goal is to turn them into public goods or, even better, common ones. Work together as a team to fight this dystopia!

Chmielnik 1241 cover the battle of Chmielnik which was fought between Polish forces under the command of the Kraków voivode, Vladimir, and Mongol troops under the command of Baydar during the first Mongol invasion of Poland in the 13th century.

Game is a part of the Medieval system by TiS and uses its mechanics. Ownership of Legnica 1241 is required to play, because it comes with no rules, only with board, scenario set up. Some units should be taken from Legnica 1241 game as well.

Chmielnik 1241 was published in the 50th issue of Taktyka i Strategia magazine.

Pszczyna 1939 covers the battle of Pszczyna fought between 1 and 2 September 1939 near the town of Pszczyna during the invasion of Poland. The battle of Pszczyna formed part of the defensive Battle of the Border. The initial victory of the Polish forces on September 1, 1939 was followed by the crushing defeat on the next day.

Game is a part of the B-35 system by TiS and uses its mechanics. Ownership of any game of the series is required to play, because it comes with no rules, only with board, scenario set up and all the counters.
family: Series: Bitwy II wojny światowej 1943-45 (Dragon / Taktyka i Strategia)

description:
Fiordy 1945 is an expansion for the 4th edition of the Bitwy II Wojny Światowej core game. It comes with map nr 3, that shows scandinavia-like area and 230 counters, depicting new German, allied and Swedish units. There are also new scenarios for the series.

Since it is expansion, ownership of the core game is neccessery to play.
family: Series: Bitwy II wojny światowej 1943-45 (Dragon / Taktyka i Strategia)

description:
Diceverse is a fast-paced duel game combining cards and dice. A rift between universes has opened, and a deadly battle for total power has begun. Players choose one of four unique armies from different universes and lead them to victory.

Choose one of four unique armies from different universes and lead them to victory. Each faction boasts its own deck and special abilities. Play cards to command your dice army, maneuver across the battlefield, and attack your opponent.

The game ends when one player's Base is destroyed or has no units left on the board.

Move men and mechs shoulder to steel shoulder across the lands of a far future earth in Desolation Terra. A fully interactable map means that how you combine your units during skirmishes is vital. Inspired by mech builder games and military mech anime, Desolation Terra sees bands of brothers bound by bonds of war, whether flesh or steel take to the battlefields to dominate your foes and seize the future.

Desolation Terra is a fast-paced skirmish-level sci-fi wargame. Humanity has advanced in the fields of human augmentation bio-engineering & begun it's expansion to the stars, but it is far from united. Rival factions battle over resources, technology, and political power, seeking control over the future of human civilization. Battlefields are fought with Tactical Combat Armors (TCA) that have the maneuverability of a fighter jet and the armor and armament of a tank, Infantry that move across the battlefield capturing key strategic points and vehicles that help push your battle lines forward.

—description from the publisher

COBR is a semi co-operative game that requires all players to work together as a team to successfully manage a crisis facing the UK Government.

If the team gets through the crisis, victory can be claimed by the player that steered the eventual outcome to align with their own hidden agenda.

Players will have to work hard and be resourceful in order to maintain the nation's stability, as failure is not an option.

—description from designer

An exciting action game for toddlers.

It's a merry mess in the kitchen of Sesame Street. Cookie Monster is eating all the cookies, although if you're fast maybe there will still be a few left for you. Follow the trail of cookies Cookie Monster has left behind, the other players also want some cookies so you'd better run...

Designed for toddlers and to be played on the floor, with a large plastic board that is easy to clean. The four coloured playing pieces are large and squat so they can't fall over, a spinner replaces the die. Children will soon be able to play The big Cookie Monster Floor Game without adult supervision.
(approximate translation of the description on the box)

Split Stories is a puzzle and communication game for 2 players (or 4 in teams).

Choose one of the four scenarios (L'Evasion, Apollo 17, Hasta Luego, L'Au-delà). Each player takes one of the two booklets for the scenario, along with any additional documents.

You can take notes, but you are not allowed to look at the other player’s booklet or show them yours.

Each player reads their introduction text silently, then both read the opening dialogue out loud together, playing the roles assigned in their booklets.

From there, you’ll need to communicate and collaborate to solve the objectives listed in your booklets.

boardgame: Stadt Land Vollpfosten: Levels – Classic Edition
description: In this variation of the classic Stadt Land Vollpfosten the players walk through 9 categories (pyramid 4-3-2-1) with different first letters.

boardgame: Cold Comfort: Hospitality in the Yukon Gold Rush
description:
Cold Comfort is a simple area-control city-building game about hospitality and hotel management in the Yukon Gold Rush. Players will earn money by hosting customers in their hotels, and they score points by building up neighborhoods and keeping them occupied.

Players are hoteliers in the Yukon Gold Rush and will cater to prospectors as they pass through Dawson City, serving each customer according to their needs. Players will take quick turns taking various actions, which include building hotels, inviting guests into those hotels, checking them out again, and growing the town

—description from the publisher

Trivia game about one of Finland's large cities, Jyväskylä. The game has 720 questions and is suitable for everyone who wants to learn more about this Finnish city at the north end of lake Päijänne, founded in 1837 and famous for sports and university life, generally regarded as the capital of Central Finland. There are 6 categories of questions: Culture, Sports, History, Buildings & streets, Businesses & restaurants, and The near environs & nature. Each route on the board is tied to one of the categories, and the first and last question on that route will automatically be from that category.

The name of the game refers to the famous meeting point Compass at the pedestrian street in the centre of Jyväskylä, a compass shape set in the paving stones and also illuminated at night. Thematically, the players have agreed to meet each other at the Compass and whoever wins the game made it in time, while all others are late.

The game is basically the Jyväskylä edition of Nähdään kellon alla!, the original version set in Helsinki. This is the fourth instalment of the main games in the Nähdään series, and the first one with 720 questions. This game is in Finnish only.

In Flocking Goats, you’ll dive into a world of high-stakes strategy and clever deception. Face off against your opponent in a battle of wits as you plot sneaky schemes to grow your flock. Some flocks, once completed, are rare and untouchable, while others are easier to gather but prime targets for theft. At every turn, you must decide whether to expand your herd, protect your goats, or sabotage your rival before they do the same to you. Attack cards let you steal, disrupt, and create chaos, while power cards boost your herding speed, and interrupt cards let you block or redirect attacks.

At key moments, you may use the Duel Medallion, a high-stakes bluffing showdown where deception is your greatest weapon. Lie or tell the truth, but your strategy could backfire if your opponent sees through you. A single misread could win you the game—or cost you everything.

Unexpected Guests
But just when you think you’re in control, chaos strikes—Belly the Wolf devours part of your flock, the Sneaky Shepherd Dog forces a hand swap, and the Bare-All Nanny exposes your cards, leaving you open to attack.

Flocking Goats is easy to learn, quick to play, and packed with tense mind games and unpredictable moments. Every round brings sudden twists, dramatic turnarounds, and bold plays that keep you on edge. With fast gameplay, huge comeback potential, and endless strategic possibilities, only the most cunning player will claim victory.

—description from the publisher

Start the game by placing the number chips on the board red side up in the positions shown on the randomly selected game play card.

Each player then lays down a chip in turn and attempts to be the first complete the grid using the remaining chips.

Play to win rows, columns and blocks and then watch as other player win them back.

Scout, sign, train and manage 234 real Bundesliga players. That’s 13 players from each club. Player names, images, star ratings and club crest on all player cards. Includes the new attacking midfielder and defensive midfielder player categories. Use the Bundesliga draft board by itself or together with the Superclub components you know and love. 
Compatible with all other game products from the Superclub universe.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Helheim: Strategic and Educational Board/Card Game
description:
Welcome to Helheim, the dark and mysterious underworld of Norse mythology. Prepare for an epic battle of wits and strategy in this immersive card-based war game. Only the bravest and most cunning warriors will emerge victorious, their names forever etched in the sagas of old.

In Helheim, your goal is to complete as many group cards as possible or defeat your opponent by eliminating all their cards. To achieve victory, you must outmaneuver your rivals, capturing their cards while defending your own forces. Carefully position your warriors on the front and back lines of the cardholders, using tactical placement and powerful abilities to gain the upper hand. Every move matters, as the tides of battle can shift in an instant.

But Helheim is more than just a fight for dominance—it is a journey into the heart of Norse mythology. As you play, you will encounter legendary figures, fearsome creatures, and ancient myths, bringing the rich lore of the Vikings to life. This blend of strategy and history makes Helheim both an engaging challenge and an educational experience.

Only those who master both strategy and knowledge will seize victory and claim their rightful place as the ruler of Helheim. Do you have what it takes to conquer the underworld and secure your place among the legends? The battle awaits!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Once Upon a Time in Phlogiston
description:
Once Upon a Time in Phlogiston is a simultaneously selected action-programming worker placement game set in a sprawling interstellar mafia empire.

After decades of conquest and expansion, the Jena Federation — a rogue immigrant ship-turned-superpower — has reached the peak of its influence across the Star Systems. But with the sudden death of its supreme leader, the Federation descends into turmoil. Players take on the roles of high-ranking lieutenants in the criminal hierarchy, each vying to become the next supreme leader.

Over the course of the game, players will gather allies, build influence, complete underworld jobs, and expand their territory across a network of planets and operations. Every round, players secretly choose their actions and then resolve them simultaneously, leading to tight strategic choices, daring bluffs, and unpredictable power struggles.

With a rich theme blending space opera, criminal politics, and high-stakes betrayal, Phlogiston delivers a deeply strategic euro-style experience wrapped in a cinematic narrative arc.

Trivia game about one of Finland's towns, Kauniainen (Grankulla in Swedish). The game has 212 questions and is suitable for everyone who wants to learn more about this small, well-to-do municipality in the Helsinki metropolitan area, which is fully enclaved by the much larger municipality of Espoo. "See you in Grani" - Grani being the nickname derived from the town's Swedish name, which would roughly mean "Spruce Hill".

This is the 9th instalment in the Nähdään series of trivia games about Finnish cities and towns, and with this game the series took a turn from a full-sized trivia boardgame to pocket-sized, single deck trivia quiz games. The mechanics have changed, too: There are far fewer cards now but each card has four questions of increasing value. The player in turn can answer those as long as they gets them right, but as soon as they fail, other players in turn will have a chance to "steal" the question.

As befitting the bilingual municipality of Kauniainen, this game is fully Finnish/Swedish bilingual; each card has exactly the same questions in Finnish and Swedish on opposite sides of the card.

Trivia game about one of Finland's towns, Hämeenlinna. The game has 428 questions and is suitable for everyone who wants to learn more about this mid-sized town in the interior of Southern Finland, famous for the medieval castle and for being the northern terminus of the first railway line in Finland (Helsinki-Hämeenlinna, 1862). "See you in Hämpton!" - Hämpton being one of the local nicknames of the place and probably imitating -hampton in English, where the a would be pronounced as ä in Finnish.

This is the 10th instalment in the Nähdään series of trivia games about Finnish cities and towns, and like the preceding Nähdään Granissa is a pocket-sized, single deck trivia quiz game - though with several hundred more questions, due to being in Finnish only which means that each card can hold two sets of four questions of increasing difficulty and point value, instead of just one.

A pirate and mermaid themed expansion adds two new villages, Bluefin Harbor and Suncrest bay, five new Allies, and twelve adventure cards which introduce the "flood" mechanic that adds an extra row to the grid.

Take your adventure to the next level with the First EndHaven Expansion: New Tides! Delve into uncharted territories filled with daring pirates, enchanting mermaids, and fearsome sea monsters.  Embark on an epic journey across the high seas, where every wave brings new challenges and exciting discoveries. Rally your courage to save the town from the impending Flood, and become the Hero who stands against the rising tide.

1 New Mechanic and 1 New Feature: Flood and Match Dice Slots on Adventure Cards

Flood: Reveal and place the top card of the Adventure Deck on a separate row. This row does not refill at the start of each day.

Match Dice Slots: Once the first dice is placed on a Match Dice Slot only dice of the same value may be placed on the remaining match slots on that card. [1]'s may NOT be used.

For the first time ever, Adventure Cards will feature Match Dice Slots, offering players even more daring and strategic options for dice placement.

2 New Towns on one double-sided card.

-description from publisher's website

This zombie and vampire themed expansion adds an Undead Deck, which adds an extra row to the grid through the new "raise up" mechanic, seven Allies, thirteen adventure cards, and two villages, Grimmshire and Wraithmoore.

A dark and ominous air descends upon the town. The long-abandoned Castle on the Hill now flickers with an eerie light, signaling the Dark Master's return. Whispers of necromancy and occult fill the night. Can you unravel the mysteries in time to prevent legions of the undead from being unleashed? Embark on EndHaven's biggest and most terrifying expansion yet.

1 New Mechanic and 1 New Feature: Raise Up and Undead Adventure Cards.

Raise Up: Reveal and place 1 card from the Undead Deck on a separate row. This row does not refill at the start of each day.
Undead Adventure Cards: Tougher more sinister creatures await in the depths of the Undead Deck. Careful that you are not overrunned by the legions of the night

Multiplayer like never before now with more ways to Draw Ally Cards
7 NEW Allies join the fray, with mysterious and powerful abilities.
2 New Towns on one double-sided card. Choose your difficulty of the Zombie Apocalypse!

=description from publisher's website

boardgame: Tsukuyumi: Full Moon Down (Second Edition) – Forces of Nature
description:
Adds the following three factions to the game:

    Sentinels - Smite the Onis and cleanse the world from its demons.
    Lords Of The Lost Sea - Wander the ocean ground and find a new home for your tribe.
    Fireborn - Set the world ablaze and let it sink in deeper into chaos!


—description from the publisher

This Egyptian mythology inspired expansion adds two more villages, Dama Oasis and The Eternal City, five Allies, and twelve adventure cards, introducing the "grand ascent" mechanic that forces one to discard the lowest value cards from the grid.

In the Distant Lands, you'll find yourself on the brink of an ancient world—a realm brimming with curses, temple guardians, and ancient gods. Here, the old deities still roam, and the strange and mysterious are commonplace. But beware, the desert is unforgiving to strangers, and your journey grows more perilous with each passing moment. Gather your resolve, for you are the only force standing between the immortal rulers and their ascent to power.

1 New Mechanic and 1 New Feature: Grand Ascent and Negative Reputation Adventure Cards

Grand Ascent: Exchange the lowest RP Adventure Card with the top card from the Adventure Deck, then shuffle.

Negative Reputation Adventure Cards: Currently, there are three Adventure Cards that feature Negative Reputation Points, each with the lowest dice values in the game. Will you complete these cards to unlock a powerful combo? Can you handle the Grand Ascent consequences that alter the playing field? Or will you leverage the low dice value to make the most of a limited roll? The choice is yours.

2 New Towns on one double-sided card.

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Tsukuyumi: Full Moon Down (Second Edition) – New Dawn
description:
Adds three brand-new factions to the game:

    Dark Crusade
    Chronomasters
    Sisters Of Seven


Turn the battelfield updside down by reversing the order of your actions or taking complete control over the Oni, or ...by bringing a Cybear to a gun fight.

—description from the publisher

These were available to backers of the Dicekeepers: Endhaven campaign: a legendary ally Ora Slumbering Goddess and the stretch goal ally Lucky, along with fifteen alternate art cards for the base and expansion decks, contained in the Card Vault pack.

Note: Lucky and Ora Slumbering Goddess are new promos while the others are all alternate artwork cards.

boardgame: Tsukuyumi: Full Moon Down (Second Edition) – Scenario Booklet
description: The scenario booklet includes 10 scenarios. Play unique board setups, special rules and unique faction pairings.

Catchables names both a set of collectible squishy foam toys and a game played with these toys.
To play, each player starts with their own Catchable and an action card. On a turn, a player throws their Catchable in the air, then performs the stunt on their action card. If they succeed, they place two additional action cards in front of themselves or other players, stacking additional challenges for a more challenging next turn; if they fail, they discard one or more of their cards.

The first player to complete a catch while performing at least five action cards wins.

This set contains two new cats (Caterina and Catastrophe), as well as 24 new action cards.

Catchables names both a set of collectible squishy foam toys and a game played with these toys.

To play, each player starts with their own Catchable and an action card. On a turn, a player throws their Catchable in the air, then performs the stunt on their action card. If they succeed, they place two additional action cards in front of themselves or other players, stacking additional challenges for a more challenging next turn; if they fail, they discard one or more of their cards.

The first player to complete a catch while performing at least five action cards wins.

This set contains two new cats (DJ Cat & Cat-tle), as well as 24 action cards.

The Village Pillage Big Box comes with every Village Pillage card ever printed and can hold them all (sleeved) in a new box.

Includes:

     Surf and Turf Expansions
     Robbin Hood Expansion
     Dark Arts and Crafts Expansion
     All Promo Cards and Tokens


Not content to just be a complete version of Village Pillage and a supremely efficient storage solution, the Village Pillage Big Box also comes with three exclusive mini-expansions of its own.


     The Festivals Expansion adds new jumbo custom wooden meeples that move around the board and power up villagers with new powers and interactions.
     The Cultists Expansion infiltrates Village Pillage with new cultist cards, each with strange new powers. The more cultists played in a round, the more powerful they grow...but when they're played directly against one another, strange things occur.
     The Feelings Expansion finally allows villagers to feel feelings. Each player draws a Feeling card at the start of the game to give them a unique play style.


—description from the publisher

Cannabiz is a new worker placement game where you play the role of a small cannabis grower. You start off with limited processing ability, but you can upgrade and expand your production facility to be able to produce a variety of consumables to fulfill customer orders. Hire new staff, learn new processing techniques, combine terpenes to produce plants that have characteristics your customers want.  Be the first to reach 20 victory points to trigger the game's end.

Cannabiz - Grow your small cannabis production company into a booming empire.

—description from the designer

Dice Batter is a dice and card-based game for 2-4 players where the goal is to be the first to score 20 points. Players roll seven dice each turn and take turns placing them on a shared cookie sheet card, matching the numbers rolled. If a player rolls a higher number, they can bump an opponent’s die from the sheet.
Once the cookie sheet is full or no more moves can be made, all placed dice are collected into a mixing bowl. The round’s first player stirs the dice with a wooden spoon and randomly selects one die to roll. The owner of the die scores points equal to the rolled number. Play continues in rounds until one player reaches 20 points.
The game includes a mix of strategy and luck, as players decide which dice to place while also hoping for a favorable outcome in the mixing phase. Optional rules allow for slight variations in play, such as whether players can shield their dice from opponents. A typical game lasts around 10-20 minutes.

—description from the publisher

Tortilla Takedown is a competitive card game for 3-4 players, where the goal is to be the first to eliminate an opponent. Players take turns playing action cards to attack, block, and counter their rivals.

Key cards include "No Way José," which cancels an opponent’s move, and "Flying Fajita," which can redirect damage and even be played out of turn to help or hurt an alliance. Players can also execute a Mega Takedown Combo, stringing together multiple attacks for a big play based on the tiles drawn.

With simple mechanics and strategic decision-making, Tortilla Takedown is a quick, interactive game of timing and tactics. The game ends as soon as one player is eliminated, making each round fast-paced and competitive.

—description from thed esigner

What’s Under Your Bed? is a fast-paced, easy-to-learn card game where players compete to build the biggest pile of monster cards by the end of the game. Each turn, players play a card from their hand, attempting to match a monster in the center or on another player’s pile. A match allows them to claim that pile, while unmatched cards create new open piles for future turns.
Adding to the chaos, special action cards—Steal, Split, Switch, and Rotate—let players shake things up by swiping piles, splitting stacks, or forcing players to swap their hard-earned collections. Every round, the stakes get higher as the deck dwindles and players scramble to secure the largest monster hoard.

With simple rules, strategic depth, and plenty of opportunities to mess with your opponents, What’s Under Your Bed? is perfect for families, casual gamers, and anyone who enjoys quick, competitive gameplay. Once the final card is played, the player with the biggest monster pile wins!

—description from the designer

Catventures is a purr-fect game all about cats. The board has a winding path made up of blank spaces alternating with spaces with a paw print. Players roll the die and move that many spaces. If they land on a space with a cat paw they draw a card from the deck. Each card has an activity that the player has to perform. They may then move the number of spaces listed on the card. If they land on a blank square play passes to the next player. The first player to reach the ball of yarn at the end of the path is the winner.

Super Bobs is an Australian skill game from the 1940s. An inclined rectangular playing board has 3 channels that players propel 3 glass balls along using a wooden cue. As the balls travel along the incline they stop on a score. If balls align across the board the players score is doubled. The highest score is the winner or the game can be played up to 500.

—user summary

boardgame: The Case of the Curiously Correct Blueprints
description:
As a member of the SHhhh, We're Reading Book Club in Hincksville, you've been patiently awaiting your turn to read the advance copy of the highly anticipated new Ells and Ernie mystery, when Mr. Hincks gives you some shocking news: your copy of the book is at the bottom of a vat of candy! Never fear, Mr. Hincks will save the day! (Which is only fair, seeing as he was the one that ruined the day.)

Mr. Hincks has recorded an abridged audio version of the book and put together a box of peculiar reference materials in order to recreate the story for you before the book club meeting. But there's one problem: the first rule of book club is, NO SPOILERS! How can Mr. Hincks recap this mystery book for you without spoiling any of the twists? You're not quite sure, but he seems to have a few puzzling tricks up his sleeves.

Can you unlock all the audio clips before the SHhhh, We're Reading Book Club meeting? What are these mysterious markups on the reference pages?? How can a set a blueprints that are correct be curious???

Answer all of these questions and more in... The Case of The Curiously Correct Blueprints!

—description from the publisher

A 12-card microgame with double-sided cards and a flipping mechanism derived from Othello. One player controls the crows (who want to throw trash all over the place) and the other trash (which wants to be neat).

Cards are played into a grid. If a player adds a new card that sandwiches the opponent's cards between two of their own, either horizontally, vertically or diagonally, those cards are flipped to the active player's side.

Each player also has a single special card which blocks adjacent placement, except by another special card.

Once all cards have been played, the winner is the player with the most cards showing.

The gameboard has recesses into which you initially fill the small red and black disks. Over the course of the game, you move all your big playing pieces to the other side of the board as quickly as possible.

You only move diagonally over disks and playing pieces of your own color, but you may change directions anytime.

Your turn always ends on a disk of your opponent's color. With this, you cover the space with a piece of your color, build a bridge and thus enhance your moving options.

If you are the first player to reach the target spaces on the opposite side with all 4 of your playing pieces, you win.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 同人ゲーム：宇宙艦隊司令官の夜(Doujin game: Night of Star Fleet Commanders)
description:
A semi-cooperative wargame based on the Japanese space opera novel/anime "Legend of the Galactic Heroes". It is currently in development and playtesting as a doujin (self-published) game.

Players lead one of the factions of the Free Planets Alliance, gather characters from the story, and have them join star fleets on campaigns, aiming to achieve great feats.

The opposing Galactic Empire is controlled by the game system, and defeating the Empire is a means to an end; the goal is to promote characters, outwit the other factions, and become the dominant faction of the Free Planets Alliance.

boardgame: Stworze: Zakazana Polana – Pre-order promo cards
description: Four additional cards - 2 Events and 2 Quest cards, which were added only for the copies ordered via pre-order campaign on the publisher's website.
Two additional Event cards, which were added only for the copies ordered via pre-order campaign on the publisher's website.

18HN is a privately developed and produced variant of the 18xx games, based in central Germany. 
It adopts the basic mechanics of Francis Tresham's classic variants and adds its own special content on regional characteristics. 1835 by Michael Meier-Bachl and 1844 by Helmut Ohley also had a major influence on the development of the game.
The players start with the same amount of starting capital and try to earn as much money as possible by buying and selling shares in various stock corporations that have operated in the Hesse & Nassau region. The game board uses a hexagonal layout on which the railroad companies place track sections, build stations and then fictitiously run their locomotives in order to earn money from which the shareholders can profit. However, the railroad companies can also get into difficulties if they cannot keep up with the technical progress of the trains.
A game is over when the bank runs out of money or a player is forced to file for bankruptcy. The player with the most personal assets then wins.
18HN joins the many variants that have followed Francis Tresham's early publications, but 18HN is a purely privately developed and published game with which the developers do not seek to make a profit. Therefore, when an order is placed, only compensation for production costs is requested.

New promotional temple card. Shuffle the card with the other temple cards during set up.
This temple requires your Cóatl to have no black pieces and/or no red pieces.
Point value:
1 requirement fulfill: 3 points
2 requirement fulfill:; 7 points

A party game that combines the world of the anime Attack on Titan with the classic Werewolf game mechanics. Players are divided into two factions: the Human faction and the Titan faction. The Human faction aims to protect their territory and identify the "Intelligent Titans" hidden among them, while the Titan faction seeks to deceive humans and eliminate them.

The game offers four levels of play: Beginner, Standard, Expert, and Ultimate. In the Beginner mode, players take on the roles of characters from Attack on Titan without special abilities, focusing on basic gameplay. As players progress to higher levels, additional elements such as "Wall Cards," "Titan Cards," "Event Cards," and character-specific abilities are introduced, adding complexity and depth to the game.

Each round consists of phases including life-and-death judgment, titan appearance, discussion, voting or titan extermination, wall destruction, and role activation. The game continues until one faction achieves its victory conditions. For the Human faction, this involves either imprisoning all Intelligent Titans or winning a final battle against the Titans. The Titan faction wins by achieving specific conditions such as equalling the number of Intelligent Titans to other players, destroying all walls, or winning the final battle.

The game is designed for 9 to 17 players, with an estimated playtime of 45 to 90 minutes. The rules can be adjusted to accommodate different difficulty levels, making it accessible for both newcomers and experienced players.

FloraVista is a card matching game where players try to curate their own botanical garden with plants from around the world.

In FloraVista, gameplay is divided into twelve rounds called Seasons. During each Season, players can draw cards and play Matching Pairs, consisting of one Plant and Region Card, to build out their Player Garden. At the start of the game, each player selects one of eight historic botanists, represented by Specialist Cards, that earn bonus points at the end of the game.

Plant Cards range in value from 1 to 5 Cultivation Points. They can only be played in certain seasons and with certain Region Cards. The Plant Cards that can only be planted in one Season and one Region are worth 5 Cultivation Points. The Plant Cards that are the easiest to play are worth only 1 Cultivation Point, but they offer players bonus abilities to offset their lower value.

FloraVista is easy to learn but rich in strategy. Players must adapt to maximize their Cultivation Points and build the best garden. Welcome to FloraVista: Our World. Your Garden!

—description from the designer

The Finnish Badminton Game is a pocket-sized, single deck trivia game all about badminton in Finland - the players, the successes, badminton halls and clubs. The game is designed by Jesper von Hertzen, who is better known for his "Nähdään" series of trivia games about Finnish towns and cities.

There are 212 questions, with each card having four questions for a maximum total of 4 points per card. The player in turn can answer those as long as they get them right, but as soon as they fail, other players in turn will have a chance to "steal" the question (except of course the player reading the questions!). The game is played until someone wins by getting a total of 21 points.

The game is fully Finnish/Swedish bilingual; each card has exactly the same four questions in Finnish and Swedish on opposite sides of the card.

Note that despite the very different theme, the designer does consider this game to be part of the "Nähdään" series of trivia games.

Blackjack Golf is the perfect combination between Blackjack and Golf. Get cards to hit the ball but be careful with fall into the obstacles or get out of the court. Finally test your luck to introduce the ball into the hole.

Welcome to the world of Castle Panic!

Monsters are attacking Castle Bravehold! Join your friends in a desperate struggle against an almost endless siege of Goblins, Orcs, Trolls, Dragons, and more. Fight the Dark Titan Agranok, make a stand against siege engines, and embark on challenging quests to survive.

This Big Box collection includes second editions of the base game Castle Panic, plus 4 expansions: The Wizard’s Tower, The Dark Titan, Engines of War, and Crowns and Quests. These sets can be combined for a varied, customizable experience and the ultimate Castle Panic adventure.

Castle Panic
The best-selling, cooperative, tower-defense game. Work together to play cards and slay Monsters before they destroy the Castle.

The Wizard's Tower
Cast powerful spells as a wizard joins your forces. Fight flying, climbing, fire-breathing Monsters, and face down the beastly Mega Bosses.

The Dark Titan
Enlist the Cavalier and Support Tokens as you make your stand against Agranok and his new Monsters.

Engines of War
