Use the Engineer to build Catapults, Ballistas, Barricades, and more. Survive the attack from enemy Siege Engines and new, powerful Monsters.

Crowns and Quests
Play as a royal or friend of the court with unique abilities that help you complete special quests to win the game.

Includes 7 promo cards and 6 promo towers.

2nd Edition includes new artwork, and custom die.

—description from the back of the box
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Players take the role of monster tamers, and capture a team of monsters to lead to battle. After assembling a team, they choose from a set of actions to form their deck and do battle against each other. Players must not only battle their opponents' monsters but also help their starter monster accomplish their goals in order to gain victory points and come out on top after 4 rounds.

The game is set up through drafting starter monsters, common monsters and forming an action deck. Monsters are then placed on the map strategically. Each player has 3 columns and 2 monsters per column, an attacker and a defender.

On a player's turn, they select a column that has not yet been selected. The attacker in that column attacks the opponent's defender in that column and the attacking player may choose to  play an action card face down. Afterwards, the defending player may choose to play a card and the attacking player flips their card after, allowing for hidden information.

After all columns attack, players may, in reverse order, discard a card to activate the effect of a monster in a column. This allows for powerful swings in the game, and requires players to plan what cards to use and what cards to keep in order to use these tactic effects. Once all columns are done, any remaining cards in hand, the discard pile and deck are shuffled together and a new hand is drawn.

Halfway through the game, players are allowed to draft one more monster, with whichever player is losing getting first choice at the new monster, allowing for comeback moments.

—description from the designer
Bohnanza has been released in several combination sets. Instead of containing the ususal base game and the original expansion, this contains the revised expansion.

A game of Australian colonial history for 2-8 players in two teams: redcoats (colonial soldiers) chase bushrangers (outlaws) through the outback.

On each turn, a player draws from one of several decks of action cards. While free and on land this will be the Pace deck, a mixture of optional movement cards (move a number of spaces on a single terrain type, more on roads and grassland and less in rougher terrain) and thematic events. Redcoats can only catch bushrangers by drawing from the Capture deck. Captured rangers draw from the Escape deck in the hope of a lucky break. Anyone in the river draws from the Swim deck, which tends to wash them back downstream towards the camp, but as bushrangers can't be captured in the water it may be the only means of escape.

Redcoats win by escorting a captured bushranger back to camp; bushrangers win if one escapes to their den.

Bohniläum, a 15th anniversary edition of Bohnanza with artwork commissioned from fifteen artists, includes 180 cards and is labeled as being for 3-7 players, unlike the original Bohnanza game in Germany, which is only for 3-5 players.

Join is for the ultimate pocket sized and super charged Space Lion experience! 2 Players, light and portable! Level up your units as you fight for control of 3 outposts. Play cards in outposts and bases, face-up or face-down. Be tricky! Be strategic!

Play cards from your hand face-up or face-down into 3 outposts or play them into your base for a base effect. After all players have played all their cards, cards battle by highest total strength in an area. Units that won are promoted and win medals.

Collect 10 medals to win the game!

—description from the designer

Red Dog Junction is a fast-paced board game of high-stakes trading, crew-building, and strategic maneuvering in the far reaches of space. As the captain of a rogue freighter, players navigate the lawless frontier, mining valuable resources, salvaging lost cargo, and making high-risk trades at the notorious Red Dog Junction. Rival captains compete for fortune, and deception is always an option.

Each turn, players take two actions, moving their ships between bases, planets, the fence at Honest Joe's Haberdashery, the Red Dog trading exchange, and deep space. They mine for ore, salvage wreckage, trade on the ever-shifting market, and recruit crew members to improve efficiency. Upgrading ship storage, hiring specialist officers, and executing powerful one-time boosts provide strategic advantages. The game ends when all the gold is stored in player bases, making timing and resource management critical.

With a dynamic economy driven by player actions, Red Dog Junction offers a blend of worker placement, deck-building, pick-up-and-delivery, and market manipulation. The game plays about 15-20 minutes per player, creating a rich and highly interactive experience that rewards clever planning, risk-taking, and strategic maneuvering in a shifting economy.

—description from the publisher

Portland's airport is expanding and airlines are racing to become the city's favorite. In PDX, you take on the role of an airline competing to expand your concourse, open destinations, and optimize your routes through strategic resource management.

Each turn, players assign workers to airport offices to secure new destinations, construct gates, open service hubs, and invest in advertising campaigns. As planes arrive and depart, airlines gain actions and service tokens representing resources such as comfort, shopping, and food — that are used to unlock new routes and enhance airport infrastructure. Efficiently managing destinations and aligning services with market demand is key to outpacing the competition.

Throughout the game, players must balance long-term growth with tactical decisions, rearranging destinations on their gates, launching flights, and leveraging executive actions for strategic advantages. Effectively manage routes to chain bonus actions and time advertising campaigns to get ahead. The game ends when the airport reaches full capacity—either through office expansion or market saturation—at which point the airline with the most prestige wins.

PDX is a breezy 2-4 player game that blends worker placement, tableau management, and economic strategy into a highly interactive airport expansion race where every decision impacts the evolving air travel landscape. The game includes variants for seasoned players and can be played with the dice-based automa in solo mode or added to a 2 or 3 player game as an extra player.

—description from the publisher

This Deadbolt Mystery Society Admit One Escape Room PUZZLE BOX is a must-have for any puzzle enthusiast. With intricate details and challenging puzzles, it's sure to provide hours of thrilling entertainment. Designed for ages 17 and up, it's the perfect addition to any game collection. Featuring the Deadbolt Mystery Society brand and complete with all necessary pieces, this puzzle box is in great shape and ready for its new owner. Whether you're an experienced puzzler or just starting out, the Admit One Escape Room PUZZLE BOX is a fantastic choice for a fun and challenging experience.

—description from the publisher

In Torpedo Los!, players take command of a German Type VII submarine during World War II, navigating the treacherous waters of the Atlantic Ocean in a tense game of cat and mouse with Allied ships and planes. As captain, you'll manage your crew, resources, and vessel while conducting patrols against Allied shipping.

This tactical submarine adventure combines resource management with decisive action. Each patrol challenges players to balance their crew's abilities, boat's technical condition, and limited supplies against the ever-present threats of destroyer escorts, aircraft patrols, and the unforgiving sea itself.

A unique spotting, detection and torpedo and anti-submarine warfare system creates a fun and engaging combat system that is easy to learn and play.

How many patrols can you survive?

—description from the designer

In mAIndset, players compete to construct the most sophisticated and valuable prompts for Generative AI tools such as ChatGPT, Gemini or Copilot by carefully drafting and placing cards representing different prompt elements.

mAIndset is a strategic card drafting game where players  draft cards representing different prompt elements (like Actions, Outputs, Context, Format, Reasoning, and Roles) and arrange them in rows and columns on their personal play area.

The game combines clever card drafting with spatial placement, as players must follow specific rules when building their prompts: each row or column can contain only one card of each type (except Format cards, which allow up to three), and cards can be part of both a row and column. Points are scored based on the length of completed prompts, connections between cards with matching symbols, and special bonuses for creating squares of cards with matching connections. The player with the highest score becomes the Master Prompter.

This game was designed as a support for learning how to become better at prompting for Generative AI.

—description from the designer

Gott Gisk is a fun trivia game that puts importance in knowing your opponents. You can gain points by guessing if your opponents guess the right or wrong answer as well as answering your questions correctly.

Gott gisk er skemmtilegt spurningaspil sem snýst um það að giska hvort andstæðingar þínir svari spurningum rétt eða rangt. Stig eru fengin fyrir að svara spurningu rétt og einnig fyrir að giska á rétta útkomu spurninga andstæðinga þinna.

boardgame: Heart of Crown: 2nd Edition – Far East Territory
description:
Heart of Crown: Second Edition: Far East Territory: Second Edition, adds a new Princess Card, 60 Common Cards and 12 Randomizers.

Notable new cards include:

    Greedy Fairy (lets you stockpile coin counters, and also move counters around between different types, eg 2 gold -> 1 succession point)
    Taxation (lets you put a taxation token (new token type) on a card in the market to increase the cost to buy it until the stack in the market is sold out – can really mess other player’s strategies!)
    Apprentice Witch (gives players without a curse card in hand or top of deck, a curse card, then lets you draw two cards and keep one)


It requires Heart of Crown to play, and it may be integrated with other Heart of Crown expansions.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Phase is heavily celestial in theme with moons, stars, and astronomical events such as: blue moons, meteor showers, and eclipses.

Gameplay is comprised on playing moon phase cards on a gameboard to make pairs and build moon phase sequences. There are also wildcards which can change aspects of gameplay; for instance: getting a second turn, scoring more points for pairs, removing cards from the board, and everyone discarding their hand.

To keep track of points, players use a pool of star tokens to denote which pairs or sequences they made. Other players may steal points by making a new pair or by adding to a sequence. Once a board is full, you gain any star tokens of your color and add them to the ones you've won during play. The goal of the game is to have the most star tokens (points).

—description from the designer

Highly Strategic: This wavelength board game requires players to carefully plan the direction of each move and consider multiple possibilities and potential reactions of their opponents, and this high degree of strategy is able to exercise players' thinking and decision-making skills. It's also a great New Year's gift.

Simple and easy to understand rules: Despite the deep strategic nature of the triggle game, the rules of this game are relatively simple and easy to understand, so that both children and adults can quickly master the game and start playing it. The portable and compact design allows you to easily take out the game wherever you are and start a wonderful game with your friends or family.

High interactivity: This chain triangle chess game is a two-player game, with frequent interactions between players, who need to constantly observe their opponents' actions and respond accordingly, which enhances the fun of interpersonal communication and competition. Whether you are relaxing at home or traveling, you can start a wonderful intellectual competition anytime and anywhere.

Cultivate spatial imagination: During this triggle rubber band game, players need to conceptualize the layout and possible development path of the pieces in their mind. It effectively exercises your logical thinking, analytical judgment and forward-looking planning ability, allowing you to grow up in the game and helping to cultivate and improve your spatial imagination.

Variety: This triangle chess game is full of variations and freshness due to the different starting positions and players' strategic choices in each game. Whether it's the excitement of a novice's first exploration or the tension and excitement of an expert's game, you can find endless fun in it and won't get bored easily.

—description from the publisher

~The Designer’s Notes~

Once, when I did a tarot reading in a certain country, I got a fortune that was so bad. I came up with an idea that one’s destiny is not determined by others, but is something created by one’s own will. So I invented a new card game called “Tottarot”. Those who draw good luck and draw bad luck will change our destiny by our own will and by bluffing the opponents. It is up to me to decide my destiny. It is myself who decide my own destiny.

〜Overview〜

Tottarot is a card game in which victory or defeat is determined by the points of Fate Cards dealt. Even if Fate Cards has negative points, you can discard it and take it away from your opponent depending on a point of your Will Tokens.
Foundations is a core set designed to provide the quintessential Magic: The Gathering experience, with legendary characters and returning mechanics from throughout the history of the game.

boardgame: Disney Princess Treats & Sweets Party Game
description:
Tea Party Game for kids: 
From mermaids to Frozen… you're invited to a Disney Princess Tea Party. Join Elsa, Ariel, Belle, Mulan, and Tiana in a Teatime Celebration.

Pour dice from the Magical Teapot to see where to move on the gameboard and how many Cupcake Cards to turn over. Be the first to collect 5 matches on your plate and you win.

Includes: 1 3 Piece Teapot, 1 Gameboard, 40 Cupcake Cards, 1 Punch Sheet (plates), 4 Teacup Movers, 1 Punch Sheet (Sugar cube tokens), 2 Dice, 1 Label Sheet
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Frog Prince

description: Go for a spin with five of your favorite Disney Princess characters! Spin and Match the character, color, shape, or pattern. Each player gets their own spinner and whenever someone spins SWAP! you all switch spinners. Spin, match, and join the party! Match the four squares on your goal card to win!

There's no place like Ravnica. Walk through familiar streets and encounter a bustling crowd of reprinted favorites from throughout Ravnica's past. Not only can you honor Ravnica's treasured past—you can obtain it too!

—description from the publisher

Rediscover everything you love to fear in Magic's Gothic horror plane with Innistrad Remastered. Feel the nostalgia with a retro frame card in every booster and feast your eyes on new movie poster cards.

—description from the publisher

A bid trick-taking game for 3 or 4 players. Omission is an [h]omage to classic partner card games. You are on a mission to silence high cards to take the win. The [w]rinkle, when an Omission card is played it omits the previous card from the trick.

Omission, it's silent but tricky.

The art of the game leans into the theme of Omission. Card suits use negative space and the special Omission Cards animate - using an extra Barrier-Grid transparency card that comes with the deck.

—description from the designer

Build-a-Cult is a dark themed card-driven engine building game that puts you as the leader of your very own cult. With 270 unique cards, there are endless possibilities to how you will build your cult. No two cults and no two games are ever the same. Amass your followers, demand their devotion, attain an abundance of wealth, and become famous. Convince your followers of the divine powers that you possess or wage war with competing cult leaders. If you choose to do some of the more unsavory techniques you may find the feds knocking at your door.

—description from the designer

A two-player trick-taking and card-drafting race game. Play as dueling pairs of athletes in a relay. Win and lose tricks to draft the “right” card and keep an eye on that finish line.

Now go! Have a Field Day.

Gameplay is divided into Trick Play and Draft Play. This is a must follow, but may always trump, trick-taking game. In other words, if you have a card of the led suit you must play it, but you could instead play a trump card if you have one. During a trick you each play two cards, one at a time in alternating order. After you each play two cards, determine who won the trick - then begin Draft Play. The winner of the trick drafts first. Draft a card then move one of your pawns to the nearest unoccupied space matching the number on the card. To win, get both of your pawns past the finish line before your opponent.

-description from designer

The King has summoned you to save the kingdom from the coming darkness. You are the lone bulwark against Chaos. And you will fight with paper, pencil and a standard deck of playing cards. The Last Guardian is a solo adventure game built on the Adventure 52 system, featuring: Deckbuilding, hand management, player choice and narrative card play; 8 different character classes for different play styles; A dozen different quests; Solo or cooperative play.

—description from the publisher

This is a two-player, dice-rolling, push your luck micro-game fashioned as Soccer shootout.

Objective: Win a shootout, best of 5. If tied, best of 1 thereafter.

Setup: Both roll a die. Larger number wins. Pick your team and role to start the game - “Shooter” or “Goalie.”

Play: The Shooter rolls the 1st die. The Goalie selects 1 of 4 corners to block using the green pawn. The Shooter rolls the 2nd die. The sum of the dice determines the location of the shot. The Shooter records the result on their team card using pawns. “+” row = goals. “x” row = blocks. Switch roles until there is a winner.

—description from the designer

Rowin is a splashy, fast-playing strategy game for two players. Line up five of your pieces in a row to win. To play, roll three dice and take turns drafting to move your pieces. Full of tactical twists, draft to block and surprise your opponent.

Rowin has great table presence with its classic look and circular starting position. It can even be played on an 8x8 checkerboard (no corner spaces) with checker pieces and three dice.

—description from the designer

Navigate the social hierarchy of a modern American high school, aiming to be the most popular kid while avoiding the jealousy of classmates. Players score points by winning tricks, earning higher scores when they match the number of tricks taken by other players.

—description from the designer

ESCAPE THE CORN MAZE before the SCARECROW!

You and your fellow Trick-or-Treaters have been transported to the Haunted Corn Maze. Take turns rolling dice and placing tiles in the coordinates shown, to create a path to escape the Corn Maze. If you draw a tile with a Scarecrow, he blocks part of your path. Place it in the Corn Maze. If you flip over 3 Scarecrows you lose, and the Scarecrow escapes the MAZE to join the rest of the monsters in "Haunted Halloween: The Trick-or-Treat Game."

Some tiles have CANDY! The person who placed the tile earns the candy.

There is also one lucky Crow tile that will CAW back the Scarecrow, delaying his escape for you. Whomever flips over the crow tile will claim the crow token to use in the main Halloween board game.

—description from the publisher

Partitions is a two-player abstract strategy game, a type of territorial game in which players control groups to score.

Components

     Hexagonal board of any size (size 5 or larger, odd number of cells)
     A sufficient number of black and white pieces


Terminology
Group
　When pieces of the same color are adjacent and connected to each other, they form a group.
Division
　A group is said to be divided or a dividing group if the board is disconnected when all the cells it occupies are removed from the board.
　A group that does not divide the board is called a fragment group.

Setup
Each player decides which color he or she is responsible for.
The game begins with the board empty.

Game Play
Starting with the white player, the players take turns placing one piece of their color on any empty cell.

Game End
The game ends when the board is completely filled.
Each player scores the number of pieces of the opponent's color belonging to the fragment group. The player with the higher score wins.

If there is a tie, all pieces belonging to the fragment group are removed from the board, and pieces of the opposite color are placed in the empty cells, and the same evaluation is made. No draws occur.

If both players have enough experience, it may be possible to end the game earlier.

Note
The way the game is scored is much like Brian Whitman's Loops, and the author noticed this similarity after creating the rules; the only difference from Loops is that in Partitions, if all of the cells that a group divides are occupied by allies, it is no longer a dividing group. This makes for some interesting differences in properties.

A treasure hunt across the mudflats where each player must bring their nature cards to the right place on the game board. Developed for the National Association for the Preservation of the Wadden Sea.

Murder in Prague is a Cinematic Murder Mystery Experience.

Step into the cobblestone streets of Prague as an international detective tasked with solving the mysterious murder of Dr. Sarah Mandl, an American doctor found dead in the historic Faust House ballroom. This groundbreaking detective experience combines cinema-quality video content with physical evidence to create an immersive investigation unlike any other.

Your detective kit arrives packed with meticulously crafted evidence: authentic crime scene photos, forensic reports, police records, bank statements, phone logs, and more. Through an interactive web interface, you'll interrogate suspects through beautifully shot video interviews featuring an award-winning international cast. Nearly an hour of cinematic footage was filmed on location in Prague, bringing the noir mystery to life.

As you progress through 20+ chapters, you'll hack into the victim's phone, analyse text messages, receive mysterious calls, and piece together the complex web of relationships and motives surrounding the murder. An original noir soundtrack composed and recorded in Prague sets the perfect atmospheric backdrop for your investigation.

Perfect for 1-6 players, this 3-4 hour experience requires no prior gaming experience - just a sharp mind and attention to detail. Whether it's an intimate date night or small group gathering, Murder in Prague delivers a premium detective experience that combines the best elements of cinema, theater, and classic murder mysteries.

Can you spot the liar, uncover the truth, and bring justice to the streets of Prague?

—description from the designer

This expansion contains 50 new cards with simpler objectives for young kids. Each card needs only 1 or 2 dice (instead of 3) to be completed, and the cards with sums have lower numbers, easier for young kids.

Ancient Grudges: Bonefields is a skirmish game set in the blasted realm of Grimgard where two ancient forces have been fighting an unending war for longer than any can remember. So long, in fact, that there are barely any left alive on either side. Spurred to dark magic to continue their ancestral grudgematch, necromancers from both sides now wander the sundered realm accompanied by entourages of undead warriors, each hoping to find some lost magical artefact or source of power that might return them to their former glory…

Hollywood Havoc is a supplement for Skirmish Ragers and contains a complete Hollywood based campaign for the Zombie Apocalypse based in the theme park Hollywood World.

This supplement contains 6 scenarios that can be interlinked into an exciting campaign or played individually. There are also new character types and new skills for Skirmish Ragers.

Hollywood World is about fame. Some people have a need for fame – who doesn’t want to be amongst the beautiful people and live the life of the rich and famous? Unfortunately for the players, they have chosen to come here on holiday on the very day of the Zombie Apocalypse...

—description from the publisher

Protocole IOMV2236472L is a politically-commited card game.

Each card represents an Alien figure with its own color and shape (features). There are 6 colors and 6 shapes, all combinations of which are represented.

Players try to score points by keeping as many Aliens as they can in their Home. However, they need to match a feature with the selected Aliens.

Each player receives two cards in their Home. All other cards are dealt as players hands.

On their turn, players place a card from their hand either face up in the Protocole, or face down in their Home. When the Procole contains 5 cards, a selection is made. The Aliens with the most common feature (majority) are selected, the other Aliens are discarded.

When all cards have been played, a scoring phase takes place. All players with Aliens in their Home not matching a feature in the selected Aliens are eliminated. Other players score 1 point per Alien in their Home. The selected Aliens are then shuffled again and dealt to the remaining players.

The game ends when the selected Aliens are 7 or less. One last scoring phase occurs and the scores are tallied. Whoever has the most points wins the game.

With 6 cards each, 3-6 players try to score points in this trick-taking game with god cards. By playing movement cards, however, the type of trump card can change in the current round, which means that the trick often does not end up where it was planned. But be careful: the player who collects the most movement cards is excluded from the scoring!

—description from the designer

A game in which 2-3 athletes take a fast ride down the ski slope. 2 or 3 brave skiers dice their way down the slope, collecting checkpoints along the way collect checkpoints along the way and still reach the finish line quickly.

—description from the designer

On a 7x7 field plantation, players move one of 4 campesinos (workers) according to their dice roll to harvest habaneros. However, if the player ends the movement on a field that has already been harvested, he loses as many habaneros as there are empty fields in the immediate vicinity or even a campesino. It therefore becomes more difficult the more that is harvested.

—description from the designer

Match colors on the tiles to score points as you create these stunning works of art.

In Color Field, players take the role of an abstract painter and try to create three works of art. Players draw and place tiles on their canvases, and score VP for matching colored sides with other tiles or with the canvas edge. The player with the most VP at the end of the game is the winner. Players can also acquire Tools that allow them to enhance their gameplay abilities.

Color Field - Master Painter's Edition includes the Bonus Promo Pack with 6 new Community cards and silkscreen wood tokens.

Let's play the most absurd game!

1.) Youngest player picks up an obstacle card first from the deck to start round 1. This is a game of 5 rounds. Read the obstacle & decide before you begin if you think you can complete the absurd obstacle in 30 seconds, 1 minute or 1 minute & 30 seconds. (Timer not included.)

2.) Try to complete the absurd obstacle before the ding! ding! ding! Times up! Each player tries to complete one obstacle challenge each round. Alternate one player each round who’s not completing the challenge to be in change of the timer and the bell.

3.) We recommend that each player should grab a pen & paper and create their own 5 round score sheet to keep track of points each round. (See scoring for details.) (Pen & paper not included) At the end of 5 Rounds each player adds up all their points. The player with the most point wins!

Scoring Completed obstacles:
- 30 seconds: 3 points
- 1 minute: 2 points
- 1 minute & 30 seconds: 1 point

Contents:
- 1 plush goose
- 3 blocks
- 2 chopsticks
- 1 shoelace
- 1 bell
- 1 spring
- 1 snake
- 2 claws
- 2 rubber bands
- 1 ball
- 2 blindfolds
- 50 obstacle cards

boardgame: The Face of Battle: Second Edition
description:
The Face of Battle: 2nd Edition skirmish miniatures game simulates the action that occurs during the peak of battle. The moments of sheer terror, panic, bravery, and intense combat. The game is dynamic, fun and exciting to play. Each player commands their forces with numerous combat, tactical and game options.

All players participate in the battle concurrently using card decks. Soldiers from all forces move, fire and engage in combat across the entire battlefield simultaneously. Action is fast and furious.

Scale is one figure is one soldier, one model is one vehicle. Figures are individually based. For use with 10mm to 28mm miniatures.

The 156 page rulebook covers nearly all aspects of World War II skirmish gaming.

Included are rules for:

     Special Units: Medics, Flag Bearers, Messengers, Heroes, Snipers and more.
     Movement, cover and terrain.
     Infantry combat including firearms, grenades,  flamethrowers, and more.
     Anti-Tank guns, mortars, hand-held anti-tank weapons, demolition charges and more.
     Rules for detailed vehicle combat.
     German, American, Russian and British stats for common vehicles.


The Face of Battle: 2nd Edition has been completely revised from the first edition. All optional styles of play were removed. Each soldier is represented by 1 to 3 cards and players will have decks of about 30 cards.

Print on demand cards are available online

Description from the publisher

​Roulette-18 Rollette uses a 20-sided Die, and a unique table layout that does not require a Roulette-sized table.
​Like traditional Roulette, Players can place: Straight-up, Split, Street, Corner, Line and Even-chance bets.

Rules

     Players place their bets on the Roulette-18® layout, just as they would in ordinary Roulette.
     A Roulette-die is rolled in a shaker and then exposed.


If the roll is a double-zero (00) all bets remain in action and the die is rolled again until a non-00 winning number is determined.

     ​According to the bets and the winning number. Dealer then collects all losing bets and pays winning bets according to the Pay-table.


Roulette Link-Bets
Roulette Link-Bets offers Split and Corner betting between numbers in the First and Third columns that were previously not possible because they are not adjacent.
​Link-Bets are made possible by the addition of a Fourth column which duplicates the First column. In other words while columns with 1, 2 and 3 existed, allowing for a split bet between 1 and 2 or 2 and 3, there is now a fourth column with the number 1 again thus allowing for a split bet between the two adjacent numbers of 3 and 1.
​e.g. Link-Splits: (7 and 9), (16 and 18). Link-Corners: (7, 9, 10 and 12) (13, 15, 16 and 18).

A long time ago, pioneers discovered an untouched island – vast mountains, dense forests, and breathtaking beaches. But the land was full of challenges: barren soil and untamed nature.

Gather your companions – Forge your destiny!
As the leader of an expedition, you must select a group of brave pioneers, whose skills will determine your success or failure. Your choices shape your strategy and make every game unique. Your rivals will form their own teams – who will triumph? Are you ready to play worker placement on a new level?

Your task: Bring the land to life!
• Gather resources – Make use of changing gathering fields and optimize your yields.
• Build irrigation systems – Prepare the soil for fertile fields.
• Plant and nurture – Transform dry soil into blooming landscapes.
• Harvest and thrive – Reap the fruits of your labor and lead your people to prosperity.
• Attract animals – Create an environment where animals settle and maintain the balance of nature.
• Perfect your engine – Use your companions wisely and combine their talents.

Every decision matters!
Through clever planting, wise construction, and building a stable ecosystem, you create a livable home. The more diverse the wildlife, the more fertile the land becomes! Whoever strategically places their companions, uses their abilities wisely, and creates an efficient ecosystem will be the first to achieve prosperity and win in Era of Pioneers.

The next island awaits you – are you ready?
Gather your companions, develop your strategy, and embark on your journey.

—description from the publisher

Game based on the funny universe of Jeff and Jean-Marc Panacloc (theme : humor). In this game, you must find the replica of a player's secret card.

Gameplay : 
4 cards are dealt to each player. 1 player (the ass, le fion in french) keep an extra card face down. The ass plays a card and the others players do the same. Then the ass announces how many cards match the secret card. 2 nd round.

If a player guesses the secret card, he wins the cards, and the secret card. If he is wrong, he can no longer make proposals. If no one guesses, the ass wins the cards.

Game end and winning conditions :
The winner is the one who wins the most cards. And the game ends when there are no more cards in the draw pile.

Chantstation plays in teams where 1 person acts as an interpret and must sing/mime or give hints on the title of a song from the 50s/60s/70s/80s . Each team earns points based on how many hints were given by the interpret.

In this puzzle tile-laying game, players create their own tide pools, carefully selecting creatures to create the most vibrant and thriving ecosystem for points.

Based on the Pacific coast tide pools found on the coast of California, you will learn first hand about the complex and often hidden world of intertidal life. Each turn, players will choose and place tiles representing different marine creatures—sea stars, anemones, crabs, and more—while strategically managing placement and the current, ever changing scoring objectives while making use of the established food chain to score the most points!

With stunning artwork inspired by real Pacific coast inhabitants, Low Tide offers both casual fun and educational depth, making it perfect for nature lovers, strategy gamers, and families alike. Will your tide pool flourish or be swept away by the changing tides?

—description from the designer

In this clever card game, you score points mainly by collecting sets of coloured Easter eggs. But different colours score differently, and the value of a set is not proportional to the number of cards in it! The challenge is to decide whether to lay down a set with fewer cards or wait for more cards to maximise your score.

You begin your turn by drawing cards. You can draw up to 3 cards this way – unless you draw a bunny card. If you already have a bunny, those two will give you points and you can continue drawing, otherwise you must stop.

In the second phase, you can do various manipulations with your cards using certain combinations of Mushroom, Flower and Insect cards, and you can even build smart combos of actions in the same turn.

The game ends when the draw deck runs out. The player with the highest score wins.


Hímes tojások (Easter Eggs) is the second title in the author’s ‘Seasons’ card game series, after the Christmas-themed Fényes díszek (Shiny Baubles). The game is for up to 6 players and has a single-player mode.

Hungarian:

Ebben a furfangos szettgyűjtő játékban elsősorban színes tojáskártyák sorozataival szerzel pontokat. Azonban a különböző tojásszínek pontozási módja eltérő, ráadásul a szettel elérhető pontszám nem növekszik a darabszámmal arányosan! Így meg kell gondolnod, leraksz-e egy kevesebb kártyából álló szettet, vagy érdemes várni még néhány jó lapra, hogy több pontot érj el.

Minden kört laphúzással kezdesz. Ezzel  akár 3 lapot is szerezhetsz a kör elején – kivéve, ha felhúzol egy nyuszikártyát. Ha van már nálad egy nyuszi, akkor azok ketten pontot is hoznak, és folytathatod a húzást. Ha nincs, több lapot nem húzhatsz.

Ezt követően különféle manipulációkat hajthatsz végre a kártyáiddal. Ehhez azonban gomba, virág és rovarkártyák bizonyos kombinációival kell rendelkezned, melyekkel, ha ügyes vagy, akár több műveletet is kombinálhatsz ugyanabban a körben.

A  játék akkor ér véget, amikor elfogy a húzópakli. Az nyer, aki a legtöbb pontot szerzi.

A tavaszi hangulatú Hímes tojások a szerző „Évszakok” kártyajáték-sorozatának második darabja a karácsonyi Fényes díszek után. Akár 6 fő is játszhatja, és egyjátékos móddal is rendelkezik.

DiagnosTHIS is the adult party game where you'll be dying to search your symptoms!

Gameplay is simple. Each round, one player reads off a Symptom card from the deck, and the other players respond with their funniest Diagnosis cards. Play until your heart's content or until it explodes.

—description from the back of the box

Similar to Apples to Apples and Cards Against Humanity, but as if you were getting unhelpful advice for your bizarre symptoms from doctors with the worst bedside manner.

Invasion Normandy simulates the campaign fought in northern France from June 6, 1944 through the Allied breakout. A 2-4 player game that can be played solo if desired.

The game is played on a hex-grid map with players taking turns activating units to move and fight in various ways. Rules covering variable reinforcements, bombing missions, offshore bombardment and defensive positions are standard. Option rules cover terrain, hidden mines, and special units.

Contents:
Map (various sizes available)
Spiral bound rulebook
Player aid
Battle Boards
Markers
Optional to purchase: units & decals

—description from the developer

Supplement for the Starmada Core rules, detailing the universe of the Imnperial Starmada and its major adversaries.

New stuff:

     new rules: updates on Cinematic Movement, Reactionless Movement and Vector Movement + Naval Movement, Engagement Limit, Attack Modifiers, Alternate Damage Table, Weapon Traits, Random Damage Allocation, new Starship Systems, new Munitions, new Equipment traits, new Weapons
     clarification on how to build the SSD
     errata and clarifications (from the Starmada Core Rulebook version 1.1 to 1.2)
     initial notes on tactics that the authors hope will continue in future publications
     background information on the five major forces (Terran Empire, Arcturan Federation, Kalaedinese Expanse, Negali Coalition and S'ssk Nesting)
     35 Starship Status Displays (SSDs) listed below

Trivia game about one of Finland's oldest towns, Porvoo. The game has 720 questions and is suitable for everyone who wants to learn more about this very historical 3rd oldest town in Finland, situated about 35 kilometres east of Helsinki and home of the largest oil refinery in the country. The game has questions in 4 categories.

The name of the game refers to the historical town hall (Swedish: Gamla rådhuset) in the old town of Porvoo. Thematically, the players have agreed to meet each other there and whoever wins the game made it in time, while all others are late.

The game is basically the Porvoo edition of Nähdään kellon alla!, the original version set in Helsinki. This is the fifth instalment of the main games in the Nähdään series, and the game is completely Finnish/Swedish bilingual.

Trivia game about one of Finland's large cities, Lahti. The game has 720 questions and is suitable for everyone who wants to learn more about this Finnish city at the southern end of lake Päijänne, famous for winter sports. There are 4 categories of questions.

The name of the game refers to the harbour at lake Vesijärvi (the part of lake Päijänne immediately north of the city, and once a bustling inland port). Thematically, the players have agreed to meet each other there and whoever wins the game made it in time, while all others are late.

The game is basically the Lahti edition of Nähdään kellon alla!, the original version set in Helsinki. This is the sixth instalment of the main games in the Nähdään series, and the game is in Finnish only.

Trivia game about one of Finland's towns, Raasepori. The game has 900 questions and is suitable for everyone who wants to learn more about this southern Finnish coastal town west of Helsinki, founded when the neighbouring municipalities of Tammisaari, Karjaa and Pohja joined together in 2009; Raasepori is originally just the name of one of the villages of the historical town of Tammisaari, but it was chosen as the name for the new town due to the old castle located there. The game has questions in 6 categories.

The name of the game ("See you at the castle") refers to the most famous landmark in the area, the medieval castle of Raasepori (Raseborg), founded in the late 14th century. Thematically, the players have agreed to meet each other at the castle and whoever wins the game made it in time, while all others are late.

The game is basically the Raasepori edition of Nähdään kellon alla!, the original version set in Helsinki. This is the seventh instalment of the main games in the Nähdään series. As befitting for this mostly Swedish-speaking town, the game is fully Finnish/Swedish bilingual.

A solitaire game designed for a double-six domino set. Each turn, rotate and group 2, 3, or 4 dominoes making sure the totals above and below the line are multiples of three to get a high score.

BYO set and download the rules to this fun puzzle. 
Standard game is played with double-six set of dominoes. 
Break 600 points and score a rare high score (double-six game). 
Can be played with double-nine or double-twelve domino sets for longer, higher scoring games.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Ironworks: melt, mold, master. You are an iron molder working at a furnace company in the late 18th century. Centered around the cold-blast furnace, you are part of a community of miners, colliers, blacksmiths, artisans, and retailers working together for the benefit of all. Furnace companies like this were self-contained communities where everyone contributed—men and women alike. Many of the men took on the jobs of colliers, miners, fillers, and molders, while the women took on the foundational work load of harvesting, caring for the livestock, and providing clothing. Part time laborers traveling through also offered labor and skill.

Although pig iron is the main commodity to rise from the molten iron that pours from the furnace, your focus is producing high quality ten-plate stoves. The stoves, whose plates are beautifully carved by talented artisans, are a revolutionary design and now every home in America wants one. Through many hours spent molding and casting these stoves, you increase your skill and thereby your reputation. A higher reputation also means higher wages. Perfect your craft, invest your earnings, and strive to become Iron Master.

• —————————————————————— •

Ironworks is a competitive, resource management, worker placement game set in rural Pennsylvania in the late 1700’s.

In this game, you play as an iron molder. Your goal is to win by getting the most reputation points at the end of the game. To do this, you’ll need to focus your attention on the furnace. Filling it is a group effort, but skill and efficiency will award only one player the Iron Master title.

Throughout the game, you will be taking actions to gather resources, acquire designs, fill the furnace, hire assistants, complete daily orders, and buy stocks. When the furnace is ready to be ‘tapped’ the molten iron will pour out. Collect the iron in your ladle in order to fill the molds in your work area. 
Complete designs in order to increase your skill. As your skill increases, so do your reputation and your wages. You would be wise to invest your earnings back into the company, buying stocks for the future. After the furnace taps three times, the end-game is triggered.

The player with the most reputation points at the end of the game is crowned Iron Master, the winner of Ironworks: melt, mold, master.

Good luck!

—description from the designer
Boardgame combining trivia questions and Monopoly property owning.  Players answer trivia questions, buy and improve properties and try to avoid going broke.  The player with the most money wins.

Stealing the Horizons is a 2-player skirmish game that asks players to do more than just shoot their way to success.  Combat is merely part of the strategy.

You'll need to solve the intricate puzzle of each map, using your faction's unique abilities to their fullest, to secure victory.

Welcome to VANEA. A mystical and fairytale world. This game takes place in the ElderForest. You are a Guardian of the ElderTree and must defend it against scary monsters, bosses and the spreading darkmoon rot. Explore the ElderForest. Collect 6 different elemental orbs to master all of these elementals. Find magical orbs to learn new abilities and collect ore to craft powerful and legendary equipment. It's all about LOOT guys!!! Hunt these monsters, let your monster farm grow and get your cute elemental companion.

Mechanics
Explore the ElderForest (every game you play you will build a different and random hex field map)
Collect more and more of the 6 different elemental spirits to master these 6 elements
Elemental battle system
Get your cute elemental companion
Find magical orbs to learn new abilities
Collect equipment pieces to create powerful and legendary equipment
ElderTree mode: Defend the AelderTree against all monsters and elemental bosses
Monster farm mode: Hunt monsters and let your monster farm grow
Defend your castle: Each Guardian must collect points and keep an eye on his own castle
Scenario "All vs All or Team vs Team": Each Guardian or team has its own task, and whoever completes it first wins
...and don't let the Loot Monster get away ;)
It's all about LOOT guys!!!

The Forgotten Garrison: Siege of Famagusta is a print-to-play board game that transports players to a pivotal yet overlooked siege of the late Renaissance. Set during the 16th-century clash between Venetian and Ottoman forces, the game allows participants to command either the tenacious Venetian defenders or the ambitious Ottoman attackers in a strategic reenactment of this historic conflict.

Featuring dynamic combat cards, the game introduces unpredictable tactical opportunities, such as reinforcements or sudden maneuvers, ensuring varied gameplay. Over 150 hand-drawn counters depict historically accurate units, including the Ottoman Great Bombard and Venetian Arquebusiers, enhancing visual immersion. Siege mechanics simulate period-specific warfare, incorporating elements like mining, moat-filling, and artillery deployment.

Designed for replayability, the game encourages diverse strategies and outcomes, supported by streamlined rules for quick sessions ideal for casual play. Its print-to-play format offers accessibility, while large, clear components aid readability. Players confront choices of honor or conquest, balancing strategy and historical context in a fast-paced, visually rich experience that revives a forgotten chapter of Mediterranean history.

Test your knowledge of Science & Vie by playing this edition of Trivial Pursuit!

Choose from 600 questions written by ‘science et vie magazine’ journalists!

6 categories of questions: space, planet Earth, matter and energy, geek and techno, human beings, animals.

Roll the die, choose the square you want, answer the question and be the first player to collect 6 cards to win!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Testez vos connaissances en Science & Vie en jouant avec cette édition du Trivial Pursuit !

Choisissez parmi 600 questions rédigées par les journalistes de science et vie magazine !

6 catégories de questions : espace, planète Terre, matière et énergie, geek et technos, êtres humains, animaux.

Lancez le dé, choisissez la case que vous souhaitez, répondez à la question et soyez le premier joueur à rassembler 6 cartes pour gagner !

—description from the publisher

The souk is buzzing with excitement as gemstone dealers and experts in rare craftsmanship have arrived in Marrakesh. Which of you will decorate your necklace with the most beautiful gemstones and use the experts to your advantage? A total of 6 modules await you and invite you to try out new strategies and tactics!

Module 1: Gemstones
Module 2: Experts
Module 3: Madrasa
Module 4: Wooden buildings
Module 5: Alms
Module 6: Luxury goods

This awaits you in Gems & Experts:

Find gems all over Marrakesh and manufacture the most beautiful necklace the world has ever seen. Trade gems that you do not need at the souk to gain valuable ressources and a leg up over your competitors.

Experts have come to Marrakesh to offer their services to you, the players. Each of them comes with powerful abilities that you can use one time to gain an significant advantage. But an expert does not work for free, so have the necessary ressources ready to pay your expert.

—description from the publisher

Throw and stamp Ofuda amulets on Yokai to prevent them from stealing food from your fridge.


The first refrigerator game! Ofuda Slap!


     Stick the Yokai on your refrigerator, grab 3 Ofuda and distance yourself.
     Throw and stick the Ofuda on the Yokai!
     Whoever gets the most hits wins.
One card special edition of Cleptómano.

Get to know yourself better!

In this card game for 3-6 players, you will help each other out by giving thoughtful advice when one is going through a hard time.

Each round one player is presenting a personal problem through a card. Everyone else now plays an advice card. The first player then chooses the best advice amongst the played cards.

As a twist on scoring, you want to keep your wins and losses in balance, because only then will you be able to truly win the game!

—description from the publisher

Read out the category, choose the starting letters,
name a fitting word: That's how simple Criss Cross Categories is!

The diverse topics and ever-changing letters in the grid ensure variety and fun with every round. If you choose the starting letters wisely, you can grab the highly sought-after bonus points!

But be careful: don’t get stuck! Other players can then challenge you with the fast-paced timer. Whoever finds clever words and stays calm will score the most points here!

—description from the publihsher

A trading card game developed by Gemblo Company and published by Daewon Media. It was first released on October 29, 2024.
The goal is to release characters from various games as in-game themes, and the only game currently participating is Goddess of Victory: Nike. Perhaps for this reason, it is mainly called Niketak or Nike card game among users.

It can be compared to the Korean version of Vice Schwaltz , and interestingly, Nikke also participated in Vice Schwaltz, which led to the first Korean version of Vice Schwaltz.

In Rasanto Wortale, a fast-paced word game, players work together to find words for each category. But there's a twist: each word must start with the last letter of the previous one!

Be creative and try to come up with as many words as possible before time runs out.

This cooperative game brings action-packed fun and dynamic excitement to your game night as you race against the clock!

—description from the publisher

Travel, explore and experience the adventures found along the spectacular Colorado Trail. Sweeping vistas, mountain peaks, rivers, lakes, and passes await as you discover and observe the amazing diversity of Rocky Mountain wildlife and vegetation. Manage your resources to advance along the trail, allowing you to experience the variety of adventures that make the Colorado Trail wonderfully unique.

Trailblazer: The Colorado Trail takes place over the course of six weeks (rounds) that represent around eighty miles of actual trail distance traveled per week. Along the way, players can experience off-trail adventures such as river rafting, climbing 14ers, hot springs, horseback riding, biking, or even taking a ride on a historic train! The more you adventure, the more columbines — Colorado's state flower, which are the game's points — you gain. Columbines can also be acquired by taking in the amazing vistas, going over mountain passes, or spending time by the numerous lakes, rivers, and creeks. The numerous flowers, trees, birds, and animals discovered and observed on your journey can help you earn needed supplies and score more columbines.

Players need to manage their supplies as they journey on the Colorado Trail, gain rungs on the elevation ladder, and move along the unique adventure path. Each player must organize and play their deck of action cards to maximize their abilities, adventures, and actions.

—description from the publisher

Garage Rock is a medium-weight, worker-placement game that transports you back to the 1980s — a time when big hair, cassette tapes, and BMX bikes ruled the neighborhood streets. Step into the shoes of a teenage garage band, fighting for glory in the high-stakes "Battle of the Bands," hosted by the infamous local pirate radio DJ, Johnny Law. As you compete for fans, you'll upgrade your skills, post flyers around town, sell band merch, and pour your heart into writing songs that make your fans shout for more! Record demos at the local studio, complete your album, and hand your demo tape to Johnny Law to get your music aired on his Pirate Radio station, broadcast from his secret treehouse.

On your turn, you'll place band members at various locations on the town board, visiting shops to gather the skills needed to qualify for one of four available gigs. Use the money earned from gigs to visit the Music Shop to upgrade your instrument or swing by the Clothing Store to boost your band’s image with a fresh new look. Pump some quarters into the copy machine to print flyers and spread the word about your band all over town. Record a demo tape that will boost your popularity when it’s played on Pirate Radio by dropping radio cubes down the treehouse cube tower.

The game unfolds over sixteen weeks of summer, with the grand finale being the "Battle of the Bands" contest. The band with the most fans at the end wins the game.

Garage Rock adds a layer of strategy, combining the excitement of worker placement and resource management with a thematic and nostalgic teenage music scene experience. It's time to build your band, rock the garage, and dominate the neighborhood!

—description from the publisher

Nomad City: Oripathy Dilemma expands and extends the gameplay based on Nomad City: The Founders.
This expansion adds mechanics related to the Infected, which can cause trouble for the players, but can also bring unexpected boons.

From The Tales of Masculus, the manliest hero of all time, comes Belts of the Blossomed. Journey through the lands as one of Masculus’ disciples. Your goal is noble and pure: be the first of your brotherhood to fill your belt’s five notches with pristine maidenhoods by courting and blossoming five maidens. The winner stall earn their rightful place upon The Scroll of Eternal Glory. If none should complete this righteous task before the chapters conclude, then all shall fail and bear the shameful stain of mediocrity and soon be forgotten.

Play as one of Masculus’ brotherhood members like Sir Oliver Klozauph, Sir Buster Highman, or Count Mawdic Upyahbum. Earn your reputation, invest in your primary skills and woo fair maidens with your Superiority, Persuasion, and of course, Ravishment. Outbid your brothers to gain access to maidens, and if you court them well and earn their favor, then spin The Bedding Wheel, and hope that fate is on your side. If it is, claim their maidenhood, and slide it into your belt’s notch. But beware, your rival brothers may endeavor to steal your maid, or your spin of the wheel may prompt the arrival of an Angry Father, Performance Anxiety, or worst yet: Moon’s Blood. Strike to better yourself and with enough effort, you may learn to overcome these pitfalls, bolstering your chances as you fight to earn that coveted place upon The Scroll of Eternal Glory, where you may inscribe your name, and the names of all who bore witness to your glory.

—description from the publisher

What do I know?? 
Quiz questions are a thing of the past!

In Quiz Me 5, you're always looking for a solution word. You get to choose which of the 5 clues you want to hear.

The catch? If you want to answer early, you'll have to settle for a less helpful clue.

Are you confident in the category and going all in, or do you let others go first and bet on their lack of knowledge?

A surprising quiz game with a bit of luck and a lot of fun!

—description from the publisher

Gentle Jack is a gothic-comedy party/drinking card game for 3-10 players where dark humor, strategic card play, and a healthy disregard for friendships collide.
Set in a world of macabre whimsy, the game uses hand-illustrated card art to breathe life to an atmosphere that sits somewhere between a Victorian séance and occult pirate fantasy. Beneath the crude (but clever) humor lies a game with more strategic texture than your average party fare.

Legend has told of the infinidungeon, an ever-shifting maze of chambers full of monsters, traps, and treasure. You know the stories of the four crystals which, when collected, will flip the dungeon, granting access to the deeper chambers. If you are bold enough and lucky enough, you’ll make it to the lair of the dragon and slay it, overcoming the infinidungeon!

—description from the designer

Welcome to Planet Sunaru! Meet its's crazy inhabitants!

Each card displays 16 unique creatures on the front and on the back, some more common and others more elusive.
 Your mission: 
Find the creature that appears on only one side of the card to win!

This colorful game is all about tiles and points. 
Create intricate patterns by taking turns to place tiles in the shared display. Whether you take a strategic approach or let your creativity guide you, place the tiles skilfully and earn points for every completed loop. Get caught up in the easy gameplay and enjoy fun, creative moments together!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Battlecry: A Game of Fantasy Battles in a Wondrous Age
description:
Step into a world of tactical warfare with Battlecry, a thrilling miniature wargame where strategic thinking and battlefield tactics determine victory. Command your forces, outwit your enemies, and lead your warriors to glory across dynamic battlefields in this new title by Warrior Poet Games.

Designed for players of all levels, Battlecry combines the thrill of miniature warfare with deep, strategic gameplay. Whether you're controlling a fearsome army of knights, honor-bound orcs, or commanding hordes of undead, every battle is a test of wits and will.

Features:

     Diverse Factions & Units: Choose from 10 different factions, each with their own unique strengths, units, and abilities – no additional supplements needed.
     Simple Combat System: Engage in intense combat using an easy-to-learn rules system that emphasizes both strategy and creativity.
     Engaging Gameplay: Each game is designed to engage and involve both players, featuring alternating activation with a unique focus on flexbility.
     For All Skill Levels: Whether you're a seasoned wargamer or a newcomer, Battlecry offers an accessible yet deep gaming experience for all players.
     Lore and Legend: Battlecry features a new world for players with a narrative to inspire stories for your battles.


Prepare your forces, rally your troops, and get ready to answer the call of Battlecry – where every decision counts and every battle is a story waiting to unfold.

Are you ready? The war is about to begin.

—description from the publisher

Solve puzzles together from the comfort of your own home!

Embark on an exciting journey around the world and help your friend solve the mysteries he’s caught up in. He’s made a discovery that’s got him being chased across continents. Use his postcards to solve the puzzles.

Can you work together to solve the riddles around the globe and help your friend?

—description from the publisher

Заповедник ("Reserve") is an entertaining game where players build a wildlife park and house different animals there.

Each participant receives 9 animal tokens. The park starts with the starting square "Start", and the rest of the tiles are placed face down. During their turn, players reveal a tile and place it on the board, so that adjacent areas continue each other. Fields connect to fields, lakes to lakes and forests to forests.

In an open area, players can place an animal from their stock: a rabbit, deer or fish - each has its own habitat. Only one animal of each species can live on each tile. The turn goes to the next player.

When one player runs out of animal tokens, the game is over. Calculate points for plots and animals, and the winner is the one with the most points.


Заповедник – занимательная игра, в которой игроки строят природный парк и селят там разных животных.

Каждый участник получает по 9 фишек животных. Парк начинает разрастаться со стартовой клетки «Старт», остальные тайлы складваются стопокой лицевой стороной вниз. Игрок в свой ход открывает тайл из стопки и вкладывает его на стол, так что бы соседние зоны продолжали друг друга. Так, поля соединяются с полями, озера – с озерами, а лесами – с другими чащами.

На открытом пространстве игрок может поселить животное из своего запаса: зайчика, оленя или рыбку – у каждого своя среда обитания. На одной территории тайла может жить только один представитель вида. Ход передаётся следующему игроку.

Как только кто-то из игроков остается без фишек животных, игра заканчивается. Посчитайте баллы за участки и зверей. Побеждает тот, у кого больше всего очков.

boardgame: Interstellar Adventures: The Sincerest Form of Flattery
description:
In the depths of space, a cryptic signal from a previously unknown planet reaches The Atlas. On board that spaceship, Captain Silna and her crew scramble to decipher the call. What follows is an adventure that pushes the crew to confront their limits.

With the mechanics of an escape room and the excitement of a choose-your-own-adventure story, Interstellar Adventures is a dynamic three-episode experience that has a role for all crew members: the code-breaker, the cartographer, and the one who just likes missiles.

Using powers of logic and deduction, the crew sets out on a mission to untangle the unknown. Exploring an obscure new world, battling ravenous plants, and narrowly escaping missile attacks, our crew embarks on a quest that tests both their wits and their teamwork skills, while also making them second-guess everything they thought they knew.

Get ready for a wild farm adventure with Go Farm, the exciting twist on a classic Card game!

Professor Puzzle presents a delightful game where all the animals have gone rogue and are in disarray on the farm.

Your mission? 
Collect complete sets of animals and imitate their sounds to win!

Join in the excitement on the farm with Go Farm - the ultimate game of animal antics and laughter!

-

Exciting Twist: 
Experience a unique twist on the traditional Card Quarters game as you chase after runaway farm animals.

Wild Farm Adventure: 
Navigate the chaos of the farm to collect complete sets of animals.

Interactive Gameplay: 
Mimic the sounds of each animal as you collect sets, adding a hilarious and engaging element to the game.

Family Friendly Fun: 
Suitable for players of all ages, Go Farm is perfect for family game nights, parties or get-togethers.

Competitive Edge: 
Compete against friends and family to see who can collect the most animal sets and emerge victorious in this entertaining game of skill and strategy.

boardgame: Raise Your Red Flag: Original edition
description:
Discover your friends' secrets with this hilarious icebreaker game! With 120 surprising Red Flag statements and 6 flags, you're guaranteed an evening full of laughter.

Read statements like “Thinks they are a social media influencer?” and vote on who fits best. Collect 10 tickets to claim the title of Red Flag Winner!

Are you ready for an unforgettable evening full of surprises? Play Raise Your Red Flag and discover who is really hiding in your circle of friends!

boardgame: Raise Your Red Flag: Not Safe For Work Edition
description:
Raise Your Red Flag Party Game is the hilarious game that makes everyone burst out laughing! The perfect icebreaker for any cozy get-together.

The Not Safe For Work 18+ Edition contains 120 funny, surprising and merciless Red Flag statements, together with 6 flags to complete the experience! Actually not suitable for work, but maybe that makes it more fun..!

Collect 10 tickets to claim the title of Red Flag Winner!

—description from the publisher

ʻĀina is a game that challenges you to use strategy and cooperation to flip a landscape from negative to positive, distribute resources for lasting wealth, and open pathways for water to flow—all in 64 moves.

The cards are also the playing board. They create a landscape of a stylized ahupuaʻa (traditional land division), and the entire game revolves around laulima (working together) to restore its vibrancy.

—description from the publisher

Quickly is a dynamic board game that immerses players in a world of fast-paced competition.

The gameplay revolves around revealing tiles to find matching pairs, with every move filled with excitement and surprises.

Thanks to action cards that players can play at the right moments, collecting pairs becomes not only a challenge but also an opportunity for strategic thinking and outsmarting opponents.

Quickly to dynamiczna gra planszowa, która przenosi graczy w świat szybkiej rywalizacji.

Rozgrywka opiera się na odkrywaniu kafelków w celu odnalezienia pasujących par, a każdy ruch pełen jest emocji i niespodzianek.

Dzięki kartom funkcyjnym, które gracze mogą zagrywać w odpowiednich momentach, zdobywanie par staje się nie tylko wyzwaniem, ale również okazją do strategicznego myślenia i zaskoczenia przeciwników.

A box exclusive to Kickstarter backers which contains all the stretch goals for the game.

Included in this box are new crew members to play as, new monsters to fight and new equipment to salvage and craft, along with miniatures for the crew and monsters and all the cards for each of them respectively.

Fuzzy Butts is a quick, high-energy family game where everyone plays on every turn. The object is to score the most points by quickly and correctly counting up specific animal butts or specific background colors.

Each card has three different animal butts with three different background colors.  On each round, two dice are rolled, indicating which animal butt or which background color to count. All players need to count quickly and 1) decide how many of the indicated butts or colors are on the face-up cards and then 2) slap their Odd or Even card down depending on the number they counted. The first player who slaps the correct card scores two points and all others who slap the correct card score one point. Incorrect Odd/Even cards score zero points. Before each new round, one more animal butt card is added so the game gets more challenging. The player with the most points after ten cards is the winner.

—description from the designer

Medieval Realms: The card game is played over a series of turns. On each round, all players simultaneously choose a card from their hand to play an another to discard.

The player who placed the higher card chooses to pick 1 card from the river of cards of the table with a number higher than the card he played. Then the rest of players do the same from higher to lower card played.

Cards can score victory points for resources, set collections, great characters and majority cards played.

After 6 rounds, the players will have a tableau of 12 cards and will start scoring.  The player with the most victory points wins.

—description from the designer
family: Versions & Editions: Card Versions of Non-Card Games

boardgame: Lost in the City: The Game
description:
Lost in the City Game is designed by artist Navin Rawanchaikul under his art company, Navin Production.  It is produced by Thai Silk Co., Ltd., to celebrate the centennial birth of Jim Thompson, founder of the company.  It is produced in a limited edition of 1,000 with the artist's personal signature and your own edition number on every package.

Lost in the City is a must-collect artist's board game transporting you and your friends on a crazy, fun-filled journey through Bankok's metropolitan maze.  Jump aboard a variety of weird and wonderful vehicles, from longtail boats to three-wheel Tuk Tuks, joining Jim and his band of adventurers as they explore Bangkok's diverse districts.  Unravel past and present, lost and found, to become champion of the city!

—description from the publisher

Designed by Thai contemporary artist Navin Rawanchaikul.  It was created as part of a multifaceted showcase exhibition at the Jim Thompson House Museum, to celebrate the birth of the the legendary Thai silk king, Jim Thompson as part of an exhibition celebrating the colorful life of the famous businessman whose mysterious disappearance over 50 years ago continues to intrigue many. Jim Thompson was an American businessman who helped revitalize the Thai silk industry in the 1950s and 1960s. At the time of his disappearance he was one of the most famous Americans living in Asia.

Delegate Dash is a round-based card/dice game where all players (the “candidates”) campaign for delegates across the USA in hopes of winning primary elections and ultimately the Presidency.  All it takes is strategy, luck and of course screwing your political opponents!

Although not strictly an educational game, Delegate Dash loosely simulates the U.S. Presidential election process without involving any political leanings.  Game play is based on actual electoral vote calculations in a simplified yet fun manner!

Interactive Game Play: Delegate Dash is designed for all players whether they love politics, hate politics, or avoid politics altogether like their crazy ex.  Basic game play is as follows:

     On their turn, all candidates draw and play cards, which allow them to add or remove delegates on the game map.
     The number of delegates determines the number of dice rolled on Primary Election rounds. The winners of these primaries collect Electoral College points.
     The candidate with the highest number of Electoral College points at the end of the game wins the Presidency! (And bragging rights) ;)


In Delegate Dash, no candidate is ever really "out of the race" at any time. Superior strategy will enhance chances of winning, but a little luck may swing the race in unexpected ways.

—description from the designer

boardgame: For Reign or Ruin: Fantasy Mass Combat Rules
description:
For Reign or Ruin is a set of miniature wargame rules for historical ancient, medieval and fantasy battles between armies. 
It was written for 15 mm fantasy and in addition to rules for all historical troop types such as infantry, cavalry, artillery and commanders, it also contains rules for heroes, monsters, swarms, undead, magic and flyers. it is independent from a specific line of miniatures or background so you can recreate your favourite battles and armies. You can download the rules, counters and army lists for free and start playing.

For Reign or Ruin is a miniature wargame simulating large scale battles with thousands of soldiers on each side, organised in units, brigades, etc. The player takes the role of army commander trying to win the battle.

For Reign or Ruin is a wargame that focuses on command rather than combat. Therefore, orders, morale and the influence of commanders requires most of your attention. Combat is simplified and is mostly used to determine the outcome of those command decisions. If you prefer complete control over your troops for movement, combat, etc. this is not the game for you. If you appreciate both the tactical challenges and the struggle of a general to stay in control, you might like it.

For Reign or Ruin was written for large scale battles, but also works for smaller engagements. It works with multiple players per side, three of more sided battles and has rules for solo play. It can be used to recreate battles from any of your favourite books, myths, films and games, or historic periods from ancient to medieval. Army lists are available for many fantasy armies, some mythological armies and historic armies and more will be added in the future. As the game rules are separated from army and troop description, you can create new troop types, monsters, etc. and the army organisation they are part of, to reflect your favourite background or an army you already have.

Each turn consists of the following phases:

         1. Movement phase
             a. Movement of elements
        
         2. Ranged Combat phase
            a. Ranged combat
        
         3 Close Combat phase
             a. Close combat
        
         4. Morale phase
             a. Stress redistribution
             b. Reform
             c. Morale check
        
         5. Command phase
             a. Spotting
             b. Orders

boardgame: Marvel Dice Throne: X-Men – Promo Card Set
description:
25 new cards for use in Marvel Dice Throne: X-Men.

Includes:
8 x Main Phase Cards
8 x Randomizer Cards
8 x Mythic Cards
1 x How To Use Card

Using six cards in your hand from a standard deck of playing cards, challenge for possession to move a soccer ball up and down a 3x3 field in order to get the best shot on goal. Whoever scores the most goals after two 45-minute halves wins the match.

-description from designer

boardgame: At The Bleeding Edge: Russia vs NATO in Eastern Europe 2020-2025
description:
This theater book aims to model a theoretical conventional conflict between Russia and the NATO alliance in eastern Europe in the early 2020s. This scenario could be an alternative to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, an escalation of said conflict, or a campaign conducted upon its conclusion. A core tenet of this book is the time frame being modelled is only the first 30 days of a potential conflict. The predictability of a NATO-Russia conflict drops quickly as the days move forward. Beyond the first month, much of NATO’s heavy units would become available which would noticeably shift the odds. Russia’s casualty count would rise dramatically over these 30 days, a situation that would make a nuclear exchange more likely. Finally, the first month would be the closest to tactical and operational parity the Russians would likely achieve. Compared to the hordes that Russia could unleash on day one, the EFPs are far smaller, with the possible exception of EFP Poland.

This scenario involves Russia invading one or more Baltic states somewhere from 2020-2025. In response, the Enhanced Forward Presence battlegroups in each of Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania, and Poland are the first line of defence along with the armed forces of the host countries. Each of these EFPs are given a distinct army list with their own combat teams, reinforcement lists, and unit statistics. The composition of these lists was determined by the presence and likely rotation of NATO formations through the regions. Every NATO nation involved has been modelled to some degree so long as their contribution is tactically relevant.

In addition to the four EFPs, six of NATO’s largest military powers have been modelled as their own army lists. The nations of Canada, France, Germany, Great Britain, Poland, and The United States have their own unique sets of combat teams, lists, and units. These army lists include many formations and units not available to the EFPs to which these nations contribute. This allows for players to play nation specific combat teams that include every relevant unit, regardless of deployment schedule but still limited to the availability within the specified time frame. Players can easily combine units and lists between the presented armies such as including US heavy formations in a fight involving EFP Poland, a deployment that likely would fall outside the 30 day window this theater book models. However, scenario play is based on the scenario specified, limiting such combinations to match play.

Being the primary opponent, Russia has its own army list with an extensive collection of combat teams and units. These combat teams represent the reality at the outset of the conflict, not the evolution after several years of attritional fighting. However, due to the nepotistic nature of the Russian Army, the reliability of reinforcements is lower, making extensive use of lists to randomise what units are present.

Each of these armies is given a section of this book detailing their major company sized formations from infantry to armour, recce, and airborne. Reinforcement lists for determining additional forces from outside the immediate chain of command are used in scenario play to help tell the narrative of the game. Platoon formations detail the structure of the disparate forces that may become involved. Extensive unit stats for infantry, transports, AFVs, and more allow nearly any battle to be modelled.

This massive conglomeration of information had to be organized in a manner that was understandable by the players as well as capturing the experience of commanding a tactical combat team within the constraints of the scenario. In furtherance of this goal, every detail, decision, and omission was made with the knowledge that players will inhabit the reality of being a company commander of their chosen combat team with all the choices, limitations and frustrations that entails. Due to the many involved nations and their intermixing in the EFPs, NATO country codes have been utilised for ease of play.

—description from the publisher

Crime, murder, and manslaughter.

Investigated by the police, but unresolved. Closed due to lack of evidence. These are cold cases that will freeze the blood in your veins because the perpetrators are still at large! True crime, hair-raising cases, 100% real.

Who will manage to solve the 50 pitch-black riddles?

A party game for adventurous adults who value enthusiastic consent and have differing levels of attraction to each other.

It is 100% free to print and play.

The game starts by slyly checking what type of mischief your friends secretly want, then facilitates increasingly daring shenanigans of that type, however far your group wants to go.

You get 1 point for each time you complete a dare with another player. First player to 20 points wins.

—description from the back of the publisher
