Would you play Spin-the-Bottle if it only chose people who WANT to kiss each other?

Choose any dare from the menu, then privately collect consent cards to see who might join you

Dares start out easy, but escalate as your most uninhibited players write new dares on the menu. How far will your group go?

—description from the back of the box

Monsters, ready eat!
Eat the smaller monsters with big monsters to collect as many points as possible. Quite simple but watch out! The monsters are hungry and will devour anything they can get their hands on.

Fit the stamps cleverly into the grid and secure the most points. Stamps is easy to learn and instantly transports you to summer vacation with its beautiful designs. Boring trips are a thing of the past! Whether at the beach, on a plane, or on your own terrace, this compact yet captivating card game for the whole family brings a vacation vibe to every game table!
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Ready to command the battlefield?

Iwo Jima Lite edition is a free, simplified P&P version of our upcoming Neva Skirmish game, “Iwo Jima: Hell on Earth.”

Relive the historic Battle of Iwo Jima and command U.S. Marine forces as they storm the island’s fortified defenses, facing fierce opposition from determined Japanese defenders.

Engage in intense and challenging combat in under 30 minutes with a minimal setup.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: A Place for All My Books: Collectible Pin Cards
description:
We have been thrilled to be able to feature the art of Naomi Lord in A Place for All My Books. Her work is so charming and warm that it really sets the tone for the world of the game. As a way to celebrate her art, we are introducing a new set of 6 exclusive Other Neat Stuff cards.

In the Other Neat Stuff deck, there are six "board games" with a set-collection mechanic. The more you collect, the more you score at the end of the game.

What if we gave players something else they love to collect in the game? And - what if we turned that into a way for Naomi to showcase existing works from her gallery? So we talked with Naomi and she identified six of her favorite pieces, a whimsical set of dragons!! And we put them into the world of the game as Collectible Pins!

So, you can swap out the board game cards - or just add them in to give players two different things to collect in the game. In any case, these will not be in the generally released games. They are a Kickstarter backer exclusive.

—description from the publisher
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
Man's best friend joins the fray! This 35-card mini-expansion introduces a new card type, the War Dog, that can be "joined" with a Regular Specialist (who becomes its Handler). These brave doggos allow Units to circumvent the 8-Specialist limit, and they even come in Legendary form as part of 2 new Legendary Units: the 26th Quartermaster War Dog Platoon, supplier of dogs to the US Marines in the Pacific, and the 13th (Lancashire) Parachute Battalion, British paras who dropped with their dogs during D-Day nighttime operations in Normandy.

Also included are 1 Award (the Dickin Medal), 1 Regular Item, 1 Badge, 1 War Story, 3 animal-themed Special Missions, and 6 Dog Servicemen!

—description from the publisher

Code Talkers were crucial to the war effort. These soldiers helped protect crucial communications from enemy ears with their Native codes. This mini-expansion is a tribute to these brave troopers, who served both in the Pacific and in Europe. Included are 3 Legendary Units: The Cree Talkers, who helped the US Air Force dominate the European skies; the 382nd Platoon of Navajo Code Talkers, part of the US Marines in the Pacific; and the 4th Signal Company, composed of Comanche Code Talkers, who landed on Utah Beach during the Normandy invasion. Also found in this 33-card pack are 6 new Code Talkers, 1 Award, 1 Ranking Specialist (the Interpreter), 1 Badge (Radio Operation), 1 Regular Item (the Native Codebook), and 3 Special Missions themed around the Code Talkers.

—description from the publisher

Poules Pirates (Pirate Chickens) is a strategic card game combining confrontation, majority and opportunism, where players take on the role of pirates ready to dig frantically for as much treasure as possible on l'Île aux Poules (Chicken Island).

But watch out for the cunning of your companions! Just one well-placed shovelful can land them the treasure you've been coveting. And as if that weren't enough, the Giant Chicken, guardians of the island, can appear at just the right moment to turn the game upside down and redistribute the balance of power in an instant.

The game is played in 12 rounds, corresponding to the number of cards in hand. All players start with the same cards (3 Giant Chicken and 9 shovels cards) and must play one card per turn, in a mechanic inspired by trick-taking, but with free management of card placement and strong strategic interaction. Once played, the cards never return to your hand, and may even end up in your opponents' treasures! Timing and anticipation are your best weapons for triumph.

With its many Trafalgar moves and constant interaction between players, Poules Pirates is an accessible (10+ years), fast (25 min) and fun game, which will appeal to casual players as well as fans of trick games and territory control!

A ruthless card game of deception, betrayal, and strategy! The Krazy King has a deadly illness, his many children come to his deathbed and he says a parent should not have a favourite child so will throw the Crown and the Fortune in to the middle of the Ocean but if he only had 1 child he would leave everything to them — creating greed within the hearts of all the Royals.

In Fight for Inheritance, you’ll need to outmanoeuvre, outwit, and outlast your rivals by avoiding assassination to be the last one standing and claim the crown.

Players use deception, alliances, wealth, and sabotage to outplay your opponents. Players buy and bribe bodyguards and use action cards to try to assassinate each other.

Keep taking turns until all other players have been assassinated and there only is one player left standing. This player wins the Fight For Inheritance and is given the crown and all the Kingdom’s fortune by the Krazy King! You have become the next Krazy Monarch!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Doggerland Hunt: Rules for Stone Age Miniature Gaming
description:
Rules for gaming the exhilarating hunting experience in the Stone Age. Players to step into the shoes of early humans, powerful megafauna, or agile herbivores. Doggerland Hunt adapts to solo play or group sessions, making every game a distinct journey through prehistoric terrains.

Included with the game is The Hunter's Tale, an exciting narrative that deepens your understanding of the challenges of harsh life in Doggerland. Doggerland Hunt promises to deliver countless hours of engaging, thoughtful play.

Key Features:

Versatile gameplay modes for solo players or groups up to twelve.
Diverse roles offer unique strategies and experiences.
Rich narrative content that brings the prehistoric setting to life.
Ideal for enthusiasts of strategy games and prehistoric history.
Simple, easy-to-learn rules that allow for a lot of player choices
Join the hunt and carve your path through the ancient landscapes of Doggerland.

—description from the publisher

The next age starts as they always have, with the birth of a new Wraith. This generation comes with a surprise: twin girls. Now two wraiths take the mantle and must defend their village from the onslaught of the Giants. And with this wave of attacks come another shock: a new Giant made of frightening, twisting metal.

The New Dawn expansion for Wraith & The Giants adds a new Twins Wraith board, requiring careful strategies with two Wraiths battling at the same time. Will you be able to topple the menacing Metal Giant?

—description from the back of the box

The Grimm Parasites have overtaken most of the universe -- würms released from their desolate planet to spread as a plague throughout humanity, twisting us into zombified mutants. But that was 663 years ago. Those still alive band together in Crews.

Clawing toward another day. Scraping together credits in what time the ’verse has left.

Sqwürmish is a stand-alone tabletop skirmish miniatures game by Brian Colin (Vast Grimm) with miniatures by Sól Blair (Malevolent Minis). Build your crew from our line of minis, or kit-bash your own version of a dismal faction.

Set in the Vast Grimm universe, it is a full hack of the Forbidden Psalm miniatures games and is inspired by and fully compatible with the MÖRK BORG.

—description from the publisher

Monopoly set in the time of Sherlock Holmes.  Immerse yourself in the world of the great detective Sherlock Holmes, with this special edition of MONOPOLY, created by the Sherlock Holmes Museum.  Featuring iconic locations, characters, and mysteries from the beloved detective's adventures.

Play as you would a regular game of Monopoly but land on spaces like Hat tax, Paddington Station, or Upper Swandam Lane.  Of course there is a Baker Street and Baker Street Irregulars spaces on the board too.  Object is to be the last player standing while everyone else goes bankrupt.  Buy and sell properties and sometimes even go to jail.  Great tokens in the form of Sherlock's hat, pipe, violin, and magnifying glass. Make sure to have a Banker as this comes with real monopoly money just like the original sets.  Also has plastic houses and hotels and the player figures are all metal.  Explore 221BBaker Street, Sherlock Holmes study, discover the Hound of the Baskerville case, and meet Professor Moriarty at the Reichenbach Falls.

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C23 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Material needed: Season 1.
Live the day the zombie invasion began, and how Season 1's survivors got to stick together!

Designed by Jeffrey Alley

A fun younger age game for all the family.

Match your cat colours OR cat types.

Match both and remove a match from all other players.

First to match 5 cats wins.

—description from the designer

Tour the board from the Amazon Temple to Avery’s Ship via the London Underground and Madagascar. Will you take a ride on Hog Wild or stowaway on a cruise ship? Build houses and hotels on your favourite Uncharted locations and watch the rent come pouring in. Take your chances with Fate and Fortune cards as you tour the board as one of the iconic Uncharted bespoke tokens.

—description from the publisher

Scaffolding is a solo roll&write game played with a single sheet and 3 regular dice.
You erect the scaffolding on a construction site. Build platform, ladders and cranes to reach ever higher places. Collect bonuses on the way and accomplish various tasks to help you reach the top of the building.

—description from the designer

The year was 1939, and  World War II was just about to escalate. The Soviet Union wanted 
to push its border with Finland on the Karelian Isthmus in an attempt to protect the city 
of Leningrad and make it secure from a potential German attack. The Soviets then proposed 
to exchange Soviet territory and secure a 30-year lease for a naval base at the Finnish 
island Hanko. Finland refused and the Soviet Union launched an attack on November 30, 1939, 
the start of the Russo - Finnish War.

With this amazing background and scenario, we at War and Honor tried to create a great game 
that will make you experience this war through all its twists and turns.

With determination, decisions, and dice, 
the winner will be delivered. 
We wish you luck!

• This is a 1vs1 war game covering the Soviet Union invasion of Finland.

• The game is set during the very harsh winter of 1939 at the start of WW2.

• You compete for land territories, victory cities and sea zones for 10 rounds.

• It is an easy game played in one evening for anyone who already owns an Axis and Allies game.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Mystery Booster is back! Mystery Booster 2 is everything you loved about the original Mystery Booster release, with playtest cards, reprints of iconic Magic staples, and artwork from across the history of the game.

Fan favourite cards return with Future Sight frames or white borders - and some cards only playable on digital releases are printed here for the first time.

Knight Fight is played on an incomplete Sudoku grid with a odd number of numbers entered. Each player has a differently-colored pencil.

The game begins with one player drawing a circle in any empty square and the other player deciding whether to move first or second. After that players take turns to draw circles in spaces without circles which are a knight's move from the opponent's previous move.

When no legal moves are available the player who has circled the most numbers wins.

Angria is a two-player game covering asymmetrical coastal warfare and corsairs in 18th century India, one of the few times in the colonial era that a South Asian power was able to successfully contest the seas with European navies. One player takes on the role of the Maratha navy and its ambitious commander, Kanhoji Angre aka Angria, while the other takes the part of his adversaries: the Mughal Empire, the British East India Company, and the Portuguese. The setting is India's Konkan mountainous coast, dominated by magnificent island and promontory forts. Players use cards to build up their capabilities on a strategic map and intercepting each other's trade before unleashing assaults on their enemies' forts. The Maratha shallow-draft ships are deadly in shallow coastal waters, but may struggle against the larger European vessels further offshore, especially in rough weather. Diplomacy also plays a key role, as the Marathas' foes are less committed to the fight and may be bought off or appeased.

—description from the designer

Layout is a game for any number of players or teams. One correctly-solved Sudoku puzzle is needed per player/team. These puzzles are cut along the bold lines to make nine-piece puzzles.

At the start of the game each player/team attempts to solve one of the puzzles by assembling the squares to make a valid Sudoku grid. The first one to do so gets a point, after which all the puzzles are passed clockwise. After everyone has seen all the puzzles the player/team with the most points wins.

Beyond Sudoku is a collection of eleven games designed as part of a contest. They all use a Sudoku grid - some a solved grid, some an unsolved grid - as a board.

Six of the games are for two players, two are for any number of players, two are also for any number but can explicitly played solo, and one (the contest winner) can be played by two to four players.

The Emperor's Suit is a trick-taking game played with a standard 52-card deck.

Each hand the deck is dealt into a hand for each player plus an extra hand plus an extra hand; each player in turn has the option of trading their hand for the extra one. If anyone does this points for the round are doubled.

Card play is must-follow, but when someone cannot follow their suit becomes trump and also becomes the suit to follow. The trick winner is the player who played the highest card in the last-played suit. Won tricks must be stored with the lead card on top.

At the end of the hand a single-suited trick scores nothing; any other trick scores the number of cards in the lead suit multiplied by the number of suits. A player taking no tricks scores 12.

After a number of hands equal to the number of players the player with the lowest score wins.

Play various games by only feeling the thickness of the cards.

Games: Guess the Thickness, Add 10, Pig's Tail, Poker, a variation of old maid (Old Man ジジ抜き Jiji Nuki), and Nervous Breakdown.

Merely ruling the world is not enough for the Neo-Kings ;in order to delight themselves with entertainment,Tech Oligarch, Pharma Boss, Crooked Politician & Religious Guru collectively known as The Cabal needed games for their pleasure
Just like 19th century kings used to bet on horses for their amusement. Similarly 21st century Neo-kings would bet on insanity of Jokers, Clowns & Jesters during their pastime, unaware that the outcasts had a secret desire to slay their heads

A dark fun eccentric party game where you choose to role play as one of the 10 lunatic Jokers, deranged Clowns & outcast Jesters collectively known as the Joklons.
Cast dark magic spells on your opponents to destabilize their sanity.

If your magical charm doesn’t work on the social media queens then try to seduce them by taking them out on dates, buy them drinks & gifts so that you can make them a part of your Neo-harem.
Watchout for cheaters, these influencer women are not trustworthy.
Protect your queens from wrath of Maya, she can trap your sweetheart in the blink of an eye & turn her into your opponent’s mistress.

Don’t let the lunatic Loner Kid come near your queens. Social media queens remind him of his abusive influencer mother. Beware he got a shotgun & he doesn’t hesitate to use it.

Engage in Joker fights to Rob, scare or screw other players to gain more assets & resources. (cards, powers, tokens, money, gemstones & antidotes)

Chant magical mantras to get secret code hints to unlock the scared dark magic Spell Book & win a glorious treasure
Flip the Judgment coin, if you get lucky, you avoid paying Neo-Tax to the Cabal
Use Joggy’s magic spell to send a friend request to your opponent & play as a team. Don’t forget deranged clowns can’t be trusted. Mad clown may bite you & run away with all your assets.
Navigate through the unpredictable insanity of Jokers, Clowns & Jesters to reach to the Cabal.

Before reaching the Cabal, Joklons must fight against each other & gain enough power & leverage to breach the security apparatus of the Neo Kings.

If you are not yet powerful enough then manipulate the Neo-kings to raise bounty against your opponents.

After you have hooked up with all 4 social media queens, Spellbound  all your opponents with your black magic and slayed The Cabal then you finally fight to defeat the Wheeler Dealer in order to change the status-quo & introduce anarchy into this world

—description from the designer

The game is set in the underwater world of SpongeBob SquarePants, specifically in the Krusty Krab restaurant, where players compete to become the Employee of the Month. To achieve this, they must skillfully prepare burgers according to customer orders while overcoming obstacles, including the mischievous schemes of Plankton.

The game is played over multiple rounds until the order deck runs out. Each round consists of four phases: Preparation, Grill, Kitchen, and Cleanup. Players gather ingredient cards, select and collect necessary ingredients from the grill, and use them to complete burger orders following specific sequence rules. Special cards, such as Plankton’s sabotage actions and ketchup as a wildcard ingredient, add strategic depth. Each character also has a unique skill that can be used once per game.

The goal is to earn the most Victory Points (VP) by fulfilling orders, completing bonus conditions, and collecting ketchup cards. The player with the highest VP at the end is declared the winner and earns the title of Employee of the Month.

Discover the epic conclusion to the tragic tale of 80s musician and polar bear, Bobby McColdsnap, as he resurfaces for one final return rock tour across the North Pole. "His music has been dubbed 'Arctic Wave' which stirs up nostalgic memories of an 80s mix tape fed through a Panasonic boombox with its bass turned all the way up to avalanche!"
-J. Howard Jones, UnKut Magazine

Xylotar Unhinged is the expansion to the 2024 trick taking deduction card game, Xylotar. Unhinged introduces two expansions, which can be added separately or combined together for all the epic 80s ballad feels.

Bonus Beats: 
Add dynamic flair and a unique style to your playing with bonus beats. Bonus Beats gives each player unique power cards which can be played only once through the game, but can those power chords be enough to push your performance to the top?

Off-Key Cards:
The core of Arctic Wave is its unhinged style mixing nostalgic melodies with raw & rapid improvisation - an explosive cocktail of rage, angst, longing, and nothing but authentic emotion. Off-Key cards inject fluffy furry fuzziness into the deduction gameplay with half-valued cards keeping musicians on their toes... or paws...

—description from the publisher

A dark fantasy card game of survival, deception, and power.

In Blightwatch, you take on the role of an elite operative navigating the ruins of a world consumed by the Blight — a mind-corrupting force unleashed by ancient magic. The last survivors fight not only to uncover lost artifacts but also to escape the growing madness around them.

Blightwatch is a fast-paced card game where players must outmaneuver, mislead, and manipulate both the Blighted forces around them and each other. Every round represents a desperate skirmish, with players racing to control the most powerful Blighted — only to rid themselves of this control before the Blight overwhelms them.

As the game progresses, operatives acquire artifacts — powerful relics that offer new abilities but may corrupt those who wield them. Tensions rise, alliances break, and in the end, only one will stand. Will you survive the Blight… or become part of it?

Features:
Strategic Card Combat – Outsmart and outplay your opponents using tactical mechanics and strategic planning.

Evolving Player Powers – Gain game-changing artifacts that shift the balance of power.

Hidden Alliances & Betrayal – Work together… until you can’t.

Immersive Dark Fantasy Setting – A rich, dramatic world of lore-driven conflict and desperate survival. Every mechanic and ability is seamlessly woven into the game’s world and story.

Blightwatch is a game of cunning, survival, and escalating tension, where the true enemy may not be the Blight — but your fellow operatives.

—description from the publisher

Cozy Cat Cafe is a tile placing game and players need to strategically place their cats and furniture in the right position to entice the customers and gain extra points by bringing ambience into the cafe. Players will compete to be the first to attract big spenders, shake things up by activating cat abilities and pull off perfect combos to transform their cafe with maximum ambience.

Each player starts with an empty Cozy Cat Cafe, gathering resources over a total of 3 rounds each with 9 turns.

Players take turns to either adopt cats, buy furniture or invite customers into their cafe. Adopting cats will cost you cookies but allow you to play cat cards, buying furniture will cost you coins but inviting the customers will bring you in resources as well as points.

Cozy Cat Cafe has 16 goal tiles for different bonus combinations each game and a variety of different customer cards for increased variability.

boardgame: The Horse and The Squirrel Flippables Game
description:
In this Candy Land style color based movement game, you get to race to the finish as a horse or a squirrel. Only horses can cross rivers and only squirrels can climb trees. You will need to get lucky with the flips to change back and forth to reach the finish line first.

The game uses flippable pawns that look like horses until turned upside down then they are squirrels. The spinner controls when you flip and when you don't.

Strategic Bingo? How is that possible? In Bingo - The Mind, events and abilities transform the classic bingo game into a thrilling strategy challenge. Players must navigate through  unique events, and special abilities that can change the course of the game. This new twist on bingo demands thoughtful decisions and clever tactics, ensuring every match is an exciting and unpredictable experience. Prepare to outsmart your opponents and claim victory with strategy, not just luck!

Pulp Romance is a solitaire card game about searching for your lost love in Europe between the wars. The two main mechanisms are placement and adjacency.

From a "journey row", you acquire cards in five suits — cities, friends, detectives, newspapers, and bankers - and place them in your search tableau, activating them, with their actions dependent on cards previously played. You need to defeat a sixth suit — rivals — when they're dealt to the journey row through management of your tableau. Custom dice give bonus abilities to your chosen character, with the game including four double-sided characters and four double-sided love loves.

It is the first expansion for the game Pulp Romance. It adds new character cards, new lost love cards, 9 new companion cards and of course a new die!

—description from the designer

The move and groove game for ages 2+, where you roll dice to create a dance that you must enact before you can complete your turn and let the next player have a go.

How to play: Roll the dice, do the dance move, flip over the audience member to enjoy the show. Once you reveal all of the audience members, the game is complete!

boardgame: Adventure Time Card Wars: Flame Princess vs. Fern
description:
Adventure Time Card Wars is the most epic card game ever found in the Land of Ooo, or anywhere for that matter! In the Adventure Time episode "Card Wars", Jake begs Finn to play his favorite trading card game. Finn accepts, despite warnings from Beemo on how very competitive Jake can be while playing the game. Finn and Jake battle against each other to decide who will be the Dweeb and who will be the Cool Guy.

Now fans can play the actual Card Wars game in Adventure Time Card Wars: Flame Princess vs. Fern, with all of the action taking place in four combat lanes.

Each turn, a player has two Action Points to spend on Creatures, Spells, and Locations, and cards cost between 0 and 2 Action Points to play. When you summon a Creature, you choose which lane you want to place it in; some Creatures have FLOOP abilities that allow you to use their special abilities. When you attack with a Creature down a lane, you might find an opposing Creature, or you might get to deal your damage straight to your opponent's Hit Points. Reduce your opponent's Hit Points from 25 to 0 and you're the Cool Guy (and you win the game)!

Each Card Wars starter set has a pair of different forty-card decks and is ready to play right out of the box. Mix the decks together to come up with your own righteous combos!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Adventure Time Card Wars: Prismo vs. The Lich
description:
Adventure Time Card Wars is the most epic card game ever found in the Land of Ooo, or anywhere for that matter! In the Adventure Time episode "Card Wars", Jake begs Finn to play his favorite trading card game. Finn accepts, despite warnings from Beemo on how very competitive Jake can be while playing the game. Finn and Jake battle against each other to decide who will be the Dweeb and who will be the Cool Guy.

Now fans can play the actual Card Wars game in Adventure Time Card Wars: Prismo vs. The Lich, with all of the action taking place in four combat lanes.

Each turn, a player has two Action Points to spend on Creatures, Spells, and Locations, and cards cost between 0 and 2 Action Points to play. When you summon a Creature, you choose which lane you want to place it in; some Creatures have FLOOP abilities that allow you to use their special abilities. When you attack with a Creature down a lane, you might find an opposing Creature, or you might get to deal your damage straight to your opponent's Hit Points. Reduce your opponent's Hit Points from 25 to 0 and you're the Cool Guy (and you win the game)!

Each Card Wars starter set has a pair of different forty-card decks and is ready to play right out of the box. Mix the decks together to come up with your own righteous combos!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Adventure Time Card Wars: Peppermint Butler vs. Magic Man
description:
Adventure Time Card Wars is the most epic card game ever found in the Land of Ooo, or anywhere for that matter! In the Adventure Time episode "Card Wars", Jake begs Finn to play his favorite trading card game. Finn accepts, despite warnings from Beemo on how very competitive Jake can be while playing the game. Finn and Jake battle against each other to decide who will be the Dweeb and who will be the Cool Guy.

Now fans can play the actual Card Wars game in Adventure Time Card Wars: Peppermint Butler vs. Magic Man, with all of the action taking place in four combat lanes.

Each turn, a player has two Action Points to spend on Creatures, Spells, and Locations, and cards cost between 0 and 2 Action Points to play. When you summon a Creature, you choose which lane you want to place it in; some Creatures have FLOOP abilities that allow you to use their special abilities. When you attack with a Creature down a lane, you might find an opposing Creature, or you might get to deal your damage straight to your opponent's Hit Points. Reduce your opponent's Hit Points from 25 to 0 and you're the Cool Guy (and you win the game)!

Each Card Wars starter set has a pair of different forty-card decks and is ready to play right out of the box. Mix the decks together to come up with your own righteous combos!

—description from the publisher

Hashtag Hustle is a strategic 91-card game featuring 19 different card types divided into three categories:


    Passive cards: Provide continuous benefits.
    Active cards: Trigger immediate effects.
    Special cards: Grant permanent bonuses and unique protections. 


The game is played turn by turn, with hidden cards in hand that players reveal on the table to build their strategy. The goal is to build and optimize your channel by gaining Followers, improving channel quality and streaming time, which allow access to larger subscriber groups and strategic bonuses. Players must make tactical choices between opportunism and strategy, including selecting special cards from six available options, such as Sponsor, Cybersecurity, moderator, secret donor, steel reputation.

On their turn, a player can:


    Play up to 2 cards (except when special effects apply).
    Draw additional cards from the Player deck or the Improvement deck (if available).
    Interact during other players' turns by playing instant cards (e.g., steal, counter, expel, hack), which do not cost an action.
    At the end of their turn, they draw back up to 5 cards.


The game ends immediately when the End card is drawn. The player with the most points wins and becomes the next social media star!

Pikes Peak Racers is the exciting and addicting dice game that puts you in the driver's seat!

Since 1916, the world's greatest race drivers have been challenging the snowy, wind-whipped summit of America's most famous mountain.  Now you and your fellow racers can compete in dash to the top.  More throttle if you dare... e-e-easy on the brakes.  And always remember - records are made to be broken.

So, now it's your turn, pick up the dice and challenge the peak!

Pikes Peak Racers is played over a series of rounds each representing one part of the Pikes Peak International Hill Climb race.  Players roll dice twice per round, locking dice aside to make sets. Players can use the red brake die to a third time, but must add the brake die to the round score. One round represents switchbacks in the road and players may only roll twice and must use the brake die on the second roll.  Each set allows the player to remove a certain number of scoring dice at the end of the round.  Since the score total represents time in the race, players work for the lowest scores possible.

Napoleon's Bloody Big Battles! is written by Dr Mark Smith (who also wrote the "Bloody Big Battles in INDIA!" scenario book). 
The 16 scenarios in NBBB cover the biggest battles where Napoleon commanded in person after becoming Emperor:

     The Wars of the Third and Fourth Coalitions (1805-1807) – 4-scenario mini-campaign
         Austerlitz                                     
         Jena/Auerstedt                             
         Eylau                                           
         Friedland                                     
    
     The War of the Fifth Coalition (1809) – 3-scenario mini-campaign
         Eckmühl                                      
         Aspern-Essling                             
         Wagram                                       
    
     Napoleon’s Invasion of Russia (1812) – 2-scenario mini-campaign
         Borodino                                      
         Berezina                                      
    
     The War of the Sixth Coalition (1813) – 5-scenario mini-campaign
         Lützen                                         
         Bautzen                                      
         Dresden                                      
         Leipzig                                         
         Hanau                                          
    
     The Final Battles (1814 & 1815) – 2 stand-alone scenarios
         La Rothière                                  
         Waterloo
    


—description from the publisher

Any Given Run Play is a game for Stonehenge loosely themed around American football. Each player uses a set of pieces representing offensive linemen, defensive linemen, and a ball carrier, and uses the cards to move these pieces around the board. There are rules for what happens when different combinations of pieces encounter each other. The first player to move their ball carrier from space 1 to space 30 wins the game.

Six-Tac-Toe is played on a 4x6 square grid. One player writes an "X" in a square and the other player then chooses whether to make the odd or even moves.

On a move a player can write either an "X" or an "O" in a square. If they create one or more "tic-tac-toes" - three matching symbols in a line - they must mark them. However, lines of more than three symbols don't count, and a line of three that could be extended is not marked.

Once all the squares are filled the player making the even moves wins if there are at least six tic-tac-toes marked. Otherwise the odd player wins.

The Way to My Heart is a cozy cooperative tile laying game using path cards to make a way to your heart. Designed to be played as a couple or solo.

Players hold 1-2 cards to use on their turn or choose the visible card from the table. Take turns adding a card to any of the 4 My Heart paths. Cards can be placed adjacent to or partially covering another card to ultimately make paths with the win conditions.

To win, each of the 4 paths must have exactly one zone of each of the 3 background types. Each of these zones must contain at least two path segments and one heart icon.

Sometimes love is a little messy, so don't worry about the side paths or if the My Heart paths are outside of the final win conditions at first. Love will find a way.

—description from the publisher

Stack City is a competitive, 3D city-building Engine Building, Resource management style board game where you represent one of four unique enterprises.

Each enterprise focuses on different strategies - building, earning money, earning permits, or acquiring action cards. Players compete to stack and expand their buildings, carefully managing limited space and resources. Every move counts as you advance on the board, earn rewards, and accumulate Economy Points.

The game ends when a player reaches 32 build spaces or all buildings that can be placed have been.  The player with the most Economy points wins.

—description from the designer

The Marines are here! This 93-card expansion introduces the US Marine Corps to the game, in the form of 12 Legendary Units. All of them use the new Marine Die, a unique “high risk, high reward” game mechanism that adds an interesting layer of decision-making when you roll your regular dice. But that’s not all! Semper Fi also offers 3 Marine Special Missions, 1 Award, 1 Badge, 2 Regular Items, 1 Vehicle, 1 War Story, 6 Marine Officers, 6 Marine Servicemen, and of course, 4 Marine Dice (green). Show the enemy what these elite soldiers can do!

—description from the designer

This 24-card mini-expansion was created to honor the brave chaplains, affectionately nicknamed "sky pilots" by the troops, who provided spiritual comfort to soldiers of all faiths. Included here is a very unique Legendary Unit, the Chaplain Corps, that comes in 6 different versions (Catholic, Protestant, Jewish, Muslim, Hindu and Sikh). The Chaplain is ADDED to your regular Legendary Unit (or regular nation) and adds a lot of options to your already-existing RWB Table. Also included are an Award, a Badge, a War Story and 3 religious Special Missions.

—description from the designer

Bluff Pastry is a bluffing game for two chefs who each start the game by rolling 4xd4 to secretly determine the pie they need to make for arriving visitors. With this knowledge in hand, the ingredients are shuffled — four each of four types — and five cards dealt to each chef. Place the remaining six cards face up in two rows of three cards to form the larder.

Over five rounds, chefs take turns placing a card from their hand face down and bluffing about the type of card it is: pastry, sauce, sweet, or savory. Using the cards in their hand and in the larder, the chef must attempt to work out for each card whether the other player is telling the truth. If they guess correctly, they gain a bluff point, then can either pick up the card from the table or take one at random from the opposing chef's hand.

After each round, the chefs each place a card from their hand into their own pie and spend bluff points to acquire ingredients from the larder that they need. After the final round, the chef's pies are revealed, and the scores tallied.

boardgame: Accelerate and Attack!: Aeons of War
description:
Accelerate and Attack! Aeons of War is a starship combat wargame design for fleet actions from Aotrs Shipyards.

The game places the emphasis on tactical decision making and manoeuvre, while providing a technology system comprehensive enough to allow a wide variety of fleet playstyles but without compromising speed of play.

Accelerate and Attack is a generic, open system that can be used with any miniatures. There is no "official" background here; even the sample fleets included are design so that they can be "plugged in" to your existing backgrounds or genre of choice! Players are encouraged to create or adapt their own fleets.

Accelerate and Attack is suited for games of about twenty or more vessels per side for an evening's gaming.

Features:


     Innovative Manoeuvre Factor system and Command and control structure that keeps the record-keeping down to ship damage and the game flowing (no orders to write!)
     Tech level system with up to 20 tech levels
     Over 30 technologies allows enormous variety and versatility in fleet differentiation even between the same tech level
     The "buckets o' D20"-based system* allows for granularity in the tech system while preserving the speed and flow of a mass-dice system.
     Two sample fleets, the alien Vivrathk Aggression and the mysterious Stone Portals, with ship markers and three starter scenarios to get the ball rolling
     170 Pages (including main rules (40 pages), Ship design and technology (68 pages), sample fleets and scenarios (32 pages)).



Accelerate and Attack! comes with two PDF versions, one for single-sided (i.e. regular printing) or digital reading and one with mirrored margins for double-sided printing.


     Or "handful-of-D20 plus the average-dice-table" for the less adventurous!


—description from the designer
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
In a close and dark future, hackers from all types carry out industrial espionage and other tasks on behalf of all-powerful mega corporations.

S3PSYS  (pronounced like sepsis) is a confrontation game for 2 players in which you try to be the first to reach 3 points through the use of PLOTS. In this war your weapons are SCRIPTS, TRAPS and HARDWARE.


         WHAT YOU NEED TO PLAY
    


- The files including 18 cards and a rules booklet
- A way to track of points, viruses and bits (some tokens of sort).


         SETUP
    


Each player starts with 0 virus and 2 bits.
Shuffle the cards and deal 5 to each players.
Keep a place for the discard: it is where the cards go once used or destroyed.
Place the remaining cards to form the draw pile side-by-side the discard.


         PLAY
    


Decide who starts.
The first player skip the draw step on the first turn.

Each turn you will in sequence do:
 1. Maintenance step: Flip face up all installed HARDWARE cards; they are now active. TRAPs and PLOTs stay face down.

2. Draw step: one card from the draw pile. If the draw pile is empty, shuffle the discard pile to form a new draw pile.

3. Actions step: By default you get 2 actions a turn that you can take among the ones listed in the ACTIONS section. (You can take several times the same action).

4. End step: If you have more than 5 cards in hand, discard down to 5. It is now the next players turn.


         ACTIONS
    


During your turn you can take actions among the following:
 - Use your whole turn to execute a PLOT: If you have a PLOT already installed you can flip it, apply it's effect and then discard it.

- Play a SCRIPT: Apply the effect indicated on the card and then discard it

- Install a card: Play a HARDWARE, PLOT or TRAP face down.

- Gain 1 bit: Take a bit token from the reserve.

- Pay 1 bit to hack an installed card from your opponent: Chose a face down card from your opponent and flip it over. If it is a PLOT or a TRAP, apply the effect and then discard it. Else simply discard it.


         COUNTERING
    


During the action phase any player can spend 1 bit to prevent the planned action from being executed. It is called a counter.

A player can counter a counter by paying 1 more bit than the opposing player.

Note: A hack on a installed card must be countered after the card has been chosen but before it is flipped over. If the hack is successfully countered the card stays untouched.


         END OF THE GAME
    


The game ends when one of the player is the winner. The winner is the one to first reach 3 points while having less than 2 virus or whose adversary reaches 2 virus.

These rules introduce a new faction (NSDAP) allowing for 6 players to play Die Atombombe.
It was published in Against The Odds Annual 2019 - 
La Vendée 1793 and comes with 6 new cards.
The folowing rules (page36) are modified when using this option:

     2.0
     2.1
     11.2
     11.3

Compete to capture areas of the board and the stunning marvels within, and earn the most majesty to win.
Rethink your strategy in this new game mode that totally changes the way the game is scored.

Cosmic Arena is a 2-player strategic card-based confrontation game for games lasting about 30 to 40 minutes, for ages 12 and up. In this game, you will build your system so that it earns you more points than your opponent's.

2 important mechanics :

     Fusion planet for have more powerful, and points maker planets.
     All cards you play go under the 'potential deck' of the opponent.


Players have turn one after one.

You're a celeste, you create the universe ! You'll create your cosmic system around a sun for have more points than your opponent. 
You have 3 power tokens, you use them for complete action like play a card (attack or creation). You can upgrade this number in make creatures on your planets. 
You can complete a lot of action in one turn : 
Create a level 1 planet, 
mix 2 level 1 for have a level two,
Optimize you potential deck,
Take cards,
discard cards,
...

When you use all or parts of your tokens, your turn is over. 
Take 2 cards from knowledge deck, exile one.
Then the opponent play. When you are uneder attack, you could defense yourself with cards, only if you can pay the price.

3 way to finish the game : no more level 2 planet available, zero cards in knowledge deck or one color planet of each type in your system. When a finished condition is done, the game stop directly. Not finish your turn.

The winner is the most points owner. The points is count with the sun informations. If you change the sun, you change the way to count points.

—description from tne publisher

boardgame: Dispersion: The Great Commission of the 12 Apostles
description:
A resource-management strategy game based on the Dispersion of the 12 apostles following the resurrection of Jesus Christ. In Dispersion, players seek to gain the most Followers to "THE WAY" by building House Churches and City Churches.

On their turn, players use Blessing resources to move their apostle piece in an attempt to build churches in the seven known regions in the ancient world (the world map is based on the Greek geographer, Strabo, whose map was in use in 30 A.D.).

Mission cards and Hardship cards are used to build churches. On their turn, players need to determine if they have to acquire more resources (i.e., Blessing, Mission, Hardship) to use on later turn; proceed to build one or more churches; or play the special action from a Disciple card that can gain them additional resources, churches or followers.

Players can Disciple cards when they build churches. They also can gain the assistance of other Apostles when establishing the last House Church in a region. The game ends when nearly all City and House Churches are built and a player lands at Omega.

—description from the publisher

Volume 12 in the Revolution Games American Civil War Series. Simulates the first day of the Battle of Gettysburg.

Gettysburg: The First Day is an historical portrayal of one of the most monumental engagements of the entire American Civil War. It was a pivotal battle where leaders on both sides had to constantly grapple with a plethora of command and control challenges. Revolution Games’ highly-popular “Blind Swords” system is perfectly suited to depict the extreme chaos of that fateful day.

This is a two player game where one side plays the Union and the other the Confederate. Using the 'Blind Swords' chit pull mechanic to activate brigades randomly. The game also handles very well for players who play both sides as solitaire.

Gettysburg: The First Day adds to the 'Blind Swords' system by adding situational innovations such as; Artillery Barrages, Union temporary CIC's, Rebel Sharpshooters, Buford's dismounted cavalry, and Union breech-loading rifles.

Also included is an optional variant for a variable march of two powerful formations, Confederate R.H. Anderson's Division and Union Slocum's 12th Corps.

This is not a so-called "monster" game; instead, Gettysburg: The First Day was designed to be readily accessible for all players whether they are familiar with the game series or not. Tense and comparatively quick-playing, varied strategy options offer excellent opportunities for replayability.

boardgame: The First Sketch: Visionary Supply Kit
description: This is a redesign of the First Sketch Booster Set, including 12 inspiration tokens, 4 d6 dice used to resolve some card effects, 6 deck dividers, 8 booster packs, 3 promo cards, and a card checklist. It also introduces the new white card backs.

Step into the realm of Escape Room Tycoon: Corporate Events expansion.

Forge lucrative contracts with major companies, while the brand awareness track empowers you to customize end-game goals, ensuring a competitive edge with a steady, permanent income stream.

Dive into the world of strategic partnerships with Escape Room Tycoon: Franchise.

Align with established companies for unique powers, leveraging the franchise track to unlock new opportunities. Seize the chance to elevate your empire and maximize your gains!

Unleash the full potential of your escape room empire with Escape Room Tycoon: Venue+ expansion.

Employ seasoned specialists for enhanced customer experiences and unlock substantial bonuses. Expand your venue with a new parking lot and establish a shuttle service, ensuring a steady influx of eager customers.
In this game, teams use hidden signals to collect 5-card sets. You can try to get a fake set scored, but the opponent team can call cap! On you.

boardgame: A Savage and Romantic War: A Wargamer's Guide to the First Carlist War, Spain, 1833-1840
description:
In the quarter-century after the fall of Napoleon, there were several wars in Europe, and this Spanish civil war was the lengthiest, and most varied. It was the first of a set of conflicts that split the nation and would continue to do so for a century and more. A Savage and Romantic War gives the wargamer all the information needed to play games set in Spain in these tumultuous seven years, and to make and paint the armies that fought.

The First Carlist War is one that is becoming more familiar to English-speakers, and can be gamed in any scale, with dedicated ranges available in 28 and 18mm. Although taking place only two decades after the latter stages of the Peninsular campaign, and sometimes over the same landscape, it has a quality all of its own. It was big enough to have full-scale battles with two dozen or so units a side, and small enough that games can be played with a brigade or two, and with no need to compromise on scale – every pair of guns or battalion can appear on the table. There were numerous skirmishes, with Carlists in their huge berets and irregular bands facing militia, guardsmen and everything in between. The sheer variety and picturesque appearance of the soldiers of four nations who fought, the involvement of larger-than-life generals on both sides, and the spectacular scenery over which it took place make this a perfect conflict for re-creating in miniature.

Those who play most Napoleonic rules will be able to use them for this war, and this work is not linked to any particular set.

The book has a short history of the war, then full details of the Spanish, French, British and Portuguese forces, including organisation, tactics, uniforms, weapons, equipment and flags. Then there are descriptions of 13 battles, each with the map and orders of battle that will make it easy to translate onto the table-top. Finally, there is a lengthy account of the Oriamandi campaign of 1837, culminating in the dramatic battle which saw the largest involvement of British troops in Europe since Waterloo.

Throughout, there is detail of which regiments did what and how they did it.

—description from the publisher

Conversation Card Games - This simple card deck has 200 engaging question cards to help spark conversation and help people connect. And a golden ticket card that leads to 200 Seek Discomfort adventures. Ignite a connection, then pick your adventure! Great games for adults and family.
    
Yes Theory - The only way to grow is to do something uncomfortable! Strengthen any relationship in your life! With 200 conversation starter cards that help you make deeper connections with teens, adults, family, friends or even strangers.
    
Get Deep - Spark was made for a wide range of ages. Use at home for dinner conversation starters for friends and family or travel with it in the car for road trip games. This epic fun game is also great for getting to know people at work or school.
    
Golden Ticket - Amp up the fun and excitement with these unique playing cards. The Golden Ticket is a metal card with a QR code that will prompt you to take an adventure. With 200 cool challenges that come from our random generator.
    
Ice Breakers - Spark is the best card game to cultivate connections, build your relationship and get to know others better. Spark is a fun games with cards good for family game night, party games, couples game, date night game, teen games, adult games and group card games.

Special Valentine’s day Mission for 2 players.
Print and play mission for the game BOMB BUSTERS.

You only need the PDF with:
- the mission card
- 2 "heart" tokens to cut

This mission is only for 2 players but you don't need to be in love :)

boardgame: Steel Wolves and German Fleet Boats: Deluxe Edition
description:
Steel Wolves is a solitaire simulation of the German submarine war against British and Allied shipping, the follow-on title to Silent War. Volume I covers the period from the start of the War in 1939 to the early months of 1943. You serve as the Supreme Commander of Uboats, Kreigsmarine. Your mission is to deploy your forces with maximum effect to destroy British and Allied shipping while limiting your submarine losses.

Featured scenarios cover actions ranging from famous short single sub patrols through short campaigns, ending with the full campaign covering 1939 to 1943. The game map breaks the Atlantic, Mediterranean, South Atlantic, and Western Indian Ocean into historical Operations Areas through which subs patrol in search of shipping. Special rules feature historical events such as the invasion of Norway and Torch; b-dienst, ULTRA, and the intelligence war; torpedo improvements, German wolfpack tactics, Super-Skippers, large vs. small U-boat philosophy, appeals to Berlin for resources, and many others. Every German sub deployed between 1939 and 1943 (as well as some what-if classes) is represented.

Steel Wolves Expansion 1: German Fleet Boats assumes that Germany never entered into the 1935 Naval agreement. German Fleet Boats assumes significant changes to the historic design strategy. It assumes that early Type I, II, and VII submarines are built as historically occurred, but prior to starting a more extensive building program, OKM decides to build cruiser/fleet type submarines starting with more Type Is and four of the even larger (projected) Type III, the designs for which were based on the IA, then followed by the VIII, XI, and XII.

German Fleet Boats alters the opening of the campaign, allowing players to play through to the end of the England Imperiled scenario, which ends at the start of Operation Barbarossa. Modifications include changes to allied task forces and the availability of additional allied units. Also included are three additional Italian Cagni class submarines.

This new edition features a mounted game board, an updated full-color rule book incorporating all changes and improvements, and an updated map and charts/tables. Steel Wolves and German Fleet Boats, Deluxe Edition, is the definitive game of the German submarine war against British and Allied shipping.

Product Information
Complexity: Medium
Playing Time: 1 hour (scenarios), 125 hours (full campaign)
Solitaire Suitability: Perfect (designed for solitaire play)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Energetic 2.0 is a new edition of Energetic, the 4-6 player cooperative board game about the future of New York City that's now used at more than  40 universities and dozens of high school around the world.

You play from 2021 to 2035, a turn each year, or from 2026 to 2040 for the contemporary timeline. Your team must work together to balance your budget, your grid stability, and your public opinion to keep the lights on, win elections, overcome climate impacts, and build 16 gigawatts of clean power for the city. Then you will have replaced all fossil fuels, provided there is a matching reduction in demand from behavior change and efficiency.

Our objective is to give players a quick grounding in what solving climate change actually means, in a physical and social sense, based on demand of energy and the types of renewable or carbon neutral generation that can supply 8 million New Yorkers.

We hope playing this game can make your role in the coming decade more effective, better informed, and more powerful, and that by providing a tangible vision of what part of the solution looks like, society can begin the necessary step of reorienting itself towards this larger goal. It's a lot of fun, too!

Energetic 2.0 is factory-produced and beautifully made, with a very large folding board and additional cards for Wildfire Smoke and Extreme Precipitation in the Event decks. Sets come with a 52 page guide to the cards and information in the game, as well as a map board, three card decks, 142 infrastructure pieces, and more.

During development, Energetic 1.0 was tested and enjoyed by people ages 11 to 60, including middle school students, MIT energy PhDs, former commodity traders, and leaders at environmental NGOs. The game is in use at organizations including Yale University; Carnegie Mellon University; Nature Energy; NYSERDA; Con Edison; Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC); and the British Government's Department for Business, Energy, and Industrial Strategy.

—description from the designer

Primordial is a strategic two-player card game where players control mighty primordial creatures in a head-to-head battle. Each player commands a team of 3 to 6 primordial creatures, with each creature having its own unique deck of cards to draw from when in the Active Position.

Gameplay is simultaneous—both players secretly select their moves by covering their cards on the table, only revealing them once both are ready. Players can choose from Attack cards or another primordial from their hand, and additionally play an Event or Arena card alongside to influence the battle.

The goal is to reduce your opponent’s primordials' health to zero. When a primordial is defeated, it is removed from the game. The first player to eliminate all of their opponent's primordials wins.

With personalized deck construction, strategic card plays and tactical decision-making, Primordial offers a unique blend of creature combat and card strategy, ensuring no two battles are ever the same.

—description from the designer

The God of Lightning and Tricks has summoned all of Earth’s mortals to compete in the Lightning Games - a gloriously chaotic contest where cunning, luck, and raw electrical energy collide. Only one can emerge as Champion, so brace yourself... things are about to get shocking!

Shocking Deal is a fast-paced, strategic card game of outrageous betrayals, devastating attacks, and unpredictable twists. Your Mission? Eliminate your opponents by destroying their Lightning Essence - the spark of life that keeps them in the game. Protect your own Essence, strike at the perfect moment, and don't blink because one wrong move could short circuit your chance for victory.

Players draw from a powerful deck where they will discover six unique card types:

• Attack Cards – Deal damage to destroy Lightning Essence.
• Defence Cards – Protect your Lightning Essence from incoming attacks.
• Retaliation Cards – Transfer or absorb damage.
• Action Cards – Mix up gameplay with various effects.
• Bad Luck Cards – Looks like lady luck just handed you a live wire, take 1 damage when drawn.
• Lightning Essence Cards – Your life force in the game. Lose them all, and you're out.

Each turn, players take two actions - drawing cards, playing cards, or a mix of both - ensuring rapid-fire chaos, high energy and electrifying banter. No one is safe. Everyone is a target and only the sharpest strategist (or luckiest mortal) will gain the favour of the God of Lightning.

Are you ready to outwit, out-shock, and outlast your fellow mortals in this addictive card game that will leave you wanting more?

—description from the publisher

A simple family boardgame created to increase awareness of how guide dogs are trained in Australia. Proceeds from the sale of the game assist the Royal Guide Dogs for the Blind Association of Australia. 
Roll-and move game with frequent card-draw spaces. The 3 decks of cards (one for each of the puppy walk section, acceptance test section, and training sections of the 104-space track) describe issues with guide dog training, and provide movement bonuses and penalties, as well as possibly causing the withdrawal of the dog from training, or even its death.
Each player begins with 5 dogs to be trained. He starts the first dog off on the track, rolling the die and moving the number of spaces shown (a six allows a second throw), and drawing and actioning any card required to be drawn on his ending space. Once his first dog has reached the third and longest section of the track (square 42 or later), he can start his second dog. 
There are no rules for player or dog interaction. Any number of dogs can apparently be on any given space, and all ignore thep resence or movement past of all other dogs.
The winner is the player who gets "the most number of dogs trained first" (i.e. reach the final space with the most surviving dogs). 
The board is coloured in a typically 1970s garish mix of hot pink, and primary yellow, blue, green and orange.
Counters are provided for up to 4 players.
publishers: Royal Guide Dogs for the Blind Association of Australia

description:
Race to help your robot recharge!

Begin each round by drawing a card. Players race to stack batteries to match that card & then get them safely back to their base. The first player to correctly match the card
wins the round. Who can get their robot recharged first!

HOW TO PLAY:
Begin each round by picking up a card from the deck. Quickly identify the battery colors on the card that you picked up. The first player that successfully stacks the batteries that match the drawn card and places them on their base without any batteries falling over wins the round! First Player to win 3 rounds wins the game! After each round, shuffle the deck and begin a new round by drawing a card.

HOW TO OPERATE ROBOTS:
Push down the robots head to activate the arms and stack the batteries. Once you have your battery combination stacked, use you fingers to roll your robot over to your base and place the stack of batteries onto the platform to recharge. BUT BE CAREFUL! If any Batteries fall over, they become defoctive and you must start all over!

—description from the publisher

The Elder Gods are coming and the only way to stop them is to destroy all 4 Gates by using every resource at your disposal! You will only succeed by skillfully deciding which items to keep and discard along your adventure.

In Hand of Cthulhu, players fight through an onslaught of monsters until they reach a Gate to worlds beyond our own. There are 12-16 cards between you and each Gate. Each card is an Encounter that is either a Bane (Monster) or Boon (Event). To fight Monsters, you must use your Items wisely and decide which Items to keep and which ones to discard. Once you get to a Gate, you will attack it and exploit its fractures to destroy it. Once you destroy the fourth gate you have successfully saved our world. But if you ever lose all of your health, you die, and if you lose all of your sanity, you go insane and lose the game!

—description from the designer

Romeo Roberts and Juliet Leonard were initially drawn to each other by the pairing of their first names.  Then, they discovered a shared love for violence and bloodshed that caused their love to bloom.  After a five-state crime spree that included robbing six banks and murdering nine people in cold blood, they were eventually apprehended.  A speedy trial and conviction sent Romeo to Riverbend Penitentiary and Juliet to Elmhurst Correctional.  But, during that time, they've never stopped writing to each other.  Each month for the last few months, they've been sending Valentine's cards back and forth. Romeo always signs his cards with an X, and Juliet always signs hers with an O. The Riverbend warden is convinced that the two of them are planning something big and the clues to their schemes are contained in those cards.  Both sets of cards have been confiscated for further evaluation.  Can you find out what these two star-crossed lovers are planning?  Given what is known about them, it's probably a lot more than just kisses and hugs (XOXO).

Toadgeon ("Жабземелля") is a two-person card game. At the start, each player chooses a faction. One of you will lead the Heroes and their allies, fighting against the threats in the Toadgeon, while the other will control the Bosses and monsters that inhabit its depths.

During the game, players will explore the rooms of the Toadgeon, play character cards, use the traits and abilities of their creature cards, and make tactical moves to gain an advantage in combat!

Toadgeon consists of over 10 unique rooms, each with special abilities that impact the course of the game.

In the “Old wine cellar” your melee creatures will become even stronger, allowing you to use them more effectively in battles. But be cautious, as your opponent will also gain this bonus!

In the “Large throne room” every action is incredibly important, as this room counts as two! Plan your maneuvers carefully to defeat your opponent and reign over this magnificent place!

However, room abilities are not always helpful. Sometimes they limit players in their options. For example, in the Cramped hallway there won’t be enough space to play a Hero or Boss, so you can only use regular assistants or monsters.

Each faction has asymmetric traits and its own path to victory.

The Heroes succeed through teamwork and collaboration with allies, while the Monsters use tricks and sneaky maneuvers to foil their opponent's plans!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to a world where every word has great power...

At the beginning of the game, you choose one of the characters, each of which has a unique ability. These features will be a useful addition to your wit!

Your goal is to make words of different lengths from letter cards to get rid of cards from your deck and hand faster than your opponent. And yet, this is only part of the journey!

What makes the game special? Magic! Namely, mysterious magic cards that will help you on your way to victory. How do they work? We will tell you about this a little later, because the great Familiar has hidden this knowledge in the depths of the Library of Ancient Memoirs (but only until our next meeting)!

Words of Magic is a competitive game, but despite this, the atmosphere at the table will always be fun and friendly.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Battles of the American Civil War June 1861: July 1862 – A Solitaire Wargame
description:
The American Civil War was a conflict between the United States (the Union or the North) and the eleven states that had seceded from the Union and formed the Confederate States of America (the Confederacy or the South). It lasted from 1861 to 1865. One of the key issues fought over was slavery and the power of the federal government to prohibit it in certain territories. Abraham Lincoln, the first Republican president, promised to keep slavery out of the territories, which was seen as hostile by the South. At the time, the North had well-established industries, but in the South there was far more reliance on large-scale farming, which itself relied on enslaved people. War began in 1861 when Confederate forces opened fire on Fort Sumter in Charleston, and ended in 1865 when the Confederates surrendered.

In the game, the Player will be commanding various units, often representing many hundreds or thousands of men each, seeking to achieve a wide variety of Objectives. Battles from the years 1861 and 1862 are included in this game.

The aim of the game is to present a simple, clear, accessible, and quick to learn game, so that players can get into the action quickly. It does not try to replace more complex wargames which often take many hours to learn and to play. It also does not pretend to be entirely realistic, and many concepts have been simplified or abstracted for gameplay purposes – it is not a simulation. Each of the 20 Battle scenarios can be played in around 20 to 30 minutes, and each one should provide plenty of replayability due some random set up of the initial forces and depending on how the Battle plays out. Note that some Battles represent a specific part of the conflict within that Battle rather than the whole Battle.

This game is a solitaire wargame. You play the game, and the enemy is controlled by the game. You will need a good handful of standard six-sided dice to play (nine is ideal, although fewer will do).

It is highly recommended that the counters provided on the final page of the game are used to play this game. Simply stick them to card and cut them out. A video demonstrating how generally to do this can be found on the Mike Lambo Solitaire Book Games YouTube channel.

On the same channel you will find an instructional playthrough video for the game, which walks players through the rules of the game. The best way to learn the game is to read through the rules and then watch that video.

Players: 1
Time: 20 to 30 minutes per battle
Complexity: 2 / 5

—description from the designer

You spin a letter, roll the dice and come up with that number of words from a category at express speed.

For each completed category, you move one step forward on the playing field. But you have to hurry. How many steps can you take before your 30 seconds are up?​

So easy to say right - but in time it will be crazy, heated and super fun!

Each player in Subspace is leading a galactic civilization with up to three alien civilizations that are either allied or assimilated into your civilization. Across 6 rounds, players may expand their civilization by colonizing new sectors, managing their resources, improving their technology, incorporating new alien civilization, expanding their fleet power and performing various actions to gain Victory Points (VPs). At the end of the game, the player with the most VPs wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Landscape X: Der Tanz des Geheimbundes
description:
As you do every year, you are on vacation at Uncle Poldi's villa on the outskirts of Bad Ischl, right next to the imperial villa. While searching for a recipe for Kaiserschmarrn, you come across coded clues to a lost treasure of Empress Elisabeth in an old cookbook in his huge library. Your uncle, whose grandfather was a cook in the Emperor's service, asks you for help. This riddle becomes an adventure that takes you not only to the Vienna Opera Ball, but also to the highest heights of the Austrian Alps, and at the same time you learn exciting facts about the locations.

Will you succeed in unraveling the mystery of Sisi's treasure - and what is the secret lodge all about? Find out on this exciting puzzle

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – Pistols and Pearls: Parallel Investigator
description:
Pistols and Pearls is a print-and-play rules set for Arkham Horror: The Card Game that provides an alternate version of Jenny Barnes, previously released in The Dunwich Legacy Investigator Expansion.

In order to play this investigator, a copy of the Arkham Horror: The Card Game core set is required.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Game: Arkham Horror TCG – Challenge Scenarios & Parallel Investigators
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Test your soccer knowledge with this electronic trivia game for up 5 players. Just turn the game on and the virtual game master will handle everything for you.


     500 True or false questions
     5 Topics: World Cup, European Soccer, Us Soccer, Great Players and Fun Facts
     The virtual game master does it all. It gives you the rules, asks the questions and keeps track of the scores.
     Up to 5 players can play
     Two games modes 7+ or 14+. Choose the game mode based on the players.

Compete against your friends for nutrients through cutthroat drafts and push-your-luck competitions to find out! Mutate to steal your opponents genes, adapt to gain nutrients round to round, and evolve for a huge bonus by game end!

—description from the designer

No job, short on cash and a friend in need. You decide to help your old friend Ace who has sent you a disturbing letter. He has heard of weird creatures moving in the night. Ace asks for your assistance as he plans to investigate members of a secret cult he has discovered as the source of this evil. Just before arrival, you get the news that Ace is found shot and mutilated in an alley. You decide to finish what Ace has started by collecting clues about the case trying to catch who is behind Ace death.

A game for 2-4 investigators trying to identify who killed Ace as well as summoning unearthly beings to kill off all key witnesses. The game ends after 15 game rounds or earlier if the monsters manage to kill four of the key witnesses.

The investigators compete for clues, possibly even stealing from each other before presenting the evidence to the police or the local newspaper. The investigator accumulating the highest total score of clues collected at the end of round 15 wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: A Rogue on the High Seas
description:
Try to deduce who the Rogue is on your Pirate ship, and hunt them down to win the game! But be careful, because you could be the Rogue too...

Use powerful Pirate abilities and bluffing to try to find the Rogue before times up!

—description from the designer

This wonderful deck of cards allows you to play 3 different head to head games, and even includes many variants for both Common and Noble Galea!

The goal of Galea was to make a deck of cards and a group of games that felt as if they could be ancient. Rules seemingly unknowable until known, a feel of ancient tradition and play, ideally to the levels of chess or poker. Each game of Galea has variants to help with this feeling of it being evolved and changed as it moved place to place, continent to continent, and hopefully it succeeded.

—description from the designer

This short but sweet card game has you and your friends going head to head against one another in a zany all-out brawl!

The goal is to be the last person standing, the only player with cards remaining in their hand. Each turn you must play a card, activating a special and unique effect that will allow you to sabotage your opponents or bolster your own hand of cards.

—description from the designer

Who among your friends is the best idol group manager?

Well there is only one way to find out, make the Stars beat each other to death through the power of song, and whoever survives clearly had the better manager. Of course using your funds to help your idols do well is key, however if you go through your funds to fast, by drawing all the cards in your deck, you become bankrupt and lose the game. Spend now and win fast? Or perhaps playing the long game here is best? Maybe you should you titer on the edge of bankruptcy to make your Stars go into an Adrenaline Rush?

The decision is up to you manager.

—description from the designer

Who among your friends is the best idol group manager?

Well there is only one way to find out, make the Stars beat each other to death through the power of song, and whoever survives clearly had the better manager. Of course using your funds to help your idols do well is key, however if you go through your funds to fast, by drawing all the cards in your deck, you become bankrupt and lose the game. Spend now and win fast? Or perhaps playing the long game here is best? Maybe you should you titer on the edge of bankruptcy to make your Stars go into an Adrenaline Rush?

The decision is up to you manager.

—description from the designer

Who among you and your friends is most sinful?

Well when playing Toward The Top you'll be competing to see who can ascend past Heaven, while trying to avoid falling to Hell. You will be constantly shifting between all knowing Devils, cooperative Humans, and silent Angels, working toward the pearly gates.

Disclaimer: This game is definitely less potent than my other works, I would heavily suggest you check out the free Print and Play document, the game rules blog, or the YouTube video before purchasing.

—description from the designer

Who among you and your friends cook up the best Warlock's Brew?

Compete against your friends for ordinary and extraordinary items of value through snappy secret auctions. Once you gain 3 of the same item, you win the game with your fantastic brew made of Horns, Tentacles, and oh so much more!

—description from the designer

Who among you and your friends is the best at manufacturing colors with cute little animal scientists working by your side?

Well, by playing Color Factory, you can find out!

Hire different animals to work in your factory so that they can produce a variety of different colors, each with different values from game to game! Then by the end of 3 lightning fast rounds, you'll find whose best at managing their own Color Factory!

—description from the designer

“Court officials and members of the jury, it is my deepest pleasure and honor to officially announce that all participants in this trial have unanimously agreed to settle this case in the honorable tradition of ‘Trial By Ball.’“

Only the best of the best can become the King of this Court!

Assemble a team of Wizards, Witches, and other fantastical beings, so they can compete against your friends in an epic round of TRIAL BY BALL! First to 10 more points than any other opponent, as usual. GO TEAM!

—description form the designer

Three modular expansions for Villagers:

"Scoundrels" adds 9 new villagers.

"Saints" adds 5 new villagers.

"Profiteers" adds 8 new villagers and a new scoring mechanism.

This promo deck is the same as Villagers: Kickstarter Expansion Pack but without the fourth module and no wooden tokens

Four players embody roles in a mystery crime dinner to solve puzzles, find the culprit among themselves and solve the murder.

Du und drei Freunde schlüpft für einen Abend in die Rollen der vier Charaktere, löst gemeinsam die kniffligen Rätsel, findet den Täter unter euch, klärt den Mord auf und verbringt einen spannenden und lustigen Abend mit „Sport ist Mord".

You’re one of a bunch of snakes from a once-thriving society on a dusty planet that humans used to call home. Now, with the humans packing up and heading back to Earth, only one lucky snake gets to hitch a ride. The snake that whips up the snazziest astronaut suit wins the ticket, while the rest get to enjoy the dusty life.

Gameplay Overview

In Snake-o-Nauts, you'll construct your astronaut suit using equipment cards arranged into three distinct piles. These piles not only represent the progression of your suit's development but also provide a clear, visual snapshot of each player's status at any moment in the game.

Our game is designed with a strong focus on welcoming new players. The intuitive, color-coded system makes it easy for everyone to see exactly where they stand, fostering a fun and inclusive atmosphere as you compete to build the ultimate astronaut suit.

Along the way, you'll have plenty of opportunities to be tactical, making strategic decisions that influence your progress and create a dynamic, engaging experience for players of all skill levels.

Geopolitix is a strategy game in the universe of 21st century geopolitics.
