Like may Euro expert games, it is based on vaious mechanics such as majority, ressouce management, territory control and engine building, but it also includes direct interactions beween players, through collaboration or competition.
Each player will play as a sovereign state of the 21st century, and they'll develop their influence around the world, their economic power, their soft power and hard power, in order to fight for their place in he international affairds and evnetually raise themselves to the prized spot of first world superpower. 
The theme is organically present in all game components and mechanics, transporting you in the pragmatic world of geopolitics where you'll be able to write a different international History in each game.

—description from the designer

The sorcerer’s apprentice Alphexi is supposed to make a tasty alphabet soup. No problem! But while the soup is being served, the head sorcerer pays very close attention to whether the letters are floating in the correct order in the bowl. Who will help Alphexi arrange the letters and then find words in different categories?

Welcome to simple earth, with its magic colour-changing board, now yours to print, build and enjoy... or even sell!

As sea levels rise and fall, and mass extinction looms, bugs mutate and fight. Will yours grow big and risk being wiped out? Or stay small and try to evade their opponents? The choices are yours, but life on simple earth is rarely predictable, so even a novice will occasionally triumph. With up to four players and an increasingly crowded planet, the aim is to make all other bugs extinct. However, extinct players can still affect the outcome, and even score tournament points, by playing on as ‘ghosts’!

Perhaps best described as a sort of ‘gambler’s chess’, simple earth is a mixture of positional strategy and dice-based risk taking. The changing sea level adds a further element of chance, and further choices, as smaller spaces magically change colour, connecting some, whilst isolating others (spoiler alert – it’s not really magic!)

Rooted firmly in evolution and designed, in part, to challenge the conventions of traditional strategy games (hierarchy, black vs. white, etc.), simple earth hopes to foster an appreciation of diversity, if not for its own sake, then at least as a way of mitigating risk.

—description from the designer

A Kickstarter exclusive mini expansion for La Fleur.

Contains 12 flower cards, 2 visitor cards, and 5 event cards.

boardgame: La Fleur: J'adore mon Amaryllis Mini Expansion
description:
Every garden party needs a musician or two to create the perfect atmosphere.

Crimson Amaryllis symbolize love, strength, beauty and determination.

A mini expansion for La Fleur that contains:
5 artisan cards and 10 flower cards

Bottom Out - The ultimate card game that sparks laughter and friendly competition! Perfect for game nights with friends or family gatherings, this game will keep you on your toes as you try to avoid being the player with the lowest card - because in this game, nobody wants to "Bottom Out".

Objective - The goal is simple: Don't Bottom Out! Avoid having the lowest card to keep your game chips. The last player standing with chips wins the round!

Easy Setup: Each player starts with 3 chips of their favorite color. Choose your dealer and get started!

Dynamic Gameplay: Players decide whether to keep or pass their card, with special cards introducing strategic twists that can change the course of the game.

How to Play:

     The dealer hands out one face-down card to each player. Keep it secret!
     The first player next to the dealer begins by deciding to keep or pass their card.
     Special card actions add excitement and unpredictability, creating endless variations in gameplay.
     After revealing cards, players with the lowest card "Bottom Out" and lose a game chip. The hand concludes, and a new dealer takes over for the next round. 


Winning the Game: Stay in the game until you are the last player with chips! The first player to win 2 rounds is crowned the champion of Bottom Out!

—description from the publisher

History books are full of great women and men who have left their mark on humanity, from automobiles to antibiotics. Heroes of the World board game showcases important figures and their work, while the players collect points by recognizing them and answering correctly to fascinating questions.

Are YOU the ultimate Baseball Fanatic?

Be the first player or team to get to 15 points.

individual play, you are asked a question, if correct, you get the card, and 1 point.

Team play, one player on asks a question of the other team, being careful not to show anyone on their team the answer.  If they answer correctly, they get the card and 1 point.  if incorrect, your team can try to answer correctly and if correct, they push the other team back one point.  IF at zero, you stay at zero.

100 Horror Themed trivia cards.  Everythign from questions about Holloween, horror writers, and phobias.

Ask the question and the player who answers correctly first gets the card as a point. There are also 3 variants on some cards.

Player with the most cards at the end is the winner.

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Silk Hero Pack
description:
After being bitten by the same radioactive spider as Peter Parker and gaining similar spider powers, Cindy Moon spent years of her life isolated in a bunker to protect the Earth from the ravenous spider-hunters known as the Inheritors. Finally freed from that bunker, Cindy now fights crime on the streets of New York as both an investigative journalist and the web-slinging superhero, Silk, and she’s swinging into your games of Marvel Champions: The Card Game! With her enhanced senses and quick reflexes letting her learn her enemies’ moves on the fly, Silk is a versatile hero who can adapt to any situation. Within this hero pack, you will find Silk, her fifteen signature cards, and a full assortment of Protection cards to aid you in keeping your allies safe. This pack also includes a bonus modular encounter set featuring Atlas of the Thunderbolts!

Anyone looking to take on the agents of A.I.M. will want to add the Silk Hero Pack to their collection! This expansion comes with a 40-card pre-built deck, giving you the chance to start playing from the moment you open the box.

Not a standalone expansion. A copy of the Marvel Champions: The Card Game core set is required to play.
family: Game: Marvel Champions – The Card Game

description:
Tabletop Smack Darts is a fun and active twist on traditional darts, but instead of throwing sharp projectiles, you’re tossing suction-cup darts at tiles on the table. Think of it as a mix of skill, strategy, and a little bit of chaos, as you aim for tiles with different point values while trying to outscore your opponents.

The game is quick to set up and easy to play—no need for a dartboard or any complicated setup. You just lay out the scoring tiles, and everyone takes turns launching their darts at them. The goal is to land your darts on the highest-scoring tiles, but you’ve got to keep your eyes on your opponents because they can steal your spot or outscore you with their own throws.

Includes 6 smack darts, 2 scoring darts, 8 Target Tiles, 2 Scoring Tiles.

With its simple mechanics and the thrill of launching darts at the table, Tabletop Smack Darts delivers light-hearted, competitive fun for groups, whether you're at a family game night or hanging out with friends. Just make sure to watch out for the occasional misfire—those suction darts have a mind of their own!

The goal of Bad Apples is to be the player with the most points after ten rounds. Each player starts with a set of apples and bidding tokens. The numbers on the apples represent the point values. Each round, every player discretely puts one of their apples into the burlap sack. These apples will be collectively auctioned off to the highest bidder.

Moving clockwise, players either raise the bid using their bidding tokens or pass. Every time a player passes, that player receives a small amount of bidding tokens as a consolation. Next, an apple is withdrawn from the burlap sack for everyone to see. The remaining players now have more insight to determine the
potential point total of all the submitted apples. The last player remaining wins the auction and collects all the apples.

—description from the designer

An educational game to teach children how to read analog clocks.

The leader holds up a yellow card displaying time with numerals (as a digital clock would).  The players then add clock hands to an analog clock face.  Thew first player to answer correctly can mark that time off a bingo-style grid of times.  The first player to complete a row or column wins.

A game of memorization and strategy. Each player starts with 4 facedown cards. Throughout the game they discover what their cards are, sabotage their enemies, and yell VEX! When they believe their cards add up to the lowest score

The VOC Treasure is an escape game. This is the story:

Bram van Amstel, your eccentric distant uncle and a successful treasure hunter, recently passed away. None of his heirs received a traditional inheritance; instead, each was given a mysterious chest filled with clues. In a letter, Bram explained that he had discovered the locations of several treasures during his final years. For you, he left one VOC chest containing instructions to find the treasure. Will you take on the challenge? Step into the shoes of a treasure hunter and embark on the adventure.

The game is based on a true story about a ship from the Dutch East India Company. This ship sank near one of the thousands of East Indies islands.

—description from the designer

In your hands you have an investigative case that you can play alone or with friends. It takes little space, a bit of time and a lot of ingenuity. But remember, one case leads to another!

Mini Crimes are boxes to carry with you in every situation: the box itself contains the rules of the game, and along with the deck of cards contained in it, it will be enough to get all the clues necessary to the resolution!

The Mini Crimes Special are new mysterious cases created especially for occasions and festivities.

For a powerful TV mogul, a day of spearfishing off the coast of Miami turns into a tragedy! Everything points to an accident, but will it really be so?

Mini Crimes - Deadly Summer is a new special case perfect to take with you on vacation during the summer season!

Dice Dunkers is a solitaire tabletop game simulating a basketball match, emphasizing key moments and realistic results.

A solo game designed for one player, ages 12+, with a playtime of 30-45 minutes.
Play a simulated basketball match in about 30 minutes using team cards and dice to simulate actions and scores. The game does not simulate every shot but recreates the impact of team and individual players.
The game consists of 16 rounds, each simulating 3 minutes of the match.
The game allows you to play with either fantasy teams or real teams from existing leagues.
Each round involves three dice rolls: one to check for substitutions and designate key players, and two to determine team events and points scored by the teams.
The game also offers optional rules, such as Gameday, tactics, and Dunkomat cards for playing against AI. A detail mode is available for resolving the final rounds

—description from the designer

Goat Simulator is a fast-paced party card game where you draw goats, play goats, and battle other goats. Gear up your goats, sabotage your so-called “friends”, and unleash chaos so marvelous it’ll make even the most stoic goat bleat. Who needs friends anyway, when you have goats?

Players draw and discard cards to equip their goats adding gear effects and points to their score.  They also play Karma cards for extra powerful effects and earn bonus points if they finish a personal mission. The game ends when a player makes it to 10 Chaos points.

Rules for a simpler kids version are included.

—description from the publisher

Wickens is a fast-paced deckbuilding battle between 2 to 4 players, full of skill and strategy where the turn of a card can change the game.

Take the role of a Wicken in a battle across universes. Portals open. The Wickens arrive. The open portals attract demons, spirits, angels & creatures from each Realm.

You draw abilities from the creatures you kill. Can you learn to harness their power to kill your opponent before they kill you?

Build a unique deck of abilities from the creatures you kill. Play with a different Wicken deck each game and build your deck turn-by-turn, creating powerful combos and ensuring a different strategy and game each time.

Will you be the last Wicken standing?

—description from the publisher

Match it up! Steal it! Strategy Puzzle Game Capture the sparkling stones (jewels). Simple but deep. This is a "ready-to-go game" that can be easily played by anyone, for friends, lovers, and families, from children to adults.

Game points: Easy to rule. It has fun strategies that lead you to victory with fewer hands! You will want to play over and over again because you will be able to win your game in a short time.

Game Rules

     Players alternate to select one of their jewels and set them in the tray.
     When the tray is filled with jewels, you can get that tray.
     When both players have no place to set their jewels, the game is ended. Players who have gotten lots of jewels wins!

Welcome to AmaZoom, the social network dedicated to photos of incredible Amazonian flora and fauna! Grab your camera, because the next contest is about to begin! Capture striking images, and become the most popular photographer on the platform by receiving the most likes on your montage.


     Take photos: choose one or more cards from the forest (a line of 7 cards), paying the value of the animal depicted;
     Post your photos: add photos to your montage (put cards partially covering each other), strategically arranging animals to meet their card’s requirements and earn more likes.
     Score big: boost your engagement by meeting optional requirements and grouping identical animals to score bonus likes and boost your popularity.


Plan each turn carefully, organize your photos to maximize likes when they’re posted, and explore different combinations and strategies to stand out in the contest by creating an unforgettable montage.

In a world gripped by relentless winter, Frost Shelter challenges you to lead your colony with limited resources and to compete with other leaders for critical supplies.

Make tough decisions to overcome devastating events and keep your citizens alive. Combine luck and strategy in intense rounds, choosing between protecting your colony, stockpiling resources, or constructing crucial equipment.  Only the most resilient leader will survive!


     Prepare for events: reveal the round’s challenge card to discover which resources are essential for protecting your city.
     Compete for resources: play the highest card (tricks) to collect the most valuable supplies, but plan carefully to avoid setbacks.
     Ensure your long-term survival: use resources to tackle the revealed events and build equipment to increase your chances of victory.


Survive the extreme cold and prove that your colony is the best prepared to endure this harsh new reality.

In Pool Party Stars, the players are hosting a grand Pool Party, and competing with each other to attract the best guests!

But there’s a twist: each host has a secret rule that keeps certain types of guests from getting in.

Uncover the secret rules of the other players while trying to get your invitees past the gate without being turned away!

In Gruntz, you'll command units like infantry, tanks, and aircraft with rock-paper-scissors-like relationships. For example, Gruntz are slow-moving and can't defeat all enemy units, but they're the only ones who can capture the control points at the middle of the map. Planes wreak havoc on the battlefield, but carefully placed Anti-Aircraft stop them in their tracks.

When you deploy tanks and trucks, you'll outfit them with different Magnetic attachments! Choose a cannon for extra firepower, or a cargo hold to transport troops. This flexibility creates awesome counterplay and tactical decision-making.

Capture the zones in the middle of the board to gain points. First to three points wins!

—description from the publisher

description:
ROVE’s crossrouting protocols have improved navigation efficiency by 50%, opening routes to new scientific discoveries and previously hidden treasures!

Sunken Secrets 6-card Mission Pack expansion for Aqua ROVE challenges players to dive deeper in search of exciting new underwater discoveries. Use the new synergistic movement cards to move in 2 ways at once. For a greater challenge, activate Endurance Mode and push yourself to complete 12 full missions!

Shuffle the 6 expansion cards into the starting deck, then set up and play as normal. Each of the new cards features dual movement, allowing the player to use 2 different movement types (but with limited directions).

All Mission Packs also include Endurance Mode. When playing in this mode, set up with a hand of 5 cards. Complete up to 12 Missions, rather than the standard 7, with higher ranking for completing each additional mission.

In The Stones of Nuria, up to 5 players take on the role of aspiring heroes. Another player can (but does not have to) take on the role of the Dark Master to put obstacles in the way of the group of heroes and take command of the hosts of evil. In each game, the heroes delve further into the forgotten history of the kingdom of Nuria, uncover the secrets of the Dark Master and confront powerful enemies.

With each fallen foe, the group of heroes gains a strength that allows them to resist evil and even avert imminent death. The Dark Master thirsts for the heroes' life force. Every time a servant manages to land a hit, he steals this powerful force in the form of bloodstones. Only this power enables him to cast the most powerful spells or draw fallen heroes to his side.

The Stones of Nuria is a fantasy adventure game that is characterized in particular by the following aspects:

     Quick to play, simple set-up and catchy rules without many special cases
     Colored areas show at a glance who can see and attack whom.
     Defeated heroes are not eliminated - all players start and end the game together
     In addition to the cooperative game, there is also a varied solo mode and the one-against-all game


And of course there are numerous treasures to be found in the vaults - valuable, legendary, dangerous, unusual and unexpected.

—description from the publisher

Step into the enigmatic world of La Quintrala, a board game that immerses you in an atmosphere of mystery and strategy. Inspired by ancient legends and folklore, each game is a journey filled with surprises, where your choices open unexpected paths and lead you to uncover hidden secrets. With carefully crafted mechanics and a unique setting, La Quintrala challenges your wit and dares you to uncover the truth behind every move. Are you ready to face the unknown and be captivated by a universe where the unexpected is the only certainty?

How the Game Works
La Quintrala is played on a board with various tiles and tokens representing different elements of the story. Players move their pieces strategically, collecting key items and solving puzzles to advance. Along the way, obstacles and challenges test their ability to plan ahead and adapt to new scenarios.

The goal is to gather the necessary elements to break La Quintrala’s sinister influence. Once a player assembles the required pieces, they confront La Quintrala and banish her darkness, securing victory and restoring balance to the game world.

—description from the designer

Adéntrate en el enigmático mundo de La Quintrala, un juego de mesa que te sumerge en una atmósfera cargada de misterio y estrategia. Con mecánicas cuidadosamente diseñadas y una ambientación única, La Quintrala desafía tu ingenio y te reta a descubrir la verdad tras cada movimiento. ¿Estás listo para enfrentar lo desconocido y dejarte cautivar por un universo donde lo inesperado es la única constante?

Gallows is a supplementary ruleset for Trench Crusade to be used with the core rule book. 
If you ever wanted to play Blackadder goes forth while being confronted with the realities of trench warfare, this is for you.

Build a standard Trench Crusade warband for 700 ducats. 
Then each turn receive orders from High Command which you will need to follow. 
Your goal as a unit is to survive the battle (often in spite of High Command's orders) with victory points scored for each soldier who survives.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Trivial Pursuit: Gilmore Girls – Quick Play
description:
Test your knowledge of the charming world of Stars Hollow with TRIVIAL PURSUIT: Gilmore Girls Quick Play edition!

Choose a question from six categories. Answer two questions in a row from the same category correctly to win a card. The first player to collect six cards is declared the Gilmore Girls Guru and wins!

100 Question Cards with 6 Categories:

     The Gilmore Family
     The Girls
     Dating 101
     All Around Town
     Do you want coffee with your Coffee
     Potluck

In Ghetto! Rated G each player begins play with no money or financial means, in other words that he starts in the "ghetto". The goal of the game is for each player to raise himself out of the ghetto, by buying and operating businesses and maximizing his financial worth. The richest player at the end of the game wins.

A basic monopoly type roll spin and move game set in New York City.

Taïki is a family-friendly must-follow trick-taking game.
At the beginning of a round, you get an equal number of Sun and Moon cards (depending on player count) then an additional card at random.
Each round you try to get as many tricks in the Sun suit as in the Moon suit. Achieving that gives you two Balance tokens per pair of tricks. Ending a round with an unbalanced number of tricks in each suit results in you discarding as many Balance tokens as the difference between your number of tricks in each suit.
After three rounds, the player with the most Balance tokens wins.

Some special cards can be played as either suit. Some others can be either the highest or the lowest of its suit.

boardgame: Catchables: Cat Wearing A Cat & Catcus
description:
Catchables names both a set of collectible squishy foam toys and a game played with these toys.

To play, each player starts with their own Catchable and an action card. On a turn, a player throws their Catchable in the air, then performs the stunt on their action card. If they succeed, they place two additional action cards in front of themselves or other players, stacking additional challenges for a more challenging next turn; if they fail, they discard one or more of their cards.

The first player to complete a catch while performing at least five action cards wins.

This set contains two new cats (Cat Wearing A Cat & Catcus), as well as 24 action cards.

Catchables names both a set of collectible squishy foam toys and a game played with these toys.

To play, each player starts with their own Catchable and an action card. On a turn, a player throws their Catchable in the air, then performs the stunt on their action card. If they succeed, they place two additional action cards in front of themselves or other players, stacking additional challenges for a more challenging next turn; if they fail, they discard one or more of their cards.

The first player to complete a catch while performing at least five action cards wins.

This set contains two new cats (Donut Cat & Lumber Cat), as well as 24 new action cards.

Based on the Pizza Cake Webcomic, players are cats, drawing 4 CAT CARDS per round and play them to annoy the human! You take turns playing as many cat cards with a matching mischief mark under the human cards that you want. Whoever has the highest cat card score wins that human card. The first player to win 5 human cards first wins (gets food).

This is a Quick-Moving game using trick-taking and press-your-luck mechanics. It always plays out differently and is fun to play multiple games in a row.

Expansion for Strong Point: A Trivia Game. Strong Point has 200 category cards, each with 5 questions to answer. Predict what how many other players will get correct to score bonus 'strong points'.

This expansion provides 100 extra category cards with 500 new questions and 2 additional sets of prediction counters to expand the base game to up to 8 players or teams.

—description from the publisher

Impellere (from the Latin word meaning "to push") is an abstract game where every move shifts the balance of power. Players take turns pushing rows or columns, shifting discs and displacing opponents in a dynamic battle of strategy. With each push, a new opportunity or peril emerges. Be the first to align four of your color orthogonality and claim victory!

Goal of the game: To be the first player who can make an orthogonality 4 in a row.

The rule book can to be printed in order to play, there is 1 optional board to print, also the player needs to provide the following components or similar to play:

- 27 Discs (9 Red, 9 Black, 9 White)

Disclaimer: the game can receive updates\small adjustments in the future following feedback from the players.

This is the game #09 of the PAC Series.
The PAC Series has the goal to deliver simple, yet engaging games with straightforward mechanics, offering a fun and challenging experience for fans of abstract games.

—description from the designer

In True Command you are in charge of the 3-6 brigades or regiments working for you, each with their own commander. Good commanders bring flexibility to battles, allowing their unit to go toe to toe with larger or stronger units. You also direct your scouts/recon to fight for information, cover your flank, or capture objectives on their own. Clever use of scouts will allow you to figure out the enemies objectives and order of battle before they understand yours, giving you a clear advantage on the battlefield.

Each scenario begins with both sides randomly being assigned their mission. Easy missions mean a smaller Order of Battle, while tough missions plus-up your forces. Then you choose your Implied Task, an additional way of earning victory. A key part of each game is trying to find out what you think the other commanders mission and implied task is, although sometimes it cannot be determined until the game ends.

Each turn is conducted by randomly pulling actions from a bag, with half being for you and the other half your opponent, while adding 3 random turn shortening actions as well. You will never know when the turn will end. Finally, both sides have divisional staff officers to assist you. You draw them randomly from your staff bag, hoping to get the critical assistance that you need, hoping to avoid drawing the dreaded "friction" markers, which waste that action instead. Depending on the scenario, one side may have a much better staff than the other (much less friction that is), but since it is random, a poor staff can still occasionally pull off miracles.

One key aspect of the staff is that you can store staff actions under in a "plan", waiting for the Plans Officer to allow you to unleash your plan. This will let you take a serious of actions in a row, potentially dramatically changing the battlefield's geometry and combat.

-description from designer

In this roll-and-write game, players roll the dice and choose three to place on their ten-by-ten grid. Placing of the dice is done based off of the shape of the pips shown on the dice. For example, if a player rolls a 3, they'd mark 3 of their squares with a 3 in a diagonal, representing the way the pips are represented on the die. A roll of a 6 would be placed with six 6s in the form of a boxcar.

The player then passes one of their 4 dice to the next player who must play that die on their board.

When a row is filled, the player scores the highest number in that row. The points are doubled if they're the first player to fill that row.

If a die cannot be placed, it goes into a slot for unplaced dice.

When one player fills their unplaced dice slots or all players fail to place a die during a round, the game ends and scores are tallied.

—description from the designer

In Pass the Pips, players roll four dice and keep two while passing two on to the following player(s).

The objective is to score as many points on your scorecard before you are unable to place dice and must fill your overflow.

When the overflow is filled, the game ends and scores are tallied.

—description from the designer

The Combo Master expansion brings another layer of strategic depth by introducing Action Rewards; a new type of sequence that after completion gives a player bonus action to use during their turn.

With Combo Master you can stack up your actions and unleash them later in the game to perform a mega combo!
A promotional, stripped-down version of Déblok (Match Madness) with minimal components. Part of a set of mini Asmodée games.
Kickstarter exclusive scenario that uses the dice from Peach Festival and components from Tang Garden to play an all new Roll & Write Scenario for Tang Garden!

Let's face it - shit happens to all of us, right? So we may as well laugh about it.

You're going to read some really messed up shit on these cards, and then rank them based on how bad they really are. Is a diaper rash BETTER or WORSE than your kid refusing to brush their teeth? Rank the card, and if our Misery Index agrees with you, you get to keep it. Correctly rank 10 cards and you win. These painfully true cards can be played on their own. So why are you still reading this box? Buy the game and get to playing!

—description from the back of the box

description:
BeFree is an immersive and educational board game that takes players on an investment journey toward financial freedom. Designed for families, teens, and groups, BeFree offers a dynamic experience where players learn how to manage cash flow, make strategic investments, and build a robust financial portfolio. The game provides real-world financial scenarios, allowing players to explore asset acquisition, manage loans, and navigate market changes with a fun and strategic approach.

How to Play:

The game is suitable for 2 to 6 players aged 14 and up. Each player starts with $100,000 in virtual cash.
Players take turns rolling the die, moving their tokens around the pentagon-shaped board, and performing the actions indicated by the space they land on.
The board features various financial scenarios, including investment opportunities, wealth skill challenges, expenses with bonuses, crisis management, and spending situations.
Players draw cards from six different categories, including "Investment," "Loan," "Wealth Skills," "Spending," "Expense with Bonus," and "Years" cards, each offering unique financial challenges and opportunities.
At the end of each full cycle (five years in the game), players can sell assets, repay loans, and assess their progress towards financial independence.
How to Win:
To achieve victory in BeFree, players must accomplish one of two main financial objectives:

Generate a stable annual passive income of $120,000.
Accumulate a total net worth of $2,500,000, considering cash, assets, and any outstanding loans.
Players win by building a strong financial foundation, mastering investment strategies, and effectively managing risks. The first player to meet either of the financial independence goals is declared the winner, with an opportunity to read the congratulatory "Winner" card aloud.

—description from the designer

A long, long time ago, humanity decided that it wanted to reach heaven. So they teamed up to build the tallest tower ever created, one that would rise above the clouds.

At first, everything was going well: brick by brick, stone by stone, the tower grew towards the stars. But one day God realized. “This is going to look horrible in my garden,” he thought, and quickly came up with a solution.

While one shouted: "Pass me the hammer!", his partner understood: "Make me a milkshake!" Another asked, "I need this to be paved," but they brought him a partner. God had changed all languages ​​and the poor builder, who was trying to explain the plan with jumps and strange faces, was fired. Thus, amidst confusion and disorder, the tower staggered, and boom!, it fell to the ground.

But if humanity is anything, it is hard-headed. A new construction team has arrived and plans to rebuild the world's tallest tower. How far will they go? Not even God knows.

In group, achieve the highest score before making 3 mistakes. La Torre de Babel'' is a game with cooperative and competitive modes.

In each round, each player with their own “language” must communicate a concept, character or place to the Builder (guesser).

It is Royal Greeting Day. Nobles from all over the continent gather to perform a ceremony where they greet and celebrate each other. However, the entire servants are on strike, so both lords and ladies will have no choice but to perform the ceremony on their own. Left at the mercy of their memory and their manners, there will be no commoner to rescue them. Any mistake could cost them the crown. Will the powerful be able to celebrate without anyone being offended?

The player in turn must pass the Crown card to another player of their choice, saying "Greetings to" and saying their name and adding a title (for example: Greetings to Roque, Lord of the Games).

Every time they greet a player they have to name all the titles that player has received and add a new one.

When someone makes a mistake, the round is over and that player will start the next round.

boardgame: Pinball Builder: Tower of the Wizard
description:
Pinball Builder allows you to construct your own Pinball table! This base set includes nine Standard Part cards and 6 Theme Part cards. As new sets are released you’ll be able to swap cards (theme and/or standard part cards) to create unique gameplay challenges for you and your friends.

Core Concepts

When you play Pinball Builder you will face unique combinations of Part cards that create the pinball table. You’ll roll your dice to activate the Part cards. Each Part card has a variety of icon effects.

The choice is yours as to which icons to activate to score points, earn multipliers and other rewards and finally attempt the Final Stage.

Object of the Game

The goal of the game, like real Pinball, is to achieve the high score. A game of Pinball Builder ends when either you lose your last ball in play or you complete one of the Final Stage effects. Use the included High Score sheet to track your progress at being the best on this pinball table!

This first expansion to Pinball Builder adds all new parts to your pinball table.

Now you can build and play a full Desperate Sun western-themed pinball table. Also included in this expansion are new base parts—The Wheel and The Rocket.

boardgame: Tiny Tina's Wonderlands: The Chaos Chamber
description:
Tiny Tina's Wonderlands: The Chaos Chamber is a trick-taking game that features characters from Tiny Tina's Wonderlands franchise such as Paladin Mike, The Dragon Lord, and Wastard, alongside classes that include Spore Warden and Brr-Zerker.

To play, each player chooses a class to start, then enters the Chaos Chamber. Using a strategic trick-taking combat system, players can blast, slash, and cast their way through the hordes of enemies like Skeleton Warriors, Trolls, Goblins, and other fearsome foes like the Banshee, Dry'l, Ribula, and more to gain legendary loot and victory points.

boardgame: Iraqi Freedom: Thirty Days to Baghdad
description:
Iraqi Freedom: Thirty Days to Baghdad simulates the 2003 Iraq War as a military conflict, and also as a systematic hunt for Iraq’s High Value Targets, otherwise known as Iraq’s Most Wanted, during Operation Iraqi Freedom. The game simulates the entire war and includes two mounted maps of Iraq as it existed in 2003, including accurate topography (such as the alteration to the Mesopotamian wetlands, drained by Saddam Hussein after the end of the Gulf War), and extending northward to encompass Kurdish territory, as well. The game includes an entirely new set of game pieces representing all of the belligerents, including insurgent units such as the Fedayeen, Ansar al Islam, Al Quds, and Ba’athists (Saddam party loyalists). In addition, there are more air unit types, including the B-2 stealth bomber, F-14, Royal Navy Harriers, etc.

Other new game pieces include smart bombs (such as the GBU-12 and GBU-31), Iraqi medium range missiles, a Patriot battery, plus numerous elite units (e.g., British commandos, U.S. Army Rangers, and of course Special Forces, Marines, and so on). The game even includes the optional U.S. 4th Infantry Division that had been scheduled to invade Iraq from Turkey (historically diverted after Turkey’s refusal to permit staging from Turkish territory). The rules include a standard scenario, a variant scenario, and a campaign game that links Desert Storm to Iraqi Freedom.

Better yet, this game includes a bonus Desert Storm supplement that includes additional game pieces, a historical scenario (with a historic 1991 set-up), plus an entire Event Cards effects system. That is to say, the Desert Storm event cards don’t merely move suit markers upon the suit track; rather, now each event has a specific game-play effect that *may* occur during game play (two possible events per each card among the two decks), multiplying the operational possibilities of the game (completing the innovation of the Desert Storm game as it was always meant to be). Moreover, the Desert Storm supplement booklet includes a page of clarifications and errata to complete Desert Storm. Highly researched and accurate, this game is a complete presentation of the Iraq war militarily, technologically, politically, as well as ecologically.

—description from the designer

description:
Mario Hugo it’s out for work and his dogs will not waste any time barking and howling for his return. Instead, they will mount the greatest party, so they can play, sing, bark and have fun in all the possible ways a dog enjoy. But beware, if Mario Hugo returns and the house it’s not in order, there will be trouble!

Dogs Alone is a set collection card game where you must try to do the best disasters collecting certain dog cards in the game while keeping every disaster in order at the end of the game so Mario Hugo won’t notice anything.

In your turn you can recruit a dog from the market or the pile to your hand and make a disaster if you can; or clean a disaster in your game zone. The dogs than you collect will be the principal way to commite a certain disaster card, meeting their requirements. Meanwhile the disaster card will be the principal way to get points to win the game. But beware, because the only way to get that points, is cleaning the mess before Mario Hugo's cards came in play for third time.

Game based on the popular Chilean TV Show called 31 Minutos.

Chnages in Second Edition:

     Improved and simpler rules
     They removed multicolor cads

Warmaster Revolution (WMR) is a fan-based 2nd edition of rules for the Warmaster game. Warmaster stopped being supported by Games Workshop (GW) in 2012, yet there are still many players around the world who love this game and are playing it regularly.

WMR first started as a Czech community-based project but has since evolved into a project supported by large parts of the international community. The Warmaster Rules Committee (WMRC), consisting of a number of players from around the world, was established to discuss rules issues and potential changes to the army lists.

The main aim of the WMR rule set is to implement the combat system from Warmaster Ancients – a second generation of the Warmaster rules system. In brief, this means a limited amount of combat rounds within one Combat Phase, among many other small fixes and enhancements. WMR also features a set of optional rules that focus on adding more dynamics to the game – (e.g. allowing units to move after failed order). Finally, a number of minor changes have also been introduced into the existing army lists.

We believe that WMR offers more dynamic, smooth and “realistic” games than the original version. But most importantly it shows, that though not supported by GW any more, the game still lives on.

National Park Adventure Game is a multi-player board game.  Collect cards as you progress around the board answering questions about our National Parks.  There is a more advanced play option.

There are Park Passes Cards, Keeper Cards, die and a game board with spaces along intersecting paths that form a square path with an X-shaped path in the middle and optional side paths.  Spaces have specific actions like lose a turn, draw a Keeper Card, etc.

Four Park Pass cards can be exchanged for one Keeper Card.  The 'spent' Park Pass cards are placed into play on the board Corner Spaces.  Players can collect these cards if they land on the corner with the card(s).  The commands on these 'spent' cards are not applicable again, as they have been spent but they can be used to purchase a Keeper Card.

To win, collect 4 on one type of Keeper Card OR collect one of each type of Keeper Card AND then land exactly on a Corner Space to access the X path to the center of the board.  Reach the center of the board to win.

Cards, both Passes and Keepers may be stolen.  If you land on an occupied space, you can steal a card from that player.  You may not steal from a player on a Corner Space.

There are alternate rules for the advanced play.

boardgame: Trick Tac Toe: A Cat's Game
description:
Trick-taking and area control collide in Trick Tac Toe: A Cat's Game. The goal of this two player competitive game is to mark your territory by placing three control markers in a straight line.

After each trick resolves, whomever played the strongest card then places a control marker on a contested location. Whomever, played the weakest, will activate the ability of the location itself.

More kobolds have arrived to be adopted by the dragons!

In this first expansion pack to Too Many Kobolds, there are over 20 new kobolds to recruit and adopt, including kobold babies and grumpy kobolds. There are also new Dragon Powers and tokens to better keep track of the various bonuses.

Have you left your travel plans until the last minute? No problem. Travel Bug: Abroad's modular Ultimate Holiday Planner will see you on your next Holiday Getaway before you know it! With only a few days left, you must hurry, working individually or in pairs, to successfully use the brochures provided by the travel agents to be the first player or team to get the greatest holiday deals for your best holiday ever!

Play cards from your hand to match the destinations and activities or move pesky rivals out of your way using a travel pass to create an awesome itinerary while avoiding the dreaded double booking! To win you must place exactly 5 tokens in a row, column or diagonal! There are also 5-star Hotels that are wild locations taking any destination or activity played, keeping them safe from all modes of transport! The Itinerary Bonus Cards let you select any destination or activity, including 5-star Hotels, if you wish!

But, just to keep things interesting, you can add the Holiday Getaways to spice up the gameplay so you must include the specific destinations and/or activities in your winning line. But it doesn’t stop there. The Travelling on a Tight Budget Mode limits your transport options, keeping them secret until you use them and the Flexible Travel Plans vary the shape of your winning holiday plan. You can even go for the title of the Ultimate Travel Guide by adding a scoring option to your gameplay.

Now, how fast can you plan your next best holiday ever?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Scream Park: Monster Fan Promo Card
description:
An additional VIP card that keeps your eyes focused on the artwork.

Available at PAX Unplugged 2024 and online for a short time after.

boardgame: Fief: France Edition – Fortune of Sea Calamity Card Promo
description: A Kickstarter exclusive promo Disaster card that may cause Calm sea, Acts of piracy or Storms and shipwrecks to the Offshore area.

boardgame: Fief: France Edition – Caltrop Opportunity Card
description: The Kickstarter exclusive promo Caltrops Fortune card allows you to stop the movement of an opponent's Lord or decrease an attacking opponent's damage when defending.

All aboard for an unforgettable adventure through the flower fields of the Netherlands! In this map for Rolling Trains you will travel from the flat, open fields of Friesland to the dense forests of Limburg, you must design tracks that navigate the country's unique geography. The Netherlands features a distinctive flat landscape, with vast, open fields, windmills, and winding canals. Much of the country lies below sea level, with extensive systems of dikes and polders reclaiming land from the sea. The countryside is dotted with charming villages, colorful tulip fields in spring, and peaceful lakes. All aboard for an unforgettable adventure!

The thrilling Railroad revolution has reached Italy, bringing with it an exhilarating expansion to Dice of Steam. Prepare to embark on a captivating journey as you lay your tracks across the Mediterranean coastline.

The northern Alps and Apennine mountains provide a stunning backdrop, while the central region is known for its idyllic countryside, vineyards, and olive groves. Southern Italy boasts rugged cliffs, volcanic terrain, and sandy beaches along the Mediterranean Sea. Picturesque lakes, such as Lake Garda and Lake Como, are nestled in the north, and the country’s coastal areas feature charming fishing villages, dramatic cliffs, and crystal-clear waters. This diverse geography offers a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. Claim your ticket, unleash your entrepreneurial spirit, master the art of steam, and leave your mark on the history of the 1900s Italy.

Cindar-4 is known as the dark spot on the Interstellar Union map of influence. That neutral planet is beloved by many mercenaries, traders and those  that morality is questionable at best. Local market is known to have everything that isn't possible to get on planets controlled by the Union. From weapons, rare aliens to items that were banned in few galaxies. Now they are contacting you with an offer that is hard to decline. Group of smugglers from Cindar-4 are willing to pay a fortune for rare materials that can be found in the newly opened mines of Interstellar Extraction Corporation.

This Expansion adds 9 cards that let you fulfill orders and sell materials under the table and earn a few additional credits. No one should notice a few missing ores, right?

The Normandy landing was one of the largest amphibious attacks in history, carried out by the Allied forces during the Second World War Light solo wargame that places the player in the role of the Allied high command during the early stages of the landing.

Lo sbarco in Normandia fu uno dei più grandi attacchi anfibi della storia, messa in atto dalle forze alleate durante la Seconda guerra mondiale.

Wargame introduttivo in solitario che pone il giocatore nelle vesti dell'alto comando alleato durante le prime fasi dello sbarco.

Increase replayability and variety with a pack of two maps for Space Driller.

Tanora - Dig through a marsh to a caverns underground where rich veins lie, waiting to be extracted. But don't get too hasty while digging. The underground is filled with deep reservoirs that you will have to navigate around. Extract the gems and avoid falling into the waters of Tanora.

Erebos - 11 - A dwarf planet far away from any known galaxy contains rich veins of ores valued by Interstellar Extraction Corporation. It's covered by ruins of an old alien civilization, did they managed to escape before the extinction of their planet? Now eggs of Swarm fill the caverns that stretch like a web under the whole planet, and your activity might wake them up.

Astra-7 is a planet filled to the brim with expensive minerals. Every corporation in the galaxy is waiting to place their hands on the hidden treasures this planet holds. Now four of the biggest corporations sent their drillers onto the planet racing to recover the most ores and gems from the nose of the competition.

Astra-7 expedition is a expansion for Space Driller that adds more interaction between players by placing against each other on one map. Dig out gems and avoid the drillers of your competition.

Provided components:
- game sheet,
- player sheet,
- rulebook,

A small group of skilled engineers from the Interstellar Extraction Company has expressed a strong interest in working on the new drillers, specially designed to handle the recently discovered materials deep within the uncharted asteroids. The engineers, intrigued by the potential for groundbreaking technological advancements, are eager to take on the task of modifying the drillers to handle the unique properties of the materials. However, the complexity of the materials' molecular structure and the unpredictable nature of their behavior under extreme conditions make the project risky. Despite the uncertainty, the engineers believe the rewards could be immense, offering them the chance to make their mark on the future of space exploration and resource extraction.

Experimental Workshop is an expansion for Space Driller that will change the way you strategise by adding a module for your game where you can upgrade your drilling operation to another level. Exchange minerals you’ve dug out for upgrades for your driller.

Provided components:
- game sheet,
- rulebook,

Scientists of the Interstellar Extraction Company created new alloys from the newly discovered ores. These rare and unique materials possess remarkable properties, making them ideal for advanced manufacturing, spacefaring technology, and even high-end weaponry. As a result, these alloys will not only fetch astronomical prices but also shift the balance of power in the galaxy’s economy, providing a huge advantage to those who control their production.

Mineral Market is an expansion for the Space Driller that will change the way you strategise during the game. Different combinations of minerals will grant you additional points at the end of the game.

Provided components:
- game sheet,
- rulebook,

Successió (Succession) is a Storytelling game consisting of 55 cards, written and designed by Roman Aixendri, in which 3 to 8 players take on the role of members of a Royal Council. As members of the court, you must decide who among you will inherit the throne.

The game's original setting places the story in a medieval (fantastic or realistic) environment, but you can choose to play in any other historical period if you prefer.

The game is designed to be easy to play, with simple rules that encourage the use of imagination, argumentative rigor, active listening, and reflection on Shakespearean-style moral dilemmas.

HOW TO PLAY?
Each player, on their turn, draws a card from the deck, reads it aloud, and answers the question it poses. The other players may then ask up to two additional related questions, which the player must respond to.

The turn then passes to the next player, who follows the same process. Each player will continue drawing cards and answering questions until the resolution card appears.

When the resolution card is drawn, all players must decide who they choose as the new king or queen, based on the quality of their arguments and the rigor of their responses. The player who receives the most votes is crowned monarch.

-description from designer
_________

Successió és un joc narratiu conformat per 55 cartes, escrit i dissenyat per Roman Aixendri en el que entre 3 i 8 persones representareu el paper de membres d’un Consell Reial. Com a membres de la Cort haureu de decidir qui d’entre vosaltres heretarà el tron.

L’ambientació original del joc situa la ficció en un entorn medieval (fantàstic o realista), però si ho preferiu podeu jugar-lo en qualsevol altra època.

El joc està pensat per ser fàcil de jugar, amb unes regles senzilles que promouen l’ús de la imaginació, el rigor argumentatiu, l’escolta activa i la reflexió davant dilemes morals de caràcter shakespearià.

COM ES JUGA? 
Cada jugador/a, durant el seu torn, agafa una carta de la baralla, la llegeix en veu alta i respon a la pregunta que aquesta li planteja. La resta de persones, en acabat, li poden fer fins a dues preguntes més relacionades amb el tema que el jugador haurà de respondre.

En acabat, es passa al següent jugador que haurà de procedir de la mateixa manera. Cada persona anirà agafant cartes i responent preguntes fins que surti la carta de resolució.

Quan aparegui la carta de resolució tots els jugadors hauran de decidir a qui trien com a nou rei o reina (basant-se en la qualitat dels seus arguments i rigor de les seves respostes). El jugador que rep més vots és anomenat monarca.

Australia’s contribution to the War in the Pacific and South-East Asia is often overlooked… but not anymore! Down Under offers 12 Legendary Units from the land of Oz, ripe with colorful outfits that cover all aspects of its various cultures. Nackaroos, Fuzzy Wuzzy Angels, Coastwatchers, Papuan Infantry, Beach Commandos and more! Also included is a new Regular Specialist, the famous Digger, that players of all nations can add to their roster for special benefits!

-description from publisher

A no-nonsense mini-expansion consisting of 48 cards, Pacific Nations offers 8 new Legendary Units, one for each nation included in the core game: USA (the Seabees), UK (the Royal Scots Fusiliers), Australia (the Royal Australian Engineers), India (the Chindits), Canada (C Force), New Zealand (3rd Division), France (Régiment de Tirailleurs Cambodgiens) and Nepal (the Devi Dutta Battalion). Each of these Units contains 6 cards and a ton of replayability.

-description from publisher

Contains 5 new research cards, their titles are board game related, who can be added to the others.  
The German and English Version of this set was part of equivalent Spielbox 1#2025 release.

description:
In Crushin’ & Crunchin’ you will control a parent giant Monster and attempt to destroy a city. Your Child also wants to help, so they will rampage as well. You must keep both the Monster and the Child strong and healthy under mounting pressure from the military. Destroy enough of the city before either is defeated to win!

To play, you'll roll 3 dice every turn and assign two to the Monster's actions, and the last will decide which direction the Child will rampage.

Once enough of the city is destroyed, the Mecha arrives! Defeat it or finish off the rest of the buildings to win.

For now, try the Thunder Tyrant against the tutorial army TEACH! More Creatures and Armies will be added over time, with the Windwing Gale creature and two new armies on the way.

Print and Play Components:
To Bring:
The Monster - 1d12
The Child - 1d8
The Army - 6d6 (⚀ ⚁ ⚂ ⚃ ⚄ ⚅)

Action Dice - 3d6

The City

-6 Food Silos cubes (red)

To Print:
Map Board
Creature Board
Enemy Board

-description from publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Arf Troopers Unit Expansion
description:
Advanced Recon Force troopers are usually found scouting enemy positions before a decisive attack. They are valuable assets to Jedi Generals, capable of delivering accurate information without alerting the enemy to their presence. ARF trooper units are outfitted with a variety of equipment, from the DC-15A blaster to the RPS-6 Launcher and are occasionally accompanied by a Jedi Knight. Their armor allows them to move quickly across the battlefield and includes tools to help ARF units observe targets and report back to their commanders.

Star Wars: Legion - ARF Troopers contains 7 finely sculpted ARF Trooper miniatures, enough for one Special Forces unit, along with new unit and upgrade cards to gain the upper hand on the battlefields of the Clone Wars.

This is a variant of the Mistigri (Lanterloo) game, 
you have to discard your cards faster than your opponents by placing the pairs of same card in front of you. 
But in this variant, have 6 different actions on a wheel that you have to randomly turn at the beginning of your turn. The action is mandatory and can be: take a card from one of your opponent, give a card, change your whole hand, etc...

—user summary

Cornebidouille, la terrible sorcière, lance un défi à ceux qui ne veulent pas manger leur soupe!
Mélangez 100 grammes de crottes de sanglier, deux cafards bien moelleux, une larme de bave de crapaud et vous obtiendrez une belle soupe préparée par Cornebidouille : la Mistigrouille!
Faites tourner la roue, débarrassez-vous de vos cartes en deux coups de cuillère à pot... Et ne soyez pas le dernier à finir la partie avec la Mistigrouille en main!

Un jeu aux petits oignons d'après l'univers de Magali Bonniol et Pierre Bertrand

—description from the publisher

D100 Space: Mapping Game combines the D100 Space rule book with cards and tokens to give the game a board game feel.

It includes 120 mapping cards, 24 Overlay Tiles, 53 Enemy Cards, and 150 Tokens.

Now when you are required to roll on table F – Facility, you will instead draw cards to determine the areas you will discover. Any doors you find will be generated by using doors tokens, and the enemies you encounter are generated by drawing from an Enemy Pool. If you happen upon a geographic area, instead of rolling on the geographic table, you draw from the Geographic Pool; which may require you to add overlay tiles.

By using the Mapping Game, you will get to create the maps right there on the table in front of you, instead of drawing it on a Map Sheet. It is compatible with all D100 Space games.

-description from designer

Trivia Traitor is a social deduction trivia game. Players are assigned hidden roles (Team, Trickster, and Traitor). Team members work together to answer trivia questions and find the Traitor. Meanwhile, Tricksters and the Traitor try to sabotage the Team and secretly turn team members into Tricksters.

The Swindler's Chest includes all gameplay content from Emerald Skulls and Goblin Vaults. The chest also includes a double-sided playmat (one side for each game) and 3 plastic trays for easy game setup and storage. Additionally, the larger box supports sleeved cards for both games!

This Swindler's Chest includes the following items (all sold separately):

Emerald Skulls (base game content)
Emerald Skulls: 7-8 Player Expansion
Goblin Vaults (base game content)
Goblin Vaults: Gundur Pack + Chaos & Order (Mini Expansions)
Swindler's Playmat

Admin Note: This entry is set up to reflect the above but the Swindler's Chest was also sold with only Emerald Skulls and its expansion. This entry is also meant to represent that version too.

description:
Sci-Me! aritmetika is a quick card game about arithmetics and scientific publishing. In the game, you are a scientist creating formulas that you try to publish. Test your logical thinking and be the first to successfully present your findings!

In the card game Sci-Me!, you are a researcher trying to publish your scientific findings. Since publications are the scientific currency, your goal is to publish and being the first to accumulate three publications. During the game, you need to use your limited time strategically and balance the risk of failure and time spent on each publication.

The main principle of the game is to order cards in a meaningful manner creating valid equations. During the course of the round a player does two actions from the set of five available actions. To obtain successful publication one needs to form true statement out of the cards in front of them. After the statement is formed, it needs to pass a random check which is harder the shorter the statement is.

—description from the designer

Thespis has gained acclaim across Greece for being the first actor to step out from the chorus. Outperforming him is sure to please the gods.

Soliloquy 6-card expansion enables solo play, pitting the player against the legendary tragedian Thespis. Work past this automated playwright’s creative interference to secure your theatrical legacy.

Set up the core game as normal, then shuffle Thespis’ 6-card deck separately and place it nearby.

After each of your turns, Thespis drafts the card directly to the right of the card you chose and adds it to his own play (beginning with the earliest incomplete act and upstaging if possible). However, Thespis does not resolve instant effects when they are added as a key scene: instead reveal and resolve the top card of Thespis’ deck. These cards carry their own specific effects that may interfere with your own plans.

At the end of the game, score your own play as normal and compare it to the included chart to determine how your play was received. For harder difficulty levels, resolve additional cards from Thespis’ deck each time one is needed and subtract Thespis’ scoring effects (not patterns) from your own score before determining success.

In a future where humanity is on the brink of extinction, what little hope remains lies in a few titanic structures, known as Lifeships. With the help of an incompetent captain and their grumpy first mate, Sasha, you’ll have to navigate a terrifying universe to complete missions and keep your Lifeship running. How will you survive, will you survive? The choice is yours! An innovative, tabletop adventure where deck-building, base-building, and tactical skirmishes collide and no two games are ever the same. In Lifeship, you and your crew are part of one of the last colony ships to leave Earth. Your job? Survive. Adapt. And forge a future among the stars. You’ll build your character, choose a class, and embark on missions to secure resources for your people. Just be sure to plan for Xeno-spiders, Piratists or an array of other deadly enemies! With hundreds of unique missions, a true solo mode, a custom website that narrates your game, and tracks your decisions, Lifeship is a game like no other.

				
				
					Gameplay & FeaturesThe game is packed with innovative features that create a tabletop experience like no other.

Simultaneous Play – No waiting for turns. Every player acts at the same time, keeping the game fast and engaging.

Innovative website companion - a living universe. Choices you make are tracked, factions remember you, characters react to your past actions, and missions evolve based on your decisions. The site takes care of bookkeeping, letting you focus on the adventure rather than managing stats and logs. Who will you befriend? Who will you betray? And who will come back for revenge?

Deck building with a twist – every decision matters. You’ll build your deck from a mix of character cards, class abilities, and equipment, tailoring your strategy to each mission. But be careful, too much salvage clogs your deck, and managing your resources is key to survival!

Base-Building & Progression – Your Lifeship is more than just a home—it’s your strategic hub.

Spend Captain’s Kredits (CK) to upgrade the ship, improve facilities, or, well… buy Taco subscriptions.

Fail-Forward Design – Failing a mission doesn’t stop the story (and trust me, you’ll fail), it just changes it. No restarts. No wasted time. Just a new path forward.

Cloning Technology – Death isn’t the end. If your character dies, they’re cloned, mutated, changed. Returning with powerful new abilities, or perhaps just an extra thumb. Who knows?

Loot & Salvage System – Scavenge for resources, but be careful, too much loot clogs your deck, and it’s worthless until you get it back to the ship.

Smart Enemy AI – Enemies don’t just attack blindly, they hide, retreat, and strategize. Even a lowly rat can outmaneuver an unprepared team!

True Solo Mode – Play Your Way. Not every game balances solo play properly, but Lifeship was built for it from the start.
Enter CEDALION, your fully customizable AI companion. CEDALION is a streamlined, strategic co-pilot. Choose from four distinct roles, Combat, Healing, Tech, or Salvage, and adapt him to suit your needs. He supports you without overshadowing you, so every decision is still yours to
make. And best of all, he doesn’t take a share of the loot.

Endless Replayability – With 48 missions per run and a second campaign mode with all new missions, no two games are alike.

—description from the publisher

Pledge your allegiance and swear your oaths, power up your annihilation drives and charge your laser blasters, welcome to Space Battles, the new age of galactic warfare!

Written by renowned rulesmith Rick Priestley, using standard playing cards, Space Battles: A Spacefarers Guide is an exciting, dice-less spaceship tabletop wargame. Quick-paced, easy to pick up, while also tactically challenging, Space Battles allows players to battle it out amongst the stars as they lead their fleets of spacecraft in a series of exciting scenarios.

While the game can be played and greatly enjoyed as a generic system, Space Battles also includes a dynamic backstory and range of unique factions. Each with their own individual playing style, five different factions are detailed within this book; the Federation, the Dahlians, the Orzo, the Anthozoans, and the Xixan. There is also the mysterious Spectral Fleet, an esoteric force of spacefarers who slip in and out of corporeality. In addition to his reputation as one of the finest wargames writers of all-time, Rick Priestley is also regarded as one of the greatest creators of backstories and lore, this is clearly evident in the unique universe he has created for Space Battles.

Space Battles: A Spacefarers Guide
167-page, full colour, A5-sized premium paperback
Core rules
Advanced rules
Index of Spacecraft Types
6 Scenarios
A Spacefarer’s Guide to Known Space
Campaigns
Summary
Counters
Making a Game Mat for Spaceship Battles
Index

Note: The Space Battles book contains all the rules needed to play this tabletop wargame. Two packs of ordinary playing cards (not included) are required.

boardgame: Seastrike 1905: Naval and Coastal Warfare in the Predreadnought era
description:
This is a full game covering Naval and Coastal Warfare in the age of the Pre-dreadnought.

The game includes everything necessary to play. All you will need is access to a colour printer, A4 Label Paper and some card.

The portrays conflict in a group of contested islands located between two nations.

Units provided include: Pre-dreadnought Battleships, Armoured Cruisers, Protected Cruisers, Torpedo Boats, land based gun batteries, urban areas, HQ's, and Marine units.   
Island Terrain, measuring and turning templates and damage record counters are all provided.

The rules accommodate Naval Warfare and interaction with coastal forces, including landing Marines to capture areas, destroy batteries ETC.

You can choose identical forces or asymetric forces based on a points system, and there is a system for Espionage/ Counter Espionage and sides may have different missions which will affect how they score for Victory Points.

The mechanisms are deliberately simple using standard D6's to hit, identify which component is hit, deal with armour penetration.  Damage is scored on ship templates or marked with counters.

—description from the publisher

Macross: Valkyrie Spirits is a Japanese doujin game that was produced for sale at game shows.  To get a copy you needed to preorder it and then pick it up at the show.

This is a solitaire game based on Decision Games Commando system.  The player plays the leader of platoon of Valkyries and is sent on various missions.

The game includes 6 pages of rules, an event chart, one map, and a set of counters.  The counters require assembly.

boardgame: Yang and Reinhard: The Legend of the Undefeated
description:
The Battle of Amritsar is a Japanese doujin that was produced for sale at shows.  To get a copy you needed to preorder the game and then pick it up at the show.

This game deals with the Battle of Amritsar from the Legend of the Galactic Heroes show. Yang, proved that he was a superior tactician to Reinhard.  The battle was the first of many battles the two would fight.  The system is based on Game Journal's All Quiet on the Western Front.

The game includes 8 pages of rules, a card reference, one map, and a counter sheet.  The counters need to be assembled.
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

boardgame: Basket Case: Horror at Hotel Broslin
description:
Sneak a peek to find the freak! Mysterious picnic baskets have been delivered to the guests at the Hotel Broslin, and one of them hides a monster waiting to attack when his basket opens!

The game is played over a series of rounds. Each round starts with the First Player, and play continues clockwise until every Player has had a turn. Each turn, a Player may play any cards from their hand, and perform up to two Actions. All of this may be done in any order, and players may take the same Action twice.

Once Players have taken a turn, the round ends by having the First Player deal out a card face-up to each Player, starting with themselves and going clockwise around the table. Players each collect these cards into their hands, then a new round begins.

During gameplay, players must try to avoid being in the same room as Belial (the monster) at the hotel. However, if they do happen to discover a Belial Basket on their turn, or even a Basket that grants rewards, keep calm. The contents inside of a basket are not revealed or activated unless a "Belial Attacks" card is revealed at the end of a round.

If a Player is caught inside the same room as a Belial Basket when a "Belial Attacks" card is drawn, they'll gain a Belial token, which loses them 3 Cheeseburgers at the end of the game. Additionally, the Player who receives the "Belial Attacks" card must draw it into their hand, which loses them 1 Cheeseburger at the end of the game.

The player who has the most Cheeseburgers after Belial has attacked 3 times wins the game.

—description from the designer

An edge-matching solitaire card game where you barricade yourself against a horde of zombies! Comes with 60 Nail & Plank Cards, 3 House Cards, 5 Zombie Cards, and rulebook.

To play the game, the Player places either 1 (shorter game), 2 (medium game) or 3 (longer game) House Cards onto the table. Every third card from the remaining deck of Nail & Plank Cards is drawn and placed anywhere around the center House Cards by matching the symbol(s) on the edges. Play continues by drawing the third card on every turn until three complete rings of cards have surrounded the center House cards.

Throughout game play, Zombie Cards will appear, which counts against the Player. When a Zombie Card is drawn, it is placed into the spread anywhere the Player wants, and they can do this up to 5 times since there are 5 Zombie cards.

A Player wins if they can complete three rings of cards around the center House Cards before the 5th Zombie card appears. If the 5th Zombie Card appears before the third ring is completed, they lose.

Advanced game play allows the Player to removed a placed Zombie card from the spread, allowing more time to complete their three rings.

A two-Player game can be achieved by alternating turns.

The "HexD3x" series has many versions of the game, and they all play the same way, with some of the games in the series incorporating fun tokens that "deactivate" symbols to match edges to help the Player complete their three rings.

—description from the designer

Get ready for a thrilling game of strategy, luck, and sabotage. Be the first to clear your hand, or knock out opponents by forcing them to draw. Strategically play onto the discard pile to see the next player up for a downfall - if they can’t play a card they’ll be forced to draw cards based on the cards you’ve played.

Components:
4 Initial Contract tiles

Changes in setup:
In step 6a of setup use the new Initial Contracts instead of the Initial Contracts from the base game

Components:
6 Neutral Urban Building tiles

Changes in setup:
Before setup add the new Neutral Urban Buildings to the corresponding pool.

Jirafas y Chacales 3 ("Giraffes and Jackals 3") is a fun collaborative game to apply Non-Violent Communication.

With giraffes and jackals as protagonists, players must achieve "Collective Growth" and "Group Openness", without drama or impositions, before falling into "Endless Communication" or "Mental Saturation".

Does it seem easy to you? It is! It is easy to learn to play, but not to win! Will you be able to maintain humanity in this crazy conversation?

Each side of the card has a phrase. The game consists of having a crazy conversation and saying the phrase of the card you play. First we will always play in DISCONNECTION mode (the cards on the side with the dark border) and, later, we may be able to play in CONNECTION mode (light border).

Basically it consists of:

     If you can put a card, you put it. Only if it matches the shape or color of the previous one.
     If you can't, take one from the deck. And if you can play it, you play it.
     When playing a card, say its phrase out loud. Yes, it will be a crazy conversation!
     If the card has an action, you do it. If not, the turn continues as normal.
     Keep going until you win (or lose). At first, the game takes you where it wants. Let yourself go... And you will lose!


—description from the designer

Petals & Perils - a two player abstract strategy game in which both players tend to flowers in the same shared garden. Both players are watering their own flowers in efforts to fully grow their plants, while their enemies might overwater the flowers until they're wilted.

At the end of the game, the player with the most fully flourished flowers wins.

—description from the designer

Once upon a time, in the heart of a bustling city, there lived a joyful family with their beloved pets; Zinon the loyal Pug, Eliana the graceful Cat, Christian the daring Hamster, and Hector the nimble Gecko. Though their lives were filled with love, the family longed for a break from the city's noise. So, they journeyed to their quiet retreat, a cozy wooden cabin deep in the forest. The first day passed in peaceful bliss, but when dawn broke on the second day, the pets awoke to an alarming sight. The cabin was empty. Their beloved family had vanished without a trace! Shocked but determined, the four friends gathered what little supplies they could find and set off into the unknown, determined to uncover the mystery and bring their family home.

Fairytale is a dungeon-crawler for families and experienced gamers alike that fosters cooperation, wonder, and unforgettable adventures.

Choose your weapon, armor, and abilities to fully customize your Hero for the challenges ahead. Select one of the many Stories to play, each one taking place in an expansive world inspired by classic fairytales. Complete missions to gather Evergems, powerful artifacts that upgrade your equipment and enhance your abilities. But hurry! Time is ticking, and the Story Dice will determine the events unfolding on your adventure and how much precious time remains.

With 4 base Heroes to choose from and 2 more from each expansion, each with a unique miniature, unique deck, and fully customizable player board, over 23 different Encounters, and 3 formidable Bosses, every game is packed with new experiences and hidden mysteries waiting to be discovered.
 —description from the publisher

Spray is a one-against-all game. One player plays the role of a graffiti writer.
Setup: Place the 5 colour cards face up on the table. Shuffle the deck of 25 district cards, district side up. Deal to each player the same number of cards that each player lays on the table in front of themself, without changing the order, overlapping so that they are all visible. 
The writer secretly chooses the secret colour card.
Game: Starting with the first player and then clockwise, the active player takes their top district card and places it, district side up, in the middle of the table next (horizontally or vertically) to a district card, there cannot be more than 5 district cards in a row or column. 
During their turn the writer also places their district card respecting the placement rules but, they may, if they wish, play their district card upside down, graffiti side up, only if the background colour of the laid graffiti is the secret colour or the laid graffiti is adjacent (horizontally, vertically or diagonally) to a district card of the secret colour.
Whenever the writer makes a graffiti the other players flip any colour cards corresponding to neither to the background of the placed graffiti, nor any of the district cards adjacent to it (horizontally, vertically or diagonally), to the no colour side.
The graffiti writer may not create graffiti if this would reveal their identity, i.e. there may never be a single colour card face up.
End: The game ends as soon as all 25 district cards have been played. Everyone plays the role of the graffiti writer once. The player who achieves the most graffiti wins the game.

—description from the publisher

This is a german expansion collection for ROVE. It contains the german version of the following expansions:


     Klirrende Kälte (Icy Incidents)
     Energetische Experimente (Alien Activity)
     Planetare Probleme (Cosmic Crisis)
     Parasit (Crawler)
     Upgrade (Reconfigured)
A version of Scene it which asks players to identify a quote or picture.

Escape From Tibbles Tower is fast-paced, small box board game where players are all Tibbles, the infinite God of time, who also happens to be a moustached tuxedo cat. Trapped within the tower by your evil self Diadaxis, and his army of corrupted kittens! You must compete to be the first Tibbles to find the exit and break out of the tower before Diadaxis, or the other players, use reality bending powers to make kitty litter of your plans!

Players will reveal tower tiles, draw, and play cards to explore the tower, and cause havoc for the other players. Compete to be the first to make it to the exit tile and escape from Tibbles Tower!

—description from the publisher

In Monkey Mayhem, players race to get the lowest hand until BANANA! Someone grabs the banana and ends the round. But look out: if the WRENCH gets grabbed, then suddenly the HIGHEST hand wins! Monkey Mayhem is guaranteed chaotic fun for monkeys of all ages!

A card game ROTTA! (Rat in Finnish) can be played by either 2-players game or 3-4 players game. In a 2 players the deck is shared between players and the point is to notice two pairs of cards as fast as possible and then collects them. Whoever succeeds to collect all the cards win the game.

In the 3-4 players game called Ratking players are trying to get rid of their cards. In the beginning of the game 1 Ratking card is deleted from the deck and all the other cards are dealt to the players. During game players pick cards from each other's hands and try to made pair of cards. The player who has a pairless Ratking in their hand at the end loses the game.
