Peppa a pig is taking her bright yellow camera to the fair so she can take some terrific photos of her friends. Cheese! And 'Click' she releases the shutter and takes a fun photo. Help Peppa Pig collect four photos of the same friend and form a set.

Children love pressing the button on the big yellow camera and seeing a photo of one of Peppa's friends pop out.

Be the first to collect a set of four photographs of your chosen friend.
This expansion to EcoLogic: Europe adds four new coastal habitat boards, along with 31 new species cards. It also allows adding one additional player.
This expansion to EcoLogic: Europe adds four new mountain habitat boards, along with 34 new species cards. It also allows adding one additional player.
This expansion to EcoLogic: Europe adds four new human habitat boards (cities and gardens), along with 47 new species cards and ten human pressure cards. It also allows adding one additional player.

You never imagined that it would come to this… The Earth shakes. The Air is foul. Fires burn. Waters rise. So much destruction. So much chaos. So much knowledge lost… And now, magic itself is dangerously out of balance. The Elements pool. The leylines are… off.

None still alive remember what pattern the leylines used to take, what the magic is supposed to look like, to feel like. The lore contradicts itself. Some accounts describe magic as a network of energy, connecting all living things, a web of sorts. Others describe a great circle surrounding and maintaining our world. However; one phrase repeats over and over in the remaining magical lore:

“…balance the elements, renew the lines, charge the circle…”  
                                                       
Whatever that means, you need to figure it out; fast. You are a Guardian, one of the world’s last remaining magic users. You have always had an almost intuitive control over your own abilities, your magic. And you are the most powerful mage left. You have made your way to Atlantis, the heart of all the world’s magic, where here, in a crumbling temple, lies the keystone to the world’s leylines. The journey has been perilous; but now, the real task begins. It falls to you and you alone to figure it out, to fix it, to bring the Elements back into alignment and restore the magic. It’s taken time and effort to get this far; but your time grows short. If you fail, magic itself will die and the world will crumble. You have 10 minutes. Good luck. You’re going to need it.

Charge the Circle is a real-time solitaire game of magical crisis management. The player takes on the role of a mage trying to save magic itself… before the Elements spiral completely out of control and destroy the world. The game takes place in Atlantis. There, the player is faced with fixing the keystone to the world’s leylines. The keystone is represented in the game by a set of nine cards. These keystone cards begin on the Elemental Side. Throughout the game, the player draws and rolls dice, representing the 4 Elements (Earth, Air, Fire, and Water) and Wild Magic. The player places these dice in particular combinations on the Elemental Side of the game’s nine cards to restore balance between the Elements and “charge” a section of the world’s magical network, the “circle” in the game’s name. The goal is to “charge” all of the cards, revealing the restored “circle” of the world’s magical network BEFORE the 10 minute timer runs out.

Charge the Circle was designed for the 2023 9-Cards Nanogame Print and Play Design Contest. Post-contest, the designer introduced the two-player mode, which pits Guardian against Necromancer in a 10-minute duel to save the world’s magic. Charge the Circle is Rachel Carpenter’s first game and is also the first game in her Minutes to Midnight series.

—description from the designer

Travel to the Grand Line in this One Piece game. Get help from one of the Straw Hats or encounter a nasty pirate. Roll the dice and move. Each place has a special event, and some places will make you draw a card with an special effect.

A new deck with over 50 unique denizen cards, which you can use to seed a new chronicle or add to the archive of an existing chronicle.

—description from the publisher

The Denizen Deck was included for free with every pledge level of the Kickstarter campaign for Oath: New Foundations.

Explore a brand-new and fully-featured solo mode that adapts to you as you play it. Face a wide range of adversaries that will follow you from generation to generation as you attempt to guide the course of history


    Unlock solo-specific content and face challenges as Chancellor, Citizen, or Exile
    Easily jump between solo and multiplayer games
    Fully compatible with Oath and the upcoming New Foundations expansion


—description from the publisher

This deluxe edition of EcoLogic: Europe includes the base game along with the Coastal, Mountain, and Human Habitat expansions, all in a single box.

EcoLogic is a beautiful logic game whose mechanics were created to show the relationships between plants, fungi and animals. Players create complex ecosystems using cards representing hundreds of living organisms, inhabiting a variety of habitats.
This set of seven species cards adds the Lynx, Lesser Spotted Eagle, Peregrine Falcon, Hoopoe, Kingisher, Spotted Salamander, and Crayfish to Ecologic: Europe.

This set of two species cards add the Aurochs and Tarpan to EcoLogic: Europe.

For each set of these cards that was purchased during the Gamefound campaign, 5 € was donated to the "Puchaczówka" wildlife clinic in Lower Silesia, Poland.

Rift Domination puts you at the helm of one of 20 asymmetric factions, each unlocking a different lineup drawn from 11 distinct unit types. Your faction shapes your approach — your decisions decide who dominates the Rift.

A turn‑based 4X strategy game, Rift Domination rewards precise planning and bold plays. Earn victory points through:

Epic fleet battles with fast, dice‑driven resolution
Tactical Occurrence cards with clear timing windows and high impact
System control & planet upgrades (choose from 8) to build economy and defense
Exploration & expansion across a contested galaxy

At its core lie fleet‑building and intense PvP interaction. Tailor your strongholds with upgrades like Artillery, Shield Generators, and Espionage Stations; time your OCC cards to turn the tide without bogging down the flow of play. With sharp faction identities, asymmetric unit access, and meaningful counterplay, Rift Domination challenges commanders to think ahead, adapt fast, and strike decisively.

Only one will rule the void. Will it be you?

-description from designer

Trick Tac Toe is a 10 card microgame where two players play for best two out of three tricks in order to claim a space on the board. You could concede early, giving your opponent the space, but then you get to control the next spot to play for.

There are no suits, but a simple rule twist where the 1 can beat the highest three cards makes each situation a bit of a threat. Once a player hits three in a row or wins five of the spaces on the board, the game has ended.

—description from the designer

The players take turns to roll and move. The game begins with the number 1 and ends with the number 60. Three dice are rolled, always at the same time. Whenever three dice are thrown, the points from only two dice are added together. The number of points the two dice have together is the number of numbers the player advances with his figure. However, if you roll 13 points with three dice, you have the advantage of being able to advance one of your figures by 13 numbers. You have the right to roll again. The same advantage applies to anyone who rolls three identical numbers.
If a figure, whether your own or another, lands on a space that is already occupied, the player who has been caught up with it must move back 13 numbers, but the player who is advancing moves forward 13 numbers.
As soon as a player's first figure goes past space 6, he must place his second figure, followed by his third figure, in order to bring all figures into play as quickly as possible.
Only one figure may stand on each space, with the exception of the spaces with red borders. Any figure on such a safety space cannot be removed by a player who moves up.
In the final game, the number 60 must be reached directly with the exception of the lucky number 13, with which the goal can be reached even if the number is over 60. The first player to reach space 60 with all three figures wins the game.

—user summary

This quiz game is all about the city of Cologne and the world. The 500 questions in different levels of difficulty and the duels make the game very varied.

There are not only different levels of difficulty, but also four different categories in which you can test your knowledge.

The aim of the game is to score the most points in the categories. And whoever gets to the center of the playing field first gets additional points.

-description of the publisher

Masterland is a PvP tactical card game that cuts mechanics to the bone. Strategic depth arises from minimal pieces and rules, thanks to clever planning and resource management.

Build your fantasy kingdom and assemble an unstoppable army: each turn you can gather a Resource, play cards and manage your hand. At turn hand, if you have deployed Units, you automatically attack the next player and steal their Energy. The last one standing wins!

The system has been designed with competition and replayability in mind: it supports intense head-to-head duels, as well as free-for-all multiplayer battles for up to 4 players.

A quick game loop and rewarding learning curve make it the perfect title to approach strategy card games, as well as an addictive filler for veterans!

—description from the publisher

Expansion for Monumental

Meet the titans and the Behemoth, a new add-on for Monumental!

Titans are powerful and mighty beings that will unleash their rage on the board and act as unpredictable hazards. These mythical entities won’t be swayed easily, but predicting their moves and controlling their rage could very well become your ticket to victory…

If your civilization has been paying attention to legends and has acquired enough Talismans, you will be equipped to handle the Titans and even use their power – neglect them and you might become the unlucky target of their rampage!

Like Monsters, Titans will appear on the board at the flip of one of the new Barbarian/Titan tokens.

As soon as they appear, they will immediately start wreaking havoc on Provinces…

When the Behemoth is in play, players gain a new action they can take on their turn: Rampage. To take this action, they must pay 5 Talisman tokens (put them back in the main supply) and then move the Behemoth directly to any Province, removing everything on the tile (Soldiers, Explorers, Warlords, Outposts and Trading Posts are returned to the player’s supply; any tokens, including Wonder tokens, are returned to the box). If a Wonder token is removed in this way, the controlling player still keeps the matching card in their deck, and it will still count towards their final score at the end of the game.

Otterwood is a turn-based game in the world of movie production. Your goal is to earn the maximum of points by producing different movies. No knowledge required to play. The game is set in an animalistic world with wordplay on famous movies and movie stars.

Each turn you have several actions at your disposal:

     Hire artists that you need to produce movies
     Extend the contract of hired artists to work with them longer
     Buy artist contracts you need or sell the ones you don't need
     Play Event cards to shake your or your opponent strategy
     Push a trend where some movie genres are more popular and easier to produce
     Update the pool of movies available to the production


At the end of your turn, you can produce a movie. You'll need a screenwriter, a director and an actor/actress. The better the artists you have, the higher amount of points you'll earn.

When a player produce his/her 6th movie, we finish the current turn and then the game ends.
Everyone count his/her points. The movies themselves grant points. Then you have bonus points if you produced more movies of the same genre or if you followed specific strategies like producing low-budget movies.
The winner is the player that have the most points.

There is an alternate win condition in the game. At the start of the game, each player receive a movie card which is really hard to make. You can produce it at the end of your turn like any other movie. However, this one ends the game with your victory. An insurance policy that you never are out of the game.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Hollow Pact is a fast-paced, semi-co-operative game that blends horror, strategy, survival, and betrayal.

In the game, you join a group of scoundrels; enter a ruined town, the lair of a deadly Beast; grab cargo precious to you, your fellow scoundrels, and that Beast; play to your strengths; plead with the spirits for aid; and find a route to escape with the loot and your life - if you can - because even if the Beast doesn't get you, your fellow scoundrels might as they follow their own hidden agendas.

One player-controlled Beast uses its powers and the aid of spirits to hunt down the scoundrels and recover what they stole. The Beast also has secret goals, and accomplishing them makes thwarting the scoundrels easier. Hollow Pact plays out this thrilling, treacherous chase, with no two sessions playing the same.

The game includes a modular map and multiple scenario setups/goals as well as solo and cooperative play variations.

—description from the designer

Travel through all Simac countries and finish first.

Players each race 2 pawns over a dotted path through 6 European countries. Instead of dice you choose 1 playing card from your hand of 3 to move a pawn.
At the start of your turn you take playing cards so you'll have 3 in your hand. If you gat a 7-card you immediately take a chance-card.

First player to get 2 pawns at the finish wins.

boardgame: Lost Ruins of Arnak: Twisted Paths
description:
Twisted Paths is the final major addition to the world of Arnak. Its highlight is a new double-sided map, bigger than the one in the base game, that features two new research tracks, each with unique mechanisms and components.

On the map, you can choose to explore its daytime side and scale the white stone of the Owl Temple. It uses unique new temple tiles that, if combined together, can become extra idol slots, and a third research token, a lantern, that brings even stronger research rewards. You can also brave the night and journey through the dark caverns of the Spider Temple. This side uses a new resource, dark tablets, used to invoke mysterious altars and place artifacts directly onto the research track to be used when ascending the track. But beware—using the dark tablets can give you strong rewards, but also cost you points should you become too ambitious!

In addition to the two new maps, the expansion also contains new guardians, sites, and assistants. It also brings some solo content—Rival tiles for the new maps, as well as Purple Rival tiles and Rival Objectives, two mini expansions we’ve previously released online as print-and-play.

Lost Ruins of Arnak: Twisted Paths was previously included in the Adventure Chest, a limited-edition storage box with an original insert designed by CGE.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Eldritch Young Investigators

Get ready for an exciting and educational adventure!

In this card game, children aged 5 and up can take on the role of young investigators ready to collect Arcane Symbols and prevent the fearsome Cthulhu from awakening.

Game objective: Choose from ten young investigators and collect Arcane Symbols without waking the Great and Slumbering Cthulhu to win.

Recommended for: Children aged 5 and up, regardless of reading ability. The game was developed with the support of students with ASD, ADHD, and ID in municipal schools.

Features: 

     Combination of action and strategy. 
     Inclusive and accessible. 
     Stimulates critical thinking and cooperation. 


Join the mission and save the world from the awakening of Cthulhu!

—description from the designer

Easy to learn but impossible to master, Atlanta Traffic is a quick card game that’s best with 3-6 players. The goal is to finish your daily commute faster than the other drivers, and before your frustration grows into a case of murderous road rage.

Each turn, choose between advancing along your own route or slowing other players down—that is, if you can avoid drawing a disaster card that will ruin everyone’s day.

Featuring real-life Atlanta delays from food spills to bridge fires and helpful advantages like Peach Pass and super speeding, Atlanta Traffic brings all the fun of GA-400 and I-85 home to your game table.

“It really captures that special sense of despair.”
- Nathan, playtester

—description from the publisher

Show off your skills with split-second decisions!

Because Summon & Magic uses an action value system, the action per card play is shorter than in existing TCGs,

you can enjoy the game with a light tempo and instantaneous power like a turn-based RPG.

Recreating the "good old TCG of the 90s,"

while retaining strong TCG elements,

we have created a competitive game that is easy for beginners to get into, like Othello, Shogi, and Chess, but that requires more thought for experienced players.

The existence of action values ​​means that even powerful cards may not be able to win, and weak cards may decide the deciding move!

Enjoy a heated showdown with a well-crafted deck.

This is a trading card game that allows you to "enter the world of the game as a magician and decide who your heroes and main forces will be."

—description from the publisher (translated)

一瞬の判断力で、自身を魅せろ！
サモン＆マジックは行動値システムを採用しているため、既存のTCGに比べカードプレイ毎の
アクションが短くなり、宛らターン制RPGのような軽快なテンポ、そして瞬発力でゲームを楽しむことが出来ます。

　「９０年代の古き良きTCG」を再現しつつ、TCGの要素を強く残したまま、
オセロや将棋、チェスなどのように、初心者も入りやすく経験者ほど熟考する様な競技性を追求して制作いたしました。
行動値が存在することにより、強力なカードであっても勝てない事や、弱カードが決着の一手を決めることも！
練りに練ったデッキで熱いショーダウン（対決）をお楽しみいただけます。

『魔術師としてゲームの世界へ立ち、英雄や主力はあなたが決める』
を可能とするトレーディングカードゲームです。

''—description from the publisher

Players alternate flinging discs into a tower with plastic catapults. First to get 4 in wins.

Includes 25 red score discs, 25 yellow score discs, 1 tower slam game, 2 slam launchers, 2 tower slam attachments

In the arenas of ancient Rome, the fight for glory and freedom never ends. In Rudicus, players take on the role of gladiators battling to earn their freedom, using a strategic weapon advantage system with a cyclical balance of power.

Each card features a gladiator with two different weapons, and players must decide how and when to use them to outmaneuver their opponents. With just 18 cards, Rudicus delivers a fast-paced, cooperative tactical experience filled with critical decisions.

Do you have what it takes to claim the rudis, the wooden sword that grants your freedom from the arena?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Relic Knights: Radiant vs Void Two Player Starter Set
description:
The universe is dying. One by one the Darkspace Calamity has consumed the galaxies of our universe. Now, only a single galaxy remains: The Last Galaxy. Only the mighty champions known as Relic Knights can overcome the Calamity and save us all from total destruction!

Relic Knights is a vibrant and colorful miniature skirmish game that features streamlined card-based rules.

Relic Knights: Radiant vs Void Two Player Starter Set comes with twelve miniatures, unit profile cards, and two Esper decks, providing everything you need to field two 35-point Cadre rosters.

These models are provided unpainted and unassembled. Some assembly and cleanup will be required.

Who? Where? or What are you ?

Every player puts a card in his/her stand without looking at it.
Then everyone can ask 5 questions to figure out what's on their card.
The first player to guess 5 cards correctly, wins.

boardgame: Heroscape: Air Marshal Zed Nesbitt Army Expansion
description:
From the moment she claimed her Wellspring and rose to power, Volarak knew that in a land controlled by winged Kyrie, air power would be crucial. Her mechanized air force engages the enemy from above and rapidly deploys strike teams to the front lines. Some of the bolder pilots even take the fight to the enemy directly. Few things drive fear into an army quite like a huge battleblimp careening into your front lines!

This expansion introduces Air Marshal Zed Nesbitt, a unique hero figure in the Heroscape Age of Annihilation. Nesbitt’s synergy with other Vorid figures makes him an integral part of a well-rounded army, providing both offensive power and strategic deployment options. His custom-engineered war dirigible allows him to execute daring maneuvers, making him a formidable presence on the battlefield!

Features
Command the battlefield with Zed Nesbitt’s GIANT battleblimp!
Use Nesbitt alongside other Vorid figures for incredible offensive power!
Highly detailed and fully assembled HUGE miniature!
Fully compatible with all Heroscape sets and expansions!

Contents
1 Air Marshal Zed Nesbitt Figure
1 Army Card

The vorids are a people of small stature, but enormous of imagination. Their capacity for invention nearly outstrips their ability to control the machines they devise. But as the Imperator always says; what’s the harm in raising a little Kayne?

Build your army and make Imperator Kayne the centerpiece of your Volarak faction forces! Kayne thrives in the chaos of combat, targeting enemies on uneven terrain with his devastating stomping power. His ability to punish foes whose level is no more than 5 levels above his makes him a versatile threat across the battlefield. Perfect for players who love high-impact, dynamic gameplay. This expansion contains 1 miniature! It is fully compatible with all Heroscape Master Sets and Expansions.

This is add-on content. A Battle Box and/or Master Set is required for gameplay.

Contents
1 Fully Assembled Premium Miniature - Imperator Kayne
1 Army Card

boardgame: Heroscape: Molten Crustaceans & Tanuki Tricksters Common Army Expansion
description:
This pack contains common units. This means you can use as many of each figure or squad in your army as point values allow!

The enormous spined crabs of Icaria are considered a delicacy by that world’s draconic masters. On Valhalla, few dare attempt such a meal, for the blood of these creatures is akin to molten lava. The Tanuki Tricksters of Volarak–motivated less by hunger than by foolhardy curiosity–are prone to poking the crabs anyway. And then poking other people, too. And then tripping them into the resultant pools and fields of lava…

Dive into dynamic gameplay with this dual-squad expansion, combining mischievous strategy and volcanic power. Featuring two common squads, this set allows players to field multiple units for maximum flexibility and impact. The Tanuki Tricksters add chaos and mobility to the battlefield, while the Molten Crustaceans bring environmental control and durability. This expansion contains five highly detailed miniatures! It is fully compatible with all Heroscape Master Sets and Expansions.

This is add-on content. A Battle Box and/or Master Set is required for gameplay.

Features
Renegade’s first original Heroscape design and first common squad!
Perfect for players who thrive on strategy, unpredictability, and terrain manipulation.
The Tanuki Tricksters add chaos and mobility to the battlefield, while the Molten Crustaceans bring environmental control and durability.
Five highly detailed premium painted miniatures!
Fully compatible with all Heroscape Master Sets and Expansions!

Contents
5 Highly Detailed, Fully Assembled Premium Miniatures
3 Molten Crustaceans
2 Tanuki Tricksters
2 Army Cards

boardgame: Heroscape: Revnan Acolytes & Grave Grim Common Army Expansion
description:
This pack contains common units. This means you can use as many of each figure or squad in your army as point values allow!

From the hopeless and lost to the vengeful dead, those with nothing left arrive on Revna’s doorstep in search of a future. Many become her Acolytes, bound by their oaths to rise from death each time they fall. Accompanying these fanatical followers are the lupine forms of Grave Grims, shepherds of dead souls from Earth drawn to the mass of living death that follows in Revna’s wake. Their howls are a warning and a curse to all who would defy her.

Reinforce your forces with this expansion, featuring a common squad and common hero that bring powerful synergies and tactical depth to your battles. Combine the abilities of the Revnan Acolytes and Grave Grim for a powerful, cohesive army centered on Revna’s dark dominion. This expansion contains four  miniatures! It is fully compatible with all Heroscape Master Sets and Expansions.

This is add-on content. A Battle Box and/or Master Set is required for gameplay.

Contents
1 Grave Grim Figure
3 Revnan Acolyte Figures
2 Army Cards

boardgame: Heroscape: Scavorith Lord of Ruin Army Expansion
description:
To call Scavorith a “killing machine” would be a disservice to the passion with which he wages war. Finding himself in Valhalla with so many new targets, the Eisenek Lord surges across the battlefield, reveling in ruination.

This expansion contains 1 miniature! It is fully compatible with all Heroscape Master Sets and Expansions.  Whether shredding enemy lines with his special attack or surging forward to keep the pressure on, Scavorith thrives on chaos, making him a game-changing force in your arsenal and introduces lethal master side abilities such as the Battle Surge and Double Burst Special Attack!

This is add-on content. A Battle Box and/or Master Set is required for gameplay.

Contents
1 Fully Assembled Premium Miniature Scavorith, Lord of Ruin
1 Army Card

boardgame: Heroscape: The Lava Fields of Valhalla Terrain Expansion
description:
As though the devastation wrought by Valhalla’s many combatants was insufficient, the very earth itself seems intent to join in the destruction. Where the many magics and machines of battle split the earth, viscous lava fields erupt and spew molten magma into deadly pools. Those who would dare cross these flaming wastelands must tread carefully, or lose more than just their feet.

The Lava Fields of Valhalla Terrain Expansion  expansion contains over 120 hexes and is fully compatible with all Heroscape Master Sets and Expansions. Transform your battlefield into a deadly volcanic wasteland, where every step could mean survival or destruction. Molten lava tiles and lava field spaces introduce strategic risk-reward mechanics, forcing players to think carefully about movement and positioning with its bold visual impact and high-stakes gameplay mechanics.

Molten Lava:

    Basic game: Figures may not move onto Molten Lava spaces.
    Master Game: Treat molten lava spaces like water spaces except for the following:


When a figure moves onto a Molten Lava space, you must roll for molten lava damage.

    Rolling for Molten Lava Damage: Roll the 20-sided die. If you roll a 20, your figure miraculously survived. If you roll a 1-19, your figure is destroyed.
    Flying: Figures with the special power Flying may fly over molten lava without rolling for molten lava damage.


--
Lava Field:

    No special rules for BASIC GAME
    Master game: At the end of a round, all figures still on lava field spaces must roll to see if they take lava field damage. Double-Spaced figures with one space on a lava field tile must roll for lava field damage. Double-Spaced figures that occupy two lava field spaces only roll once for lava field damage.
    Rolling for Lava Field Damage: In turn order, all players must roll for all their figures on lava field spaces. Roll one combat die. if you roll a skull, that figure takes a wound. Otherwise it is safe. Players may choose what order to roll for their own figures. 


This is add-on content. A Battle Box and/or Master Set is required for Heroscape gameplay.

Before the grand clockwork towers of Noxdorma can climb from its rolling plains, the land’s finest minds must plan their rise. Locations must be chosen, building materials gathered and measured, crews hired or drafted, and the land itself prepared to host a new wonder.

Sometimes preparing the land involves removing its inhabitants. After all, pesky homes and irritating wildlife stand in the way of progress. Decker specializes in this sort of work. Among the people of Noxdorma, his notorious skill at calculated demolition earned him the nickname “Burrowbreaker.” Here on Valhalla, he is the perfect choice to scout locations for Volarak’s ambitious designs. And clear them, if necessary.

Decker the Burrowbreaker was an Organized Play promo by playing in local game store tournaments in June and July of 2025! Later available directly from the publisher's website.

boardgame: Heroscape: Raelin the Kyrie Warrior Promo
description:
Raelin the Unshackled, Raelin the Free, Raelin the Reckoning. These names are whispered among the armies of Jandar and Utgar alike as Raelin takes flight alongside Loviatäk and makes for Utgar’s throne. And as she does, she remembers the years this path has taken.

She did not expect to find Loviatäk, daughter of Taelord, during those dark days in Utgar’s camps. Her father had aided Utgar in laying waste to Valhalla, but Loviatäk speaks of building a stronger future for the clans of the Kyrien mountains. Raelin’s bond with her has become strong over a year of flying into battle side by side, and their goals align for one final mission: to bring vengeance to Utgar’s door, and to bring him to justice for all that he has taken from Raelin and from Valhalla.

1 pre-painted miniature and 1 card.

—description from the publisher

Elemana Chronicles: Shrine Wars is a MOBA-inspired miniature arena game featuring alternating activations, hassle-free setup and requiring only three miniatures per player to play.

Each round, players take turns activating their Hero and Monster models to vanquish their foes and seize control of the Mana Shrines. As the game progresses, the Heroes and Monsters level up and gain powerful items, culminating in a finale in Round 6. The player with the most Mana Essence at the end of Round 6 wins the game.

—description from the designer

The Wild Gardens: Foraging Club expansion adds four new playable characters and eight unique game-changing player abilities to Wild Gardens.

In this expansion, each player will start with their own unique character, now equipped with their own special abilities that help them in their efforts to become the best forager! Foraging Club also includes a new gameplay variant, Magic Tour Bus, where players start with ingredients and Serve and Cook happen simultaneously which speeds things up for players who prefer a shorter game.

You are the owner of one of the most evocative resorts in the North of Sardinia and you must manage your spaces in this incredible stretch of coastline. Make your stay special in this piece of paradise and if, at the end of the summer, your Resort will be the most famous in Sardinia, you will gain victory leaving an indelible memory in the heart of your guests.

A simple but deep worker placement game where each action will require a different amount of time to be performed, forcing your workers to stay still for multiple rounds. Build bungalows, hire staff members, book and welcome tourists; find the right time to earn Money and invest It to fulfill tourists' wishes, make your Resort achieve the most fame and win the game at the end of the seventh round.

In 2010 Sun, Sea & Sand made a name for itself, introducing Corné van Moorsel as a cult author. Baja Sardinia resumes the original game and, after a new development, brings it again in a more modern guise and an astonishing artwork: multiple ways to earn money and fame points add more depth to every choice and the addition of different characters grants high replayability without extending game length.

-description from developer

What does it take to put together a Mardi Gras Parade?  Will your Krewe be the first to build a complete parade? Using Bands, Marching Groups, Dance Teams, and of course Floats construct your very own Mardi Gras Parade!

Be the first to build a 10 Unit Parade using the Unit Cards while avoiding breakdowns, cold weather, and the dreaded RAINOUT!
Organizing a Mardi Gras Parade is incredibly complex.  Geaux Parade captures a glimpse of that complexity and excitement while you compete against friends and family to be the first to build your very own parade.

Players: 2-4 | Time: 15-20 Minutes | Ages: 8 and up (Rule option for younger players)

To start the game each player is dealt 7 Unit Cards. The Action Cards and remaining Unit Cards are placed face down in the center of the table.  The top Unit Card is turned over and placed next to the Unit Card pile.  This becomes the discard pile. On your turn you may draw either the top face up or face down Unit Card.

Each turn consists of Three Actions:
Unit Card – Start your turn by drawing a Unit Card and adding to your hand.
Action Card – Draw an Action Card and complete the action if able.  
Build Parade – On your turn you can play as many Unit Cards as they would like to build or add to your Parade on the table face up in front of you. If you do not add to your Parade and have more than seven Unit Cards in your hand you must discard a Unit Card to end your turn.

To win the game you must build a 10 or more Unit long Parade.  All restrictions must be met for all cards in the Parade.  It may require more than 10 Unit Cards to meet all restrictions on the cards.

Unit Cards: There are 70 Unit Cards consisting of 10 Bands, 10 Dance Teams, 40 Floats, and 10 Marching Groups.  These Unit Cards will make up each player’s hand to start and be used to build a parade. Many Unit Cards have restrictions on how they can be played.  The Unit Cards and the restrictions are color matched.

Action Cards: There are 40 Action Cards that define an action that the player will make on each turn.  Actions consist of Discards, Draws, Steals, Passes, and the disastrous RAINOUT!

Special Cards:
Tandem Floats - Some of the Float Cards are Tandem Floats.  Tandem Floats must be played in a combination of at least two cards.  It may be possible to play three or even four Tandem Floats in combination, but all restrictions on these Tandem Float Cards must also be met. The Tandems do not have to match thematically.
First Float/Last Float - Some of the Float Cards will have the restriction of First Float or Last Float.  If you use a card with this restriction, it must be the first or last float in your parade respectively. You do not have to start/end the parade with one of these cards.

No-one is certain how old the world of Valhalla truly is. It is a place filled with magic, knowledge, and ancient secrets. While the proud Valkyrie Generals fight to find and secure the Wellsprings, more common folk turn their attention to other impressive prizes: ancient artifacts of terrible power that can be uncovered in the most unlikely of places. With rewards ranging from blessings of strength to bolts of instant death, only a fool would pass up the opportunity to claim a glyph for themself.

Expand your arsenal and battle for Valhalla with the Herp. Choose your scenario, build your battlefield, select your army, and fight to win!

This is add-on content. A Battle Box and/or Master Set is required for Heroscape gameplay.

33 permanent and temporary glyphs, treasure glyphs, and ancient artifact glyphs including the Glyph of the Bolt of Witherwood!

其惟春秋 ("Through the Spring and Autumn") is a card-driven war game for up to six players, blending historical realism with deep strategic gameplay. Step into the roles of the era's most remarkable powers: the Zhou royal court, the formidable states of Chu, Jin, Qi, and Qin, and the influential School of Confucius. Command armies, forge alliances, navigate diplomacy, and expand your dominion. Recruit brilliant statesmen, wage wars of conquest, and contend for supremacy—but beware, for those who rise too high risk becoming the enemy of all. Victory demands more than conquest alone. Territory and prestige are vital, but wisdom and restraint are just as crucial. A ruler who overreaches may be scorned by heaven and cast down in ruin.

The core system and gameplay of Through the Spring and Autumn are inspired by classics like Here I Stand and Virgin Queen. The designers expand upon the card-driven genre with a wealth of mechanics reflecting the rich heritage of pre-Qin China, such as coalition gathering, folk song collecting, court summoning, talent seeking, fighting against ancient tribes, and a divination system based on the I Ching's sixty-four hexagrams.

—description from the designer

Mythiko is a trick-taking, shedding card game where you play as a god of Ancient Greece, influencing humanity in Music, Literature, Tragedies, and Science—but make sure to take credit for your divine gifts!

The game unfolds over five ages (or three for a shorter game). In each age, players compete to empty their hands by playing sets of cards that must be higher than the previous set played. The player who plays the highest set before all others pass starts the next round. This continues until all but one player have played all their cards.

Scoring:

The first player to go out earns the most points.
Other players who go out later earn progressively fewer points.
The last player remaining with cards gets zero points for that age.
Each god has a unique power, granting special abilities that provide strategic advantages.

While only certain cards are worth points, the key to victory lies in shedding cards efficiently and winning tricks with high-scoring cards. After five ages, the player with the most victory points is crowned the ultimate deity!

YOU HAVE THIRTEEN HOURS IN WHICH TO SOLVE THE LABYRINTH, BEFORE YOUR BABY BROTHER BECOMES ONE OF US, FOREVER

In this magical new twist on a classic board game, you can explore the fantasy world of Jim Henson's Labyrinth and help rescue Toby from Jareth's clutches. It's a Cluedo mystery like you've never solved before as you try and discover where Toby is hidden how Jareth has protected him and who must face the Goblin King and rescue Sarah's baby brother.

Do you need to defeat a snake in the Bog of Eternal Stench to save Toby? Or must you break the enchantment of a crystal to save Sarah's brother?

SO THE LABYRINTH IS A PIECE OF CAKE? LET'S SEE HOW YOU HANDLE THIS LITTLE SLICE.

Along the way, you will face other challenges in your adventure, from trouble with the Fireys to riddles set by the Four Guards. Nothing is what it seems in the Labyrinth, so keep an eye on the doors and collect staircases to make your final accusation and see if you have figured out how to save Toby.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

boardgame: Heroscape: Valkyrie Trials – Gulløx Wing Render Glyph Promo
description:
Beginning in June 2025 - Organized Play will have Valkyrie Trials that players can compete and win prizes in.

A promotional prize of the Gulløx Wing Render Glyph game piece with rules summary Army Card can be won.

Unlike the figure promos, this glyph will not be purchasable as a separate item, but will be included in a future product.

Gulløx Wing Render
Permanent Treasure Glyph - While attacking an adjacent figure with a normal attack, you may re-roll one die that did not show a skull.

On the hunt for fame and priceless treasures, groups of adventurers explore the legendary temple at Luxor. Their ultimate goal is the tomb of the pharaoh, but many treasures can be found as they search?

As the parties explore, the challenge unfolds: The player who manages to quickly get their team of adventurers to the tomb, while salvaging as many treasures as possible, will be the winner.

Luxor offers variable game play – the path that leads to the tomb is different for every game, and can even change during play!

A fast-paced family game with an innovative movement mechanism and an ever-changing game board.

Includes:
Luxor
Luxor: The Mummy's Curse 
Luxor: Queenie 1 – Swarm of Scarabs
Luxor: Queenie 2 – The Snake God's Boon
Luxor: Queenie 3 – Secret Missions
Luxor: Queenie 4 – The Treasure Chamber
Luxor: Exclusive Queenie (Queenie 5)
Luxor: The Gift of Luxor (Queenie 6)
New Surprise Queenie

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

boardgame: Fallout: Miniatures – Wasteland Wildlife Rules Packet
description:
In the Wasteland Wildlife Rules Packet, you’ll find everything you need to pick up and play the miniatures released alongside it: the Mega Sloth and Snallygasters!

Each Rules Packet varies slightly, but will always contain the following:

     Unit Card and AI for the new miniatures for use in Fallout: Wasteland Warfare.
     Vault-Tec Approved Profiles for use in Fallout: Wasteland Warfare.
     Faction Cards for both the new and old Factions in Fallout: Wasteland Warfare.
     A new Scenario for Fallout: Wasteland Warfare using your new miniatures.


In addition, Rules Packets may include:

     Rules for the new miniatures to be used in Fallout: Factions.
     New Region Tables for use in Fallout: Wasteland Warfare.
     New Weapons and Items for use in Fallout: Wasteland Warfare'.
     New Rules for Fallout: Wasteland Warfare and Fallout: Factions.
     Errata Updates for Fallout: Wasteland Warfare and Fallout: Factions (these will also be attached to our standard Errata document releases).
     And more not already listed!


—Description from the publisher

Have you always wanted your own zoo? Now’s your chance! Fantasy Zoo is a fast-paced, easy-to-understand card game where you build unique enclosures and populate them with magical, imaginary creatures.

Build your dream zoo in Fantasy Zoo!

Forest, mountain, desert, and aquatic enclosures are waiting for their residents – cute, dangerous, or incredibly rare beings! The animal cards and enclosures are on both sides of the same card, so you’ll need to make clever decisions: expand your zoo or welcome a new resident?

Language-independent and with easy-to-understand rules, you can dive into the game right away. But if you're looking for a real challenge, the advanced game mode offers extra excitement!

—description from the designer

Mindig is szerettél volna egy saját állatkertet? Most itt a lehetőség! A Fantasy Zoo egy pörgős, azonnal érthető kártyajáték, amelyben egyedi kifutósorokat építesz, és varázslatos, képzeletbeli állatokkal népesíted be őket.

Építsd meg álmaid állatkertjét a Fantasy Zooban!

Erdei, hegyi, sivatagi és vízi kifutók várják lakóikat – cuki, veszélyes vagy éppen elképesztően ritka lényeket! Az állatkártyák és kifutók ugyanazon kártya két oldalán találhatóak, így ügyesen kell döntened: bővíted az állatkerted vagy inkább egy új lakó érkezik?

Nyelvfüggetlen és gyorsan érthető szabályok garantálják, hogy azonnal belevethesd magad a játékba, de ha igazi kihívásra vágysz, a haladó játékmód extra izgalmakat tartogat!

—description from the designer (translatred)

The mysterious ore "Roots" holds the power to manifest imagination, plunging a country into war when dragon-people use it to storm the capital. To fight back, an organization extracts ink from the ore and creates I-mage, a doctrine of magical artwork.

Eat/Paint is a dueling card game where players pick between the vastly different playstyles of Dragon or Mage, then dive into a tactical battle that can be symmetrical or asymmetrical based on the roles chosen.

As a Dragon, use Roots to energize and reshape your body, but build carefully since Dragons start fragile. As a Mage, use Ink to summon an army, but be frugal as running out of Ink means a lengthy refill. Select your color of Roots or Ink to further customize your gameplan, then out-think your opponent and realize your victory!

Astro Asterid needs help repairing derelict space ships! Help her manage resources to fix key system in this fast paced mint tin game!

Build randomized ships with key system parts and corridors, and then use the deck of cards to determine which key systems are in need of repair. Gather resources from storage rooms and use them to fix systems quickly. But be careful! Key systems will continue to malfunction as you play, and the more resources you hold, the slower you will be!

Can you help Asterid fix the ship before resources run out?

—description from the designer

Add 20 scoring goals for even more gameplay variety in Goblin Vaults! The 10 cards are double-sided, with a unique scoring goal on each side. 1 card was previously released as a stand-alone card called Chaos & Order. The scoring cards feature new types of card patterns and different objectives.

Just shuffle them with your existing goal cards during setup and you're ready to play!

Includes: 4 basic scoring cards, 6 suit scoring cards, 1 rules card, and 1 replacement scoring card to correct a typo in the base game

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Create wonderful memories with a larger group of friends and family enjoying Emerald Skulls together! This expansion adds all the components needed for a 7th and 8th player to play.

The rulebook in the base game explains how to add a 7th and 8th player.

Includes: 4 betting markers, 28 gear tokens, 2 player aid cards.

-- publisher

A hilarious puppeteering twist on the much-loved game of charades. Players split into two teams. Once a player has picked a stick with their charade word, they choose a member of their own team to be their puppet. The player then maneuvers their teammate into various positions to act out the word, getting their team to guess as many words possible within 30 seconds. The puppet teammate does not know what the charade word is, and there's no talking allowed! Each word guessed correctly counts as one point; the team with more points wins at the end of the game, after as many rounds as the players choose. Pack contains 50 double-sided printed sticks.

Fetchez la Vache! 3-in-1 Game Set includes:
Custom-designed beechwood board, 2 dice catapults, 21 meeples (20 human, 1 bovine), 4 dice, 1 doubling cube, 30 checkers,  and rules for THREE games.

1 - Fetchez la Vache! A madcap backgammon-based game that pits two teams against each other in a race to capture a cow and leave the field with it alive, all while dodging catapulted farm animals and scathing insults French taunts. (Note: this activity requires the Monty Python’s Cocurricular Mediaeval Reenactment Programme core book.)

2 - Squashez l'Anglaise! Flip the board over and let fly with this point-based, historically accurate, terribly violent castle-siege simulator.

3 – Backgammon! A fully functional backgammon board (for those who wish to appear smarter than people who play checkers but can’t be arsed with chess).

Specifications and components:
A custom-designed and illustrated birchwood game board.
Approximate dimensions: Open: 17.13" long x 21.65" wide x 1.48" high (43.5cm x 55cm x 3.75cm); Closed: 17.13" long x 10.83" wide x 1.48" high (43.5cm x 27.5cm x 3.75cm).
21 printed wooden meeples of varying shapes and designs (knights, priests, retainers, and the like...plus the cow, of course).
2 fully functional and highly dangerous to mice wooden dice catapults.
A pair of woefully under tested, wonderfully launchable dice.
A pair of cow bags for storage. Storage of game components. Not recommended for cows.
Oh and all the bits for backgammon, too: 15 black checkers, 15 white checkers, and 1 doubling cube
Castle wall divider for increased difficulty and more realistic farm animal trajectories.

What is it about?
The aim of Würfelakrobat Brüche is to see who can present the best solution after rolling the dice on calculation methods.

The players define any dice as the solution dice. With the 
dice, they now try to find the best solution using the four basic arithmetic operations addition, subtraction, division, multiplication (plus, minus, times), divided) to get as close as possible to the value of the result cube.

Important: Anyone can choose any solution cube (as well as different ones).

It is also permitted to use imaginary brackets (keyword: division and multiplication before addition and subtraction).

How does this work?
The dice are taken out of the box. Everyone agrees on a 
time limit. One player rolls all the dice at the same time. The 
dice remain as they were rolled - and everyone keeps their fingers fingers where they are. Now all players search for possible ways to calculate as described above. After the time has elapsed each player presents their solution. The player whose calculation result comes closest solution cube receives a point (chip). This can be be more than one player.

Example

Solution cube = 3/4; other cubes = 1/2 1/2 1/8 1/6

Calculation method:
 1/2 : 1/2 = 1 * 1/8 : 1/6 = 6/8 reduced by 2 = 3/4, in this case the result of the solution cube is achieved.

—description from the designer

The players roll the 4 paw dice to score points. They can re-roll any of the dice twice to avoid annoying blue and orange paws and/or to achieve better combinations of the paws on top.

—description from the designer

Seeking the truth behind the discovery of "Roots" and the Dragon invasion, two new fighters enter the fray. Their struggles will change the fate of the battle for the country.

As the first expansion for Eat/Paint, Out of jinX adds two new characters and two new colors, opening up further possibilities.

The dragon Elt gains bonuses from keeping special cards in his heat zone, while the mage Miko uses her discard pile for summoning and power. Black coloration excels in card and resource manipulation, while White coloration is focused on costly actions and building field presence.

These additions bring the number of possible deck combinations from 12 to 30, allowing players vastly more expression in their route to victory.

Here's the scoop! This ice cream cone is melting fast in this Ice Cream Meltdown Game.

The first player to place all their toppings in the ice cream slime and add the cherry wins. But watch out for that fly - yuck! When the ice cream starts to drip, you gotta be quick.

Product features:

Includes: 1x Gameboard, 1x Ice Cream Cone, 1x Cone Stand, 1x Ice Cream Slime, 1x Spinner, 18x Slime Charms, Instructions
Ice Cream Slime that really melts!
Land on a fly and it goes in the ice cream instead of your sprinkle
Win the game by adding your sprinkles and finishing with the cherry on top

Poor Jack planted a magic bean he received from a stranger. From there, all the adventures went quickly... A dangerous climb to the top towards a castle hidden in the clouds. Hiding from a giant. Finding the hen that lays the golden eggs. What an adventure it was! But now it’s time to escape the castle by getting as many golden eggs as possible. Can you outwit the angry giant and free Jack?

HOW TO PLAY:

     Reveal as many bean cards as possible.
     Be careful with repeated cards.
     You will win by getting the most points for having bean cards and golden eggs.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Jack and the Beanstalk

description:
Mythic Wars is an epic strategy board game where players lead powerful gods and their factions in a battle for dominance over the World.

Each turn, players issue strategic orders by deploying gods onto the map and taking actions such as moving armies, engaging in battles, gathering resources, recruiting units and constructions.

The game unfolds over five ages, with worldwide events affecting players’ decisions. The game ends when a player gathers enough Cosmic Tears through battle or various missions. Careful planning, tactical combat, and strategic diplomacy are key to achieving ultimate rule over the realm.

—description from the publisher

Kill Team: Brutal and Cunning pits the belligerent Ork Wrecka Krew against a band of wily Ratling marksmen in a tense battle in the Undermuzzle Barrens. There’s a third drop of kill teams and datacards to round out the available teams, while Lieutenant Titus makes his debut as a solo release, and mechanical horrors beyond mortal comprehension arrive in Legions Imperialis.

Kill Team: Brutal and Cunning contains two kill teams. The Wrecka Krew is made up of a Boss Nob, five Orks, and two Bomb Squigs, while there are 10 Ratling operatives and a battlemutt lead by a Ratling Fixer. It also includes Killzone Upgrade: Compound Siege, comprising two bunkers, six fire steps and six stockades for use with Killzone: Volkus. The 72-page softback Brutal and Cunning dossier is packed with lore for the mission and kill teams, plus rules for the Wrecka Krew and Ratlings, and the Volkus Compound Mission Pack contains PvP and PvE scenarios. You’ll also get all the datacards and tokens you need for both kill teams, as well as an Ork transfer sheet with 438 transfers.

Brutal and Cunning is the first expansion for the latest edition of Kill Team.

—description from the publisher

On planet U, there live some strange beings, called komupples, who love some very curious fruits called upples. These fruits have a shape similar to a square apple, of various colors, which grow from the earth, and in many cases have very peculiar effects on these beings. Have fun changing shape, jumping, eating dozens of delicious upples and being the most gluttonous.
Millions of possible configurations await you, where thanks to your intelligence and a pinch of luck, you will test whether you are the biggest upple eater.

Upples is an original strategy game, for 1 to 4 players. In which you will have to eat as many super upples as you can, while planning strategies, clever movements and fun combinations of jumps. At the same time, you compete with your friends and opponents for the most delicious upples.

The objective of the game is to eat as many super upples as you can while using the best strategy of jumps and card combinations, to be the player with the most super upple points.

Starting with the youngest person at the table and going counter-clockwise (only the first time, in subsequent games the first person must rotate). Each player has one turn, which can be extended as many times as they are able to eat or use super upples or special upples that allow an extra jump. The super upples obtained are kept by each player, and the rest of the tokens are returned to the top of the box, face up. And only if the box runs out of tokens, they will be available again, shuffling them and placing them face down beforehand.

In each valid jump, the player will “eat” the tokens on which they fall and remove them from the board (except for tunnels, or other players' character tokens).

At the end of each turn, each player is responsible for ensuring that there are no gaps left on the playing field behind them before the next player's turn begins. In other words, if the pieces are eaten or removed, they must be replaced by randomly drawing new pieces from the box at the end of each turn.

If a player is unable to make any moves in their current state, their turn ends and they are transformed into the opposite state. That is, if their state is shy (white komupple), they will become angry (red komupple with red hair), and if their current state is angry (red komupple), they will become shy (white komupple with white hair). If they are unable to make any moves in the next turn, even in this new character state, they will be swapped with the opposite card on the playing field (for example, if they were in a corner, they will be swapped with the card in the opposite corner, losing their turn again).

In GLUTTON MODE, the game ends when the cards in the box are finished and the last card is placed on the playing field. The person or team that has managed to eat the most super upples, taking into account their value, wins the game, adding up the total of their points.

There are 4 crazy game modes, it is designed for up to 4 players or teams, from 8 years old and up:

In training mode, you will have the opportunity to practice the best combination jumping strategies. Designed for a single player.

In glutton mode, with 2 to 4 players or teams, you will have to show who is the biggest upple eater on planet U.
In naughty mode, in addition to eating as many upples as you can, you can take some of the collected upples from other players by jumping over them.

In survival mode, you must collect as many upples as possible as quickly as possible, before a golden super upple is eaten, and the player with the fewest super upples on the table is eliminated.

—description from the publisher

Black Diamond is a quick solitaire game played with a traditional deck of cards. Place cards from the black suits (clubs, spades) to a diamond-shaped grid around four diamond cards, following these placement rules: suits must alternate, and the values must equal the sum, difference, product, or quotient or any number of touching diamond cards. Be careful, though - whenever a card limit has been reached or there are no legal moves, precious new diamond cards must be added to the grid. If you run out of the diamonds, it's game over, but if you manage to place all black cards to the diamond, you win!

The game is a linguistic and associative card game focused on exploring Ukrainian dialects. Players take turns acting as the leader, who selects a card with a dialect word and provides an associative hint without directly revealing the meaning. Other players choose a card from their hand that best matches the hint, and all selected cards are shuffled and laid out for voting.

The objective is to guess the leader's card correctly while also misleading other players into choosing your card. Players score points based on successful guesses and how well their own cards deceive others. The game continues through multiple rounds, with each player having a turn as the leader. The winner is the player with the most points, accumulated by correctly identifying words and making strategic choices.
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
Be the first to 1000 Miles/Kilometers.  Your favorite and classic card game only faster.  Different attacks, different defenses and different rules to amp up the fun.  Also a 300 Miles/Kilometer card to really speed up your travels.

New cards include: Accelerator, Review mirror, u-Turn, Inspection, Theft, Power outage, and Pollution Alert.
A promotional, stripped-down version of Qui est-ce? (Guess Who?) with minimal components. Part of a set of mini Hasbro games.
Using three piles of illustrated, textless cards (one for characters, the second for magical items and the third for locations), players take turns creating a shared story.

Inspired by the game For the Queen, Sur la Route is a card-based story-building game.

Our group get on board a vehicle heading to the destination of our dreams. From the preparations to the final destination, we get to know each other while making surprising encounters along the way. Some of us may well make surprising revelations about themselves along the way.

The game is played as follows: turning clockwise, we take turns drawing a card and reading the text out loud. We carry out the instructions, then discard it. Then, it is up to us to decide whether we progress towards the destination by advancing the pawn representing our vehicle.

The position of said pawn indicates the progress of our journey and the deck from which to draw. The game will end when it reaches the "End of the Journey" space. When this happens, we will read the corresponding card to conclude our story.
Be the first player to score 200 points by moving as many of your marbles as possible across the board to your home zone and to have the fewest marbles captured by your opponent!

Bobail is a two-player strategy board game played on a square board with a 5x5 grid.

Each player has five tokens of different colors that start on the baseline of the board closest to them. A single token of a third color called the Bobail starts in the center of the board. To win, either surround the Bobail or move it to your baseline. On a turn, a player does these two actions in this order:

Move the Bobail: Move it one square in any direction; if you can't move it, you lose (because the other player has surrounded it).
Move one of your tokens: Move it any direction to the furthest square possible without leaping over other tokens.
On the first turn, the starting player skips the Bobail move.

Dungeon Crawl is a game of selfish teamwork and rolls of the dice.

Enter the depths of this dangerous labyrinth as one of four mighty heroes. Players will battle 4 random monsters to reach the end of the dungeon. On their turn, players will make attack and damage rolls. Monsters and characters will progress as the game goes on, with players getting chances to rest, heal and collect loot cards along the way. Room Cards, After World Cards, Ally Cards and D20 Cards all add an extra twist to your game.

There are two ways to win: 1. Being the player with the most coins, or 2. The last player standing after a final Player vs Player battle.

—description from the designer

Aetherdrift is a racing-themed expansion for Magic: The Gathering that takes place across three worlds: the aether-powered capital city of Avishkar, the undead-infested dunes of Amonkhet, and the volcanic jungles of Muraganda. Ten teams of racers compete for a one-of-a-kind trophy: the Aetherspark, an artifact imbued with the power of a planeswalker spark that allows its bearer to traverse the Blind Eternities without the need for a planar portal.

The set is packed with vehicles and mounts, and introduces the new "Start your engines!" mechanic, which allows players to build up speed over time to power up their cards. Two commander decks were also released alongside the main booster set: "Living Energy," themed around the energy mechanic and the plane of Avishkar (formerly known as Kaladesh); and "Eternal Might," themed around zombies and the plane of Amonkhet.

boardgame: Shards of the Jaguar: Kickstarter Exclusive Elements
description:
A ziplock bag with the following Kickstarter-exclusive items:


    String bag
    Special Rituals module
    Event module
    Safe Route solo scenario

An inside joke generator, masked as a competitive game based around the ritual of family dinner conversations.

The Experience:
All players are always involved – Every round, each player compares everyone at the table and categorizes them.

Players are incentivized to make hard choices - Matching at 1, 2, or 3 place settings gets each player 1, 2, or 4 points. Because of the point structure, players are incentivized to ensure people are ranked more or less every round.

Every round, players laugh! - Every round players either reveal how they think, or get to learn new things about the other players – all of which makes players laugh.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tank Aces of WWII: Lafayette Pool – 28 Squad to Company level scenarios
description:
Tank Aces of WWII is a new series from Britton Publishers that focuses on the careers of famous tank commanders. 
Each one will examine remarkable events from their combat careers in stand-alone scenarios.

Players will understand the skill required to survive on the battlefield and, in some cases, against seemingly overwhelming odds.

Realistic small unit tactics should result in accurate military outcomes.

However, certain liberties regarding force ratios are taken for the sake of playability. 
 In these scenarios, the rules should be applied logically.  Although the rules may not specifically address every possibility, common sense should always prevail.  
Whenever a rule seems to contradict common sense, abandon the rule.

Description from the publisher

Lafayette Green Pool (July 23, 1919 – May 30, 1991) was an American tank-crew and tank-platoon commander in World War II and is widely recognized as the US tank ace of aces.

Free print and play brigade to division level game about Jubal Early's sudden out of nowhere surprise attack on Washington DC, down the *eastern* side of the Potomac River.
Fords along the Monocacy River to be discovered.  Bridges can be burned - across the Potomac terminally.
Historical forts are present as well as a tiny but thin/tall President Lincoln, for decoration. :-) And note on the Net "The Fort Stevens Incident" by the Jack Miller Center.
Lots of Fog of War and panic and frustration on both sides.
9 turns.  8 miles per hex. Usual I go, you go turn sequence with my trademark Disruption mechanic enabling momentum of advance.
"Winning: Rebels holding WashDC at end of any Union turn wins them strategic victory worth 2 games.  If they don’t, Union wins the/1 game. But if Union has (had) 2 more divisions than Rebs, it’s a draw.
Turn Sequence – each side, Rebs first then Union: Bridges burned? (75%, or 25% if enemy on other end.) All moves (with fording attempts), then attacks with any tactical advances into defended hex (only) with possible enemy Recovery from Disruption at that point.  Supply line check. Victory check.  Opponent’s turn next.
“Stacking” and Operational Movement:   Units move separately.  Maximum of 3 pieces in a hex – never more – but only 2 may be infantry divs, and +1 Movement Factor to stack and +1MF to attack. No ZOCs. Inf and arty move 2 hexes, cavalry 4 - +1 if along 2 uninterrupted road hexes. Emrgncy Div can only be in Wash or a fort.  Units cannot move or attack across un-bridged or un-forded river, but may at cost of +1MF may move/attack across un-forded/un-bridged creek."
Grant had no use for Lew Wallace - the post bellum author of Ben Hur - after Shiloh, but Wallace saved the Capital with his early, irritating delaying action.
The battle is the subject of a very good and accurate LionHeart film No Retreat from Destiny for which I have a review on IMDb.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Heroes Placement (originally named ヒーローズプレイスメント in Japanese, abbreviated as HP or ヒロプレ) is a core-oriented TCG for girls developed by Silverblitz, featuring customs, landscapes, and local specialties from various regions of Japan.

Players can choose one card from a variety of 1-cost cards as their starting card and build their own deck (which could be 40 cards, the standard 50 cards, or even 100 cards depending on the situation). At the start of the game, they draw six cards from the top of their deck face down to form their field, which represents their health. You win the game when you destroy all six of your opponent's fields or when your opponent's deck runs out.

boardgame: Hellboy: The Board Game – Baba Yaga Monster Booster
description:
Additional boss Baba Yaga.
The expansion introduces rules for surprise threats. These cards introduce dangerous enemies (even bosses) into play without any warning. The surprise threat from this expansion adds Baba Yaga as far away from agents as possible, but no further than two rooms away from the agent. Baba Yaga does not activate until an enemy disturbs her. The boss uses ranged attacks (the first 5 attacks with a range of 8, the following with a range of 5) and instead of melee attacks, flies away from agents to a ranged attack distance.

boardgame: Hellboy: The Board Game – Hecate Monster Booster
description:
Additional boss for the game.
The expansion introduces rules for surprise threats. These cards introduce dangerous enemies (even bosses) into play without warning.
The surprise threat from this expansion adds Hecate to an area with as many witches as possible. If there are no such areas, Hecate appears near the main agent with two witches. If there are no witches on the field at the end of the round, Hecate and her surprise threat card are removed from play.
Hecate can use a ranged attack against one agent in her line of sight, or a melee attack against all agents in her area. If at least one point of damage is dealt, the agents are stunned.

The Introspection Expansion adds a new layer of depth to the asymmetrical mechanics for each Olympian of Hybris, Disordered Cosmos.
It's a new difficulty mode that makes winning through enhancements more challenging and more controllable between players.

The Titan Pack adds 12 new Primordials with various and mysterious powers!
Add these primordials to the existing ones for a new experience during your game sessions!

—description from the publisher

The Primordial Promo Pack includes unique game-changing Primordial for your games!

—description from the publisher

Leap to victory!

Join the action as four frogs hop across a pond, leaping over hungry crocodiles and chasing down glowing fireflies.

In this fast-paced, puzzle-like board game, you’ll need to think quick and outwit your opponents to guide your frog to victory.

Be the first to collect three fireflies of your matching colour and return to your lily pad to win. But be quick, as turns are on a timer, so you must be quick on (or off) your feet.

Do you have what it takes to outmanoeuvre your opponents? Jump in and find out!

—description from the publisher

Welcome the Sparkling Seas in the golden age of piracy!

The Pirate Code is a deck-building sandbox game where you are left to chart your own storied legacy. Each card in your hand presents you with a compelling choice - do you leverage their resource icons, allowing you to complete contracts, attack others, bury treasure, resupply, and more? Or do you discard them to move, potentially activating their powerful and unique ability as well? This choice underpins each turn as you wrestle with how best to utilize your crew.

No two victories in The Pirate Code look exactly alike. There are many avenues to make a name for yourself, and as Captain, you’ll need to decide which are worth your time.

There are a variety of contracts, which offer you a mixture of Victory Points, Coins, and Reputation for completing them. Coins can be buried for victory points, and other players can attempt to dig them up. Reputation is checked several times during the game, awarding points to the most Infamous players.

When the time is right, you may even be willing to turn your cannons onto another player and attempt to procure their cargo in a way that befits a real pirate. If they’ve got nothing worth stealing, you can always blow a few holes in their hull and sail away to safety.

Improve the efficiency of the crew through leadership and sometimes, tough love. Spend Hearts to mitigate randomness. Red hearts allow you to draw new crew, letting you cultivate the perfect crew for the job. Black hearts allow you to reroll dice, turning a missed shot or failed gamble into a winning one. Leveraging these hearts will cause your Captains Log to advance, awarding new abilities to your crew based on your style of leadership.

—description from the publisher

TIMEOUT Blue Edition is a new edition of TIMEOUT

TIMEOUT is an intense and dynamic trivia game, where time is an essential factor. The playeres have a limited time to answer their questions, which gives an engaging and fun game, with an intensity that can't matched by other trivia games.

At the start of the game, each player is allocated an agreed amount of minutes. Think fast as times ticks down for each question. The fast you answer, the less time you lose. If you can't answer you automatically lose 20 seconds, and your opponents can earn extra time by answering your question.

Last player to run out of time is the winner of the game.

With 2,800 all new questions.

In Malagasy, four tribes live together peacefully, in symbiosis with nature. Spirit guides on the player boards help players explore the island and the ideal locations of the majestic baobabs that are considered sacred. During your turn, you can discover a new territory by placing a new biome tile receiving the favour of the spirits on the corresponding tracks or you can move your explorer to discover the animals and plants that live with you. When you interact with sea tiles, you collect new culture cards that favour your tribe. During this adventure, don't forget the sacred baobabs: if you can find the best place for each of them according to the patterns on the baobab cards, you will earn additional victory points.

—description from the designer

Promo card marked PP1 that was included in Spielbox magazine #1/2025.

You can move your marker 1 step of any coloured track. At the end of the game, this card will give you 5 VPs.

You step into the paws of a feline deity, leading secret cults of street cats. Collect followers, perform mystical rituals, and strive to become the most revered cat god. The game blends humor, strategy, and a light-hearted take on cult dynamics, all set in a world dominated by fluffy, worshipping felines.

Players start with a small group of devoted cat followers and take turns performing actions such as recruiting more cats, casting ritual cards, and using special items to gain advantages. Rituals award "Honor," the key resource that determines a deity’s status. Strategic use of resources and interactions with other players (whether through cooperation or sabotage) adds depth to the gameplay.

The game continues until a set number of rounds are completed or a player reaches a specific Honor threshold. The one with the most Honor wins, claiming the title of the supreme deity.

BONESWAY: Luminis Refuge is a game where players take on the role of a squad of recruits facing their first mission.

Exploration unfolds through chapters and scenarios, with players earning rewards by solving puzzles that incorporate various game elements such as cards, punchboards, and texts.

Throughout their journey, they will engage in intense battles against monstrous creatures, each controlled by a unique deck based on its type. To survive, they must strategically use weapons and abilities, some of which are designed to work in synergy.

Beyond combat, uncovering the fate of a missing squad, tasked with escorting the village’s population to the Metropolis, will be crucial.

At the end of each chapter, players receive a score that determines their rewards. The overall final score will shape the story’s conclusion, leading to different possible endings.

—description from the publisher

Gain an additional 10 Prime and Apex classes for a total of 25 different Rover evolutions to explore, along with two new full Quest Chains further exploring the wilds of Chorus. Flex your tactical team-building to its limits as you explore Chorus.

An highly intuitive and quick to learn tabletop card game about competitive sandwich building, with the goal being for players to claim the largest number of sandwich cards by the time the deck runs out of cards to play.
It's a fast paced game, taking an average of 7 minutes to learn the rules, and with each round only lasting about 3-5 minutes depending on number of players and if are playing with an added ingredients expansion pack. So play style is based on the assumption of playing multiple rounds in a single sitting.
It's very low barrier for entry make's it appropriate not only as a quick party or travel game, but for those new to tabletop gaming, and for audiences like children and the disabled, as it was made with accessibility in mind.

—description from the designer

Parc La Fontaine is an adaption of Le Plateau for a standard deck of cards.

As in Le Plateau, players create formations on the board, which involves connecting board sides with counters. However, to place a counter on the board, they must win tricks whose cards correspond to the board's spaces.

Currently, Parc La Fontaine consists of four games:

Quartet is a four-player, fixed partnership game similar
to Whist. The number of points your team wins varies directly with the number of sides you connect. However, if your team connects all sides of the board, the other team earns points!

Trio is a three-player game. Before play starts, players bid how many board sides they can connect. The player who wins this auction plays against the other two, who play as a temporary team.

Solo is a four-player adaption of Trio. You can also play it as a fixed-partnership game.

Balance is another four-player, fixed partnership game. Your team has a limited number of counters. If you play all your counters without forming a winning formation, the opposing team automatically wins!

For the print-and-play version, you'll need a standard 52-card deck, one of the boards provided with the rules, two sets of counters of contrasting colours, and a way to keep track of scores.

—description from the designer

Baskaria Challengers is a competitive board game for 2 to 4 players, where each Challenger takes on the role of a skilled character striving for honor and victory. Only the most capable will earn their place among the legendary heroes of Baskaria.

Throughout the game, Challengers must make strategic decisions and manage their resources wisely to complete missions, earning badges that mark their achievements. As they navigate the diverse and perilous lands of Baskaria, they will face challenges that test their skills, adaptability, and determination.

With each passing Red Moon (round), the competition intensifies, pushing Challengers to prove their worth. When the final Red Moon sets, the Challenger with the most badges will claim victory, securing their place among the heroes destined to restore hope to the world.

—description from the publisher

Take on the role of Majin Panda and step into the ring in this exciting solitaire free for all! Choose your style of wrestling and use your skills and wit to out-last and eliminate 48 other wrestlers from the Book It universe! Utilize the corners, push around your opponents, and try not to get trapped!

Book It! Battle Royal is a unique take on solo games like solitaire and Freecell. Move wrestlers around the ring trying to arrange them from highest to lowest, pitting heels against faces, tossing out the lowest numbered wrestler until Majin Panda is the only one left standing!

—description from the publisher

In Rush of Ikorr, you will assume the role of a powerful Avatar, infusing and summoning legendary champions, building iconic locations, and casting mystical spells to claim your dominance in this battle for ultimate possibility! Join two other Avatars to defeat your foes and fight toward a common goal of the Rush of Ikorr.

Rush of Ikorr redefines the TCG experience by blending the allure of ancient mythologies with innovative gameplay. Unique Infusion cards provide the ability for card customization and the option to engage in 3v3 team battles allow for unprecedented strategic depth. This game isn’t just about collecting cards; it’s about crafting legends, making it an ideal experience for anyone who loves innovation, strategy, and epic mythological tales.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game: Fire Uni Starter Deck
description:
