The Fire Uni Starter Deck combines the Attack and Agility of the Fire Neopet with the generosity of the Soup Faerie, Legends, Equipment, and more to battle your opponent and become the CHAMPION of the BATTLEDOME!

Starter deck includes everything for you to get started on your journey into the Neopets Battledome TCG. Both new and seasoned TCG players will enjoy this new take on a trading card game with its unique dice battle mechanic and eye-catching design.

Gather your Neopets, collect your favorite cards, challenge your opponent, and let the adventure begin!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game: Starry Acara Starter Deck
description:
The Starry Acara Starter Deck combines the Defence and Agility of the Starry Neopets with the aide of Aethia the Battle Faerie, Legends, Equipment, and more to battle your opponent and become the CHAMPION of the BATTLEDOME!

Starter deck includes everything for you to get started on your journey into the Neopets Battledome TCG. Both new and seasoned TCG players will enjoy this new take on a trading card game with its unique dice battle mechanic and eye-catching design.

Gather your Neopets, collect your favorite cards, challenge your opponent, and let the adventure begin!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game: 25th Anniversary
description:
It’s time to party! In celebration of the 25th Anniversary of Neopets, the Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game 25th Anniversary Set introduces new variants of the iconic Neopets and card types to commemorate this momentous occasion. This set brings in alternative versions of the adorable Neopets and must-have Paint Brushes by introducing the Silver, Celebration and Baby variations.

New card types: Map Pieces and Locations allow players to discover and experiment with the Lab Ray, transforming your pets into new unique forms like Burlap, Chocolate, Mosaic, Robot and more.

Find these cards and more in booster packs to add to your collection and enhance gameplay while celebrating 25 years of magic and wonder with Neopets.

—description from the publisher

Promo Piece - extra cat that can be used during a game of Nekojima to dangle from a cable.

Rules: 
When a player obtains the Culture district indicated by the
dice or chosen by a player, they must hang the Maneki-neko cat on the cable installed during that turn after placing their Denchuu. If a player obtains the Culture district again, they must then take down the Maneki-neko from its previous location and hang it on the new cable. The Maneki-neko must be taken down and hung after the Denchuu is placed and before any black cats from the base game are hung, if there are any. If, after hanging the Maneki-neko, there is no cable available of the same color as the drawn cube to hang one or more black cats, the active player keeps the black cube(s) for their next turn.

Darkleaf Gambit is a two-player, turn-based, asymmetrical game that takes your most beloved chessboard and transforms it into the mythical forest of Haggedorn.

Begin this classically competitive game by taking control of one of six clans, each offering a unique set of abilities, strengths, and weaknesses. Although champions from one clan to the next share similar shapes—pawns and rooks, knights and queens—the way in which these units move around Haggedorn varies dramatically. Some units protect while others manipulate the terrain while others seek only to destroy.

Each turn, you and your rival roll from a pool of dice to determine what deeds can be performed. Metal-core chips track the status—both boons and curses—of your clan’s units in Haggedorn. Once powered up, cube tokens may be spent to unlock powerful clan skills. Game progress is tracked by each player on a beautifully illustrated dual-layer board.

Maximizing the game’s asymmetry, every clan’s path to victory is also different. You may be forced to protect your king from the ranged attack of the Huldra or a close confrontation with The Murg. Ferrymen seek to push units into the void, while Erde care only for claiming Haggedorn’s sacred territories. Wildero Wibo transform your units into pawns of their own, while Hexen curse the very ground upon which you walk.

Be the first to reach your clan’s victory condition to ascend as the last true power in Haggedorn.

—description from the publisher

In 7 Magics, you and your companions are apprentice sorcerers with a crucial mission: to restore order to the seven ancient magics created by the Great Crystal, now destabilized by Darkness and its minions. To succeed, you must play spell cards, strengthen your wisdom, and engage in epic battles where cunning and coordination are essential. But there’s an extra challenge: communication is limited, and only through observation, memory, cooperation, and the precise execution of magical stances can you enhance your spells and bring balance back to magic. Can you hold back the Darkness before it consumes everything?

—description from the designer

En 7 Magias, tú y tus compañeros son aprendices de hechicería con una misión crucial: restablecer el orden de las siete magias ancestrales creadas por el Gran Cristal, desestabilizadas por la Oscuridad y sus esbirros. Para lograrlo, deberán jugar cartas de hechizos, fortalecer su sabiduría y enfrentarse en batallas épicas donde la astucia y la coordinación serán fundamentales. Pero hay un desafío extra: la comunicación es limitada, y solo mediante la observación, la memoria, la cooperación y la ejecución precisa de posturas mágicas podrán potenciar sus conjuros y restaurar el equilibrio de la magia. ¿Podrán contener la Oscuridad antes de que consuma todo?

Este cierre le da más dramatismo y urgencia, invitando aún más a la inmersión. ¿Te gusta este ajuste o prefieres mantenerlo tal cual?

—description from the designer (Spanish)

In Queremos Show, two teams compete in a wild and fun game of challenges, betting, and speculation. Each round, teams choose a challenge and start betting on how many times they can complete it. The stakes get higher and higher until doubt creeps in, and someone shouts "We Want a Show!" Now, the rival team must prove what they claimed. How far will you push your luck before getting called out?

—description from the designer

En Queremos Show, dos equipos se enfrentan en un alocado y divertido juego de desafíos, apuestas y especulación. En cada ronda, los equipos eligen un reto y empiezan a apostar cuántas veces pueden lograrlo. Las apuestas suben y suben, pero cuando la duda aparece, alguien grita ¡Queremos Show! Ahora, el equipo rival debe demostrar lo que prometió. ¿Hasta dónde te atreves a llevar tu apuesta?

—description from the designer (Spanish)

The aliens are attacking Earth! You’re on a mission to defeat them and destroy their mothership! WILL YOU SAVE THE EARTH?

In a card game reminiscent of vintage video game shooters, in Aliens Attack! you must fire your cannons at advancing waves of invaders! You win if you survive long enough to destroy the mothership after it arrives in the Combat Area.

Aliens Attack! is a solo game that comes with rule for a 2-player variant. There are also 4 different motherships to attack, all of their own strengths and very few weaknesses!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Burum card game combines the perfect balance of strategy and luck, designed to bring energy and excitement to any gathering. Burum is the game that makes Sweeping Fun! With custom card decks tailored to the game’s unique rules, Burum accommodates groups of 3 to 12 players, making it versatile for both intimate settings and lively parties. Its engaging gameplay ensures everyone stays involved, creating memorable experiences every time you play.

Burum is played over several rounds and can be enjoyed for 15 minutes or 2 hours. The object of Burum is to be the first to get rid of all of your cards—both in your hand and also face down and up on the table in front of you. On each turn, you play cards equal to or lower than the one on top of the play pile up to a total of 4 of the same number. If you play a 4th one or the special Sweep card, you get to sweep away the play pile and play again. If you only have higher cards, the pile is yours. Add up the values of your remaining cards at the end of each round. The person with the lowest total at the end of the game is the Winner. Be sure to get rid of your down cards or you may get a high point surprise at the end of the round. Simple to learn, but surprisingly fun!

We believe Burum is more than just a game—it’s a way to connect with loved ones and enjoy meaningful moments together. Whether you’re hosting family game night or simply looking for a fun, interactive way to spend time, Burum is the perfect choice. Add it to your collection today and discover why sweeping the table has never been this exciting.

Your New "Go To" Card Game
-Easy to Learn
-For Ages 8 and Up
-Accommodates groups of 3 to 12 Players
-A perfect balance of strategy and fun
-Easy to transport for play at home or away
-Play for a long or a short time

—description from the publisher

In Damora, players step into a world of shifting alliances, hidden destinies, and high-stakes conflict. Every player has a unique win condition, pushing them to adapt, strategize, and outmaneuver their rivals in a world filled with danger and opportunity.

Players will:

Explore & Expand – Claim territory, gather resources, and construct powerful structures.
Engage in Strategic Combat – Utilize class-based abilities and tactical positioning in fast-paced battles.
Pursue a Hidden Destiny – Each player has a secret win condition that changes their approach to victory.
Face the Dominion Threat – A powerful AI-controlled faction looms over the land, forcing players to decide when to cooperate—or betray.
Experience Evolving Gameplay – Twilight & Story Cards introduce unexpected events that alter the course of battle.
With a mix of territory control, asymmetric strategy, and immersive storytelling, Damora offers an ever-changing gameplay experience where no two matches are the same. Will you rise as the ruler of Damora, or will your empire crumble in the wake of battle?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Дуэль Двух Миров (Duel of Two Worlds)
description:
Дуэль Двух Миров  ("Duel of Two Worlds") is an exciting mix of fast games, references to collectible card games and unusual mechanics for a board game. Turn into an all-powerful magician: put on a long robe, remember a couple of spells and call for help melee fighters or, for example, brave archers who will attack the enemy from afar. Having encountered an enemy on the border of two worlds, one on one, you must use the only chance to prove that you are a powerful magician.

The MOBA (Multiplayer Online Battle Arena) genre is very popular in computer games, but is almost not represented in the tabletop world. Дуэль Двух Миров corrects this unfortunate oversight. You will have to lead an army of fairy-tale creatures, which you will send into battle in the hope of reaching the enemy portal.

The playing field is a segment 6 cells long, at the ends of which there is space for decks of creatures and spells. Each turn, you will take out one creature and send it along the left or right flank towards the opponent's portals. At the end of each turn, you draw spell cards that strengthen your warriors, weaken enemy creatures and help you in every possible way in a difficult battle. The main goal is to reach the opposite edge, and if you fail, then destroy all opponents.

The movement of your army occurs according to the standard scheme: one cell forward at the beginning of the round. Your warriors are bright individuals, and each of them, from the rank and file to the champion, has unique features. Some fly, some can jump from flank to flank, some hide from large creatures due to their small stature. Fans of tricky combinations will have plenty to choose from.

In this fast-paced card game, the first player to get rid of all their cards wins! How? By making color and shape associations with the cards arranged clockwise in the middle of the table to achieve a perfect match. Kudos is a simple family card game in which associations and attacks are decisive for victory.

Winter is coming to Chipmunk's land.
Chipmunks from other village gathered to get some Acorn

숲에 겨울이 찾아왔습니다.
여러 마을의 다람쥐들은 겨울을 대비하여 도토리 원정대를 꾸립니다.
원정대원들은 서로 다른 마을에서 왔기 때문에 서로 협력하기도 하고 배신하기도 할 것입니다.

every turn. Ram(leader of fellowship) see cards from the top of the decks. and play his card face-up
than. other players paly their cards face-down.

open all decks and collet reward.

after that. Ram will be changed Clockwize.

Armed with ingenuity and imagination, children will have to help, through the telling of never-before-heard stories, unlikely heroes on an exciting adventure full of unexpected events and obstacles. The goal of the game is to conquer the story's happy ending and defeat the ultimate antagonist.
To enter the clash with the antagonist, characters will have to pass 4 challenges for each type of obstacle card, for a total of 8 challenges. For each obstacle card earned, players can decide whether to draw an item card, or, think of a new special ability to add to the character, as long as it is consistent.
Overcoming obstacle cards can be done in different ways, depending on the skills and items available to each player.

3 additional Quest promo cards

Q-P1
Your guild is the first to collect ten coins. ("Deine Gilde hat als erste 10 Münzen.")

Q-P2
Your guild is the first to gain a total of seven coins in the income phase. ("Deine Gilde erhält als erste insgesamt 7 Münzen in der Einkommen-Phase.")

Q-P3
In your guild, two rows or columns with adventures have been completed. ("In deiner Gilde sind 2 komplette Reihen/Spalten mit Abenteurern.")

boardgame: Rumpelstilz und seine Freunde spielen Verstecken
description:
A fun game for little people with little people, where the aim is to beat and outwit your opponent as many times as possible. 
You need luck and the right instinct.


     place the tokens on your board in such a way that your opponent cannot see where anything is.
     swap your board with your opponent.
     reveal the tokens one by one. Whether you win or lose points depends on who you reveal.


Ein lustiges Spiel für kleine Leute mit kleinen Leuten, bei dem es darum geht, dem Gegner so viele Schnippchen zu schlagen wie möglich. 
Man braucht Glück dazu und die "richtige Nase".


     Du legst die Spielmarken so auf dein Brett, dass dein Gegner nicht sieht, wo was liegt.
     Tausche dein Brett mit deinem Gegner.
     Deckt nach und nach die Marken auf. Ob du Punkte gewinnst oder verlierst hängt davon ab, wen du aufdeckst.

What year was the first polio vaccination given? Was it before or after the end of the Vietnam War?

How to play:
DRAW AN ANCHOR CARD - Draw the top card of the Years deck and reveal the year on the back – this is the anchor card for the round.
ARRANGE NUMBERS IN ORDER - Either cooperatively or competitively, players attempt to place a series of Cards in the correct order in their timelines in relation to the anchor card.
REVEAL & SCORE! - Score points by placing as many events as possible in the correct chronological order - either win or lose as a team, or crush your competition!

How many spider species are there in Australia? Is that number more or less than the height of Godzilla in feet?

How to play:
DRAW AN ANCHOR CARD - Draw the top card of the Facts deck and reveal the number on the back – this is the anchor card for the round.
ARRANGE NUMBERS IN ORDER - Either cooperatively or competitively, players attempt to place a series of Cards in the correct order in their card line in relation to the anchor card.
REVEAL & SCORE! - Score points by placing as many Facts as possible in the correct numerical order - either win or lose as a team, or crush your competition!

See Shout Spell has 100 letter cards. In a round, 16 cards are dealt out face down in a 4x4 array.  Then everyone helps turn them all face up, and player race to shout out a word that uses the available letters.  If it's a valid word they collect the letters in it for their personal pile.  Players continue calling out and collecting words until no more can be found. Then any remaining cards are shuffled into the deck and another 16 cards are laid out for the next round.

The game lasts as long as the players choose -- e.g. 5 rounds, 10 rounds, until the deck is played out, etc.  The player who has collected the most letter cards wins.

boardgame: Ezra and Nehemiah: Spielbox Scroll Tiles
description:
A set of 6 extra Wood and Gold Scroll tiles, original offered through Spielbox Magazine.

Gain 1VP Immediately. Gain 1VP per complete set of 1 Flipped Development Tile and 1 golden space reached on the Altar Track at the game’s end.
You may gain the printed benefit of up to 2 different Walls you have built when preparing for each Sabbath.
Gain 1VP Immediately. You may always spend any 2 of Cinders, Stone, or Wood in place of Gold when placing Scribes. 
Gain 1VP for each Farmer and Labourer you have at the game’s end (those placed during the final Prepare for Sabbath step).
Gain 1VP Immediately. Gain 1 Silver when placing Stone in the Temple.
Gain the immediate reward a second time when turning over each Development Tile.

boardgame: Rabbits and the Tortoise: The Great Race
description:
Rabbits and the Tortoise story brings a fresh twist to the classic Tortoise and Hare tale, transforming this iconic fable into a racing game!

In this game, the hares are racing not only against the tortoise but also against each other. For 1 to 4 players, each player guides their chosen rabbit. It's up to you to see whether your rabbit has learned a lesson from the story and whether it takes its job seriously.

Dice-rolling rabbits race on the board against a tortoise that moves once per turn. While the goal is to come in first, second place isn't too bad either. Surprise cards you encounter along the way can move you or the tortoise forward or backward. Additionally, with your super cards, the winner of the game can change until the very last moment.

Also, a special version of the 'Rabbit & Tortoise' storybook, tailored for the game, comes in the box.

-description from publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Tortoise and the Hare

boardgame: Tiny Epic Dungeons Adventures: Deluxe Edition
description:
The Deluxe Edition includes The Skyward Revenge mini expansion which introduces a modular side-quest chapter that acts as its own play session and can be integrated at any point in the storyline.

Additionally, the mini expansion brings new Mobs and Minions to the Tiny Epic Dungeons System. Each presenting a unique challenge that will test even the best players!

InfiniBrix is a rules light, story-first tabletop game that empowers families and friends of all ages tell wild, wonderful stories and play epic action scenes with their own construction bricks and miniature figures! If you can build it, you can play it!

InfiniBrix is a game where one player helps guide the story as the "Game Manager," while the rest take on the role of Figments - aka "Figs" - on heroic adventures!

Play happens in three phases:

- BUILD MODE - create your Fig in minutes with our story-focused, multi-genre character creation system.​
- STORY MODE - collaboratively create crazy stories with no-prep, fill-in-the-blanks adventure paths, then play through to see how your Figs face epic challenges!
- ARENA MODE - build, break, and modify Figs, terrain, and items for zany tactical action, whether a battle, capture-the-flag, or a massive title showdown in a sport you dream up!

Resolve every challenge with our descriptive, fiction-first dice pool mechanic, where every advantage adds a Triumph die, and every Challenge a Trouble die!​  Will you battle to victory?

—description from the designer

This game puts you in the shoes of a soothsayer. In a competition, you take it in turns to interpret the symbolic cards drawn by the customer. The customer will award a grimoire to the diviner who has given the most revealing interpretation!

Mostkala is a game of storytelling and improvisation for young and old alike.

A game that's easy to understand.

It can also be a development tool that facilitates communication, creativity and self-expression.

1 to 4 players compete to create a winning circuit by snapping together a path of SNAP CIRCUITS parts between their light and the communal battery pack.

Easy to play: Set up the board, draw three cards, plan your path between the battery pack and your light. The first one to make a circuit and turn on their light, wins!

Every game is unique. Play offensively, defensively, or even use parts of another
player’s circuit to win! Utilize the ‘Skip A Turn’ and ‘Wild Card’ at the perfect time to light up victory.

Trial by Fire is a print & play roguelike dungeon-crawling game. Using only a pen and a quest sheet. You must explore a deadly dungeon, battling monsters, collecting treasures, and surviving until you reach the exit.

The game is played over a series of rounds. Each round, players use their action points to move their characters or attack an enemy into a grid representing a dungeon. When one or more players reach the exit stairs, the game is finished, players count their score, the highest score win the game.

—description from the designer

Players are rock bands touring Finland trying to succeed. The goal is to sign a record deal, compose a hit song and get a headline gig on a rock festival.

Players use movement cards to travel across Finland and use action cards to get achievements, manage their reputation and rock credibility.

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Rivals: Royalty Pack 2
description: The Vampire: The Masquerade Rivals Expandable Card Game Royalty Pack 2 contains 28 cards immortalizing each winner from a "Prince of the City" tournament.

Life is unpredictable. Even when you make a choice, the outcome is never entirely in your control.

In this game, players shape life paths by forming sequences of event cards that define their characters' growth, competing to fulfill the most wishes and claim victory.

—description from the designer

Scavengers is an entry-level sci-fi strategy board game where 2-4 players compete for dominance on a hostile alien planet. The goal? Be the first to collect 10 Warpium, a rare and powerful energy source. With four streamlined phases per turn, players deploy units, expand territory, and engage in tactical battles, making for a fast-paced yet deeply strategic experience.

The game’s modular tile system ensures that no two playthroughs are ever the same. With over 8 trillion possible battlefield configurations, each session offers fresh challenges and opportunities. Players must adapt their strategies on the fly, exploring new terrain and navigating the ever-changing environment.

Adding to the excitement, the fate of Warpium and the dreaded Dreadclaw strikes are determined by a unique D12 and D4 dice system, keeping players on edge with dynamic and unpredictable events. Every turn presents new risks and rewards, forcing players to balance calculated strategy with adaptability.

Whether you're a seasoned strategist or new to tabletop gaming, Scavengers delivers an accessible yet engaging experience. With intuitive mechanics, endless replayability, and high-stakes decision-making, it’s a game that will keep players coming back for more. Will you outthink your rivals and claim victory, or be left scavenging for survival?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Stadt Land Vollpfosten: 3 in 1 – Kartenspiel Trio
description:
A collection of all three games of Stadt Land Vollpfosten: Das Kartenspiel in a box.

Contains: 

     Rotlicht Edition (Red light edition)
     Junior Edition
     Classic Edition

This is a card game inspired by the well-known Werewolf hidden role game from 1986.
But in this version of the game there are no werewolves, but an assassin, who each night eliminates one of the other players, with the aim of eliminating the entire town with the help of the Collector (a sort of reserve assassin, who comes into play if the assassin is discovered)

Each player is secretly assigned a role - assassin, common citizen, or one of the many special roles. There is also a Moderator who controls the flow of the game.
The game alternates between night and day phases. At night, the assassin secretly choose a player to kill. Several other roles are active also (like the investigator, who will try to discover the assassin's player). During the day, the player who was killed is revealed and is out of the game. The remaining players then vote on the player they suspect is the assassin. That player reveals his/her role and is out of the game.

The assassin win when there are an equal number of non-bad roles and bad-roles (assassin + collector). Citizens win when they have killed the assassin and the collector.

—user summary

It’s some year in the near future, maybe... like... 2043 or something. There’s been an attack on the power grid of every major developed nation on the earth. An attack of this scale is simply unprecedented. As a result, a vast majority of nuclear power plants have malfunctioned, and of that group, many have experienced major explosions. Fortunately for you, you're alive, but you’re just outside the blast radius. It’s everyone for themselves. Friends and family are now your enemies. It’s you or them. Survive or die. Throw on your backpack, because you’re going scavenging.

Blast Radius is a strategy-focused card-matching party game in which players compete to find loot to complete objectives. Blast Radius is designed specifically to be easy to pick up and learn by gamers of all experience levels! There's no need to memorize pages and pages of rules, micro-mechanics and other mumbo jumbo. Blast Radius is a fantastic way to kill 25-45 minutes with friends and family! Blast Radius uses a system in which players are all working to complete their own objectives by collecting the necessary items that satisfy them.

A player's turn begins by drawing from the main item deck, which counts as their first of 2 moves. The first draw is a always a gamble. Will you draw a weapon so you can defend yourself, or will you draw a bear trap and become incapacitated? After your first move, which is always drawing from the main item deck, you have to choose what you will do as your second move. The options are: draw again from either the main item deck or the discard pile, playing an action card such as a machete to steal from your opponents, trading item cards with other players, or trading in/completing an objective!

As you learn the game, you'll find different strategies for keeping your opponents from completing their objectives. Plan to only win Blast Radius if you're determined and willing to always be thinking 1... 2... or even 3 steps ahead of everyone else at the table. Expect conflict. It's basically unavoidable.

—description from the designer

Blindly seek out cheese with only your untapped senses. Are the other players bluffing? Are you? Be careful, though. If you reveal a blind mouse, others will get the reward. Reveal three and the game is over.

Players will each create a pile of face down cards in front of them that may be cheese or it may be a mouse. Once everyone has placed at least one card in their stack, they can continue to place card or start the bidding. Use your best skills at reading the other players while keeping your own Poker Face to determine where the best cheese may be placed – but be careful not to reveal a mouse.

-Description from Designer

In Garbage Gobblers, you will lead your gang of street critters to raid the neighborhood trashcans and score today’s meal. Garbage will pile up in four trashcans throughout the game, and you’ll need to make clever use of your Gobblers to make the most of even the nastiest scraps.

On your turn, you may add garbage to the trashcans, replenish your Gobblers, or start a trashcan raid! During a raid, rival gangs may jump into the fray, while special Gobblers can stir things up and mess with your plans. Once the dust settles, only the gang with the highest strength gets to claim the trashcan’s garbage!

It's not all about strength though! The different Gobbler types affect how you score, and you might even end up with a stomachache if you’re not careful.

Your goal? Out-scavenge your opponents and create the ultimate Garbage Feast, earning you the highest score by the end of the game!

—description from the designer

An expansion to the Super Fun Family Card Game with Crazy Questions and Silly Answers!

The main concept of Kids Create Absurdity is to give families a game they can play together, from the ages of seven to ninety-nine. Where everyone can let loose, dance and have fun, but more importantly where they can interact with each other in a world where technology is changing how families socialize. Kids Create Absurdity enables confidence building through storytelling, creative thinking and performance it will reintroduce the bond between families. It is a game that will build self esteem and social skills for all generations.

When you play our family game you will engage in full family interaction while Dancing, Singing, Making A Fart Sound, Telling a Funny Story, Making Silly Faces and most of all having lots of fun!!!!
​
—description from the publisher

A simple card game using a normal deck of playing cards. It has a very interesting element of strategy. It is not always wise to try and play out your cards as quickly as you can, because the less cards in your hand, the greater the chance you will not be able to take a turn. It is a game of subtle balance.

SADDLE RULES

OBJECTIVE 
Saddle is a two player card game, the objective of which is to get rid of all of your cards or be the last to take a turn.

THE DECK 
A standard 52-card deck, ranked from ace (1) to King.

SETUP 
Each player starts with 12 cards dealt face down. Two cards are placed separately face up to begin two discard piles. The remainder of the cards become the draw pile.

 PLAY 
Players take turns matching a card from their hand by rank or suit with the top card in one of the discard piles, but not both. The card placed is called the saddle. Based upon that, one card or more may be added before the turn ends. (this is not a requirement) Any additional cards placed must either be all of the same rank as the saddle or sequentially up or down from it. (not both) Added cards may be of different suits. At no time do players pick up any new cards. A player wins if he gets rid of all his cards or his opponent is unable to take a turn. 
 SCORING 
After each game the winning player gets one point for each card remaining in his opponents hand, and when one player reaches 13 points he wins the match.

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity: Cards of Humor and Whimsy expansion
description: An expansion of 30 cards from the "original 1923 edition of Cards of Humor and Whimsy".

Triple Snap!

The first player to sucessfully photograph 3 animals in the same turn takes this card and gains 3 prestige.

In Horizonte, two simultaneous tricks will be played: the Dragon Trick (represented by the sky, where the higher number wins) and the Hound Trick (represented by the earth, where the lower number wins).

Players need to predict the horizon, which is the difference between the number of Dragon Tricks and Hound Tricks they will win.

—description from the designer

The wedding planner has canceled at the last minute on the day of the wedding…

Everything has been running smoothly so far, but now that the guests need to be guided to their tables, chaos looms. The name cards are printed but have not yet been assigned to a specific place. Can you save the wedding, or will it end in a minor key?

In Linky Lines, it’s all about unraveling connections between words and guests. Analyze the words on the table and use your insight to determine which guest matches which word. Then, place the name cards on the seating plan according to your logic.The better you understand and work out the connections, the more points you earn, thanks to the other players.

Whether you play cooperatively or competitively, Linky Lines rewards your insight and creativity. Who will make the sharpest connections and win the game?

—description from the publisher

Goblins have infested the old fort that once guarded the river at Joffrey’s Crossing. Travellers are being attacked, farms are being raided, and people have begun to avoid the once busy road. Desperate to end this trouble, the local inn keeper has put out a call for help. Are you skilled and lucky enough to clear the den of goblins?

Dungeon Blitz is a new series of fast-playing solo adventure games.

In each adventure you create a character and undertake an exciting and dangerous dungeon crawl with little more than your trusty weapon and wits. All you need is some paper, a pencil and three six-sided dice. The dungeon is randomly generated and you will map it as you play. As you discover new locations you might be attacked by monsters, set off a trap or find wondrous treasure. Eventually, you will find the terrible monster at the heart of the dungeon and either defeat it or die trying!

Play the Dungeon Blitz books in any order. Carefully manage your resources, collect treasure and find wondrous magic items as you explore dangerous crypts and dark dungeons. If you survive the dungeon your hero will level up and set out on the road to their next exciting adventure!

—description from the publisher

Play Number Snap, or be the first to draw a bug. Find the secret number, or race to 100. All 25 games have been designed by teachers to target and develop maths skills from simple number recognition and ordering, to addition and subtraction, prime numbers, multiplication, times tables and more.
Kids will become confident number crunchers as they practice and improve their skills. Each game is linked to the Foundation and KS1 National Curriculum.

25 fun filled mini maths games with dice and counters.
Designed by teachers for practicing Early Years and KS1 maths skills for 4-7 year olds.
Covers counting, number recognition, addition, subtraction, doubling, halving, multiplication, division, prime numbers, place value and more.
A separate activity on each card. Clear, colour coded instructions.
Builds solid foundations in number work, helps to prevent maths anxiety, boosts confidence and an enthusiasm for numbers.

—description from the publisher

Help Hector collect his nectar in this deliciously fun, mathematical card game.
Have fun learning to count in 2s, 5s and 10s with this scrumptious mathematical card game.
Count the bees, collect your treats, but watch out for the mischievous twist!
Develops competence in addition; introduces multiples in preparation for learning the 2, 5 and 10 Times Tables.
Designed by teachers to support the KS1 maths curriculum.

Hector’s discovered a scrumptious source of nectar and his waggle dance will show the other bees how to find it. But they’re not very good at directions and keep getting lost!
Can you help Hector count the bees into their hives as they bring the nectar back? Hector will need his bee friends to help make his yummy honey, delicious honey cake and luscious chocolate honeycomb for everyone to enjoy!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
An enormously fun, magical card game. Brilliant for learning letter sounds; an imaginative, witchy adventure for those who can already read!
An exciting and thoroughly engaging game to help young children learn their letter sounds.
Enormous fun for children of all ages; enjoyable for grown-ups too!
Beautiful illustrations capture and stimulate the imagination.
Designed by teachers; a smart, fun way to learn.

Helda and her cat Maude are entering the annual broomstick race. Can you help Helda collect the ingredients she needs to conjure up a very special, speedy, magic broom? Can you find the only magic wand that’ll help her win the race? Watch out for Maude’s mischievous tricks – they’ll certainly slow her down!
Ideal for young children learning their letter sounds, enormous fun for children who have started to read. Enjoyable for grown-ups too!
Engaging, thoroughly addictive with beautiful illustrations to capture and stimulate the imagination. Designed by teachers for smart learning - ideal for ages 3+

—description from the publisher

Little Demons is a fast-casual screw-your-neighbor card game. Win souls, lose friends!

The game is played in rounds in which players compete to claim a soul. Players will draft cards to keep in their hand and then take turns playing actions via cards in order to claim the soul until everyone runs out of cards. then the card totals are tallied and the player with the most power over the soul claims the card.

The first player to claim 3 souls wins the game.

boardgame: Last Light: The Experiment Alien Faction
description:
The Experiment is a brand new alien faction released for the first time in the Last Light 2nd Printing Gamefound campaign.

Choosing the Experiment allows you to choose an opponent's alien power to use for the entire game.

5 New Aliens Factions made available for the first time during the Gamefound Second Edition campaign.

Myrkravi - When you or your opponents play the Trade action, you get some of the spoils.
Leezai - Gives you additional attack with one small or medium ship.
Onkara - Use a medium ship to mine like it was an extractor.
Scylex - Your small ships can't ever be destroyed.
Chillix - Generate a ship ability card for free.

I Don't Like Onions is a trick-taking game where players must make the perfect bowl of onion soup.

Players receive a secret recipe at the beginning of the game. They must balance their ingredient pool so that when the game ends, they have the ingredients they need for their recipe. However, they must also offload ingredients they don't want to the other players.

The player who plays the lowest card in the trick chooses the rule for the next one. You can never get too complacent in a kitchen with multiple chefs!

I Don't Like Onions is a challenging trick-taking/ trick avoidance game that requires players to manipulate the rules in their favour while reading other players' intentions.

—description from the designer

Life as a Kinkster is HARD!

If you’re not looking for a new play partner, you’re looking to add to your collection of play toys or adding new touches to your already AWESOME dungeon.  Sometimes all at the same time!

The Dungeon, where you play, has sprung from the blood, sweat, and tears of your most creative expression. But, let’s be honest, mostly tears. Yeah, building things is not your forte. So you take the time to pick out just the right things. Some of those who have beheld your vision wept openly, knowing immediately that your dungeon was superior to theirs. Others were just jealous of you…obviously.

You navigate the minefield of negotiation like a champ. Your play partners are impressed with your skilful wielding of toys and tools that others might consider weapons. You are so good at it that every now and then you find yourself the victim of scurrilous whispers that result in the loss of a prospective play partner.

This is it! Your dungeon is ready, your toys are lined up, your play partners have consented and you’re ready to play. Now it’s time to find out…can you do it without injury to them…or yourself?!

—description from the publisher

In The Spiritualists, 2 players take on the role of Victorian-era psychic mediums, out to "prove" they are the real deal.

In front of a gathered crowd, it is up to these 2 spiritualists to 'commune with the dead' to win the cards that the spirits favour.

Every round, however, the stakes get higher, as the spiritualists must place tokens on their card mat to indicate which cards the spirits DO NOT want them to win (cursed cards).

Each favoured card is worth positive points, but the cursed cards are worth more in negative points. Players must do their best to avoid winning cursed cards and manipulate their opponent into winning them instead.

—description from the designer

Prince Géza's firstborn son, István (Stephen), embarked on building a Christian, European state in the Carpathian Basin. Although he found supporters both at home and abroad, many opposed the ambitions of Vajk, who had only recently been baptized. István had to face countless dangers and obstacles, and his success was far from guaranteed. Forging a unified kingdom out of the Hungarian principality was no small task.

The István the King? board game explores the lesser-known aspects of the struggle that shaped Hungary’s fate: everyday hardships, political interests, the scheming of allies, and the opportunism of opponents. Players take on the roles of key figures in these events, each striving to fulfill their own goals.

Triumph with István—or against him—using your army, wealth, or unwavering faith, and shape the future of the Hungarian people for the next millennium!

Mutio Vitelleschi, the Jesuit General, has discovered a terrible secret: in many countries, the order is on the verge of being banned. In response, he summons several of his most loyal followers to Rome and sends them on secret missions. Their task is to establish new missions worldwide, ensuring the survival of the Jesuit community.

However, since all these secret missions serve the same goal, the protagonists often end up working against each other when they would be better off cooperating. Challenges abound—pirates, various temptations, seemingly impossible tasks, and unexpected obstacles all stand in the way of completing their mission.

It takes time for them to realize that their mission can only be fully accomplished if, at the right moment, they put aside their differences and support each other.

The Golden Pear Expansion opens up the opportunity to compete for the attention of influencers (and yes there are yet more easter eggs here) and to demo your games at the world’s best board game conventions. In doing so, you’ll gain powerful new abilities; building your engine further and making your Meeple Inc experience even more thematic. The influencer mechanism adds in more player interaction and nuance to the gameplay. Timing your moves becomes very important!

With a massive neoprene mat convention hall space, metal pear tokens, reviewer cards and extra content for the main game.

Step into the spotlight and become a movie star in this hilarious and dynamic party game! In Take One, players take turns stepping into the roles of Film Director, Movie Star, or Voice-Over to bring movie scenes to life.

Each round, one player becomes the Film Director, drawing Character Cards and Scenario Cards to set the stage for an epic scene. The Movie Stars must act out the scene — without speaking! The Voice-Over provides all the dialogue, narrating the action in real time. But beware! The director can throw in a Plot Twist at any moment, changing the scene in unexpected ways!
At the end of each round, the director awards Score Cards based on performance, creativity, and entertainment value. Then, roles are reassigned, and a new scene begins.

Do you have what it takes to be the audience's favorite? The player with the highest score at the end of the game earns the title of The Ultimate Movie Icon!

Ready, set, act — time to steal the show!

—description from the publisher

You are a Christmas elf tasked with delivering 24 Christmas presents with the help of your co-elves, represented on the cards. To accomplish this, you reveal cards from the deck, one after the other. After every revealed card you have the choice to end your turn voluntarily and deliver all the presents granted by the already revealed cards, or to continue and reveal the next card.
But be careful: As the Advent season is a stressful time for Christmas elves, they are prone to spontaneously falling asleep. One sleeping elf is no problem – the rest continues working as usual, but as tiredness is contagious, the others start yawning as well. And whenever another sleeping elf is revealed, all of them instantly fall asleep: You have busted your turn and deliver no presents at all.
After one of the players has delivered 24 presents or more, finish the current round – the elf with the highest total of delivered presents wins the game.

—description from the designer

The successful quiz show on TV programme with Esther Sedlaczek is now also available as a board game for the whole family! Just like in the TV show, you compete against each other in teams and have to answer questions from 8 different and popular categories. The further you get in the category round, the better your chances of reaching Olympus first in the final.

—description from the publisher

The game is based on the popular Ukrainian TV show "Хто зверху?" ("Who is on top?"), which is an adaptation of Battle of the Sexes. Players compete in a fun, party-style battle of wits and skills, engaging in various challenges to determine which team will come out on top. While traditionally divided by gender, teams can be formed in any way to maximize enjoyment.

The game consists of six rounds, each featuring different mini-games that test players' creativity, knowledge, and teamwork. Examples of challenges include assembling words using lettered finger caps, guessing words by lip-reading, identifying famous people by asking yes/no questions, and arranging items in the correct order. There are also truth-or-lie storytelling rounds and physical challenges where players must answer trivia while performing tasks like squats. Each round is timed, and teams earn points based on their performance. The team with the lowest score after each round gets a chance to answer a bonus question for extra points.

The goal is to accumulate the most points by successfully completing the challenges. At the end of the game, the team with the highest score is declared the winner, answering the game’s central question: Who is on top?

"A long, long time ago in the grim darkness of a far-flung solar system, an eternal war rages among three formidable factions. The resourceful and technologically adept humans, masters of machinery and innovation; the enigmatic Eldar, ancient beings consumed by their quest to preserve the cosmic balance; and the malevolent Xenos, relentless agents of chaos and destruction, emerge from the shadowy realms to sow discord and strife. Each race, driven by their distinct ideologies and unyielding determination, battles for supremacy in this distant corner of the galaxy."

Tinny Tactics is a tabletop wargame set in grimdark space, packed in a small tin box. Inside the box, you will find three balanced armies and two battlegrounds that will let you play endless scenarios with your friends.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Prelude to Leipzig: Napoleon's Spring 1813 Campaign
description:
Prelude to Leipzig: Napoleon's Spring 1813 Campaign is an Operational level board-wargame.

This is a prequel to John Theissen's Campaign of Nations. That game tackled the autumn 1813 campaign that culminated in the climactic Battle of Nations, covering those events with his clean, streamlined approach to operational hex-and-counter gaming. In this game, he turns his attention to the spring campaign. Historically, Napoleon failed to achieve the decisive victory he was looking for, resulting in a summer truce - giving his enemies precious time to plan and prepare for the battle that would signal the end of his rule. But perhaps your French Coalition can realize his ambitions? Or maybe your Prussian and Russian forces will stop him in the spring?

Like Mr. Theissen's other titles, what happens is up to the players. Pursue your objectives aggressively for an exciting clash. Dilly-dally for an indecisive campaign. This approach, which trusts players to use the tools they've been given, has won these designs their ardent fans. In an attempt to bring these joys to a wider audience, the mapsheet - our largest-ever, measuring 32" x 32" - uses roomy extra-large hexes, with terrain effects printed directly on the map.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Dead Man's Hand Redux: The Bad and the Ugly
description:
This 44 page source book  contains all the information you need to bring 13 new Factions to the streets of Dead Man’s Hand.

A copy of Dead Man’s Hand Redux rulebook is needed to use this product.

Like The Good, The Bad & The Ugly is not necessarily a reference to the moral framework of the factions and legends represented. You may wish to view them in one light or another, we leave that choice to you.

The 13 factions in The Bad & The Ugly are: US Cavalry, Banditos, Bushrangers, Confederados, The Family, Kelly Gang, Pinkertons, Renegade Apaches, Regulators, Spirit Walkers, The Count, The Baron and Malevolent Seven.

New Legends of the West are: The Bad, The Ugly, Buckshot Roberts, Ely Fergus, English Bob, Frank Daniels, Pat Garrett, Maeve Newton, Rafe Melmont, The Scarred Man, Jonathan Pain, Delores Wood

The Ugly section illustrates rules for the supernatural factions in Dead Man's Hand (Spirit Walkers, The Count, The Baron, Malevolent Seven) and the gribbly, nightmarish creatures that they sometimes control.

Also includes 12 new scenes in 4 new Acts.

Fart of War is a hilarious party game where you score points by defeating other players' Fart cards with stronger, smellier Farts!

Each player builds a "Fart Army" by collecting and modifying Fart cards. On their turn, players can draw cards, play cards, or attack an opponent’s Fart cards. Modifier cards strengthen or weaken Farts, while Special Action cards add strategic twists. Attacks resolve by comparing fart strength, with the defeated Fart added to the winner's score pile (the "Cloud"). The game ends when a player reaches 15 points or the deck runs out, with the highest score winning. Expect plenty of laughs, strategy, and, of course, fart noises!

—description from the designer

The cycle of farming is eternal — plowing, sowing, harvesting, and ensuring that every mouth is fed. Life on the farm is simple yet demanding, requiring both strategy and resilience to thrive.

Agricola: Special Edition revitalizes Uwe Rosenberg’s iconic board game, bringing it to life with new artwork and refined usability. This special edition includes all previously released expansions and introduces Irrigations, a brand-new expansion that challenges players to manage water resources to sustain and grow their farms.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
A once-abandoned asylum now harbors something far worse than its crumbling walls suggest. People are vanishing, eerie chants fill the air, and strange lights flicker in the darkness. The ritual is not yet complete — there is still time to act. As elite B.E.L.O.W. agents, you are the last line of defense against the horrors that should not exist.

With a Lovecraftian theme,  B.E.L.O.W.: Asylum is a fully cooperative, one-shot action-horror board game that blends deep strategy with an immersive, ever-evolving narrative. Play as unique, asymmetric characters, each with distinct abilities, as you uncover the truth hidden within the asylum’s haunted corridors. Face off against bizarre and lethal threats, adapt to unpredictable challenges, and master protective rituals to halt the growing evil.

Players must activate 4 artifact seals around the board and finish the ritual to defeat the Monarch.  With randomized board tiles, exploration cards and ritual rings, there is great replayaility.

—description from publisher

Return to the world of Dragon Eclipse in this standalone sequel that takes you centuries into the past — before the chaos, before the Eclipse. Step back in time to an era of peace and honor, where tamers strive to prove themselves in the legendary Grand Tournament.

But this year’s tournament is different. A dark force wielding the sinister element of Metal threatens to shatter the balance. Beyond rivalries and intrigue, a far greater evil is stirring, and the world's fate may rest on the tournament’s outcome. Will you rise as a champion — or fall to the coming darkness?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Los Banditos: The Train Heist Card Game
description:
Los Banditos is an Old West themed Train Heist Card Game where Banditos (players) take it in turns to build up four trains using carriage and caboose cards. Score points using a locomotive or escape car card. The Bandito with the most points after five rounds is the winner.

However, Banditos can use actions cards to change up the game. Steal cards from other Banditos, double up points on a train, change direction of play, and more, to disrupt other Banditos.

The cards of Los Banditos Card have been beautifully drawn with plenty of small details so you notice something new every time you play. Saddle up Amigos!

Dive into the world of wordplay with Cross Spin, the ultimate game for word puzzle lovers!

The goal is to be the first to solve bite-sized crosswords. Each round a puzzle board is selected and category cards are flipped for each word slot. Each player now races to complete the puzzle by putting an answer for any category into any location. If you're stuck, spin the board and unlock new word combinations. Since any answer can go in any slot no round will ever be the same! When the first person finishes, share your answers and tally up points for each word correct. Choose a new puzzle board and category cards and play again. First to 25 is the winner!

—description from the publisher

Survive the Shadows of Neo Colombo. In a city where secrets are currency and danger lurks in every shadow, Synthetic Sins invites you to take on the role of Garrett Takao, a fixer framed for a crime he didn’t commit. Navigate a gripping solo adventure of stealth, action, and survival as you uncover the truth behind a sinister conspiracy. With over 25 handcrafted missions, you’ll sneak past guards, disable high-tech security, and outsmart ruthless foes—or dive into thrilling firefights when the stakes are high. Modular maps and strategic gameplay make every mission a tense and immersive challenge. From the shadows of back alleys to the sterile halls of corporate towers, every moment brings high-stakes action in a world where technology and survival collide.

In Synthetic Sins, each mission has a preprogrammed setup and objectives.  Modifier cards are drawn through out each mission, adding randomness and replayability.  If players fail to achieve the objects or lose all their Health points during a mission, they lose.  However, missions can be replayed after reshuffling the Modifier deck. An optional, non-lethal mode is also available to play.

—description from the publisher

Stalk the dastardly werewolf that is hunting your village. With the help of a Big Game Hunter, a Tracker, a Priestess, and a Doctor, shoot wildly into the night in hopes that you strike true. But be careful because if you don't shoot first, then you just might be the one who gets hunted tonight.

Bandits have stormed Main Street, and the Law stands as the town’s last defense. In this fast-paced, 18-card asymmetric dueling game, every showdown is a test of wit, risk, and tactical decision making. With clever team construction and ever-shifting strategies, no two duels play the same.

Each side — the Law and the Bandits — commands eight unique units. Select four to take the field, while the other four will load into your ammo deck. Do you play it safe with a steady hand, or gamble on high-risk, high-reward shots? With tense 5–10 minute rounds, every match provides a chance to test winning strategies and adapt for the next battle.

—description from the designer

Mirror Agents is a two-player hidden-movement spy game in which players act as hunters and hunters. They move their agents in secret through the city to collect intel and hunt their opponents.
Players choose two actions each turn that can secretly move their agent on their hidden board and/or manipulate the Intel cubes on the main board. Using the information collected by which actions were chosen each turn and how they affected the board, players attempt to track their opponent and chase them down with their guard, who moves openly on the main board.
They are also using those actions to collect Intel cubes, which they use to complete mission cards as they work towards completing their secret objectives.
The game is won by being the first to complete their secret objective or capturing their opponent 3 times.

During the second world war, submarines came into their own as instruments of war. In the Pacific theater, US submarines disrupted Japanese shipping of supplies, material and personnel. Unlike the German wolf pack strategy, US submarine captains usually operated independently. As the captain of the USS Lox, a (fictional) Salmon-class boat, you are responsible for 58 officers and enlisted submariners. Your mission is to disrupt Japanese war operations by sinking as much shipping as possible.

USS Lox is a solitaire card game that uses a standard deck of cards to model a US submarine patrol in the Pacific during WWII. It borrows from the rules of cribbage.

—description from the designer

Get ready for a fast paced brain bending challenge with this unique category game you’ll need sharp eyes and quick thinking to win.

Flipkart until you spot a pair of matching categories, then race to find the common letter on both cards. Be the first to shout an example that starts with that letter. Guaranteed to deliver excitement and laughter every time!

—description from the back of the box

Praestigium is a competitive game for 2 to 4 players. It's an expert game and the average playing time is 2 hours.

Each player embodies a headteacher of a magic school and manages this school represented by a gameboard.

There are two ways to win: to reach the amount of Prestige or to kill the headteacher.

By managing the school, the player will be able to :

     recruit students and teachers from a pool then put them in slits
     learn spells by placing cards in the schoolyard
     attack the opponents schools by casting spells from their spellbooks with associations of spells.


The Prestige can be earned by the success of your dices from the spells or recruiting teachers for example.

—description from the publisher

In Search of Our Pre-Trianon Heritage

The uniquely developed quiz game aims to help players explore the history, settlements, built and natural heritage of Hungary before 1920, as well as the famous figures born in these regions. Additionally, it seeks to spark interest in the Hungarian communities beyond the current borders and their cultural values through a playful and engaging experience.

Through duels, missions, and elements of luck, the game presents an exciting dynamic between individual and communal interests: to achieve ultimate victory, players sometimes need to help each other, and in many cases, they must make joint decisions or answer questions together. However, being too cunning may sometimes lead to unexpected consequences.

Piritorin Ylitys  is a board game for 2 people, a cartoonish variant of the Chinese game Jungle or dou shou qi. The setting is Piritori, an infamous market square in Helsinki (Piritori could be translated as Amphetamine square), and the characters are caricatures of the regulars (f.ex. security guard, junkie, snooping neighbor). The other player tries to get from the Metro station to the Shop on the other side of the board, with the opponents goal the opposite, getting from the Shop to the Metro station.

—description from the designer

Centuries after the historic first human landing on the moon, humanity has reached unprecedented levels of technological advancement, mastering space travel and venturing far beyond the confines of Earth. As interstellar exploration became the new frontier, four of the largest and most powerful corporations on Earth saw boundless potential for profit in the uncharted vastness of space. With their sights set on the unknown, they began to compete fiercely for control over the cosmos.

The discovery of a new planet—one with the perfect conditions for terraforming—sent shockwaves throughout the business and scientific communities. As the construction of a massive, state-of-the-art space station began in orbit around the newly discovered planet, the corporations quickly recognized that control over this station would give them a stranglehold on the planet's future.

Now, as the space station takes shape, tensions mount among the corporate giants. Each one is determined to not only claim a dominant role in the terraforming efforts but also to secure exclusive rights to the planet’s resources and strategic advantages. The race for power, wealth, and control over the future of space exploration has only just begun.

Void Ventures is a print&play space base construction game where players take roles of the representatives of the four corporations that are tasked with overseeing the construction and expanding the influence of their corporation. You are tasked with creating a parts of a space base on the newly discovered planet while gaining the most influence for your corporation. But watch out, other corporations also want to gain the most influence over the space base with their construction.

—description from the publisher

Munchkin expansion sets 2, 3, and 4 are released together in Norwegian. These three expansions introduce the ability to play as an orc or a gnome, become a bard entertaining for dear life, acquire a loyal steed or hireling (or even both), and feature outrageous weapons, unpredictable equipment such as weapons, armor, and other useful tools, as well as terrifying enemies of all kinds.

In Save Us All, a narrative cooperative game, players take on the role of survivors in a post-apocalyptic world overrun by creatures known as the Putrid. Each character has a unique skill (combat, stealth, survival, or communication), essential for solving events and progressing through the story.
Players must explore locations such as a natural history museum, an abandoned university, and an infested medical center, all while fighting off enemies, managing resources, and making critical decisions.
The unique "Deep Self" card system adds extra tension: in moments of danger, a player can sacrifice an ally to save themselves… but at what cost?
Save Us All blends immersive storytelling, cooperation, and moral dilemmas in a hostile world where every choice matters.
Survive all three chapters to complete the game!

—description from the publisher

Dans Save Us All, un jeu coopératif narratif, les joueurs incarnent des survivants plongés dans un monde post-apocalyptique infesté de créatures appelées "Putrides". Chaque personnage possède une compétence unique (combat, furtivité, survie, communication), essentielle pour résoudre des événements et progresser dans l’histoire.
Les joueurs doivent explorer différents lieux comme un musée d’histoire naturelle, une faculté abandonnée, et un centre médical infesté, tout en affrontant des ennemis, gérant leurs ressources et prenant des décisions cruciales.
Un système de cartes "Moi profond" ajoute une tension supplémentaire : en cas de danger, un joueur peut sacrifier un allié pour sa propre survie… mais à quel prix ?
Save Us All mélange narration immersive, coopération et dilemmes moraux dans un univers hostile où chaque choix compte.
Arrivez au bout des 3 chapitres pour finir le jeu !

—description from the publisher (French)

Super Trivia is an exciting educational game featuring 1200 carefully selected general knowledge questions covering daily life, culture, history, natural science, and geography — things you should know!

Enjoy 6 question types that add variety and engage other players: solo answer, swap option, miss & pass, quick-fire, closest guess, and 200 image questions.

Roll the dice to receive a question and answer correctly to earn a card. Collect the most cards to win!

De Beste Belegger is a strategic board game, developed by a team of Argenta employees. The game confronts the players (from two to eight people) with the financial products and legal possibilities in Belgium with which you can build up a fortune, for example investing in real estate. During the game, the players are subject to the economic situation, which has an impact on the returns in the game. A quiz with four hundred multiple-choice questions allows the players to gradually discover the secrets of the financial world. Whoever has been able to build up the largest fortune at the end of the three life phases wins the game.

Stellar Descent is a space themed solo TTPRG board game.

Embark on a thrilling solo adventure set in a science fiction universe with this card-driven boardgame. You find yourself aboard an invaded starship, exploring new locations, engaging with enemies, and uncovering hidden loot. As you delve deeper into the ship, you'll investigate mysterious clues while battling to survive. Your ultimate goal? To discover the key that will allow you to navigate the starship back to Earth. Every choice you make will shape your journey in this tense, strategic space odyssey.

—description from the designer

The Accessories Box includes the following:


     1 Supplemental Game Mat
     6 Tear Dice
     1 Dice Tray
     5 Tear Markers
     Tear Tracker Board


—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Unleash a world of flavors with Savor, the enchanting card game where strategy and taste go hand in hand. Ideal for ages 7 and above, this game promises to spice up any gathering, accommodating 2 to 7 players in a whirlwind of culinary delight.

Unique Features: 

     Explore Global Cuisines: Each card is a gateway to discovering international dishes, inviting players to a virtual feast of flavors.
     Educational Play: Savor subtly educates players about diverse food cultures, ingredients, and the art of gastronomy while ensuring an engaging playtime. 
     Inclusive and Easy-to-Learn: With rules simple enough for children yet engaging for adults, Savor is a hit across generations, making it a versatile addition to any game collection. 


Product Line Details:

     Family-Friendly Fun: Crafted for inclusive play, Savor bridges age gaps, creating memorable family moments around the dining table or living room floor.
     Strategic Depth: Players are challenged to think ahead, plan their flavor combinations, and adapt to the ever-changing "menu" of cards to win. Product Specifications:
     High-Quality Components: Savor features sturdy cards with a linen finish for durability and a tactile, luxurious feel.
     Artistically Rendered: Vibrant illustrations capture the spirit of each flavor, turning every card into a visual treat.
     Bilingual Instruction Booklet: English and Spanish rules ensure wider accessibility and understanding.


—description from the publisher

On 10 May 1940, the Phony War came to an end. Germany launched Fall Gelb (Case Yellow) with the goal: the destruction of the French First Army and control of the Low Countries and the part of France north of the Somme and Aisne Rivers. By the end of May, the Wehrmacht had concluded Fall Gelb with stunning success. On 5 June 1940, the Germans launched Fall Rot (Case Red), which called for the complete destruction of the remaining Allied forces in France and the occupation of the whole country.

This scenario pack has ten scenarios spanning the six weeks from the battles along the Meuse River to the Mediterranean Riviera, and is compatible with MMP’s Advanced Squad Leader System.

—description from the publisher

Available from the Tantrum House Season 11 Kickstarter.

Soil cost: 3
Base VP: 2
Type: Tree
Habitat: Sunny, Wet
Name: Lychee
Max Growth: 4
Canopy VP: 5
Sprout: 4
First Ability: Immediate -> +2 Growth
Second Ability: Composting -> Remove 1 Growth, then Compost 2 cards from your hand.

Giant worms have ripped a hole in spacetime, shattering the universe into six dimensions! You are explorers racing to map this anomaly, understand it, and avoid the ravenous worms. Strategically connect vibrant tri-color hexagons to collect shards, expand your network, and ultimately gain dominance over these shattered dimensions. Will you tame the infinite or get lost in the void?

Each turn, draw a tri-colored hexagon to your hand, place a tile, and earn shards for matching colors. As the board expands, a network of color-coded holes emerges, amplifying the value of your shards. Send explorers into the holes, unlocking powerful abilities. But beware! Worms travel through holes too, threatening to destabilize the dimensions and remove your explorers. Race to strategically place tiles and deploy explorers to maximize your gains and avoid the worms' destructive path.

—description from the publisher

Welcome aboard the starship Ecliptica.

Something’s gone terribly wrong. There’s only enough life support for one of you to make it home. Naturally, you’ve all agreed to settle this with a totally fair, reasonable, and absolutely not cutthroat game: one survives, the rest take a short, sharp walk out the airlock.

HOW IT WORKS
Every player gets a Role Card. Each Role gives you a unique power to manipulate, influence, and—ideally—not die.

Each round, players discuss, then simultaneously vote on who they want out. You do this by pointing at the player, then giving them a Voting Cube.

Voting Cubes = bad. Get 5 Voting Cubes, and you’re airlocked. Gone. See you in the void.

Dead players aren’t done. Echoes (eliminated players) still vote and influence the game—sometimes deciding who goes next and who goes last.

Repeat until one player remains… or none.

If that sounds like your kind of chaos, go ahead and grab the game now. If you need more convincing, keep reading—then buy it anyway. You won’t regret it.

WHAT'S IT LIKE?
Think Werewolf, but all the roles are public. This isn't about sniffing out a hidden traitor. It's about arguing, pleading, scheming, and persuading everyone else that you should live and they should not.

The twist? Eliminated players are still in it. Echoes vote together every round, and they decide who dies last. You want to eliminate the other players—but not at the cost of upsetting them.

WHY IT ROCKS
Easy to learn. Discuss. Vote. That’s it. Role abilities add depth, but most groups are up and playing within five minutes—even if no one’s played before.

Huge groups welcome. This game thrives in parties and big game nights. It scales effortlessly even with 15+ players.

Replayable forever. With 20 Role Cards and up to 20 players, there are over a million unique combinations. Want more? The custom edition lets you make your own Roles—so you can have infinite games.

And remember, in space, no one can hear you scheme.

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Protected Growth Promo Card
description:
Spring Season Promo 2025 of FryxGames, which you can buy on its own, or get for free if you order something at their webstore from March 1st to May 31st.

Promo card X73.
Plant and Event tag
Cost 2 M€
Requirement: Oxygen must be 7% or lower.
Gain 1 plant per power tag you have

As investors in grocery chains expanding across the Texas Hill Country, players aim to make the most money possible - this is done by acquiring shares in grocers, expanding distribution networks of chains they’re invested in, and advertising in town to increase demand (and thus profit).

—description from the designer

boardgame: Grendel: The Game of Crime and Mayhem
description:
Leaving a trail of blood and corpses, Grendel has executed the leaders of the 23 most powerful crime families in the US. While Grendel now dominates the criminal underworld, the assassin’s series of coordinated attacks has also, predictably, created a power vacuum. Now, some upstarts think that New York City – the crown jewel of the East Coast–is up for grabs. Grendel needs to set things straight but wherever he goes, the werewolf, Argent, is soon to follow. Will the cold and calculating Grendel secure his grip after the so-called “Seaboard Massacre” or will the Mob rise up and take back what was theirs? Will Argent, the tireless hunter, finally corner his prey or will the Police put an end to this rampant crime spree?

Fight for control of the city as one of the four factions, each playing differently than the other. Focus your aggression, knowing that power and, ultimately, victory hang in the balance. Weigh your decisions carefully as each one could tip the scales in your favor.

Play out one bloody night in New York as Grendel, the Mob, the Police, and Argent seek to secure control of the city. While the Mob attempts to rally their forces and regain a foothold, Grendel sweeps in to subdue them once more. The Police work to forestall all conflict in the city, while Argent hunts Grendel.

During a round, players will take turns using their faction’s unique mechanics to deploy and move units, place Influence tokens on the board, draw cards, and put Aggression tokens in the Towers. When a Tower drops, that triggers a Mayhem. After 3 Mayhems, the player with the most points wins!

—description from the publisher

Blocks Rock! is the competitive educational game in which two players, or two teams of players, compete to build a color-and shape-specific structure in the shortest amount of time.

—description from the publisher

Step into Match Hatchery, a captivating puzzle game where polyominoes of magical eggs tumble into your hatching grid! Match groups of eggs to incubate them and collect a menagerie of creatures friendly and fantastic. Stack and align the eggs with cunning precision to spark chain reactions and unleash spectacular hatching combos. Can you corral the coolest creatures into your nursery in this fast-paced challenge that weaves enchanting creature-collection with a delightful twist on strategic stacking?

At the center of the play area is the coop, 2 cards that define a 3x3 grid. Each turn you place a coop card into the grid, and take a group of a single color of eggs from the selection of polyomino shapes to place into your incubator. When 4 or more eggs in a row or column are connected, the eggs hatch and you score! Sets of eggs can be arranged to drop down into combos, and every egg you hatch unlocks abilities for quick, light, and engaging strategies.

In the card game Blubbern, your goal is to strategically place cards on four piles so that the same sea creature appears on all of them, earning you scoring cards. With a mix of luck and clever strategy, you'll collect scoring cards to build the longest sea creature row. The game ends when no water cards remain or when someone has collected eight different scoring cards, and the player with the longest creature row wins!

boardgame: Чарівний світ: Джерело сили (Magic World: Source of Power)
description:
'Чарівний світ: Джерело сили' ("Magic World: Source of Power") is a board game inspired by Ukrainian mythology. Players take on the role of rescuers, striving to restore mythical heroes by reuniting them with their lost homes and luck. The game has cards representing these heroes, their homes, and the luck required to restore their magical strength.

During gameplay, players take turns drawing and playing cards to assemble complete sets consisting of a hero, their home, and their luck. Strategic decision-making is required to optimize card collection and prevent opponents from completing their own sets. The game continues until a predefined number of heroes have been saved, which depends on the number of players.

The ultimate goal is to rescue as many mythical heroes as possible. The player who successfully restores the most heroes by the end of the game wins.

Mini Market is a progressive game with 2 game play variants. The easier variant is intended for children from the age of 3, whereas the more difficult variant is intended for children from the age of 4. 
The player's task is to put all the items on their shopping list in the trolley and reach the cash till. The players roll the die and move their trolley piece exactly by the number of fields indicated by the throw. The colors on the die don't matter. The player decides in which direction they want to move. Players can move horizontally or vertically. Only one trolley may occupy one field. The player also can't go through a field which is already occupied by another trolley.
If the player stops at a field next to a crate containing a product from their shopping list, they can take it. 
The first player to reach the cash till empties their trolley and checks, if they have collected all their items from their shopping list. If so, they win the game! Otherwise, they'd need to go back and collect the missing items.
The 2nd game is very similar. The only difference is the additional caution board which makes an obstacle on the way.

Thanks to its simple rules and appealing, large elements, the HOP HOP game is suitable for children as young as 2.5 years old. 
Meet the adorable Pupu Bunny!
It's the Babies' new friend. Today, they are going together to the burrow to eat a delicious carrot cake. Many Adventures and frolics await them along the way. Join them and enjoy the play while completing fun tasks. O, bunny! Wait for us! Hey, look! The funny bunny is inviting you to play and frolic together. 
Lay out all tiles in three colours, so that they form a road. At one end, place the bottom part of the box depicting Eddie Teddy's house. This is going to be your finishing line. Place the bunny right before the beginning of the road. This is a cooperative game. In your turn you roll the die and move the bunnyto the nearest tile in the colour indicated by the throw. Switch the tile to the back side and follow the activity involving the bunny. For every 2 tiles you win the activity, you will get a carrot. If you collect 3 carrots, the Pupu Bunny and the Babies will be able to make a carrot cake. 
The game ends once you bring the bunny to the burrow and it jumps inside th house through the three-colour door. 
At the end, you can count all the colledted tiles and carrots. Will you manage to bake a carrot cake?

The Brickville town keeps growing bigger and bigger. New buildings are being built every day. Find the right elements and help complete another project before bad weather thwarts your plans and prevents you from putting up the building in time. 
You will move the construction man cooperative along the street by the number of steps thrown by a dice. When the man lands on a red, yellow or green coloured field, you can take the brick in the same colour to built your construction. If he lands on a blue fiel with water drop, put one droplet token into the rain gauge. If he lands on a purple field, you have to remove a brick from the construction area.
