Your task is to collect all bricks before you reach the finish field or before the rain gouge fills with droplet tokens. If you manage to do so, you can put up a building according to one of the projects shown on a drawn card.

Korean feat about election. 
The 36 pieces of wooden token with sun, Star, moon and cloud and each sign with the numbers from 1 to 9 will be shuffeled and evenly spread to all players.

In your turn you can play combinations of 2-3 tokens with different numbers in a row (street) or the same numbers with different signs (twin/triple) in front of you.
The next player can only play a higher combination as the player before in this round. Hierarchy of signs is defined by Sun > Moon > Star > Cloud. 
If no one will play a higher combination as you did, you win this round and you can start a new round.

The first player who plays his last token will win the game immediately.

boardgame: Tour of Duty: USS Barney – Greyhound of the Fleet, Cutting Edge of the Sword of Freedom
description:
A small solitaire game in Operations Magazine Issue 31, where you are an engineer on a WWII destroyer dealing with mishaps, crew, captain and other misfortunes, all while just trying to serve your tour of duty.

From the Rules:
"Every turn you roll a single die once to determine the random event table and again to select a particular event. For each event, choose (as your decision, not randomly) one of two courses of action and adjust the ship's status markers as indicated . . . Set these markers up by rolling one die for position (except TOUR, which always starts at the Welcome box). Flip the coin to see if it starts heads {plus] or tails [minus]up. During the Player Options Phase, you can turn your sweat into some ship improvement, or not."

There are 5 tracks, each from 1 to 6, that you need to keep above "1" before your Tour (a separate track) ends.  These tracks are Hull, Plant, Engineer, Captain and Crew. If ANY fall below "1" you lose the game.

Play with the jungle animals by shooting the discs into the goals! 
Two game versions are possible to play:

     Be the first to get all the animal counters to the jungle.
     Get 5 points by shooting the ball through the goals. A coloured dice will select the goal to shoot through.

boardgame: Mickey's Stuff for Kids: Memory Match Game
description: Memory Game with Disney characters. You don't need to find pairs but the single characters, were you have 9 on your personal 3x3 board.

boardgame: Слово за слово: Замес (Word by Word: The Mess)
description:
In Слово за слово: Замес ("Word by Word: The Mess"), you need to roll dice and make words from the letters that appear. What makes it difficult is that the time limit and number of letters per word are limited, and the more common a letter is, the fewer points you will earn for using it!

You will have 7 rounds to score valuable points. At the beginning of the round, roll 6 dice with letters and start the timer. While the timer is running, each participant comes up with a word containing at least one dropped letter. However, the more letters there are, the better! The invented word is pronounced out loud and fits into the appropriate line for this round. After one of the players has uttered a word, no one else may use it until the end of game.

The round ends when time runs out or all words are written. Then errors are checked and points awarded: the more letters used, the higher the score. The author of longest word gets 3 bonus points. Table with letters earns additional points for each crossed-out letter, that is used. Then turn goes to next player in a circle.

After 7 rounds, points for words, bonuses, and crossed-out letters are added up. The player with the highest score wins. If several players score equally, they all share the victory.

—description from the publisher (translated)
Exclusive to the Festival de Jeux, Cannes, special edition of Unanimo. The same gameplay as regular Unanimo Party, but with 100 game related themes.

Purpose of the game is to create complete sentences with your own pieces or crossing them with other sentences previously made. The longer the sentences are, the more points you earn, up to the target score. Sentences can be unrealistic, imaginative or paradoxical as long as they are correct in terms of grammar and they receive approval from the other players. 
Each player draws 15 pieces of puzzle with one word from a shuffeled bag. During your turn you play one sentence, each word is 1 point. Words used from the table and is used as crossing worth 2 points rather than 1. 
Winner is the player sho first reaches or exceeds the score set as a goal.

boardgame: Dominion of the Spear: Expansion – Fight 36 Battles of the Ancient World
description:
This is an expansion for the Dominion of the Spear solo wargames rules and contains 36 ancient and medieval historical battles with each having a very brief background, army lists, suggested historical deployment, and very brief description of what happened in the actual battle and the consequences.

Includes a discussion on terrain and optional rules on activation and reserves that allow more player input, changing the feeling from watching a battle to being the generals directing the troops. 
Needs the Dominion of the Spear rules.

Designed to be played solo - refight the battles and see if history changes! Then try different deployments for the armies.

The battles are:

     Megiddo 1479 BC, 
     Kadesh 1274 BC, 
     Arrapha 616 BC, 
     Thymbra 536 BC, 
     Marathon 490 BC, 
     Plataea 479 BC, 
     Gaugamela 331 BC, 
     Ipsus 301 BC, 
     Raphia 217 BC, 
     Cannae 216 BC, 
     Magnesia 189 BC, 
     Carrhae 53 BC, 
     Taurus 39 BC, 
     Edessa 260 AD, 
     Immae 272 AD, 
     Adrianople 378 AD, 
     Chalons 451 AD, 
     Badon Hill 517 AD, 
     Dara 530 AD, 
     Taginae 552 AD, 
     Yarmouk 636 AD, 
     Nechtansmere 685 AD, 
     Tours 732 AD, 
     Brunanburh 937 AD, 
     Lechfeld 955 AD, 
     Civitate 1053 AD, 
     Hastings 1066 AD, 
     Manzikert 1071 AD, 
     Dorylaeum 1097 AD, 
     Hattin 1187 AD, 
     Arsuf 1191 AD, 
     Ain Jalut 1260 AD, 
     Bannockburn 1314 AD, 
     Kulikovo 1318 AD, 
     Kosovo 1389 AD, and 
     Agincourt 1415 AD


—description from the publisher
family: History: Wars of Alexander the Great
History: Wars of the Diadochi and Syrian Wars

description:
The battle is evolving, the computations are new. Even more challenge, strategy and intrigue.
CLASH: Parallax is an Action Card expansion for the card game CLASH: A Galactic Sport. Parallax is 13 Action Cards (4 of each) and 2 all new Event Action Cards (54 cards in total).
Download the Parallax Action Card book in the files section for a look at each Action Card, their FAQs and more.

Card games where the most "cash" wins! Easy to learn and fun to play for all ages. Featuring stunning artwork from US currency these premium card offers a variety of fast-paced games of strategy, speed and bluffing.

Rules booklet for 10 games included with the game (and more games and rules on the website). A few of the games are:

Bank Robber - Take "money" from your bank and secure it in the vault before you get robbed!

Dead Presidents - A trick taking game of dead men.

Cash Grab - A speed game and frantic fight for the big bucks!

Ben Rummy - The multiplayer twist on Gin Rummy where the most "cash" wins.

Dollartaire - A solo game to clean out the bank.

and many more!

—description from the publisher

Dominion of Pike and Shot is a very very simple set of solo wargames rules, built on the Dominion of the Spear game engine, allowing one to fight two Renaissance armies (1495 - 1667 AD) against each other in around ten minutes. 
They are written for the game to generate a plausible and with some imagination an amusing narrative. 
Although figures can be used, battles can easily be fought on paper (no measuring) using pen or pencil and two different coloured six sided dice.

95 army lists are included divided into the periods: Italian Wars, Ottoman Turk Wars, Northern and Eastern Europe, England and Scotland and Ireland, Dutch Revolt (80 Years War), French Wars of Religion, Thirty Years War, English Civil Wars, Wars of the Polish Deluge, and Around the World. Armies consist of 3-6 units or commands and the points system is included. There is a complete play through of a battle between a French army and a Spanish army in the latter part of the Italian Wars.

A very brief introduction to Renaissance Warfare, and Designer Notes are included along with a quick reference sheet on the back cover. 
The rules include rules for pre-battle artillery bombardments, artillery, rallying units, Spanish Tercios, and different types of cavalry such as Reiters and Cuirassiers.

—description from the publisher
family: History: Classical Age of the Ottoman Empire

boardgame: ミニミニシヴィライゼーション (Mini Mini Civilization)
description:
This is the highlight of the game. A Euro game that is 10cm square and fits on a single notepad.
An hour-long civilization development simulation game, now available on a notepad.
A game of choice, interaction and dilemma.
A pocket-sized travel companion that doesn't use pieces or dice. Solo play is also available.

Each player's civilization is made up of a 5x5 grid. At the start of the game, 9 squares are blank, and the numbers 5-20 are randomly filled in for the remaining squares with 1 of the numbers being the player's civilization starting point/square.

On your turn, branch out from your starting square to any of the 8 orthogonal connecting squares. Compare the number to the previous player's chosen number and gain benefits depending on whether your new square's number is higher/lower, odd/even.

Benefits range from exploring (for food), improving science & industry, increasing your arts & culture. After 25 rounds, score points from your balance of industry, production, and science and also gain bonus points for excelling at certain categories compared to your neighbours.

Protect the Mothership is a solo card game using a standard 52 card deck.

Protect the Mothership is part of the Space Piracy collection of solo standard deck card games.

In Protect the Mothership, your mothership is under attack! It is currently stalled in space and the enemies show no sign of backing off. In order to escape, you're going to have to get the warp engine back online. To do so, you need to get your repair ship into position so she can do her work. You better put those fighters to work too before time runs out!

You start the game with a mothership and 3 helper ships (2 fighters and 1 repair ship). You must fight off waves of enemies while taking advantage of any opportunity to repair your mothership's warp drive.

—description from the publisher

You've got just a little time before art class starts. Can you help your group vibe by swapping students around?

In Vibes, players take turns swapping one of their cards with someone else's card, after which the player you swapped with takes the next turn. What do you do if nobody is picking you? When it's not your turn, you may raise a card from your hand with the promise to reveal it if you're picked. If that happens, play your raised card face up in front of you before the active player swaps with you; they'll have more options, and you can play bells in this way.

Your group of five cards — whether in your hand or revealed in front of you — will vibe if they have either all the same shape or all different shapes, and any number of players can win! If more bells are face up than the number of players, the game ends, so be careful when revealing cards as the game might end before you can swap...

Les Nominés du Néombre is a cooperative adventure and strategy card game. It features a campaign lasting several dozen hours, divided into short 30-minute games for 1 to 4 players.

The rules are designed to be easily understood and accessible to less-experienced players, and the mechanics are revealed as you progress through the campaign, allowing for an advanced depth of strategy as the difficulty increases.

The game immerses you in a unique, referenced universe thanks to several hundred hand-crafted line illustrations.

Adventure pitch:
Since the Neombre rift incident, the Vermalian lands have become hostile and populated by aggressive creatures.
Play as a group of explorers in search of answers in a cooperative adventure where every battle tests your individual strengths and your team's strategy.

Take a trick-taking journey: Earn the most points collecting tricks over the course of 4 hands.

Bid well to score easily. If you miss your bid by one trick, you may still add Trek points from one of your tricks. Trek points are the highest number of the lead suit, subtracting the lowest number of the same suit. Pay attention: The suits are asymmetric!

The Trek suits are Hiking, Biking, Horse and Skiing. 
Traveling by Car or Plane is too easy: Those cards are trump and those tricks won't score Trek points.

Needed for scoring: Paper and a pencil or pen

—description from the designer

Challenge Accepted is a team-based party game where each team adds a task to perform or pasaes the turn. The team that didn't pass needs to perform the task consisting of all added cards to the challenge. The team gets the points, if successful. Once you obtain 23 points, your team wins!

A team shedding game inspired by Hearts and Cribbage.

Players will play cards from their hand trying to diffuse the bomb (play to exactly 30) or they'll try to blow up the bomb (play over 30) so they can shed out their hand of cards faster.

Each round starts with some card passing, so players know a bit of what their opponents or teammate has in their hands.

The goal is to have the fewest points, but only the lowest score on a team is counted, so rushing and blowing up to shed out might be worth it.

—description from the designer

The ball is in full swing and the wine is flowing!
But a threat is lurking among the guests...

Baiser Mortel is a hidden-identity game in which vampires try to discreetly bite the guests at a ball.
You'll need all your wits to avoid being vampirized.

In Baiser Mortel, players participate in a peculiar ball with humans and vampires alltogether but don't know who's who. They will waltz with each other in a danse of death whose tension will rise while inevitable end will come closer.

At game start, there is only one vampire among the guests, Vampire Alpha, holding the deadly kiss. All other players are humans and don't know, obviously, who is the vampire.
Once the ball has started, players exchange valse cards and use powers to gather information or spread confusion. When a human receives the deadly kiss, he becomes a vampire too.
Vampires group will grow as the ball goes on and they win if they become more numerous. Humans win if they eliminate the deadly kiss holder before that.

—description from the designer

"The world has gone mad! Suddenly everyone wants their own Big-Eyed Fluffypede! This is the chance of a lifetime to become a famous Plushie maker. Unfortunately, materials are running low and you have to collaborate with other crafters to make your dreams come true. Take your time, sneak your signature style in the Fluffypedes and try to finish them first. This is the moment you have been waiting for!"

Fluffypede is a quick and easy to learn card game where players are building a series of "Fluffypede" while trying to out-score the rest.

The objective of the game is to be the first player that completes 3 Fluffypedes. A player completes a Fluffypede if it reaches exactly the desired number of legs during their turn. Most of the time, this will be achieved by adding cards with legs to the Fluffypede.

On your turn, play any card from your hand, with your preferred half up, and add it to the end (the right side) of any Fluffypede on the table. The half on the top part is the "active" part, and the only one that has any effect on the length of the Fluffypede. Some of the card effects multiply, rotate or delete other cards. Some add curses or change how the Fluffypedes are scored.

If the card you are playing makes a Fluffypede achieve the victory conditions on the Head, you score that Fluffypede.

—description from the publisher

Take Take Boom is a betting and dice rolling game. First you place a betting chips on orange, white or yellow color on a play mat. After that you roll the dice showing if you can take all the chips in one color or if it say boom and all chips on a color is lost. After that it is another players turn. The winner is the player with most chips at the end of the game.

The best-selling book "Monsters and Spirits of Taiwan"(妖怪臺灣) by Lian Jing Publishing (聯經出版社) has collaborated with 2Plus Board Game Design to launch an original monster strategy game!

The game collects and compiles records of various Taiwanese gods, demons, and strange stories. These stories of gods and demons coexist with this land in a magical way; in the mountains, in the sea, and even in the human world. Some of them can leave mysterious legends and be passed down from generation to generation, while others are gradually forgotten...

Players play the role of respected members of their villages, and use the residence and migration of villagers to strengthen the village's momentum and score points. Sometimes they also need to rely on the power of gods and demons to boost their scores.

Game Objectives
The game is played over four rounds: spring, summer, autumn, and winter. At the end of winter, the player with the highest score wins, having built the most prosperous village in an era where gods and demons intertwine.

Introduction to the rules
The game is divided into four seasons. In each season, a corresponding number of villager dice will be drawn according to the number of players and rolled into the secret dice plate.
Starting with the starting player, players take turns taking their turns. After the action and task phases are over, it is the player on the left's turn.

In the action phase , choose one of the following three actions to perform:

     Population: Pick two dice from the secret realm and place them on your village card.
     Rumor: Pick a dice from the secret realm and place it on any God or Monster card to perform the function of that card.
     Faith: Choose a dice from your village card and place it on any god or monster card to execute the function of that card and obtain the legendary representative of that god or monster.


During the mission phase, check the dice combination on your village card to see if it meets the requirements of any opened mission card. If applicable, you can choose to complete a task card.
Completing different types of task cards will earn you different rewards.
After completing the task, add a new task card immediately.

At the end of the season, when all the dice in the secret realm dice plate are taken, the current season will end immediately and the "Influence of Gods and Demons" will begin.
The two cards with the fewest dice among the gods and demons will be forgotten and removed from the game.
In the player's village, there are three regions: mountains, plains, and seaside. The player with the highest dice points in each region can get 4 points.
A corresponding number of villager dice are drawn according to the number of players and rolled onto the secret dice plate. The next season begins with the player to the left of the player who ended the round.

Game End
When winter ends, the game ends and players calculate their scores.
If the player has a legendary object on the temple board, he or she can get points corresponding to the seasonal object.
The player with the highest score, which is the sum of the points gained from legendary items and the points gained during the game, wins. In an era where gods and demons intertwine, a thriving village is built.

Duellen is a game where each player challenge another player each turn. One player draws a card and read a challenge printed on that card, and then chose another player to compete with. The challenges can be to say as many car brands as possible, or as many US states as possible, or to stand on one feet the longest. The player that did the challenge better gets the cards. The player with most cards in the end of the game is the winner.

In Upland, players compete in a hidden movement puzzle as they work to either hunt or evade. A player controlling a trio of pheasants moves around the board, attempting to stay out of the zone that the hunting dog is able to smell. Things like wind direction and terrain throw some curveballs at both the hunters and the pheasants as they work to see who wins before the dog is too tired of running to continue and the game comes to an end.

Simple mechanics and a tight board give the game a pleasantly tense feeling, and creates some wonderful moments when the birds suddenly flush.

—description from the publisher

Words to Die By takes your game night from good to legendary, blending laughter, absurdity, and just a touch of existential dread. Step into the director’s chair of your very own horror/slasher movie, channel your inner screenwriter, and deliver the most over-the-top last words ever as you and your friends create hilariously dramatic demise scenes worthy of an Oscar (aka the Golden Scythe, our very own Death Oscar). How you win is to get three "Golden Scythe Nominations." The winner then gives an acceptance speech with a little twist.

Each round has a different director that is rotated around the players. They read a "Death Scene" card and the rest of the players choose a "Dying Word" card that they feel best fits that scenario. The director collects the "Dying Words cards that we chosen and then redistributes them to the players who will act out the line that someone in the group chose. The director can also add in the "Directors Cut" card that can add a flair to your performance, like deliver your line in slow motion, or say it in a pirate voice. The director then nominates what they deem is the best performance and gives the "Golden Scythe Nomination" card to that person. Then the person who wrote the line for the winning scene is given the "Writer's Award Nomination" card. First person with three Golden Scythe Nomination cards wins.

Time for an acceptance speech worthy of the Oscars....but the person with the most Writer Awards gets to choose three "Dying Word" cards and put them face down in front of the winner. The winner must not look at the cards until they are ready to say the line, then grab the card and say it in their speech. (example: Thank you, thank you, I want to thank my mom, who always told me...(Insert Dying Word Card, and find a way to incorporate naturally in the speech). Do this until all cards are gone and then finish your speech.

—description from the designer

Equalizer Game is an incomparable educational board game where numbers are manipulated on the game board to exploit their full potential. An exciting pastime for any occasion, it is just the right game for anyone who understands the rudiments of mathematics. It is designed to develop interest and mental agility in arithmetic. The game is played by building numerical equations with number tiles arranged horizontally or vertically on the game board in a manner similar to that of the crossword puzzle.

Each player strives to obtain a high score with his equation by making full use of their number values and manipulating then to get the added benefits of the Special Square.

—description from the publisher

In this totally crazy party game, the aim is to explain and guess piquant terms. But be careful! Each word must start with the letter rolled on the dice. Gibberish guaranteed. So don't start stuttering!

—description from the publisher

Oh no, winter's coming and the weasel has stolen all the squirrels' nuts before falling asleep at the foot of the tree!

In Opération Noisettes, help the squirrels to recover their entire stash, but beware: the weasel only sleeps with one eye open!

Will you manage to get all the hazelnuts back into the tree without waking her up?

How to play this collaborative board game?


     Roll the dice and retrieve one or more hazelnuts from the weasel
     Gently place the hazelnut in the hole of the tree indicated by the dice
     Be careful, if the hazelnut falls into the tree and falls on the table, a leaf is removed from the weasel
     If all the hazelnuts have been stored in the tree before the weasel has no more leaves on it, you won!

In a world rebuilt after environmental collapse, two specialists communicate across distances without modern technology. One travels the wilderness atop a giant cassowary, monitoring recovery efforts. The other maintains a vital waystation, where homing pigeons and signal fires bridge the distance between them.

Gameplay takes place over six days where each player performs three different tasks during the Day, Dusk, and Night. One player (the Roaming Player) is moving around the board, while the other player (the Waystation Player) stays still.

The Roaming Player moves during the Day action, sends a pigeon at Dusk, and responds to a signal fire at Night. Meanwhile the Waystation Player during the Day gathers elements from the water to create chemicals, at Dusk receives the pigeon sent by the Roaming Player, and at Night uses the chemicals to light a color-coded signal fire.

Although their actions may seem primitive, the brilliance in using nature to communicate shines through in incredible ways in Homing. Learn of the synergy between an infrasound-making cassowary and infrasound-hearing pigeon. Learn how to change the color of a flame by using the common elements that are found in brackish water.

Work with nature and retrace the movement of the Roaming Player by the seventh day in Homing!

—description from the publisher

The king demands that the best craftsmen of the kingdom create the most beautiful stained glass windows on the highest towers of the cathedral so it can be seen all across the kingdom. It’s a new opportunity for the artisans to share their skills and create work that will be seen for centuries.

Spires of Ironcliff is an expansion that change the rules of Cathedroll. You will create Stained glass windows on four of the highest towers of the cathedral. Beauty of the window is not the only thing you’ll need to worry about but also the speed because other artisans will try to create their order before you.

In this expansion, the board has been changed so now there are four columns to fill instead of six and instead of just rolling a dice to decide which column you will fill you compare it to a table so each dice gives you a choice of two different columns.

Dye traders from all corners of the continent converged upon Ironcliff, bringing with them vast quantities of vibrant, exotic-colored glass. This highly coveted merchandise was eagerly sought after by artisans from every trade, from jewelers and glassblowers to textile masters and painters. The traders’ arrival marked a bustling moment of renewal for the city, as each stall and shop was replenished with hues never before seen, from deep cerulean blues to fiery crimson reds.

More Glass is an expansion for Cathedroll that brings more variety and changes the strategy you will take while playing. Create the most beautiful stained glass glaziers that will be seen for centuries.

This expansion increases the amount of combinations of colors that is used in game which makes the game harder.

In the town of Ironcliff, the cathedral of the town rose like a beacon, spires tower over the city piercing the sky. Its walls stood strong, but the windows were bare, waiting for the artistry of the master craftsman. The King announced that every artisan in the city is ordered to create the stained glass that will be put in the windows of the cathedral. The Master that creates the most beautiful stained glass windows not only will be rewarded their weight in gold but will be also announced the master of artisans and their accomplishments will be known across the land.

Cathedroll: Young Artisans is a simplified version of Cathedroll roll & write game where you are tasked with creating stained glass windows that will be placed into the Cathedral of Ironcliff. Beauty of the window is not the only thing you’ll need to worry about but also the speed because other artisans will try to create their order before you.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Step back in time and embark on a journey to 1900s Brasil in this map for Dice of Steam. A land of captivating contrasts, vibrant culture, and natural beauty beyond comparison. Whether you’re exploring bustling cities or the lush wilderness, Brazil promises an unforgettable experience!

The most important cities are:
Brasilia - a bold vision brought to life by Brazilian president Juscelino Kubitschek, who sought to move the capital from coastal Rio de Janeiro to a more central location. At this time, the city existed only in the form of a grand, modernist blueprint designed by architect Oscar Niemeyer and urban planner Lúcio Costa. Construction was underway, with workers laboring to transform the arid Brazilian Highlands into a futuristic city filled with sweeping curves, expansive boulevards, and iconic buildings.
Manaus - a bustling hub of the rubber boom, positioned at the confluence of the Negro and Solimões rivers in the heart of the Amazon rainforest. The city’s wealth during this era was reflected in its grand architecture, including the opulent Teatro Amazonas, an opera house built with materials imported from Europe. As a gateway to the vast Amazon region, Manaus was a vibrant blend of indigenous culture, European influence, and economic prosperity, though it also faced challenges from the decline of the rubber trade in the latter part of the century.

Between these cities lie crucial rail lines that can grant additional market value to companies. Claim your ticket, unleash your entrepreneurial spirit, master the art of steam, and leave your mark on the history of the 1900s Brazil.

—description from the publisher

Step into a world where the ancient and the modern come together in perfect harmony. Dice of Steam: Egypt will take you to the 1930s is a land of mystique, adventure, and unrivaled beauty, offering travelers a glimpse into the cradle of civilization. Whether you are exploring ancient monuments or the bustling streets of Cairo, Egypt is a place where every moment feels like stepping into a living history book.

The most important cities are:
Cairo - The heart of Egypt, a dynamic metropolis where the legacy of the Pharaohs mingles with the vibrancy of modern life. Marvel at the towering Great Pyramids of Giza, standing majestically against the backdrop of the desert, and experience the splendor of the Sphinx. Wander through the bustling bazaars, where you can haggle for spices, textiles, and jewelry, or take in the treasures of the Egyptian Museum, home to the riches of Tutankhamun’s tomb.
Aswan - a serene and picturesque city on the banks of the Nile, known for its tranquil beauty and historical significance. As a gateway to southern Egypt and Nubia, the city was a vibrant mix of ancient monuments and colonial influence, with its peaceful streets lined with palm trees and grand Victorian-style buildings. Travelers flocked to Aswan to visit the stunning Philae Temple, dedicated to the goddess Isis, and to marvel at the majestic Nile cataracts.

Between these cities lie crucial rail lines that can grant additional market value to companies. Claim your ticket, unleash your entrepreneurial spirit, master the art of steam, and leave your mark on the history of the 1900s Egypt

—description from the publisher

For millennia, Odin and Zeus have tolerated and respected each other, but a shortage of followers and a thirst for glory have pushed them into an unwanted war with uncertain and fickle gods.

Each player controls a pantheon, either Greek or Norse, with a deck of 17 cards.

Each turn, a player draws the first card from his deck. He can either discard it or play it. If he discards it, he draws a new card, which he is obliged to play. Playing a card means placing it orthogonally adjacent to a card already in play. When a card is placed in play, apply its effects and compare its strength with that of adjacent cards.

Adjacent cards of lower strength are captured, i.e. they become the allegiance of the card played.

Mythic Arena proceeds in this way, alternating quick turns, until a 4-card by 4-card square is formed. Score 1 point by creating alignments of 3 cards of your allegiance, and 3 points by having the most allegiance visible at the end of the game.

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – First Contact Mission Pack
description:
First Contact in any mission is the most pivotal and having the right team for the job leads to success. The First Contact Mission Pack #4 brings a new level of play to Star Wars™: Shatterpoint!

Introducing new objectives and struggle cards which brings new opportunities for tactical and thematic games. This mission pack adds everything needed for more engaging play. Prepare for battle and gather heroes for the next mission!

What’s included
• 1 Mission Card
• 9 Struggle Cards
• 1 Punch Sheet

The latest mission pack is here for Star Wars: Shatterpoint. This new pack includes a mission card and 9 Struggle cards, First Contact Mission Pack (Mission Pack #4) adds more to the games of Shatterpoint with new layouts and struggle cards for players.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Deploy the Garrison Squad Pack
description:
The Deploy the Garrison Squad Pack brings the elite forces of the Empire to the front lines. Director Krennic, made popular by Rogue One: A Star Wars Story, is joined by the imposing Death Troopers and instantly iconic Shore Troopers.

Their gameplay brings exciting abilities to Star Wars: Shatterpoint and provides Imperial players with long range and multiple support options. For players looking to grow their collection with the forces of the Empire, this is a fantastic addition.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – All the Way Squad Pack
description:
The All The Way Squad Pack brings the Rebel characters from Rogue One: A Star Wars Story to Star Wars: Shatterpoint. The incredibly quotable Cassioan Andor is Joined by K2S0 and the Rebel Pathfinders in this exciting and thematic squad pack.

Using a variety of abilities, these characters bring more gameplay options to players who enjoy the Rebel Alliance. Paired on release with SWP51 and Director Krennic, this will build for an exciting gameplay addition to Star Wars: Shatterpoint.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Requesting Your Surrender Squad Pack
description:
The Requesting Your Surrender Squad Pack brings characters from the heart of the Clone Wars to Star Wars: Shatterpoint! Taking it right to the Separatists, Jedi Master Kit Fisto, his Padawan turned Jedi Knight, Nahdar Vebb and Clone Commander Fil along with Fil’s Clones are in this pack.

Providing a strategic addition to the Jedi and Clone units in this pack, players will get another big boost of Jedi and Jedi support units. These are dynamic poses that show off how well Star Wars: Shatterpoint can capture the Clone Wars era.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Салон Пана Фарта (Salon of Mr. Fortune)
description:
Салон Пана Фарта ("Salon of Mr. Fortune") brings the excitement of gambling into a strategic and social tabletop experience. Designed for 2 to 15 players, it challenges participants to make calculated wagers on different aspects of revealed cards, including color, type, and quantity of symbols. Players can also bet on whether others will succeed or fail in their predictions, creating an engaging mix of strategy, risk assessment, and player interaction.

Each round consists of multiple phases: betting, card revelation, and payout. Players take turns placing wagers on specific attributes of the upcoming cards or on the success of other players' bets. Some spaces reward accurate predictions, while others pay out if a certain feature is absent. Special power tokens add an extra layer of strategy by allowing players to manipulate outcomes, modify bets, or redistribute winnings. The game offers two modes: Capital, where players accumulate wealth to reach 100 coins, and Tournament, where players aim to outlast their opponents by maintaining their betting tokens across multiple rounds.

In the realm of Onirea, the seasons span five centuries, a fragile cycle of peace where nature regenerates in perfect harmony.

But now, the Machinists are extracting the very essence of the Incarnation, the sacred being entrusted with safeguarding life from the impending, merciless winter.

As the world plunges into chaos, the last of the Mages, the final bastions of hope, must unite to heed the Incarnation’s call.
Battling against time and corruption, will they succeed in saving Onirea before it’s too late?

Advance your heroes and team spirit in this cooperative adventure game with modernized mechanics: strategic exploration, innovative deck building, tactical card combat, and team-based resource management.

Quick setup, originality, replayability.

—description from the designer

Quillico - "The Every Which Way Word Game" -- has 134 octagonal letter tiles: 98 black letters, 31 red letters, and 5 wild tiles. The tiles are spread face down on the table, and each player draws 10 tiles and turns them face up.

The first player spells a word in the center using their tiles, and after that each player spells a word using their tiles that also uses at least one letter already on the table. The tiles being octagonal, the words can go in any of 8 directions, as long as they are in a straight line. Any adjacent letters must be part of a line of letters making a valid word. After playing their letters, they score the word(s) formed -- 1 point per black letter, 2 points per red letter -- and draw tiles to fill back up to 10.  Instead of playing a word, a turn may instead consist of discarding 3 tiles and drawing 3.

A wild tile may be used to replace a tile on the board, as long as the tile being replaced is used that same turn in making the new word. Any number of wilds may be used in the same turn.

After no more turns can be taken, the player with the highest score wins.

Paws, Paper, Scissors – a fun family card game where you play as teenage monsters competing with classic rock, paper, scissors combinations. On your turn, choose a gesture card to beat your opponent’s move. Use strategy and quick reflexes to be the first to get rid of all your cards and earn a token. The player who collects two tokens becomes the champion in this monstrously fun twist on the classic game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shape Me!: What shape is this?
description:
In Shape Me! What shape is this?, all players co-operate, trying to put down cards of the same shape in the same order. When the lead player says a word from a subject card, what shape do you think matches that? Everyone has their own ideas, so things might get confused — and the shape cards are placed face-down, so who knows what to think? When all the players have placed five of their cards face-down, the cards are turned up and checked. If the cards all have the same shape in the same order, all players win!

The game can be played at different skill levels, and as you play, the game gets harder. But for variation, you could choose to play an easy game, or a very hard game.

—description from the designer

Be the first to get rid of all your cards!
In Crack Story, players build a story together using their cards as the first letters of the words they add.
To add a word, you must first repeat out loud the Story from the beginning without making a mistake.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Soyez le premier à vous débarrasser de toutes vos cartes !
Dans Crack Story, les joueurs construisent ensemble et tour par tour une STORY en utilisant leurs cartes comme premières lettres des mots qu’ils ajoutent. 
Pour ajouter un mot, il faut d'abord répéter la Story depuis le début sans se tromper.

—description from the publisher

Try to fill your game board as completely as you can in Linyo using only four lines.

Each player has a player sheet showing a grid of squares in three colors, some of which feature a star. To set up, roll the four dice, which show the three colors equally across their faces. Place a circle on four squares in your grid that match the colors rolled.

On a turn, the active player rolls the four dice; if all dice show the same color, they can set three dice to the colors of their choice, but otherwise the dice lay untouched. Each player must then start one line (from a circle) or extend one line, passing through at least two squares that match the colors rolled. If the line hits a star, a player can extend that line one additional space.

If a player cannot extend a line by at least two spaces, they must cancel a line with an X. Additionally, they must X any line that cannot be extended. When one player has Xed all of their lines, the game ends, and whoever has marked off the most spaces wins.

Clockwork Carnival is a board and card game. Race your family and friends to fill all the places on the clock. Increase your score by matching colors and fun clock faces.

Each turn, players play cards from their hand that add up to the number of a point on the clock they want to build. They score those cards by either matching or not matching colors AND by either matching or not matching faces. Each turn, you must either draft a card from the draft pool and may fill a space on the clock OR discard any number of cards and draw back up to 5 and NOT fill a space on the clock. Game ends when one player has played all 12 points of their clock, which can only be filled once per game. Each round the person in last gets to draft first.

Nothing ruins a good picnic like bees. Buzz bees over to your opponent's picnic and try to steal their food. Will you take food cards, grab a bee card, or go for a cover card? Should you save your bees or use them now? Which foods are worth collecting, and which should you protect with a cover card? What foods are the other players collecting?

Earn points by collecting food cards. Bonus points for a set of 3 matching cards. Use a cover card to prevent your opponent from taking it with their bees. Take an opponents card by flipping the bee card and the food card. If the tool on the back of the bee card matches any of the 3 tools on the back of the food card, you've successfully swatted the bee away! If not, your opponent steals all food cards of that type.  Lose points for any unused cover or bee cards when the game ends.

A mysterious Harbinger has arrived with a dire warning: The Veil between the world of Darklings and the Real World is thinning and the Beautiful Monsters are starting to come through. Collect magical artifacts to survive the coming End Times.

Players will try to collect magical artifacts over multiple rounds. Each round will have a Beautiful Monster sneak through the Veil. Collect the best artifacts by the time the third thinning event happens to survive the End Times.

In the heart of a secluded grove, the Ælderwood (Pronounced ALE-dur-wud) stands as the sacred nexus of existence. To secure the tree’s favors, you will engage in bargains with five elemental fairies. But be warned, dealings with fairies are inherently one-sided…

The Ælderwood is guarded by five fairies, each a custodian of the elements of creation. To secure the tree’s favors, you will engage in bargains with the guardians. But be warned, dealings with fairies are inherently one-sided and you must try to make the best deals possible for yourself while leaving less desirable options for everyone else.

Some say Kur was the very first dragon, regardless- he had been around for a long, long time. Fact is, Kur has had eons to gather his dragon treasure and he has always had a fondness for shiny rocks. You and your horde had heard that Kur was getting old and lazy. You also like shiny rocks, but you are young and fast. You gambled that your youth and numbers would give you the advantage, so you raided Kur's lair. Brave or stupid - it doesn't matter - your gamble has paid off and you escaped with as many gems as you could carry. Now comes the hard part, how to divvy them up. You want to be fair, these are your friends, but not too fair...

Each player tries to penetrate the opponent's opposite protection zone and occupy their last POINT with one of their own pieces. The first to do this wins the game.

Each player always rolls all three dice. For each number rolled on a die, another piece must be moved. Pieces may only be moved straight ahead - and only forwards! Any other pieces  may be jumped over. It is also possible to jump several times in a row. Each piece can enter the first row of the opponent's protection zone as normal; then it may only move in the direction of the arrows! Other pieces may be jumped over in the protection zone.

Opponent pieces are beaten by moving directly onto their space. For each opponent's piece that you beat, you immediately receive a protection block. Protection blocks can only be placed on hatched fields within the protection zone. Each protection block forms an obstacle that must be beaten in order to get past it.

The joker stands on the first red dot of the protection zone and is intended to prevent the opponent from quickly entering the protection zone. If an opponent enters the protection zone, the joker can be used if a 6 is rolled. The numbers on all dice can be used to beat the opponent's piece, individually, in pairs or in threes; the joker can jump over the protection blocks. The joker is then put back and can be used again next time. Other pieces may 
not be moved.

The red dots in the middle of the game board may be jumped over, but not occupied.

—user summary

boardgame: Landfall: A Cozy Volcano Island-Building Game
description:
Race to build land formations with your volcanoes on a 3D island that grows as you play! Landfall is a light, learn-in-minutes game where you move volcanoes around a 3D island that grows as you play.

in Landfall, 2 to 4 players use their volcanoes to shape an island, with the goal of being the first player to build one each of the three formations: Mountain, Valley, and Plain. Your ability to plan ahead, block, and even steal tiles from your opponent will be your keys to victory!

-description from designer
publishers: Evan and Josh's Very Special Games Company (Very Special Games)

description:
Melpomene, the Greek Muse of tragedy, has come to the festival to bless the playwrights with inspiration!

Muse of Tragedy 6-card expansion introduces masks, which can be used as any element when scoring patterns.

Shuffle the 6 expansion cards into the base game deck and play as normal. Each new expansion card has a mask icon alongside the normal icon types. These serve as “wilds” when scoring patterns at the end of the game. The expansion cards also feature new effects and scoring conditions.

A universal plug-in-play game expansion that changes any game into a party game. Draw from over 100 ability cards that fit seamlessly into any indoor or outdoor game ever made. Perfect for game nights, holiday parties, barbeques, tailgating, bachelor/bachelorette parties, etc. Drunk Imposters changes every game into a new hilarious version by giving players ability cards to use against other players during gameplay, adding countless new game elements and strategies to every game.

—description from the designer

The year is 1942. In the frozen ruins of Stalingrad, survival is a battle against the odds.

A sudden German offensive has shattered Soviet lines, leaving your squad cut off deep behind enemy territory. With supplies dwindling, enemy patrols closing in, and the brutal Russian winter tightening its grip, every decision you make could mean life or death. Can you navigate the war-torn streets, outmaneuver or fight off the enemy and find your way back to Soviet lines? Or will your squad be just another forgotten casualty in the ruins of Stalingrad?

Surviving Stalingrad is a card-driven survival game for a single player, packed into a portable mint tin with just 36 cards. There are no dice, no luck-based mechanics—only tough choices. Every encounter card presents a new challenge, from desperate scavenging missions to deadly enemy encounters. You’ll need to manage your actions and resources, have your soldiers work as a team, and decide when to press forward or find cover.

Your goal is simple: reach Soviet lines alive. But survival is never guaranteed—will your entire squad make it, or will some be left behind in the ruins?

-description from designer

boardgame: Mystery Squad Detective Series Case 1: An Unmarked Grave
description: Johnathon Hawthorne was found in a graveyard face down in Whitechapel and we need you to solve the case. A fully immersive murder mystery that you can play at home, solo or cooperatively. No internet required. Examine over 35 pieces of evidence on a 140 year old cold case.

Gauntlets of Glory is a fantasy-themed tabletop game for one to eight players that blends the exploration, scavenging and last-man-standing survival of a battle royale game with skirmish-style wargaming. Players build teams of heroes by selecting a race and class for each hero. Heroes start the game with minimal equipment and are placed on a battlefield where they navigate varied terrain, explore ruins, collect weapons, and plunder chests for armor, gold, and magic items. But time is of the essence! Heroes must eliminate all other opponents while avoiding being trapped outside of an ever-shrinking “safe area”. The winner is the player who controls the last hero, or team, that is still alive!

—description from the designer

Discover a forgotten land (well... a Draw Card) in the Discard Pile with the special Wayfinder rule! And don't forget to yell "UNO!" before the ocean calls you away!

—description from the back of the box

The new Wild Wayfinder Card replaces the normal Wild card in the game. Once played, the player must go through the deck top to bottom searching for any +2 or +4 card. Once found, they place the card on top of the discard pile, then the next player in order must draw the appropriate amount of cards.
If the card is a +2, the color of the card is the color that continues the game. If the card is a +4, the player chooses the color that continues the game.

Even the Oddities is a trick-taking game designed to be played with a standard 52-card deck. Included in this BYO Cards edition are 7 tracker/bonus point cards and 1 gem. The hook is that each trick "Even" or "Odd" cards increase in number value. To score, take 0 or 3 total tricks and/or go for bonus point cards.

Dealing in witchcraft has its rewards and drawbacks. Since magic affords you an extended life it’s inevitable that most covens come to an end. “It’s not you, it’s the last 300 years.” As the “divorce” is underway, your fellow witches cast spells to alter the value of cards in your collection. You must play the game to keep your oddities and curiosities.

—description from the 'designer

The Emperor has grown paranoid about a potential overthrow of his rule. His current machinations have his sights set  on a village where the villagers have resorted to speaking to others in whispered songs fearing the Emperor’s wrath. The Emperor has tasked his ninja masters to lead missions he has personally selected. Can you lead the way in completing the missions?
 In A Shadow Serenade, the players are moving 1 or 2 villagers each turn to try to match what they have on their Mission cards. They can vary from 1 villager on a bridge to much more complex with a specific villager not being able to be seen by two specific other villagers.

Players will be able to play Missions even when it is not their turn. Players will have to keep an eye on their opponents as they play watching for tells when an opponent reacts to a certain villager being moved.

The wily ninja players though have other tricks up their sleeves though. Mission cards can be discarded to move that specific villager to an empty build, which can lead to them being able to play a different Mission. The players can also coerce an opponent to give them a matching Mission by revealing one from their hand to that opponent.

Players will find plenty of challenge as they play again and again seeking new strategies to outwit their opponents.

—description from the publisher

In Showbiz Tails, players become the top TV show directors by enticing the most famous Superstar Staffies to join their cast.

Collect sets of three matching Co-Stars to bring Superstar Staffies into your show.

The player with the most points from Superstar Staffies wins!

—description from the publisher

The new operators come with their miniature, profile and linked tokens and components. New for this pre-order and created by the original game designer, this expansion gives you 8 new operators to build out your attacking and defending teams, introducing a wealth of new options and tactics to your 6: Siege games.
The Skyscraper map focuses on movement and sniping, while Villa presents multiple options for vertical play.

Gun Dodge is a fast-paced, action-packed party game for 2 players, designed to replicate the intense feeling of a wild west shootout. Players take quick actions with brief pauses to catch their breath. A die is rolled at the start of the round to determine how many actions both players can take. At the end of each round, all cards are drawn back into your hand.

We are also excited about the potential of this project and are actively seeking a publisher to bring Gun Dodge to a wider audience. We hope players will enjoy the game as much as we’ve enjoyed creating it.

Core Game Mechanics:

Psyche
Prediction
Player Balance
Programmed Actions
Guessing

For more details and to access the workshop download, please visit the link below:

https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3433335310

Trick-taking game with two gimmicks - there are two of each card  in the deck and if both are played in the same trick they are both "banished" and the trick ends immediately, and the aim of a round is to have the second most points.
publishers: ぷあぷあイデオロギー (Poor Poor Ideology)

description:
Reichbusters Reloaded is a mission-based cooperative board game with miniatures. Each game is a battle pitting the Allied Heroes you play against the soldiers of an alternative Third Reich full of Weird Science.

You control a highly trained, elite commando with extraordinary abilities. During your turn, you can play Action cards specific to each Hero to move them around the board and accomplish your mission objectives. You’ll face opponents who are superior in both numbers and firepower, and you’ll have to work with your comrades if you hope to emerge victorious. In Reichbusters, players win – and lose – together! During your missions, you can choose a direct or more discreet approach. However, be aware that the alarm will ring out through the base’s corridors sooner or later, and you’ll have to make your move!

The enemy forces are made up of Wehrmacht soldiers, but also terrifying mutant creatures from the Vrilmeisters’ experiments. All Enemies are controlled by the game and follow simple rules, after all the Heroes have taken their turn.

Rounds are played until an endgame condition is met and a winner is declared.

Autumn has arrived and brought beautiful colors to the forest – it’s the perfect time for a little race!
The critters plod, amble, scamper and scurry from tree to tree.
They all like a good snack, though, and can’t resist the various
temptations they discover along the way.

Aim of the game: Be the first to make it through the forest with your critter to win the race.

Wicked poachers have captured all of the creatures that normally roam free in the savannah! Work together to free them: Wait until nightfall, then go nab the keys to unlock the cages. Hurry though – and be careful – else the poachers might catch you, too!

Aim of the game: Work together to free the twelve animals before the poachers realize.

In turn, draw a tile from a bag and use either the key, the shown animal or both to free an animal from the board, or rouse suspicion among the poachers. Be careful when freeing the animals though, as freed animals of the same species placed next to each other will make a racket and warn the poachers!

It's walk day at Dino Parc! The masters are out and about, accompanied by their faithful pet dinos. A race is soon organized.

The whole gang sets off down the central alley, watched over by Billy, the park warden, who can't bear to see the dinos too far from their masters!

Which dino will be the fastest through the park gates?

Roll dice and move your keeper or dino on the track. If you land on a bench symbol, draw a bench card to gain an advantage or give a disadvantage to your opponents.

Hurry, hurry, there's not a second to lose! The moon is barely shining, and already the Potion Express pilots are taking to the skies to meet the orders of the village witches.

Pumpkins, mushrooms and earthworms - it's going to be a rough night!

Be the first to deliver 3 Potions to win the game. (4 Potions in a 2-player game)

Ingredient cards are shuffled, half face down, half face up, and 9 are placed on the table in a square grid. On your turn, select a row or column, flip all cards in that row or column, and take all face up ingredients. At the end of your turn, use the gathered ingredients to brew as many potions as you can, keeping any remaining ingredients until next turn.

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): Barik Farblast – Star Player
description:
Barik Farblast is a Hall of Famer whose use of crafty ball-launching technology has garnered him a reputation as one of the best throwers in the league – quite the feat for a Dwarf!

If your team needs a Thrower who can land a pass from anywhere on the pitch with pinpoint accuracy, prepare to be disappointed – there's no such player in Blood Bowl! However, it you want the nearest thing to it, Barik Farblast is your ma– er, Dwarf. His 'ballzooka' may be a Secret Weapon, but it gives him an edge over any other Thrower in the game in terms of range and precision.

Crumb Snatchers is a light set collection set stealing card game where the kids horde cookies before mommy gets home.

Crumb Snatchers has an exciting ramping mechanic where the game speeds faster and faster before coming to it's exciting conclusion.

As the game speeds up, players build cookie stacks, steal cookies from other players and try to have the most cookies by the time Mommy gets home.

The player with the most cookies when the endgame is triggered, wins.

—description from the designer

Dive into an exhilarating two-player battle game where the ultimate goal is to assemble the most powerful Dino team! Experience the thrill as you score points by storming into your opponent's territory or vanquishing their Dino. Don't miss out on this exciting competition—join us and prove your skills in the arena!

The Dinos are categorized into five colors, each representing different elemental attributes that can both complement and counter one another. These colors are fire, water, electricity, wood, and earth. The game map consists of a 4x6 grid. At the beginning of the game, players randomly select a set of Dinos and place three of them in their base. Each Dino possesses unique abilities, and the color of the Dino determines which dice the player can use.

During their turn, players take the dice corresponding to the colors of the Dinos they have on the map. After rolling the dice, they can allocate them among their Dinos to perform actions such as attacking, moving, and using abilities. The player who successfully moves one of their Dinos to the opponent's base wins.

In Katz Fatz, you try to get rid of your cat cards and score as many points as possible with happy cats. You can foist the grumpy cats and minus points on your fellow players. But be careful, there are cat toys in the game, and that confuses all the cats.

Katz Fatz is a card game in the style of Mau Mau, but here each player has their own discard pile. They can use this themselves, but so can the other players.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Search for a Giant Squid: The Game
description:
Embark on an underwater adventure like no other in Search for a Giant Squid: The Game, which sets young explorers on a thrilling quest to find matches, discover hidden marine animals and piece together a mysterious giant squid.

Navigate the game board using a mix of strategy and memory, looking for matches while encountering fascinating sea creatures and overcoming underwater hazards along the way. Pick the right gear token at the start of each trip or risk encountering an obstacle that sends you right back to the surface to start over. Score victory if you're the first player to find all the matches on your pilot map or the last matching piece of the mysterious giant squid.

boardgame: Toy Mini Brands!: Add to Cart Game
description:
Get all the Brands in your hands with this fun game featuring some of your favorite toys and games as collectible Mini Brand figures. Roll the die on your turn to move around the gameboard and collect tiles that match your shopping list. Hurry around the toy store and be the first player to find the three items on one of your wish list cards to win.


     Go On A Toy Shopping Spree: Hurry around the toy store and be the first player to find the three toys on one of your wish list cards to win



     Included Mini For Toy Collectors: Includes four collectible toy mini brands figurines


How to Play:

     Choose your wish list cards
     Roll the die on your turn to move around the gameboard
     Collect tiles that match your shopping list
     Be the first player to find the three items


on one of your wish list cards to win!

—description from the publisher

Really Get Into Character: Become your favorite characters in this more-than-charades Disney family party game that tests your Disney knowledge & your acting skills!

From Abu to Zazu, who are you? In this fun Disney family party game, you take turns as different secret Disney characters and answer questions from your friends as that character! Flex your acting skills as you roar like Simba or sing like Ariel. Can they figure out who you are? Score more points for correctly guessing sooner than anyone else. Immerse yourself in the magical world of Disney like never before!

How to play:

     Choose your secret character
     Players take turns asking questions & prompts


from the cards to try to figure out who you are • Answer as your character! The more convincing, the better!

     Score points for correctly guessing characters


the fastest

—description from the publisher

Urban Battle Junior Edition is a game designed for two players, played on a map board of squares. Playing pieces represents individual tanks or armored fighting vehicles. It is a fun and more simplistic look at urban fighting than Urban Battle.

Urban Battle Junior Edition is designed for 2 players. The goal of the game is determined by each Mission, provided in the Brochure. Each Mission has several game setups.

Three modes of play are offered for the gamers tastes:
Basic Mode – a relaxed and fluid game with the influence of luck via dice
Absolute Mode – no dice with a greater tactical and strategic feel to gameplay
Realistic Mode – the game now drills down to realistic firing angles via more detailed lines of sight requiring a ruler or some type of straight edge.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Slingers is a fast action, western themed, shooter game focused on movement through the board and using weapons and action cards to boost your attacks on opposing players.

The game can be played with 2-6 players and the goal of the game is to be the last cowboy standing by killing off the rest of the players.

The game consists of 102 cards, a coin, a 4-sided die, a 6-sided die, 6 player pieces, 4 7x7 square boards that connect to form a 14x14 map.

Each player will start off with 5 cards. During your turn you:

     Draw a card
     Play any amount of action cards and/or swap weapons
     Roll a 4-sided die to see how many squares you can move
     Do your movement
     Play any amount of action cards and/or swap weapons
     Choose to attack a player!


Attacks with weapons will deal damage depending on the weapon you use and the distance you are from the player you attack. For example if you were 3 squares from your opponent and you fired a Peacemaker revolver, you would do 1 damage per shot hit. You roll a 6-sided die to see how many shots are hit. (Peacemaker does 4 damage with no distance with a 1 dmg drop off per square)

Players start with 40 health and play until they are dead. There are also healing cards that can be played at any time and "reaction" cards that you play when being attacked such as the DODGE card that lets you dodge an attack.

—description from the designer

Urban Battles is a game whose concept goes back to Panzer Strike: France 1940 and later Tank Chess. Each playing piece represents an individual tank or armored unit. Set up takes no more than 1-3 minutes and is scenario dependent.

Urban Battles is designed for two players. The goal of the game is determined by each Mission, provided in the Brochure. Each Mission has several game setups.

Three modes of play are offered for the gamers tastes:

     Basic Mode – a relaxed and fluid game with the influence of luck via dice
     Absolute Mode – no dice with a greater tactical and strategic feel to gameplay
     Realistic Mode – the game now drills down to realistic firing angles via more detailed lines of sight requiring a ruler or some type of straight edge.

Twilight Peaks is a 2–4 player trick-taking card game where you play as an oracle, racing to be the first to build spires and collect stardust falling from a fissure in the sky.

In Twilight Peaks, both the players who play the highest and lowest cards in a trick get to collect cards from it.

Players use collected cards to build spires by stacking them in descending order within the same suit. A new card must always be smaller than the top card of its respective spire. If a player is forced to place a card that does not follow the descending order, the entire spire collapses and is discarded.

Managing spire construction carefully is crucial for maximizing points and avoiding costly collapses. At the end of each round, players earn victory points by having the highest total score in each suit. The first player to reach the required victory points wins!

—description from the designer

Cornered is an abstract strategy game played with 18 game pieces on a 6 x 6 grid. The board includes symbols for where all the pieces start. There are 8 squares and one circle piece of each of the two colors.

The circles only slide on the board--meaning they can move one space in any direction as long as it is open, and the circles cannot move into the perimeter (darker) spaces on the board. The squares can hop OR slide on the board into any open space, but they cannot hop and slide in the same turn. Squares must be able to land in an open space two positions away in a straight line in order to hop over another piece. Squares can hop over any other game piece on the board (your own and your opponent's included).

Players alternate turns moving one piece each turn. The objective is to hop your own squares over the opponent's circle. When a square is hopped over the opposing circle, it is removed from the board. The first player to clear all of their own squares wins the game.

—description from the designer

Unleash your strategic genius, playing as one of four beloved book characters, to outflank your opponents and dominate the board!

This game can be played with 2-4 players, as well as by yourself! Enjoy the solo play mode where you play against the board, with customizable levels of difficulty. Gameplay lasts about 15-30 minutes. For those with younger family members who want to join, the content is appropriate for all ages.

This game can be enjoyed by anyone whether they are familiar with the Love, Lies, and Hocus Pocus universe or not. If you're already a fan of Lily and her intrepid friends, you'll love the beautiful new art and strategic interaction inspired by the stories you've read.

—description from the publisher

Standoff is a 30-60min fixed-deck battle-royale card game.

Each player will command 8 unique super-powered champions and pit them against other players' champions to claim victory as the last player standing!

The Master Gatherer, a mysterious cosmic being, scours the universe for the most worthy of champions to compete for glory, honor, and power in the highest-stakes arena this universe has ever seen: Standoff. However, each team of eight mighty champions must be commanded by an overseer. The Master Gatherer invites you to choose a team and become an overseer. Will you accept his invitation and enter Standoff?

Many different champions have joined Standoff, all with their own unique abilities and fighting style. More overseers entering the arena mean more challenges you must face. Play to your champions strengths at the right time and victory will be yours!

—description from the designer

Do you have what it takes to turn a mess into a masterpiece? The game is simple: make the best use of your rough shape in just 45 seconds. It’s not the shape that matters — it’s how you use it. One player holds up a secret word card (they don’t know what it is), revealing the word to the other players who race to grab a card with a funky shape from the center. Using their dry erase markers, players illustrate the secret word incorporating their shape. After 45 seconds, they reveal their drawings. If the guesser can figure out what the word is, they award the card to whoever they think used the shape best. The more creative, the better shape you’ll be in to win.

How to play:

     Spread out the funky shape boards
     One player holds up a secret word card (they don't know


what it is, revealing the word to the other players

     Race to grab a shape card, and using their dry erase markers, players illustrate the secret word incorporating their shape.
     After 45 seconds, they reveal their drawings. • If the guesser can figure out what the word is, they award the card to whoever they think used the shape best


—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces
Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
In Tranquility: The Descent players work together to descend into the depths in search of Tranquility.

Players take turns placing cards to pave the way for their teammates. Journey together through glittering caverns and underground glades in this exploration of the earth. Like all games in the Tranquility series, The Descent is played in silence. Deep underground you will need to find new ways to cooperate in order to overcome the perils that lurk beneath the surface.

Players must complete the full grid of 40 cards. Starting at the surface players must descend through 5 layers of 8 underground caverns in order to complete The Descent. Rows are completed in order and cards may be freely played anywhere within the row you are working on. Each row must be completed with numbers ascending from left to right before a new row can be started.

The goal of the game is for the team to complete the grid before any player runs out of available actions.

—description from the designer

Drowning in unread messages? Do you get tens, hundreds, or even thousands of junk emails daily? Well, we have a SOLUTION for you...

Play You've Got Junk, the game that puts your clickbait-detecting skills to the test! We've scoured 100% REAL inboxes to find the most ridiculous and unhinged junk mail that exists. Will you be able to recognize the real spam and fool everyone else with convincing fake answers?

How to play:

     Each round, one player reads 100% real spam subject line with words missing out loud.
     All players write a believable word or phrase to fill-in-the-blank while the first player secretly writes the real answer.
     Once revealed, everyone votes on which they think is the real answer.
     If someone picks your answer, give them one of your Junk Cards.
     If you pick the right answer, you unsubscribe from one of your cards. Unsubscribe from all your cards to win.


—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
Cards, dice, and backstabbing… Welcome to Kouba, a game of cunning where betrayal and unexpected twists are at the heart of every match! Here, honor has no place—only the most devious, daring, and ruthless players will have a chance to win.

In Kouba, players compete in a merciless tournament, where every duel can turn the tide of the game. Roll the dice, use your cards wisely, and trap your opponents before they outplay you. But beware… every defeat earns you a KOUBA, a penalty that brings you dangerously close to elimination. Collect four KOUBAs, and the round ends! It’s time to count victory points and prepare your next strategy.

Bluff, manipulate, and betray at the right moment, because here, anything goes! A simple smile can hide a trap, and your “allies” this turn might become your worst enemies in the next.
A fun, fast-paced, and ruthless game, perfect for those who love strategy, deception… and especially the thrill of betraying their friends with a smile! So, are you ready to play? Or rather… ready to betray?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Monopoly: Дива України (Monopoly: Wonders of Ukraine)
description:
Ukraine is incredibly beautiful. Ancient castles, green groves, turbulent rivers and bustling cities - all this is it. We can enjoy the views from the heights of the Carpathians, or we can drive along the metro bridge in Kyiv and watch the Dnieper flow. We can listen to the cries of seagulls in the Odessa port, or we can wander through the narrow streets of Lviv and smell the coffee and fresh croissants. Every corner of Ukraine is beautiful. And in this game you can travel through it without leaving your home. Monopoly Wonders of Ukraine has united our entire country, from the fantastic Crimean fortresses to the Donbas arena itself.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Kids and parents go head-to-head in fun challenges to see who really is the household champion. It’s a kids vs parents showdown with games straight from the beloved TV show, Bluey. Each round, the parents will draw a challenge card from the deck and read it aloud. Match the color on the card to the special challenge – trivia, activity, etc. – and face off. The first team to win 4 challenges and reach Bluey’s house wins.

How to play:

     Split up into two teams — kids vs grown ups!
     Each round, the parents will draw a challenge card from the deck and read it aloud.
     Match the color on the card to the special challenge — trivia, activity, etc. — and face off!
     The first team to win 4 challenges and reach Bluey's house wins!


—description from the publisher

boardgame: SPQR: The Battle of Alesia 52 BC
description:
In SPQR Battle of Alesia 52 BC, you recreate the historical final battle of Alesia in 52 BC, during Caesar’s Conquest of Gaul.

In the heart of Gaul, 52 BC, a battle unfolded that would reshape history. Julius Caesar had been carving a bloody path through the Gaul lands. Vercingetorix, the famed Celtic warrior-king, confident, rallied the scattered tribes of Gaul into a desperate stand on a fortified hilltop in Alesia. From within the encircled settlement, he watched as a vast Gaul relief army of some 250,000 men gathered to finally crush the brutally outnumbered Romans, a storm cloud promising a final, glorious clash.

Unknowingly, Vercingetorix had cornered Caesar into taking one of three potential actions; assault an entrenched enemy in the Alesia oppidum, lay a long-protracted siege with the specter of a large army of Gaul tribes that could arrive at any time from his rear, or retreat. He placed Caesar in an untenable position.

Julius Caesar then did something extraordinary, defying the convention and military doctrine of the time, even by today's standards; Caesar elected a fourth option. He ordered his army to construct two massive walls and fortifications; one facing and encircling the Alesia oppidum, and the other the outside. He encircled himself and his legions within the two walls, thus deliberately placing himself in a position to be attacked on two fronts. However, the walls were not hastily built parapets, it was an intricate system of fortifications, towers, pits, and earthworks designed to place the attackers at a great disadvantage. This is where Julius Caesar’s military genius and unflinching commitment to the conquest of Gaul would be tested in the crucible of combat. A defeat at Alesia meant the end of Roman incursions into Gaul and the most brutal defeat in the history of the Roman Army. The stakes could not be higher for Caesar, Rome, and his legionnaires.

The day of reckoning arrived. The massive Gaul relief army surged against the Roman circumvallation lines. Inside Alesia, Vercingetorix’s warriors, driven by both hope and desperation, slammed against the Roman contravallation wall. It was a battle on two fronts, a desperate gamble for freedom for the Gauls.

Will you lead the Roman Legions to glorious victory, or be annihilated in the muddy fields of Alesia far from the light of Rome?

Available Now: https://www.bluepantherllc.com/products/spqr-the-battle-of-alesia

Promo Video: https://youtu.be/lIW3SApoaY8?si=8cP33F3VChSd0J3e

Solitaire Game (Roman)
Chit Pull 
Card-Driven ROBATA and Events
Simple differential D6 combat mechanic
Easy to Set Up and Play. 30 Min. playing time
Five-page rulebook plus additional color examples of play, detailed how to play, and historical background.

—description from the designer

The Unstable Elements Expansion Pack expands the game to four players with more tiles, more volcanoes, and a much bigger island. It also adds a deck of brand new Land Formation cards that turn the game into a land-building free-for-all and introduces "Elemental Cloud Rings" that give your volcanoes new abilities once you earn them.

—description from the publisher
publishers: Evan and Josh's Very Special Games Company (Very Special Games)

boardgame: De Bellis Fantasiae: Fantasy Wargame Rules
description:
DBF is a set of wargame rules that allow players to fight the great battles of fantasy literature, film, myth, or of one's own imagination, with miniature figures of between 6mm to 28mm scale, using basing that is standard across many rules.

Based on fast play mechanics drawn from Phil and Sue Barker/s famous historical rule set, De Bellis Antiquitatus, battles of a flexible size can be fought to completion within 1-3 hours.
