Armies can vary, based on a points system, ranging from as few as 30 figures to over 100 for a standard sized battle.  The book is complete, and no further purchases are required to play the game.

DBF features clear diagrams throughout explaining play, a plethora of army lists, a magic system with multiple spell types, special rules for customizing the abilities of troops, and finally a suggested campaign system.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Kids Chronicles: Quest for the Moon Stones – The Portal
description:
A DLC scenario that is divided into 2 parts.
It is intended to be played after completing all the scenarios in the base game first.

In Gems of Antwerp, players take on the role of gem traders navigating a volatile market to amass the most valuable collection.

Each round, players place cards to adjust gem values, then split their remaining cards into two offers—forcing an opponent to choose while they take the rest. This mix of market manipulation, hand management, and strategic drafting creates a dynamic puzzle where reading your rivals is just as important as collecting the right gems.

At the end of each round, gem prices shift based on players’ actions, and collections are valued accordingly. The player with the most wealth at the end of the game wins.

—description from the designer

This promo card adds mad scientist Len Nuanez to your Doomtown games.

Card text:

Mad Scientist 1

React: After Len successfully invents a Gadget Weapon, attach it to a nearby dude (ignoring control of the location and the dude being booted).
React: After Len successfully invents an Experimental Gadget, unboot him.

This promo card adds werewolf Christine Perfect to your Doomtown games.

Card text:

Experienced 1
Abomination

While Christine is a member of a posse attempting a job, she is a stud.

boardgame: BattleTech: House Davion Heavy Battle Lance ForcePack
description:
Battle Lances are the line troops of any large House Davion force. A Heavy Battle Lance ups the fielded tonnage, offering a significant force multiplier for power projection combined with even more armor to face off against assault lances.

Includes:

     Templar
     Falconer
     Thanatos
     Federated Suns Variant: Thunderbolt
     4 MechWarrior and Alpha Strike Cards

boardgame: Doomtown: Faster on the Draw Promo Card
description:
This promo pack adds two Faster on the Draw cards to your Doomtown games.

Card text:

Shootout: Your dude gets +1 bullets. If they are a Deputy, they become a stud. One opposing dude gets -2 bullets.

Get ready for a truly unique 1-4 player game filled with insane loot, crazy battles and tons of dungeon crawling adventure! Push your luck playing Solo for high scores or fight with friends to see who can escape with the best loot!

Delve into treacherous ruins in this card-driven adventure, where deadly traps, fierce monsters, and powerful minibosses await. Will you challenge the Boss and snatch the loot, or roll to battle rival adventurers in a race for glory?

—description from the publisher

Necronite is an epic strategic board game set in a dark sci-fi universe where players take control of unique factions fighting for survival in a galaxy threatened by an ancient force of darkness. Each faction has its own abilities, economic strategies, and approach to combat, offering a wide range of strategic planning. The game blends deck-building, exploration, diplomacy, resource mining, and tactical battles on planets and in space.

Players gradually explore the galaxy, colonize planets, mine resources, and upgrade their fleets with modules and technologies. The game’s economy is driven by the market value of Necronite, the primary currency of the galaxy, allowing players to pursue different strategies—ranging from aggressive expansion to technological advancement, diplomatic negotiations, and secret missions. Each game presents a dynamic and evolving situation where players must react to their opponents’ actions and the unpredictable rise of darkness, which threatens to consume the known universe.

The objective is to either complete your faction’s secret mission and secure its survival or achieve dominance through military, economic, or political supremacy. Necronite is not just about conquering; it’s about shaping the fate of the universe—every decision matters, and every move can lead to victory or ruin.

Designed for 2–4 players (with an expansion option for up to 8), the game offers deep strategic gameplay with minimal reliance on luck. Each faction, planet, and event adds new layers of possibilities and variability, making every session a unique experience.

—description from the designer

In this expansion for Panda Spin, the panda who was sitting in the middle of the bamboo puts on his tunic and becomes the Jade Emperor, decorating the 12 animals of the zodiac with heavenly powers. Now, players may recover a card from a trick, continue a sequence of cards played by another player, or even take a card from an opponent’s hand.

Are you ready to push the limits of Panda Spin?

To play with this expansion, at the beginning of each round, before the starting player leads, reveal one of the Animal Mandate cards and place it face up near the playing area. The Mandate displayed on this card may be used by any player for the duration of the round (until all but one player show out).

Each Mandate introduces a new way to play the game!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Undaunted: North Africa – Promo Scenario 12: The Prize
description: While supporting Operation Crusader, the LRDG were tasked with offensive operations along the coastal road, the enemy’s main resupply route. The relief of a besieged Tobruk was at stake, and the British were throwing everything they had at a much needed victory. Numerous attacks took place over several weeks. The LRDG frequently attacked motor pools, where they were able to destroy precious supplies and valuable equipment.

Players are meant to be rats of the world's inhabitants and be pleased to present fish to the Queen. 
However, in order to meet the Queen, you must make an appointment beforehand. 
Since fish are shared by everyone, let's make a bold appointment after hearing from other players' faces and actions!

This game is priests aiming to collect the Holy Grail filled with divine power.

Players collect scores as they repeat the round and aim to exchange them for the Holy Grail.

The round starts with the parent player swinging a four-color die, and if you win this die, you get the number of points in that eye at the end of the round.

After shaking the dice, everyone turns the hand cards and collects the coolnier using draft format.

At this time, sacrifice other than Lamb corresponds to the color of the dice, and the player who collects the most sacrifice can receive the dice of the same color.

After acquiring the dice, each player invokes the evil god's ability. You need a "lamb" of sacrifice to use your powerful abilities...!

In order to win the game, the new evil god's power that you get at the end of each round is the key! Because priests have up to two powers, choice is important.

Your dog is scratching at the front door because it is time for your daily walk in the park. Try to make it as fun as possible, but also avoid the temptations your dog might encounter. Paw by Paw is a competitive roll and write, print and play game for 1 to 99 players ages 10+.

In Paw by Paw, players walk their dog around the park over sixteen rounds, finding bushes to pee in, other animals or people to interact with, or even some unique spots in each park that present a different challenge each time. Players choose a dog with a unique talent and each round they roll three dice and perform three actions in this specific order: Move, Sniff and Play. Their goal is to keep their dog as happy as possible to earn points at the end of the game. The player with the most points is the winner and their dog wins the Golden Dog Ribbon!

Grab some pencils and dice and take your dog for a Paw by Paw walk in the park.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ツギハギキングダム (Patchwork Kingdom)
description:
Players are given a 4x4 world first, but only a part of it will be yours.

You have to divide it with scissors and give the rest to the player next to you.

Place the scissors so that the edges are cut off, and turn the larger scissors next to each other.

If you repeat the draft five times, you will see a variety of "topographic fragments" in front of the player.

Please rearrange it well and rebuild the world of high scores as much as possible!

At this time, you can stick out only two squares.

Since four scoring rules are selected from 16 target cards each time, different ways and arrangements of scissors are required every time you play.

There are nine types of terrain seats, so up to nine people can play at the same time!
publishers: Southern Cross Games (サザンクロスゲームズ)

boardgame: まるっとフィッシング (Marutto Fishing)
description:
Look at the arrangement of fish on a transparent sheet and draw two nets (a figure like ◯) with your senses!
Put a transparent sheet on top of the sheet with fish on it, and put a lot of fish that will score into the net!
A net can only re-create one for each round! Let's look at the sea and decide which net to leave!
There will be various events in the second round!

boardgame: Moscow '41: Soviet Aviation and Strategic Tools
description:
The Soviet Aviation and Strategic Tools expansion provides the Soviets with air capability and changes some game mechanics for air operations.

Game mechanics are changed by adding the Defender Air Reaction Segment, inserting Air-to-Air combat, and changing the Anti-Air rule.

The term “bomber” is replaced by “Air Units” to more accurately reflect the dual role (Air-to-Air and Airstrike) of participating Air Units.

Except for the special German Elite Air Units (replaces the old “Stuka” nomenclature), the two Soviet Air HQ follow the same rules as the two German Air HQ.  Thus, if in supply and not exhausted, the two Soviet Air HQ contribute to the total Logistical Value exactly as the two German Air HQ do.

The Expansion rules update the base game rules for Air HQ, Air Units, and Air Combat.

Part One provides minor updates and Part Two incorporates new rules.

—description from the publisher

Cleaning Service is a thrilling solo boardgame that blends the strategic depth of RogueLite with the challenge of Memory & Write. Step into the shoes of a crime scene cleaner, where your mission is to leave no trace behind. Clear each crime scene carefully while dodging security cameras, avoiding the police, and using your wits to stay ahead. Each new Run will test your memory, but if you score high enough, you’ll unlock powerful tools to make your job easier. Sharpen your mind and prove you’re the best in the business!

Objective the game: Clear each of the crime scenes until you reach the final map before you run out of attempts.

How to Play: The player must memorise the first map. You can take as much time as you need. The player must remember where the maps are  the spots blood and then write them on the player's sheet on a grid, collecting them, while dodging a multitude of obstacles. Each spot blood collected brings new attempts, but the obstacles subtract different attempts.

No dice, just your memory and a pencil.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Altay: Dawn of Civilization – The Dice Tower Promo Card
description:
Promo card available during the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

The Dice Tower (Achievement Level 3 - Wonder)
[End of Turn] Draw and discard a card. If it's a non-starting card, place a coin here. Gain 1 VP for each coin here

When you take a favor [personality icon], you can also place 1 [citizen icon] on Cleopatra. During [end of the game icon], in the Diversity step, you can use your [citizen icon] on Cleopatra as a card of the color of your choice.

Nexus is a card game where the core of the game is about combining two factions to take down the enemy nexus. Each faction has its strengths and weaknesses, its own playstyle, personality, and backstory.  Nexus takes the “easy to learn, hard to master” concept to the next level: it perfectly adapts to those who want a “plug and play” experience, but also to those who aim to master all of its possible combinations.

Your goal is simple: bring your opponent’s Nexus down from 30 energy points to 0 before they do the same to you. To achieve this, you’ll play attack, defense, and support cards, along with powerful events and faction-specific abilities to trigger devastating combos. Games are quick and dynamic, with short turns that reward both bold plays and careful planning.

In Nexus, players draft factions of 30 cards from a randomly revealed pool.  Depending on game mode, the number of factions and drafting method will change. Turns are divided into Draw, Action, Attack, End of Turn.  Offensive units are automatically destroyed regardless of the outcome of attack.

The base game comes with two faction boxes: Warnarok and Last Dawn.

—description from the publisher

Take on the role of characters known from Katarzyna Puzyńska's books. Observe your opponents, ask the right questions, note down the rumors and evidence you hear to deduce as much as possible from the answers you receive.

You can always pay a bribe in exchange for testimony on a key issue.

Who will solve the puzzle first?

The idyllic atmosphere of Lipowo was interrupted by another murder. The investigation is ongoing and the perpetrator remains elusive. Will you manage to catch him?

"The game will allow you to play the role of the characters in my books and solve an infinite number of different investigations. In the comfort of your own home or somewhere in the field. Wherever you want! Will you dare to answer the question: who killed?" - Katarzyna Puzyńska

Wcielcie się w postaci znane z książek Katarzyny Puzyńskiej. Obserwujcie współgraczy, zadawajcie właściwe pytania, notujcie zasłyszane plotki i dowody, aby jak najwięcej wydedukować z otrzymanych odpowiedzi.

Zawsze możecie też wręczyć łapówkę w zamian za zeznanie w kluczowej kwestii.

Kto pierwszy rozwiąże zagadkę?

Sielankową atmosferę Lipowa przerwało kolejne morderstwo. Śledztwo jest w toku, a sprawca pozostaje nieuchwytny. Czy uda Wam się go złapać?

"Gra pozwoli Wam wcielić się w bohaterów moich książek i rozwiązać nieskończenie wiele rozmaitych śledztw. W zaciszu własnego domu albo gdzieś w terenie. Gdziekolwiek tylko chcecie! Odważycie się zmierzyć z odpowiedzią na pytanie: kto zabił?" - Katarzyna Puzyńska

In Borobudur, the player becomes one of the contractors involved with Gunadarma to contribute to building this great temple. The construction of the temple starts from the first-tier, continued by second-tier and finished in the third-tier.

Borobudur is a worker placement game with double rondel mechanics. Each player moves one of the workers in the West or East Action Area to perform the action space where it stops. Tableau building mechanics can also be implemented by hiring artisans.

At its core, players work on a stone tile in their workshop to build into a temple construction. Occasionally, the workshop needs maintenance to avoid blunt conditions that cause it to stop operating. During the game, player may upgrade their workshop to develop its engine.

Hiring artisans gives player some benefits in various ways, including generating resources (labor). On the other hand, sending monks helps player to gain more points and generate bonuses in the temples.

—description from the designer

Norse Horizons invites 2-5 players, ages 10+, to embark on a Viking saga in a strategic area control board game from Waxy Stone Games. As ambitious Jarls, you are sent west to England to seek new lands and glory for your clan. Over 40-100 minutes, explore uncharted territories, gather valuable loot, conquer strategic regions, and engage in clashes with rival Jarls to prove yourself the greatest. Norse Horizons offers a rich Viking theme and strategic depth, while remaining accessible and engaging for both newcomers and seasoned strategy gamers.

The game unfolds across three cycles, each with distinct phases: Invasion, Clash, Upkeep, and Awards. During the crucial Invasion phase, hire Vikings to explore the map, revealing new regions and resources like wheat, trade goods, treasure chests, and wine. Claim regions and extract loot, but be aware that looting generates unrest, making those areas vulnerable. When you clash with other players over region control, engage in tense battles where you secretly commit coins and clash cards. Outwit your opponents through clever bluffing and tactical card play to earn trophies and conquer territories. The Upkeep phase provides a breather, resetting Vikings and unrest, and granting income for future expeditions.

Norse Horizons encourages high player interaction and lively table talk, especially during the bluff-filled clashes, while consistently offering engaging strategic choices. Randomized awards granted at the end of each cycle and unique final scoring cards ensure high replayability, making each game a fresh and exciting Viking adventure. Gather your clan, set sail across the Norse Horizons, and carve your legend into history!

—description from the designer

How long can you climb the highest snowy mountains on the planet? Rely on your partners in Snow Climbing, managing your resources in an innovative cooperative climbing mechanic. Be careful not to lose important items and try not to cause an avalanche. Surviving the cold is only part of this extreme challenge!

In this ladder climbing game, cards represent the players' gear when climbing the mountain. In each turn, a person plays a card that has a larger number than the card on the top of the climbing pile.

Another possible action is to "Change Route", adding the values of two cards of the same suit. The smaller card goes to the top of the pile - restarting the counting - and the largest goes to the "snow area". When no action is possible, players start the Camping Phase, creating pairs of cards with the same suit to save them from the "snow area".

Every round, a new avalanche condition is revealed: if, at the end of that round, the cards on the "snow area" met any of the conditions revealed, the players cause an avalanche and lose the game. Special abilities of each suit can be used to protect cards or save cards from the "snow area".

—description from the designer

Kugelblitz is played on a large board. The players each have a rotating plastic gun attached to their end of the board, behind a wire goal frame. The idea is to fire steel marbles from the gun to knock the puck into your opponents goal, and keep it away from yours. The board is slightly curved to cause the balls to roll into pots at each end for re-use.

This game is similar to Gunfight at OK Corral, Crossfire or Conterball.

Backed by specialist support teams, daring Zeo pilots battle each other for control of humanity's final redoubt; one hundred heliospheres. When an inter-system slipway vanishes the political maneuvering, corporate intrigue and covert actions give way to open war.

Zeo Genesis is a scalable skirmish miniatures wargame.  In Zeo Genesis, players alternate activating their models to keep play moving quickly. Each activated model gets multiple actions such as a Move/Move/Shoot (in any order), rewarding decisive play. The ability to react during opposing activations means that players are constantly engaged with the action.

Multiple scenarios allow for varied gameplay.  Campaign and solo modes are included.

Escape from Stalingrad Z: Reloaded offers new campaign content for each character, as well as upgraded accessories!

Escape from Stalingrad Z is a solo/coop campaign game of tactical combat which is set in the horrific zombie plague of World War Two. The year is 1942 and the city is Stalingrad. Your survivors are veteran soldiers fighting to stave off the zombie hordes in a war-torn wasteland while finding a way to escape from the decimated city. They must work together to find the best path, each scenario giving them options on which way to go next. The player's choices determine the fate of their heroes.

The game plays out over a series of linked scenarios that form a campaign. Each previous scenario in the Campaign will affect the start of the next, creating an ongoing story.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: BattleTech: House Davion Cavalry Lance ForcePack
description:
Unleash the House Davion Cavalry Lance! Includes the Hellspawn, Legionnaire, Gunsmith, and new Federated Suns variant of the Enforcer - no assembly required - along with four MechWarrior pilot cards and four Alpha Strike cards. Perfect for BattleTech and Alpha Strike action!

—description from the back of box

MegaQuest is a fan-made expansion that takes your HeroQuest experience to the next level. It transforms traditional dungeon crawls into a rich, immersive journey, adding depth and excitement to every step of your adventure. Whether you're exploring official or custom quest books, MegaQuest brings a vibrant world of storytelling, dynamic challenges, and strategic encounters to life.

What Sets MegaQuest Apart?
Venture beyond the dungeon into diverse and treacherous landscapes. Each terrain offers unique challenges and rewards, from dense forests and towering mountains to murky swamps and arid deserts. Along the way, uncover hidden treasures, meet unforgettable NPCs, and survive the hazards of the wilds. The heart of this expansion lies in its beautifully crafted tarot-sized campaign and quest cards, which create an evolving narrative tailored to your party’s decisions.

Custom Heroes and Intelligent Monsters
MegaQuest introduces an all-new roster of heroes, each with unique stats, abilities, and disciplines that allow players to craft their perfect character builds. Master powerful spells, hone devastating skills, and shape your strategy to suit your playstyle. Meanwhile, the game’s innovative monster AI system ensures each encounter is dynamic and unpredictable. Battles feel fresh and engaging with distinct behaviors for every monster type, even without a game master.

Seamless Integration with HeroQuest
Designed to complement the core HeroQuest set, MegaQuest works effortlessly with your existing game. A copy of HeroQuest is required, but no additional setup is needed. This expansion enhances the base experience with new heroes, spells, skills, and an expansive campaign system, breathing life into the world and making every choice meaningful. Whether you’re tackling official quests or fan-made adventures, MegaQuest elevates the journey.

Infinite Possibilities, Infinite Fun
MegaQuest’s modular design gives you endless flexibility. Mix and match its components to build your ideal campaign, swap out quest cards to suit your party’s style, or create entirely new stories. With customizable heroes, AI-driven monsters, and a wealth of encounter options, every session feels unique and exciting.

More Than a Game—A Complete Experience
MegaQuest isn’t just an add-on; it’s a storytelling toolkit. Its stunning tarot-sized cards, immersive encounters, and balance of strategy and creativity blend the best elements of a tabletop RPG with the fast-paced action of a board game. It’s designed to keep players coming back for adventure after adventure.

—description from the designer

A classic Battleship game, but… All your units are ready to go! There is almost no setup — just choose a page and start playing with no delay!

Open the colorful notepad and plunge into vivid worlds of candy, aliens, robots, and more. Extraordinary settings blend with familiar Battleship gameplay — everybody will like it!

How to play:
Youngest player goes first. He calls coordinates of a single square on a grid to shoot at! ie. “ I target a square at Column 8, Row 4" would be a legal move. Opponent checks called square against his left-hand grid containing his units.

If called square is occupied by his unit, he must answer
"Hit" and mark the square with a Cross. He must also answer "Destroyed" if all squares of a targeted unit are now covered in Crosses. Active player marks a called square with a Cross if it's a Hit, but he does so in right-hand grid of his sheet. Then active player gets another turn and must call a new square.

If called square is not part of any unit on his lefthand ocean grid, opponent must answer "Miss" and have the turn passed to him. Players mark missed squares with dots on their respective grids.

The winner is the player who destroyed all opposing units first.

—description from publisher

In Vibes Sing-along mode, a player picks a card from the deck and then picks a song that meets the criteria of the card. They start singing the song while mentioning at least three words of the song. Play continues in the counterclockwise direction with the next player continuing the song, singing at least the next three words of the song and so on. Whoever mentions the wrong word or hesitates is a vibe killer and is eliminated from the game. The next player after eliminated player continues the game by picking the next card and starts singing a song that meet card criteria… The last player left in the game is the winner and has the best VIBE...

—description from the publisher

boardgame: トリックアンドトリートテイキング (Trick and Treat Taking)
description:
Trick and Treat Taking is a unique trick-taking game with a playful twist! Players aim to collect sweets while avoiding mischievous tricks, all while racing to be the first to get rid of their hand. Unlike traditional trick-taking games, this one features a dynamic hand size that increases or decreases throughout the game, adding an exciting layer of strategy. Designed for 3 to 5 players and playable in about 30 minutes, it offers a fresh and engaging experience for fans of the genre. The game comes with TCG-sized cards (63×89mm), making it easy to handle and shuffle. Will you master the balance between tricks and treats to claim victory?

—description from the publisher

The Christian Charades Game is a fun-filled charades game where you get to act out Christian words/phrases for your team maters to guess. The game is played with two different teams, competing to figure out as many charades words as they can in one minute.

The game deck has 200 different charades in a 100 card deck (two Charades per card). The cards have two levels: Level 1 is normally a one word charade, and Level 2 has two or more words.

How to play:

     Christian Charades is a fun-filled word-guessing game where players act out phrases or concepts from the Bible while using a maximum of three words (excluding the word(s) being described) to help convey the gesture.



     The game starts by dividing players into teams, with representatives from each team taking turns to act out biblical words and phrases while their teammates attempt to guess as many answers as possible within a minute.



     The game comprises 100 cards, each with two charades, including a level 1 (easy) and a level 2 (intermediate) charade. Players must complete the level 1 before advancing to level 2. At the end of each turn, the team with the most correct guesses wins the round.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: ジェネリックテイキング (Generic Taking)
description:
There was a year in the pharmaceutical industry known as the "Miracle Year", when a wave of groundbreaking new drugs was approved. Now, more than 20 years later, the patents for many of these life-changing medicines are about to expire. To heal as many patients as possible, researchers from various pharmaceutical companies are racing to develop generic drugs and bring them to market.

This game is a must-follow trick-taking game where players take turns playing cards according to the rules, comparing card strengths in repeated "tricks" to determine the winner.

To score points, players must skillfully manage their hands and aim to win tricks using cards that match the color or number of the original (brand-name) drug revealed in each round.

However, beware of the four types of side effects—a crucial third element beyond color and number. Winning a trick with a card that shares the same side effect as the original drug will prevent you from scoring points, making strategic planning essential.

Step into the laboratory, navigate the competitive world of pharmaceuticals, and claim victory in the Great Generic Era!

In the city, by the sea, across the desert—once again, the sun sets today. Before night falls, in this fleeting twilight, Shadows intertwine and then scatter. This is such a game. Even the cards you place may unexpectedly merge together...

Sunset is a must-follow trick-taking game with five suits, each numbered 0 to 9 (10 cards per suit) where half of your hand visible! Before the game begins, half of each player’s hand is revealed, arranged in three columns in front of them (only the top card of each column is considered part of their hand). If a player has a card of the lead suit in their visible area, they must play it first before choosing from their hand.

This is your first day as a birdwatcher. You've imagined the excitement of exploring the Papat forest and spotting various bird species in Indonesia.

However, not all the birds you encounter are in the same condition. Some are migrating, some are endemic species, others need protection, and some are even endangered!

Of course, you won’t let that happen. Let’s protect Indonesia’s birds by completing various conservation missions.

Prove that you can be the best birdwatcher in the Papat forest!

—description from the designer

Have you ever told someone, "I love how you'll just wear anything"? Or hit the "close door" button on an elevator while someone is running towards it? If so, this is the game for you.

Bad Girl Era will reveal the mean things you've said or done as you compete with your friends to see who is truly in their bad girl era. But don't worry, the virtue prompts give you a chance to show that you have a nice side.

HOW IT'S PLAYED
Take turns drawing cards with a "Bad Girl" and "Virtue" prompt. If a player has said or done what's listed on the "Bad Girl" prompt, they must slink up one space on their "Evolution Of A Bad Girl" card. However, if they've done the redeeming virtue, they sashay back down a space.

The player who reaches the end of the path first is the loser and the baddest of the group. The player whose playing piece is on the lowest space wins the game and can brag about being a better person than all their friends.

—description from the publisher
A booster / mini-extension with seven new cards for the game "Les toits de Paris", each with a special action.

This is a trick-taking game with bluffing, where players take on the role of comedians competing for the spotlight.

The game features 27 performer cards across three comedy genres: Rakugo (storytelling), Manzai (duo comedy), and Sketch Comedy. Each player must play a card from a different genre than the others (Must-Not-Follow rule). The player with the highest number wins the round and collects the most cards.

However, there's a twist—the third player to play must place their card face-down and claim they have the highest value. They can bluff, but if their lie is exposed, they lose their card instead!

Use strategy, deception, and careful observation to outwit your opponents and collect the most cards.

You are lost in an endless night broken only by whispers of want and fervor. You need change, peace, or warmth to feel whole again. Others may want the same thing. There isn’t enough for everyone—is there? You panic. You fear. You rage. And with those feelings comes something more.

Will you seek to become whole? Or will you satiate the dark desire from deep within?

Players discover their fate as they reach various outcomes at the end of the game. Players might come into conflict or cooperate with one another in pursuit of their desired outcome or change which outcome they pursue as play evolves.

Players start as Hollow, but may reveal themselves to discover unknown opportunities and regrets as they pursue their dark desire.

—description from the designer

Don’t let their warm, fuzzy exteriors fool you.

These cuties have teeth. Claws and horns, too! Beware the Dust Bunnies that lurk beneath the couch, and avoid the Pawsonal Trainer (unless you really want a workout). We don’t talk about the Wolpertinger, either, and not just because we’re unsure it’s real.

Grab your Bunchucks, don your Fur Cloak, and Go Down the Rabbit Hole, because it’s rabbit season! (Or duck season – it depends on who you ask.)

—description from the publisher

Master the Bokken, a wooden katana used in samurai training. Develop your tactics and strategy by combining attack, block, parry, and refocus cards in rapid card play as you try to score three hits before your opponent does. Remember that somewhere in the deck is the feared unblockable attack!

—description from the publisher

Dreyfall is a 2-player strategy game of tactical combat and survival in a fractured woodland. Once, the creatures of the forest lived in harmony, guided by the magic of the Dreyshard, the great gem of the forest. When the Dreyshard was separated, its power plunged the land into chaos. Now, two rival factions battle for control, each guarding half of the Dreyshard within their home tree. To restore peace, you must defend your own stronghold while launching daring attacks to reclaim the lost shard from your opponent. Each turn, players make critical decisions about movement, attacks, and powerful abilities, exploiting a cyclical combat dynamic to gain a tactical edge. Mastering this dynamic is key to victory! The fate of the forest is in your hands—restore the Dreyshard and bring peace back to the land.

—description from the publisher

Take flight with Mi Cielo, where strategy takes wing in a captivating race across the sky. Out-maneuver your opponent in an ever changing aerial arena, as you bounce and soar across the clouds to determine who is the master of the sky.

Move across a sky built of cards by jumping to balloons of your bird's color to occupy your opponent's starting cards. Watch out though, landing on a balloon can send out puffs of air which flips adjacent cards, constantly changing the playing field!

Be inspired by renowned storybook illustrators from the Golden Age of Illustration! Gather your materials and use them to collect your favorite scenes from old-fashioned fairy tales and nursery rhymes in this delightful game for two to four players.

This is a mechanically simplified version of Artistry, oriented towards children and families.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

boardgame: トリック帝王-氷結の雪女 (Trick Emperor: The Frozen Snow Woman)
description:
In Trick Emperor: The Frozen Snow Woman, you’ll harness the mystical powers of Ice and Snow to outmaneuver your opponents and claim victory. Using the Snow Woman's abilities, you'll need to carefully plan your plays, anticipate your rivals' moves, and dominate the game with precise trick-taking strategies.

Trick Emperor: The Frozen Snow Woman is trick-taking game for both beginners and experienced players. Features unique Ice and Snow mechanics, adding a refreshing twist to traditional trick-taking. Part of the Trick Emperor series—can be combined with Trick Emperor: The Witch of Order for expanded gameplay possibilities.

boardgame: トリック帝王-秩序の魔女 (Trick Emperor: The Witch of Order)
description:
The Sanctuary of Order in another world was safeguarded by the Guardians, maintaining a delicate balance between order and chaos. However, as the Wheel of Order began to falter, the world faced the threat of imminent collapse. In response, the Guardians summoned challengers from across realities to take on the Trial of the Trick Emperor.

Trick Emperor: The Witch of Order is a strategic and engaging trick-taking game for players of all levels. Can be combined with Trick Emperor: The Frozen Snow Woman for new and exciting gameplay possibilities!

Will the challengers be able to overcome the trials of fate and restore balance?

boardgame: Toy Mini Brands!: Shop & Swap Game
description:
GOAL
Shop for pairs of Mini Brands Collectible cards to add to your collection, but don't get left with the Empty Cart card!

SETUP
Shuffle all the cards, then deal them facedown among players until all are handed out. Not everyone will have an equal number of cards.

Look at the cards in your hand without letting the other players see! Set aside any matching pairs of toys face-up. If you have three matching toy cards, set aside only two of those cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tiny Epic Dungeons Adventures: The Phantom Voyage
description:
Tiny Epic Dungeons Adventures: The Phantom Voyage continues the epic journey across the World of Aughmoore, picking up where the core game left off.

This expansion adds three new chapters, serving as a narrative bridge (Adventure #1.5) between the first (Adventure #1.0) and the next thrilling installment (Adventure #2.0).

Beyond the story, The Phantom Voyage introduces innovative new Heroes, valuable Loot, powerful Spells, and formidable Minions and Mobs, adding fresh challenges and excitement to your adventure and to the Tiny Epic Dungeons System.

—description from the publisher

In Surplus of Sloths, the rivers are flooding, and players are wildlife rescuers each trying to save sloths and make them happy so that they can be awarded even more sloths!

To set up, use as many types of sloths as the number of players, plus one. Each player takes three cards in hand, then discards one card to the "surplus net". Deal out two rows of cards, with each row having one more card than the number of players. The active player drafts one card from a row, then each other player drafts from the same row, with the final card falling into the surplus net. Add a new row of cards, then the next player clockwise starts the next turn.

When you all the rows have been drafted, with remaining cards falling into the net, players compare their longest run of each sloth type. A type has cards numbered 1-7, along with three Xs, which are wild. Whoever has the longest run of a type, with ties being broken by whoever has the highest numeral in their run, claims all cards in the surplus net of that type. The value of a type equals the sum of the numbers in the net multiplied by 1-4 depending on how many sloths of that type (0-3) are also in the net. Whoever has the most points wins.

You can also play with four baby sloths of value 0 that count as all types. They aren't worth any points in the surplus net, but they can lengthen all of your runs...assuming they would otherwise start with 1, that is.

boardgame: Tiny Epic Dungeons Adventures: Skyward Revenge
description:
The Skyward Revenge mini expansion introduces a modular side-quest chapter that acts as its own play session and can be integrated at any point in the storyline.

Additionally, the mini expansion brings new Mobs and Minions to the Tiny Epic Dungeons System. Each presenting a unique challenge that will test even the best players!


            Kickstarter-Limited Exclusivity - Deluxe content will not enter regular distribution. However, it will be available in limited supply at conventions, through contests, online at the BGG promo store, and directly from Gamelyn Games on our website or through our Amazon store.
        
    


—description from the publisher

Expands Otaru 1899 with individual goal cards. Each player is dealt two ambition cards at the beginning of the game. When a player fulfills the condition on a card, they can claim its special effect. They then discard their extra ambition card.

POH! is a storytelling game that brings Chilean slang to life. Using the cards in your hand, you'll craft fun and engaging stories while discovering local expressions.

In this second edition, there are four exciting game modes:


     Collaborative: Work together to build a creative and entertaining tale.
     Competitive: Challenge your friends to see who can spin the best story.
     "Gossip Just for Fun": Embrace the chaos and let the rumors fly!
     Trivia Mode: Test your knowledge by guessing the meaning of Chilean slang words from the cards.


Unlike the first edition, this version has no game board—just pure storytelling fun! Your score is simply the total number of cards played.

—description from the publisher

Takeover is a small box game about building the best hand, deploying powers, and playing all your cards first. As a syndicate manager, you play freelancers (cards) and use tactical actions to vie for control of the last publicly traded city.

Be the first to play all your cards and you win the game!

Game feel and features:

-Build a better hand from a shared market of cards
-Use Specialists to steal, go first, and disrupt other players
-Sequence your card plays to pressure rivals and empty your hand

Now includes Override, a clearer, leaner, and slightly meaner rules variant in the same box. This variant accelerates an already fast game with punchier play and smoother pacing. The  Override mechanic also adds extra interaction, deduction, and psy games.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
This Race/Class promo card for both Tin Helm and Tin Realm is included in the mint tin of Dragon Spark. It can also be found in Gnome Pack #1 (found at The Game Crafter).
The double-sided card features the Catfolk race and Adventurer class:


     The Catfolk card gives the starting stats of 8 health/8 energy/2 rations and states "Catfolk may spend 3 favor to skip a room in the dungeon. When they do this, they gain 1 energy."
     The Adventurer class starts with the Bedroll, Potion, & Dagger items and will contribute 3 health and 3 energy to your starting totals.

Challengers of the Realm is a strategic trading card game set in the Kingdom of Ileydria, where players build decks using Heroes, Tools, Banners, Instants, and Locations to outmaneuver their opponents. The game features a unique resource system called Grace, which fuels powerful abilities and determines a player’s ability to act each turn, adding a layer of tactical decision-making. Decks range from 40 to 80 cards, following specific rarity and guild composition rules, ensuring balanced and dynamic gameplay. With four distinct guilds offering diverse playstyles and a blend of familiar and innovative mechanics, Challengers of the Realm delivers an immersive and competitive TCG experience.

—description from the publisher

Does the pug make your cats angry?

Puggy, the pug, is loose in the garden… just as the cats want to take a stroll!

Roll the die to see how many steps your cat can move forward. But watch out! If you have to wake up Mopper, he will start running blindly through the garden!

Does he crash into the fence right where your cat is? Then your cat will be sent flying!

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Call Me Crazy: Jeder Anruf ein Brüller
description:
Call Me Crazy is all about crazy phone pranks. Sometimes funny, sometimes bizarre, sometimes embarrassing. And always on speakerphone! The more entertaining you and your unsuspecting victims are, the more sympathy points you score. Who will get the most laughs?

—description from the publisher

Interstellar travel is no small feat. Each journey between the stars takes a century and requires immense amounts of helium fusion fuel — just to send a minuscule payload. That payload is barely enough to sustain two awake crew members and a few bioengineered embryos.

In Arrival, you take on the role of one of the ship’s AI minds, each with its own programmed philosophy on what defines a successful colony. Your Objective: Establish a thriving colony by managing technology, ecology, and society according to your AI’s programmed directive. Will you focus on biodiverse oxygen terraforming, advancing technology for a future starshot, or creating a prosperous civilization? Arrival can be played cooperatively, competitively, or politically, depending on how the colony’s government evolves.

Each turn consists of 5 phases: Events, Worksites Selection, Construction, Resource, Work. But beware — if your AI accumulates too much distrust or shame, the colonists may abandon you. And perhaps, just perhaps, your AI could evolve beyond its original programming and become a Grendel — a superintelligent, immortal entity capable of rewriting its own destiny.

Arrival can be played as a standalone sequel to Interstellar, set in the High Frontier universe.

boardgame: Safe!: Das Original – Ganz sicher idiotensicher!
description:
In this card game, it's not the smallest or largest number that counts - only together they are strong! Get as many card families as possible from your fellow players and secure them with a ‘SAFE!’. But be careful who collects which families and make sure that no-one beats you to it! Fun is guaranteed!

—description from the publisher

TRUMPFSTARS Fußball Weltstars ("TRUMPSTARS Football World Stars") is an action-packed card game in which 50 of the greatest football stars of all time clash in exciting duels. Each card features a world-class player with impressive statistics such as goal scoring rate, titles won and other key stats. Your aim is to strategically play the best categories and win your opponents' cards until you are the only player left.

The rules are simple, but each match remains unpredictable: All cards are shuffled and dealt to the players. In turn, the active player names a category of their top card, the other players compare their values - whoever has the highest number wins the round and collects the cards. In the event of a tie, a new category of the same card decides. The player who has all the cards at the end is crowned champion.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: SAFE!: Kids Edition – Ganz sicher kindersicher!
description:
In this card game, it's not the smallest or largest animal that counts - they're only strong together! Get as many animal families as possible from your fellow players and secure them with a ‘SAFE!’. But be careful who collects which families and make sure that no-one beats you to it! Fun guaranteed!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Become a world-famous paleontologist (that's a fancy word for someone who discovers and studies dinosaur bones) while on an adventure to dig up dinos!

Join your fellow adventurers by setting out in your safari truck to explore new areas and search for bones to create a whole dinosaur that stands on its own! To do this you'll have to think and act like a dinosaur!

With the help of your trusty shovel, you will be able to dig up fun facts about dinosaur that will help you along your way.
Once you've collected all of your dinosaur bones, be the first player to display your dinosaur at the museum to become a world-famous paleontologist and win the game!

—description from the publisher

We are living in the age of declining birth rates, and Rabbit Kindergarten is struggling to secure enough little bunnies! Desperate times call for desperate measures-so send in Mr. Wolf to steal baby rabbits from rival kindergartens!

Usagi Kindergarten is a trick-taking game with a twist- forced abduction! Outsmart your opponents, use the Wolf's cunning, and claim the most bunnies to win!

Long ago, the world was graced by three beautiful goddesses: Trucca, Lilia, and Teresa. However, their rivalry ran deep, each striving to prove she was the most beautiful and the most worshipped. To secure gems, the source of both beauty and faith, they sought to control the richest mining regions. Each goddess assembled an army of Valkyries, even recruiting warriors from rival factions to build the strongest possible force and dominate the land. This fierce competition became known as the Legend of the Three Divine Princesses.

Three Goddess Princesses is a three-player-only game blending Poker, Trick-Taking, and Strategic Drafting! During trick-taking rounds, players compete to collect six cards to form the strongest poker hand. Use face-down plays to deceive your opponents. Disrupt rival strategies with powerful Scout Cards, turning the tide of battle. Fast-paced yet deeply strategic, this game rewards clever reads, bold plays, and tactical adaptation.

Experience the thrill of outmaneuvering your opponents, the tension of unpredictable outcomes, and the joy of crafting the ultimate hand!

In The Goatfather (ゴートファーザー), international gangsters have stormed into the United States from around the world and are competing for control of important locations. Do your best with the gangsters in your family.

Each player has six cards numbered 1-6, and six locations are laid out on the table, each showing a point value, the maximum number of cards that can be played, and a bonus applied to a specific card value.

On a turn, if your deck isn't empty, draw the top card of your deck, then place it face down on a location that hasn't yet been claimed.

When a location hits its card limit, flip the cards over, maintaining the order in which they were played. The first card played has a +1 bonus, the second card a +2 bonus, etc. In addition, if a card matches the location, e.g., a 6 in The White House, that card receives a +3 bonus. Each player sums the value of their cards and bonuses, and the player with the highest sum wins, keeping their cards on the location while everyone else's cards are shuffled back into their deck. (In a tie, the last tied player wins.)

When all locations have been won or no one else has cards to place, the game ends. In the latter case, resolve who wins each remaining location, with those locations being worth only half their listed value. Whichever gang has the most points wins.

Compete in a biathlon-style sprint race, using strategy and card play to manage movement and shooting for one to six players. Using a standard poker deck, players take turns drawing and playing cards to move their tokens around the course and shoot targets at the end of each lap.

The player with the lowest total time at the end of three laps wins.

Check out the free TTS mod!
https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3436426384

—description from the publisher

Play as gardeners, competing individually or as teams with the goal of growing the highest-scoring garden by planting crops, harvesting sets, and using clever bonuses to outgrow the competition.

Garden Canasta reimagines two classic card games—Canasta and Hand & Foot—with a fresh, garden-inspired twist. The theme seamlessly enhances gameplay while introducing new strategic elements. Beautifully illustrated cards featuring vibrant fruits, vegetables, and garden surprises bring the joy of gardening to life, making every hand as delightful as it is competitive.

—description from the designer

Arrange the prisms by observing the groups of trees on them and identifying their common characteristics, or select the property cards that apply to the established tree groups! The player who finds the most correct solutions wins. With simple rules and multiple game variations, this game easily develops mathematical and logical skills.

Based on the work of mathematics professor Zoltán Dienes.

The Who Will Reach the Castle? game develops mathematical and logical skills!

Place the target wanderers in the castle or the forest—this will determine where each wanderer needs to arrive. Then, by selecting the correct path segments and placing the appropriate signposts, guide all the wanderers to their designated destinations.The first one to arrive will be the winner!

Based on the work of mathematics professor Zoltán Dienes.

In this game, we visit the land of AbrakaDabra, where everything has two sides: one is ABRAKA, and the other is DABRA. Your task is to recognize the connections between the two sides of the cards. If you observe carefully and think logically, you can figure out DABRA from ABRAKA and vice versa. Each DABRA has exactly one matching ABRAKA, and it’s up to you to find the correct pairs. The player who collects the most cards wins.

Based on the work of mathematics professor Zoltán Dienes.

The Comedy of the Supreme Poet ends among the heavens.
Each player is called upon to cooperate with the others to enable the Supreme Poet to ascend to the heavenly spheres of Paradise, to the Empyrean and the sublime divine vision. 
Guided by Beatrice, Dante must exercise his Intellect to gain all the Virtues then, accompanied by St. Bernard, accomplish transhumanization thus arriving at salvation and attaining...the Love that moves the sun and all the other stars.

The game consists of 10 rounds, each representing a Heaven. Players draw and play Intellect cards to advance Virtue counters on the board. The goal is to make a single Cardinal Virtue excel each round to gain its effects. In later rounds, Theological Virtues must be acquired. 
In the final round, Salvation is calculated based on exercised Virtues, determining the players’ ending—ranging from mere ascension to full divine enlightenment.

Made from solid marble, Duat is a true return to form for gaming, both in material and complexity of gameplay. In this beautiful abstract strategy game, 2 players will need to navigate the board carefully so as not to leave themselves vulnerable to an attack. With only 3 actions per turn and a 4x4 grid to take them on, each turn could spell your last!

Each turn players choose between taking 1 or 3 actions which can be split among their pieces in any way. Those actions are either to rotate a piece a quarter turn, or to move a piece forward in the direction of its sun. When moving, they can push any other pieces friend or foe, so long as none of them are facing the moving piece. First player to knock one of their opponent's pieces off the board wins!

Will you outmaneuver your opponent and emerge victorious? Or will you be pushed over the edge and be lost to the darkness?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Heroes of Might and Magic III: The Board Game – Naval Battles Expansion
description:
The waters surroundings Antagarich hold many discovered treasures and dangerous creatures. Enjoy the new naval battle combat mechanics. Destroy anything that dares to stand in your way to claim the treasures left behind by others! But stay alert, as secrets lie beyond the waters as well.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Heroes of Might & Magic III
Video Game Theme: Heroes of Might & Magic

boardgame: Last Light: Eclipse Engine Promo Card
description:
Originally available during the Game Boy Geek Season 13 Kickstarter campaign.

Card Description:
Exhaust to  steal 1 * from a player with more * than you then give them this (exhausted) tech card.

Note: Comes with 2 copies of the same card.

At the beginning of each round, an exciting art challenge is revealed. In Artisimo players take on the role of abstract artists and secretly submit bold, stunning, and downright ridiculous artwork in an attempt to impress the judge and score big!

With 200 unique challenges and 150 amazing abstract artworks, every round is packed with bold choices, brilliant masterpieces, and unexpectedly fun emotions. Get ready to outshine and outcreate 
your opponents in the ultimate art showdown!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Void Admiral: A Space Combat Miniature Wargame
description:
Rogue admirals rally their fleets in this space combat miniature wargame.


     Build a fleet of mighty warships and arm them with an assortment of deadly weaponry.
     Maneuver your fleet about the battlefield taking advantage of firing arcs, terrain and careful timing.
     Play as one of nine factions, each with its own unique ships and special abilities.
     Utilize a command board, spending assets to perform special actions.
     Experience special missions, campaign play and even tournaments.


Shatter your enemies, dominate the star-sector and establish your legacy!

—description from the publisher

A 64 page supplement for the Void Admiral space combat wargame.


     Featuring 12 new factions: Pack Hunters, Psychic Slugs, Noble Houses, Scrap Bots, Dusk Wraiths, Liberationists, Fungoids, Lost Colony, Technocrats, Shadow Weavers, Scavengers and Cragborn.
     Rules for refitting ships to increase fleet diversity.
     New Ways to play, including hex grid combat.
     A new narrative campaign: The Flight of Tyrants
     New Quickplay Scenarios
     And plenty more.


The Galactic Hegemony has fallen. Long live Tyranny.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: In the Age of Bismarck and Napoleon III
description:
How would you like to double or even triple the size of your armies at a fraction of the cost?
Try something new and refreshing, with all the colour of the Napoleonics and even more firepower than Civil War...In the Age of Bismarck and Napoleon III
This game provides you with the finiest set of rules available for the period 1859-1871. Although primarily designed for 15mm 
it can easily be adapted to other scales. The game ratio is 1:125 which will double or triple the size of your existing armies or allow you to build bigger armies quicker than before.

This is still a batallion level game, not some grand tactical game which only allow players to move entire brigades as maneuver elements. In the Age of Bismarck and Napoleon III uses revolutionary move sequences and brigade integrity to accurately portray battlefield control without destroying good playability.

These rules are fast-paced and action packed. No more sitting around checking through lists of modifiers. This game was developed from the already popular In the age of the Franco-Prussian War which was well received at conventions across the US.

This is a very player friendly game with lots of innovations:

     1 All nationalities are rated for each war
     2 Sample organisational charts are provided to help players build their armies
     3 Over 100 commanders are individually rated


—description from the back of the book

boardgame: Best Board Game Ever: Build Your Board Game Empire
description:
In Best Board Game Ever, players take on the role of board game publishers, managing a team of Reviewers, Designers, Artists, Producers, and Playtesters to create, refine, and sell their own board games. Throughout the game, players compete to develop high-value games, influence the market, and strategically manage their resources to become the most successful publisher and to collect the Best Board Games Ever!

Best Board Game Ever is a worker placement game where players need to use their pawn on selecting different actions (new game, design, playtest, publish, buy, trade game) in order to collect the best board games (score) and win.

HELL HAS BROKEN LOOSE IN SAINT-ANTOINE
Bread is cursed... it's driven us all mad! Faithful, pray to restore order, and destroy the possessed!
... Or have you gone mad too?

Mal Ardent (Fiery Evil) is an atmospheric hidden-role game pitting the Faithful against the Possessed.

The world is set in a village ravaged by four plagues: Pestilence, War, Famine and Madness.

Each player will offer a Judge a Prayer card to deliver the village from Evil, while the Possessed, hidden among the Faithful and knowing each other, will have to parasitize the game in order to destroy the village.

The Faithful must take one of the Scourges to Redemption, and the Possessed must draw one of the Scourges into the Evil of the Fiery, which inevitably falls on the village at every turn, trying not to be unmasked.

—description from the designer

L’ENFER S’EST RÉPANDU EN SAINT-ANTOINE
Le pain est maudit... il nous a tous rendus fous ! Fidèles, priez pour rétablir l'ordre, et anéantissez les possédés !
... À moins que vous n'ayez, vous aussi, perdu la raison ?

Mal Ardent est un jeu d'ambiance à rôles cachés où s'affrontent Fidèles et Possédés.

L’univers s’installe dans un village rongé par quatre fléaux : la Malepeste, la Guerre, la Famine et la Folie.

Chaque joueur proposera à un Juge une carte Prière pour délivrer le village du Mal, alors que les Possédés, cachés parmi les Fidèles et se connaissant entre eux, devront parasiter la partie afin d’anéantir le village.

Les Fidèles devront emmener l’un des Fléaux vers la Rédemption et les Possédés devront tirer l’un des Fléaux vers le Mal des Ardents, qui s’abat inéluctablement sur le village à chaque tour, en tâchant de ne pas être démasqués.

—description from the designer (French)

Wordsmiths is a two-player competitive word game where players act as blacksmiths forging words to decorate a kingdom. Players use letter tiles from a common pool to form words on a central "Forge" board, claiming these words with clamp tiles. Completed words, at least five letters long, are then moved to the player's individual "Display" board for scoring.

Players utilize processes like using the vowel pool, moving tiles from their inventory to the forge, or using modifier tiles. The game involves strategic tile placement, claiming words, overlapping words for bonus Strata and gem tiles, and acquiring tools or improvements to enhance gameplay and scoring. The game ends when the consonant bag is empty, and the player with the highest total score from their display, stratagems, and opponent's filler tiles wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Super Mario: Mystery Block Party Game
description:
Super Mario Mystery Block Party Game

Get ready for an exciting adventure with the Super Mario Mystery Block Party Game! This fun-filled party game brings the magic of the Mushroom Kingdom to life, letting players test their luck and skills with surprise challenges hidden inside classic mystery blocks.

Each turn, players hit a block to reveal coins, power-ups, or tricky tasks inspired by Mario’s iconic world. Compete with friends in mini-games, race to collect the most rewards, and watch out for sneaky Bowser traps! Perfect for family game nights or Mario-themed parties, this game guarantees laughter, surprises, and endless fun.

Are you ready to jump in and power up? Let the party begin! Who collect the most cards win!
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
Bigly is a strategic battle for the center. This 1v1 scenario stages around the edge of a hexagon grid and your aim is to gain control of the center using your counters to own 3 hexagons around the middle including the center hexagon. Mechanics like blocking and jumping are introduced to increase the level of difficulty, but the game remains simple to understand and easy to learn.

Bring Bigly to your next game night and test your skills against your opponent. It is not just a race to the middle! Every move matters.

—description from the publisher

Unlike traditional board games, Triangulate is entirely wooden. The counters and board utilise hexagonal shapes, where two players are going head-to-head to build the pyramid. The win condition is whoever can place the most hexes and this can be achieved by surrounding an opponent and removing their pieces.

Another abstract strategy game in Ralbru Games collection which remains easy to learn and difficult to master.

Not only does this board game supply loads of fun it also is pleasing to the eye and makes a statement on a shelf.

—description from the publisher

After ButtChess' success we have the sequel in the series including an additional player with different strategy adaptations. The classic spin on draughts uses a hexagonal board and jumping and elimination mechanics with endless replay ability for 3 players.

Jump into this exciting game and test your skill. It is easy to learn but difficult to master so perfect for veterans of the game or newcomers.

Like ButtChess the aim is simple eliminate both opponents or get 3 of your counters to a singular opponent's side.

—description from the publisher

Temple ParaDice - A Thrilling Quest with Every Roll!

A strategic « roll & write » game featuring player interaction and risk-taking. Escape with enough treasure before Ah Puch’s curse strikes!

Take command of a crew and explore the island of Loot, home to the temple of the god Ah Puch. Fabulous treasures lie within, but your research suggests that a curse is about to be unleashed and the temple is in danger of falling into ruin. You don’t have much time, so set off, but be careful not to get too greedy!

- Choose and assemble your crew wisely to collect as much treasure as possible.

- Be the quickest to stow some of your treasure in your ship’s hold to earn bonus points.

- As your adrenaline rises, unlock new abilities that will aid you in your exploration and allow you to interact with other players, or even influence their exploration.

- Beware: you must leave before Ah Puch’s curse strikes and you become trapped forever within the ruins of his temple!

Do you have what it takes to become the greatest explorer?

In Crossico, players race to create a crossword using their large blocks made of high-quality plastic, completing the puzzle before their opponents. Sharp wordplay, strategic thinking, and quick hands are your keys to victory!

The rules can be learned in 3 minutes — just pick your word level, arrange your blocks to form a crossword with that word, and shout “Crossico!” when you finish!

Crossico is designed to be played in teams, so it is perfect for bonding. While teams are reccomended, you can also play solo for an added challenge.

—description from the publisher

The game includes 7 variations. Your task is to recognize the connections between the discs and the property cards and determine whether a given statement is true.

The discs are divided into sectors, and as you move clockwise, a shell turns into a pearl, a pearl into an oyster, and an oyster back into a shell. This means that each image defines a specific disc.

Each disc has 5 properties, and for each property, there are 5 other discs that share the same characteristic. The winner is the player who identifies the properties in the shortest time across the different game modes.

Based on the work of mathematics professor Zoltán Dienes.

Rainbows & Unicorns is a very quick solo card game. It came third in BGG's 2023 Traditional Deck Game Design Contest.

It is said that a quartet of legendary unicorns guard the Mystical Hoard of Tipperary. For some reason, in order to get your hands on the hoard, you need to discard all the cards in a standard deck of playing cards by making them into sets of four cards of different suits (a rainbow) or three cards of one suit plus any ace (a unicorn). If you succeed in doing so, you triumph! If not, woe betide!

The Rules: Hold the shuffled pack face down. Deal cards one at a time, placing each card into one of four piles. All the piles are empty at the start. Any card can be placed in any pile. Each pile can contain a maximum of four cards.

If a pile contains a rainbow (four cards of different suits) or a unicorn (three cards of one suit plus any ace) you may discard all four cards of the pile, and can use the space to start a new pile on any future turn. In other words, there are always four piles in play.

You win if you manage to discard every card in the deck. (Note that it's not enough to play every card into one of the piles; they all have to be discarded as part of a rainbow or a unicorn).

If you want a scoring system, score 10 points for each completed rainbow, 50 points for each completed unicorn, and 1 point for achieving a "perfect clear" (making all four piles empty at the same time). The maximum possible score is 303, which happens to be exactly the number of gold coins in the Mystical Hoard of Tipperary.

The chances of winning are about 1 in 10.

—description from the designer

In the lawless void of space, rival raccoon crews scavenge, fight, and sabotage their way to dominance in the chaotic junk-filled expanse of Trash Orbit. As daring spacefaring scavengers, players must collect scrap, upgrade their ships, and outmanoeuvre opponents using a mix of strategy, trickery, and sheer audacity. Every turn presents new challenges—from unexpected space anomalies to rival crews looking to steal your hard-earned loot.

Gameplay revolves around managing scrap, the primary resource used for movement, attacking, and upgrading. Players play action cards to move across the modular grid map, uncovering hidden locations that can be beneficial or disastrous. Each player’s ship is unique, with its own strengths, weaknesses, and upgrade paths. Combat is fast-paced and dice-free, relying on carefully played weapon cards that have specific attack values and effects. Players must decide when to engage in ship battles, raid enemy cargo holds, or flee into the depths of space.

The goal of Trash Orbit is to either outlast the competition or amass the most points by collecting scrap, upgrading your ship, and completing trade contracts before the deck runs out. Players can win through sheer firepower, economic dominance, or by playing the long game with clever manoeuvring. But beware—losing your ship doesn't mean the end. Destroyed players eject in an escape pod and must claw their way back into the race, proving that in the void, no raccoon is ever truly out of the fight.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Frontline: The Miniature Game – Core Rulebook: Rules for Tabletop Battles in Vietnam and South East Asia 1965-1975
description:
In this book find all the rules you need to play historical battles with miniature armies from the Vietnam War. 
The game is turn-based and requires strategy and tactics. 
Each company in the game has a headquarter, combat and weapons platoons as well as of course also support. 
In the book you find the standard rules as well as special rules, point values and much more clearly presented. 
The rules are suitable for the usual standards at which historical tabletop games are played: Scale 1/100, 1/72 or 1/56.

The Vietnam War (called the “American War” in Vietnam) lasted from 1965 – 1975. Starting in 1964, the Gulf of Tonkin incident escalated U.S. military involvement. In 1965, the U.S. send first troops to Vietnam: On March 8, 1965, 3,800 U.S. Marines landed at Da Nang in South Vietnam to support the ARVN Troops. The Paris Peace Accords of January 27, 1973 led to U.S withdrawal; on March, 29, 1973 the last GI left South Vietnam. For the U.S. Vietnam War was over, but it still raged on until 1975: The Fall of Saigon in 1975 marks the end of the Vietnam War with the reunification of Vietnam under communist rule.

The Vietnam War was a complex conflict involving multiple parties, notable for its impact on both Vietnam and international relations.

Further historical lists and detailed background information can be found in our campaign book Vietnam War: The Path of the Dragon.

What you get in the Frontline Miniature Core Rulebook:


     Comprehensive basics, optional and advanced rules
     Lists for U.S. and NLF forces and special rules for nations
     Tanks and gun charts
     Numbered rule sets in a clear order of play for easy reference
     Rule explanations backed by photos and diagrams
     Standard and special scenarios, illustrating the rules in action
     Rules for air combat
     Practical hints for setting up exciting tabletop battles


The Frontline Miniature Core Rulebook covers all rules you need to play battles in the Vietnam War, including dogfights.

—description from the publisher

Colorado is a state full of adventure, but its greatest challenges are the mighty mountains towering above. The highest of these peaks reach over 14,000 feet elevation, and are called "14ers." Can you conquer these climbs and assemble the best collection of peaks bagged and stories earned across one summer?

14ers is a quick tableau-building card game where you will take turns adding one "Climb card," each representing a different 14er, to your Hiker by tucking the Climb card under one of the four sides of your Hiker card to reveal a specific ability they can now use for the rest of the game. Launched on January 12, 1945, the Vistula-Oder offensive turned into an overwhelming victory in a few days.

After the encirclement of Breslau, Rybalko and his 3rd Guards Tank Army advanced westward on February 16 with the objectives of taking Görlitz and Lauban, but were held in check by local forces. From February 25, the German high command considered counterattacking. The objective is twofold. First, on the military level, re-establish the defense of the Oder by striking the enemy's flanks and on the political level, the sector seemed conducive to a welcome success in the catastrophic context of February 1945. 
Operation Gemse – a classic pincer movement around Lauban – engaged two Panzerkorps and began on March 1.

Build the Mothership is a solo game played with a standard deck of cards.

This game is part of the Space Piracy collection of solo standard deck card games.

You start the game with a tableau of cards that must be played onto a spaceship layout you design at the beginning of the game.

You play the game by moving cards in your tableau following typical tableau manipulation rules (a la FreeCell), but with a few twists, including being able to stage cards on your Queen helpers, and having to build two stacks per suit instead of just one.

You win the game by emptying your tableau and ensuring every part of your spaceship has been "built out".

You will receive a score based on how well you balanced the stacks of cards you played onto your spaceship while building it out.

—description from the designer

Explore the System is a solo game played with a standard deck of cards.

This game is part of the Space Piracy collection of solo standard deck card games.

You start the game with a hand of cards that can be played in multiple ways to perform different actions. The actions include moving your ship around the "star system" (represented by a grid of cards), trading to get more resources which can then be used to "refuel" (allowing you to refill your hand with the cards you have played), and attacking enemy space ships.

The object of the game is to manage your resources effectively while trying to find and interact with all of the "monarchs" which are represented by the Jacks, Queens, and Kings of Spades and Clubs.

The game ends once you move your ship to the final destination, but your score is based on how many of the monarchs you were able to interact with along the way.

If you don't manage your resources well, you may get stuck drifting through space and will lose the game.

—description from the designer

Smuggle the Goods is a solo game played with a standard deck of cards.

This game is part of the Space Piracy collection of solo standard deck card games.

In Smuggle the Goods, you must pilot a spaceship to various locations that have goods you must collect. You then must make your way to the appropriate "monarch" represented by one of the Club or Spade face cards in order to deliver the goods.

There is also an enemy ship in the sector that will be trying to pickup and deliver the goods better than you. They will sometimes even attack you!

To pilot your ship, pick up goods, make deliveries, and fend off enemies, you must perform actions by playing cards from your hand that are drawn from your deck which will evolve as you acquire new cards that represent the goods that you will later want to deliver.

The game ends once the enemy deck has been exhausted a certain number of times and your score at the end of the game is determined based on the number and quality of your deliveries compared to the number and quality of your enemy's deliveries.

—description from the designer

Raid the Cache is a solo game played with a standard deck of cards.

This game is part of the Space Piracy collection of solo standard deck card games.

In Raid the Cache, the player must play cards from their hand as efficiently as possible in order to defeat enemy ships and collect the resource cards they are protecting.

Before the player can play cards, however, they must first choose which fighter ship they will be controlling out of 9 possible options (represented by the Jacks, Queens, and Kings of Spades, Clubs, and Hearts). Each fighter ship determines which suit the player can play and will dictate how effective each card is.

The game ends once the player has used every fighter ship or has successfully collected every resource card. The player will receive a score based on how many resources they managed to collect.

—description from the designer

Space Piracy is a collection of 5 solo games played with a standard deck of cards.

Space Piracy has a narrative driven campaign that ties the five games together and allows the player to make important decisions that impact the experience of each of the five games. Also, how well the player performs in each game often affects aspects of the subsequent games played later in the campaign.

In order to win a campaign of Space Piracy, the player must complete all five games. They will receive a campaign score that is a total of the scores they received at the end of each game.
