Hurry inside, the crowd is eager to witness today's beating, and believe me, you don't want to keep them waiting, especially when they bought all kinds of throwable junk for half price outside the arena.
Okay, now that the gates are closed, I'll briefly explain how this goes. As soon as the gates open, you run like hell and pick up every piece of armor and sharp object you can get your hands on, and then you're off to pummel each other.
Any doubts? No? Well, the last one standing wins. And remember that the dead don't get paid, so avoid dying as much as possible.

In Tank Up you will take on the role of a fighter in a chaotic arena where, unlike the great coliseums of Rome and the past, you will not be given weapons or armor at the beginning, but you will have to arm yourself in each round to be able to face your opponents in the best way.
Turn by turn you will have to adapt your strategies and choose the attacks and protections that benefit you the most in order to fight better than the rest and thus emerge victorious.

GAMEFLOW

     Each player has 20 life points.
     The game ends when a player defeats all of the other opponents, taking away all of their life points, or when all of the attack stacks are gone.


Equipment phase:
You have one free draw and one free armor equip.

     Exchanges, extra draws, and extra equips are given by the equipped armor cards.
     Only one change can be made per equip phase, no matter how many change icons you have.
     If the attack cards run out, the player with the most life wins.


If there are 2 or more players tied, the attack cards may be shuffled and returned to play to make a tiebreaker turn.

Attack Phase:

     The lightest player may choose between drawing an extra attack card or making an extra attack.
     The heaviest player rolls a 6-sided temporary armor die, which can be equipped on any part that does not have armor.
     Every player is entitled to one free attack. Extra attacks are given by the equipped armor cards.
     Each player must activate the abilities of their armor before assigning their attacks.
     For the hit check you must roll the armor dice, and the result is the body part or piece of armor that the attack hits.
     Melee use 1 die for the hit check.
     Shots use 2 dice for the hit check.
     Magic deals area damage, meaning you can choose an adjacent armor space above, below, to the right or left, and that part will also receive half the damage rounded up, but not the effect.
     If at the end of any combat phase there is only one player left with life points, he is declared champion of the arena and wins the game.


—description from the publisher

Your move controls your opponent's Next move. Who can impose their will?

NiNE is a strategic and deep-thinking game with a very simple premise; win tic-tac-toe. With nine tic-tac-toe boards in play at once, and your decision affecting where your opponent plays, Nine will keep you and your opponent on their toes.

But wait... don't forget that you can flip your opponent's tokens by sandwiching them with yours. Oh, and the game wraps around the board.

You have never thought so deeply about TIC-TAC-TOE!

How to play:
The Nine game board is made up of a large tic-tac-toe board in red.  Then in each box of the large tic-tac-toe is a smaller one.  Therefore, there are nine small boards and one large one.  The goal of the game is to win a tic-tac-toe on the large board.  The way that you claim a region on the large board is by winning the small tic-tac-toe in that region.

The player whose age is closest to nine goes first.  Wherever they play in the small tic-tac-toe board will dictate what region the opponent must play on the big board.

And so the matching of wits begins.  Your play dictates where your opponent plays.  The strategy begins to unfold.  Once a player scores three in a row with the small tokens in a small tic-tac-toe board, they claim that region by placing the large token on it.

If a player can play a token so that it sandwiches a continuous line of opponent tokens, then all the tokens in between will flip.  This creates the possibility of stealing a lot of tokens!

The ability to flip also wraps around the game board, forcing players to see even more possibilities.

Winning is pretty simple.  Just make a tic-tac-toe.  But as you can see, it's just not such a simple feat to accomplish!

—description from the publisher

will battle creatures and bosses, uncover treasures, complete quests, and use spell cards to shape the game in various ways.

Ruinborn  consists of three stages. Players use Threat trackers to monitor the stage's Threat level, which starts at the lowest point and increases  when the player encounters a creature or when the round ends. Each stage has specific Threats. When the Threat level reaches one, it activates immediately and remains active until the stage ends.

The game ends when the boss of the final stage is defeated or its threat level reaches the maximum. The player with the most souls at the end wins.

—description from the publisher

In this fast-playing medieval mouse card game, 2–6 players compete to build the most powerful royal court—using clever combos, a pinch of strategy, and maybe a wedge of cheese.

On your turn, you'll place Court or Cheese cards face-down into your tableau, use Action cards to outwit rivals, or discard weak cards to draw better ones. Win the game by completing three-of-a-kind Court sets—made easier with the wild Big Cheese—but beware opponents with sneaky combos of their own.

Despite its short 5–15 minute playtime, Squeaky Kingdom offers a satisfying mix of hand management and tactical timing, perfect for families or as a breezy filler game. Cute mice, royal roles, and a little courtroom thievery? Yes, please!

To reproduce the photo, players must correctly place the figures on the board.

The player who gets it firts, obtain the check card.

The winner is the one with more cards with these are all finished.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Indomitable Survivor: Roaring Harmonica Kale
description:
Roaring Harmonica - Kale is packaged in a graphic box that includes:

1 x Roaring Harmonica - Kale hard plastic miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
1 x Layered Maxilla White Lion indomitable resource card
1 x Roaring Harmonica pattern card
1 x Roaring Harmonica gear card
1 x Large matte art print

The shaggy beast opened its jaws, revealing row upon row of glistening teeth. It gripped the stone-faced ground with its exhausted hands, releasing a terrifying roar. The bowel-shaking rumble grew into a head-splitting assault. Kale shuddered, bathed in the roar of his doom. His body sprang into one final act of defiance, throwing himself headlong at the howling maw. A massive pale hand fell lifeless across Kale's heaving body. The beast slumped over his blade, frozen in a silent roar.

Wildhearts Academy is a deckbuilding game played over 4 rounds. While players will begin the game with a symmetrical starting deck, the choice of their Student and Familiar immediately set them apart from their opponents! Players can also challenge themselves, engaging in the fun solo test against other students!

Each round, players will take turns, taking a single action each turn, until all players have passed or played their entire hand. Then, players resolve each of the 3 Fields, scoring them and gaining their unique win rewards.

—description from the designer

The game includes a large map, high-quality pieces and dice, a full deck of playing cards and playing notes.

The goal of the game is to become a monopolist as quickly as possible.

The board game is designed for all current and future businessmen aged 8-108.

The game gives the "opportunity" to buy and sell houses, factories and resort areas on the streets of Yerevan and in the Republic of Armenia.

Lorica segmentata is the name of the plate armor worn by Roman legionaries under the Empire. This game is an expansion for OPPIDUM. It reproduces skirmish fights on the borders of the Empire, whether in Britannia (modern Great Britain), Germania (modern Germany), Dacia (modern-day Romania) or the Near East. These are asymmetrical fights against opponents in chariots, entrenched behind a barricade or on the rooftops of houses, archers on horseback or heavily armed cavalry.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Owzat is an Australian game of skill that emulates the game of Cricket. Two sides compete with a variation of bat and ball where a fielding side rolls a ball down a ramp, along a path or wicket with the aim to hit a yellow block (the stumps). Other small yellow blocks are placed around the wicket as fielders to indicate runs scored or batting players out.

The batting team has a batter that uses a small wooden cue to strike the oncoming ball back out into the field to where runs can be scored and totaled before all 11 batsmen are out.

Sides swap roles and the side with the most runs wins the game.

Advertisements of Owzat during 1944 and 1945 show that purchasing the game cost quite a sum of money during those war years.

—user summary

This was supposed to be the wedding of Gabby’s dreams—but everything is going wrong. Initially, she suspected nothing, but as the chaos grew, she began to think there might be foul play!
Could it be the gold-digging mother-in-law, the overprotective father, or perhaps the self-serving best man? There’s only one way to uncover the truth—by going undercover as a guest

Quick Facts:
Style: Mystery whodunnit game
Duration: 6-10 hours
Level: 4.5 out of 5 Ages 14+, mature themes
Players: 1+ players
Internet needed? Yes

Dive into the intrigue with this detective puzzle mystery game tailored for teens and adults.

No assembly is needed; internet access are required.
Detailed solutions and hints available; players will always be able to progress even if you cannot solve a puzzle.

—description from the designer

Underwater Cities: Data Era is a second expansion for the Underwater Cities base game. Underwater Cities: Data Era can also be combined with the first expansion, Underwater Cities: New Discoveries.

In Data Era, players receive a new resource type - data discs. Data discs provide additional actions along with new buildings called data centers, and a new city type, information cities. These new data centers and information cities provide players with new ways of scoring.

Delicious Games has also included new, upgraded components in this expansion! The new components can be used with the base game and the New Discoveries expansion.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Kunterbunter Bauernhof is a creative dice game in which players color in funny farm animals. There is a lot to think about, as some herds of animals have to be painted in the same colors and others in different colors. Good planning is required depending on how the dice fall and which pencil is chosen.
This children's game for 1-4 people aged 5 and up combines creativity with forward thinking and teaches children to make decisions in a fun way.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Kunterbunter Bauernhof ist ein kreatives Würfelspiel, in dem die Spieler witzige Bauernhoftiere ausmalen. Dabei gibt es einiges zu bedenken, denn manche Tierherden müssen in den selben Farben und andere unterschiedlich ausgemalt werden. Je nachdem wie die Würfel fallen und welcher Stift gewählt wird, muss gut geplant werden.
Das Kinderspiel für 1-4 Personen ab 5 Jahren verbindet Kreativität mit vorausplanendem Denken und übt auf spielerische Weise Entscheidungen zu treffen.

Leichte Spielregeln machen Kunterbunter Bauernhof zu einem perfekten Spiel für Zwischendurch. Der einfache Einstieg ermöglicht allen Personen ab 5 Jahren sofort loszuspielen. Würfeln, Buntstift wählen und alle können gleichzeitig los malen. Aber aufgepasst: Die Tiergruppen sollen entweder alle gleich oder ganz unterschiedlich ausgemalt werden. Am Ende kann es also ganz schön kniffelig werden. Der kunterbunte Bauernhof lädt die Kinder ein, kreativ zu werden und Kühe, Schafe, Hühner, Pferde, Obstkörbe und Heuhaufen in Farbe zu tauchen. Wer zuerst 5 Tiergruppen richtig ausgemalt hat, gewinnt das Spiel. Mit den Maxi-Buntstiften bleibt die Freude am Malen lange erhalten.

—description from the publisher

One player takes on the role of the fox (1), another the vixen (2), and the others each take on a hunter and his dog. 
At the start of the game, the foxes sit in the main hole in the middle, and the hunters, each with their dog, sit on one of the 4 red circles in the corners of the game board. The hunters and dogs try to kill the foxes, while the foxes try to win one of the 4 corner circles. Whoever completes their task first wins.
To advance from one space to the next, a die is rolled. The order of rolling the die is:
1. Fox, 2. Vixen, 3. Hunter I, 4. Dog I, 5. Hunter II, 6. Dog II, etc.
The hunters can only advance on the black lines and only with a roll of 1, 2 or 3; but can shoot from one space to another - unless these are only connected by red lines - and if they hit with a roll of 5 or 6, they can roll again if they roll 4.
The dogs advance if they roll 1, 2, 3 or 4, but only on the blue lines with 1, 2, 3; on the red lines only with 1. If they have caught the fox or vixen, they kill them on the next roll - if they have not moved out in the meantime - with a roll of 5 or 6, and another roll if they roll 4.
The foxes advance on all lines with 1, 2, 3 or 4, and on the red lines without even rolling the die when it is their turn. Each player can forego his throw if he wants to remain in a favorable position.

The game was published about 1918.

—user summary

The main element of this adorable family board game is cheese—what else could it be? And the other two? Mice and cats, of course! During the game, the little mice hide inside the cheese, and each player stacks cheese slices on top of each other so that the mice remain visible through the holes. The player with the most mice peeking out of the cheese wins!

Allenby's and Lawrence's Desert Wars is a fast, playable, intense Operational level micro-wargame about the 2 wars against the Turks in Palestine and Western Syria waged by the British and by the Arabs. The British wanted the Turks out of Palestine and the Levant to replace them with British and French control, while the Arabs and TE Lawrence were fighting for Arab independence, and their nuanced victory conditions reflect that.

Games have 10 bimonthly turns. The British, Turkish, and German forces are confined to the grid.  Lawrence and Arabs can sweep anywhere out of the open desert. Strategic and first priority Trans-Jordan Railway supply deliveries to off-map Medina can be protected by Turks only to a degree from Arab attacks.

- Division to corps to Hejaz (camel) and Howeitat army level.
- I-Go-You-Go wargame system using combat odds outcomes and different levels of resilience from combat disruptions, although outright eliminations possible.
- 2 British Re8 air units, 1 German Rumpler C1 air unit.  German "Pasha" advisors to help Turk resilience and coordinate Turk defenses.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The game is themed around sock-shaped athletes racing in a marathon in Mexico. Players take on the role of assembling teams of these "scarpetos" and competing to reach a target number. The game's vibrant and unusual artwork enhances its whimsical theme, making it stand out among other fillers.

Gameplay revolves around simple math and strategic card play. Each turn, players either place a card in front of them to add to their total or play a card to increase the target number for all players. Special "sneaker-stealer" cards allow players to take an opponent's card, adding a layer of interaction. The winner is the first to reach the exact target sum, or the player closest to it when the deck runs out.

Welcome to Schooled — the ultimate underwater strategy showdown! Inspired by nature’s own survival tactic, where fish band together in schools for protection, this head-to-head 2-player battle challenges you to build and protect your own aquatic army.

The rules are simple but the strategy is deep: take turns positioning your fish, outsmart your opponent, and aim to have the most surviving fish when the final piece hits the water. Bonus points await those daring enough to venture into deeper waters, where risk and reward collide in epic fashion. The player with the highest combined score of surviving fish and deep water bonus points wins.

Easy to learn yet challenging, Schooled invites you to dive into a fast-paced, fish-eat-fish battle where every move counts. How will you outmaneuver your rival and rule the underwater realm? Get ready to make a splash—your school awaits!

—description from the designer

In Familie Sparky Jump! players control two figures. You go around a track, hoping to land on a field where you may attempt to jump in a pool. The first player who manages to land two figures in the pool, wins the game.

The game was published by Spar, a groceries store.

FlipLets is a one or two player word building game using
26 two-sided A-Z letter tiles.

Goal:
The goal of the game is to use all of your letters to build a network of interconnected words. 
Solo Setup:
Grab three random vowels and 12 random consonants.

Two Player Setup:
Both players grab three random vowels and half of the consonants.

Game Play:
Arrange your letters into words and connect them all together. A word must contain at least three letters. However, when playing with children, they can use two letter words. You can use either side of the tile. Play until all letters have been placed and connected (no orphans).

Tip: Since a few tiles have letters with different values, try to place higher-value letters.

Scoring: Add up and record the value of all the letters. Play at least three rounds.     
  
—description from the publisher

Imagine yourself enjoying a warm afternoon in a flower field, watching friendly insects going about their day and having a lovely time with your family and friends. That is until the annoying bugs ruin your peaceful picnic by buzzing around, crawling all over your food and making you regret ever going out. But will that stop you?

Welcome to Buzzin’ Picnic. In the game, you and your friends are out on a picnic, taking turns guiding Friendly Insects to Insect Hotels while keeping your food safe from Annoying Bugs.

As you play, earn points for every Annoying Bug card that you slap with a Slipper. Plant Flowers to attract Friendly Insects from other players' Flower Fields, and win the game by earning the most points and filling your Points Pile with Friendly Insect and Annoying Bug cards.

Of course, it wouldn‘t be much fun if things were that simple! To make things more interesting, we came up with a few clever ways for you to mess with your friends and loved ones!

—description from the publisher

Taking classic rummy to the Zoo.  Bright colors and vibrant animal cards make this rummy game attractive for kids and the whole family.

Players choose their favorite animals to add to the play deck, personalizing the game experience.

Goal cards define the sets needed for each hand.  They show examples of the sets, and remain on the table for all players to see.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 当头一棒：诺门罕战役 (A HEAD-ON BLOW: Battle of Khalkhin Gol 1939)
description:
当头一棒（A Head-on Blow）is an operational level wargame simulating the battle of Khalkhin Gol/Nomonhan in 1939 between the Soviet Red Army and Japanese Kwantung Army. The series of border conflict started in May 1939 as small scale skirmishes between the border troops of Mongolia and Japanese puppet Manchukuo. The action quickly escalates as both Soviet Union and Japan intervened with military force directly. During June, both sides reinforce the area with more troops. In July, the Kwantung Army strike first, launching 2 major offensives in succession, both failed to achieve their goals. As the frontline stables in a stalemate, Soviet Army conducted a surprise counter offensive on 20th of August, trapped and destroyed the Japanese 23rd division, fully ejecting Japanese forces from the disputed area.

The game portrays the actions during July to September, where both side has major formations in the area and the fighting was fiercest. Still, both side spend much time to stockpile ammo and supply for operations as they are both far from logistical bases. In the game, each turn have 5 action round, and in each action round, player play a card from hand and decide if they want to conduct an offensive or logistical round. Offensive rounds allows unit to move faster and conduct attacks, and usually allow powerful bounces from the round’s played card, yet requires the expenditure of supply points. Logistical rounds only allow the player to conduct movement, but replenish the supply points and also allows for replacement of reduced/lost units.

During this conflict, the Japanese army shows a clear upper hand in infantry tactics and low-level leaderships, while the Soviet army have a telling advantage in armored units and artillery (both in quality and quantity). These are taken into account by the unit quality shifts, armored value shifts and artillery units (can conduct barrages alone or fire in offensive support). The Red Army units also suffer from a significant lack of training and coordination at the start of the conflict, which is showcased with the Soviet training rules: at the start of game the Soviet units are severely handicapped, only by participating in battle, the gained experience then will slowly remove these restrictions.
Historically, the Japanese plan is over-ambitious and underestimated their opponents by a vast degree. Deploying the inexperienced 23rd Division instead of the elite 7th Division, pulling back its only tank units early on, and reluctance to commit additional forces in time all contributed to the ultimate defeat. The game allows the players to explore “what-if” scenarios by allow the Japanese player an option each turn to correct some of these strategical mistakes, but beware, the high command would expect more from the increased investment, meaning the victory condition will become more and more demanding.

The game have 3 scenarios, each having a different starting date: The first Japanese offensive, the second Japanese offensive, and the Soviet counteroffensive.

Components:
1 A2 size map
140 counters
2 Player aid cards
2 Set-up charts
40 Cards
1 6-sided die

Le village aux 5 dragons is a game created by Clément Fournier and illustrated by Lucile Leca. It's a competitive exploration game for 1 to 5 players of 10 yo and more. It lasts 1 to 2 hours.

In this game, you're an adventurer traveling in strange dungeons always more dangerous. You'll gain treasures and experience to improve your skills and equipment. You'll fulfill quests and maybe kill one of the 5 dragons and take its treasure...

—user summary

Le village aux 5 dragons est un jeu créé par Clément Fournier et illustré par Lucile Leca. Il s'agit d'un jeu d'exploration compétitif inspiré du jeu de rôle, pour 1 à 5 joueurs de 10 ans et + et des parties allant de 1 à 2 heures.

Dans ce jeu, vous incarnerez un ou une aventurier-ère parmi 10 origines et 10 métiers possibles pour partir à l'aventure dans des donjons à la fois étranges et féeriques.
Accumulez des richesses et de l'expérience, remplissez des quêtes et améliorez votre équipement dans ce jeu d'exploration tactique !
Enfoncez-vous toujours plus loin dans les donjons afin de repartir avec un maximum de butin, mais attention à ne pas dépasser vos limites car les monstres seront de plus en plus dangereux à mesure que vous progresserez, et les dragons, de plus en plus proches ...

—description from the publisher

Le MEGALO is a game created and self-published by entrepreneur Jacques Zwahlen, head of the Veillon mail-order company, and sold in the company's catalog, distributed to all Swiss households in the 90s and 2000s.

It's a mixture of Monopoly and a game of goose on the theme of human psychic development from birth to death.

To win, players must acquire a certain amount of Pep's (the game's money), notably by collecting a royalty when another player stops on a square they own, and four psychology “masteries” that can be acquired by stopping at least once on several consecutive squares. Diplomas already earned by other players can be stolen during the game in two different ways.

Players progress along the game track by rolling dice. At regular intervals, they must overcome psychological “resistances” which can, at the player's option, either be “mastered” (by means of a successful dice roll, to move on to the next square), or forced (by discarding a card in hand to jump 9 squares, if they are not countered). The player must also be in possession of certain cards in order to cross specific squares. Finally, instead of advancing along the game track with a roll of the dice, the player can freely choose to “step back” and strategically return to an earlier square.

boardgame: That's Dope: Family Game Night Edition
description:
Find out how well you really know your family in the party game That's Dope: Family Game Night Edition. Players take turns guessing who voted "Dope" or "Nope" to hilarious, controversial topics.

Each round, one player reads a topic card, then everyone else secretly votes whether they think the topic is "Dope" or "Nope" using one of their black voting cards. If the active player correctly guesses how many people voted "Dope", they win the topic card, and whoever wins the most topic cards after five rounds wins the game.

That's Dope: Family Game Night Edition can be combined with the That's Dope: The Party Game for Strong Opinions base game.

Expand the possibilities to build new AracKhan Wars faction decks with this expansion containing 19 cards for each of the 4 factions and a small 8-pages booklet.
Assert the Grey Order’s dominance with 19 faction cards:
• 6 Core Box cards in order to develop your archetypes.
• 13 exclusive cards (8 creatures and 5 spells) to create unique decks.
Preserve the Nakka’s sanctuary with 19 faction cards:
• 5 Core Box cards in order to develop your archetypes.
• 14 exclusive cards (10 creatures, 3 spells and 1 land) to create unique decks.
Defend the Whitelands with 19 faction cards:
• 2 Core Box cards in order to develop your archetypes.
• 17 exclusive cards (13 creatures, 3 spells and 1 land) to create unique decks.
Empower the Blight to ravage AracKhan with 19 faction cards:
• 2 Core Box cards in order to develop your archetypes.
• 17 exclusive cards (14 creatures, 2 spells and 1 land) to create unique decks
This expansion’s booklet contains:
• An update on the Grey Order’s progress.
• A reminder of the deck construction rules.
• The complete list of the cards present in this expansion.
• A new deck list composed of cards from this expansion and the Core Box.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: That's Dope: Safe For Work Edition Vol. 1
description:
Find out how well you really know your family in the party game That's Dope: Safe For Work Edition Vol. 1. Players take turns guessing who voted "Dope" or "Nope" to hilarious, controversial topics.

Each round, one player reads a topic card, then everyone else secretly votes whether they think the topic is "Dope" or "Nope" using one of their black voting cards. If the active player correctly guesses how many people voted "Dope", they win the topic card, and whoever wins the most topic cards after five rounds wins the game.

That's Dope: Safe For Work Edition Vol. 1 can be combined with the That's Dope: The Party Game for Strong Opinions base game.

boardgame: That's Dope: After Dark Edition Vol. 1
description:
Find out how well you really know your family in the party game That's Dope: After Dark Edition Vol. 1. Players take turns guessing who voted "Dope" or "Nope" to hilarious, controversial topics.

Each round, one player reads a topic card, then everyone else secretly votes whether they think the topic is "Dope" or "Nope" using one of their black voting cards. If the active player correctly guesses how many people voted "Dope", they win the topic card, and whoever wins the most topic cards after five rounds wins the game.

That's Dope: After Dark Edition Vol. 1 can be combined with the That's Dope: The Party Game for Strong Opinions base game.

Introducing Miracula — the captivating expansion for Stupor Mundi, featuring two enthralling modules: The Postponed Crusade and The Imperial Projects. Note these were included in Stupor Mundi: Master Print Edition but being released by themselves for those with the retail release of Stupor Mundi.

The Postponed Crusade
Pope Honorius III urges Emperor Frederick II to launch a long-awaited crusade. Though Frederick's heart may waver, he must maintain a facade of loyalty and piety. As his trusted vassals, you have access to resources meant for the holy war — but beware! Use them too freely for your own ambitions, and you may provoke the Pope's fury and risk excommunication. Balance loyalty and self-interest as you navigate this delicate power struggle.

The Imperial Projects
Driven by his unrelenting passion for beauty and grandeur, Frederick II dreams of an empire adorned with breathtaking architectural marvels. He summons his vassals to partake in The Imperial Projects — a race to construct the most magnificent castles and fortifications. The promise of eternal glory and the Emperor's favor hangs in the balance. Will your citadel rise above the rest and secure your place in history?

With Miracula, dive deeper into the world of Stupor Mundi, where ambition, loyalty, and cunning strategy intertwine. Are you ready to shape the fate of an empire?

—description from the publisher

CODER.IO is a strategic race game. It features 37 map tiles that are used to create unique levels that each time present a new challenge. Your objective is to collect the most points, by racing other players for the diamond tokens and picking up new code cards. Play one of the four robot characters: Bit-Bit, Hexa, Byte, and Glitch – each with a unique design and backstory

Each round, all players simultaneously build their code to move their robot across the map.

Each level introduces a new game mechanic and presents a new challenge. Avoid obstacles, collect additional cards, sabotage other players or rush to the finish - the choice is yours but do not forget that you have limited lives and only the first robot to reach the finish at each level receives the diamond token.

At the end of level 6 (or level 9 in the expansion!), players count their points from diamond tokens and code cards. The player who collected the most points wins the game!

—description from the publisher

In Ah Ouinnn? Édition Famille (aka Oh Really? Family Edition), a game created by visual artist MC Marquis, players try to correctly guess the order of preference of other players among five completely different topics. The more accurate your guesses are, the more points you score. Producing laughter and discussion, this game is great for family time. Learn more about the people you play with.

Tensions are reaching a fever pitch with only two weeks remaining until the highly anticipated launch of D1Ring, a groundbreaking electronic ring. As the clock counts down on the launch, a chilling ransom note reveals the shocking news that George Devenport, the co-creator of D1Ring, has been abducted.

Could it be the result of jealousy from his business partner, Bianca? Or does the stressed event organizer, Brook, harbor a motive? Perhaps Ava, the founder of a rival company, is seeking to sabotage the competition. Is the impatient investor, Max, involved, or could the intern, Sarah, hold a surprising secret?

Quick Facts:
Style: 3-part mystery game
Duration: 5-7 hours, 1.5-2 hours for each part
Level: 5/5 Ages 14+, mature themes
Players: 1+ players
Internet needed? Yes

Dive into the intrigue with this 3-part puzzle mystery game tailored for teens and adults.
Use a mix of tangible physical evidence with interactive digital gameplay for an immersive journey.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Video Game Champion: Reputation Promo Cards
description:
Four promo cards available from Arcane Wonders: one Play, one Beat, and two Behavior Reputation Cards. No instructions are provided, but the iconography indicates:

Play:
Play any ten Games - 10 points

Beat:
Beat two easy, two normal, and two hard Games - 12 points

Behavior:
Having three Magazines and two Presents - 10 points

Having seven Bucks - 8 points

Presumably, you should shuffle these cards in with the other Reputation cards and draw the appropriate amount according to player count during setup.

boardgame: Nidavellir: Thingvellir – The Hat, The Mythic Headgear
description:
This is a promo available from The Dice Tower.

Rules:
During setup, set The Hat aside. Shuffle the Age 2 Camp cards, then put The Hat on top of the deck. This means at the beginning of Age 2, The Hat will be one of the 5 available Camp cards.

As soon as an Elvaland takes a card from the camp, put the lowest coin available from the Royal Treasure onto The Hat. Keep doing this each time a card is taken until an Elvaland takes The Hat and all coins on it.

They put The Hat into their Command Zone and leave the coins on it.

At the end of the game, The Hat adds the sum of all of the coins on it to the owner's Final Bravery Value.

The “Twitterer in Chief” can’t stop himself from attacking the checks and balances of the United States government. Civics in Chaos is a game to Make America Smart Again. Game play happens in the Congress where 11 swing votes determine whether or not a bill becomes law. Tweets will disrupt the rule of game play with absolute chaos. Will the bill be passed? Will it be blocked? Or will the tweets disrupt it all. Beware of the sneak hacks from the Russians and maybe, just maybe, if you’re lucky, you will Impeach the guy.

Play one-on-one or as teams. One side is trying to pass a bill into a law, the other is trying to block them. The round ends when all the cards have been played from the Trump deck.

The battle involves swing votes in Congress. The team attempting to pass a bill goes after those swing votes. The opposing team tries to take them away.

Congress gets in the way—doesn’t it always?—with its  arcane rules that change the conditions for winning.

The battle for swing votes gets dirty. Political attacks are constant.

Protect your votes or prevent your opponents from voting. These cards are powerful no matter what side you are on.

Chaos explodes from the White House. Whenever a Trump card is drawn, players are at the mercy of the Tweet, Leak, or Alternative Fact that comes their way.

Victory or Martyrdom is a solitaire action roleplaying game set in the time of the Prophet Muhammad, peace be upon him, in which players take on the role of a mujahid, or Islamic warrior, and attempt to improve his attributes, skills, defeat enemies, acquire better gear, and defend his faith, with the goal of surviving into old age and retiring after a lifetime of adventure. Player warriors can join the Prophet in defense against those men who wish to destroy the burgeoning revival of monotheism, march in the ranks of the Rashidun armies and drive back Persian and Roman invaders, choose various political allegiances in civil war, and more.

The player begins between the years 624 and 670 CE as an Islamic warrior of an age of the player's choosing. The player fills out his warrior's attributes and skills then begin their adventure, resolving a sequence of events, including random events, between periods of encampment and battle, until retirement (voluntary or involuntary), or the warrior's death, either in battle, grevious wounds, or by old age.

Dice and cards are used to resolve combat, with morale, battlefield perception, and battlefield conditions (representative of terrain, weather, and so on) all affecting the course and ultimate resolution of the battle phase. Outside of battle, the player may undertake pilgrimages, train his physical and mental attributes, practice with various weapons, horses, accumulate abstracted gold pieces, and purchase lands, among other things.

The game officially ends if the player voluntarily (or involuntarily) retires, is killed, dies of old age, or reaches the end of the game in the year 732 CE.

—description from the designer

This is a set collection game where you explore various fish using a unique oxygen management mechanism. As a diver, you must carefully manage your most vital resource—oxygen—to secure the marine creatures you desire before others do. The deeper you dive, the more oxygen you consume, but the rewards are greater, as you’ll encounter rare and beautiful sea creatures!

However, it won’t be easy. Menacing sharks lurk in the depths, ready to disrupt your dive. Can you navigate the ocean, manage your limited oxygen, and collect the sea creatures you seek?

This is a unique climbing game for 2 to 5 players, inspired by Korea’s traditional tightrope walking culture and its rich storytelling. Just like a Flying Artist maintaining a precarious balance in the air, numbers fluctuate, and suit powers shift dynamically, creating an engaging and strategic experience. Feel the thrill of skillfully playing your cards while immersing yourself in the vibrant colors and cultural essence of Korean tradition.

—description from the designer

Party game: On receiving the patata, responds to an imposed theme and throws the potato to an opponent.
If unsuccessful, the player wins the theme card and associated points (1 to 3, depending on difficulty). After 10 rounds, the player with the fewest points wins the game.

A game of tactical warfare in World War 2, designed for 15mm figures but easily adapted to other scales. Each side typically controls a company of infantry plus a few tanks. One model figure represents one soldier or vehicle.

The game is played on a square grid to eliminate measuring. It uses handfuls of dice to simulate the uncertainty of combat whilst still producing a result that tends towards the mean.

The emphasis is on effective use of troops rather than minute detail of weapons systems. The rules include a simple scenario generator.

The game is supported by a separate "Company Architect" book which contains options for creating historically plausible companies from a large selection of combatant nations. The publisher also offers an extensive range of 15mm figures for use with the rules
family: Series: Poor Bloody Infantry – WWII Miniature Rules (Peter Pig)

boardgame: Perspectives Demo: The Cases of the Rue Morgue
description:
In Perspectives, each player has key information to reconstruct what happened and find the solution.

This demo is about a short case with three acts.
Compare photographs, reports and clues... without looking at the documents of the players on your team!
Will you find the details that connect all the pieces of the puzzle?

The game principle is simple and requires practically no instructions: You open Act I, divide the two cards in it, describe your pictures and try to answer a guiding question. If this is done, you put the cards back aside, check the solution and continue exactly with Act II. Finally, in Act III, there are no more cards and you have to clarify the last question with the past information.

boardgame: Pokémon TCG: Scarlet & Violet – Prismatic Evolutions
description:
From fluffy and fiery to freezing and fanciful, Eevee and its many Evolutions hold a special place in the hearts of Pokémon Trainers across the land! Discover their unique new powers as Stellar Tera Pokémon ex, and look for treasure of your very own across a grand gathering of Pokémon ex from Paldea and beyond. Glittering gems and hidden patterns await at the end of the rainbow in the Pokémon TCG: Scarlet & Violet—Prismatic Evolutions expansion!

—description from the publisher

Outmaneuver your opponent in a mysterious swamp that Shifts with every glance and sinks with every step.

The Mire is a 2 person strategy game in which players take turns moving, swapping, and locking down tiles with the goal of backing your opponent into a corner so they are unable to move on their turn.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Wetlands / Swamps / Bogs / Fens / Marshland

description:
It’s almost time to do a jazz!

In ThreeHorn, players will draw and discard a single card each turn while attempting to put together the classiest, brassiest jazz trio before someone calls “Places!” After that happens, each player gets one last chance to avoid being caught with the lowest value when it’s time for the show. Everyone starts with three Record tokens, and the player with the lowest value in hand will lose one during scoring!

Thing is, in this jazz economy, you’ll be playing at a different venue every round which could cause any number of wild rules changes. One round, you might be given a few extra choices as far as what to draw… and then next round you might not even be able to look at your hand at all.

The last player with Record tokens remaining wins the game!

Eppi is an interactive point-and-click style adventure book series with a mission for parents and kids to play together.

In part two of this interactive puzzle book, you play the main character, Eppi and, as the game progresses, you will also get the opportunity to use various other characters to help you solve puzzles, detect clues and collect objects you might use cleverly at individual locations.

Based on #pointnclick computer games, this hilarious story takes you on a fabulous trip with cheeky characters. Are you able to uncover the conspiracy against Eppi?

Warning from the publisher: this adventure book might turn your kids into #readers!

—description from the publisher
The best way to add more ways to score with you offerings. But beware, the Celt gods are a bit more tricky!

A fast and furious card game about the wildest knight tournament in the world – Pretzel Tournament. The wild and crazy design of the game will give you unforgettable emotions and will make all players laugh more than once.

The game lasts until you inflict eleven wounds on your opponents or earn the same number of trophies, to, of course, win the tournament! The rules of the game are extremely simple, each round you call creatures into battle closed, and simultaneously reveal them with all players. Your creature must be stronger than the creatures of the players to the right and left of you. Having won, you receive a trophy, having lost a wound.

Think of a unique word that fits each category and letter. Longer words score more points, but with each round you limit your word length options. It pays to be quick, the first person to write their word gets to start the timer for everyone else. Fail to think of a word will earn you 0 points, as will having the same word as someone else.

This is a family game of mindfulness and speed. Players are oceanographers trying  to dive and explore the depths of the sea.
 Each player has their own board with various objects and characters on the seabed. Each round, players open one card with a task – which six images they will have to find and close with tokens on their boards. The one player who correctly placed his tokens before the others wins the round. He takes the task card as a trophy. The game has 10 rounds in total. The winner is the one who has collected the most trophies.

The game has both simple and difficult tasks, so not only toddlers can play, but also older children. And funny illustrations will delight everyone at once: there are mermaids, sharks, and even sunken ships! However, there are also unexpected artifacts (for example, a slipper floating away from someone).

Food Fight Frenzy is a dice rolling, speed, pattern recognition, and dexterity game for 2-6 players. Each player has a stack of tiles and starts with 5 face up. Each turn all the dice are rolled and it's a mad dash to place your tiles on top of the dice showing the matching icons. When there's no more available moves a player says "STOP" and the turn is over. Be careful though, if you call stop too early or have any of your tiles fall off the dice you're out of the round. First player to get rid of all their tiles is the winner.


     Speed pattern recognition and dexterity game
     72 tiles depicting cute animals being hit with food and 13 oversized dice with the matching food items
     Quick to learn and even faster to play with each game only taking about 15 minutes
     Be the first player to get rid of all your tiles and you win. 


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dweores: The Last Five Great Tribes
description:
Dweores is an easy-to-learn area control, tile placement and blind bidding strategy game for 2-5 players set in the comic universe of Dweores by John Ottinger

Players take on the role of one of five Dweores tribes (Farmers, Miners, Inventors, Warriors or Pilots) and compete to be the first to connect ten of their tribes icons on the game board.

Players achieve points through strategic tile placement. During each round, players secretly bid using supply cards to win the right to place revealed tiles from the available tribe stacks. The highest bidder for each supply type places their chosen tile on the game board, aiming to create connections with icons that match their Tribe. Successfully placing these icons adjacent to each other earns points, and the first player to reach 10 points wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Adventures of Conan: Queen of the Black Coast Expansion
description:
The treacherous waters of the Black Coast are filled with reavers and corsairs, as well as the infamous she-devil of the sea, Bêlit —the Queen of the Black Coast. And you can explore them all with a Map Extension of these powerful Kingdoms.

New Adventures make these hazardous Encounters even more beneficial for Conan’s foes. Scenarios change the game setup meaning no two games are ever the same and Intrigue Events offer riches all round. With new Plots and Encounters, you can discover everything the Southern Kingdoms have to offer.

—description from the publisher

In the hush of twilight, where moonlight threads through ancient leaves, the Wee Folk stir. They dance in patterns unseen by mortal eyes, leaving trails of magic etched in shape and shadow.

Wee Folk is a two-player game of gentle rivalry, where you’ll slide cards like stones across still water, weaving polyomino patterns to echo the hidden sigils of the fae. Where strategy wears the robes of story, and each game is a spell softly cast.

Wee Folk is a 2-player card game where you are attempting to arrange various Wee Folk into pattern in order to collect them.

Collected cards might include actions which may be spent to alter a player's personal tableau. Cards shift in a "Candy Crush" style whenever sets are removed from the play area.

—description from the publisher

Have you ever wanted to go to the place where everything is Sunshine and Rainbows?

Well, welcome to Kaleidoscope, the land of perpetual light and colour.  But alas, trouble is amuck as the neighbour to the southwest is trying to take over!  The good people of Kaleidoscope need your help.  In this lightweight, super cooperative, set collection game you and your friends, along with help from Resplendence and Roy G Biv, will work together to Rebuild the Rainbow, Restore the Light Beacons and Banish Suhmeeryan and his Darkness back to the Obsidian Moors forever! (Or at least until the next time you want to play the game)

—description from the designer

A 1v1 card game in which you play cards to deal damage and change which of three healthpools you or your opponent has active.

Each player gets a secret card that removes damage  from one of their healthpools at the end of the game!  Work out which healthpool your opponent is hiding from you, and try to hide your own!

Quarrel aims to provide a short, easy to learn experience with a good amount of novelty and replayability, providing an engaging filler for 15-20 minutes between other games, but also something that is enjoyable for several matches in a row.

—description from the designer

Game background:  In 1910, the Governor-General of Taiwan, Sakuma Samata, planned to conquer the aboriginal tribes by force. The Japanese police began to suppress the aboriginals of Taiwan by force, forcing them to submit and surrender. This was the "Five-Year Aboriginal Management Plan"! The five-year Aboriginal Management Plan planned to achieve the conquest of the "Northern Aboriginals" in the first two years and fully advance the Aiyong Line, and in the third year, to focus on conquering the "Taroko Aboriginals". Can Japan complete the plan and achieve the encirclement and suppression of the indigenous people?

Game introduction:  "War of Aboriginal Affairs" is a combat-level war chess game suitable for single players. Players play the role of the Governor-General of Taiwan Governor's Office and lead his police to attack the aboriginal tribes. In each round, players can use the garrison or the border to assist in your crusade. Be careful of the sneak attacks of the aboriginals and protect your governor and police. At the end of six rounds, if there are no aboriginal chess pieces on the map, the player wins.

Number of players: 2
Game time: 30-45 minutes
Game accessories: a double fog full color 5*7 inch postcard (this game requires 3 dice)
Game price: 30 yuan per 1-5 pieces, 25 yuan per 6 or more pieces, shipping fee: 10 yuan for 1-5 pieces, 20 yuan for 6 or more pieces (by ATM transfer) Please write to inform: quantity and delivery method

boardgame: Clash of Steel: War of Unification – American vs German Complete Starter Set
description:
Welcome to Clash of Steel. It’s 1948 and the war has started again.

Based on an Alternative History where the war in Europe finishes in 1944 with the surrender of Germany, but restarts just four years later in the face of Soviet aggression.

Players take to the tabletop with one of four initial nations in Epic Tank-On-Tank Combat using Highly Detailed Multi-part Plastic Miniatures, battling over objectives in an attempt to control the battlefield and defeat their enemies.

Featuring Dynamic Mission Based Game Play to ensure that every battle is unique and cinematic.
How will you re-write the history books?

The Clash of Steel Starter Sets contain everything a player needs to get started, including rules, miniatures, dice, tokens and much more…

—description from the publisher

Expand the possibilities to build new AracKhan Wars faction decks with this expansion containing 19 cards for each of the 4 factions and a small 8-pages booklet.
Assert the Grey Order’s dominance with 19 faction cards:

     6 Core Box cards in order to develop your archetypes.
     13 exclusive cards (8 creatures and 5 spells) to create unique decks.


Preserve the Nakka’s sanctuary with 19 faction cards:

     5 Core Box cards in order to develop your archetypes.
     14 exclusive cards (10 creatures, 3 spells and 1 land) to create unique decks.


Defend the Whitelands with 19 faction cards:

     2 Core Box cards in order to develop your archetypes.
     17 exclusive cards (13 creatures, 3 spells and 1 land) to create unique decks.


Empower the Blight to ravage AracKhan with 19 faction cards:

     2 Core Box cards in order to develop your archetypes.
     17 exclusive cards (14 creatures, 2 spells and 1 land) to create unique decks.


This expansion’s booklet contains:

     An update on the Grey Order’s progress.
     A reminder of the deck construction rules.
     The complete list of the cards present in this expansion.
     A new deck list composed of cards from this expansion and the Core Box.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: AracKhan Wars: A Trail of Ashes
description:
As their offensive progresses, the Grey Order unveils some of their plans. In the northwest, it intends to prevent any alliance between the Whitelands and the Nakka, while attempting to take control of the latter’s primordial forests. A little further south, the order of patriarchs of the Western forges seem to be trampling on the old trade agreements giving access to the precious Hilgard steel, one of the pillars of the Order's power.
The peoples that are under attack will not allow themselves to be dominated as resistance is organized. Unbeknownst to all, a sinister evil is emerging in the South West that could well become the greatest danger AracKhan has ever faced... For or against the Grey Order, players will have to give their best in order to win the wars of AracKhan!

Content of this expansion:

     The Grey Order has 3 new exclusive faction cards to establish its dominance.
     The Nakka, the Whitelands and the Blight strike back: 1 new card for each faction.
     Bring the clashes to the borders of Byartur with the new 48-spaces battle mat.
     The new Terrain mode and its 8 cards will allow you to customize the battlefield with each game. 
     The 48-page Strategy Guide is filled with lessons for any AracKhan Wars player wishing to progress: strategic reflections, faction-by-faction analyzes, study of the 6 exclusive cards of this expansion and new faction deck lists. These will all be great assets to win the game. Terrain mode rules, a short story on Seneschal Arys Riven and a recap of the game structure, integrating all game modes, complete this essential read.


—description from the publisher

Cosmic events are unexpected but quite often beneficial to your journey. Get this 14 card micro expansion for Micro Cosmos to boost your gameplay!

—description from the publisher

Discover the world of "Crime Maps"!

A mysterious criminal case takes you to the north of Germany: After a robbery in the Museum of Hamburg History, the skull of the notorious pirate Klaus Störtebeker, who was executed on Hamburg's Grasbrook on 20 October 1401, is missing. As well a historical map of the city which, according to legend, shows the way to Störtebeker's gold treasure, which has been missing for centuries.
Are you ready for a merciless treasure hunt between the North and Baltic Seas? Concentrated investigative work is required, which will take you from one place to the next using your powers of deduction and a keen eye: solve tricky puzzles together, find the locations you are looking for on the maps and catch the perpetrator!

A crime map game in the truest sense of the word: By combining exciting puzzles with various country and city maps, ‘Crime Maps’ offers a completely new kind of crime game entertainment!

In Oquur: The Story of Mons you play a cooperative deck-building game in which death of a character may be a favourable result. Keep an eye out for enemies that may invade your deck, as they are not that easy to get rid of. Defeat the boss to win and do not let it defeat you.

Players have a hand of cards they can play during their turn in a round. They can play coins to gain new cards, weapons to defeat enemies or actions to gain additional benefits. Enemies will become part of player decks, and should be managed properly to avoid them taking over a player.

Tactical sacrifices, playing to the strengths of your character and team work are a must to fight the foes that have settled themselves in the mountainous regions of Mons. Rely on your teammates and make sure that you can keep your home safe.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C24 – Want a mini for Xmas
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C24 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Material needed: Season 1.
Live the day the zombie invasion began, and how Season 1's survivors got to stick together!

Designed by Céline Mathieu

After finishing construction in the city, your firm is going all the way to the coastline. It's time to build bridges to connect islands, and don't forget your statues! Planning and tactics will still be your best assets to beat your opponents in Tower Up: Paradise Islands, the first expansion for the game Tower Up.

—description from the publisher

As Autumn ends, shepherds return home, preparing their flock for the coming winter. But sometimes they linger a bit, trying to get the most from the last warm days - and knowing that, the cleverest of them try to prepare in advance, seeking guidance from the Spirits.

The Winter Expansion for Aridnyk adds a whole new board for you to advance, new unique tiles to place, an all new way to trade with the Spirits, and last but not least - a new Spirit! Ascend on the Winter Track to gain boost bonuses and earn new Winter Tiles which can be placed after all of the regular tiles, providing up to 3 additional decisive
turns and new strategies to make these turns a blast!

—description from the publisher

All the Carpathians are filled with magic, but there are places where it manifests with increased power: primeval woods as old as the land itself, bottomless lakes, cliffs towering above the highest treetops and vast gold meadows, climbing as high as the mountain tops. You have to be truly lucky to find them and even luckier to learn their secrets. This won't be easy-to do that, one should seek guidance of the Spirits, keepers of the Land.

In Sacred Grounds expansion to Anidnyk there are 5 large multi-hex tiles with unique effects. To place them, players must collect Spirit guidance tokens, trading them for the Tile and an Endgame quest, allowing them to collect generous rewards.

—description from the publisher

Dungeon Post is a solo dice-placement game where you take on the role of a determined postal worker braving treacherous dungeons to deliver crucial packages. Each delivery takes you through perilous locations—fiery volcanoes, rogue AI wastelands, or ancient ruins—where you'll roll and assign dice to overcome challenges, manage fatigue, and outwit obstacles. With strategic item use and careful dice allocation, your goal is to successfully complete three deliveries and prove that no terrain is too dangerous for the Dungeon Post!

With the same whimsical art and humor you've (maybe) come to love from Quests Over Coffee, Dungeon Post delivers a fast, fun, and sometimes absurd adventure where every customer is important, even if they live at the bottom of an ancient, trap-laden crypt. Wherever the journey takes you: "Through dungeons old and dangers true, the Dungeon Post will deliver to you!"

—description from the designer
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Holidays and celebrations are a crucial part of every culture - and Ukrainian Hutsuls are not an exception. As seasons pass and Spirits manifest themselves, players will celebrate their arrival with unique events, bonuses and mini-games
based on the Highlanders' traditions.

Mini-expansion for Aridnyk with 10 Festivities cards.

—description from the publisher

It’s Restaurant Week in Bistro Bay! Restaurants are competing in food challenges while also planning an epic meal to impress a celebrity critic. As one of 1-5 players, you’ll be sending your Chefs out into the city to gather ingredients to create amazing meals and demonstrate that your restaurant is the best in town.

Each round new random ingredients are placed in various locations throughout the city. Players simultaneously and secretly plan which locations to send their three chefs to, hoping to collect the best ingredients. Some chefs are fast, but can only gather one item, while others can carry three items, but arrive late. Rumors are also available at locations and provide guidance on what the critic desires.

Challenges, revealed in rounds 1 through 6, offer the players opportunities to earn stars for crafting dishes with specific ingredient requirements. After round 7 the players must create an epic meal to impress the celebrity critic and cater to their appetites. A multitude of different critics, rumors and restaurateurs mean every game is fresh!

—description from the publisher

The Deluxe Kickstarter Edition comes with the A La Cart expansion, the Primal Promo Pack, the Lucky Duck Promo Pack, and all unlocked Deluxe Edition stretch goals from the Kickstarter campaign, which include upgraded components, more Zous Chefs, more Restauranteurs.
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
Captain "Mako", a veteran of the sea, stood at the helm of his fishing boat, the Invincible, as a storm raged around him. The waves rose like menacing giants, lashing the ship with relentless fury. The wind howled like a hunting wolf, but Mako did not waver. His eyes were fixed on the horizon, where he knew the perfect catch awaited him, hidden in the tumultuous depths... Welcome to The Fisherman, the board game that puts you in command of a deep-sea fishing boat ready to sail uncharted waters in search of the perfect catch. Choose your ship, your crew, the items to load on board, and prepare the fuel. Are you ready to set sail?

—description from the designer

boardgame: After the Virus: Extended Promo Character Pack
description:
This is the 2025 official Fryx Games printed release of 4 Promo playable characters for After the Virus and it includes an extra character not included in the 2018 Print & Play downloadable Promo pdf.

Jesse is a new Promo character for this release and is a farmer. He is designed to be used with "The Long Cold" expansion for After the Virus.

This 2025 Promo Pack sees the original three characters from the 2018 downloadable Print & Play pdf (available on the Fryx Games Website) of promo characters (Angela, Bill & Mac) plus Jesse.

What is also new is that, for the first time, these promo characters are all printed on Punchboard just like the other original 4 characters in the base game and the 4 in the expansion.

Unleash a new level of cribbage excitement with Cribbage Clash, the revolutionary cribbage board that transforms the classic game into a dynamic, strategic battle! Forget the same old track – Cribbage Clash features a modular design of uniquely shaped puzzle pieces that fit together in countless configurations, offering endless possibilities for gameplay. Build a regulation 120 hole board or put all the pieces together to build a 600 hole board.

There are many optional add-on pieces that transforms a standard cribbage board into a strategic battle field. All add-ons pieces are optional and can be place anywhere on the board that you want.

Add-on pieces include the following:

No Pass Lanes:  A no pass lane allows only one player through the lane at a time, no passing or jumping a player that is in the no pass lane

Forward/Backward Boosts: Gain an edge or set your opponent back with spaces that propel you forward or send you reeling backward.

Dice Roll Challenges: Test your luck with dice roll spaces that dictate your next move.

Jail Time: Land on a jail space and face a penalty, adding a risk/reward element to your strategy.

Beer Time: Enjoy a few beers while you opponent get to keep pegging points.

Time Out: Have you been naughty? Take a few turns to think about it.
Round about / Detour: Navigate tricky round about and detour spaces that can either advance you or send you in a loop.

Stop Zones: Strategically placed stop zones force players to pause, creating opportunities for calculated plays.

Return to the wonderful world of Mycelia! Along with magical dewdrops you’ll clear Prisma drops from your forest to earn favor and collect gifts. Gifts add more magic to your team of wondrous creatures to help you on your mission. Are you ready to continue the adventure?

In this enchanting expansion to the popular deck-building game, you add more magic to the forest. The goal remains the same, collect leaves and befriend mushrooms to help you clear your board of dewdrops and now Prisma drops. Prisma drops earn you favor and favor helps you acquire valuable gifts. The magical gift cards, designed to help draw up more of the helpful mushroom cards, are sure to delight.

Prisma builds on the expansions from the base game to add depth for all gamers and those who are ready for more magical Mycelia memories.

Everyone plays at once in the Connect 4 Frenzy game! It turns classic Connect 4 gameplay into a disc-bouncing free-4-all.

Bounce discs for 4 in a row: It’s teams red vs. yellow! Players bounce discs off a table into the grid — at the same time! Sink discs, steal spots, and be the first to get 4 in a row to win.

4 ways to play for extra challenges:

     Go for 4 in a row
     Go for the corners only 
     Go for just the center
     Or for a more chill way to play, take turns bouncing 1 disc at a time.


—description from the publisher

Fossil Find is a domino-based board game that places the players in the mind of a paleontologist as they make their way around the game board collecting, trading or ditching the numerous artifacts (dominoes) they come in contact with during the game.

Relic Wars is a ruthless battler and exploration game set in a dark, fantastical world, featuring stunning medieval-inspired artwork with a sinister edge. It throws players into brutal combat on ever-changing battlefields, where no two games are ever the same. Victory demands either crushing your enemies or seizing three legendary golden chalices and surviving the chaos — but the moment someone holds that third chalice, the hunt is on. Every turn is a mix of tactical clashes, strategic spell combos, and game-changing upgrades, where even the best-laid plans can be shattered by Divine Intervention. The game’s perfectly paced length keeps tension high without overstaying its welcome, delivering an effortless, satisfying experience that always leaves players saying, “let’s play again.”

In Relic Wars, players become ruthless warriors, each with unique abilities and hidden motives, locked in a brutal battle for three golden chalices. The path to victory is yours to forge — crush your enemies in ruthless combat or seize the relics and outlast the chaos.

Explore a dynamic, ever-changing battlefield filled with treacherous terrain, powerful relics, and deadly encounters. Uncover arcane artifacts, master devastating spells, and upgrade your warrior as you fight to survive.

Two paths. One victor. Will you claim the chalices or be forgotten in the ashes of war?

boardgame: All Souls: A Relic Wars Expansion
description:
Leave the mortal realm behind and step into the plane of souls, where the search for life, redemption, purpose, and vindication begins anew. In Relic Wars: All Souls, the classic battle royale is transformed, offering players a unique twist: death is no longer the end but the beginning of a ghostly new chapter. This expansion introduces an entirely new layer of replayability, engagement, and strategy to the Relic Wars universe.

The All Souls expansion includes:
Four ghost character miniatures
Four ghost character player cards
New terrain tiles, including the haunting Graveyard and The Castle
New Relic War Tokens, such as the Shroud of Turin, The Crown of Thorns, Longinius the Centurion’s Spear, Air Spell and Holy Water
Immersive scenarios designed to challenge players with fresh strategies and dynamics

Victory in Relic Wars remains multifaceted. Players can still claim triumph by being the last living character standing or by collecting three Holy Grails. However, ghosts now have their own path to victory: by gathering three Holy Grails, they can achieve a narrow but satisfying win. Upon being KO’d, players don’t simply watch from the sidelines. Instead, they re-enter the fray as ghosts, sowing chaos and vying for their own spectral victory.

—description from the publisher

Watercolor is a game of gentle oneupsmanship. Two players will play higher and higher numbers in hopes of being the first to run out of cards in their hand and collect a Prize card. Players will use digit cards to compose their numbers (for instance, a 5 and a 3 can make a 53 or 35).

Every time a player plays a number, they’ll have to take a card that was already on the table into their hand, which means their best-laid plans will oft go astray in dynamic and exciting ways.

Players will get a new Skill card each round, and can use them to disrupt their opponent or buttress their own plans. The first player to collect two prize cards wins the game.

Brutal Beatdown - Savage is the second standalone release in the Brutal Beatdown series. You don’t need the first game Brutal Beatdown - Infestation to play.

Aside from new fighters, items, enemies and special cards specific to each iteration of Brutal Beatdown, this campaign is introducing new stuff to mix!

Endurance Mode: See if you can win against 8 straight opponents while your Health, Skill and Energy stats dwindle from fight to fight.

Tag Team Mode: Partner up with another fighter and square off against two opponents to see which team combination will be the victor.

As an added bonus, you can even combine Tag Team with Endurance Mode to see if your team can go the distance!

—description from the publisher

Sedlec Ossuary, 16th Century AD.

The Black Plague and Hussite Wars have overcrowded the graveyard. Help the Bone Collector, a half-blind monk, by exhuming graves and arranging the skulls inside the crypt.

You are novice monks, competing to create the best arrangement of skulls.

Dig up graves from the graveyard to reveal cards, take cards into your hand to collect skulls, and arrange the cards from your hand into a stack. Whoever better honors the deceased’s last wishes will score more points.

The Bone Collector will then judge each player’s completed stack and declare one as the most exceptional.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

boardgame: Konja: Ob Da Ah T! Promo Card
description:
Konja Kickstarter edition promo card.

Game effect: buy for 1 gold, sell for 3 gold

Kickstarter promo card.

Game effect: ‘Spend 1 Power to claim a Spell from the Spell discard pile.’

Baget Relic card from French edition of Konja.

Game effect: ‘Gain 2 power tokens’

Welcome to the Kingdom of Ain! Beast Slayers is a competitive or cooperative Strategic fantasy Card and Dice Game that will pit Beast Slayers against each other or together as a team for fame and Glory. Each player will embody a Monster Hunter who will need to take on contracts, track down monsters and slay them in order to gain gold and reputation. But your travels will not be without danger and the land of Ain has many hidden surprises for whoever steps down the wrong path. In the end, players will need to gather reputation to succeed and become the most renowned Beast Slayers in the Kingdom.

—description from the designer

Your ship is the Sea Dog.  It's your home, it's your mode of transport, and it's your way of life!  The open sea is the home for thee, and you're not alone.  Sail the high seas and face untold threats on the water, skies, land and aboard other vessels!  Don't let the Sea Dog sink, because the captain always goes down with the ship.

This massive pack contains; 4 Adventurers, 4 Quests, "100" Dungeon cards, a total of 48 images that are all unique to this set!
This non-random aquatic themed Odyssey pack is used to expand your Dungeon Crawler gaming experience.  A bonus 12 promotional cards will be included in this deck!

—description from the designer

The American Civil War was the bloodiest conflict and a defining event in the history of the United States. The tragedy of the conflict was amplified by the fact the battles were brutally fought with Napoleonic tactics while using new and more lethal weaponry. As a result, over the course of nearly four full years of war, roughly 700,000 Americans were wounded or killed in battle along with another 400,000 soldiers dying from disease.

Blue & Gray Deluxe Edition consists of the original eight games from the SPI Blue & Gray I & II Quadrigames. The battles depicted are among the most important of the American Civil War: Shiloh, Antietam, Fredericksburg, Chancellorsville (Hooker & Lee), Gettysburg (Cemetery Hill), Chickamauga, Chattanooga, and The Battle of the Wilderness.

The game mechanics used in this series are based on the well-received Borodino/Napoleon at Waterloo system. Terrain is a significant factor, with everything from rolling farmland to untracked wilderness represented. Battles will often see a back-and-forth struggle for control of critical terrain. Rules for night turns are also provided. A standard set of rules are used, common to all eight games, while each game also has its own set of exclusive rules, historical set-ups, and player and design notes—all from the original SPI games.

Blue & Gray Deluxe Edition features new artwork, double-sided mounted game boards, enlarged counters, player aid cards, numerous optional rules gleaned from other game editions, and new variant counters and markers for use with several variants from Moves and Strategy & Tactics magazines. Reintroduce yourself to these classic games from SPI’s golden era and refight some of the best-known battles of the American Civil War which decided the fate of a nation at war with itself.

—description from the publisher

description:
An action game for up to 4 players in which you have to feed your own sharks and at the same time protect your own fish from being eaten.

The starting player shakes the fish and then lets them fall through their hands so that they fall onto the table without flying far apart.
Fish lying on their side are fish, fish standing upright are sharks. Sharks eat all fish (not other sharks) that are in the direction of their mouth. The sharks receive one point for each fish they eat, and a minus point for each fish they eat. 
However, the result displayed after dropping the fish can still be changed by placing the white fish. Beginning with the starting player, the players now take it in turns to carefully throw their white fish into the crowd in such a way that the changes are as positive as possible for them. White fish are scored like other fish, but can also become sharks during the action.

—description from the publisher

Parlament is a political tactics game for 3-5 players aged 12 and over. By correctly distributing and deploying their party officials and campaigners, players can consolidate their influence on legislation, government policy and in parliament and thus help shape the country's politics.

The aim is to win recognition points with attractive policies and ultimately end your political career as a respected politician. Well, of course everyone is also trying to make money. Idealism simply has its limits.

—description from the publisher

A tactical card game for 2 people for the majority on 6 landscapes.

Each player receives an identical deck of cards and takes 3 cards into their hand.
The starting player chooses two cards and places them

     place one on top of the other face down in their hand so that the top card covers a landscape on the bottom card,
     then places these two cards on one of the cards on display so that only the upper of the two landscapes remains visible.


The player may arrange the cards to be discarded as they wish - turning the cards changes the order of the landscapes shown.
The round ends when all the cards have been discarded. The opposing blocks are then scored. To do this, the cards are pulled apart so that all the landscapes are visible.

Play continues until one player has scored 5 points in at least three landscape categories and at least one point for each landscape. If both players meet this requirement, the player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Bible is Funny Volume II
description:
The highly anticipated second installment of The Bible Is Funny Card Game, with all-new prompts that can be used on their own or combined with the original cards for extended game play!

Before I leave the house, I always make sure I have my phone and my:
Jawbone of a Donkey - Judges 15:15 (NIV)
Bread baked over cow dung - Ezekiel 4:15 (NLT)
Five Gold Rats - 1 Samuel 6:4 (NIV)

In the same format of 70 prompts and 165 Bible verses spread across 200 cards, The Bible Is Funny Card Game, Volume II invites players with a lot, a little, or no Bible knowledge at all to match out-of-context verses with prompts about everyday situations to win the funniest pairing. The verse cards feature obscure, ludicrous, or downright outrageous Bible verses or objects pulled completely out of their original context. The prompt cards offer contemporary situations into which those Bible verses can be inserted.

Perfect for Bible enthusiasts and novices alike, this game offers fun for the whole family, whether you’re gathering around the coffee table, crossing the Red Sea, or hanging out with your friends in a fiery furnace.

In Indagini Minute you will find a precise number of portraits related to as many culprits and descriptions of related investigations. For each investigation, you will need to understand:

     what happened
     who the culprit is
     associate the face and name with it


It is an investigation & deduction game based on the search for clues and successive eliminations. It is possible to arrive at the identification of the culprits in two ways: a direct, more standard approach requires you to connect all the clues about the culprit scattered among the various cases; that by elimination, on the other hand, is achieved by excluding all non-guilty people and those who have already been matched to other cases. What is special about these cases is that the writing involves seeing clues even where there are none.

The product features 3 game modes:

SOLITARY MODE - Rely only on yourself!

COOPERATIVE MODE - Collaborate in solving the cases!

COMPETITIVE MODE - Challenge each other to see who can total the most Points!

boardgame: Шёпот за стеной: Промокарты предметов (Terrorscape: Niza Gams Edition Item Promo Cards)

Pack of five item cards:

     Скелет в шкафу ("The Skeleton in the Closet")

     Куб-головоломка ("Cube Puzzle")

Bid, trade, and win at the Barter Market!


Enter the vibrant Coscomatepec market, where you and other bidders will try to get the most valuable food.
Potatoes, lettuce, tomatoes, eggs, bananas, and blueberries are the most prized treasures. As a clever farmer, you'll use your best strategies to get the most valuable products or try to get yours as valuable.

But be careful! The market is unpredictable. New demands, shortages, and even thieves can change the rules of the game in the blink of an eye. Will you be able to adapt and overcome the challenges to become the winner?

Barter Market is an exciting and ever-changing auction game for 2 to 4 players, ages 8 and up. With quick 20- to 30-minute games, this game will captivate you with its dynamics and the excitement of the auctions.

Are you ready to experience the thrill of bartering?
