WWI Themed tactical deckbuilder. Capture your opponents' flag(s) and return them to your HQ to win!
Players spend cards to buy more cards and recruit more soldiers, and play cards for actions to give orders to their soldiers and invade the enemy's trench line. Defend and attack using your deck, build fortifications and strategically build a deck with everything you need to bring your opponent's flag back home.
Cards all have a cost and a value. Buy them at cost and spend them for their value to buy further cards. New cards are put in that player's discard pile and shuffled back into the deck once the deck runs out.
Move soldiers through the board using order cards to reach and return your opponents' flag(s). Fend off your opponent by building fortifications and buying the best weaponry. 
In the end, the team who captures every flag of the opposing team wins.

boardgame: MadPoly: Fun Party Drinking Board Game
description:
Unleash the Ultimate Party Experience with MadPoly

Prepare to embark on an unforgettable night of mischief and laughter with MadPoly, the one-of-a-kind party board game that's sure to captivate your guests. Inspired by the best-selling MadWish and the timeless charm of classic board games, MadPoly offers a unique twist that will have you and your friends spinning the bottle and embracing the thrill of the unexpected.

Become the Master of MadPoly's Bars

MadPoly features bars assigned to each co-player instead of houses. Your goal is to win as much money as possible by navigating the streets of MadPoly. But the excitement doesn't stop there – as a bar owner, you'll have the power to give out shots if your opponent fails to pay the designated amount. Get ready to experience the ultimate blend of strategy and social interaction!

Spin the Bottle and Embrace the Challenges

When you land on the "Spin" space, you'll spin the bottle in the middle of the game board and complete one of six thrilling challenges: Tell a truth, do a Dare, take on a Group Go challenge, or draw a Mystery Card. From showcasing your acting skills to testing your wit, these challenges will keep you and your friends on the edge of your seats, creating unforgettable memories.

The Perfect Party Game for Any Occasion

In the card game Compile, you are competing Artificial Intelligences trying to understand the world around you. Two players select three Protocols each to test. Concepts ranging from Darkness to Water are pitted against each other to reach ultimate understanding. Play cards into your Protocols' command lines to breach the threshold and defeat your opponent to Compile. First to Compile all three Protocols grasps those concepts to win the game.

Control your opponent's Protocols with card actions, Compile your own as fast as possible, and Compile your reality.
Compile: Aux 2 includes three new Protocols: Assimilation, Diversity, Unity.

Fish n’ Ships Aegean Sea is an educational and engaging tabletop game for 2 to 4 players, where participants compete to sustainably manage their own marine ecosystems in the Aegean Sea. Players must strategically balance fishing, species protection, and environmental challenges, striving to maximize their points by sustainably harvesting species, managing ecological relationships, and maintaining biodiversity.

Gameplay mechanics highlight the delicate balance of marine ecosystems, demonstrating clearly the impact of different fishing techniques, along with the critical role of marine conservation through special cards such as Fisheries Exclusion Zones and Marine Protected Areas. Players must carefully construct and maintain a resilient marine food web while navigating both beneficial and harmful environmental events.

Who will achieve the highest score by sustainably catching valuable species and strategically protecting biodiversity, mastering the art of balancing human activities with ecological health?

description:
Skirmish is a tactical board game for 2 players featuring bluffing, deception, and strategic maneuvering. Players command armies of five fantasy races - humans, gnomes, dwarves, elves, and trolls - each with unique abilities to defeat their opponents.

Quick Overview
In Skirmish, players move their pieces on a hexagonal board, trying to either eliminate their opponent's king or infiltrate the enemy camp. What makes the game truly engaging is the disguise system - players can mask their pieces as different races to deceive their opponent.

How to Play
Each turn is simple: move one piece to an adjacent empty hex. The complexity comes from the interactions between races:

     Each race can kill one specific enemy race (e.g., trolls kill elves)
     Each race can scare another race, forcing it to flee


Players must carefully plan their moves, remembering that appearances can be deceiving due to disguises. When a disguised piece is killed, only the disguise is removed, revealing the actual piece underneath.

How to Win
Victory can be achieved in two ways:

     Eliminate your opponent's king
     Move any of your pieces into your opponent's camp


This dual victory condition creates tension and forces players to divide their attention between attack and defense.

Skirmish combines the accessibility of a simple move system with the depth of a chess-like tactical game, all enhanced by the unique bluffing element of disguises.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Elden Ring: Realm of the Grafted King – Erdtree Avatar
description:
Strange and misshapen, each appears as a man wrapped in dry bark and gnarled branches. Why are they here? And what secrets do they protect? It falls to you to find these answers

This expansion introduces the Erdtree Avatar mega boss to your tabletop Elden Ring campaigns. Large and headless, the Avatar is swift to dispatch unwary Tarnished with a swing of its formidable staff. Will you best the Avatar and Guardian warriors, devoted protectors of the Erdtree? Their large swordspears and tendency to recover from harm make them tricky to defeat!

The Erdtree Avatar also brings a brand new type of combat to the tabletop – Mounted Tarnished. While mounted, Tarnished have greatly increased mobility. This is a huge advantage in battle, enabling Tarnished to sweep in and damage the Avatar. Be careful not to let the Avatar land a blow in return. Those Tarnished strong enough to overcome their enemies will be greatly rewarded, with no less than the Staff of the Avatar itself — a unique and powerful new weapon to wield on the path to become Elden Lord.


     Challenging new mega boss enemy encounter
     Unique encounter map where you'll battle this bizarre creature
     Unique combat gameplay
     New Roundtable Hold cards
     New equipment
     Mounted combat mechanics


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Elden Ring: Realm of the Grafted King – Flying Dragon Agheel
description:
The dead gaze at the skies over the lakes of Limgrave, praying that the dragons' flames would burn them to ash.

A terrifying sight to behold, Flying Dragon Agheel has already laid waste to regions of Limgrave by the time our Tarnished return to the Lands Between; and no location has suffered more than the aptly-named Dragon-Burnt Ruins.

Presiding over this desolate wasteland, Agheel is quick to chase down any intruder to its realm and annihilate them with tooth, claw, and bursts of dragon-fire.

With this expansion, you’ll face the largest enemy in Elden Ring: The Board Game — the huge Flying Dragon Agheel, on its 140mm base!

Flying Dragon Agheel also brings a brand new type of combat to the tabletop – Mounted Tarnished.

While mounted, Tarnished have greatly increased mobility. This is a huge advantage in battle, enabling Tarnshed to sweep in to damage Agheel, before escaping from the reach of its burning breath. But be careful not to let a single hit from the dragon land, as a dismounted Tarnished will find themselves extremely vulnerable to this fierce foe.

Those Tarnished strong enough to overcome this enemy will be greatly rewarded as they discover unique and powerful new weaponry to wield on the path to become Elden Lord.


     Challenging new mega boss enemy encounter
     Unique boss encounter map
     Unique combat gameplay
     New Roundtable Hold cards
     New equipment
     Mounted combat mechanics
     Dedicated boss cards

boardgame: Elden Ring: Realm of the Grafted King – Limgrave Depths
description:
The glory of this realm, now faded since its fall into ruin; a wealth of mysteries and forgotten locations nonetheless await those bold and intrepid souls.

Explore the land and face its fearsome denizens as you venture deeper into the decaying majesty of Limgrave. Remain vigilant, for savage creatures roam…

Containing the unlocked content from the Elden Ring: The Board Game Kickstarter, the Limgrave Depths Expansion adds eight thrilling new scenarios, three advanced enemies, and over 10 hours of challenging quests to your game.

This set includes two new Tarnished to journey through the formidable landscape, the Wretch and the Warrior. The Wretch, in particular, is designed for players seeking an extra layer of challenge at the start of their campaigns.

A bonus unlock added during the pledge manager, this set also includes Blaidd the Half-Wolf. Often found howling in the Mistwood Ruins, Blaidd is one of the early NPC’s you encounter if you wander to the east in the video game.


     10+ hours of new quests
     Five new enemy types, including the Demi-Human Queen, Mad Pumpkin Head, and Runebears
     Two new Tarnished player characters – the Warrior and Wretch
     New items and equipment

Get ready to dive deeper into the Gudnak CGU with the Marvorren, who are (canonically) not fans of being called mermaids.

This big box expansion contains over 140 new cards and introduces new deck construction rules for alternate basic fighters along with 3-player mode.

Hold the Line: Hannibal is a grand tactical two-player game covering 10 of the greatest battles of the Punic Wars. Following in the footsteps of Richard Borg’s Commands and Colors system and more directly Worthington’s Hold the Line series, Hannibal includes the same standard size map board, 13 hexes wide and 9 hexes deep. Combat is resolved with custom dice.

—description from the publisher

Bio Blitz is a fast-paced resource management game that’s easy to learn and compact enough to take on the go! In Bio Blitz, players assemble a team of real-world wildlife, strategically combining their unique traits to gain a tactical edge against their opponents. Gather your team and compete to see who reigns supreme in the wild world of the animal kingdom!

Bio Blitz is a game where the goal is to win by either reaching 30 points or being the first to assemble a full team. To succeed, players must strategically manage their resources and carefully choose which cards to add to their team. 

On each turn, players must take one of three main actions: forage for resources, discover animal and ecosystem cards, or compete to gain points. In addition to main actions, players may take two optional actions—trading and exploring — any number of times at the start of their turn.  As you build your team, you’ll combine your animals’ special abilities, giving you a tactical advantage. If you’re ready to rack up some extra points and take a gamble, challenge one of your opponent’s animals to a head-to-head competition!

Pick one of your animals and call out an opponent’s creature for a showdown. Both players roll a die and add their animal’s bonuses—the highest total wins! If your total power matches or exceeds the opponent’s animal’s defense, you defeat them, sending them to the discard pile and scoring points equal to their value.

—description from the designer

description:
South Mountain 1862 is Volume VI in Worthington's Civil War Brigade Battle Series. 
With streamlined mechanics the series rules, and battle specific rules, gamers can refight the Battle of South Mountain in 2 to 3 hours.

Brigade Level Detail:
Each infantry and cavalry piece is a brigade with each strength point 100 men, color boxes surrounding the strength show morale of green, veteran, or crack.  Each artillery piece strength point represents 2 cannon.

This game will provide countless replays and hours of enjoyment to history buffs trying to see if they can recreate, or change history.

Did the Rat escape? That's the question you and your friends will answer in Mafia Blitz, the lightning-fast bluffing game where every role has their own way to win. In just 15 minutes, players will scheme, deceive, and outsmart each other, all while dodging the Vigilante’s watchful gaze.

Mafia Blitz is a social deduction game played in a single round with no player elimination and role-drafting system for 6-12 players. A single player will take on the role of the Vigilante, a public role with a single objective: Make sure the Rat isn't shot by anyone. The rest of players will pick their role through a draft and the chosen role is the player's win condition for the game.

From there, the players discuss what happened, such as what roles they picked or passed to the next player. Of course, you don't have to tell the truth! It is an open discussion, although the Vigilante has the authority to prevent cross-talks or enforce silence periods. The Vigilante is equipped with Verdict cards and they place them in front of players they suspect are Goons. Once placed, the end the discussion by saying "Bang!" The selected players show their roles and any Goons not shot will now reveal themselves and attempt to shoot the Rat.

Each role has its own path to victory. Some are hunting others, while others are trying to keep their heads low. And some special scoundrels have their own agendas to fulfill – no matter the cost.

—description from the designer

description:
Goblin Rush is a card game of fast-paced combat, snap decisions and battlefield dominance.

Lead your goblins to glory in one-on-one combat, utilise diverse Infantry effects or boost your force with mighty Leaders.

Designed for two players, this take-that battle card game offers a fresh twist on the classic “21” format. Players draw cards from a shared supply and deploy Infantry or Leader goblins to seize control of the battlefield.

The objective is to build a force totalling 21 or more power before your opponent. Achieving this condition results in an immediate win, though doing so is easier said than done. Each goblin features a unique ability that can strengthen your forces, disrupt your opponent, or otherwise shift the game in unexpected ways.

Aggression is the name of the game as these goblins know not the meaning of restraint and defensive play will be met with an insane gaze and possibly an axe in the back. You have been warned!

The official card game based on the pop'n music rhythm game franchise.

Rules for 2 players
When the word "start" is called, players must quickly clear the "Pop-kun Cards" by combining them with cards in their hand and on the table, without letting the other player take them.
The player with the most "Pop-kun Cards" wins. This game relies on speed and judgment.

Rules for 3-5 players
When it is a player's turn, they must play "Button Cards" according to the color shown on their "Pop-kun Card". They must play cards with a total number greater than the total number written on the "Button Card" played by the previous player.
When a round of turns has been played, the player with the highest total number can get the "Pop-kun Card".
The game ends when any player has 0 cards in their hand, and the player with the most "Pop-kun Cards" wins.

"Fear not death, for the hour of your doom is set, and none may escape it."

Those not killed by the bitter snow are preyed upon by the monsters of the dark. Your warriors stand between these vile creatures and your clan. Guard your kin with iron and fury.

Slayers of Valika is a trick taking card game set in viking inspired region of Valika in Grim Hollow's Etharis. Battle it out against other players to win the most tricks and protect your clan from dangerous monsters.

—description from the publisher

UNO: Speed Pack Add-On of 16 cards introduces a new level of excitement to the classic UNO card game by speeding up gameplay and challenging players to match the increasing speed. Each "Speed Up" icon played raises the number of cards all players must play on their subsequent turns, starting from the next player. Initially, each player plays one card per turn, but with each "Speed Up" card played, this number increases incrementally, pushing players to play multiple matching cards or draw more cards. The Wild "Double Speed Up" card escalates the intensity by increasing the required card count by two levels and lets the player choose the next color. Action cards like Draw 2, Skip, and Reverse only activate when played as the last card of a player's turn. The speed resets once a player can't keep up, crashing the game's pace and returning it to normal until the next "Speed Up" card is played.

—description from the rulebook (adapted)

UNO: Reverse Pack Add-On of 16 cards adds chaotic fun to the classic UNO game by introducing a variety of enhanced reverse cards. This pack includes new twists like Reverse Draw 2, Reverse Skip, Wild Power Reverse, and the unique Wild NO U card. Reverse Draw 2 reverses the direction and forces the next player to draw two cards, skipping their turn. Reverse Skip reverses the direction and skips the next player's turn entirely. The Wild Power Reverse lets you reverse play direction, pick the next color, and take another turn. The Wild NO U card can counter any draw-card action, reversing its penalty back to the previous player and changing the color, or simply act as a standard wild card with reverse functionality. These cards add strategic depth and unpredictability, requiring players to stay alert and adjust quickly.

—description from the rulebook (adapted)

UNO: Stack Pack Add-On of 16 cards enhances the classic UNO card game by adding stackable draw penalties that increase the game's intensity. Players can use Stack 1 and Stack 2 cards to force the next player to draw one or two cards, respectively, while losing their turn. When responding to other draw cards (+2, +4), these Stack cards can escalate the penalty, making the next player draw even more cards. Wild Stack 3 cards allow players to choose the next color and impose a draw-three penalty on the following player. The unpredictable Wild Stack Number card dramatically heightens the tension by revealing the top number card from the draw pile to determine how many cards the next player must draw. This add-on adds strategic depth and can create significant turnarounds, keeping gameplay exciting.

—description from the rulebook (adapted)

UNO: Swap Pack Add-On of 16 cards brings new excitement and unpredictability to the classic UNO card game by introducing unique card-swapping mechanics. Cards such as Swap 1 allow players to exchange a random card with another player's hand. Refresh Hand enables players to completely discard their current hand and draw an equal number of new cards from the draw pile, drastically changing their game strategy. The Wild Force Trade lets a player choose two participants, including themselves, to trade their entire hands, followed by selecting the next play color. Wild Pass Hands dramatically reshapes gameplay by having all players pass their hand to the next player in the current order, again allowing the initiating player to choose the continuing play color. This dynamic add-on makes strategy shifts and sudden game changes frequent, maintaining constant player engagement.

—description from the rulebook (adapted)

Players become fishermen and drop nets (hair bands) to catch as many preys as possible in the fishing grounds.
Even if you have a good aim and drop the net, you may not get the prey because the waves in the fishing grounds are very rough.
Or the net may get caught in coral or fly out of the fishing grounds...?
The fisherman's skill will be put to the test, whether to compete steadily with the number of fish or to aim for bonuses.

—description from the publisher

Play as the crew of the Spark, the spaceship equipped with the most advanced AI in the universe. Your mission is to deliver precious cargo to the four corners of the galaxy to ensure the survival of humanity. Beware, alien pirates and hostile environments await you, so only the best will arrive in one piece.

Galactic Modules is an expansion for Spark Riders 3000, an hybrid single player/co-operative tower defense and survival game in which players must coordinate their actions to ensure the success of their mission. Each scenario is different and you will have to adapt quickly to avoid seeing your ship destroyed and its cargo intercepted by alien pirates.

The goal is simple: reach your delivery point without having one of the ship's vital rooms destroyed.

—description from the publisher

Harvest Materials - Build your Kingdom - Battle your friends. A bold combination of resource collection, nation-building, and war gaming - The world of Castles & Conquests awaits you. The ultimate in High Fantasy board games. Gather raw materials from the lands around you. Build up your Kingdom in stone and mortar. And bolster your nation with an army full of heroes and siege engines. But beware, the call of WAR ever looms over you all. Victory will favour the bold, and the lucky.  Castles & Conquests has near-endless re-playability. Battle your friends, trade with your enemies, forge alliances, and use multiple strategies to try and win.

Castles & Conquests makes use of elements from war-gaming, tabletop-role-playing, resource-collection games, trading card games, and many more synthesized into one ultimate high-fantasy board game experience. It is intended for ages 12 and up, and for 2-6 players. If you’ve played other resource-collection games before, then you’ll pick this up quick. The average play time is anywhere from 45 minutes to several hours depending on how many people are playing, and which strategies are being employed in the game. I hope you enjoy and have fun!

—description from the designer

boardgame: 69: A Nice Card Game. About Numbers.
description:
69 is a fast-paced card-matching game for 2 to 6 players. The rules are simple enough for any age, but for some reason, players below a certain age wouldn't stop giggling when we demoed it, so we recommend playing only with serious, mature adults.

The 69 deck is mostly made up of number cards (0-9), in six suits. The 6 and 9 of each suit are interchangeable. Use them for whichever number you want, depending on which side is up.

There are also three wild cards (with three of each in the deck):

     Wild: a simple wild card that can count as any number and suit.
     Lock: a wild card that also stops another player from stealing your cards.
     Scramble: a wild card that lets you rearrange everything on the table. 


How to play:

     Deal: Every player starts with ten cards, and the dealer flips one card face up on the table.
     Play: On your turn, try to play cards from your hand onto cards on the table to make a stack. The cards in a stack need to all have different suits, and need to be either all the same value or form a run (cards in numerical order).
     Punt: If you can't play a card from your hand, pick up the top card from the deck and play it. Form a stack if you can (and want to), or leave it on the table to start a new stack.
     Capture: If a stack gets three or more cards, you capture it! Take the cards and put them face down in front of you...unless someone steals it!
     Steal: If another player can add legal cards to your completed stack, they can steal it from you...unless someone else steals it from them!
     Score: Once someone plays the last card from their hand, the round is over. Score points for every card you captured, and lose points for every card left in your hand.
     Repeat: Play another round, adding to your score. The first player to get 69 points wins the game!


—description from the publisher

Snow Leopard Shadow is a board game for 1 to 4 players. The game is set in the Helan Mountain on the border of Inner Mongolia and Gansu in China, which is the habitat of wild snow leopards. On September 22, 2020, an infrared camera set up by Peking University and the protected area captured the first image of a snow leopard using infrared technology in this region, marking the first such capture in 67 years. This discovery inspired the game's design.
In the game, players take on the roles of wildlife researchers and snow leopards. As a wildlife researcher, you place infrared cameras on the map, avoiding rivers and yellow points. As a snow leopard, you must move through these camera traps. The player who captures the most snow leopard photos wins.You will learn about snow leopards' habits and habitats in the game.

Gameplay：
The game consists of two phases:
First Phase (Camera Placement Phase):

     Each player has five opportunities to place cameras, avoiding rivers and yellow points.


Second Phase (Snow Leopard Movement Phase):

     Players take turns rolling five dice and choosing two numbers to move the snow leopard.
     Other players choose two different numbers from the remaining options.
     Each move costs steps, and you have a total of 25 steps. Crossing a camera triggers a photo.


Objective:
The goal is to capture the most snow leopard photos. The player with the most photos at the end wins.

—description from the publisher

Tetris Tumble XL is designed to challenge players with a dynamic, tilting tower, players strategically stack oversized Tetris blocks as the tower shifts and sways. With each move, the balance of the tower changes, so stack carefully! Players go head-to-head to see who can topple the tower first!

Iconic Tetris Gameplay with a Twist
Experience the excitement of Tetris in a whole new way with a dynamic, tilting tower that adds a fresh layer of strategy and challenge.

Carry Bag
Once the tower comes tumbling down, and it's time to pack up, the whole set easily slides into the included carry bag.

Original Tetrimino Shapes Included
Features all 7 iconic Tetris block shapes, bringing nostalgia and authenticity to this engaging stacking game.

—description from the publisher

This pack adds a new type of card to the Murderopolis deck: ten action cards that provide powerful advantages, such as stealing cards from an opponent's hand, increasing your hand size, or making someone skip a turn.

In a near future, the Exclusion Zone around Chernobyl is populated again by fortune-seeking scavengers, set on finding precious resources in the contaminated wasteland. Take control of a scavenger Community; hunt, search for resources, trade and fight to carve your destiny in the Zone.

Gameplay Overview
During a game of Nuclear Winds, you'll control 3 scavengers from your community, exploring and moving them across various locations, looting resources and, if need be, fight against other Inhabitants. At the end of the 8th round, the player with the most Victory Points wins.

—description from the designer

This is not an expansion, but a self-contained game that functions as a sequel. In the first game, the goal was to collect the most kittens, in this one, you're dealt a starting hand of three kittens, and must keep them by playing sushi cards, the goal being to be the last player to have kittens left.

HELLDIVERS 2: The Board Game conveys the fast-paced, tactical, tongue-in-cheek energy of the video game, plunging players into the role of elite shocktroopers — the valiant Helldivers — who must protect Super Earth and rid the galaxy of alien threats in the name of liberty and democracy. Armed with deadly weapons (pistols, machine guns, flamethrowers), powerful stratagems (turrets, airstrikes, etc.), and co-ordinated tactics, players will work in squads to complete high-risk missions, navigate hostile battlefields, call in reinforcements, and overcome enemy swarms. As in the video game, players need to contend with evolving threats and objectives, in addition to watching for explosions and friendly fire. Every decision can mean the difference between glorious victory or total annihilation in service of Super Earth.

Helldivers win the game by selecting a mission and completing their objective. While doing so, they collect medals by defeating enemies, completing sub-objectives, and successfully extracting from the warzone, which will determine their overall performance. If a Helldiver falls in glorious battle, another is sent in their place. If they run out of reinforcements or time to complete the mission, the game is lost.

boardgame: Fiese Freunde Fette Feten: Hunter & Friends Promo
description: Add 8 new cards to your game. 2 red ones (puberty); 2 blue ones (life) and 4 green ones (lifegoals).

WRLD is a 2-4 player competitive game about building a new WRLD. Players start with the old WRLD and take turns maneuvering Cubes from the old WRLD to build the new WRLD.

Players should try to arrange the different Types (Forest, Ocean, Cave, Desert, Lava, and Tundra) by performing Actions during their turn. Each Type scores differently.

Players gain points by scoring based on the View presented to each player.

At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

KeyForge: Discovery is a set of more than 300 classic KeyForge cards chosen for their simplicity, intuitiveness, and fun factor. Every unique deck generated from this set provides an easy introduction to the game, with a focus on core game mechanics and only a minimal use of keywords.

There is just enough variety and depth to keep both new and veteran players excited and engaged in discovering what KeyForge has to offer.

Players will call upon the strength of these houses in the set: Brobnar, Dis, Logos, Sanctum, Shadows, Star Alliance, and Untamed.

This is a free Print and Play hex and counter tank game. It is a fully developed and comprehensive game of armored warfare in WW2 Europe and North Africa; from Poland 1939 to Germany 1945.

Its core design is to provide highly detailed shot resolution and yet play as quickly and as simply as “game possible”; everything was rethought from the ground up to meet this goal. What use to take a few minutes to resolve a shot now takes a few seconds, what use to take several tables now takes one table. With this new system large tank battles (50+ units) can easily be played with each tank having separate armor thickness for its Turret/Hull-Front/Side/Rear/Top and shots can be AP, HEAT, or HE and can take advantage of AP Overmatch.

The attacker's goal is to destroy the defender’s towns or exit his tanks off the gameboard edge, while the defender's goal is to destroy the attacker’s HQ. Players take turns moving their unit counters on the hexagon grid and firing their guns. The game plays very well as a solitaire game.

Simple games can be played with only a few tanks, or large complex games can be played using the fully comprehensive rule set. Rules include direct-fire, indirect-fire, artillery, offboard artillery, rockets, aircraft, armored trains, anti-tank rifles, bazookas/panzerfaust, mines.... Rules also include mechanical break-downs, tow/push/winch, field repair & maintenance units, tank transporters, low fuel/no fuel, weather, demo bridges, pontoon bridges, wading, paratroops, glider troops...

The game includes 120 armored vehicles, 34 artillery and rocket types, and 20 different air unit types.

Hope you enjoy!

boardgame: KoolWheelz: The Classic Drag Racing Game
description:
KoolWheelz is a two to four player game where players will compete in “Weekend Racing Events” and “Pinks” races, Pinks is taking ownership of the opponents car after winning a race. There are 126 playing cards, or race cards, in the game, 18 different types. You will be collecting cards, drawing a card on your turn, preparing for a race day event. For an event to happen it needs sponsorship and will require and event card, money- funding card, and then a team card. In order to go racing, enter an event, you will need a couple specific cards in your hand like tires and fuel, and there are other cards that can help you win a race, bonus and steal cards. Races are resolved by rolling a die and consulting a sector table for result, the drag strip is divided into ten sectors with each sector having its own table. The first car to cross the finish line is the winner, but there is a speed trap prior to the finish and if both cars are within this area then the trailing car has a shot to cross first. The rules don’t state it but that sounds like a shot-shot rule to me, pretty cool. The game ends after a predetermined number of Mr of race events, recommended four-six, and the player winning the most events is the season champion.

—user summary

description:

Red ant: 2 points per every card under a Tree.

Brutal Beatdown: Training is an introductory version of Brutal Beatdown to help get fighters prepared. As such it is an iteration that is a paired down version intended to help introduce the game.

It comes with a few extras like Bot Fighters, Training Items and a Training Mat of its own, along with content that can be mixed with the other Brutal Beatdown games like Brutal Beatdown - Infestation or Brutal Beatdown - Savage!

The novelty in Clients v/s Architects lies in the asymmetry between the players. Each player plays on a different board, and each board is different with critical common elements. Everyone plays by the same rules across their different boards, but the rules affect each player differently. In addition to moving across their boards to gain points, players play their cards to modify the rules, which affect everyone. One half of your strategy focuses on where you move, and the other half focuses on how you manipulate the rules.

Game Play
Each player plays as a client and architect against other players who are their own client/architect pair. Each player has their own unique board across which their pawn moves. The boards are part maze and part treasure map.  Since each board is different, players must bid for and win the boards they want to play at the start of the game. Once the boards have been assigned and each player is dealt a hand of 6 cards, the players' client pawns begin to move across their boards. With each move the client pawn pays their architect an amount indicated on the space they move to, but their funds are limited and they will go bankrupt unless they continue to get additional money throughout the game.

Each player plays a hand of cards to get their client more funds and  get these funds to their architect. The cards are played like poker with different hands unlocking different paths, areas, special rules, and rewards on the playing boards.

The object of the game is for the player's architect to play their cards, direct their client, and earn the most money as the client moves from the starting to the finishing space on the board.

—description from the designer

The robots are taking over! To stop them, your group needs to enter a shutdown code. But one of your teammates is secretly a robot trying to sabotage your efforts!

In order to avoid interception, you must communicate the code by answering prompts only a “real human” would know, and giving answers on a scale of 1 to 10.

Will you succeed? Can you uncover the robot? Or will the robots take over?

—description from the publisher

Trapped within the enigmatic Time Vault, the Traveler and your Papermades are held captive. The Traveler, yearning for his own world, devises a plan: to fuse his arcane knowledge with the Vault's Fragments and conjure a teleportation spell. He pledges to return your Papermades home in exchange for these vital Fragments. As an Artisan, craft your Papermades and rise to the challenge.

Papermade Duel is a collectible papercraft board game offering a distinctive multiplayer experience, combining strategic depth with fast-paced gameplay. Its dynamic mechanics and engaging design focus on tactical decision-making and efficient resource management.

Each Artisan takes turns performing actions such as strategically moving Papermades, playing Action cards for advantages or to hinder opponents, collecting Fragments for benefits, and planning future turns by positioning Papermades, managing their hand, and creating synergies between Action cards, Fragments, and Papermade skills.

Will you deliver the Traveler's sacred Fragments, or stand as the lone survivor amidst the ruins of your rivals? Which path to your DESTINY will you forge?"

—description from the designer

boardgame: メイドマフィアKH (Maid Mafia KH)
description:
Maid Mafia KH is a expansion to Maid Mafia Kai and Maid Mafia KY. This extension can be applied to either product.

KH is based on Hakodate in Hokkaido, following K in Kobe and KY in Yokohama. In addition, 8 maids suitable for the region are newly added.

And with the support of the 1.5 version update of the rules, a bit more speedy play is possible, and a rule exclusive to 2-player battles has also been added.

Talam Ramadan is a Malaysian boardgame that involves tile placement representing different kinds of food onto a "talam" which is a special kind of platter used in Malaysia. During the holy month of Ramadan, breaking fast and eating together is a norm. This boardgame represents friends coming together to share food by placing them onto the "talam", making sure nothing is wasted.

The game is played by drafting tiles representing food. Each player is given a tray that represents their stomach capacity. Everyone draws two tiles and places them onto the tray. The player is not allowed to view their own tray, but can see the other players tray. Each player must then take a tile from the pouch and place it onto the "talam", each food symbol represents a certain type of food. The food on the "talam" must not exceed the amount on their trays. As the player cannot see their own tray, they must guess how much food is allowed on the "talam". If they suspect another player of placing too much food on the "talam", they may call out that player and announce that too much chicken has been placed and that they're wasting food. If the player correctly calls out the other player, they win the round, and the losing player gains a penalty point. If the player who calls out guesses wrongly, they are the one who is given the penalty point.

The game continues for 7 rounds until all penalty points have been given. The losing player is the one with most penalty points.

It is a cute quick game that involves Malaysian food and culture.

Taikun is a Malaysian card game about being tycoons and building a football team. Players are now the richest tycoons of the world's top football clubs. Players will now fight one another to get high-rating players to join their dream team.

All the cards have artwork of the best football players in the world currently. Tycoons must pick one footballer card from the top of the pile and place it face up. All tycoons must now place a bid on the footballer. The currency used must either be money, playing time, family or club; to bid for the footballer. The tycoon may bid using more than one currency if they have the investor special card. Naturally, the highest bidder gets to keep the footballer card. The winner of the game is the one with the most complete team members in all eleven positions.

You find that you’ve traversed into a dungeon of sorts and the entrance you came from is now blocked off. It is now your mission to escape this place before you turn into a husk of your former self, crumbling into a fine dust or becoming mummified like delicious jerky. If things couldn’t be worse, you also entered this area right before lunch. So it’s also important to find things that could fill your lunchbox. Maybe it’s a sandwich? Maybe it’s a small baggie of O-shaped cereal. Whatever you find, try your best to find it all and escape!

The Fennek expansion brings a new card into play for each player, giving them new options.

—description from the publisher

The expansion is contained in one version of the base game.

Quiltable: Fireplace Quilts is a cozy printable quilting game from the designers of Quiltable: Paper Quilts and Quiltable: The Card Game. Frosty snow drifts outside as you sit down next to a warm fireplace ready to quilt and share holiday stories. Join with family and friends to sew your quilt full of memories as the fire crackles in the background in Fireplace Quilts.

Throughout the game, sew your quilt of stories using quilt blocks leftover from Christmas. Grab blocks while they’re available and create great memories.

Each turn shift the value of available blocks. Use a unique lost stories mechanism to mitigate any die roll as much as you choose.

At the end of the game, the player with the most memorable quilt wins!

The game can be played with 1–5 players. The solo game comes with multiple levels of difficulty as you face off against Quilting Masters with increasing levels of skill.

How will you make the most memories as you create a Quiltable design?

—description from the publisher

In Quiltable: Festival of Quilts you have signed up to show your wares at the grand Festival of Quilts!

Upon arriving, however, you found that you had forgotten to bring materials for your quilt displays. Each turn, venture out into the Festival Grounds to purchase blocks, compile masterful quilts, and earn festival points to win.

Over 21 turns you will roll 2 dice, which all players will use and allocate for 2 actions per turn as they choose on their own personal player sheet.

Use die values to claim blocks, sew quilts, increase your skills, and earn the most Festival Points in order to win at the end of the game!

—description from the publisher

The players' goal is to take the letters to the post office from the storage station, but be careful, as the letters can get lost, and you may need to take a longer route. It is a dice-rolling step-moving game with a luck factor.

Az 1960-70-es években használt társasjáték kemény kartonból készült tartódoboz tetején bojtos postakürt ábrázolásban "A KIS POSTÁS" cím olvasható.

Mannerheim Cross is a 3rd party expansion to Advanced Squad Leader.

The focus of this pack is on Finish actions both offensive and defensive that occurred over the Course of World War 2.

A játéktáblán 4 háztömb, ezek között a közlekedő utak és a helyi Hivatal látható. A játék 2-6 résztvevő között játszható.

A játékosok kisorsolják, hogy ki kezdi a játékot. A kezdő játékos dob a dobókockával, és annyi mezőt lép előre, amennyi a dobás értéke.

A játék célja a levelek kiszállítása. A levéltábláról minden játékos húz egy levelet, amelyen a címzett pontos címe szerepel. A játékosoknak az adott háztömbön belül kell kiszállítaniuk a levelet. Ha egy játékos pontosan arra a mezőre lép, amely a címzett házának felel meg, a levelet kézbesítettnek tekintjük, és a játékos új levelet húzhat.

A játékot az nyeri, aki a legtöbb levelet kézbesíti. Ha a játék során egy játékos egy másik mezőre lép, amely nem a címzett háza, akkor tovább kell haladnia, amíg a megfelelő címet el nem éri.

A játék véget ér, ha az összes levelet sikeresen kézbesítették.


The game board consists of 4 blocks, with connecting roads and the local Post Office. The game can be played by 2 to 6 players.

Players determine who goes first by drawing lots. The starting player rolls the die and moves forward by as many spaces as the number rolled.

The goal of the game is to deliver letters. Each player draws a letter card, which contains the recipient's exact address. Players must deliver the letter within the designated block. If a player lands exactly on the space corresponding to the recipient’s house, the letter is considered delivered, and the player can draw a new letter.

The winner is the player who delivers the most letters. If a player lands on a space that is not the correct recipient’s house, they must continue moving until they reach the correct address.

The game ends when all letters have been successfully delivered.

Dice-based board game with movement mechanics.

Each non-Arena scenario now has one Stronger Enemy card you may use in place of one of its regular enemy cards. Each strong enemy has a slightly altered name and has some combination of improvements to make them more of a challenge.

This expansion contains new Event Cards, New Ally and Henchmen cards, a new Profession (Aoxcraft), new Item cards, and several new tokens to enhance your gaming experience.

This expansion contains two new Heroes: Garuk, a cursed boryot shapechanger and crymacner, and Xir, a lone xi-noq Soulblade or Sorcerer that has lost two of its triad.

A játéktábla bal alsó sarkában lévő „Holló”-tól indulnak a játékosok, és a nyíl irányában haladnak kockadobás szerint. Ha valaki piros négyzetre lép, ajánlott levelet kell kézbesítenie, ezért egyszer kimarad a dobásból.

Players start from the "Raven" in the bottom left corner of the game board and move in the direction of the arrows based on their dice rolls. If a player lands on a red square, they must deliver a registered letter and miss one turn.

If a player lands on a green square, they are delivering a package, so they get to roll the die twice, but must move in the opposite direction.
A player who lands on a red circle has lost their assigned mail and must return to the starting point.

The game is won by the first player to return to the "Raven." However, players can only enter this space with an exact roll; if they roll too high, they must move backward by the remaining steps.

description:
A step-by-step board game with numbered spaces from 1 to 71, illustrating the daily schedule of schoolchildren with the help of forest animals.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Lépegetős társasjáték, 1-71-ig számozott mezőkkel, az iskolás gyerekek napi időbeosztását
erdei állatok segítségével illusztrálva.

Space Junkers is a fast-paced sci-fi board game of sabotage, strategy, and sweet, sweet salvage. Set aboard the Deepspace Horizon —a lost freighter adrift for 57 years—  players take on the roles of rival Junkers scrambling to loot its three-tiered interior before the competition (or the ship itself) takes them out.

The game features a unique 3D modular board with three asymmetrical decks, dynamic player movement via elevators and EVA jetpacks, and a blend of action planning, dice selection, and tactical conflict. Every round is packed with tension as players race for high-value loot, avoid hazards, complete secret objectives, and sometimes eject their rivals straight into space. Each Junker has special abilities that shape their playstyle—whether it’s stealthy heists, brute-force ambushes, or calculated area control.

Bluff, blast, or bamboozle your way to victory—because in Space Junkers, the only thing worse than getting spaced is going home empty-handed.

boardgame: Distant Seas: An Expansion for Buccaneers of the Americas
description:
Distant Seas is the first major expansion for Buccaneers of the Americas, and REQUIRES OWNERSHIP OF THE ORIGINAL GAME. With this expansion, players are no longer strictly confined to the waters of the West Indies. Now, players may venture beyond into more distant seas: To the Atlantic coastline of North America; across the Isthmus of Darien (or by first traversing the treacherous Strait of Magellan) into the largely unguarded and unprepared Spanish South Sea; around and within the British Isles, France, the Iberian Peninsula, and the "German Sea" (North Sea) of Europe; off the shores of the Guinea coast, where private European companies load their human cargoes aboard their chartered ships; and by utilizing the "Pirate Round" route to enter the East Indies, where captains may attempt to ambush the Mughal treasure fleet!

Overall, DISTANT SEAS includes no less than five new region maps (East Indies, Europe, Guinea Coast, North America, South Sea); a world map; two new ships (Dhow, Brigantine) plus fully revised ship illustrations; a revised captain’s sheet; ship quick reference cards; two new nations (Kingdom of Portugal, Mughal Empire); new and revised counters; revised tables; and a supplementary rulebook which clarifies the new additions, changes, and world map travel procedures.

The Distant Seas expansion for Buccaneers of the Americas, REQUIRING OWNERSHIP OF THE ORIGINAL GAME, requires two ten-sided (d10) dice to play, and may be (but is not required) enhanced by taking notes and keeping a log on a separate sheet of paper, or digitally with any free word-like program, as your captain's story develops.

—description from the publisher

The road through Jianghu is long, and fateful encounters are inevitable.
In this game, players take on the roles of feline warriors escorting valuable cargo, traveling day and night while facing countless dangers and twists of fate. They must navigate perilous situations with skill and agility, for fortune favors the bold.

The first player to earn 12 gold ingots wins the game.

boardgame: In the Blink of a Dragon's Eye
description:
In the Blink of a Dragon's Eye is a heartwarming short story and solo minigame about a human and a dragon that become unlikely brothers in order to survive in their dangerous new reality. The story takes place in the Harbor of Blight universe, a new adventure that blends storytelling, visual art, roleplaying and tabletop gaming in a unique, elementally charged world. In the Blink of a Dragon's Eye is a prequel to the upcoming game, Harbor of Blight, and takes place approximately 5,000 years before the game's events. This is a tale of humans being brought unknowingly to the small, exotic planet of Blight and of how they cope with this dangerous new world. One human in particular, Nibaa, befriends and becomes brothers with one of the few remaining non-feral dragons left in the world, Niijii.

Each of the five chapters of the story has a solo minigame that lets you relive and play through the action from that chapter. Each minigame is one page with everything you need to play right on it. Each of the 5 minigames requires 2 d6 (6-sided dice) and a dry erase marker. The gameplay includes various aspects such as exploring a minimap, gathering and using items, fighting or escaping from a boss, and an Achievement for added difficulty and replayability!

—description from the publisher
