description:
Never Mind the Matchlocks is a complete set of pike and shot rules for gaming small battles in the English Civil War (and thereabouts!)

Although based on the popular Never Mind the Billhooks medieval rules, Matchlocks is a stand-alone game, published in a self-contained 32-page magazine style, full colour, booklet.


     Two armies of around 150 28mm figures are required for play, along with a handful of six-side dice, and tokens and cards - which can be downloaded.
     Players can use any basing system they prefer. The ability to remove individual casualties/figures is preferable, but casualty markers or dice can easily be used instead.
     For a standard infantry division of 12 figures a total frontage can be anywhere between 90 – 150mm. For cavalry squadrons of 8 figures a frontage of 160 – 200mm is ideal.
Using the classic 'tiddlywinks' mechanism, players attempt to be the first to squidge (propel) their winks into the 4-inch high netball baskets.

Chronicards History of the World is a French card game where each of the sixty nice and large cards explain one big humanity achievement from the invention of writing to the first foot on the moon. Everything revolves around the one date per card and each card have to be placed between the other cards while forming a long timescale. Your goal is really simple, put each of your card in place, one after another, one player after another and build a common timescale of the history of the world. Guess where your card goes and it's a card less that separate you from an empty hand and victory. And a misplaced card means drawing another card.

Jeu de carte sur la thématique de l'histoire du Monde où l'on classe les évènements depuis l'invention de l'écriture jusqu'au premier pas sur la lune sur une frise chronologique construite ensemble sur la table. Au fil des cartes posées les écarts de date se rapprochent et le but est de savoir où poser chronologiquement les cartes que l'on possède sur la frise de l'histoire du monde que construisent ensemble tous les joueurs. Le jeux comporte 60 cartes très belles, qui décrivent longuement la date et l'évènement en question pour enrichir la discussion tout en essayant d'être le premier à se débarrasser de toutes ses cartes.

A dieselpunk, cloud pirate tactical tabletop skirmish game playable in Solo/Coop for 3d printing in 12mm scale.

It is specially designed for 3D printing enthusiasts, painting lovers, and fans of immersive strategy games!

In Sector AL-IX, you take on the role of a sky pirate captain and his fleet. Aboard your makeshift anti-gravity ships, your goal is to amass as much loot as possible to maintain and expand your crew by completing various missions. Your adversary is the powerful "Compagnie du Nord", which will use all means to counter you. The "Compagnie du Nord" has unlimited resources... It's up to you to find the right strategy to evade them.

Civil is an abstract strategy game for 2 players. Lead your faction in out-maneuvering your rivals, influencing military operations in promoting your interests, and securing a future where your faction can prosper.

Civil asks players to manage the timing of multiple game layers to race to a winning score. Players use area majority influence to play mission tiles to block piece movement or to disrupt tile play sequences by their rival. Playing tiles allows for promoted pieces to race across the board, with arrival timing determining overall score. Finally, un-promoted pieces engage in mancala-like movement to shift area majority influence. Planning the timing of these game layers is made more difficult by alternating player lead each Round.

—description from the designer

Looks can be deceiving, and that’s doubly true when you don’t even have the complete picture. Carefully put together these abstract images to clue your teammates in or you’ll risk total confusion!

Create unique images to communicate your secret clue in this cooperative picture-making game. Take turns channeling your inner artist to complete all the clues and win!

Combine partial shapes and abstract images to portray a secret word. Failed guesses make later rounds harder!

—description from the publisher


Shaper is a party-style cooperative picture making game that uses just 9 cards.

Players take turns to combine the shapes on the cards to create a picture that represents a randomly chosen word.
If the other players correctly guess what word the picture represents then points are scored. If they guess wrongly then a card is discarded making future rounds more difficult.

Score enough points to win the game. If too many cards are discarded then you lose.

With more than 400 words and clues, and over 160 shapes, every game will shape up differently!

Shaper was a finalist in Button Shy's 18 card challenge 9 cards per game contest.

—description from the designer
family: Contests: Button Shy Game Design Contests and Challenges

description:
Keeper Chaos is a delightful zoo-themed board game designed for families and children ages 5-12. Perfect for 2 to 5 players, this colorful and beautifully illustrated game offers an exciting blend of strategy and luck that ensures hours of fun for everyone.

In Keeper Chaos, players step into the shoes of zookeepers, tasked with caring for various animals on their "Keepers Track" cards while navigating the unpredictable challenges that arise i As you work to collect your animal tokens, you’ll face unexpected events that can change the course of your game, adding an element of surprise and excitement.

This game is specifically crafted for those children who are ready to move beyond the simpler mechanics of classics like Candyland but might find more complex games like Catan a bit overwhelming. Keeper Chaos offers a perfect introduction to strategic gameplay. Its straightforward rules make it accessible for younger players, while the strategic depth keeps older players engaged, making it a true family favorite.

—description from the publisher

A delightful twist on a classic kids' game...

Cluck Cluck Moose! is a whimsically illustrated card game perfect for families and children ages 4 and up! Designed for 2-5 players, this fast-paced game promises quick, action-packed rounds that keep everyone engaged and laughing.
Why You'll Love It:

     Super Simple Setup: No complicated rules or long instructions—just open the box and let the fun begin!
     Quick Gameplay: Enjoy short, thrilling games that are perfect for quick rounds or extended play sessions.
     Family-Friendly: Whether you're playing with little ones, siblings, or grandparents, everyone can join in on the silly sounds and fast-paced action!


Objective: End the game with the fewest Moose Cards to win!

How to Play:

     Shuffle & Draw: Grab a card and hope for safe Chicken Cards—don’t forget to say "CLUCK!" when you draw one!
     Watch Out for Moose! : Draw a Moose Card? Yell "MOOSE!" and keep it in front of you!
     Use Your Wild Cards: Lucky Wild Cards let you ditch those pesky Moose Cards, while Pass It Cards allow you to pass your moose troubles to your friends!


The game continues until all cards are played, and the player with the fewest Moose Cards wins! If there's a tie, the champion is decided by the most Chicken Cards!

—description from the publisher

You knew that going it alone wouldn’t be easy...

Whether you're scraping together credits for a Pirate Lord Overdon, unwittingly conscripted into service for the Terran Alliance, or dashing from Base to Base for the desperate denizens of the Centaurian Federation, one thing is certain...

Being an Independent Shipper isn’t easy, but it's worth all the trouble the System can throw your lonely way!

With this Solo Expansion, choose from six thrilling scenarios. You'll look for new pick up and deliver Opportunities and also trade-in to buy New Ships with special abilities. But watch out! New Encounters may hinder your progress.

The Dog Star Shippers Lone Shipper Solo Expansion delivers loads of exciting possibilities to the solo experience!

—description from the publisher

A two to four player game about players competing to either keep Joe asleep all night or wake him up. All players play cards to manipulate a shared deck which represents the state of sleepiness of Joe, with the bottom of the deck being him waking up and the top him sleeping soundly all night.

Players take turns playing a card each turn out of action, permanent, and cancel cards.

The components are 57 card cards and one 'How to play' booklet.

It is a family-friendly game which can be enjoyed by young and old.

—description from the publisher

The board game is based on Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs. Who will be the first dwarf to stop the evil witch and help Snow White and the prince? It’s a classic roll-and-move board game, but be careful—if you don’t roll the right number on certain spaces, you’ll slide back!

A törpe birodalom társasjáték. Játékszabály a doboztetőben. Tartozékok: játékmező, fekete dobókocka, 10 db figura (királylány, királyfi, boszorkány és 7 db törpe csákánnyal, lámpással)." -
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Snow White

description:
A couple days after the Japanese surrender the Red Army and Navy unexpectedly and prematurely launched an amphibious occupation of little Shumshu Island at the top tip of the Kurile Islands ... unsupported by our US Navy ... as an apparent stepping stone to the rest of the Kuriles and possibly even Hokkaido of the Home Islands.  (Imagine a North Japan as well as North Korea.)
But the Japanese fought, and things did not go well with 5 of the 16 Landing Craft Infantry Large we had Lend-Leased the Russians lost, and some accounts claim the Russians losing 3 times as many men as the Japanese while the battle lasted - the reverse of our bloody Iwo Jima loss ratio.
The Japanese even had their toy store tanks which were very effective in the complete absence of any of the far superior Russian ones.
The Japanese also had air supremacy, thanks to their 4 antiquated Ki-43 Hayabusa fighters and 4 naval bombers.
A member has pointed out to me that Shumshu is in Pacific 1941-45 (2021), so I guess mine's not first, after all, but that is a much larger scale map - more hexes - down to company and lower level, so slower gameplay in the same AM/PM turns, plus 1 more day.
10 AM and PM turns and very simple I go you go game mechanics but with my Disruption system and another feature to show the qualitative difference between the Russian troops and the Japanese who were veterans of both Nomohan in Manchuria and even Guadalcanal!
And the wretched terrain we also have on the Aleutians and in many northern locales.
Nut-cracking Russian (101st Division) 4-factor regiment unit pieces are substitute pieces providing an extra combat factor and command control advantages enjoyed by the Japanese.
No "zones of control" except when retreating.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Quick Set is played in a series of up to three rounds in which players play cards from their hand to a team play pile in front of them one at a time to send the ball over the net.

Each touch made has constraints, shown on the bottom of the cards, on what touch be can played to follow. Once the ball is over the net, possession transfers to the other team. Play continues until one team is unable to send the ball over.

The last to successfully get the ball over the new scores the round.

—description from the publisher

A party game combining five methods of play. Guessing a ‘secret identity’ via 10 questions, getting your team to guess a word without using certain words, humming a tune, charades, and using drawings to give clues.

Walking down a dark dank hallway at your friend's party, you were told, "The Bathroom" is at the end of the hall. A menacing form appears from the shadows. Dash it all! You realize too late; you forgot to remove your “On Patrol” badge (your Super Pocket Protector). “Hay! You're On Patrol! Now you have to battle me! I am Doctor Devil. And you are?” Duty calls, so to speak, the toilet will have to wait. "I am "Super Who?", you declare. "I accept the challenge for I will ever do BATTLE to save New York City!" Doctor Devil looks at you strangely and asks, "Super Who?" Hands on hips and head thrown back, you announce, "Who Indeed!" bellowing a laugh, followed by the play of your first Action.

You are an adventuring Hero. Become a legend, as you defeat all who oppose you in epic battles to “Save New York City”! Use Super Moves to Bash, Jump & Throw, as many foes as possible and when all is over, gain your rightful recognition as the “Hero Extraordinaire.”!

Party Heroes:is an “Activity” for ages 8 to very Adult, you can add to most gatherings: parties, conventions, block parties (you name it).  The Goal: Players build a hero, then vie for most recognition (points) near the end of your gathering (1 hour or more) points are gained via Battles with other Heroes (guests).  The best of the best in the end "Battle Royal" for the  title "Hero Extraordinaire".

Every thing you need to get started & host "Party Heroes" is included (in pdf and png formats). 
Just print, play, battle and enjoy.

—description from the designer

There’s work to be done in the forest and, as usual, nobody wants to be the Warden. All the animals are hoping someone else will step up. A meeting has been called, with representatives from each of the five most influential species: Badgers, Deer, Foxes, Owls, and Rabbits. Each, in turn, refusing to be the Warden until only one is left. However, if an animal who refused to take charge winds up getting caught giving orders? Well, that won’t go well for them.

Must-follow trick taking. Trump each round is determined by players drafting what they do not want to be trump (which also becomes their pain suit). 5 suits, with each suit containing four 1s, three 2s, two 3s, and one 4. Players can play one or two cards of the same suit into a trick (4s must be played alone). The winner of the trick collects all the cards played into the trick and scores 1 point per card - UNLESS there are any of their pain suit in the trick, in which case they only collect the pain suit cards and score -1 point per card.

A 2 player (1v1) card game using a standard 52 deck.

Picture cards have special abilities, hands are won with highest scores (five rounds each game, highest total score wins after five rounds

Each player chooses a king, jack and queen of a single suit - the other two sets of J,Q,K are put to one side (each player chooses either a red or black suit)

The remaining 40 cards are shuffled to create the draw deck

Each round a player is given four cards from the draw deck - cards are worth point values and Aces are worth 11

After looking at their cards - each players says together "Jokers Boss (3 syllables)_ - after which they either their four cards face down in front of their three court cards, or on top of the joker

If they play on the joker then before the cards are revealed, that player can play one of their Jack Queen or King, after which it is flipped over.

King - Add 8 points to the players hand OR can be used to flip one of the other players court cards - this cannot be done to a court card just played by the opponent

Queen - Deducts 8 points from the opponents hand score OR if the opponent has an Ace in their hand the Ace can be reduced to 1 (this choice is with the player who plays the queen)

Jack - Adds 8 points to the players hand as with the King OR if the opponent either has a run of four numbers and/or 4 of the same suit - the opponent does not score this hand (even if they win) and loses all of their current score pile

If after special cards, you have the highest scoring hand, you score all the cards in your hand but NOT any J,Q,K

The opponent discards their losing hand

Scores are tallied after five rounds - any J/Q/K left unplayed is worth four points at the end of the game

Highest score wins, a tied score means players draw a card at random from their shuffled score pile and the highest draw wins (repeat until there is a winner)

The planet, once abundant with life, was destroyed by the greed of its human inhabitants, prompting nature to reclaim it through rising water levels that drowned all but the most adaptable creatures. However, an advanced alien species known as the Overseers, beings who transcend space and time, discovered the remnants of humanity and became fascinated by their irrational behaviors—traits they saw as perfect for entertainment. Using their technology, they combined human DNA with sea creatures and created Aquaball, the most thrilling game in the universe. Now, the planet, renamed Ballmionhoth, serves as a luxury resort for the Overseers, where humanoid sea creatures battle in Aquaball under the guidance of human coaches whose decisions add unpredictability. With survival tied to the game’s outcome, players and coaches must compete or face an unknown fate. As the next season begins, your role as a coach is crucial—lead your team to victory or discover what happens to those who refuse to play.

The game revolves around 3 rounds/tournaments. If you lose in the final one you lose the game. If you are not the one with the least points in the final, you win. By using many cards on the coach's hands and other attachable cards, you can add many layers of unpredictability. These all stem from the players themselves which you also buy. These players have four skills with specific interaction which decide the game. The currency system is a closed auction kind of market where everyone makes their bids and reveals at the same time for the cards and players. You must the setup your lineup and prepare for 1 of the three rounds. The first two rounds offer a very powerful card among other merits if they are won.

—description from the designer

Players place their pieces on the "Start" marked space and determine the order of play by drawing lots.

Players take turns rolling the dice, starting with the youngest player, and move forward the number of spaces indicated by their roll. If they land on a space already occupied by another piece, they knock it out. The knocked-out piece must restart from the starting mark of the corresponding latitude zone where it was eliminated.

A player can only advance to the next latitude zone if they roll exactly the number needed to land on the start-marked space of the next zone. Each player continues rolling until they advance to the next zone. When moving forward, players must avoid stepping back into previous zones, and if they land on an occupied space, they knock out the existing piece.

For example, if a player is ready to leave a latitude zone and needs to roll a two but rolls a five, they must move forward two spaces, then move three spaces back. If, during this backward movement, they land on an occupied space, their piece must return to the starting position of the previous latitude zone and restart movement from there.

The game is won by the first player to successfully reach the 90-degree-marked point with an exact roll after passing through all the latitude zones.


A játékosok bábúikkal ráállnak a „Start” jelzésű mezőre, majd sorshúzás útján megállapítják a dobási sorrendet.

Sorrendben dobnak a kockával a legkisebb korú játékos kezdve, annyi mezőt lépnek, amennyit dobtak. Ha olyan mezőre érnek, amelyen már másik bábu áll, azt kiütik. A kiütött bábu annak a körnek a rajtjelzéséről, amelyik körön kiütötték, újraindul.

A következő szélességi körbe csak akkor léphet a játékos, ha a megfelelővel a szélességi kört pontosan annyit dobott, amennyivel éppen a rajtzáró mezőre jut vissza. Minden játékos addig dob, míg az aktuális körén túl nem lép. Minden továbblépésnél meg kell előznie a mezők között visszafelé haladókat, és ilyenkor ki is ütheti őket, ha olyan mezőn kell beállnia, ahol bábu áll.

Például: Ha a játékos megfelelő valamelyik szélességi kört elhagyhat, tovább mehet, amennyiben kettőt kell dobnia, azonban ötöt dobott, akkor az újabb mezőkön hárommal hátra. Ha a hátrafelé tett harmadik lépésnél lépkedés közben már van bábu, akkor visszatér az előző szélességi kör rajta meg kell kezdeni újra a haladást.

A játékot az nyeri meg, aki az utolsó fokokkal jelölt körön is végighaladva, pontos dobással eljut az 90 fokkal megjelölt pontra.

SHIFTED is a fast-paced, highly interactive social deduction wallet game where players take on the roles of blue-collar space workers called GEWPERS, tasked with doing busy work around their ship. However, one of them is the SHIFT, a shape-shifting alien bent on converting all life into its kind, and another is the VOID, a nihilist who would rather see everything fail.

Each turn, a Job Coordinator must complete a task by playing a poker-style hand. However, each job requires help from other ship members to complete the hand. One must decide who is genuinely working toward success and who is secretly manipulating the mission for their own win condition.

The game ends when one of the three teams reaches their goal: GEWPERS win by successfully completing jobs, the SHIFT wins by converting other players, and the VOID claims victory when enough jobs have failed.

—description from the designer

Do you know where Pankow is? Or what a “Mieke” is? Yes? Then Ausgerechnet Pankow is just right for you. By placing location cards, you’ll learn about Berlin and Brandenburg in a playful way. Fun quiz rounds that test your knowledge of the region and its people promise variety throughout the game.

Wissen Sie, wo Pankow liegt? Oder was eine „Mieke“ ist? Ja? Dann liegen Sie bei Ausgerechnet Pankow sicher richtig. Durch Anlegen von Ortskarten lernen Sie spielerisch Berlin und Brandenburg kennen. Abwechslung im Spielverlauf versprechen die amüsanten Quizrunden, in denen Kenntnis über Land und Leute gefragt ist.

World Wonders: Europa Wonders Pack adds ten new wondrous monuments from Europa to World Wonders, along with a new gameplay mode.
This mode features 2 secret, own wonders at the beginning, which could only be built by you. With this different requirements all games get more variety.
The expansion also comes with cards to use the new wonders in the normal game mode.

The party game for creative thinkers: pantomime, descriptiom or maybe even drawing! The words to guess are visible to all – but which word has the active player picked? Who can tell, and who is the quickest to guess?

—description from the back of the box (translated)

Das Partyspiel für kreative Blitzmerker! Gestikulieren, umschreiben oder vielleicht sogar zeichnen! Die gesuchten Begriffe liegen offen aus – doch welchen Begriff hat sich der Mitspieler wohl ausgesucht? Wer weiß es und errät den Begriff am schnellsten?

—description from the back of the box

Legless in London is a unique board game set in a Victorian London where players navigate life by choosing a player character who must overcome challenges of mobility and personal goals. Players strive to achieve victory by accomplishing two out of three specific goals outlined on their Character Cards, such as finding love, acquiring property, or completing personal ambitions.

Throughout the game, players can purchase Artificial Legs, each offering unique enhancements. Artificial Legs influence gameplay through key attributes like Appeal (affecting social interactions and relationships), Mobility (determining movement range), and Efficiency (affecting work capabilities and earnings). Not all Artificial Legs are equally beneficial, and players must carefully consider their options, manage maintenance costs, and decide when to use or sell them strategically.
The game includes Location Cards that set diverse scenarios across various locations such as theatres, cottages, or slums, each offering different actions like working, finding love, buying property, or fulfilling personal objectives. Players encounter random Street Events that can impact their health, finances, or opportunities in unpredictable ways, adding elements of risk and chance to every turn.

Each turn, players can choose either to move around the city—potentially encountering random Street Events—or perform an action at their current location, such as working, finding love, or purchasing Artificial Legs. Successful completion of actions awards players Victory Tokens that mark progress toward their goals.

With mechanics emphasising resource management, strategic decision-making, adaptability, and a nuanced narrative around living with a disability, Legless in London provides a thoughtful, engaging, and socially aware gaming experience.

—description from the publisher

Heckle Havoc is an improv-driven party game where one player tells a story while others interrupt with Heckle Cards to twist the narrative in unexpected directions. Heckles like "Not That!" and "Why?" force the storyteller to adapt on the fly, making each round unpredictable and chaotic. Players earn points for seamlessly incorporating heckles and weaving in their hidden objective before the story ends. With a mix of storytelling, hand management, and quick thinking, Heckle Havoc is a high-energy game of creative chaos.

Exquisite Corpse: Monster Rummy is set-building card game similar to the classic card game Rummy. But instead of building sets of numbers, you build MONSTERS.

You are rival mad scientists obsessed with world domination. You must create the most fearsome monster army possible and use it to destroy your enemies. Grow new monster parts in the lab and combine them with old monster parts dig out of the graveyard. Completed monsters meet on the battlefield to fight for domination!

—description from the designer

In Arkitects, players take on the role of Noah’s family, strategically arranging animals, fences, and hazards on their Ark while ensuring balance and avoiding conflicts. With limited space and ever-changing card flips, players must carefully plan their placements to complete objectives and maximize their score before the flood arrives.

—description from the designer

Don’t Spell PIG is a quick and strategic word game where players take turns placing letter tiles on a 4x4 grid. The goal? Force your opponent to be the one to spell "PIG." It’s simple to learn but full of clever tactics, making every match a battle of wits.

On their turn, a player places one of their letter tiles (P, I, or G) onto the board, trying to create traps that leave their opponent with no choice but to complete the word "PIG." However, careful planning is needed, as every placement could set up future moves. To keep things balanced, no letter repeats in the set, ensuring a fresh and challenging game each time.

The game ends when a player is forced to spell "PIG" or when the board is completely filled. If no one has spelled "PIG" by the end, it’s a draw! With its compact design and easy-to-learn rules, Don’t Spell PIG is a fun, family-friendly game that’s perfect for game nights, travel, or a quick challenge anytime.

—description from the designer

A mysterious wanderer has arrived in Magoria, and with him, the rules of illusion are changing. The Arcanist’s power grants new ways to perform, with supernatural workers aiding magicians and Enchantments transforming tricks into something far beyond mere sleight of hand. Arcane Shards provide an untapped source of power, while the Arcanist’s shifting influence brings dynamic challenges each round. Those who dare to embrace the unknown will rise above the rest.

Trickerion: Arcane Arts expands Trickerion: Legends of Illusion with new worker types, new ways to prepare Tricks, and a twist to the game's Action economy through Arcane Shards. The Arcanist, a wandering master of forgotten arts, moves between the Downtown, The Market Row, and the Dark Alley, and wherever he stops, players gain access to a set of  Arcane Actions. Through the Arcanist, magicians can now recruit four new Supernatural Workers - the Specter, Golem, Trickster, and Shapeshifter - each with unique abilities. These workers follow special assignment rules, making them efficient but requiring careful planning.

The expansion also introduces Enchantments, a new mechanic that allows players to modify their tricks permanently. By spending Enchantments instead of owning certain components, players can enhance their performances with lasting magical effects. Each Enchantment grants a bonus whenever its trick is performed. Additionally, players can now acquire Arcane Shards, an advanced resource that functions similarly to Trickerion Shards but offers increased action efficiency, even in the Theater.

With its' new action economy, customizable tricks, and powerful supernatural allies, Trickerion: Arcane Arts offers a fresh, dynamic experience while seamlessly integrating with the base game and its expansions.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The World Council: The Future Council Expansion Pack
description: Included 3 new packages, Black Technology, Alien Attack and Leaders Card (Basic Game Upgrade) . A total of 64 cards with an amazing transparent card design, new actions and game modes, bring you to the whole new level of The World Council.

boardgame: Clue Junior: Marvel Avengers – Loki's Big Trick!
description: Find out Loki's big plan to fool the Avengers in this Marvel themed version of the Clue Jr. series. You will need to find out what Loki stole, who he impersonated, and when he escaped to win.
family: Game: Cluedo Junior / Clue Junior (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
As the owner of a successful Independent Car Dealership, you’ve always wanted to push your business to the next level!  That’s why when you learned that THE most successful Dealer across the whole city was looking for a partnership in order to expand to the regional market, you knew this was THE opportunity!

Thankfully you have an insider, and your source is telling you that in order to get the partnership, one would need to have the best Consumer Service Index possible.  Of course, selling lots of cars would also help!

So much to do, and so little time!  You will need to improve your team; recruit and train them into a 5 star making machine if  you want to even stand a chance.  Time to work on building that Customer Service team, and get as many satisfied clients as possible if you want to secure the lucrative partnership!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Conquering Hell: The Battle of Montfaucon, September 26-28, 1918
description:
“I deemed an attack on Montfaucon without any artillery preparation and after dark when we could not see the barbed wire entanglements, against a position held by a large number of machine guns and heavy guns whose fire could be brought to bear on it, would be extremely inadvisable.” –Reaction by Colonel Claude B. Sweezy, commander of the US 79th Division’s 313th Infantry Regiment, on receiving orders for his regiment’s attack on September 27th.

Conquering Hell is a moderate complexity level game of the battle for the heights of Montfaucon during the Meuse-Argonne Offensive. The heights at Montfaucon dominated this sector of the German defenses, and taking them were crucial to the American Meuse-Argonne offensive that sought to breakthrough once and for the enemy’s Hindenburg Line. The US artillery conducted a three hour saturation bombardment that exceeded in ordinance weight all of that fired by both sides in the American’s Civil War, and cost over $180 million (over $1 million a minute). Much was at stake militarily and politically for this American-led offensive, as the war’s end was increasingly apparent for all of the combatants, and how any peace negotiations would be conducted, as well as who would be best suited to set the terms of any peace treaty, would in many ways be measured in the blood and treasure spent on this battlefield. Can you do as well or better than your historical counterpart?

Conquering Hell uses the same design as The Yanks Are Here! The Saint Miheil Campaign. Infantry units are Regiments, tank units are battalions, a hex is a third of a mile across and day represents six hours of time. The game is easy to learn/teach and true to the history. Like The Yanks Are Here! the Fighting Eagles game can also be linked so Conquering Hell so the air battles fought in Fighting Eagles have context and effects on the Montfaucon battlefield.

—description from the designer
A promo card for Passengers.
A promo card for Passengers

boardgame: One Card Dungeon Adventure Pack: Harbour Clash
description:
From unfathomable depths, an ancient power stirs. The dark abyss, home to forgotten creatures, opens wide, releasing a threat that rises with the tide. The waves carry a chilling omen of destruction.  Only those brave enough to face the unknown can push back the darkness and save the harbor from impending ruin.

One Card Dungeon: Harbour Clash is a standalone adventure that follows the same setup and rules as the base game and the M’Guf-yn Returns expansion, with new features: a new Dungeon card, a new class, and the new Item module!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Card Dungeon Adventure Pack: Eternal Peak
description:
In the highest peaks, where the winds howl and the snow never melts, the White Sorcerer has unleashed an ancient storm. His icy magic corrupts everything it touches, turning the world into a frozen wasteland of despair. Only those who dare to brave the cutting winds and pass through the blizzard can break his grip and restore light to the darkened lands. But beware: the White Sorcerer’s power grows with every gust that rises.

One Card Dungeon: Eternal Peak is a standalone adventure that follows the same setup and rules as the base game and the M’Guf-yn Returns expansion, with some unique new features: a new Dungeon card, a new class, and the new Item module!

Work together to build one tower, but play smart to be the first to place all your blocks! Draw action cards to add or remove blocks and hope to pull the wrecking ball to bring the tower crashing down!
Exciting, strategic, and made from sustainable  wood.
From the back of the box.

All blocks are evenly divided among the players. In the middle of table there's a stand with a wrecking ball and a deck of cards.During a players turn, that players turns a card face up. There are 3 types of cards:

     + and a number, place as many blocks on the stand as the number indicates.
     - and a number, remove as many blocks from the stand as the number indicates.
     wrecking ball. Pull and release the wrecking ball against the stand. The player whose turn it is distributes the fallen blocks among the other players.


The first player to place all of his blocks is the winner

The famous Blue Ice Diamond, one of the world's largest diamonds, has been stolen from a high security bank vault. The diamond seems to have vanished into this air, and the police are out of clues.

Players must examine all the evidence and look for clues in newspapers, witness statements, autopsy reports, phone records, maps, photos, surveillance videos, websites and more. Players must find suspicious details in the evidence and reveal the killer.

Ashlands is a chaotic strategy card game set in a dark fantasy world, where players take on the roles of leaders of different reigns fighting for dominance in a post-apocalyptic medieval world where Dragons reign.
The game focuses on resource management, tactical negotiation, and strategic decision-making, with each faction having unique abilities and playstyles.

How is Ashlands played?

     Players build their reign and manage resources to recruit units, cast events, and settle their resources.
     The game is played in turn-based rounds, where players take actions such as gathering resources, deploying units, and attacking opponents.
     Each unit has special abilities that can change the flow of battle.
     Events and environmental hazards add unpredictability, forcing players to adapt their strategies.


How Do You Win?

Victory is achieved by being the last reign on the board.

—description from the designer

Koko Mploko is a fast-paced word association card game. Players take turns drawing cards with categories. If two players have matching symbols, they must quickly say a word related to the other’s category before their opponent does. The faster player wins the round, takes the card as a win point, and the game continues until the deck is exhausted. Whoever has the most points at the end is the winner. It encourages quick thinking, vocabulary skills, and reflexes, making it a fun party game.

Outfox is the game that combines strategic thinking with concentration skills.

Outfox is a strategic and ever-evolving board game that challenges players to outthink and outmaneuver their opponents through cunning tactics and adaptable gameplay. Designed to level the playing field with dynamic board configurations and shifting mechanics, Outfox ensures that no player has an advantage from prior experience—every game is a fresh challenge.

Set in a world of wits and strategy, players take on the role of clever tacticians, carefully positioning their pieces to control the board and outfox their rivals. The objective is to anticipate moves, adapt to changing patterns, and ultimately outmaneuver opponents to secure victory. Unlike traditional abstract strategy games, Outfox introduces a dynamic element where no two games play the same, making foresight and flexibility essential to success.

Outfox is designed to grow with its players, offering depth for those who seek mastery while remaining accessible to new challengers. Whether you're planning your next move or reacting to a shifting board, every decision in Outfox counts—ensuring a game of skill, strategy, and endless possibilities.

High Frontier 4 All Module 6 -Odyssey (former names Expansion 1 and also Careers) adds your individual presence into the High Frontier universe, as shown by 2 explorer tokens of your color. Your first explorer shows your location in space, and your second explorer shows your status (overall career progress). The career paths placard is common among players, and adds job competition among your fellow explorers. Odyssey also adds exploration, so that Sites can be surveyed by adding exploration hexes that reveal features such as regolith and volcanoes. A difficulty level listed on these features is the minimum status to prospect them, replacing the size roll found in Core. You also need a particular surveyor to prospect, for instance a submersible is needed to prospect a sub-ocean. The status levels are C, B, A, and Odyssey ends one round of turns after someone reaches the highest status A. You win Odyssey by reaching the highest status (ties broken by VP), which replaces the VP victory conditions found in Core and other Modules.

>Number of Players: 1 to 6.

>Modules: Can be combined with any Module in the High Frontier 4 All universe, but requires Modules 1 (Terawatt & Futures) and 2 (Colonization).

—description from the publisher
A real-time dexterity game where up to three players compete to launch a motorized rotating Jett at the right time to release their packages. First player to release three packages wins.

Odysseus is a solo roguelike survival board game, where the player plays Odysseus and tries to reach Ithaca. Like all roguelike games, the journey is brutal, and players will not succeed at first, with defeat being a certainty. But each defeat brings along with it experience, knowledge, and power that carries over to the next game in the form of meta progression, i.e. persisting upgrades. Thus each time players will manage to go a little bit further, until finally they get strong enough to reach Ithaca and reclaim their kingdom from the villainous suitors.

'To escape captivity, Odysseus and his comrades blinded the Cyclops Polyphemus, a blasphemous act that incurred the wrath of Polyphemus’ father, the god Poseidon. The Olympian god decided not to outright smite Odysseus… no, a different punishment was more fitting. He cursed the mortal, whenever the sea would claim him, not to find death and the way to Hades, rather to be washed ashore, on the sandy beaches of Troy, on the eve of his departure for the journey back home, to the island of Ithaca. 
But, maybe it was the will of the Fates, for Odysseus to have in his possession the magical bag of Aeolus, which used to contain unfavorable winds. When Odysseus’ ship sunk, and Poseidon’s curse took effect, indeed he woke up on the shores of Troy, but thanks to the magic of Aeolus’ bag, he carried with him all memories, recollections and experiences thus far. The hero decided he would one day break Poseidon’s curse and finally return home'

—description from the designer

Featuring classic gameplay and gameboard, this game contains 330 questions in 6 categories: Universifacts, Who's who, Science and Technology, Biomedical Sciences, Spiritual sciences, Behavioural and societal sciences. Aaaah! It is full of bugs!!! 1,2,3,4…BAM!! On your marks? Ready? Go!

Anyone who finds insects and other creepy crawlies disgusting will overcome their fear thanks to this card game because in this game they can swat them to their heart's content. If they say the same number and also turn it over on the cards. And as an adult you just want to join in.

Children learn to count in a playful way (both counting and counting back) but have to pay close attention. It requires some extra concentration but that is exactly what makes the game so much fun.

The rules of the game are printed in the game box so that you will not easily lose them.

Let's Go to The Moon game is a rather obscure space game from the late 50's.

The game consists of a board that shows a path of 17 squares from a rocket launch site to the moon. Players choose a colored wooden playing piece and spin a rocket shaped spinner to determine number of spaces to move along the path. If a player lands on 4 or 6 they go into a satellite orbit, delaying progress.

The first to reach the moon is the winner.

The game was registered by Alfonsas Burchikas on the 4th Sept 1958 cited in the Catalog of Copyright Entries, Third Series. Part 1: Books and Pamphlets Jul-Dec 1958: Vol 12 No 2. pg. 250. Library of Congress, Copyright Office.

—user summary

The Svalehale (Old World Swallowtail/Papilio machaon), is the first promo for Biotopia, published in Danish and a limited quantity.

The Svalehave starts in exile (Next to the Missions), and can only be played from there. As usual it takes an action to play the card, but you do not have to pick up the card before playing it. There is only one Svalehale in play, so once played you can't lose it.

The Svalehale is an endangered butterfly.

The Svalehale scores X amounts of points, where X is equal to the number of missions you have.

Simino is a tile-laying strategy game for 1 to 4 players. The game challenges players to place domino-like tiles with two colored halves onto a shared board, aiming to strategically position their pieces while disrupting opponents. The goal is to be the first to get rid of all personal tiles, which earns points based on the remaining tiles in opponents' hands. The game includes both competitive and cooperative modes, as well as a solo variant.

During a turn, a player places a tile onto the board, ensuring that at least one color matches an adjacent tile. Tiles can be stacked up to three levels high, introducing penalties for opponents when stacking occurs. Special rules apply to doubles (tiles with identical colors), which can trigger chain placements and additional penalties. The game ends when only one player has tiles left or when all players consecutively pass due to lack of valid moves. Points are calculated based on remaining tiles, and the game continues over multiple rounds until a player reaches 17 points or after 7 rounds.

Additional game modes include team play, a solo puzzle-style variant, and special rule sets where white and black colors modify stacking conditions. These variants add extra layers of strategy and replayability to the game.

boardgame: The Adventures of Conan: Nemedian Chronicles Expansion

Performances can earn you money, but ultimately, you are in it for Fame: the more famous you are, the better Tricks you can learn, and the Magician with the most Fame points will win the game.

To celebrate the 10th anniversary of the first Kickstarter campaign, Mindclash Games is releasing a special Anniversary Edition of Trickerion. This edition includes:

Collect at least 5 "people of Ammon" cards and the "land of Jershon" card and you win. But watch out! The Lamanite army is ready to spoil your fun. With multiple "action" and "play now" cards, this game is always changing.

As St. Nicholas’s companions, players compete to see who can capture (or sacrifice) the most, and the naughtiest children. Characters use their strengths, upgrades, and action cards to help them capture the worst kids in town. The worse their behavior, the more points they are worth. Whoever has the most points when either the town is filled with nice kids, or when a Kid would need to be replaced, but there are none left in the deck, is the winner.

Pick a companion, receive their card and accessories. Shuffle the "kids" deck and place 6 upright to create the "Town," placing any nice kids to the side until the Town is full of naughty kids. Shuffle the Actions deck and deal three to each player.

Each turn is a Draw Phase, Play Cards Phase (as many as you can), Capture Phase to get kids in your sack, and watch out for St. Nicholas!!!

In Battle Pages you will protect your kingdom from a wave of enemies, using runs of dice to mine gems and tactically place units throughout the map. If you can survive 6 waves and the boss, you win!

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Commander & Padawan Ahsoka Tano Expansion

The first player to build a continuous barrier connecting two edges of the same color on their board wins!

Two levels of play: without a die for a simpler game and with a die for more strategy.

boardgame: Don't Look Back: Sweet Tooth – VHS Expansion
description:

Everyone in this surreal apartment building loves rumors and especially loves gossiping about them with their neighbors.
Your goal is to come up with the best background story for a given rumor that is spreading around town and get crowned as the Rumor Freak.

How to play:

     Every player chooses a character and takes the corresponding Character Card in front of them, along with a marker.
     A random player starts the game by saying, "Rumor has it that..." and draws one card from each deck, placing each card faceup in the following order to complete a sentence: WHO > DID WHAT > WHO > SPICY DETAIL. This player is now the active player.
     Start the sand timer. All players (including the active player) have 1 minute to come up with a scenario and a background story for the rumor (why it happened, how things ended up that way, secret motives, or whatever you like). Be as bizarre and disturbing as you can be. Keep notes on your Dry-Erase Card so that you don't forget.
     Once the sand timer is up, each player unravels their story, starting with the active player and moving clockwise. Try to be as convincing as you can and persuade everyone you have the real story.
     Then, every player votes for the best story by writing their favorite character name on their card. And no, you cannot vote for yourself.
     When everyone is ready, reveal your votes. Every player gets as many points as the votes he/she has received. If someone is voted by all, he/she gets one extra point.
     Circle the number of points you received on the numberline of your Character Card and update it after every round to keep track of your total points.
     With each new round, someone new becomes the active player.
     The game ends when all players have been the active player once (for 5-6 players) or twice (for 3-4 players).
     The player with the most points is deemed the absolute


Rumor Freak of the Town.

—description from the publisher

description:
Summer is here and the minivan is packed and ready for a good old fashioned family road trip!  Roll the dice and plot a cross-country course to popular cities and regional landmarks.  Will you take the scenic route through the mountains or detour to visit your friends and family along the way?  It's time to buckle up and make some memories!

Are We There Yet? is a roll-and-write game for 1 or more players who are competing to take the most memorable family road trip.

Each player begins the game with a personal map sheet.  Each round, players simultaneously draw line segments on their maps based on the values rolled on the dice to zig-zag their way around the country.  After drawing each line segment, players update their travel logs based on the locations they have visited.  These locations include cities, landmarks, mountains, houses, and rest stops--each of which scores in a different way.  After 2 weeks on the road (14 rounds), players review their travel logs, and the player with the highest score wins!

—description from the publisher

Quarry is a strategy game for two. Win by being the first player to build 3 full towers. Towers are built up of tower pieces played during each turn. Each player has their own colour and style of tower pieces. Players place, move and replace their pieces on the hex grid board in order to get into positions that help them build their towers, to win the game.

—description from the designer

Mystery Tricks is a cooperative trick-taking game for 3-5 players. Players play with their hands face down having only knowledge of the suit of the cards in their hand.

Through trick play, players must find out a suspect, a crime, a monster, and the gang member who is solving the mystery. If one of each of these cards is left in each player's hands at the end of the round, players get the chance to guess the card in their hand. If everyone guesses correctly, the players win!

—description from the publisher

The vastness of space holds a secret. Amidst the asteroids, strange mound-shaped bases have been erected, housing crowds of unusual inhabitants armed to the...antennae. The calm before the storm is shattered by the sudden blast of a plasma cannon, fired by a brave ant. This is the signal for another battle to begin! From the anthills, teams of ants equipped with grenades and laser guns set out, ready for anything to capture strategic flags. The war has raged for so long that no one remembers who started it or why. Only one thing matters — who will emerge victorious?

ANTgravity is a dynamic tactical game in which cosmic ants battle each other for control over the flags, while also struggling against...unusual gravity. Manage your ants, use futuristic technology, destroy asteroids, acquire new weapons, and most importantly, outsmart your opponents! Join the epic clash to see whether you can outwit your rivals and master the gravity to claim victory.

In more detail, each player commands their own team of ants, utilizing their unique abilities and advanced space technology. The main objective of the game is to capture flags scattered across the board and deliver them to your base. Deliver a set number of flags or secure more than others when time runs out, and you win.

In each round, participants take turns activating one or two ants. Each ant can both move and use a weapon or equipment card to attack enemy ants in the line of fire or destroy asteroids. During movement, players must consider gravity rules, which may cause an ant to land in an unexpected place. In space, any ant that moves or gets hit is subject to gravitational pull in the direction indicated by the arrow on its token until it encounters an obstacle where it can land: an asteroid, another ant, or the cosmic void...which is not a location from which ants return.

An ant that picks up a flag can begin its journey back to the base. By eliminating enemy ants and destroying asteroids, players earn energy tokens that can be used to play additional cards, creating unique tactical combos.

—description from the publisher

This game is GIRL POWERED. We wanted a game for girls that was actually something girls would want to play. That's why this game isn't just made for girls, it's made by girls.

Be the first player to get 10 points and at least 2 cards from each category while having fun with your girls (or boys, or NBs - honestly, anyone can play) and sharing all of your deepest, naughtiest secrets.

How to play:

     On your turn, choose 1 card from the row of 3 cards that are in play — read it silently, in case there is anything that needs to be kept secret! (Each card has a point value in the top-right corner.)
     Then, unless it says otherwise, read the card aloud and do what it says. Tell your truth, do your dare, or get the whole squad involved!
         TRUTH: You must answer the question honestly to keep the card.
         DARE: You must fully complete the dare to keep the card.
         SQUAD: All players must participate — each card will tell you how to determine who keeps the card.
    


Winning the game:
The first player to get to 10 points with at least 2 cards from each category wins the game and the title of the Ultimate Girl's Girl!

—description from the publisher

Chronicards World Wonders is a French card game where each of the sixty nice and large cards shows one of sixty Wonders of the World all along human history. Everything revolves around that one date per card and each card have to be placed between the cards already on the table while forming a long timescale. Your goal is really simple, put each of your card in place, one after another, one player after another and build a common timescale of the history of the world wonders. Guess where your card goes and it's a card less that separate you from an empty hand and victory. And a misplaced card means drawing another card.

Jeu de carte sur la thématique des merveilles du monde où l'on classe ces merveilles sur une frise chronologique construite entre tous les joueurs autour de la table. Au fil des cartes posées les écarts entre les dates se rapprochent et le but est de savoir où poser chronologiquement les cartes que l'on possède au milieu des autres cartes. Le jeux comporte 60 cartes très belles, qui décrivent longuement la date et l'évènement en question pour enrichir la discussion tout en essayant d'être le premier à se débarrasser de toutes ses cartes.

Frederick II, Holy Roman Emperor and King of Germany and Italy, was a man of extraordinary culture, energy, and ability. His contemporaries called him "stupor mundi" ("the wonder of the world"), and many historians consider him Europe's first modern ruler. The kingdom Frederick established in Sicily and southern Italy ran on an efficient bureaucracy similar to modern, centrally governed kingdoms.

As one of Frederick II's vassals in Stupor Mundi, you will summon allies from around the Mediterranean, build structures in your castle, and promote specialists to improve your kingdom. You may improve your chances by upgrading your castles: towers house additional allies, walls store more resources, and keeps aid in managing action cards. Through a clever card-selection mechanism, you will have to choose actions carefully, some of which will trigger edicts that affect Frederick's palace, and determine the rewards players may receive from their allies. Do you stay loyal to the Emperor, or do you support independent allies? Keep a close eye on what your opponents do and form alliances at the right time — or go your own way when things get too heated...

The Master print edition of Stupod Mundi is a special, limited edition that contains:


     Deluxe components, which depending on the edition, are either chunky wood or PVC minis of the Castle pieces, Specialists and ships
     A special Master print box, with unique artwork by Maciej Janik
     The Miracula expansion, which contains two modules that will expand your Stupor Mundi gaming experience. 
     A special insert designed by Black Magic Inserts, including individual player trays, a resource tray and room for all components of the game, including the expansions.

Chronicards French Monuments is a French card game where each of the sixty nice and large cards shows one of sixty French Monuments all around the country. Everything revolves around that one date per card and each card have to be placed between the cards already on the table while forming a long timescale. Your goal is really simple, put each of your card in place, one after another, one player after another and build a common timescale of all the cards. Guess where your card goes and it's a card less that separate you from an empty hand and victory. And a misplaced card means drawing another card.

Jeu de carte sur la thématique des Monuments de France où l'on classe ses cartes sur une frise chronologique construite entre tous les joueurs autour de la table. Au fil des cartes posées les écarts entre les dates des cartes se rapprochent et le but est de savoir où poser chronologiquement les cartes que l'on possède au milieu des autres cartes. Le jeux comporte 60 cartes très belles, qui décrivent longuement la date et l'évènement en question pour enrichir la discussion tout en essayant d'être le premier à se débarrasser de toutes ses cartes.

Chronicards Art history is a French card game where each of the sixty nice and large cards shows one of sixty great work of art in the whole human history. Everything revolves around that one date per card and each card have to be placed between the cards already on the table while forming a long timescale. Your goal is really simple, put each of your card in place, one after another, one player after another and build a common timescale of all the cards. Guess where your card goes and it's a card less that separate you from an empty hand and victory. And a misplaced card means drawing another card.

Jeu de carte sur la thématique des l'histoire de l'art où l'on classe ses cartes sur une frise chronologique construite entre tous les joueurs autour de la table. Au fil des cartes posées les écarts entre les dates des cartes se rapprochent et le but est de savoir où poser chronologiquement les cartes que l'on possède au milieu des autres cartes. Le jeux comporte 60 cartes très belles, qui décrivent longuement la date et l'oeuvre en question pour enrichir la discussion tout en essayant d'être le premier à se débarrasser de toutes ses cartes.

boardgame: Saddle Bum: Cinematic Wild West Miniature Rules
description:
Celebrating the 10th anniversary of my Shootin Iron miniature game, I decided to revise and rename the original 1st edition of the game.  Upon rereading, I actually think the 1st edition was a more unique...and possibly better, game.  I hope you enjoy reading and playing it as much as I do.

Revisiting and replaying this has been a joy, and reminded me of the initial fast play and easy to learn goal of the first edition.

—description from the designer

Is it the smell of jet fuel or the thrill of the mission electrifying the tarmac? Bombs and missiles are loaded under the wings of fighters ready for combat. Pilots check their navigation and targeting systems while engines roar all around. With a nod, the mission leader launches the first strike package.

Intelligence reports a deadly wall of enemy air defenses, backed by agile interceptors. Meanwhile, enemy strike aircraft keep pounding ground forces. It’s time to turn the tide. It’s time to launch a COMAO — a Combined Air Operations mission!

In COMAO, you take command of complex air operations in a tense tactical showdown. The high command assigns you objectives that must be neutralized by the air assets under your operational control — fighter jets, bombers, helicopters, drones, satellites, and even ground or naval systems (available in expansions).

Each target destroyed earns Victory Points. The first side to reach 10 points wins the game and seizes air superiority!

We've worked hard to model, simplify, and streamline the complexity of modern air warfare to create an accessible and immersive experience. Like a real cockpit, the game is designed to ease your cognitive load, letting you focus on making the right decisions.

Fueled by discipline, countless test missions, and a deep passion for military aviation and wargaming, COMAO offers players — aviators and enthusiasts alike — a glimpse into the demanding world of air operations, where every resource is powerful, precious, and never unlimited.

Take the lead. Command the skies. Deploy your air power.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dune: Imperium – Pivotal Gambit Promo Card
description:
According to Dire Wolf's X, this card will be given to competitors at the 2025 World Series of Board Gaming Dune Imperium Uprising tournament.

Agent ability: Trash this card to gain a troop, AND Add  to the first place reward for this conflict.

Reveal ability: Gain 1 Persuasion and 2 battle points

This card is shuffled into the Imperium Deck at setup.

boardgame: Tales of Nine: La Montagne Noire
description:
Tales of Nine is a narrative puzzle game for 1 player only.

Explore places, fight monsters, collect treasure and experience an Adventure in 3 Chapters.

La Montagne Noire (The Black Moutain) -
Countless years have passed since the Great Black Dragon seized the legendary dwarf city of Dugrimmar... But today, you, Paladin of the Kingdom, have decided to defy him and take the Sceptre of the Ancients, to offer your people a glorious future!

The game turn is divided into 3 phases:


     The Hero phase, in which the player moves the Hero card to another card and pays the latter's entry cost. Then retrieve the card from his "backpack".
     The Monster phase, in which any monsters that can, move closer to the Hero.
     The Adventure phase, when the player completes the game zone.


You'll need to anticipate your moves and apply the various card effects to reach your goal and find your way out.

The player win when the "Exit" card get to the "backpack".

An “Infinite” mode is also available, allowing players to play one game after another, trying to achieve the highest score.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tales Of Nine: La Forêt Maudite
description:
Tales of Nine is a narrative puzzle game for 1 player only.

Explore places, fight monsters, collect treasure and experience an Adventure in 3 chapters.

La Forêt Maudite (The Cursed Forest)
Back from your journey of initiation, you don't recognize the forest of your childhood. What happened during your long absence? Fortunately, you're now a seasoned druid and can't wait to reach the heart of the forest to unravel the mystery...

The game turn is divided into 3 phases:


     The Hero phase, in which the player moves the Hero card to another card and pays the latter's entry cost. Then retrieve the card from his "backpack".
     The Monster phase, in which any monsters that can, move closer to the Hero.
     The Adventure phase, when the player completes the game zone.


You'll need to anticipate your moves and apply the various card effects to reach your goal and find your way out.

The player win when the "Exit" card get to the "backpack".

An “Infinite” mode is also available, allowing players to play one game after another, trying to achieve the highest score.

—description from the publisher

Director’s Cut Eros adds a new miniature to the game depicting Jennifer transformed into a Maniac! It also adds a new mechanic: Events. These cards are drawn each turn and have immediate effects on all Survivors, the Maniac, or on individual Survivors in play! The box also contains devastating new Maniac cards dedicated to female characters.

Director’s Cut Thanatos adds a new miniature to the game depicting the Maniac with an angry face! It also adds a new mechanic: Nightmares. These cards are drawn each time you suffer a Fright and can have effects on the Survivor involved, but also on other players! The box also contains devastating new Maniac cards dedicated to male characters.

boardgame: Tales of Nine: Le Désert Infini
description:
Tales of Nine is a narrative puzzle game for 1 player only.
Explore palces, fight monsters, collect treasure and experience an Adventure in 3 Chapters.

Le Désert Infini (The Infinite Desert) - 
You've arrived at the place indicated on the map provided by the Order of Merchants. But there's no camp here, only an endless expanse of golden sand. Your mission couldn't be clearer: find the Scarlet Dagger's lair and assassinate their leader. It's the only way to put an end to the caravan attacks.

The game turn is divided into 3 phases:


     The Hero phase, in which the player moves the Hero card to another card and pays the latter's entry cost. Then retrieve the card from his "backpack".
     The Monster phase, in which any monsters that can, move closer to the Hero.
     The Adventure phase, when the player completes the game zone.


You'll need to anticipate your moves and apply the various card effects to reach your goal and find your way out.

The player win when the "Exit" card get to the "backpack".

An “Infinite” mode is also available, allowing players to play one game after another, trying to achieve the highest score.

—description from the publisher

The Alone expansion adds about 40 Action and Movement cards that make the Maniac more intelligent and managed by the game. These cards allow him to make random and unpredictable choices and to perform remote actions that will make him more than lethal, increasing the challenge and replayability. This expansion is for fearless Survivors because if facing the Maniac in company is already a great challenge, facing him alone is a real nightmare!

Note 1: The Maniac Alone is an expansion to the board game The Maniac. You need a copy of the board game The Maniac to play it.

Note 2: The game The Maniac already contains the "solo" mode. This expansion "enhances" and expands the "solo" mode.

Note 3: As with all other expansions and versions of the main game, this expansion will also be translated into English, Italian, Spanish, German, French and Polish. You will be able to choose the language you wish to receive it after the end of the campaign in the Pledge Manager when you enter your shipping information.

boardgame: Уютная опушка: Мини-дополнения "Таланты" и "Мечты сбываются" (Creature Comforts: Talents and Dreams Come True Micro-Expansions)
description:
Уютная опушка: Мини-дополнения "Таланты" и "Мечты сбываются" ("Creature Comforts: Talents and Dreams Come True Mini-Expansions") is a collection of two mini-expansions.

The Talents Micro-Expansion adds ten unique player powers to Creature Comforts via five double-sided cards. The cards are the same size as the Traveler cards in the base game. Each card is self-explanatory, so once you draft your Talent card, you know all the rules you need to play with your new talent.

The Dreams Come True Micro-Expansion provides a deck of twenty-five Dream cards. Each Dream card is a secret goal for you to achieve. You start the game with two Dream cards, but as you fulfill them, you take more. Each Dream card you fulfill provides a few extra points, but they can really add up!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
House of Suits resembles an investment game, whereby players take turns placing one card into a common market and one card into their personal investment . The goal is to collect cards of those suits that will do well in the market.

THE DECK

Two players and a 60 card deck, with six suits each numbering from Ace(1) to 10. The game can be played with four, five  or six suits.

THE DEAL

If playing with four suits distribute 10 cards face down to your opponent and yourself, beginning with the opponent. If five suits distribute 11 cards and six suits distribute 12 cards.

THE PLAY

Each player must set aside one card not to be revealed until the end of the game. Players then take turns laying down two cards and picking up two cards. One card must be placed face up into the market in the middle of the table, and the other face up in front of themselves. All of the cards on the table must be grouped according to suits, and once the pickup pile is gone players take a final turn from their hand.

THE POOL: WINNING AND LOSING SUITS

At the end of the game, turn up the two cards that were set aside and add them into the market. Then add up the total value of the cards in the market according to their suits. If any grouping of suits have totals that match each other, discard them. They are not to be included in the scoring.

If six suits are remaining (only possible with a six suit deck) the two highest are the winning suits, the two lowest are the bonus suits, the third is neutral and the fourth is the losing suit.

For five suits (only possible with a 5 suit deck) the two highest are the winners, the lowest is the bonus, the third is neutral and the fourth is the loser.

Four suits: The two highest are the winners, the lowest is the loser and the third is neutral. (no bonus suits from this point on)

Three suits: The two highest are the winners and the lowest is the loser.

Two suits: The highest is the winner and the other is the loser.

If only one suit remains, it is the winning suit.

SCORING

Add up the winning cards in your investment and subtract from that total the losing ones. You can incur a negative score.

Bonus points are calculated differently. To win a bonus suit a player must have a higher total invested in that suit than his opponent. He is then awarded the total value of the cards in the market of that suit.

After four games the player with the highest score wins, or players can play two games of four suits five suits and six suits. After six games the player with the highest score wins.

In a nutshell: LIFE (on Icarus) is a board game where players evolve as a species in a randomly generated, hyper-realistic stellar system based on our current understanding of planetary formation, exoplanet data, and the early evolution of complex life on Earth (roughly coinciding with the Cambrian period).

Winner is the most successful species in terms of thriving in the environment.

Note:
Life on Icarus is based on the current scientific understanding of exoplanet research, the early evolution of complex life on Earth, and general knowledge about cosmic phenomena impacting life and evolution.

—description from the designer

The sky darkens, the ground begins to shake; the great Mount Vesuvius pours smoke, rock, and fire into the sky. It is 79 CE, and this is Pompeii’s last day. As pillars and roof tiles fall and shatter to the ground at your feet, you must navigate the chaos to ensure you make it out.

In Escape From Pompeii, players collect resources from around the board to give as offerings to the gods. Earn their favor (victory tokens) to win, and, before the last tile is turned, flee through one of the city gates to survive. On an increasingly obstructed board, you must make the most important decision of all: when to run.

The game ends when the final volcano tile is laid. The player who has escaped the city and has the most favor tokens wins.

—description from the publisher

What is a Deck Hand game? It is an 18 card game that is used in conjunction with a standard Poker deck.

The brinded cat has mewed, the hedge pig has whined, and all the neighbourhood witches have gathered to brew their most potent and powerful potion. Add ingredients to your cauldron, leave them to simmer, and at the end of the game use your concoction to cast as many spells as you have secreted away. Make a final offering to the blood moon and coax the black cat to your side, and the player with the greatest grimoire will reign supreme!

In Boil and Bubble, you are witches placing ingredient cards into your personal scoring cauldron throughout the game. Every round you will also be bidding on and winning other spell cards that you will use to score with your stored ingredients. You can also make offerings to the full moon or attract your black cat familiar for extra points at the end of the game. The player with the highest score wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Das Ponta-Spiel oder die klugen Ratsherren
description:
Each player places their pieces in the other player's territory. The five councilors stand in the first line, the ten aldermen behind them. The players take turns moving. Each player takes three steps along the lines of the game board, i.e., from point to point.

Aldermen may move in any direction, except away from the river toward the interior, although they may move sideways.

The councilors, on the other hand, are allowed to move in any direction, including backwards. The councilors also have the right to capture opposing councilors and aldermen. Capture is achieved by removing an opposing piece that is on the third point of the move and placing one's own piece in its place. If all councilors on one side are captured, the right to capture passes to the aldermen, but they may only capture forwards and sideways.

The three moves may also be distributed among two or three pieces. Those who cross the bridge try to occupy the white points on the courtyard wall in their home territory. The first player to occupy all seven points wins the game. Pieces that land on a white point may not move from it during the game.

The game was published about 1910.

—user summary

What is a Deck Hand game? It is an 18 card game that is used in conjunction with a standard Poker deck.

After making your way through a treacherous jungle (ugh…that rope bridge!), you have reached the hidden archeological site. Hopefully the map you've cobbled together from various sources and scraps is accurate. Hopefully you’ve collected the right tools to help manage any obstacles. Hopefully you’ve arrived at the site before the other intrepid treasure hunters. The competition is fierce and to the victor, go the spoils! Dig it?

In The Dig: Treasure Island, players compete to claim the most points worth of treasure over 2 rounds. To collect treasure, they must first draft cards and assemble a “Map” (a tableau of 12 cards) in front of them. Then, at the end of each round, “Treasure” cards will be awarded to the players whose Maps best match the patterns shown on the Treasure cards. (Players may also use “Equipment” cards to adjust their Maps to their benefit.) At the end of two rounds, the player with the most valuable Treasure wins.

—description from the designer

The thieves have sneaked into the mansion of the billionaire Mr. Paipai. The treasure they are after is a white card, rumored to be worth as much as a diamond. However, Mr. Paipai has set a trap. Don't be fooled by the fake Paipai card and get the white card.


     Rules explanation A simple but shallow card game that can be started in 10 seconds
     Contents are only white cards. No graphic design or printing
     Aha moment when you understand the game

Spring has arrived in the forest, and all the birds are searching for a mate to lay eggs! In a chaotic "squawk-ophony" of calls, each player must make the sound indicated on their card to find their potential partner. If successful, both players earn an egg!

Beware of PÈRE OK (Parrot) and other cunning birds that mimic calls—falling for their tricks could cost you dearly! The winner is the player with the most eggs when all have been collected.

A game that will turn your home into a lively aviary, filled with guaranteed laughter!

—description from the publisher (translated)

In this fast-paced party game, two clue-givers bet on how few words they’ll need to have their teammate(s) guess five secret answers, starting at 25. The lowest bidder takes the card and must beat the challenge within their limited clues--and time! Every word counts in this energy-fueled board game that keeps family and friends on the edge of their seats!

Early Bird is a cute and quick bluffing game where players compete as hungry birds trying to find a worm.

Each round, players choose a tile to land on and give it a peek (while putting on your best poker face). Then, try to distract your friends with some strategic bread crumbs before swooping in to snatch a worm.

An optional advance variant lets players use the food they find along the way to activate powerful abilities.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Saddle up for DIGGING BANDITS, a competitive Wild West board game of treasure hunting and treachery. An ancient map whispers of a golden fortune buried deep beneath the shifting sands, but you ain’t the only bandit listening! Expect direct conflict and strategic card play: block paths, steal loot, and collect WANTED posters to eliminate the competition. Will your mix of luck and cunning lead you to find the GOLD and cash it in first, or will you outlast your rivals to be the last bandit standing? Only the desert knows!

Players move clockwise around the game board by rolling two dice. The digging mechanic is central to the game, with players using Dig cards to uncover layers of land, seeking valuable items like gold and WANTED posters while avoiding hazards such as dynamite. The game also includes a rich layer of strategy, with Draw cards that allow players to hinder each other, build and destroy bridges, or steal items, creating an intense, ever-shifting battle for victory. Special "db Doodad" power-up cards give players game-changing boosts, while bluffing, alliances, and out-of-sequence card play add psychological and social elements to the experience.

The game is designed for 2-4 players, ages 10 and up, with an average playtime of 30-60 minutes. It includes both competitive and cooperative play modes, with a 2v2 team variant and a quick-play option for shorter sessions.

—description from the publisher
publishers: Think Inside The Box Creative Inc.

description:
Roll the moose-shaped dice to determine the number of spaces to move your piece around the game board. Avoid MAYHEM cards... or try to quickly recover from them. Use MEAL cards to gain energy and move forward. The winner is the first player to reach the FINISH and declare “Don’t Moose with Me”!

Playing the Game
--On your turn, roll two moose and move your piece the combined number of spaces. After you move your piece, draw a card from the stack and follow the directions. 
--MAYHEM cards may move a piece forward or back, cause you to lose a turn, or more! If your piece lands on a space already occupied by another piece, both players must “SPAR”.
--MEAL cards are used as fuel throughout the game. Move your piece forward the number of spaces on the card. 
--Make Your Own Mayhem! Add your own scenarios to the game. Write your own event and number of spaces to move on each Make Your Own Mayhem card.

The year was 2005, and the largest animal recorded alive was the blue whale. But suddenly, the world was shocked by the appearance of terrifying creatures that could have the blue whale for a light midnight snack. Gargantis, Toxicor, Tomanagi, and others rampaged across the United States as the rest of the world watched in horror, in an event that has become known as the 2005 Rampage.
