Though many cities and famous landmarks were demolished, after a stunning battle for supremacy the monsters disappeared into the wilderness, the world was left to pick up the pieces. Now, many years later, the colossal creatures have discovered the rest of the continents of the world, and all of humanity are threatened by their gargantuan struggle.

Monsters Menace the World is a 2-4 player game where each player controls a Monster that they use to menace the planet in order to gain Infamy, as well as one Military faction to slow down or stop the other players’ Monsters from doing the same. Play as Chupacoloso, Konk, Nessie, Strigoika, Tomanagi, Zorb, and The Minions. The player with the most Infamy wins!

Features


     Control a monster and menace the planet to gain Infamy!
     Play as Chupacoloso, Konk, Nessie, Strigoika, Tomanagi, Zorb, and The Minions.
     Stop other players' monsters using your Military faction and cunning strategy!
     Features fresh and updated gameplay!
     Stunning art and graphic design along with detailed miniatures!


—description from the publisher

Combat Commander: Vietnam is a card-driven board game that adapts Chad Jensen's original Combat Commander system to cover tactical infantry combat during the Indochina conflicts from 1961 to 1975. One player takes the role of the Free World Forces (ARVN, US, Special Forces, or Free World Allies) while another player commands the Communists (NLF or NVA), with unique Orders of Battle and Fate decks for each of these six factions.

Combat Commander: Vietnam comes with 24 scenarios selected to highlight the diversity of engagements, formations, periods, and terrain featured in the Vietnam War and adjacent conflicts. The scenarios include parts of famous battles such as Ap Bac (1963), Operation Starlite (1965), Long Tan (1966), and the siege of An Loc (1972), but also many more obscure engagements such as the South Korean defense at Tra Binh Dong (1967), ARVN forces at Prey Veng fighting NVA in Cambodia (1970), and Hmong guerrillas fighting off NVA encroachment of Skyline Ridge (1972). The game also comes with a complete Vietnam War random scenario generator (RSG), capable of generating nearly any possible engagement among the major fighting formations at the scale of platoon to company from 1961-1975.

Combat Commander: Vietnam also includes a full Campaign mode that can be played as either one of eight historical campaigns, or a randomly generated campaign using the random campaign generator. Campaigns in Combat Commander: Vietnam focus on “one scale up” from standard engagements, by placing players into the role of battalion commanders launching a month-long operation. Players will confront issues normally above the scale of Combat Commander, such as finding and fixing the enemy, attrition and interdiction of opposing forces, and the impact of military operations on the “village war”. Using a modified version of the standard random scenario generator, the Campaign mode generates engagements relevant to what is occurring on the Campaign display.

Highlights:

     New mechanical changes including Break Contact, Fire Support, Objectives, Patrol Posture, Fate Deck Shifts, and more to adjust to the new conflict.
     New Orders (e.g. Withdraw), Actions (e.g. Anti-tank Weapon), and Events (e.g. Ghost Soldiers) appropriate to the Vietnam War.
     Six new factions (each with a Fate Deck, Counters, and Order of Battle).
     New weapons and other counters including recoilless rifles, civilians, traps, advisors, and many more.
     24 maps spread across six regions (I-IV Corps in South Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia) based on 1:50,000 period maps, archival photos, and descriptions of engagements. 
     New random scneario generator system for generating scenarios for any factions, period, and region in Indochina between 1961-1975. 
     24 scenarios highlighting the diversity of factions, regions, and periods included in the base game box.
     A new campaign mode, playable as one of eight campaign scenarios or using the random campaign generator.


—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Camino de Santiago is a strategic board game - a dice game with a double-sided board. The action takes place on the northern and southern routes that lead through Spain or Portugal to the Cathedral of St. James in Santiago de Compostela. The routes mark sacred, historical and other places worth visiting. For visiting these places, players gain Place cards and tokens. 
Using throws of six wooden dice, players choose a path, change directions, but their goal is always to reach the Cathedral of St. James. To make a move on the board, the player must roll Shoes or Map and Gourd on the dice. The game ends when one of the players reaches Santiago de Compostela. Players add up the points collected from Place cards and tokens. The player who scores the most points wins. (Publisher's description)

The Lepak Game Mini is the newest edition of The Lepak Game. At its core, it is an interactive party game that involves placing card responses to a blank question card. Questions and responses for this game is based on the Malaysian experience and culture.

The person who places the question card is now called the Boss, and players must bodek or please the Boss with the best responses. The Boss chooses a response of a player, and that player wins the round. The winner becomes the Boss for the next round. This continues until someone wins 7 rounds.

From the game developer: 
What’s Awesome about The Lepak Game Mini:

     Updated content with 150 cards with a beautiful new design
     Includes explanations of the Malaysian slang so that it’s friendly to Malaysians and non-Malaysians alike
     It’s portable and fits nicely into your (batik) bag
     Sparks fun conversation: invites sharing of personal stories and creative persuasion over a range of topics such as culture and current events.
     Easy to play with no right or wrong answer, just pick the most Malaysian response!


—description from the publisher

An additional sky-blue sapphire cube piece and ruleset to expand the possibilities of the base game.

This expansion makes the game combinations more opaque adding a small cube piece which reflects back sky-blu light.

In the little package the game expansion comes in, apart from the 2 wooden cubes, you will find instructions and a graphical info about the further colour combinations linked with the new piece, like for example grey.

In Quacks, players are charlatans — or quack doctors — each making their own secret brew by adding ingredients one at a time. Take care with what you add, though, for a pinch too much of this or that will spoil the whole mixture!

Each player has their own bag of ingredient chips. During each round, they simultaneously draw chips from their bags and add them to their pots. The higher the face value of the drawn chip, the further it is placed in the pot's swirling pattern, increasing how much the potion will be worth. Push your luck as far as you can, but if you add too many cherry bombs, your pot will explode!

At the end of each round, players gain victory points and coins to spend on new ingredients, depending on how well they managed to fill up their pots. But players whose pots have exploded must choose points or coins — not both! The player with the most victory points at the end of nine rounds wins the game.

Quacks: All-In Edition includes the original The Quacks of Quedlinburg base game, as well as two expansions previously released separately — The Witches and The Alchemists — as well as deluxe components.

Pixies Flower Power booster of 14 new cards, still designed by Johannes Goupy and illustrated by the artist Sylvain Trabut, will now allow you to play from 2 to 6 players.  
It adds 2 simple rules that will really change your approach to the game and scoring: 

     Two-colour cards have been introduced to ‘simplify’ your life (but with a malus on other colours)
     At the end of the round, each face-down card scores 5 points (minimum).


—description from the publisher

Award-winning educational board games helping children to practise reading and spelling. These board games come in a variety of themes ranging from sport, space, nature, vehicles etc.

The board games focus on children learning common exception and high-frequency words in English.

The games themselves are simple - just roll the dice, move your counter, stopping off on the word tokens to read or spell a word.

—description from the designer

Harbour of Fortune is a strategic board game for 2–4 players, set in 1653 New Amsterdam.
Players lead rival merchant families, building canal houses, sailing ships, and navigating political power to amass wealth.

Designed around custom playing cards, each family plays by its own strengths.

Build canal houses along the bustling harbor, sail ships to secure trade routes, and invest in ventures to grow your fortune, all while navigating disasters and outsmarting opponents. On your turn, roll dice to determine actions, play custom cards like the Red Ace of Hearts or Diamonds for a “Great Investment,” and use wild cards to trigger unexpected events. Claim the title of Director-General, collecting taxes from opponents to gain an edge. The game ends after 8, 10 or 12 rounds, and the player with the most money, earned by controlling canal houses, household growth, amassing wealth, and achieving key objectives, wins. This game features premium components, including custom theme designed (poker) card decks, ships, canal houses, dice, and a Director-General hat, bringing the colonial era to life with historical depth and strategic gameplay.

—description from the designer

What a mess in your room! It’s almost bedtime, but all your toys have decided to go on an adventure and they all got lost! Help them find their storage boxes before the stars shine in the night sky!
For that, you have to reveal a toy token and look at it closely.
Store it in the matching chest or place it in your cosy bed if it is not visible on a chest illustration. Be careful not to reveal too many stars, which will bring you closer to nightfall and the end of the game. Is everything tidied up? Check your chests and start a new game with new, more difficult chests!

—description from the publisher

Let's make the world greener together... As quickly as we can!

Replace polluting factories with clean factories, forests, water, green energy sources and animals. The player that makes their continent(s) green the fastest, will win the game.

On your turn, you roll the die and move your pawn forward that many spaces on the track. Depending on the space you land on, you  place one of your 'green' pieces and/or remove one of the polluting factories in your continent(s). Disaster spaces will force you to remove one of your pieces from the board instead. There are also special spaces that allow you to draw a card and use its special effect.

The player that places all their pieces and removes all polluting factories from their continent(s) first wins the game.

(Dutch version)

www.munji-thegame.com

Blackmail is a game of cunning, bluff, luck and nerve for 2-8 players. There are two ways of playing—either monetary or by the removal of clothing.

Players move round the board blackmailing other players buy gaining facts from cards picked up as they move around the board. This gains or loses them money. The player with the most money by the time all Blackmail cards have been used is the winner.

boardgame: 三国群英录：出奇制胜 (Legends of the Three Kingdoms: Unconventional Tactics)
description:
As the Three Kingdoms' tripartite balance took shape, each state continued to develop its strengths to expand its power.

The Tiger and Leopard Cavalry, an elite force handpicked from Cao Wei’s finest warriors, was known for its fierce combat prowess.
Eastern Wu's naval forces, the very foundation of its nation, grew ever stronger.
Meanwhile, the ingenious mechanical inventions of Shu Han’s Chancellor Zhuge Liang were unparalleled in their time.

More and more heroes and warriors joined the struggle for supremacy, and in this turbulent and chaotic era, only those who could employ brilliant and unexpected strategies would achieve ultimate victory!

—description from the publisher (translated)
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
Auberg-inn is a management and strategy game where you play as a medieval Scottish innkeeper. Over six days, you must attract the best guests, upgrade your establishment, and navigate unexpected challenges to build the most prestigious inn in the kingdom. But be warned—between rowdy drunkards, demanding nobles, and travelers with very specific needs, you’ll need to be cunning to boost your reputation!

Each day follows a structured cycle: you welcome guests, meet their demands, invest in improvements, and deal with random events that can shake up your plans. You can buy furniture to enhance your inn’s prestige, hire staff to improve service quality, or even play dirty tricks on your rivals—spreading nasty rumors or sending troublesome customers their way.

The goal is to have the most famous inn by the end of the six days, just in time for the arrival of the King of Scotland. Your fame score is determined by satisfied guests, purchased upgrades, and financial management… but watch out for shame, which can ruin your reputation! With accessible yet strategic mechanics and consistently tough choices, Auberg-inn delivers an immersive and unpredictable gaming experience.

—description from the designer (translated)

Auberg-inn est un jeu de gestion et de stratégie dans lequel vous incarnez un aubergiste écossais du Moyen Âge. Pendant six jours, vous devez attirer les meilleurs clients, améliorer votre établissement et gérer les imprévus pour devenir l’auberge la plus prestigieuse du royaume. Mais attention : entre les ivrognes bruyants, les nobles exigeants et les voyageurs aux attentes bien spécifiques, il faudra ruser pour optimiser votre réputation !

Chaque journée suit un cycle précis : vous accueillez des clients, gérez leurs exigences, investissez dans des améliorations et affrontez des événements aléatoires qui peuvent bouleverser vos plans. Vous pourrez acheter du mobilier pour augmenter le prestige de votre auberge, embaucher du personnel pour améliorer la qualité du service, ou même jouer quelques coups bas contre vos concurrents en envoyant des rumeurs désobligeantes ou des clients peu recommandables chez eux.

Le but du jeu est d’avoir l’auberge la plus célèbre à la fin des six jours, juste avant l’arrivée du roi d’Écosse. Votre score de célébrité dépend des clients satisfaits, des améliorations achetées et de votre gestion financière… mais gare à la honte qui peut ruiner votre réputation ! Avec ses mécaniques accessibles mais stratégiques et ses choix toujours tendus, Auberg-inn offre une expérience immersive et pleine de rebondissements.

—description from the designer

A fast and fun card game filled with whimsical space critters fighting for galactic supremacy!

In PEW! PEW! PEW!, players draft cards by passing hands to build a unique team. Then, it’s time to deploy: each player places their units face-down on planets, trying to outsmart their opponents.

Once all units are in place, the battle begins! Units are revealed one by one, triggering abilities in a tense, back-and-forth showdown. Finally, strength is compared in the PEW! PEW! PEW! phase to determine who claims each planet. The player who controls the most planets at the end wins the game!

Fast, easy, and full of laughs, PEW! PEW! PEW! is all about making the right moves at the right time and having a blast while doing it.

—description from the publisher

In the 一带一路 (Belt and Road) board game, each player achieves peaceful development and win-win cooperation by signing agreements with stations along the China-Europe Railway Express, transporting goods by train, shipping oil back via maritime orders, and supporting the growth of domestic companies.

Gameplay
Players take on the roles of business developers managing trade routes, signing agreements, and optimizing resource production. Players will establish companies, transport goods via rail and sea, and fulfill trade contracts to generate wealth and victory points.

Objective
The game is a race to accumulate 8 victory points.

Stalingrad Relief: December 1942 is a division/regiment-level game covering German efforts in December 1942 to open a supply corridor to German 6th Army and 4th Panzer Army units surrounded at Stalingrad.

The rules for Stalingrad Relief are based on the Series: Battles in the East series of games spanning many of the World War II Eastern Front battles between 1941 and 1945. Some are well known, while with others recent archival material allowed them to be accurately recreated for the first time.

The game mechanics are an evolution of the SPI classics Four Battles of Army Group South and Panzergruppe Guderian. These have been updated and incorporate later game mechanics, such as SPI/TSR/DG Cobra: Game of the Normandy Breakout.

Game scale is 3–5 miles (5–8 km) per hex. Each game turn is 2 days. Most units are German regiments and brigades, Romanian divisions and regiments, and Soviet mechanized and tank brigades or infantry and cavalry divisions, with some specialty battalions and other formations.

Originally published in World at War magazine #107 (Apr-May 2026).

Game Scale:
Game Turn: 2 days
Hex: 3-5 miles / 5-8 kilometers
Units: Regiment to Division

Game Inventory:
One 22 x 34" full color map
One dual-side printed countersheet of 276 5/8" counters (4 are blanks)
One 16-page full color rulebook

Solitaire Playability: High
Complexity Level: Medium
Players: 2 or more
Playing Time: 4-8 hours

Note: Players will need to provide one 6-sided die for game play.

Burma 1945 is a conflict simulation of the decisive campaign in Burma from November 1944 to May 1945. In this game, the Allies are on the offensive, trying to reconquer most of Burma from the Japanese before the onset of the monsoon season.

Each Game Turn represents about 15 days for 12 total turns. Each hex on the map represents 25 miles (some 40 kilometers). Units are armies, corps, divisions, brigades, regiments, and battalions. Air units represent transport and combat aircraft.

22x34 inch game map and a sheet of 228 9/16 inch counters

Operation Lorraine-Alsace 1944 is a two-player alternative history wargame intended to investigate the operational parameters that would have been in place during the first eight days of fighting across eastern France had Hitler decided to launch his winter counteroffensive there instead of the Ardennes.

The primary goal would have been to cause sufficient casualties in US 3rd and 7th Armies so as to remove them as effective offensive forces for the coming 1945 campaign season. A secondary objective would have been to cause such embarrassment to Gen. Charles DeGaulle’s government as to paralyze it politically, and in that way also remove France’s rejuvenating combat power from the 1945 campaign season.

Such a German effort, if maximally successful, would likely have caused a three- or four-month delay in what we know as the historic timeline of the Western Allies’ advance into Germany. In turn, that would have allowed Hitler to concentrate greater forces in Poland to seriously slow, or even fully defeat, the Soviet offensive there in January, causing a similar delay in the Red Army’s seizure of Berlin.

Taken altogether, the ultimate result of a major German success in Operation Lorraine-Alsace could well have meant one or two German cities would have received the first atomic bombs, in place of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, late in the summer of 1945.

This game is the third volume of a series, the Alternative Battles of the Bulge Series. Each game uses a situationally adapted version of the same overall system to collectively cover all the various plans Hitler considered historically for his 1944-45 winter counteroffensive.

Each game turn represents one day (24 hours). Each hexagon on the map is five miles (8.1 kilometers) from side to opposite side. The maneuver units for both sides are primarily divisions but also include some separate brigades, regiments, battalions and companies. Allied air supremacy is – just as it was historically – hampered by the winter weather prevalent across the fighting front for most of the period covered by the game, and it is therefore represented only abstractly in regard to its effects.

22x34 inch game map and a sheet of 176 5/8 inch counters
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Arras 1940 is an operational level simulation of the battle fought during the Battle of Belgium which took place between 19-23 May 1940. Arras uses a simplified version of the Grand Operational Simulation Series (GOSS) rules that depict WWII combat at the battalion level.

Each hex on the map is one mile from side to the opposite side. Units are battalions and companies. Each AM and PM game turn (GT) represents about one-half of available daylight hours. Night GTs extend through the hours of darkness.

22x34 inch game map and a sheet of 280 1/2 inch counters.

Pensacola 1779-1782 is a two-player, strategic-level wargame simulating Spanish, French, and Patriot operations against British and Loyalist forces in the territories of Louisiana and East and West Florida, from the Mississippi basin to Georgia, during the American Revolution. The campaign involved all major participants in the Revolution and was something of a microcosm of the larger global conflict of which the Revolution was just one theatre. The resulting operations saw British control of the northern coastline of the Gulf of Mexico come to an end.

Each game turn represents three months. Each hex on the map is approximately 40 kilometers (24 miles) across.
22x34 inch game map and a sheet of 176 5/8 inch counters.

Forgotten Front: Italy, 1944-45 is a conflict simulation of the last year of World War II on the Italian Front between the Allied forces (British, United States and their allies) and the Axis powers (German and Italian fascists). It lasts from Operation Olive, August 1944, to the end of the war in May 1945. In this game, the Allied player is on the offensive, while the Axis player attempts to stop the Allies. The Axis player must use his resources carefully and counterattack whenever possible to delay the Allied advance and gather as many resources as possible for the German war effort. Terrain and weather are on the Axis side, while resources and airpower are on the Allied side, as well as a massive partisan movement against the Axis forces behind the front lines.

Each Game Turn (GT) represents approximately one month, for nine GTs. Each hex represents 6.2 miles (10 kilometers). Units are divisions, brigades, and regiments. Support markers represent specialized forces (airpower, artillery, etc).

22x34 inch game map and a sheet of 176 5/8 inch counters.

Eagles of Rome is a grand tactical system for battles of the Roman Era, from the Republic through the Empire to the early Byzantine era. This particular game is Scipio. It covers the battles of the Middle Republic, mainly during the Punic and Macedonian Wars (roughly 246 to 100 BC). Players command armies of various types of forces, from Roman legions to Macedonian Phlangites and Carthaginian companion cavalry, with distinctions between melee and fire combat, plus special tactics. Morale is a critical factor.

Scipio is played in scenarios. Each scenario represents a historical battle. The rules are in three sections. The first contains the Standard Rules common to all games in the series. The second is the Standard section for specific battles. The third is the Optional Rules section, which gives rules that players can use at their option.

Each hex on the map represents 100-200 yards across, depending on the battle. Each game turn represents 15-45 minutes, depending on the intensity of the action. Units represent three to ten Roman maniples, one or two Hellenistic taxis, or amorphous groupings of irregulars (1,000-3,000 men) based on the era and efficiency of the army. Leaders represent single great commanders, war chiefs, or the staff of a legion or other higher formation.

22x34 inch game map and a sheet of 176 5/8 inch counters.

Tsaritsyn: The Volga, 1918-20 is a wargame covering the Russian Civil War campaign revolving around the city of Tsaritsyn (later Stalingrad, then Volgograd). The game pits Red Russian (Bolshevik) forces against the White Russians (anti-Bolsheviks plus some Allied support). The original campaign stretched from July 1918, when the White Russians first besieged the city, through the White capture of the city in June 1919, and the final Red counteroffensive which retook the city. Tsaritsyn was critical as an industrial city and river port. The White Russian General Deniken also used the city as a base to launch one wing of his offensive towards Moscow in 1919.

Each game turn represents six weeks to two months of operations. Each hexagon (or hex) on the map represents 20 kilometers (12.5 miles) across. Each unit represents anything from a regiment up to a division. Operations markers represent specialized forces such as airpower and agitprop (agitation and propaganda).
22x34 inch game map and a sheet of 176 5/8 inch counters.

First Sino-Japanese War, 1894-95 is a wargame covering the war between the emerging Empire of Japan and Manchu China of the Qing Dynasty. The historical campaign saw the Japanese armed forces win a considerable victory against their Chinese and Korean foes, but this was not preordained. In addition, a significant Japanese reverse, either at sea or on land would have had major political repercussions in Tokyo. The game has two players: Japanese and Manchu (the latter includes both Chinese and Korean forces). The Japanese and Manchu armies have inherent differences, and these are modeled by the rules. The Japanese have a national force organized along modern lines for the period of 1894-95. The Manchu armies are a mix of quasi-feudal and semi-trained units. Basically, the Japanese player is trying to beat the historical record while the Chinese are using their advantages, such as they are, to turn the tide.

Each Game Turn represents one month of operations in summer, spring and fall, and two months in winter. Each grid line is about 50 kilometers across. Ground units represent anything from battalions through divisions and larger hordes. Naval units represent one to three battleships or cruisers, or a flotilla of torpedo boats. Map covers northeast China and the Korean Peninsula. It uses a square grid with point to point movement, similar to GO.

22x34 inch game map and a sheet of 176 5/8 inch counters

Tet Offensive 1968 is a solitaire wargame of the 1968 Communist offensive in Vietnam, covering the period from the opening attacks of January through the subsequent second wave offensive of May and the subsequent Allied counteroffensives. There is also an option to extend the game to the third wave offensive of September 1968. In Tet Offensive, the player controls Allied forces (US, ARVN, SEATO (Free World) allies) while the game system controls the Communists (NVA, VC). Communist mobile forces will strike critical Allied bases and cities, and if they capture them, can win the game. The Allies must use a combination of mobility and firepower to react to threats while also striking against enemy bases. Communist actions are generated by the Infiltration Table, which deploys their reinforcements to bases on the map, and then moves them from those bases to various objectives in South Vietnam. There are also Dau Tranh offensives, enhancing Communist attacks.

Each game turn represents anywhere from one week of intense fighting to one month of refitting and reinforcement. The map is an abstracted depiction of South Vietnam, along with some areas of Laos, Cambodia, and North Vietnam. Units represent anything from US and Allied divisions down to Viet Cong cadres.

22x34 inch game map and a sheet of 176 5/8 inch counters.

Todos p'ra mesa, já! is an educational game about food sustainability. The game challenges players to prepare a healthy meal while assessing the environmental impact of their food choices. Each ingredient has hidden costs, such as water consumption, land use, and pollution. In addition to individual competition, there is a semi-cooperative element represented by the food waste and production cost piles, highlighting global issues. Throughout the game, players realize how certain decisions affect the environment. At the end, a discussion on waste and sustainability encourages more responsible food choices. The goal is to raise awareness about sustainable eating and reducing environmental impact.

Welcome four new adventurers in need of salvation! These Arcane Adventurers reward you with precious Mana to recharge your Alchemy Station. But beware, if you don't heal them in time, you'll face their unique Ghost Vengeance events!

Oh ～ I'm late!
You, who turned around the corner, slammed each other and the memory splattered.
Exploring your memory from the messy spiritual world,
Let's all come back to reality before the spiritual world collapses!

It is a strange festival that runs into rare behavior all at once. Becoming attention around you.
Rest assured even if you have a nervous breakdown like but are not confident in your memory! Because others will tell you!

This expansion for Early Bird includes two new game modes

Early BirdZ - a zombie worm is in the loose! Adds some bite to your work hunt and a new bird brained path to victory.

Early Birds Aren't Real - We're being watched. There's a robot among us. Can you find the truth in time? Adds a social deduction game mode with secret role cards.

boardgame: Guards of Traitor's Toll: Busy Streets
description:
This packed expansion set adds magic items, sergeants and patrons, a host of new Traits, Plots & Secrets, plus enough Recruitment cards, counters, dice and figures for a 3rd and 4th player.

Choose your allegiance to one of the great lords of the city, giving you special extra goals to achieve during a game. Recruit an experienced sergeant to bolster your squad. Discover magical potions and spell scrolls (which can be tricky to control!).

Valhalla is a skirmish wargame set during the early days of the Viking expansion, a time when the wolves of the north burst forth out of Scandinavia seeking fame and fortune by trade or terror. As leader, will you lead your band of warriors to fame and riches or a glorious death - to Valhalla?

The game is designed to use a few figures on a fairly small table-top. These are the basic rules you will need to know in order to start playing. There are additional rules, supplied at no cost, which are presented as part of specific scenarios.

These rules seek to be historically accurate while at the same time trying to emulate the feats of derring-do we see in the movies. Each warrior can accumulate wounds (or "hits") as the fight goes on. Even though our hero may eventually win the fight, there is no guarantee that he will come away unscathed!

—description from the publisher

The game is played on a 13x13 board, with black and white stones.

On each turn, each player dispatches a stone in an orthogonal direction starting from a friendly stone already on the board, as far as he likes provided it never encounters and non empty cell. When it stops it grows a pentomino, a tetromino, a trimino or a domino, provided those cells are empty.

A group of stones with no liberty are captured and removed from board.

When both players pass, each player counts the number of stones he has on board, plus the empty cells inside his territory (i.e., empty cells where the adversary cannot dispatch stones). Wins the player with more points.

Captain Avis is the innovative board game, created by Avis Comunale Viareggio, Game Science Research Center with Scuola Alti Studi of Lucca (Imt), University of Florence and with the support of Avis Toscana and iCARE Viareggio, which continues to receive appreciation. In fact, after being presented on various occasions including at the Didacta Fair in Florence and at the Unicorn Festival in Vinci, Captain Avis was present at Lucca Comics & Games and at the Health Festival.

It is a collaborative board game to promote in a fun way among young people - potential future donors - but not only, the values of donation, cooperation and the importance of making blood and plasma available in the community. After an experimentation with interesting scientific implications, it will be presented with new updates and innovations. The game has been used in various classes in Versilia and this has made it possible to develop a 2.0 version that is even more appealing and effective.

In the game, players contribute to creating a map on the table made up of tiles with characters and blood types, trying to obtain the highest possible score: it is a cooperative game, in which players all win or all lose, and is based on "game-based learning," learning through activities that involve games and playing, engaging and motivating participants.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Capitan Avis è l'innovativo gioco da tavolo, ideato da Avis Comunale Viareggio, Game Science Research Center con Scuola Alti Studi di Lucca (Imt), Università degli Studi di Firenze e con il supporto di Avis Toscana e di iCARE Viareggio, che continua a ricevere apprezzamenti. Infatti dopo essere stato presentato in diverse occasioni tra cui alla Fiera Didacta a Firenze e alla Festa dell'Unicorno di Vinci, Capitan Avis è stato presente al Lucca Comics & Games  e al Festival della Salute.

È un gioco da tavolo collaborativo per promuovere in maniera divertente tra i ragazzi - potenziali futuri donatori - ma non solo, i valori della donazione, della cooperazione e l'importanza di rendere possibile la disponibilità di sangue e plasma nella comunità e dopo una sperimentazione dagli interessanti risvolti scientifici verrà presentato con nuovi aggiornamenti e novità. Il gioco è infatti stato utilizzato in diverse classi della Versilia e questo ha permesso di studiare una versione 2.0 ancora più accattivante ed efficace.

Nel game i giocatori contribuiscono a creare sul tavolo una mappa fatta da tessere con personaggi e gruppo sanguigno, cercando di ottenere il più alto punteggio possibile: è un gioco cooperativo, in cui i giocatori vincono tutti o perdono tutti, e si basa sul “game-based learning”, l'apprendimento attraverso attività che coinvolgono il gioco e il giocare, coinvolgendo e motivando i partecipanti.

—description from the publisher

Send It is a restricted variant of Go: a player can only drop stones where he is able to 'see'. This restriction is compensated by the possibility of dropping four stones per turn. This second rule provides a tool for quick invasion of almost surrounded areas, leading to complex tactical battles. The other drop restriction (one stone per group per turn) prevents the quick formation of living groups (i.e., groups that cannot be captured because they control, at least, one cell).

boardgame: Imperial Miners: Portal Games 25th Anniversary Promo Pack
description:
A set of 6 cards for Imperial Miners, in Polish, given away with online orders at the 25th Anniversary of Portal Games in 2024. The logo of Portal Games with a 25 Lat (25 years) is visible on all 6 cards.

The 6 cards are:

     Scena Zbrodni (I)
     Uranopolis (I)
     Podziemia Leśnogrodu (II)
     Obrońcy Gravehold (II)
     Jaskinia Na Przeklętej Wyspie (III)
     Pikantna Pieczara (III)

boardgame: Jednym słowem: Dzień Gier Planszowych Promo
description: A set of 4 Polish Just One cards published by Rebel on the occasion of Boardgame Day in Poland (Dzień Gier Planszowych) on 10.10.2024. The cards list games published by Rebel mainly.

boardgame: The Resistance: Avalon – Ekskalibur Promo Card
description:
Promo card Ekskalibur published by Portal Games in 2015.

Description: 
He who holds Excalibur in his hands has extraordinary power. Power over Evil and Good. He should use it with great caution and prudence, lest he accidentally strengthen the forces of Evil.

Excalibur can be added to Avalon as an additional player ability. Excalibur gives a Fellowship member the ability to change the outcome of a Mission by swapping a Mission card played by another player. When choosing a Fellowship, the Leader gives one player not only the Fellowship token, but also Excalibur . The Leader cannot give Excalibur to himself.

During the Mission Phase, the Fellowship members secretly decide whether to support or sabotage and lay out the appropriate Mission cards face down in front of them. Before the cards are gathered and shuffled, the player with Excalibur may order one of the Fellowship members to swap a played card. After the cards are swapped, the player with Excalibur looks at the card he ordered to be withdrawn (i.e. the original card he ordered to be swapped). This will let him know the player's choice and whether his decision benefited or harmed the Mission. The Leader then collects and shuffles the played cards as normal before they are revealed.

Example: Tadeusz, who is the Leader, chooses Agnieszka for the Team. He believes that Agnieszka is on the same side as him and gives her Excalibur. He adds himself and Kamil to the Team. After all players have accepted the Team, Tadeusz, Agnieszka and Kamil choose Mission cards. Agnieszka decides to use Excalibur and tells Kamil to switch the card he played. Agnieszka looks at the card originally played by Kamil (Success) and sees that her decision was wrong and led to the Mission's failure. The Leader collects and shuffles the played Mission cards, and it turns out that there are 2 Successes and 1 Failure among them.

Intrigue Cards from The Resistance and Excalibur

In Avalon: King Arthur's Knights, players may use Intrigue cards from Resistance . In such cases, Excalibur's ability should be used before I've Got My Eyes on You is played. Additionally, Excalibur cannot be used to change a Mission card that has been openly played as a result of an Under Watch card.

货车满当当 (My Loaded Truck),centered around delivery trucks transporting goods, offers three progressively challenging modes to help children practice addition and subtraction within 20 while developing a deeper understanding of numbers and quantity. By turning math into an engaging experience, it makes learning fun and helps parents overcome the struggle of getting kids interested in arithmetic.

The excitement doesn’t stop at the satisfaction of completing deliveries—the real thrill comes from the strategic competition and lively battles between players as they race to claim valuable cargo.

—description from the publisher (translated)

A stack-based game where each stack size correspond to a different Chess piece. Since stacks can share pieces, that means that powers can be transferred among adjacent friendly stacks. Only the King has a special non-stack royal status.

—description from the designer

Players, on their turns, drop/move a stack (there's a limited amount of stackable pieces) and optionally drop two walls into empty hexes. Stacks without liberties are captured.

The goal is to build the highest score of territory plus captures.

Check Xana's complete rules.

—description from the designer

Even Odds is a tile-based trick-taking game for 3-6 players.

In Even Odds, the challenge is to stay calm and keep your score low! Don't be fooled by the high numbers - here, winning means having the lowest score at the end. With limited information about your tiles, get ready for lots of twists and turns and guaranteed laughs.

The objective of Even Odds is to finish the game with less points after three rounds. Whoever plays the highest tile in each dispute wins and scores points. However, you can only see the back of your tiles during the whole game, while you see the front of your opponents’ tiles.

Play it, risk it and try to be the winner.

—description from the designer

In Conquest: Mystic Invasions, players take on the role of powerful sorcerers, tasked with recruiting wizards and using their skills to conquer mystical towers so as to build the grandest of all kingdoms.
Challenge your opponents in epic battles, strengthen your towers with enchanted upgrades, and summon magical monsters to defend them. At the end of the journey, the player who gets the most victory points will be crowned the great Mystic Conqueror.

—description from the publisher

In the Middle Ages, intrigue, conspiracies, and manipulation forged and destroyed kingdoms. Castles were the center of political, social and cultural power at the time, and every effort was made to maintain and expand that power. But only a select group possessed the necessary influence to take part in court conspiracies and intrigues.

A vacant spot awaits at the kingdom's council table, reserved for top counselors. One of its members has mysteriously died and their place needs to be filled. Do you have the wits necessary to assume such a prestigious position?

In Council, players will have the opportunity to test their strategic and tactical skills in a competition for the coveted spot at the court's high council. Through rolling and selecting dice, players must spread their influence, win over members of the court, forge alliances and fulfill decrees to ascend to the position 1 of King’s counselor and be victorious.

Get ready for an epic dispute, full of emotions, tricks and twists.

—description from the publisher

Festung Schneidemühl 1945 is a Tactical war game set during the battle for the Gate of the Pomeranian Wall between January and February 1945. Players command Soviet and German battalions, striving to achieve historical operational objectives.

The game features a hexagonal map where each hex represents 250 meters of real terrain. The mechanics are based on an initiative system, artillery support, and a complex simulation of unit strength and quality. The key to victory is optimal resource management and anticipating the opponent's movements.

The game realistically depicts the siege of Fortress Schneidemühl, where defenders must effectively manage limited forces and resources, while the attacking Soviet forces must break through the defenses using infantry, artillery, and armored support.

Although the game operates at the battalion level, with hexes representing approximately 250 meters, its gameplay focuses on strategic-level decision-making. Players must manage entire divisions, coordinate support units, allocate reserves, and consider long-term operational goals. Therefore, while the physical scale is tactical, the nature of the gameplay justifies its classification as a strategic wargame.

boardgame: まっぷたツートンソウル (Mapputa Two-Tone Soul)
description:
Mapputa Two-Tone Soul is a game in which you search for your destined partner who has the same past life as you.

First, each player is dealt a card that shows what they were like in their past life, and they secretly check what's inside.

Then, they arrange five image cards with various words on them in order of how close they are to their past life, and by turning over the cards with the furthest images first, they deduce and search for a partner who has the same past life as them.

When each player is convinced that the other person is their partner, they receive a heart-shaped chip.
The faster you collect a chip, the higher your points are, so it's first come, first served, but getting the answer wrong is meaningless.
Conversely, you can also patiently observe and try to figure out who your partner is, or you can choose not to take any chips until the end.

Once all the image cards have been turned over, everyone announces the contents of their past life cards.

Pairs who correctly picked their partner receive the heart-shaped chip as a point, but pairs who got it wrong lose their chips. A player who chooses not to take a chip can sometimes steal the chip that their correct partner has taken and add it to their score.

This is repeated three times, and the player with the most points wins.

Flip tiles, test your speed and play anywhere with Match Madness Flip. This waterproof travel friendly version can be played solo or with two players. Includes 60 puzzle cards and 40 online challenges!

Flip the card, match the pattern with your tiles, be first to say MATCH!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
Step into the world of Omaaj, a Collectible Deck Game where your ultimate goal is to assemble a stunning six-card image that represents the theme of your deck. Omaaj challenges you to balance immediate actions with long-term strategy. Will you be the first to reveal your full picture and emerge victorious?
It's the Mesozoic Era versus Celestial Bodies. Pay homage to your favorite subject!
Omaaj is a deck manipulation game that plays like a CCG, but it is the themed decks that are collectible here.  Each deck features incredible art, great gameplay, and inspires further learning about the topic of each deck with exclusive access to our Codex, explaining the relevance of each card.

—description from the publisher

Minitaur is a solo roll-and-write game where players must navigate a maze, retrieve a ball of red wool, and return to the entrance while avoiding the fearsome Minitaur. Equipped with just a pencil, an eraser, and three six-sided dice, players will draw their path through the labrynth, carefully planning their moves while the Minitaur relentlessly pursues them.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Stalinist Russia
description:
The year is 1931, and the Soviet Union is forging ahead with its grand experiment: Stalin’s Five-Year Plan. The Party has declared that industry must expand, infrastructure must modernize, and the nation must surge toward self-sufficiency—no matter the cost. Across the vast Soviet landscape, railway planners work tirelessly to construct a transport network capable of meeting the regime’s ambitious demands.

Yet, scarcity looms. The collectivization of farmland has uprooted rural life, and grain is diverted to fuel urban expansion, leaving resources ever more difficult to procure. Distribution is paramount, and only those who can efficiently manage the state’s railways will secure their place in the Soviet machine. Meanwhile, the gears of progress turn swiftly, bringing powerful new locomotives—but at an ever-increasing price. To hesitate is to fall behind, and in Stalin’s Soviet Union, those who fail to meet expectations do not go unnoticed.

Will you rise as a stalwart architect of the Five-Year Plan, ensuring the success of Soviet industry? Or will you find yourself outmaneuvered, outpaced, and cast aside as an enemy of progress? The choice is yours—though history has never been kind to those left behind.

Super Power Card for "Brains".

Card effect:
Wayward Routes: Once per game, you may ignore the placement rules for 1 tile. The next time you play the game, you may activate this card to use an especially powerful ability. Give the card then to another player after you use it

Super Kem’s Équipe de France is a fast-paced party card game played in teams of two. Players attempt to collect four identical cards representing players from the French national football team. Once a player has four of a kind, they must discreetly signal their teammate, who then rushes to hit the bell and call out “Kem’s!” to score points. However, the opposing team can intercept by recognizing the secret signal and calling “Contre-Kem’s” first.

Objective / Victory Condition
The team that accumulates the most points by successfully signaling and calling "Kem’s" or catching their opponents' signals ("Contre-Kem’s") wins the game.

This mini expansion adds 3 new tiles, that can be used after finishing all the campaign achievements.
The tiles are 2 monuments (of Friderick Chopin and Józef Piłsudski) that are located in Japan, and one tile of Japanese garden located in Poland.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Bullet: Heartbeat is an unofficial fan-work, and is not endorsed or approved by Level 99 Games.

Four new full-hearted heroines wish to prove their mettle! Fight with Sedatives, Portals, Fire bending and... Rubiks cubes!?
Battle against each other or cooperate against a heroine's powerful boss form!

This is an unoffical fan-expansion by Filip Kull (TheNiceZombie). Bullet♥︎ or Bullet★ is required to play.

Do you really know your stuff, or can you play a blinding bluff?

Ultimate Arrogance is the high stakes naming game in which ego, nerve, and a killer poker face are the keys to success. Simply list the most answers under each topic to win... or trick your opponent into thinking you can!

Will you risk it all or play it safe?

—description from the back of the box

Battleship x3 is a camping version of Battleship, expanded for a group of three players. You can fight in a sea battle as two according to the classic rules, or you can play three in a new way! A game for three expands the variations of the battle, adds a spirit of rivalry or teamwork, for example, creating alliances of the navy. Other technical features: waterproof cards for multiple games with erasable markers, small overall size and very light weight (less than an ounce). The game (without markers) can be sent in a regular postal envelope! Therefore, this game is part of a series of optimized Mail games! In addition, the publisher has prepared a separate PDF version of the game for fans of independent Print & Play. Fair winds in the sails of your fleet and good luck in the accuracy of shooting cannons!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Canvas: Finishing Touches – Deluxe Upgrade Pack
description: If you have a retail edition of Canvas: Finishing Touches, purchase this to upgrade it to the Deluxe edition.

boardgame: Critter Kitchen: Rudy the Reindeer Promo Card
description:
A promo offered as a gift to the backers of Critter Kitchen on Kickstarter.

-Zous Chef - Rudy Reindeer

If you recruit Rudy, for each carrot that Rudy picks at their location, you may gift it to another player to gain 1 star.

boardgame: От моря до моря (From Sea to Sea)
description:
От моря до моря ("From Sea to Sea") is a cooperative area control game set in the ancient Near East during the reign of Tigranes II the Great of Armenia (1st century BC). Players take on the roles of Tigranes II and his sons, working together to unite the regions of the Near East under one powerful kingdom, while resisting the increasing influence of the Roman Republic.

Each round, players strategically use action cards to move armies and leaders, negotiate or fight for control of territories, collect resources, build cities, and manage internal stability. Players must carefully balance their actions to expand efficiently and counter threats, including rebellions and Roman interference.

To win, players must successfully control all 12 regions before Roman influence becomes overwhelming. Cooperation and strategic planning are essential for victory.

Welcome Travel to Europe! Your goal is to visit 12 European countries and collect souvenirs (stamps) from each one. All the stamps are custom-made with landmarks of different cities, designed by an artist in a retro style from the 19th century, and there are special lucky stamps too. The base game includes 64 souvenir stamps. The game board is very compact and can fit into a standard envelope when folded. It has a unique and impressive unfolding system that expands it to A4 size. The game also features links to additional information about European landmarks, offering educational and interesting historical facts for curious players. You move around the board by rolling a die or flipping two coins (any coins with heads and tails will do). Whoever collects five sets of stamps first wins the game, but it's not as easy as it sounds… Stamps can be lost, traded, or sent/received via mail. You'll need both luck and caution!
This game is lightweight and compact, making it perfect for sending in regular postal mail. Therefore, this game is part of a series of optimized MAILGAMEs!  Whether you're heading to a friend's party or going on a hike, this game fits easily into your pocket. It's ideal for a casual evening of gaming fun, suitable even for kids.
In addition, the publisher has prepared a separate PDF version of the game for fans of independent Print & Play.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
This goodie consists of 5 Surprise tiles. During setup, replace as many Coin tiles as you'd like with the Surprise tiles of your choice (though we recommend using no more than 3 per game). On the Surprise tiles, the Tentacle face is replaced by an Effect face (Surprise tiles are placed Effect face down during setup). During the game, when you perform the Score action, all Surprise tiles magnetized to your cube must be placed in your personal area, Effect side up.

Tiles: Padlock, Wild Object & Color, Intense Color, Curse of the Deep (x2)

Called Goodies n°2 on publisher web page.

—description from the publisher

This goodie consists of 5 Surprise tiles (6 when included in the first print run of Octocube). During setup, replace as many Coin tiles as you'd like with the Surprise tiles of your choice (though we recommend using no more than 3 per game). On the Surprise tiles, the Tentacle face is replaced by an Effect face (Surprise tiles are placed Effect face down during setup). During the game, when you perform the Score action, all Surprise tiles magnetized to your cube must be placed in your personal area, Effect side up.

Tiles: Wild Object, Wild Color, Treasure, Kraken, Permutation.

6th tile was blank and called Imagination.

Called Goodies n°1 on publisher web page.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pole Position: Circuits Pack 1 Italy & China
description: This is an expansion for Pole Position with two fictional circuits. Each circuit has a special rule. The base game is required to play. The box contains the folded double-sided board with a circuit on each side.

Welcome to Cosmoblivion! Settle colonies and conquer territories in a cosmic adventure where princesses reigns supreme over the cosmos! In this deck-building space odyssey, your goal is to amass 25 Galaxy points to claim the throne as master of the universe!

In Cosmoblivion, you manage a deck of cards where you have to balance combat and development. Each turn, strategically buy new cards that you put on top of your deck ready for battle. Generate precious resources throughout the game and naturally refine your deck.

Combat involves conquering planet simultaneously between players. The winner gain a special power with the planet, the losers destroy one of their card. As you progress, cleaning your bad cards will help you catch the other players!

Adapt your strategy based on the dynamic card market. Mix and match Alien, Human, and Robot cards to optimize your Space credit production and secure your path to victory in Cosmoblivion!

—description from the publisher

Animal Parade is a solitaire game for kids and adults. The deck comprises beautiful animal cards, which help you be the first to complete your parade! You must be the first player to earn 10 points on the scoreboard to win the game.
This game is designed for Adults and kids to enjoy. In "kid mode," you can play with younger kids.

—description from the publisher

Black Pool is a modern variant of the classic German card game Schwimmen. It is designed for 3 to 6 players and uses two 32-card decks (Skat decks).

Each player tries to support a suit (color) with the highest total value by collecting matching cards and performing actions like card swapping, bluffing with duplicate cards, or reviving cards from the graveyard pile.

Unique actions include “Wake the Death,” “Double bluffing,” and the powerful “Straight Flush” which ends the game instantly.

The goal is to avoid supporting the weakest suit on the table at the end of the round. Strategic timing, deduction, and a bit of luck are all essential to win.

—description from the designer

Black Pool (auch bekannt als Regional Swimming Pool) ist eine moderne Abwandlung des klassischen Kartenspiels Schwimmen. Das Spiel entstand in den 1970er Jahren im Svartsjö Slottspark bei Stockholm im Rahmen einer internationalen Jugendbegegnung und wird heute in deutscher Sprache gespielt.

Gespielt wird mit zwei 32er-Skatblättern (insgesamt 64 Karten). Jeder Spieler versucht, eine Kartenfarbe mit möglichst hoher Punktzahl zu „unterstützen“. In jeder Runde kann eine von sechs taktischen Aktionen gewählt werden, darunter klassische Kartentausche, das Auffrischen des Boards, das Schieben oder das Einleiten des Spielendes („Passen“).

Besonders sind die Mechaniken „Wake the Death“ (ein Tausch mit dem Graveyard), das Bluffen durch Doppel-Karten („Doppeln“) sowie das direkte Beenden des Spiels durch einen „Straight Flush“. Ziel ist es, am Rundenende nicht die Kartenfarbe mit dem niedrigsten Gesamtwert auf dem Tisch (Board und Hände aller Spieler) zu unterstützen.

Black Pool kombiniert klassische Elemente mit sozialer Interaktion, Strategie und einem Hauch Täuschung.

—description from the designer (German)

5Goddesses is a strategic trading card game that brings together divine forces in a battle for supremacy. Players take on the role of one of 5 powerful factions, each representing a unique aspect of existence, from life and death to the forces of nature. The game’s rich lore is woven into every card, where each goddess possesses distinct abilities, powers, and followers that players can use to outwit their opponents. With its artwork and immersive world-building, 5Goddesses transports players into a mythical realm where strategy, resource management, and tactical skill are paramount.

At its core, 5Goddesses combines elements of deck-building and combat mechanics, offering a new take on the TCG genre. Players must assemble their decks of divine cards, carefully balancing offense, defense, and support to gain the upper hand. Not all cards are shuffeled together in one deck, instead you set up each card type deck separately, reducing the luck of the draw. Also you attack in lanes, opening up tactical play.

The game also features a resource system that requires careful management, as players must collect and allocate divine energy to cast their powerful abilities and summon legendary creatures to fight for them.

Also each faction pack contains all available cards of that faction, thus no need to open boosters and hunt for certain cards. The TCG element comes through special versions (special art, foil, full art, etc.) of the cards, but those are not necessary to play.

—description from the designer

A deck collection, resource gathering, crafting a game. Themed around being a little goblin, collecting trash all to build gifts for the goblin king. All while casting rats, crows, and more to sabotage other players and win the game!

The fast paced, wild nature, allows for quick gameplay for most ages. Accompanied with cute art and a goofy theme it allows players to gain favor for the goblin they like the most!

Comedy and fun is at the center of this game all while focusing on a simple rule set that can be expanded on with game modes and  expansions making is even more rambunctious.

—description from the publisher

In Thief, you will take on the role of either crafty Robbers attempting a daring heist or valiant Cops trying to thwart their plans. Each team uses their cards to steal/return Treasures while moving their car along the road to the crime scene. In this tactical game, you need to keep a close eye on your opponents to make sure the game ends with you in first place. Use your cards to steal (if you are Robbers)/return (Cops) or move your car along the board. The goal is to hold more treasure when the first player crosses the finish line. Be strategic with your choices and work with your team to beat your opponents.

How to play:

     Choose your side – Play as either the COPS or ROBBERS. Your goal is the same: have more treasure than the other guys.
     Timing is everything – Get your team's car to arrive just in time so that your team has the most treasure...because the other team is already on their way.
     Let the race begin – The ROBBERS try to collect as much treasure as possible before their getaway car arrives. The COPS try to grab as much treasure as possible while waiting on backup to arrive.


—description from the publisher

Cultivate Beauty and Harmony in This Card Game for 1 or 2 Players.
Plant flowers on your kokedamas to create unique patterns and complete Zen objectives. Play solo or in cooperative mode and find the perfect balance in your garden!

Objective:
Create a Japanese-inspired garden by planting flower cards on Kokedama cards and fulfilling Zen objectives.

Mechanics:
On each turn, draw a card and choose to plant a flower, add a Zen Recognition objective, or transplant a flower.

Key Features:

     Solo & Coop: Play alone or with a friend!
     Zen Aesthetic: Evokes the tranquility of a Japanese garden.
     Strategic Decisions: Carefully select flowers and objectives.


—description from the publisher

Cultivá belleza y armonía en este juego de cartas para 1 o 2 jugadores. Plantá flores sobre tus kokedamas para crear patrones únicos y cumplir objetivos Zen. ¡Jugá en solitario o en modo cooperativo y encontrá el equilibrio perfecto en tu jardín!

OBJETIVO:
Crear un jardín de inspiración japonesa plantando cartas de flores en cartas de Kokedama y cumpliendo objetivos Zen.

Mecánica:
En cada turno, robá una carta y elegí plantar una flor, agregar un objetivo de Reconocimiento Zen, o trasplantar una flor.

CLAVE DE CARACTERÍSTICAS:

     Solo y Coop: ¡Jugá solo o con un amigo!
     Estética Zen: Evoca la tranquilidad de un jardín japonés.
     Decisiones Estratégicas: Seleccioná cuidadosamente flores y objetivos.


—description from the publisher (Spanish)
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
BLURT OFF! is the social trivia party game. It is a competitive experience of speed and wit that challenges you to think on your feet in response to the game's thrilling twist. The real excitement of the game kicks in after the answers to the cards are revealed. Players take turns reading their own answered questions aloud, and it's a frenzied race between the other players to see who can BLURT OUT the correct answer first.

Can you master the art of thinking before you speak? BLURT OFF! promises endless laughter and replayability with answers changing with every new player you bring to the table. Let the rapid revelations begin, where every question is a burst of fun and every answer brings you closer together!

—description from the publisher

An edge-matching solitaire card game where you try to save the cats from the Crazy Cat Lady! Comes with 60 Cat Cards, 3 Logo Cards, 5 Crazy Cat Lady Cards, and rulebook.

To play the game, the Player places either 1 (shorter game), 2 (medium game) or 3 (longer game) Logo Cards onto the table. Every third card from the remaining deck of Cat Cards is drawn and placed anywhere around the center Logo Cards by matching the symbol(s) on the edges. Play continues by drawing the third card on every turn until three complete rings of cards have surrounded the center Logo cards.

Throughout game play, Crazy Cat Lady Cards will appear, which counts against the Player. When a Crazy Cat Lady Card is drawn, it is placed into the spread anywhere the Player wants, and they can do this up to 5 times since there are 5 Crazy Cat Lady cards.

A Player wins if they can complete three rings of cards around the center Logo Cards before the 5th Crazy Cat Lady card appears. If the 5th Crazy Cat Lady Card appears before the third ring is completed, they lose.

Advanced game play allows the Player to remove a placed Crazy Cat Lady card from the spread, allowing more time to complete their three rings.

A two-Player game can be achieved by alternating turns.

The "HexD3x" series has many versions of the game, and they all play the same way, with some of the games in the series incorporating fun tokens that "deactivate" symbols to match edges to help the Player complete their three rings.

—description from the designer

A Digital-Print&Play Escape Room Adventure for Solo Players or Groups!
Also playable in a fully digital version, no printables required!

Deep beneath the earth, in the eerie darkness of underground tunnels, a team of researchers faces the unknown. Their mission? To save the young daughter of renowned archaeologist Victor Vasileiou, director of the Archaeological Museum of Heraklion in Crete. 
After a theft shrouded in dark magic, she vanished without a trace, along with the Phaistos Disc and the Arkalochori Axe.

Guided by a skilled archaeologist and a gifted medium, the team ventures into the Palace of Knossos, where a strange apparition has returned the Phaistos Disc but the girl remains trapped in a dark and dangerous labyrinth.

Now, they need your help. Will you join them in uncovering the secrets of the maze, solving its intricate puzzles, and rescuing the young prisoner before it’s too late?

Can you respond quickly?
Do you have a good memory, or are you great at bluffing?
These skills come in handy in this hilarious and chaotic card game about cats, dogs, and... POOP!

In this fast-paced card game, you take care of a cat and a dog. You feed them, let them poop, and, of course, they enjoy a nap now and then!
Make sure you always keep track of how much food the dog and cat have in their bellies. This way, you can quickly play the right card.
The first person to get rid of the cards in their hand wins instantly!

—description from the designer

Gamefound Exclusive promo pack of 12 extra action cards.

River Bunny Boom x 6
Mayor x 4
Joker x 2

The Starstones stand at the northern and southern entrances of the grove, their alignment guiding the flow of celestial energy within. When in harmony, they empower the Wildseeds for awakening rituals. Yet, each time Wildseeds move away from a Starstone's proximity, it reacts, altering the balance of energy.

This 2-card expansion alters the size of the "board" and adds another obstacle to navigate when trying to perform awakening rituals. When forming the Grove in Step #2 of the base game Setup, place a Starstone as the middle card in each row while dealing the Ancients, expanding the Grove to two rows of five. One Starstone should be facing front, and the other facing back.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Awaken the Ancients: The Dream Eater
description:
As the awakening rituals begin, The Dream Eater seizes the opportunity to launch its attack on the dream world. However, destroying a Wildseed in the waking world releases its remaining essence into the dream world, empowering the slumbering Ancients to strike back. The Dream Eater must be defeated before the last Ancient awakens to ensure the dream world remains safe until their return.

This 2-card expansion completely reverses the strategy of avoiding seed removal! The faster you remove seeds from the Grove, the more damage you deal to The Dream Eater. However, be careful not to remove too many too soon, as it may leave you unable to awaken all the Ancients. It's recommended to get familiar with the base game before diving into this expansion.

—description from the designer

Class of '89 will take you back to the days of big dreams, bigger hair, and the ultimate high school send-off, so break out the neon and dust off your mixtapes — it's time to create the most legendary yearbook spread ever!

It's your senior year in high school, and as President of the Yearbook Club, it's your responsibility to make sure every standout moment is captured. Compete to create the most kickin' yearbook by sending your uniquely skilled staff all over the school. Assemble a nerd herd full of brainiacs, a team full of musclehead jocks, or a clique of style mavens who always look funky fresh. From the drama on the football field to the most wack after-school clubs, every righteous memory must be immortalized!

Compete to design the gnarliest yearbook spread before the final bell rings. Points can be gained from clever tile placement, strategic set collection, and sweet proximity bonuses as each teacher's class is filled with matching students. Of course, trading signatures will earn much-needed street cred. Don't wig out as only the most awesomely organized yearbooks can win, and nothing is more bodacious than one that is alphabetized.


     Say Cheese, Dude! – Use worker placement to snap the most cringy, classic, and totally radical senior year moments to make your yearbook the best in class.
     Like, Totally Strategic! – Tile placement, set collection, and proximity bonuses will build a yearbook layout that's wicked awesome. 
     ABC or Bust! – Only the most tubular yearbooks follow that all-important A-to-Z order. Mess it up, and your choice creation might just be bogus.


Traverse the memories from an iconic era that brought us everything from The Breakfast Club to Saved by the Bell.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Stars of Akarios: Legendary Weapon Upgrade Pack
description:
The Legendary Weapon Upgrade Pack expands the capabilities of the base game by adding legendary weapon upgrades that make players' ships more powerful and unlock new tactics. Unlike standard weapon upgrades, these cards have special properties that can dramatically change the course of a battle. Increased accuracy, increased damage, special effects against specific enemies - each upgrade adds depth to the strategy and allows you to adapt the ship to your style of play.

The set consists of 6 Legendary Weapon Upgrade cards which can be added to the game at any time.
All these improvements are available on any market. A player can spend 40 credits to purchase one Legendary Weapon Upgrade.
Only 1 Legendary Weapon can be installed on each ship.

The City of Kanashī is calling!

As urban designers, the mayor is asking you to build to new greed district of the city. They will retain the services of the designer that will most show their know-how and will grow the most plants and trees around the district’s important buildings.
 Toshi Tōshi is an area control game with a trick-taking mechanism in which you need to win  or lose tricks at the right moment. You play only ONE hand of 10 cards, so make them count!

The winner of the trick decides which buildings are constructed in which sectors. On the other hand, the non-winners will place Nature tokens around the buildings to increase their influence. 
At the end of the game, the player that has the most tokens connected to a building will win that building.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
designers: Suzuki Takayuki (鈴木 タカユキ)

description:
Pickleball Slam is a light, fast-paced card game played in two phases: the Draw Phase and the Play Phase. Players acquire and use three types of cards (phase cards, number cards and twist cards) to help them win. In most cases, the game is every person for themselves. But with four players, a two-on-two "doubles" match is possible.

Use strategy and luck of the draw to build your shots, play your shots, and win the point (round) to collect a token. Counter a VOLLEY with a TARGETED TWIST and finish off the point with an ERNE. Or go for the SHAKE & BAKE and win the round. Just like Pickleball, the last shot wins the round. The first player (or team in doubles) to collect three tokens wins the game!

Rat's Run! is a fun party game with a theme where you become a hungry mouse and steal food from a cat without its knowledge.

Are you going to gather a lot of delicious food near your cat at once and fill your stomach?
Or will you collect food little by little from the far corner and fill your stomach safely without your cat knowing?

[Rat's Run!] is designed to enjoy psychological warfare and bluffing in the stage of collecting food cards, and the chewy tension of the dice in the cat attack stage.

Mares is a game designed for 2–4 players that combines action management, card collection, and strategic decision-making mechanics with a unique and immersive theme: the sea. It’s perfect for anyone looking for an engaging gaming experience, whether they are beginners or seasoned board game enthusiasts.

The goal is to be the player with the most points at the end of the game. Points are earned by observing marine creatures and completing missions. The deeper you go, the more valuable the creatures you observe — but the greater the risks. Descending comes with penalties that apply at the end of the 6-round game. You can get rid of these penalties, but you’ll have to choose between removing them or continuing to observe.

To achieve your goals, you have 4 actions per round, and each action you take blocks it for all the other player until the end of the round.

Will you be able to become the diver who observes more valuable creature?

—description from the designer

Last Camper is a survival game in which the longest survivor wins. Gather firewood, which is the point of survival, and survive in a rough forest. Will you be the last camper?

Each camper (player) starts with 4 cards and 13 firewood. 
Turn the hourglass back to start the game.
Each player survives or attacks with two cards each turn. 
When you have done your job, you will get two new cards.
If the hourglass is out, all camper will lose one firewood. (*Flipping the hourglass again) 
When the firewood reaches zero, it loses. 
The one who survives to the end wins.
If firewood (survival point) achieves 26 or more, it wins.

Mission Mosaic is a collaborative drawing game in which players all team up and deliver secret codes. Players must work with others to draw pictures so that the person sitting on the right can guess the secret codes.

Rune Stones is a deck-building, hand management game by acclaimed designer Rüdiger Dorn. In Rune Stones, every card has a unique number on it. Two cards are always played together, and the higher numbered card is removed from the player's deck. You have to be careful which cards you buy and how you play them to not lose your best cards. Players will use their cards to gather gems, forge them into Artifacts, then combine those into Rune Stones, which grants a special ability for the rest of the game. The more artifacts used in making a Rune Stone, the more points it scores, so players must decide whether it is best to gain abilities early or save to score more points.

On your turn, you may choose from one of three options:

1. Summon Creatures: Take new creature cards from the display by playing the appropriate amount of spell power from cards in your hand.

2. Play Cards: Play two cards from your hand, which will give you resources, new cards, or points. However, every card has a unique number, and the higher numbered card of the two is removed from the game (the other is placed in the player's discard pile).

3. Forge Artifacts: Players spend gems at the 6 different Dwarf forges to make an artifacts. Once they have at least two artifacts of different colors, these may be made into a Rune Stone, which grants a special ability and a significant number of points.

The first player to 65 points triggers the end of the game, and then the player with the most points at the end of the round is the winner.

This Big Box Edition includes:
Rune Stones
Rune Stones: Nocturnal Creatures
Rune Stones: Enchanted Forest
Rune Stones: Queenie 1 – New Rune Stones
1 Surprise module
1 Printed plastic die
You have to collect keys of different colors by answering questions throughout the game, and at the end you check if you have the keys with the necessary colors to open the lock and get out.

The aim of the game is to get to know each other better. Each player chooses between two difficult choices. The other players try to guess your choice. In this fun, intriguing and unpredictable process, you will not only experience good emotions, but you will also get to know the other players better, and hear what others think about you and your decisions when making tricky choices. It's a great way to find out what your friends, colleagues and family think of you and how they perceive you. An important condition of the game is that the players must know each other at least a little.
The game is full of hypothetical, unrealistic situations - choices you will never have to make in real life. In each situation you will have only two choices: A or B. There are no more choices. Sometimes the choices offered may sound illogical or even frightening. However, this is just a game. We advise you to make your decision without trying to infer, add to or create additional context - choose only from what is offered in the context of the choice situation. Later, when you explain and comment on your choice, you will be able to make arguments, explain your thought process and share your impressions.
Please also note that you should make your choice based on what you are thinking at the time. It is quite likely that you will make a different choice the next day. This means that you can play this game with the same people more than once, and each time you will learn something new, both about others and about yourself or how others see you.

Bulls & Cows Light is a linguistic tabletop game based on the classic Bulls and Cows game, which has been around for over 100 years!

The game can be played by two players (the traditional way) or as a team game, either with a host leading the play or competing against two opposing teams.

This version comes in a minimalistic Light edition: it weighs less than 1 ounce and is compact enough to fit into a standard envelope, making it part of our MAILGAMEs series.

Designed for outdoor adventures and hikes, this waterproof game uses erasable markers, so it's reusable and perfect for multiple plays.

Objective: Classic number or word-guessing challenge where you try to figure out what your opponent (or the host) has chosen. If matching digits/letters are in the right places, they're called "bulls," but if they're in the wrong spots, they're "cows."

If the person (or team) who made the first move also cracks the secret number or word first, the other player gets an extra turn to try and even the score.

In addition, the publisher has prepared a separate PDF version of the game for fans of independent Print & Play.

—description from the designer
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue
Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Toodles is a colorful board game centered around rearranging candy-like tokens to match patterns on objective cards.

Players take turns moving a single token to an adjacent empty space, aiming to create color combinations that match their objective cards. Successfully completing a card awards victory points, and players replenish their hand with new cards after each turn.
