The game continues until the deck of objective cards runs out, and players tally their points based on completed tasks and finishing order. Variants introduce additional movement options and different starting setups for added variety. The player with the highest score at the end wins.
The first expansion for the Magyar népmesék társasjátékhoz. With 7 more stories.

Can't you stand anymore? Run to the bathroom now! Johnny Cagoni will be waiting for you… Throw a card, imitate the pedorrets, turn the knob and… pull! If Johnny jumps through the air you will have lost and, even worse, the stinky toilet paper will shoot at you. Everything will depend on the cards you choose!

A game about east German island Hiddensee. The title is not German but Low German (niederdeutsch) meaning the "The sweet and small country"
Players move with their pawn along the track around the island by roll of a die. According to the space landed on, they must answer questions, follow instructions like lose a turn or change the weather. If the weather changes, you must go back 6 spaces unless you have the appropriate equipment. The player who first reaches the goal space wins.

This is a quiz game with topic science. 
The level of questions is defined by different coulour of question cards. Answering green (microscope) cards gain 1 Point, orange (robot) cards gain 2 points and yellow (rocket) cards will gain 3 points.
In your turn you will move the relevant numer of spaces on the board by rolling two dices. Answer the question according to the field of landing. In case of joker field you can choose the question level. 
The game finishes after ones move on the board will end after the finish line. Then you have to answer a final question (level of your choice) for double points. If you miss the right answer you stay on the finish line and answer another question at your next turn. 
Onces a player answers the final question correctly, everyone is scoring his collected question cards and the winner is who collected the highest score.

Goal of the game is to get more victory points than the opponent by scoring more goals or catching the golden snitch. 
The quidditch playing field is put in the middle of the table. Every player gets 30 cards and takes 3 cards. Then every player selects one card and put it face down in front of them. The cards are turned face up at the same moment and compared to each other. 
The game ends when either all cards are played or when the searcher of a team is on the same place as the snitch.

Corunna 1809: Moore vs. Soult depicts the last battle of 1808 campaign, when General Moore rushed on Sahagun, threatening the flank of the dispersed French forces. The battle ended with the pitiful retreat to Corunna and embarkation of the decimated British army on Royal Navy ships. If Marshal Jean de Dieu Soult had defeated General Moore on Corunna's fields, British losses would have been catastrophic. Perhaps there would not have been future successes of sir Arthur Wellesley in coming years, because the British government would have withdrawal from Iberian Peninsula.

The French army has advantages in artillery and cavalry, but it is not easy to exploit them. Guns must be set on good positions and cavalry maneuvers in difficult terrain. The French player can dismount his dragoon regiments and he can use them as infantry in skirmish order.

The British player has in his disposal good infantry, which performs very well during fire combat. His goal is to stay on the battlefield long enough to gain additional victory points. Of course the French player must prevent it, but bad prepared, to early conducted attack can last the whole effort.

The game "Corunna 1809: Moore vs. Soult" uses most of the rules from the game Albuera 1811: Beresford vs. Soult .

—description from the publisher
Take Arsenal to the top of the league in Superclub with the players of season 2023/24!
New expansion for Superclub with the Chelsea players for season 2023/24
Expansion for Superclub with the Liverpool players of season 2023/24
Expansion for Superclub with Man. City players from season 2023/24
This expansion contains the manager kit to play with Liverpool in Superclub.

An 18-card game about laying cards and fighting dragons! In Dungeonlayer, players will take turns adding cards to their lairs and creating paths for their heroes to reach dragons, loots, and more. After all cards are played, set collection is scored and the player with the most riches wins!

—description from the designer

You are locked in a mysterious place and try to get out. You have to work together to solve some logic games and mathematical tasks which you'll find during your game. 
If you manage to solve all the games, you win!

This game consists of four different escape games for families:

     Secret of Orient Express
     Treasure Island
     Villa of Mr. Gold
     Ghost House

You are locked in a mysterious place and try to get out. You have to work together to solve some logic games and mathematical tasks which you'll find during your game. 
If you manage to solve all the games, you win!

This game consists of three 3 different escape games for families:

A Calamity of Witches is a coop-competitive card game where players squabble amongst themselves as members of the four Witch Houses, represented by their unique and powerful Sigils.

Save members of your House (cards of your own suit) by winning tricks with the highest card, or play the lowest card to trigger that card’s suit specific power and help turn the tides in your favor. Make deals to avoid the brunt of a House’s Attack, grow your power, and ensure the Calamity doesn’t consume you all.

Every Witch House must save a certain number of members of their House by the time the cards run out, otherwise the Calamity takes root in the losing houses, and complete corruption becomes inevitable - AKA everyone loses. If every House survives, then the House with the highest number of cards collected of their own Suit wins.

—description from the designer

The youngest player goes first by flicking the spinner uand seeing where it lands... Grab one or two pods and crack them on your own head or your opponents, of if you are lucky, miss a go. 
The winner is the person with the least amount of empty pods or who escaped the slime.

Each player chooses his colour and puts his 6 pieces in the corresponding corner (camp). He then has to move pieces over to the diagonally opposite corner. The player who gets all pieces over to the opposite corner first, will be the winner. 
In proper order, the players move one piece at a time straight forward, to one side or diagonally - you must not move a piece back towards your own camp. 
You may either move a piece to an adjacent free field or jump over one of your own pieces or a piece belonging to another player. You are allowed to jump over a piece, if the field behind it is free. You may take one jump or several jumps in the same move. You may stop jumping whenever you want to.
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

boardgame: Reef Project: New Crew Tiles Promo
description:
Small promo expansion for the Reef Project, first made available through the Tantrum House Season 11 crowdfunding campaign. It includes 6 new Crew Tiles:

2 Red Crew Tiles
2 Blue Crew Tiles
2 Green Crew Tiles

Activating the new Red Crew Tile allows the player to either gain 1 of each resource or trade in 1 of each resource for 4 points.

Activating the new Blue Crew Tile allows the player to immediately collect the benefit pictured on the oxygen tank tile beneath the player's diver marker.

Activating the new Green Crew Tile allows the player to lose 2 energy in order to gain 3 points.

Easy drinking and activitiy game, not useable for children. The player roll the dice and moves the spaces on th board. Follow the instuction on the landing field. All players have to move at least one total round. After they passed the start field again the players are trying to "finally home" for ending the game. If a player does not match exactly to this field "finally home" he has to run another round.

There is no winner, the game has the focus on having fun with the activities.

By means of this game, children can learn to count from one to ten and to recognize numbers on a dice, the corresponding number digits and the numbers shown by fingers. 
Therefore every child gets 3 boards with a specific number of objects. The youngest player starts and roles the dice. The dice shows symbols for fingers, digits and dices. According to the dice role, the child searches in the corresponding small pieces for their number. 
The first child who manages to add all the little pieces to their board wins.

This is a game for 2 players. The start player roles the 2 dices and both players try to calculate (adding, reducing, multiplying or dividing) the 2 numbers as fast as possible. The first player is allowed to put the number face down. 
The player who is the first one to have all numbers face down wins.

Void Bastion is a strategic survival and resource management board game set in a modular station buried deep in the Arctic. In a world where empires are powered by Arctic energy and secrets lie beneath ancient ice, players take on the role of station commanders, tasked with expanding, protecting, and maintaining humanity’s last scientific stronghold.

The station is constantly threatened by disasters: meteor strikes, internal sabotage, fire, water, electromagnetic anomalies, and personnel loss. Players must deploy a limited pool of crew tokens—each with unique abilities—to construct modules, fulfill contracts, activate technologies, and survive. Resources are tightly interwoven: energy, science, and food fuel everything, from shield activation to healing crew.

Each game unfolds over 14 rounds, divided into development, disaster, and recovery phases. Players must anticipate incoming threats using Disaster Warning cards, link modules for synergistic production, manage hospital overflow, and strategically sacrifice or protect their personnel.

In the end, only those who adapt, innovate, and care for both their infrastructure and their crew will survive the Void.

—description from the designer
Rules and components for a 2-player variant of the base game, published on IoGioco 8.

The thing descended from the night sky like a drop of resin, at once swift and sluggish. It shone a bright, cold, pale white, like that of the sickly Moon. For all they knew, it came thence. Where it landed, a silver-white bloom spread, and an unseen veil settled across the sleeping polis.

Corona charged at it, even before the first cry of madness rose.

—description from the publisher

Seizing the opening, Meleager was the third to strike, the argobronze spear piercing the thing’s AT field, searching for its heart… but there was no heart. And that was no AT field.

Yet Meleager did not recoil in fear, a true descendant of his father, the boar-slayer, but followed through with his ill-fated thrust, salvaging what he could, if not the wound then his life, and if that proved too much, at least the lives of his companions.

—description from the publisher

It moved in unpredictable, looping arcs, its dance as deadly as it was mesmerizing, its scythe-arms cutting through the stone of the soaring towers as readily as it rent bronze and flesh and bone. A chilling, gruesome display that froze the hearts of both mortals and Titans.

Porphyra did not freeze. And, living up to her namesake, she did not flinch either. She clashed with the thing that twice-over towered above her, and made it stop its onslaught, if only for a moment.

—description from the publisher

Rhodes claims it has created the Desiccated Sea in its never-ending search for natural resources, which feels oddly self-conscious for an expansionist empire. Few realize the hegemons have little choice in the matter: either they take the responsibility, projecting an image of devastating power, or they have to admit they are at the mercy of a capricious, uncaring world.

The Harbinger has survived in the Scylla’s stomach for three days and has been baptized in its digestive acids. The hunted has now become the hunter. A taste of things to come.

—description from the publisher

Time’s Woe is an enemy to all: to the Moirai who preordain fates, to paracausal Primordials preying on Delphi, to Time itself. And to you. Their name and their humanity – if they ever had one – are lost to aeons. All that remains is the contempt and the flight. 
Being a paracausal entity itself, Time’s Woe plays with fate, time and positioning, as reflected by the gear cards included in the set.

—description from the publisher

Things can always escalate. There are more things in heaven and earth, Odysseus, than are dreamt of in your philosophy. Now you will witness one of them.

There’s hatred in it, and there is anger. The prey wounded the creature. Severed its arm. Made it run. 
That was then.

Now...

The Blackbeak Pursuer can replace your normal Hermesian Pursuer in your campaigns – if you’d like to die.

Why are we here? Just to suffer?

I did not want this. Any of this. I just wanted to live my life. Why did it have to be me?

Descend into a shadowy underworld of twisting tunnels and toxic canals. Face perilous new mechanics, from room interactions and flooding hazards to debilitating afflictions and side quests lurking around every bend. With 50 room cards, 25 monsters, 10 maps, 2 bosses, and a sprawling dungeon board, you'll find endless surprises. Plus, recruit 3 new heroes—the Bugbear Cleric, Kenku Alchemist, and Fey Druid—each equipped with their own meeples and unique powers.

—description from the publisher

Venture into an ancient gothic stronghold ruled by cunning vampire lords. Each castle section offers distinct maps and eerie chambers where secrets and dangers abound. Choose your entry point, forge your path, and determine the ending you deserve. With 70 rooms, 20 monsters, 8 maps, 3 bosses, and more treasure than your rogue can carry, this expansion also introduces 3 heroes: the Goliath Warlord, Tabaxi Vampire Hunter, and Tiefling Trickster (Illusionist), alongside 12 hero meeples.

—description from the publisher

Embark on the ultimate solo adventure with A Rogue’s Tale, a sprawling campaign expansion that unites the entire Rogue Dungeon experience into a single, cohesive mega-game—greater than the sum of its parts. At its heart lies a massive 17"x11" world map, where you’ll travel seamlessly between cities bustling with intrigue, sleepy villages, treacherous wilderness, ancient tombs, flooded sewers, and imposing castles. Dive into these diverse locations, conquering their challenges and unearthing secrets, then return to the world map to continue your journey without missing a beat.

With a streamlined wilderness hex-crawl system, you'll encounter unique events across six terrain types and uncover quests that develop your hero and story. Whether you’re delving deep into dungeons, slinking through castles, or exploring sewers and forgotten tombs, every element connects to create a living, breathing world of adventure. Featuring 350+ cards for events, quests, loot, skills, and dungeons, A Rogue’s Tale brings tabletop RPG depth without the need for a DM—an epic, unified experience of exploration, combat, and story.

—description from the publisher
This expansion adds two cat tiles that get added to the bag. Players score 1 point if they have 1 cat tile and 3 points if they have both cat tiles.

Jurassic Crisis is a semi-competitive, PvPvE survival board game for 1–4 players, inspired by the video game ARK Survival Evolved. You must survive on an island inhabited by dinosaurs while mastering various mechanics – from resource management and positioning to character development, base building, and dinosaur taming.

boardgame: Valley of the Kings: Ultimate Edition
description:
When you die, you can take it with you!
Take on the role of Egyptian nobles at the time of the pharaohs, preparing for their death and burial in the Valley of the Kings. Using an innovative deck-building mechanic with a crumbling pyramid, players fill their tombs with food, canopic jars, statues, amulets and other treasures. The player who collects the most valuable artifacts in his or her tomb wins the game!

This boxed set consolidates ALL previous releases in the Valley of the Kings line plus an all new Archaeology expansion: 

     Valley of the Kings (also referred to as the base game)
     Afterlife
     Last Rites
     Necropolis Expansion
     NEW Archaeology

family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Digvijaya is a three-player game in which religious movements adhering to Hinduism, Islam, and other faiths compete to convert the population of India over the centuries from 1000 to 1700 CE. One player represents Hindu reformers, another Muslim missionaries, and a third represents the tradition of trying to harmonize the two faiths. Players build their movements through cards that represent the means by which religions spread, from holy wars and messianic rebellions to temple-building, mystic poetry, and faith-healing. Movements begin as small but zealous bands of believers, then expand to encompass rulers, farmers, traders and tribes, but a movement whose theology strays too far from its origins will eventually stagnate and disappear.

—description from the designer

Little Lords is a strategic territory-claiming board game where players lead their woodland civilizations in a battle for dominance. Using resource collection, tactical staking, and unique player abilities, you must expand your territory while fending off rival civilizations through attacks, stealing, and even charming their meeples to your side. With dynamic movement, powerful ability cards, and high-stakes endgame decisions, every turn is a test of strategy and adaptability—only the most cunning leader will claim victory!

In Little Lords, players take turns moving their meeples around the board, gathering resources, and placing towers to expand their territory. Each faction has unique abilities that give them an edge—whether it's the Black team’s superior combat skills, the Blue team’s unmatched speed, the Red team’s overwhelming numbers, or the Green team’s resourcefulness in building. Players can also use powerful ability cards to disrupt opponents, gain advantages, or shift the tide of battle in their favor.

The goal of the game is to claim the most land by securing territories with towers while defending against rival factions. Strategic placement, resource management, and tactical confrontations all play a key role in deciding the victor. With multiple paths to victory, every game is a fresh challenge that requires clever decision-making and adaptability.

Perfect for players who love strategy, area control, and a bit of lighthearted competition, Little Lords offers a dynamic and engaging experience with charming visuals and accessible mechanics. Will you rise as the true ruler of the land, or will your rivals outmaneuver you? Prepare your forces, stake your claim, and prove yourself worthy of the crown!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Murder Mystery in a Box: Podcast Murders
description:
A popular podcaster was murdered in his hotel room at the Forrest City True Crime Convention at the Bayside Hotel.

The goals for your investigation are: 1) determine the potential suspects for the murder of Justin Dyer, 2) discover the circumstances surrounding the murder, 3) identify the killer, and 4) uncover the motive of the murderer.

Start by reading the assignment letter to learn specifics of the case and then begin examining all the contents found inside the box.

When you have finished your investigation and are ready to reveal the solution and conclusion of the mystery, you will need to go to the publisher's website. There is no hint system online so you will only need internet access for the solution.

boardgame: Murder Mystery in a Box: Murder at the Freak Show

When you have finished your investigation and are ready to reveal the solution and conclusion of the mystery, you will need to go to the publisher's website. There is no hint system online so you will only need internet access for the solution.

boardgame: Murder Mystery in a Box: Quarantine
description:
Infected persons have been quarantined in a special facility to control the spread of the disease. One of those persons was murdered while in quarantine under the supervision of the CDC.

Your role will be to: 1) Trace the transmission of the virus from one person to the next to figure out how each person was infected and by whom, 2) Investigate the murder of the patient in quarantine and identify the killer, and 3) Uncover the motive for the killing.

Start by reading the assignment letter to learn specifics of the case and then begin examining all the contents found inside the box.

When you have finished your investigation and are ready to reveal the solution and conclusion of the mystery, you will need to go to the publisher's website. There is no hint system online so you will only need internet access for the solution.

boardgame: Murder Mystery in a Box: A Christmas Murder (John Lee)
description:
John Lee dressed as Santa Claus was found murdered in the woods behind the house of the mayor of Forrest City. The goals of your investigation: 1) determine the facts and circumstances relating to the killing of the victim, 2) uncover the identity of the killer, and 3) determine the motive for the murder.

Start by reading the assignment letter to learn specifics of the case and then begin examining all the contents found inside the box.

When you have finished your investigation and are ready to reveal the solution and conclusion of the mystery, you will need to go to the publisher's website. There is no hint system online so you will only need internet access for the solution.

description: A new official Fan created Bonus-Legend for The Legends of Andor. Play as the magical heroes in a new legend in the land of Andor.

Each round, new cards add a new game condition.
At the end of each round, this condition (bonus or malus) will be kept by the player with the highest or lowest score.

—description from the designer

Travel through six exciting and diverse locations filling them with colors! Visit the hilly village of small dwellers, feel the breathtaking freedom in the land of flying islands, ride along the railways of a technological city, open a treasure chest in the pirate land, stroll through medieval quarters, or find a lamp with a genie in the magical east!

Each turn, you will guess which colored balls will roll out of the wonderful color mill. Use their colors to paint areas of your board. Try to make successful predictions, effectively choose areas to paint and skillfully apply effects to collect more coins than your opponents and finish this colorful journey as a winner!

—description from the publisher

The Mexican holiday Day of the Dead is the one time of the year that the veil between the world of the living and the world of the dead is the thinnest. At this carnival, all are welcome: take your neighbor’s hand (skeletal as it might be) and join the dance!

Coordinate the dancers’ movements by revealing cards laid out in a circle and applying their effects. Create chain reactions by moving cards or removing them from the circle altogether in order to collect even more cards. But be careful and try to keep track of the symbols hidden on the back of cards: while some dancers might grant you a token of good luck, others could very well jinx you!

Create a circle of face-down cards in the middle of the table, and choose a player to start. Then, each player takes turns revealing a card from the circle, applying its effect (optional), and resolving any chain reactions (if any). The turn then passes to the next player in a clockwise order.

The game ends when there are no more cards left in the circle. Players then turn their cards face up and proceed to score. Each light skull is worth 1 victory point, while each dark skull is a penalty point. The player with the highest number of victory points wins!

—description from the designer

Compete to win in this fun, fast game of shifting and speed-stacking!
Beginning with all the pieces in their starting setup on the stand, flip a card and race to match the image shown, using only one hand and grabbing only one piece at a time!
Shift the pieces from pillar to pillar, grab the Trophy from the center, and claim Sweet Victory!

—description from the designer

Expand the award-winning game Earth with the Animal Kingdom expansion, which introduces 36 new animals, each with powerful asymmetrical abilities that change how you build your tableau. Whenever you use a leaf, your chosen animals activate, generating resources, triggering abilities, or unlocking new scoring opportunities. At the end of each round, a global animal ability activates for all players, providing everyone with an opportunity to plan ahead to leverage its effects.

No two animals play the same. Each one interacts with your tableau in a unique way, challenging you to shape your ecosystem to maximize their potential. Animal Kingdom brings your tableau roaring to life with animeeples that roam your tableau, interacting with the flora and terrain you've cultivated.

From the burrowing pocket gopher to the soaring golden eagle, each animal breathes new life into Earth.

—description from the designer

In this game, Penguins and Ice Bears meet for the very first time to hunt red Rubies, because, you know,they’ve totally never crossed paths before thanks to the whole North Pole vs South Pole thing.
But don’t overthink it. They meet, and frankly, I have no idea how it happened either. Also, the Penguins have a bit of a thing for blowing up parts of the caves, because what’s a good game without a little destruction, right?

But fear not! They always manage to escape just in time.
No animals were harmed in the making of this game.

Everyone will leave with Rubies in the end but only one can win this game!

—description from the designer

description: Geralt on Roach is an exclusive playable character with a player board and a plastic miniature.

Rock beats Scissors, Scissors beat Paper and Paper beats Rock. 
But in the end, be prepared for surprises, as only the right 
number of wins matters.

Play your cards strategically, because winning doesn't always matter. Prepare for exciting rounds in which you only score points by winning zero, two or five matches.

Zero Two Five is a card game in which your objective is to win 0, 2 or 5 tricks(the numbers represented by Rock, Scissors and Paper respectively). The first player to get three score tokens or the player who gets the most points is the winner.

—description from the publisher

레드룸 is a fierce survival game in a mysterious room. Survive to the end and become the last one.
Or find all the numbers in the red room to unlock the secret and escape!

Players move to the room they want to start from among the border rooms. Players move cross-sectionally on their turns, instantly flipping the tiles where they moved and applying the effects of the room.

Players can move around the room on their turn, and use up to two supply cards. When done, pull out a new card.

If the life token goes to zero, you lose. 
The one who survives to the end wins. 
The player who arrives in the last closed room and opens the room wins. (*The first to know all room numbers) 
If you get all the closed room numbers right, that player wins.

Underspire is a campaign expansion for Massive Darkness: Dungeons of Shadowreach. This new campaign expansion will include powerful new items, brand-new areas to explore, and new enemies to face off against, all in this epic campaign!

Like the previous Massive Darkness 2: Heavenfall expansion, Underspire is an expansion that provides all the necessary rules and components to play in Campaign Mode. It consists of 14 Quests that form a single ongoing campaign with multiple branching quests. Lightbringers will battle through countless foes and fulfill multiple objectives, accumulating experience, skills, and equipment as they go. Their actions and choices at the end of each Quest will have a huge impact on the fate of Shadowreach!

—description from the publisher

Who can defeat the dragon for the big score?

The first edition was so much fun that we just had to draw more maps!

In this quick-playing doodle game, 1–4 players simultaneously draw paths through a dangerous dungeon. Draw cards, draw a path through your dungeon, and rack up points! Collect loot and fight monsters . . . or run away screaming!

Get ready to draw, drink, and sabotage your way to victory!
Ink: The Drinking Game is a chaotic party showdown where art meets deception—and the more you drink, the worse your drawings get (and the funnier they become).

Split into teams, roll the dice, and face off in a sketch battle like no other. One player draws real clues, the other tries to throw everyone off—both on the same canvas! Will your team guess the answer before the Saboteur strikes? Or will the other side swoop in and steal the glory?

Throw in some rock-paper-scissors, strategic steals, wild Fate Cards, and tipsy logic, and you’ve got the perfect recipe for unpredictable laughs, loud accusations, and legendary game nights.

Can you stay sharp as your brain gets blurry? Welcome to Ink.

—description from the designer

Dwarven Kibbutz is a strategic semi-cooperative eurogame for 2–5 players (best at 4), where players take on the role of dwarven heirs rebuilding a fallen kingdom — while secretly competing for the throne.

Players must develop land, manage specialized workers, manipulate the market, and form temporary alliances to ensure the survival of their shared society. But while the community must thrive to avoid collapse, only one heir will ultimately claim the crown… unless none do.

What makes Dwarven Kibbutz stand out?

A Semi-Coop Built for Tension and Resilience
    •    Dual Traitor System
The game supports two types of betrayal:
    •    A hidden traitor may be dealt at the start, playing in secret from turn one.
    •    Alternatively, any player may openly betray the group mid-game by accepting a loan from Malrik, switching sides, and pursuing a personal Usurper victory.
    •    No Kingmaker Problem
Players who fall behind can change their win condition. There’s always a way back into the game — no need to play just to help or hinder others.
    •    True Cooperative Stakes
The Prosperity Tracker reflects the group’s stability. If it collapses to -3 and isn’t recovered, a popular revolt ends the game — and everyone loses, including Malrik.
No one rules a fallen kingdom.
    •    A Dynamic Market with Political Impact
Players influence supply and prices directly. Control the Market Tile to adjust values or trigger a crash — affecting everyone’s strategy and the realm’s prosperity.
    •    Alliances of Convenience
Temporary alliances allow shared control over tiles — but may lead to conflict, overpopulation, or internal bidding wars. Collaboration is always strategic, never guaranteed.

Victory in Three Layers

     Group Survival: Did Prosperity collapse? If so, the game ends and no one wins.
     Factional Success: If the realm endures, determine which faction prevailed — Balin’s loyalists or Malrik’s rebels.
     Individual Victory: Among the winning faction, the player with the highest VP becomes ruler of the kibbutz.


Dwarven Kibbutz combines euro-style precision with social tension and emergent storytelling. It’s a game of negotiation, risk management, strategic betrayal, and moral compromise — where winning means surviving first, and ruling second.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Jump Away! The Platformer Card Game
description:
Jump Away! is a cooperative game where players unite to help a brave knight escape the dragon and return to his kingdom. By playing platform cards and using special abilities, players work together to build the fastest and safest path. But beware—every mistake brings the knight closer to the dragon's hungry jaws. Will the knight make it home in time, or will the challenges prove too much?

The goal of the game: collect four golden keys before the dragon catches the knight. Each turn, players lay out platform cards for the knight to jump on. Each platform brings either a bonus or a complication.

Work together to avoid falls and hopeless situations. Collect energy to use unique abilities. Use the advantages of the platforms to escape the dragon's jaws.

In Regroup! Panda Crew you organize panda painters to attack and defend from every other player. Only one panda captain will emerge victorious from the painting competition!

Each turn you choose a card and place it over another card on your grid seeking to match offensive/defensive melee or ranged pandas. Some cards have map pieces and if you arrange them in a specific pattern, you gain a bonus card that is stronger.

Once all the players have placed and organized their card, the round ends and the combat begins. Each player deals or absorbs damage (melee or ranged) based on the connected pandas of the same type. The last player standing, wins!

—description from the publisher

Galice is the application of VR Parton's Alice Chess to the game of Go.

The game is played on two 7x7 boards.

On his turn, the player either (a) passes, or (b) swaps a stone between boards and then drops a new stone. The remaining rules follow mostly those of Go.

The rules permit suicides, which can be useful when there is no better move left.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Zarumilla: The Peru Invasion of Ecuador 1941
description:
This little known military offensive in July 1941 involved the Peruian army attacking Ecuador over a long standing border dispute. The attack was a minature "blitzkrieg" involving Czech 38 tanks, US and Italian aircraft against an unprepared Ecuador (think, the destruction of the German Army Group Center in 1944). While WW2 had nothing to do with it, the Peruian use of paratroops even caught the eye of Hitler in their accolades. The Ecuador troops fought well against infantry but when combined with tanks and aircraft bombing, most fled. The attack lasted just a week. While the game is easy, it does require planning with its unique system where units that move and attack are weaker than those who have not moved before attacking.

Game scale is 1-1.5 mileshex with daily turns. Units mostly company or platoon. 1 11x17 inch map, 280 counters.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Monster Hunter World: The Board Game – Kirin
description:
An extremely rare sight, Kirin is said to envelope itself in pure electricity when provoked. Prepare for a thunderous challenge against this elder dragon — it’s been known to call lightning down from the sky!

With the Kirin expansion, you’ll get this highly-prized elder dragon and all its quests, rare weapons, and powerful equipment-crafting options. That includes:

     New campaign quests
     New arena quests for single-session boss battles
     New choice-driven narrative


Every craftable Kirin weapon type, for all 14 hunters, from the video game
Plus, because Kirin is an elder dragon with a 5-star quest level, you’ll have a whole new way to win your Monster Hunter: World campaign! Will your party be the ones to conquer this elusive creature?

—description from the publisher

The annual Coconut Festival grandly opens on the tropical paradise of Coconut Island, so it's time to compete for coconut treasures while trying not to have them blow up in your face.

To start Coco Boom, each player gets a hand of 6-8 cards depending on the player count, then set three cards aside, and lay the other cards face up. Cards are green cocos ($300), perfume cocos ($500), golden cocos ($1,000) or bomb cocos (-$500). The round's start player chooses two cards in hand, while everyone else chooses one, after which all of these cards are shuffled together. The start player then guesses a type of coco (green/perfume/gold), then reveals the top card. If they're wrong, shuffle all revealed cards into the deck, then pass it to the next player. If they're right, they can stop and keep all revealed cards or make another guess and reveal another card. If you have two revealed cards in a turn, they're each worth $100 more than face value; with 3+ revealed cards in a turn, they're each worth $200 more.

If you reveal a bomb, all players race to place their hand on the card, with the last player taking it. Shuffle all correctly-guessed cards into the deck, and pass it to the next player. Once all the cards have been claimed, start a new round, with the starting player shifting around the table.

When the starting player has fewer than two cards in hand or a player has claimed cocos worth $3,000+, the game ends, and whoever has the most coco cash wins.

boardgame: Monster Hunter World: The Board Game – Picking Bones
description:
Shield your eyes from the stunning flash attacks of Tzitzi-Ya-Ku. Withstand the paralysing strikes of the serpent-like Great Girros. And dodge the rolling ball of pain that is bone-studded Radobaan!

Like the monster expansions some of you may be familiar with from Iceborne, Picking Bones introduces:

     3 brand new XL and L monsters
     15 new campaign quests
     9 new arena quests for single-session boss battles against any of the monsters, at any level, with any of the 14 hunters
     New choice-driven quest narrative set in the Coral Highlands and Rotten Vale
     Every craftable weapon type for each monster, for all 14 hunters, from the video game

boardgame: The Nine Sons of The Dragon
description:
As the old saying goes: A dragon have nine sons, but each one is different. They have different natures. There is a rumor in the clan that the one who tames the most dragon sons will gain the power of the dragon and lead the clan to prosperity. But all this is by no means easy. Although some dragon sons are docile and approachable, there are also fierce and difficult to tame, eccentric ones that need to be catered to and managed slowly. Now that the nine dragon sons have appeared in the world, as a candidate for the clan leader, are you ready?

The Nine Sons of The Dragon is a chess game that supports 2 players and uses mancala as the main mechanism. In this game, both players take turns to place or move chess pieces, and tame the dragon card by stacking 3 chess pieces in the same grid. Reasonably use the opponent's chess pieces and the characteristics of the dragon, and the player who gets more points will become the winner.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
I designed this game to flip the classic asteroid apocalypse on its head - the real fight happens long before the asteroid hits.

The game takes place in an alternate 2126, where sci-fi weaponry merges with modern technology and age-old political maneuvering shapes the struggle for survival.

Engaging mechanics keep you immersed from start to finish - beginning with asteroid discovery and bureaucratic roadblocks, escalating to social upheaval and the race to fund orbital weaponry and destroy the asteroid.

Doomsday manager offers a tough and challenging experience, but with simple rules and a quick setup. It keeps players engaged from start to finish, evolving with dynamic progression - unlocking new features and changing gameplay as you go.

—description from the designer

One alphabet. Six words. Sounds easy. Good luck!

Bunce, English; informal

     A delightful word game suitable for all ages; noun
     A deck of cards with letters & values from which a hand is drawn and the best word played; adjective
     A bonus for playing all the cards from your hand; noun


A crosswords-inspired word game using cards. Using the English alphabet, each player has a deck of 27 cards with each card having a letter and value with one "wild" card. Each round a player will draw a specified number of cards. From that draw the player will try to create a word from the previous player's word.  Points are scored from the cards in the new word.  Unused cards from the draw are counted against your score and discarded. Letters on the table not used in the word scored are discarded leaving only one word on the table at a time. After the players have drawn all the cards from their deck the player with the highest score is the winner.

—description from the designer

After exploring the Avian Archipelago, our festive crew of panda pirates sail back to celebrate pandapalooza with other aspiring panda initiates who practice their party and pirate skills to join the legendary crew.

In this expansion, you can find more minigames and a new Treasure Map board that's not only a different shape, but also offers a different feel than the board in the base game, increasing player interaction and decision-making while reducing the luck factor in the fourth phase (Treasure hunt).

Some key differences:

You must spend coins to buy Chest tokens in numbered spaces.
Benefits in the position track offer a player choice: Gain many coins (even buying a Chest in 1st place) OR claim fewer coins plus a token that allows you to roll an additional die on future movements to advance more.
The routes are longer, there’s more variety of effects in the spaces, and the sorting of numbered spaces is different from the base game board.
Longer routes reward you with Item cards that you can use in this phase to interact with other players… now you can force another player to reroll or steal coins from their supply.

—description from the publisher
Rules for Vampires, Gnomes, and Chaos Dwarf teams, and more star player stats.  From Spike magazines 16, 17, and 18.

By adding the included -1 cards to the deck, both the bidding and the trick-taking become more challenging.

The biggest changes are to the setup of Magic Trick. Use the number of suits in the base game rules. Then, shuffle the -1s of the chosen suits in with the playing cards from Magic Trick. Then set one random card of each suit aside without looking at the front. Then proceed as normal.

The cards make gameplay and scoring more challenging. When sorting the cards for your neighbor, remember that -1 is less than 0. Otherwise, gameplay is the same. Each captured -1 is worth minus one point. If a player bid with a -1 card (i.e. if that is the card up their sleeve), they will miss their bid, since it is not possible to take negative tricks. Do not use the Prestige Bonus when playing this way.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Kansas City: The Tricks and Trains Expansion
description:
There are two mini expansions for Kansas City included. The first (“Set One”) are new, asymmetric scoring variants. The second set (“Set Two”) are special powers.

This mini-expansion was designed for the Tricks & Trains event in 2025.

—description from the designer

This booklet contains several pages devoted to the mecha in the Japanese Battletech game.

The book contains information on the Wasp, Stinger, Locust, Phoenix Hawk, Shadow Hawk, Griffin, Marauder, Archer, Wolverine, Battlemaster, Thunderbolt, Rifleman, Crusader, and Warhammer.

Each robot that is covered has diagrams, history, and data for it.

Sweeten the fate of the bravest heroes with this expansion to Apothebakery. The Master Alchemist provides upgraded acrylic and wooden components for the base game, as well as two micro expansions, Mana Springs, and Rainbow Bakes.

Mana Springs - Dive into mystical rejuvenation with the Mana Springs micro expansion! Inside, find five Mana Spring locations to shuffle into your Wilderness card deck. Visit these magical springs in the landscape to recharge your Mana reserves on your Alchemy Station!

Rainbow Bakes - Inside this packet, you'll find four enchanting Rainbow Bakes ready to be whisked into your Diagnosis deck. These magical cakes can transform into any color icing, making them the perfect bake to help you complete your Secret Objectives!

Become the Vice Archmage of the Empire with the help of Dragons, Dragon Riders, and the additional characters of The Archmage and The Emperor, to enjoy a new enhanced Imperial Twing experience.

In the Imperial Twing fantasy card game, you are a witch, wizard, mage, sorceress, or other character capable of controlling the magical energy of The Orn and you are striving to become the new Vice-Archmage of the Empire.

Use and develop your spells, enjoy your steadily growing entourage of grim looking creatures and battle your competitors with everything you have, all the while hoping that the Orn is on your side. Collect, steal, or conjure the magical objects you need. Most importantly, get a hold of the Magical Teddy of Agony.

The Dragon's Demand expansion for Imperial Twing adds new game elements, including powerful Dragons, additional and changed rules, asymmetric play and more.

—description from the designer

boardgame: AracKhan Wars: The Song of Destiny Core-Box
description:
In AracKhan Wars, players lead a powerful faction and its heroes, represented by a compact 23-card deck, and face off against opponents on the battlefields of AracKhan. Its unique gameplay blends bluffing, with cards played face-down each round, and area control, where cards primarily interact with adjacent ones. The system is simple and intuitive. The Song of Destiny is a stand alone game and does not require any of AracKhan Wars modules to  be played.

The players select an adventure to play, which provides the narrative background, a specific ruleset, objectives, & victory condition(s). Each player then choose a 23 card faction deck, using a pre-built deck or constructing their own deck, according to rules, advice, & deck-lists provided. The game lasts 9 rounds (8 rounds in 2v2), and each player starts the game with a hand of 7 cards (9 in 2v2).

The Song of Destiny consists of the Prologue/Core box, the Song of Destiny II (4 new factions), the Song of Destiny III (4 new factions), and some accessories.

—description from the publisher

Cosmic Conquest is a fast paced memory and strategy card game, which is easy to learn and quick to play.

In deepest space a wormhole has opened up, and is pulling through unsuspecting characters from across the universe… Aliens… Humans… and Robots.

These three opposing factions are now in a race to colonise 9 new planets, and seal their place as the dominant force, while one maverick tries to sabotage their efforts, and create galactic peace among the warring forces.

—description from the designer

A dexterity game where you use plastic catapults to fire plastic poops into a toilet.

—user summary

The game can be played by one or more people. Each person is assigned a different color and quantity of stool, and the distance between the toilet and the transmitter can be freely placed. The farther this distance is, the more difficult it is. Win the game.

—description from the publisher

Played in a HexHex board of size 10, starting with a pattern of neutral stones already in place.

A Go-like game where neutral stones are movable and remove liberties of both player's groups.

On his turn the player either (a) slides a neutral stone over a line of empty hexes, and then drops a friendly stone, (b) drops a friendly stone, or (c) passes.

After two passes the game ends, winning the player with more stones plus controlled territory (including neutral stones).

In the magical world of Black Clover: Star Festival, players join the annual Star Festival, a high-stakes competition where the Magic Knight squads compete to prove their valor and skill. Each player steps into the role of a squad leader, commanding their team through challenges across bustling cities, serene forests, and ancient ruins. From quests to gathering favors and recruiting knights, every decision shapes your squad's journey in a quest for stars—the ultimate symbol of victory. As rivalries grow and strategies are tested, only the most strategic squad will rise as champions of the festival.

A customizable worker placement game, Black Clover: Star Festival has you placing customizable squads in different locations for both location and worker actions. Gain the most stars to become the ultimate champion of the festival.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Promo boss card "Porkusdoom" included as a stretch goal for the Super Boss Monster Kickstarter.

This boss allows the player to draw and place 2 hero tokens anywhere on the map, killing the monsters on that tile.

It also allows the player to score 7 extra points at the end of the game if there is a band of 5 monster tokens anywhere on the map.

boardgame: Dungeon Kart: Super! Promo Spell Card
description:
This promo card is a stretch goal for the Super Boss Monster Kickstarter campaign.

This spell allows the player to search the spell decks to find and hold onto a Zombie, Bomb, Firebolt, Haste, and Payday spell.  The player cannot gain another spell until all of these have been used.

This promo card is a stretch goal from the Super Boss Monster Kickstarter campaign and was given out at GenCon 2024.

This career card lets you spend three time tokens to gain two money tokens.  Once promoted, three time tokens will get you three money tokens and move you up on the happiness track.

boardgame: End of Empire: Lord Dunmore's War,  1774
description:
End of Empire: Lord Dunmore's War, 1774 is an Strategic level board-wargame expansion for owners of End of Empire: 1744-1782

A small package in a zip-lock , which contains the counters for the Lord Dunmore's War bonus scenario and replacement counters for the base game.

Components:

    28 replacement counters
    6 counters for the bonus scenario
    1 additional unit counter for the base game 
    5 additional markers
    2 sheets of rules and replacement instructions
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
It's time to hit the trail! Leave the campfire behind, and make your favourite S'mores recipes with the ingredients at hand. Every round you'll roll dice and have a different selection of marshmallows, chocolate, and graham crackers, and you'll have to draw them on your page and try to make the best recipes. Fill up rows and gain valuable bonuses, and roast your opponents by creating the most spectacular S'mores. Fill your sheet as best you can, or you might get burned by the end of the game!

Overview
S'mores Galore: Roast and Write is a roll and write game where each round, dice are rolled, giving all players a number of marshmallow, chocolate, and graham cracker symbols to write on their sheet. Players will place them trying to complete the recipes on display for points at the end of the game. Use the ingredients in another recipe for even more points. Complete rows and columns for special bonus and wild ingredients you can add to your page. At the end of the game, the player with the highest score wins.

—description from the designer

The Mud and Blood Mini-expansion adds 18 cards to the box, for Verdun trick-taking card game.

This module contains:

     4 Dual-sided Mud'n'Blood Reference cards (one for each player), both in English, German and French (total of 12 cards).
     Tactical cards (2 copies) "Weather Changes" which cancels the Mud and Blood card in play.


Module rules are explained on the main rulebook of Verdun trick-taking card game.

Area 51 Pick Up is a fast-paced, push-your-luck card game where players take on the role of Bun-Aliens (adorable alien bunnies) attempting to rebuild their spaceship and escape from Area 51—all while dodging agents determined to capture them!

Gameplay Overview:
On their turn, players draw cards from the pile, aiming to collect all six spaceship pieces needed for escape. However, every draw presents a risk—hidden among the cards are agents and hazards that could jeopardize the mission! Players must decide whether to push their luck for more rewards or play it safe to secure their progress.

Winning the Game:
To win, players must complete the spaceship by assembling all six numbered pieces while ensuring at least one Bun-Alien remains free by the end of the final turn.

The game is lost if:

All Bun-Aliens are captured by agents.

The spaceship is incomplete when the final turn ends.

Turn Structure:
Draw Cards – Players draw cards one at a time, stopping at five cards or choosing to stop earlier to avoid risks.

Defend Against Agents – If an agent or a Raccoonnaissance Agent appears, players must discard specific cards (such as Stun Buns or secured ship pieces) to continue.

Use Equipment – Players can use special equipment to manipulate the deck, avoid dangers, or cancel effects.

Secure Ship Pieces – Gather and assemble spaceship parts, but beware—each secured piece adds more agents to the deck, increasing the challenge.

End of Turn – Players manage their inventory, discard excess equipment, and return certain cards to their respective piles.

Player Count and Turn Limits:
Solo Mode: 8 turns

2 Players: 4 turns each

3-4 Players: 3 turns each

5-7 Players: 2 turns each

Area 51 Pick Up blends cooperative strategy with press-your-luck mechanics, making every decision crucial. Will you risk it all for one more card, or will you play it safe and secure your escape?

—description from the designer

This is a Chutes and Ladders style game with an 18 1/2 X 18 1/2 board. Movement is determined by die roll, and two dice are included. Players move up, sideways, or diagonally through the 29 x 29 grid, seeking cards representing Food, Supplies, or Guides.

Emerald Echoes is a narrative puzzle adventure featuring beautifully-illustrated puzzles and tactile artifacts. Through the eyes of a historical researcher, players uncover the story of a legendary alchemist and her companions. Told over four chapters, Emerald Echoes is a standalone sequel to The Emerald Flame.

Within each chapter, there is a collection of unlabeled items, along with a series of green puzzle folders labeled numerically in the order they should be solved (1.1, 1.2, 1.3, and so on). There are thirteen puzzle folders in total. There are also some blue folders that do not contain any puzzles but should be opened as part of the same numerical sequence to reveal more pieces of the story.

Each green folder contains at least one written document to investigate — this should be used as a jumping off point as you piece together which items are needed to solve the puzzle. Within each puzzle folder is also a pocket with a card labeled “Analysis,” featuring incremental hints, as well as the solution, for each puzzle.

—description from the designer

5 years after the release of the geopolitical simulator Epoca Moderna (2020), the author Gola Pierpaolo creates the board game The Lobbyist. The title, set in the present day, sees players sit at the head of powerful Lobbies that corrupt prominent figures of today's society by hiring them through Cryptocurrencies, then acquiring influence and manipulating the world market in the planisphere, influencing the states. The gameplay is enriched by 20 espionage tiles and additional objectives during and at the end of the game. The game ends when the first player takes control of 10 sovereign states. 100 nations faithfully reproduced with governments, religions and morals, 50 lobbyists to increase the longevity of the always different games, make The Lobbyist one of a kind!

It's been hundreds of years since a magical rift opened in the firmament. This rift contains infinite power, but access is limited and dangerous. Great speed and cunning will be required to harvest as much energy as possible. For this reason, the gods have sent their creatures to gather the maximum power, become a supreme deity, and dethrone Zeus. You must keep your creatures alive while you strive to become the supreme deity of Olympus. To achieve your goal, you will have to accumulate the greatest amount of power or demonstrate your superiority in combat by winning trophies.
Dominari is an evolving strategy card game for 2 to 4 players that's suitable for both the whole family and even for those with a more demanding gaming experience, as rules can be added for a more advanced experience.

Each player embodies a Greek god with a bestiary of mythological animals at their disposal. Their bestiary allows them to hunt other enemy animals to steal or eliminate other enemy beasts, or try to be the smartest and fastest to acquire magical powers without being seen.

You can win the game in several ways:

     By collecting numbered magical powers and having the highest score.
     By eliminating opposing creatures.
     By eliminating the player from the game.


To acquire magical powers, you must secretly place one of your creatures in one of the four locations where those powers are located. Once each player has placed two mythological animals, all the animals will be revealed to determine who wins the power this turn and which beasts survive.

Everything counts at the end of the game: contracts, collections, magical powers, living creatures, killed creatures.
EVERYTHING COUNTS toward WINNING the game. BUT YOU CAN LOSE EVERYTHING if someone manages to kill 3 enemy animals. Because whoever collects 3 trophies in combat immediately wins the game.

The game can also be a deck-buster, allowing players to implement different play strategies and adapt to their opponents.

—description from the publisher

Bunnington Valley invites players into a light-hearted, charming world where they embody crafty and adventurous bunnies on a mission to collect the most valuable harvest from the local farms.
The journey is filled with excitement and challenges as players travel from farm to farm, dodging the watchful eyes of farmers and their ever-alert farm dogs. Each turn as a bunny “traveler” presents a mix of strategy and surprise, with opportunities to gather precious Harvest Items and deliver them safely to their Cellar. But beware—every step of the journey is fraught with risks like Farm Dog chases, tricky Constables, and unexpected twists.

Over 100+ Whimsical Large Playing Cards help players on their fun-filled and exciting journey as players travel from farm to farm, dodging the watchful eyes of farmers and their ever-alert farm dogs. While easy to learn, each turn can present a mix of strategy and surprise. But beware—every step of the journey is fraught with risks.

Helper Cards offer opportunities to outsmart opponents by snatching cards or shielding against threats, while Secret Tunnels provide daring escape routes. Dealing with the Farmer’s Constables can be tricky, and the chaos of Farm Dog chases can send players scrambling backward, creating moments of laughter and clever maneuvering. Perfect for friends and families, the game blends luck, strategy, and humor in a whimsical competition, so hop into the fun and may the best bunny win!

The objective of the game is to be the player that has the most “Harvest” points based on the type and quantity of the various harvest items they have been able to bring safely back to their cellars, when the Farm Cards run out and the game comes to an end. Harvest Points are earned whenever you are able to make it back home to your farm and deposit the harvest you were able to “acquire’ in your trip. Harvest Points are gained from six types of Fruit and Vegetable Harvest Item Cards: Apples, Strawberries, Blueberries, Carrots, Potatoes, and Cabbages.

—description from the publisher

Wing Commander is a compendium of additions and expansions for the Blood Red Skies game. Inside you’ll find advice for beginners, advice for designing your own scenarios, more rules to expand MiG Alley games, a short carrier war campaign, and a longer more expansive campaign that allows for narrative progression. Additionally, complete lists of plane statistics and their availability throughout the war are provided. Finally, the games author, Andy Chambers, provides full errata and answers to the most frequently asked questions.

A copy of the Blood Red Skies supplement Air Strike! is needed to use this book.

—description from the publisher

Fiends and Light is a 6 in 1 electronic board game. The game board has 4 different platforms which can move independently or together depending on the option that the player chooses. This board mechanics gives rise to different forms of gameplay. There are 6 different games to play on Fiends and Light - a strategy game, a roleplaying game, 2 casual games and 2 resource manipulation games.

Genesis is a strategy game, where the Fiends faction faces off against the Light faction in the Genesis War. Each player has 3 races, either Dragons, Orcs and Demons, or Angels, Animalia and Elementals. The races are represented by cards. Each card has a spell, ability, skill and racial effect. The health and attack power of the cards sum up to 18 points. Each race is strong against another race, but weak against another race.

In Genesis on the game board, players have to place marble units representing the race onto the platform, and use the controller to move the platforms to gain a competitive edge against the opponents. Units face off each other on the bridge and beyond. When facing off, players have to roll dice, use the spells skills and abilities of the units, or choose to engage in defence instead of offence. The first unit to reach the opposing end of the game board wins, or that a deck of race cards gets exhausted.

Guidance is a roleplaying game. Players have to place pious believers in the gods Lovelyn from Light and Kylvaron from Fiends to go to the opposing enemy's battlefields to bring back the souls of the fallen warriors. Pious believers, when embarking on their journey, will gain heroic feats necessary to overcome the scenarios on the bridge, after which they have to listen to the hints of the gods to engineer a movement on the platforms using the controllers, to get the results that Lovelyn and Kylvaron want, before the pious believers are allowed to bring back the souls of the fallen warriors.

In Chase the Livestock, Demon Lord Azazeel is hungry and orders his minions to cook food to satiate his appetite. The minions then go onto the game board to chase after livestock. The livestock are represented by units with special abilities to run away from the minions by the player using the controller to control the platforms to move the livestock away from the minions, gaining a competitive advantage in escaping.

In MasterChef, Queen Seraphina thirsts for the perfect recipe. She orders her minions to look for food ingredients. Food ingredients are sentient and does not wish to be cooked. Using the platforms, players will play a mini-Sudoku game to clear the platforms of the numbers representing the ingredients.

Color Abacus uses a proprietary design of an abacus stand to play the game. The abacus stand is used to record the color and number of resources to gather in the initial phase of the game. There are a total of 10 different rows of resources. After gathering the resources, players will have to play a numbers stacking game, similar to UNO, where the color and number of the resources matter in deciding who cops out of the round of numbers stacking.

boardgame: Cold Case VR: The Case of the Fatal Follower
description:
Cold Case VR is a new and immersive way to experience murder mystery games - step inside the story and hunt the killer like never before.

Dust off the files from this unsolved case and get justice for Hailey Rose, a promising, young content creator who may have attracted the wrong attention online. Examine the crime scene and other key locations to look for clues in 360° virtual reality. Consult maps, statements, newspaper reports and other physical evidence. Take the hunt online to track the killer through social media and hack into accounts to gather evidence. Dive into an immersive story and uncover the truth with up to 3 hours of gameplay for 1 or more players.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pirates of the High Teas: …And a Bottle of Rum
description:
The Bottle of Rum expansion includes 8 wooden rum tokens and 4 new Captain cards for use in the Advanced Mode: The Captains.

Captain cards provide bonuses if you serve their favorite treats, adding new layers of strategy. In the base game box, there are 14 Captain cards, and the Bottle of Rum expansion brings that total to 18 Captain cards.

When a player serves a meal in the Captains' Quarters, they may collect one or more Captain Rewards in addition to their regular Bounties and Rewards. The Captain card shows the bonus requirements and reward. Player's may receive the same Captain's bonus multiple times if they meet the bonus requirement multiple times (in the same service or different services).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kartano: Das gnadenlose Kartenspiel um Fluch oder Segen
description:
Take on the role of a notorious pirate and go on a treasure hunt. The aim of the game is to grab and hoard as many treasures as possible. But watch out! You are in danger! Get your treasures to safety before you end up empty-handed.

You can play Kartano with two or up to six players. To start the game, simply shuffle all the cards and place them in the middle of the table. Each player starts with no cards in their hand. Each turn consists of an action and a card draw. The action is always optional, while drawing a card is mandatory.

You start the game by taking the top card from the deck, visible only to you, and placing it in your hand. Once you have drawn the card, it is the next pirate's turn.

If you have collected a few cards, you can begin to stockpile your treasures. You need a certain number of treasure cards before you can start hoarding them. The fun part is that you can deceive other players with treasure cards that have no value. During the game, you can swap hoarded decks with other players. It is always wise to remember which deck had zero value.

You can also draw certain function cards during the game. These can help you collect more treasure, steal treasure from others or, in the worst case, lose all the treasure in your hand.

There are three 'Fiasco' cards that bring the game to an end. When the last Fiasco card is drawn, the game is over and you can start counting your hoarded treasures. Do you still have unhoarded treasures in your hand? Well, they do not count towards your score. So hurry up and hoard your treasures quick before you lose them again within the next round.

The player with the most treasure points wins the round.

A standard Kartano game consists of seven rounds. A single round lasts an average of 15 minutes. Kartano is suitable for ages 12 and up.

So weigh anchor and set sail - aye-aye!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pirates of the High Teas: Golden Ganaches
description:
Four gilded Dish cards that add a little more chocolate and a lot more treasure to your menu.

Includes 4 Unique Dish Cards (with gold foil):

     Dark Chocolate Bon Bombs
     White Chocolate Cheesecake
     Ruby Chocolate Bombe Cake
     Milk Chocolate Layer Cake


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pirates of the High Teas: Coursair's Courage Promo Card
description:
A unique Tea card that matches the Pirates of the High Teas custom blend! (A delicious oolong with lemongrass, butterfly pea flower, and enough lemon to stave off scurvy)

—description from the publisher

On September 6, 1939, German troops entered Kraków, beginning its occupation. Just 12 days later, on September 18, the first underground resistance newspaper was published, and soon after, more clandestine organizations began to form. In this game, your mission is to create a resistance movement. Sometimes, you will have the support of loyal friends, but beware — there may be traitors among you. Will you rise to the challenge?

In Persecuted: Kraków, players cooperate to search districts for useful items cards and boost their stats. They can also move using action points, try to  avoid German guards and control the amount of taxes. Another option is a day's work (rolling a die). Sometimes, players can also use their characters' special abilities. Victory is achieved when each player has 7 resistance points.

In competitive mode, players can try to claim a cooperative victory, but one false move and one of them can turn into a collaborator. From that point on, their task is to make all the other players collaborators and then beat them for points.

An 18 card expansion for Grimgrove!

Grimgrove Mini Expansion introduces terrifying new challenges and powerful rewards for seasoned adventurers.

This expansion adds:

Deadly new Enemies - 9 Cards.

Powerful new Items - 3 Cards.

Unpredictable new Events - 6 Cards.

In Visit Porto, you play the role of a tourist exploring the city of Porto. The aim of the game is to plan a sightseeing route as efficiently as possible, selecting and including different tourist attractions. When it is your turn, you can either carry out one of the actions on the board or take a break and skip your turn.
VisitPorto is played over as many rounds as it takes for one player to successfully complete 5 routes.

The game Visit Porto was commissioned by Porto's City Hall and will have a minimal distribution on the market.

—description from the designer

Poupar €´ Ganhar is an educational game to provide basic notions about financial management and the physical properties of money. In this game, the players will be assuming the role of a young buyer with a limited budget set by his or her parents. The board simulates a supermarket, where prices are set, but as the game progresses things can get cheaper or more expensive.

The players will have a limited number of rounds to acquire all their parents have on the list, being at every moment tempted by delicious treats that they can buy. Every time they acquire a product their parents wanted, they get a merit pig.

In the last stage of the game, they can use any amount of money they still have left, plus activate their parents help, in the form of the merit pigs, to get a "dream object", like a console, cell phone, bycicle, etc.
You are helping the rat to collect as much as possible of Cards Sets. The rat who will reach the highest score from sets collected will win the race and be called the PACKRAT of the game.

boardgame: AIvory Tower: Artificial Intelligence and the University of the Future
description:
AI is taking over the Ivory Tower — will you embrace it, regulate it, or resist it?

In AIvory Tower, a fast-playing four-player card based game, players take on the roles of faculty, administrators, students, and AI researchers —navigating the rise of artificial intelligence in higher education. Every decision shapes the future: Will AI streamline grading or erode academic integrity? Will automation enhance research or replace human scholars?

Debate policies, negotiate alliances, and cast decisive votes in dilemmas that will define the university’s fate. Balance funding, ethics, and AI influence as you work toward your personal victory — whether it’s full AI integration, ethical regulation, or the preservation of traditional education.

—description from the designer

Rise of Myths: Cthulhu ia roguelite deck-building game in which you will improve your deck after each attempt, as you face the Great Old Ones and their minions, while they try to destroy your body and sanity. Take on the role of one of the daring adventurers and face the threat that looms over you alone or as a team, inspired by the works of H.P. Lovecraft and his collaborators. Prepare yourself for an epic battle. Every move can make the difference between salvation or absolute doom. 

In RoM: Cthulhu, players will join forces to combat monsters in this deckbuilding game set in Lovecraft's world.  Each game will be different as you encounter randomly generated encounters and locations. Each player character and Great Old One location comes with unique attributes and mechanics, offering a variety of end-game possibilities. Rules also allow for different difficulty levels to suit each player.

"Family Values? Nah, Family Valuables!" You and 2-10 greedy relatives were living it up on the galaxy’s finest space station—until a fire turned your vacay into a screaming, stuff-grabbing frenzy! The rescue shuttle’s pods are here, but in Relative Space, it’s every family member for themselves. Hurl your ukulele in to nix their sock monkey—because nothing beats the glee of screwing over your family mid-catastrophe. It’s a chaotic blast of ensuring your items make it on the pods as you battle to save your loot. First to pack everything jets off triumphant, waving bye-bye to the flaming rubble and their tears!

In Relative Space, each player has a pile of 12 cards representing their valuables, with the 5 cards at the bottom of the pile facedown. In turn order, players must play one card to an available escape pod. The card played must be clear of cards on top, and should be of equal or high value than the top card in the escape pod. First player to pack all 12 of the cards in front of them wins!

—description from the publisher

Family Football, (and Family Football PLUS, an enhanced version,) is a free, downloadable American Football card and dice game.

Using actual teams, based on actual professional seasons. Available for free are ALL professional teams, from 1950, forward, along with a limited set of college teams.  Play ANY team against ANY OTHER team, regardless of season.
Designed to play an entire game in about 15 minutes. So simple to play, grandparents can play with their grandchildren.

Merriam-Webster defines cattywampus as "askew, or awry”, or diagonally out of place.

In Cattywampus, two artists pair up, each sketching half of a bonkers image, from 2 words chosen in secret. Imagine sketching a breakdancing pizza or a jousting llama, without knowing how their teammate is drawing their half of the combination! Once completed, the rest of the table must guess the crazy mashup.

boardgame: 寄り添って時間旅行 (Time Travel Together)
description:
Mission-based trick-taking games gained popularity with The Crew: The Quest for Planet Nine and became even richer with The Crew: Mission Deep Sea.

This game takes that mission-based trick-taking concept and evolves it into a two-player experience.

In traditional trick-taking, dealing all cards reveals too much, while missing cards can limit missions. To solve this, we’ve introduced a small additional system.

Predicting the color distribution of your opponent’s hand and determining the right amount of wins is key.

This game distills trick-taking fundamentals—prediction, execution, and result.

Specifically, when you follow a suit, the difference in card values determines how far a piece moves. The mission conditions specify the movement destination.

Landing exactly where you need to may seem tricky... but don't worry! We've fine-tuned the system to ensure balance. Leave that part to us!

—description from the publisher

Its a busy night at Club Spooky and you’re trying to assemble the coolest group of ghouls. Here’s hoping your Rivals don't steal them... and they actually stick around...

Each round you’ll be using your “Talking Point” tokens to bid for the attention of the Creatures entering the Lobby. One-up your rivals long enough and you’ll get to add them to your clique. Each creature has a set of traits and a goal. If your clique meets their goals at the end of the game, you’ll score points. Whoever has the most points at the end of the game, wins.

—description from the designer

SkyLinks is a vertical stacking game using magnetic cubes with letters, numbers and symbols.

Strategically build a tower while challenging yourself to think vertically, match cleverly, and ascend to new heights of fun. Score as you attach cubes and expand the tower.

Components:

     50 magnetic cubes with letters, bigrams, numbers and symbols
     bag
     3x3-inch steel base.


Setup:
Place all cubes in a bag. Take a random cube and place on the base. Each player grabs two cubes. Grab a pen and paper for scoring.

Note that the first cube can be rotated, examined and reoriented by the first player to attach their cube.

Game Play:
All players play simultaneously attaching cubes to create a tower, but must call out their connections before attaching cube(s).
Each player adds one or more cubes from their hand to the tower.
Connections are made as follows:

     a word with at least three adjacent letters
     a math equation with three numbers
     a pair of identical symbols.


Forming a word: 
Add one or two cubes such that their adjacent faces form a word of at least three letters. At least half of the letters must be in the same direction. Letters can traverse an adjacent corner.
Forming a math equation:
Attach two numbers to an existing number such that a simple arithmetic equation is formed, for example 2+5=7.
Pairing symbols:
Attach a symbol (like a star) to a matching symbol, face to face.

Cubes can be placed vertically and horizontally.

Scoring:
2 points per equation
1 point per symbol match
1 point per letter in complete word
It is possible to score multiple form connections at the same time.

Penalty:
If a player causes one or more cubes to collapse, lose one point per cube. Return the cubes to the bag.

Winning the Game: 
The game ends when all cubes have been placed.
The player with the most points is the winner.

—description from the publisher

In the party game Peanuts: The Doctor Is In, Charlie Brown and the gang have plenty of problems, but luckily they have the ever-helpful Lucy Van Pelt ready to dole out advice. Voice your problems so that others can respond using classic Peanuts comic panels as advice for life's toughest questions. Stuck with unhelpful cards? Use your "Good Grief!" token for a new hand of cards.

Whoever offers the best advice wins the card, and the player with the most points after a set number of rounds wins.

You and your friends have died, and one of you is probably to blame! Trouble is, you’ve all forgotten what happened, and you're not sure if it might have been you...

You’ve got five minutes to figure out which of you is responsible for your accidental death. When the game starts you’ll be dealt a hand of cards that gives you some information about what happened, but importantly, not enough information to know if it was you or not. So you’ll have to carefully ask questions and do some deduction to figure out who’s responsible without outing yourself if it turns out it’s you.

—description from the designer

Be the first to create a phrase that is both funny and grammatically correct using seven cards.

Shuffle the cards and give six to each player. The rest form a face down deck.

Each turn a player draws a card and either 1) places it in front of them to either help build their phrase or counteract an attack card an opponent previously placed in front of them 2) places an attack card in front of another player 3) discards a card from their hand to the bottom of the draw pile.

Prepare for launch! In Set in Space, you're an astronaut exploring the vast reaches of our solar system. Each turn, launch your rocket and visit three unique destinations, collecting sets of planets, moons, space craft and more.
